As the apocalypse arrived, humanity’s original grain production was nearly wiped out, and raising poultry and livestock was naturally out of the question. Meat resources would become precious, even more so than alcohol in some respects.

After all, alcohol could still be brewed using greenhouse-grown grains, but pork, beef...

Even if the country had established underground farms before the apocalypse, such goods would remain luxuries for the privileged few and would not flow into ordinary shelters, unlike before the apocalypse where they were accessible to everyone.

Though the country had devised plans prior to the apocalypse for obtaining food for shelters post-apocalypse, neither mushrooms nor rat meat were viewed as comparable to pork or beef in taste.

Of course, there was hope for the taste of artificial synthetic meat?

As demand creates markets, artificial meat might become popular in the near future, with improvements in taste making it more meat-like becoming a basic requirement.

But for Chen Xin, this provided an opportunity to anticipate whether the upgraded greenhouse would feature animal husbandry functionality.

If he could gain access to animal husbandry, perhaps Chen Xin could secure a stable source of meat.

While humans won’t die from not eating meat, malnutrition is not the state to survive an apocalypse. Consuming animal protein is necessary for maintaining human health and nutritional balance.

Despite the difficulty of filling one’s stomach in the apocalypse, being well-fed and well-nourished would significantly enhance one’s quality of life in such a world.

However, one issue did trouble Chen Xin: where to source animal breeding stock.

The system upgrade might enable the shelter to rear animals, but Chen Xin’s system only had the upgrade function, and he didn’t know if acquiring animal husbandry capabilities would come with a few live animals.

In this environment, trying to find live animals himself was no easy task.

But perhaps larger shelters might have animals? Not to mention, mice and worms, two of the key food sources prepared by the country before the apocalypse, were planned to sustain humans alongside mushrooms.

Yet the idea of eating mice and worms still unsettled Chen Xin, who wasn’t yet in an "eat anything" mindset.

While rat meat counts as a legitimate ingredient before the apocalypse, albeit a bit exotic, it could be a good source of animal protein during the apocalypse if hygiene could be managed.

As for earthworms, most people didn’t actually eat them before the apocalypse, but in "Wandering Earth," after humanity moved underground following Earth’s freezing, edible earthworms became one of the main food sources, making this concept widely known.

The current Blue Star environment is not much different from that in "Wandering Earth," given that the environmental challenges are somewhat similar, though Blue Star hasn’t reached the extreme conditions depicted, nor have people moved into kilometer-deep underground cities.

These differences are of course due to the technological gap between fiction and reality. After all, while digging a kilometer-deep hole was somewhat feasible for real humans, constructing a kilometer-deep city was beyond current capabilities.

Moreover, humans don’t need to dig such a deep hole.

In "Wandering Earth," building such a deep underground city was a practical need, whereas in reality, to avoid the extreme cold in apocalyptic weather, digging sufficiently deep to harness geothermal heat for warmth is a feasible solution, but existing technology limits this, and it’s not truly necessary.

With current technology, humans can build underground cities, like Montreal in Maple Country which has a 17-kilometer underground city constructed since the mid-20th century, over half a century ago now.

But the depth of this underground city is only that of conventional underground structures, equivalent to modern underground pedestrian passages or shopping streets, not the exaggerated five-thousand-meter depth of "Wandering Earth."

Realistically, undertaking construction at such depths is nearly impossible for humans.

Though the Montreal underground city may not be deep, it can withstand the annual four to five months of sub-minus thirty-degree long winters.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 46 Considerations for Heating (Requesting Recommendations and Favorites)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

For humanity, environments of dozens of degrees below zero indeed represent life-prohibitive zones utterly unsuitable for human survival. However, even in the most hostile polar environments, humans have nonetheless established permanent polar research stations, and these stations are not deeply buried underground but instead placed directly on the surface.

Temperatures of dozens of degrees below zero are indeed perilous death traps, but with current human technology, building a warm and comfortable research station in such an environment is not a problem.

Take the polar research station built by Flame Country as an example. The interior of the container houses is sprayed with a 120mm thick polyurethane insulation layer, and then decorated with outfitting panels. This setup is both warm and stylish and can completely withstand the bitter cold of dozens of degrees below zero in the polar environment.

Of course, the station itself does not generate heat. No matter how well insulated, heat exchange still occurs, so the research station is equipped with heating devices.

This proves that with existing human technology, there is genuinely no need to dig several kilometers underground to face extreme cold temperatures.

With current human technological capabilities, as long as sufficient insulation and protection are in place when building a shelter, and reliable heating equipment is equipped, it is entirely possible to ensure that a shelter can be warm and comfortable in an environment of dozens of degrees below zero.

However, in this era when the apocalypse has arrived, ensuring reliable heating equipment is not so straightforward.

Taking polar research stations as an example, there are generally two main heating methods, electrical heating and fuel heating.

Since research stations typically generate electricity 24 hours a day, larger stations generally use indoor electric heaters for warmth. This method is simple and easy to install and maintain, allows easy temperature control, and is clean. However, most of the station’s electricity is often used for heating, and when external temperatures are low, it can easily cause generator overload and, if improperly used, can lead to electrical fires.

Some research stations use oil boilers as the primary heating equipment, similar to ordinary heating in the northern regions of Flame Country. This requires extra installation and maintenance of heating equipment but reduces the risk of electrical fires and effectively decreases generator load.

Additionally, some small summer research stations only have simple gas heating equipment, using special gas heaters to burn bottled liquefied gas or natural gas indoors. However, it is essential to ensure ventilation and constantly monitor the carbon monoxide levels in the air to prevent carbon monoxide poisoning.

These three heating methods are all well-developed and reliable, each with its pros and cons, but all are capable of meeting the heating needs of a shelter.

According to Chen Xin’s knowledge, the three large shelters in the city use additional boiler heating systems. However, they use coal, not oil.

Flame Country’s oil reserves are not particularly abundant, but the coal reserves are relatively quite sufficient. After all, Flame Country ranks ninth in the world for oil reserves but third for coal reserves.

Moreover, oil must be processed from crude oil into gasoline, diesel, and kerosene before it can be burned. In contrast, coal can be directly burned after extraction, so as long as railway transport can be guaranteed, burning coal is undoubtedly more economical, stable, and reliable than burning oil.

Therefore, it’s an excellent choice for large shelters to use boiler heating. As for additional maintenance work, in the post-apocalyptic world, manpower is the least valuable resource. A large shelter housing tens of thousands of people will never worry about finding workers.

For not-so-large shelters, there is no need to adopt such means. As long as they can guarantee electricity supply, they can use electrical heating without the need to install heating boilers.

For private shelters like the one belonging to Chen Xin and Mo Qingyan, installing a heating air conditioner or preparing two electric heaters can basically meet the heating needs. Installing a boiler would just be a waste of money and impractical.

However, this matter does remind Chen Xin that upgrading the heating system should be scheduled in the shelter’s upgrade plan.

Even though he has prepared electric heaters and electric blankets for himself, wouldn’t upgrading for greater comfort be desirable? Why insist on wearing cold-weather gear indoors and wrapping in three layers of blankets when sleeping?

Chen Xin thought about this, habitually took out his notebook, and began jotting down and sketching his next shelter upgrade plan.

On the notebook, based on the previous sequence recorded by Chen Xin, items such as electricity supply, lighting, food, and medical care were listed. Chen Yu thought it over and added heating as a project before lighting.

Upgrading the power supply system is undoubtedly the top priority because, among the other four projects noted in the notebook, except for the non-priority medical category, the other three projects would increase the shelter’s electricity consumption. Therefore, upgrading the power system to increase the shelter’s power generation is an inevitable task.

Furthermore, Chen Xin must consider the fuel consumption caused by power generation. Based on his current electricity usage, on average, ten liters of gasoline are required daily for power generation. As electricity consumption increases, fuel consumption will inevitably rise, so Chen Xin must consider both gasoline replenishment and acquiring more sustainable and efficient power-generation methods.

Under normal circumstances, the only long-term sustainable and efficient power generation method Chen Xin could consider would be nuclear power—an approach he hardly has access to. But for Chen Xin, who has a system, geothermal power generation and geomagnetic power generation are currently in the realm of science fiction and laboratory research but are within his future upgrade plans.

Therefore, regarding securing efficient and sustainable power, Chen Xin is not worried. Continuously upgrading the shelter will suffice. With enough survival points, Chen Xin believes that even advanced technology such as antimatter reactors or artificial sun-like power sources could be upgraded.

Though these science fiction technologies seem too remote for Chen Xin and require astronomical survival points, under the magnificent power of the system, they are indeed things to look forward to.

While the future holds promise, it’s too early to worry about such things now. The most important task at present is to re-cultivate the area vacated after harvesting vegetables in the greenhouse and sow new vegetables so that there will be ample harvest next month to exchange for the materials needed for upgrades with Mo Qingyan.

However, considering the material exchange, Chen Xin thinks he should first make a list to see what materials he currently needs. This will help him target what he wants when trading with Mo Qingyan and allow her to prepare in advance, thus saving time.

Even with Uncle Ding’s connection on Mo Qingyan’s side, obtaining materials still requires time.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 47: Farmland and Upgrade Materials
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Since it was decided to exchange vegetables for supplies, the greenhouse was clearly the area Chen Xin most needed to upgrade now.

Currently, half of the land in Chen Xin’s greenhouse is planted with carrots, which still need about two months until harvest. The other half was previously planted with lettuce and cabbage, which have just been harvested. Chen Xin needs to fertilize the land again, then till it and sow new seeds.

Fertilizing the land involves tilling, commonly known as plowing or cultivating the soil.

This is done to break up clumps in the soil and loosen it, facilitating crop growth. While fertilizing, the nutrients are also incorporated into the soil layers, allowing plants to better absorb the nutrients.

Tilling is one of the most important parts of farming, and it’s also one of the most labor-intensive tasks, which is why in ancient times, oxen or even warhorses were used for plowing.

Relying solely on human strength to till a plot of land is extremely exhausting, while using animal power saves human labor, increases efficiency, and saves time.

After all, plowing with an ox is entirely different from plowing by hand.

However, Chen Xin’s greenhouse is too small, and he doesn’t have an ox, so he can only till it himself with a hoe, bit by bit.

Fortunately, the area Chen Xin needs to till is not large, and since the temperature in the greenhouse is constant, there’s no need to rush. He only needs to finish and plant the seeds.

Even so, Chen Xin spent most of the day tilling fifty square meters of land and sowing seeds.

Looking at the tilled land, Chen Xin hammered his lower back, feeling that indeed farming was exhausting work. He also considered upgrading his farming tools while upgrading the greenhouse to improve the efficiency of tilling.

As the greenhouse is upgraded, the planting area will certainly increase, leading to an inevitable increase in workload.

Although there’s not much else to do in the shelter, and farming is fine, this physical labor is quite challenging for someone like Chen Xin, who hasn’t done agricultural work for thirty years. Plus, relying solely on manual labor is fine for small areas, but for larger areas, using machinery is the right way to improve efficiency.

Perhaps he could ask Mo Qingyan for a few small agricultural machines? Although survival points are valuable and can be used to upgrade tools, Chen Xin felt he shouldn’t waste them, especially since small agricultural machines aren’t difficult to obtain even in this post-apocalyptic era.

In addition to small agricultural machines, Chen Xin also planned to get a few supplementary lights, which are essential tools in the greenhouse and necessary materials for upgrading it.

As for other materials needed for upgrading the greenhouse, it’s just steel and cement, basic construction materials essential for expanding the greenhouse.

Of course, if Chen Xin included the equipment needed for hydroponic planting in the materials for the upgrade, he would not only expand the land area but also add another layer of hydroponic planting trays, doubling the planting area of the greenhouse.

This is the difference between using materials for upgrades and using survival points; using survival points only allows for one of two upgrade options given by the system, without the ability to choose both in one upgrade.

But with material upgrades, there’s no such trouble. As long as all the necessary materials are available, there’s no need for a choice of two, and both options can be achieved in one upgrade.

Regarding the functional differences brought by the two upgrade methods, Chen Xin felt it might be a feature set by a high civilization when designing this system to add enjoyment to the survival game.

After all, for individuals capable of playing survival games in high civilizations, they seek a better gaming experience.

Simply staying in one place and upgrading items with accumulated survival points seems somewhat passive, leading to a poor gaming experience with no real sense of participation.

Maybe at the start, one would spend some time setting up a hiding place or a small survival hut, but with stored survival points, there’s no need to gather resources or work to improve living conditions.

But collecting upgrade materials oneself is different; using hard-earned resources for upgrades and watching the place one built improve greatly satisfies those players’ sense of achievement and participation.

Additionally, collecting resources and upgrading also saves survival points, indirectly encouraging system users to upgrade with resources.

After all, resource upgrading saves some survival points; while collecting materials, enough survival points can be accumulated, which invisibly increases players’ survival chances using resource upgrades.

Especially since resources can be stored in advance, as long as there are enough resources and survival points, upgrades can begin.

Those who directly upgrade using survival points have weaker item functions and their progress is slower compared to those using resources.

For high civilizations, this might just be a game, but for Chen Xin, these are lifesaving items that greatly enhance his chances of surviving the apocalypse.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin hurriedly made a list to confirm what he needed.

"One hundred kilograms of steel, five bags of cement, a set of hydroponic planting equipment, supplementary lights..." Chen Xin listed his required resources, shaking his head: "These are quite a lot of things. To exchange for these, I might have to bleed dry."

The greenhouse had half its area planted with carrots, maturing and harvestable in three months, while the other half’s vegetables had a very limited yield. Before upgrading the greenhouse, relying on that production might require an entire month’s yield.

However, there’s no need to rush; he can first have Mo Qingyan help collect materials through Uncle Ding’s connections, using this month’s production as a deposit, and settle the remainder next month when the carrots are harvested.

With those materials, Chen Xin can upgrade the greenhouse, quadrupling its planting area.

Moreover, using the system for upgrades, he saves the construction time required for normal work, making it more time-efficient.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 48: Live long and prosper
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

According to Chen Xin’s estimation, if he could get the materials to upgrade the greenhouse from Mo Qingyan this month and plant vegetables in time, he would not only harvest seven times the current vegetable yield next month but also collect the carrots planted last month, which would be quite a bounty.

However, with such a large harvest of vegetables, he would need to handle them promptly, as Chen Xin couldn’t possibly consume all of them himself. The only option is to trade them for supplies.

After all, the large freezer he exchanges from Mo Qingyan can hold only a few hundred pounds of vegetables before it’s full, which is just his monthly harvest. If the yield truly increases sevenfold, he certainly wouldn’t have enough space for all the vegetables.

After careful consideration, Chen Xin decided to stick to his original plan and plant upland rice.

If he uses the materials for an upgrade, simultaneously increasing the planting area and hydroponic growing trays, Chen Xin could plant upland rice on the ground and move the vegetable planting to the hydroponic trays.

This way, he could harvest enough grain for himself while having enough fresh vegetables to trade for supplies.

After all, the upgraded greenhouse would be 200 square meters, with an equal area of hydroponic trays. Two hundred square meters of land for upland rice could yield about 600 pounds of rice annually with two harvests each year.

The vegetables on the hydroponic trays, with his current planting ratio of half carrots and half cabbage and lettuce, would yield about 600 pounds of cabbage, 600 pounds of lettuce, and roughly 300 pounds of carrots each month on average.

Among these, although carrots are harvested every three months, they are more storage-resistant and undoubtedly a premium trade material.

Calculating in this way, Chen Xin suddenly found his resources abundant, with a significant surplus of both rice and vegetables.

He could trade most of the monthly vegetable output for supplies to upgrade his shelter, improving his life conditions. He could also exchange for other things to enrich his meals, such as meat and alcohol.

These outputs could also be traded for other things Chen Xin needed, for example, small farm machinery necessary for cultivation and firearms for self-defense.

Of course, Chen Xin doesn’t necessarily have to trade for a gun. He can use vegetables to trade for supplies and then upgrade the weapons cabinet, unlock the firearm manufacturing blueprint, and make firearms himself.

He was just unsure of what kind of gun he could make. If it’s something primitive, the reliability and usability would be worse compared to traded firearms.

After all, he could get a pistol from Uncle Ding through Ding Ning, while manufacturing himself could result in a simple firearm without rifling if the upgrade level isn’t high enough.

Such guns aren’t unusable, but indeed impractical and not easy to carry around.

However, upgrading the weapons cabinet is a necessity. As the saying goes, "Do not harm others, but always be wary of others." Having a reliable weapon for self-defense is also a guarantee for surviving better in this post-apocalyptic world.

With this in mind, Chen Xin added the materials needed for the weapons cabinet upgrade to his list, deciding to use this month’s vegetable output as a deposit to contact Mo Qingyan and Uncle Ding for the supplies on his list, with the balance settled after expanding production following next month’s greenhouse upgrade.

Although this payment method might seem unreliable in the post-apocalypse, with his relationship with Mo Qingyan and Ding Ning, Chen Xin believed he could still get these items.

After listing all the items, Chen Xin prepared to set up his communication antenna to connect his shortwave radio, so he wouldn’t have to travel to the city whenever he wanted to communicate with Mo Qingyan and others.

The temperature wasn’t too low at the moment, so a trip wasn’t too problematic, but as the weather got colder, going out would be a hassle.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin took out the military shortwave antenna made in Lucia from the metal box, carrying the case out of his shelter.

The best place to set up the antenna was, of course, at the top of the mountain where his shelter was located. However, considering the maintenance and cable setup would be troublesome on the mountaintop, Chen Xin chose to set up the antenna halfway up the mountain.

There was a pre-made concrete base here, though previously burnt to a mess by a fire. After sweeping away the surface ash and residue, the concrete-cast base remained intact, albeit looking charred.

After cleaning the concrete base, Chen Xin took out the shortwave antenna from the box, set it onto the concrete base, and screwed in the bolts into the pre-drilled holes, securing the antenna in place.

After setting up the antenna, Chen Xin needed to connect it to the shortwave radio inside the shelter.

Fortunately, the cables had already been buried underground, so there was no need to lay new ones.

Chen Xin opened the grid of the ventilation outlet under the concrete base, pulled out a cable, and plugged it into the socket under the antenna.

After doing all this, Chen Xin didn’t forget to put a camouflage cover over the antenna to prevent it from being discovered and damaged.

Though hardly anyone could appear outdoors in this post-apocalyptic world, those who could are certainly not ordinary people. If someone discovered the antenna, stole it, or got ideas about his shelter, it would create trouble.

So to mitigate unnecessary trouble, Chen Xin covered the shortwave antenna with a layer of camouflage fabric, making the entire antenna look like a small black dirt mound.

Looking at his handiwork, Chen Xin dusted off his hands with satisfaction and returned to the shelter.

Inside the shelter, Chen Xin also took out the shortwave radio, placed it on the reserved table in the living room, and connected the cable’s other end to the radio.

Turning on the radio and adjusting the channels, Chen Xin made his first call: "This is Writer, calling all fellow survivors, please respond if you hear. We are still alive, Live long and prosper."

This phrase was an agreed-upon signal from before. After the disaster, everyone would say it when making contact to confirm their identity and survival status.

Chen Xin waited, but the radio remained silent, with only faint electrical currents humming, making him feel somewhat dejected.

Just as Chen Xin was about to turn off the unresponsive radio, a reply finally came through: "This is Farmer, glad to hear your voice, Writer. We are still alive, Live long and prosper."

"This is Driller, I have ample resources and can accommodate any fellow in need of refuge. We are still alive, Live long and prosper."

"This is Climber, doing well here, glad everyone made it through the disaster. We are still alive, Live long and prosper."

"This is..."

As one response after another echoed through, hearing everyone’s replies filled Chen Xin with joy and excitement, proving that all the companions in the survival group were still alive.

The last response also came at that moment: "I’m Dragon Girl, I’m doing well too, it’s great that no one is out of touch! We are still alive, Live long and prosper!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 49 Exchange of Needs (Please Recommend, Please Collect)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Being able to reconnect with friends after the disaster and confirming that everyone survived is undoubtedly the most joyous thing.

Through brief exchanges over the radio, Chen Xin also affirmed everyone’s current situation. He learned that although they might have encountered some minor issues, everyone was doing quite well overall, which made Chen Xin very happy.

After all, the people in this survival group are familiar friends from before, and if anyone didn’t make it because of this disaster, it would undoubtedly be quite an unpleasant thing.

Moreover, this is no longer the peaceful environment of the past. Having just gone through an apocalyptic disaster, everyone’s hearts are actually a bit anxious.

In such times, being able to meet familiar people or things, although it might not be of much use, can somewhat provide some solace to the soul, giving everyone that little bit more to hold on to in this post-disaster land.

In the exchange of necessities among everyone, Chen Xin also learned about their current status.

Because of previous ample preparation, and stockpiling enough supplies, as well as building their own shelters, the members of the survival group were all in their shelters, successfully avoiding the earlier disaster. Some well-equipped group members even had the capacity to take in others.

However, while everyone survived and is doing relatively well, problems are not absent.

Most group members, although they have shelters and relatively sufficient supplies, are experiencing the same problem Chen Xin had earlier, which is the lack of domestic water.

Although everyone relied on water recycling devices to manage temporarily, in the long term, they would inevitably fall into a situation of having no water available.

Regarding this issue, Chen Xin didn’t have any way to help everyone, but the group member known as the "Drilling Expert," who worked in the drilling industry, suggested drilling wells as a solution to the water supply problem.

However, this suggestion faced construction difficulties because the shelters are all built underground, and also because the "Drilling Expert" is from Lin City, which couldn’t provide immediate assistance.

Fortunately, while there is a shortage of domestic water, the problem isn’t severe. For now, it just means living a little more frugally and paying extra attention to water conservation.

"If there’s a water shortage, drilling a well can solve the problem. Even if indoor construction is difficult, a well can be drilled outdoors with pipes leading inside. I’ve done this kind of work, and it’s not difficult."

In the communication channel, the "Drilling Expert" offered his professional knowledge to provide solutions for a few shelters facing water shortages. He also stated if needed, he could immediately bring people from Lin City to help those currently lacking water.

However, it is not something that can be accomplished quickly for him to come from Lin City.

"Expert, don’t come here; no one knows what the road conditions are like now. For safety reasons, we should find a solution on our side." Upon hearing the "Drilling Expert" suggest bringing people over, Mo Qingyan immediately intervened to stop him.

Although Flame Country wasn’t the main affected area when the meteorite fell, a lot of meteorite debris still hit the ground, and it’s unknown whether it damaged the roads.

And even if the roads are clear, coming from Lin City to our city still involves significant risks.

Therefore, after some discussion, Mo Qingyan suggested offering help to the shelters lacking water from this city.

At least she could use her connections with the SWAT team to borrow a watering truck previously used for municipal greening to help these shelters replenish freshwater with its tank.

After all, everyone’s shelter has water recycling devices, and one freshwater supply can last a long time.

"Let’s handle it this way for now. Ah Ning and I will find Uncle Ding to get in touch with the city government to see if we can help resolve this issue." Mo Qingyan said this in the communication channel, indicating that, for the time being, the government is still everyone’s trusted first choice.

In the face of major disasters, the handling methods of the Flame Country government have built strong public trust. Even though it’s a doomsday world now, everyone is still willing to trust the government unless it collapses or loses credibility.

After everyone finished discussing these things, Chen Xin finally brought up his intent to trade supplies with Mo Qingyan.

"Hey, how big is your greenhouse? You can offer so many vegetables for trade?" Upon hearing Chen Xin wanted to trade supplies, the first to speak wasn’t Mo Qingyan but rather a group member named "Old Farmer."

Since he personally farms and also does hydroponics, he has a sense of how much a greenhouse can produce.

For this reason, he was quite surprised by the amount of supplies Chen Xin wanted to exchange, as he roughly calculated that Chen Xin would need at least half an acre to produce so many vegetables in two months.

"I dug my greenhouse to 200 square meters, but now I’m planning to expand it, add more hydroponic trays, and stockpile some supplies. This way, if the shelter suffers any damage or needs expansion in the future, there won’t be a lack of resources." Chen Xin concealed the current state of his greenhouse and offered a simple explanation.

Having traded vegetables with Chen Xin once before, Mo Qingyan knew he could provide what he promised, so she didn’t ask much more and readily agreed: "This shouldn’t be a problem. When Ah Ning and I go find Uncle Ding, we’ll mention it to him. Since you can provide so many vegetables, Uncle Ding and his team will surely be willing to trade supplies with you."

"Thanks a lot, Dragon Lady." Chen Xin sincerely entrusted Mo Qingyan.

"It’s no big deal. Just give me a bit more vegetables for food later." Mo Qingyan responded heartily.

After wrapping up these matters, Chen Xin ended the call with the others and turned off his radio.

Mo Qingyan agreeing to help certainly put Chen Xin at ease. If this plan succeeds, the upgrade speed of his shelter can significantly increase.

As long as this channel remains stable, enabling monthly vegetable-for-supplies exchanges, Chen Xin can save many survival points and greatly enhance the upgrade pace of his shelter.

After all, this time he is exchanging supplies based on the maximum material demand given by the system.

According to the system’s rules, if he can provide all materials as required by the system, the survival points needed for an upgrade would be just one-tenth of the usual amount.

Take Chen Xin’s current plan to upgrade the planting greenhouse, for instance. If he upgrades using only survival points, the two upgrade plans require 2000 points and 3500 points, respectively.

But now, since Chen Xin can provide all the materials needed for both upgrade plans, the survival points required for the upgrade drop to 400 points, which is due to him opting for both upgrade plans at the same time.

It’s the same with other upgrade projects; as long as Chen Xin can provide enough materials, the required upgrade points can be reduced by a zero.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 50 Luncheon Meat
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

However, to achieve all this, Chen Xin must secure a stable supply channel to ensure he can obtain sufficient resources to meet the needs for system upgrades.

In addition, he must ensure that his shelter can produce enough to be traded for resources.

Otherwise, even if there is a trade channel, it would be for nothing if he has nothing to trade.

Under these circumstances, the greenhouse becomes the key to everything. Only by ensuring the greenhouse’s yield and subsequent upgrades can everything Chen Xin desires proceed smoothly.

"In that case, upgrading the kitchen and weapon cabinet will have to wait." Chen Xin worked on a spreadsheet on his computer, tallying the resources needed for the upgrades while adjusting the upgrade priorities of various facilities according to his actual needs.

Currently, on the list that Chen Xin has re-adjusted, the top upgrade priority is naturally the greenhouse, which he even marked in red as the most important item.

Next is the generator, as it’s crucial for the normal operation of the shelter.

However, upgrading the generator is not extremely urgent. If Chen Xin is willing to use more fuel, extend the daily power generation time, or connect the second backup generator to the power system of the shelter, he won’t need to worry about the power system in the short term.

So, although the upgrade priority of the power system is high, it’s not something that needs immediate upgrading.

Next is the weapon cabinet. After all, going out without a self-defense weapon would make Chen Xin feel insecure.

As for what’s below the weapon cabinet, it’s not the communication system upgrade, which isn’t very necessary for Chen Xin, nor the kitchen upgrade he had long planned, but the transportation vehicle, his SUV.

This is actually a very understandable necessary choice. An SUV is a good choice for transportation during peaceful times, but in this apocalyptic setting, especially when Chen Xin needs to transport vegetables outside for resource exchange, a vehicle not designed for cargo obviously won’t suffice.

This time, the resources Chen Xin traded with Mo Qingyan are only half of his monthly output. If the greenhouse upgrades successfully as planned, even if all the ground is used to grow dry rice and the hydroponic growing trays are used for vegetables, he can harvest twelve hundred pounds of vegetables and about eight hundred pounds of carrots once matured in the ground.

Altogether, this amount of vegetables weighs close to a ton. If Chen Xin only uses his SUV for transportation, he might have to make several trips to transport it all. Not to mention other aspects, just the gasoline consumption is a problem that Chen Xin has to face seriously.

So, considering the future transportation needs, upgrading the vehicle or acquiring a new cargo vehicle through resource exchange becomes inevitable.

Mo Qingyan does have a pickup truck, which was previously used to help tow a freezer to Chen Xin’s shelter. However, that’s Mo Qingyan’s vehicle, and borrowing it occasionally is fine, but he can’t possibly ask to borrow it every time he needs to transport vegetables, right?

Although Mo Qingyan may not mind, Chen Xin still feels it’s not very appropriate.

As for the issue of using cars in increasingly colder temperatures in the future, Chen Xin isn’t too worried.

Even though most car manufacturers in the world haven’t bothered to design cars that can be used in polar climates, cars themselves can still function normally in environments well below zero.

This has been well demonstrated in the past in the northern part of Flame Country, especially in the Northeast region, where winter temperatures often drop to minus thirty or forty degrees.

In such weather, although using cars is a severe test, the vast majority of cars can still operate normally.

However, anti-freezing is indeed a problem that needs consideration. After all, while the car can take it, humans may not be able to withstand minus several tens of degrees, truly potentially life-threatening cold.

Recording these thoughts on the planning sheet, Chen Xin finally shut down the computer, touched his slightly hungry stomach, and prepared to get something to eat.

Thanks to the first harvest of vegetables, Chen Xin’s food is now much more abundant. At least he doesn’t have to eat just one bag of self-heating food every day. He can have vegetables and luncheon meat cans together and occasionally add canned pork for an extra meal.

The richness of the food not only increases the total energy Chen Xin ingests daily but also makes the nutrition he receives more balanced and varied, avoiding the risk of malnutrition due to food scarcity.

In the living room "kitchen," since the kitchen upgrade has been removed from the priority list, Chen Xin’s current "kitchen" is only an induction cooker and a set of outdoor cooking utensils that he used in the past.

Besides that, there’s also a simple cutting board and a fruit knife that can be used for cutting vegetables, all cooking supplies Chen Xin used for outdoor activities or wilderness survival before.

Originally, there was a folding gas stove too, but that requires an additional gas cylinder connection. Given the apocalyptic situation, Chen Xin feels that the box of canned liquefied gas he has should be reserved for critical times.

Before the vegetable harvest, all Chen Xin could eat daily was self-heating food, so he only used a small pot from his outdoor cooking gear that could serve as both a frying pan and a food container.

But now with vegetables and cans, at least for meals, Chen Xin can prepare stir-fried cabbage or lettuce, or stir-fry vegetables with luncheon meat, even pan-frying luncheon meat.

As for the canned pork, bearing in mind that once they’re gone, it would be hard to get more, Chen Xin doesn’t splurge by having it every meal but plans to consume a can every week, making it last as long as possible.

After all, pork might become very scarce in the future. In contrast, luncheon meat cans, even if they contain cockroaches or mice as ingredients, would likely remain a future staple for Chen Xin.

Thinking about this, Chen Xin ripped open a packet of self-heating food, added a little water to it, and set it aside. Soon, the heat pack inside the package reacted with the water, releasing heat.

Meanwhile, Chen Xin took out the leftover half of the luncheon meat from the morning, sliced it thinly on the cutting board, then placed a frying pan on the induction cooker. After heating the pan and adding oil, he put the slices of luncheon meat in to fry.

The rich smell of meat spread with the sizzling sound, enticing Chen Xin, who hadn’t smelled such aromas in a month.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 51: Qin Lan (Seeking recommendations, seeking favorites)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As usual, Chen Xin was eating his meal, but today he treated himself to a can of pork, as a little improvement to his diet.

While eating, he was chatting with Mo Qingyan through the shortwave radio placed on the side, discussing matters related to supplies.

After a month of gathering, Mo Qingyan had already obtained all the supplies Chen Xin needed through Uncle Ding’s connections, and Uncle Ding also agreed to Chen Xin’s installment payment terms. These supplies had now been delivered to Mo Qingyan.

"The ingredients, should I deliver them to you tomorrow, or will you come over to pick them up?" Mo Qingyan seemed to be eating as well, as even through the radio, Chen Xin could hear Mo Qingxin and Ding Ning’s squabbling over food.

However, this didn’t disrupt Chen Xin’s thoughts, and without much consideration, he replied, "You help bring them over. My vehicle can’t carry that much stuff anyway, and you can also take this month’s vegetables."

Mo Qingyan found nothing unusual in Chen Xin’s choice and agreed readily.

"It was really hard on you to gather this batch of supplies so quickly, thank you, Dragon Maiden." Chen Xin was genuinely grateful to Mo Qingyan for her help. Although their relationship was indeed very good, a thank you was still necessary.

"Saying such things is too formal. Besides, Uncle Ding was the main one who helped with the supplies, I only passed on the message for you." Mo Qingyan responded as straightforwardly as usual but also reminded Chen Xin, "Though Uncle Ding agreed to your request to complete the rest of the supplies next month, he asked to send someone to see you, saying he wants to confirm whether you can afford so many supplies... Ah Xin, what do you think about this matter?"

"How do I think about it? How can I think about it? Either I sit and think or stand and think, unless I lie down and think?" Chen Xin joked, but he understood the implication in Mo Qingyan’s words, becoming wary.

Uncle Ding said he wanted to send someone to check it out, maybe just to confirm whether Chen Xin could pay as he claimed, but what if someone got greedy?

Chen Xin and Mo Qingyan were very close friends, one of those solid types of friendships, and he trusted her enough. Ding Ning was also an acquaintance of Chen Xin’s; they had survived in the wild a few times together, and their relationship had always been good, which was why Chen Xin had let the two of them come to his shelter last time.

But with Uncle Ding, Chen Xin had only interacted with him a few times because he was Ding Ning’s dad, and all he knew was that his surname was Ding, and he was the captain of the city’s special police unit.

Besides that, Chen Xin didn’t even know his full name and had always just called him Uncle Ding.

Perhaps a respectable special police captain wouldn’t covet Chen Xin’s supplies, but what about others?

Chen Xin owned a well-equipped, self-sustaining shelter, an asset that could easily incite envy in this post-apocalyptic world.

The reason most people don’t commit crimes is not because of their moral superiority and self-control, except for a small minority, but rather due to the deterrent force of the law, making them aware of the high cost of crime and thus fearful of the punishment it might bring.

This is the significance of establishing laws by a nation, not to punish you after you commit a crime, but to deter you before you commit one, preventing you from daring to commit the crime.

But with the onset of the apocalypse and the collapse of the old social order, the original surveillance system fell apart, crime costs dropped sharply, and crime rates rose accordingly.

The person with inappropriate intentions that Mo Qingyan encountered earlier is enough to illustrate the dark sides of human nature growing in such an apocalyptic environment.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin asked through the shortwave radio, "What does the person Uncle Ding sends want to see?"

"What else? The old man is just afraid I’ll be fooled; a little bit of supplies is a minor issue, but if someone tricks his precious daughter, he can turn the whole city upside down, believe it or not?" replied Ding Ning, her tone showing a lot of dissatisfaction with her own father, yet there was a touch of pride in there.

Chen Xin shook his head at this, understanding roughly what Ding Ning meant. She thought Uncle Ding merely wanted to send someone to check if Chen Xin could pay, or perhaps to ensure Ding Ning wasn’t swindled into handing over resources.

Such concerns are human nature, but for Chen Xin, it was a reminder that he needed to be serious about the security of his shelter.

After thinking for a moment, he asked Ding Ning, "So, who does your dad plan to send? Someone from his team? There’s nothing much to see here, you two already came to check last time, right? No need to have anyone else come. I don’t want strangers knowing where my shelter is."

"Don’t worry, I’ve talked to my dad about this, and you know the person he’s sending." Ding Ning’s tone carried a hint of laughter, clearly understanding what Chen Xin was worried about. "You know Sister Lan, right? Do you think she’d harm you?"

"Qin Lan? Your dad’s letting her come?" Chen Xin was somewhat surprised and a bit embarrassed, evidently not expecting the person Uncle Ding chose.

Qin Lan was one of the few female special police officers in the city’s unit, not the desk job type, but a real special police officer serving as a sniper.

As a rare breed within the police system, combined with her decent looks, Qin Lan naturally became the police beauty of the city police department, attracting many suitors.

However, Qin Lan wasn’t interested in finding a partner within the police system, having dated Chen Xin once through Ding Ning’s introduction.

"What’s the matter? You’ve broken up for so long and Sister Lan isn’t bothered by it anymore." Ding Ning had introduced Chen Xin to Qin Lan back then, so she was well aware of what went on between them, "Besides, in the entire special police team, Sister Lan’s your closest relationship; can you trust anyone else?"

Ding Ning’s words left Chen Xin speechless.

He and Qin Lan had only split due to incompatible personalities, no one’s fault really, but since they became exes, the relationship turned somewhat awkward regardless.

But it was indeed as Ding Ning stated, he didn’t have such good relations with other members of the special police.

The reason, after all, was because of Qin Lan, as there were quite a few in the special police team who were pursuing her.

"Alright then, what time will you guys come tomorrow?" At this point, Chen Xin could only acquiesce, as if it was Qin Lan coming, he felt reassured.

"In the morning, we’ll come over after breakfast," Mo Qingyan replied to Chen Xin, setting the time.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 52 Harvesting Vegetables
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Brushing his teeth, washing his face, and after a long time, shaving his beard with a razor, Chen Xin looked at his clean reflection in the mirror. He touched his freshly shaven chin, his expression somewhat inexplicable.

He didn’t even know why he was tidying himself up like this. Logically speaking, in this apocalyptic world, he didn’t need to go out or meet anyone, so even if he didn’t shave, it wouldn’t matter.

But last night, he still set his alarm half an hour earlier, making it so he’d wake up earlier and tidy himself up, just like when he used to go on dates with Qin Lan.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but press his face with his hand.

It had become second nature. Every time he met Qin Lan, Chen Xin would make sure he was neat and tidy.

After all, Qin Lan was a great beauty, and if Chen Xin went to see her looking scruffy, he’d feel it was inappropriate.

With these old memories in mind, after tidying himself up, Chen Xin finished his breakfast as usual.

Once he confirmed over the radio that Mo Qingyan and her team had set out, Chen Xin headed towards the greenhouse, intending to harvest the vegetables before they arrived.

The crops in the greenhouse had all matured. Under Chen Xin’s meticulous care, both the cabbages and lettuce were growing well.

In fact, even the carrots planted two months ago could technically be harvested, but they were still only thumb-thick. While edible, harvesting them now would lead to a significant loss in yield, so Chen Xin decided to wait until next month.

Harvesting cabbages and lettuce didn’t require pulling the whole plant out of the ground. Doing so was not only likely to damage the crops but also inefficient, and could turn the neatly arranged plots into a mess.

The correct way to harvest them was to use a sickle to cut the cabbages and lettuce at the base, which maximized the harvest of mature crops while saving time and effort.

However, Chen Xin didn’t have a sickle. He used a long dagger from the weapons cabinet instead. It was a multifunctional knife he used during outdoor survival. Although it wasn’t particularly suitable, it was usable in the absence of proper tools.

Luckily, having gone through a harvest last month, Chen Xin had gained some experience, and given that the field wasn’t large, he quickly harvested all the cabbages.

Just as Chen Xin was piling the cabbages together for easy packing later, he heard the radio in the living room start to sound.

Worried that he wouldn’t hear Mo Qingyan’s calls while harvesting in the greenhouse, Chen Xin had turned the radio volume up to the max to ensure he could hear it even inside the planting greenhouse.

After briefly wiping his hands, Chen Xin returned to the living room and sat in front of the radio.

"Ingredients, ingredients! This is Dragon Lady, please respond! Please respond!" Mo Qingyan was calling over the radio. Her voice was already loud to begin with, and Chen Xin had turned up the volume, making her voice particularly earsplitting.

Chen Xin quickly turned down the radio’s volume, rubbed his ears, and responded, "I hear you. Why are you shouting so loudly?"

"I’m at your doorstep, hurry up and let me in!" Mo Qingyan, upon hearing Chen Xin’s response, lowered her voice slightly but still urged him.

"I know, I know, I’m opening it now." With that, Chen Xin went towards the shelter’s main door and opened the two gates of his shelter.

Outside the gate, a truck was parked, loaded with the supplies Chen Xin needed. Mo Qingyan, seeing Chen Xin step out, had already rolled down the window, waving at him.

Chen Xin smiled back at Mo Qingyan, but his gaze involuntarily fell on the figure driving the truck. It was the first time he’d seen Qin Lan since they broke up.

But neither Chen Xin nor Qin Lan in the truck showed much reaction.

"Ingredients, can we drive the truck inside?" Mo Qingyan asked Chen Xin as she got out of the vehicle, examining the shelter door behind him.

Chen Xin glanced back at his door, then at the truck they came in and shook his head, "I’m afraid it won’t fit. Last time you came with a pickup, which was smaller, but this truck’s head is too high. It might get stuck inside."

"Alright, I anticipated this beforehand." Mo Qingyan wasn’t surprised and nodded as she moved towards the truck bed, "Come help unload!"

Chen Xin walked over as Mo Qingyan climbed into the truck bed, pulling out a dolly. She smiled at Chen Xin, proudly saying, "Luckily, I brought this, or it would have been hard to move things."

Seeing Mo Qingyan take out a dolly, Chen Xin realized that the supplies he exchanged for were too numerous, and since the truck couldn’t get into his shelter, it would have taken him all day to move everything without it.

"You’re always so thorough, Dragon Lady." Chen Xin complimented Mo Qingyan and took the items she handed down from the truck, placing them on the dolly.

Qin Lan, having parked the truck, walked over to help with moving the items.

Upon seeing Chen Xin, Qin Lan didn’t forget to greet him, "Long time no see, A Xin."

"A Lan..." Chen Xin looked up to greet her, but in his moment of distraction, he almost dropped the bag of cement Mo Qingyan handed him. Fortunately, Qin Lan reached out to help, preventing the bag of cement from dropping onto Chen Yu’s foot.

"You still lack exercise like before." As she spoke to Chen Xin, Qin Lan effortlessly placed the bag of cement, which Chen Xin almost couldn’t hold, onto the dolly.

Seeing how easily she managed, Chen Xin awkwardly stepped aside and said, "I just didn’t hold it steadily, and I use my brains to work, not muscle. How can I compete with you?"

Realizing that he repeated something he’d said during a fight before they broke up, Chen Xin winced internally.

"A Xin, you really haven’t changed at all! Just like before." Qin Lan smiled at Chen Xin, taking another bag of cement from Mo Qingyan and placing it on the dolly, "It didn’t matter back then, but now with our current circumstances, you really should work on your fitness. Even if not for..."

She paused, realizing that although she was still urging Chen Xin to change his bad habits like before, they were no longer in a relationship, making her words inappropriate.

Chen Xin didn’t mind, he simply nodded at Qin Lan, agreeing, "I should exercise more. Otherwise, the farming alone will wear me out."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 53: The Dragon Maiden Who Grows Mushrooms (Seeking recommendations, seeking favorites)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

With the help of Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan, Chen Xin quickly moved all the supplies they brought into his shelter and stored them in the storeroom.

As the supplies were too numerous, the storeroom ended up being packed to the brim, forcing some items to be placed in the garage next to Chen Xin’s SUV.

"Why did you trade so many supplies if it was only for ingredients? Most of them are building materials." Mo Qingyan took off her gloves, brushed off the dust from moving supplies, and looked curiously at the mound of items before her, wondering why Chen Xin needed so many resources. "Even if you wanted to expand the greenhouse, you wouldn’t need so much, right?"

Standing beside her, Qin Lan, upon hearing Mo Qingyan’s question, also looked at Chen Xin with curiosity, eager to know why he exchanged for so many supplies.

"I just thought to remodel the shelter before the temperature drops below zero!" Chen Xin laughed, giving an explanation that was not a lie but didn’t fully reveal the truth either.

Actually, expanding the greenhouse does require plenty of materials, but Chen Xin had clearly acquired extra during the trade.

The disaster hadn’t struck long ago, making it relatively easy to gather supplies, so it was wise to stockpile as much as possible now before the situation worsens and resources become scarce.

"Remodel the shelter? You alone? Can you do it, or do you need me and Aning to help?" Mo Qingyan seemed startled upon hearing Chen Xin’s intention to renovate the shelter, as she did not recall that he possessed skills in that area.

Faced with Mo Qingyan’s offer to help, Chen Xin, though grateful for her willingness to assist at such a time, shook his head and declined, "No need, the workload isn’t large; I can handle it myself. There’s not much else to do in the shelter anyway, so it can pass the time."

Inevitably, Chen Xin couldn’t let Mo Qingyan come to help; otherwise, it would be hard to explain if she found there was nothing to do.

"Besides, I don’t have room here for you guys to stay. If you come over to help, you’d have to travel back and forth every day, which would be too inconvenient, right?" Seemingly trying to distract her, Chen Xin added, "Besides, in these circumstances, doesn’t your shelter also need reinforcement?"

"That’s true, running to and from your place every day, you’d have to take care of food and lodging..." Mo Qingyan thought for a moment and felt it indeed troublesome, and she agreed with Chen Xin’s rationale, "For my home, it looks like I should fix the walls, and also seal the door over at the first-floor shop."

Regarding Mo Qingyan’s thoughts, Chen Xin quickly suggested, "What you really need to reinforce is the gate in your yard. The old iron gate was fine before, but now, a car could easily crash through it, so it’s better to fortify it."

With Chen Xin’s remark, Mo Qingyan suddenly realized that her shelter indeed had several vulnerabilities.

"Got it. I’ll go back and remodel my shelter according to your suggestion, A-Xin!" Mo Qingyan seemed to take Chen Xin’s advice seriously and decided to refurbish her shelter.

Listening to Chen Xin and Mo Qingyan discuss shelter renovations, Qin Lan found herself somewhat unable to contribute to the conversation. She was staying at the collective shelter of the city police department, unlike these two, who had their private shelters.

However, Qin Lan still reminded Mo Qingyan, "Actually, the three floors above your home can be utilized. Although the temperature has dropped and living on the surface isn’t feasible, on the flip side, couldn’t the cold be used to store supplies?"

"That’s a good point! When the surface temperature drops to below minus several tens of degrees, it turns into a natural freezer, right? Anything that doesn’t fear freezing could be placed up there, couldn’t it?" Mo Qingyan, reminded by Qin Lan’s idea, excitedly snapped her fingers, showing considerable enthusiasm, "This way, I can free up some space in my original storeroom and don’t have to rush to clear out the wine cellar."

Hearing Mo Qingyan mention the wine cellar, Chen Xin immediately thought of her wine collection.

Because Mo Qingyan’s bar only catered to members, the prepared wines were all genuinely high-quality, many of which were considered collectible.

These wines were nominally prepared for the bar, but the rarities weren’t available just anytime. Even with Chen Xin’s good relationship with Mo Qingyan, he could only occasionally enjoy them.

With this in mind, Chen Xin advised Mo Qingyan, "Qingyan, you could convert the wine cellar into a greenhouse. Although you can’t grow vegetables or rice like on my side, you could cultivate mushrooms or hydroponic vegetables."

"Turn the wine cellar into a greenhouse to grow mushrooms?" Mo Qingyan pondered seriously for a moment about the feasibility of such an idea, "But if I do that, my wines won’t have a place to go."

"Your wines...honestly, in an apocalypse, they are precious, non-renewable resources and should ideally be preserved well," Chen Xin commented, clearly mindful of the wines and then quickly added, "However, right now, the key is to fill your stomach first. Qingyan, your shelter lacks self-sufficiency and relies entirely on your stored resources. But how long can these resources last? If you don’t think about it now, it will be very dangerous in the future."

Chen Xin’s words made Mo Qingyan reflect, realizing she indeed needed to figure out what to do after exhausting her supplies.

However, concerning mushroom cultivation, Mo Qingyan was clueless, having no idea how to start under the current circumstances.

"As for your wines, instead of letting them go to waste, why not trade some with me?" Chen Xin remained keenly interested in Mo Qingyan’s fine wines.

"So you had your eyes on my wines! I was wondering why you pushed me to convert the wine cellar into a greenhouse!" Mo Qingyan, upon hearing this, playfully pouted and said after some thought, "Trading another batch of wines with you is not a problem. I initially planned to clear out the wine cellar, sell off the stock, and free up space for additional storage.

Now, since we can use the upper floors to store supplies, converting the wine cellar into a greenhouse might not be a bad idea. But I don’t understand mushroom cultivation, so even if the conversion happens, it’s uncertain if it’ll be useful at all!"

"Regarding that, the police station has a complete set of mushroom cultivation manuals, initially prepared by the government for every shelter. I can copy a set for you, and also sell you some mushroom growing kits," Qin Lan said, offering Mo Qingyan substantial help at that moment.

Delighted to hear Qin Lan could provide technical support, Mo Qingyan immediately expressed her gratitude to her.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 54: Predictable Food Crisis
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In fact, Mo Qingyan also knew how important it is to have a greenhouse that can produce food in an apocalyptic environment, but their shelter was built when the house was constructed, and the greenhouse was not considered at that time. Instead, the underground floor was designed as a bar.

This resulted in Mo Qingyan’s shelter having no self-sustaining food capabilities.

In the short term, this is certainly not a problem. Whether it’s exchanging goods with others or acquiring food through other means, they can manage to get by.

Moreover, because the underground space is sufficient, Mo Qingyan also stored a lot of supplies, enough for her, Mo Qingxin, and Ding Ning to eat for two years.

If it were just a nuclear war or some other apocalyptic disaster, one or two years would be enough for them to wait for post-disaster reconstruction or rescue teams to come to the rescue.

But now everyone is facing a century-long extreme cold night, with the surface becoming a frozen environment for a hundred years, completely unsuitable for any crop cultivation. Humanity’s food production can almost be said to have been completely written off by 99%.

In such a situation, the only thing people can rely on is the material reserves before the disaster and these planting greenhouses built in shelters underground that aren’t as affected by temperatures.

Chen Xin may not know the situation yet because he’s far from the city, but Mo Qingyan, due to being close to the City Hall Shelter and the shopping center shelter, somewhat knows the situation. Shelters in urban areas have already begun to control the use of supplies.

Although the main food source is still pre-disaster stored grains, mushrooms grown in planting greenhouses and rats being raised have also started to be incorporated into the food supply.

In the Flame Country, the technology for mushroom cultivation is already quite mature, which is why the government opted to use mushrooms as the main food source for post-disaster sheltering.

Mushrooms, being the main obtainable food source in the current environment, if managed properly, can yield significant output. In a suitable environment, a 100 square meter planting area using a five-layer cultivation method can produce even at a conservative estimate of 30 jin per square meter, reaching 75,000 jin a year.

This yield is not low, but to ensure food supply for a shelter, though small shelters find it hard to build greenhouses, they don’t need too vast planting areas to ensure food supply, thus not having much of an issue.

Just like Chen Xin’s shelter, with a 100 square meter planting greenhouse, if all planted with mushrooms, it would be enough for Chen Xin to eat his fill and still have surplus yield to make mushroom wine and drink himself under the table.

But for large shelters, the problem gets more severe, and the bigger the shelter, the more serious the issue becomes.

The larger the number of people, the more food is needed, and the corresponding greenhouse area required will also be larger.

After all, upon hearing that 100 square meters can produce over 70,000 jin of food in a year, one might think it’s a lot, but with a permanent population of 300,000 in the city, thanks to the pre-disaster "leave no one behind" policy of the Flame Country government, at least over 90% survived in shelters.

Calculating based on one ton of food consumption per person per year, 270,000 people would require at least 360,000 square meters of mushroom growing area to meet consumption needs.

Because the surface cannot grow any crops anymore, all food sources are from underground greenhouses, but shelters evidently don’t have that much space to set up planting greenhouses.

And 360,000 square meters is 3,600 mu, 540 acres, 36 hectares, which is equivalent to half of the Forbidden City, 1.7 Bird’s Nests, or 51.4 standard football fields in area.

Such an area, if it were before the disaster, would not be an issue, excluding rural farmland, just the labor reform farm near the city had 36,500 mu of farmland, making food production not an issue at all.

As for constructing planting greenhouses, before the disaster, building them on the surface would merely be a matter of money, without any difficulty in terms of construction.

But with the disaster at hand in the apocalyptic world, due to the severe cold, building greenhouses on the surface is impossible. To ensure sufficient food production, one can only dig out enough space underground.

And digging out such a large underground space would take quite some time.

Even if the major shelters have enough manpower and possibly stored enough tools before the disaster, carving out such a vast space is a massive project.

In other words, unless other factors intervene in the future, food shortages will be an inevitable issue for the major shelters.

Fortunately, the apocalypse has just begun, and all major shelters have enough material reserves to sustain for a relatively long time.

This period is golden for each shelter; whether they can tackle the upcoming food crisis depends on whether they can dig out enough planting greenhouses during this time.

So it is foreseeable that food will become the most in-demand necessity in the future, and ensuring a stable supply of obtainable food will become the top priority for survival in this apocalyptic world.

Whether it’s Chen Xin or Mo Qingyan, or other people in other shelters, if they want to survive in this apocalypse, they must first solve this problem.

Fortunately for Chen Xin, with the help of the system, food is no longer a challenge for him.

"Alright, with Ah Lan’s help, your problem over there is also resolved now, Dragon Lady." Chen Xin looked at Mo Qingyan, a smile in his tone as he said to her, "Now you just need to clear out the wine cellar and convert it into a greenhouse."

"Hmph, if I clear out the wine cellar, you’ll start eyeing my collection, won’t you? Let me tell you, if you want my wine, you’d better bring something good to trade!" Mo Qingyan snorted at Chen Xin.

Looking at Mo Qingyan’s demeanor, Chen Xin burst into laughter, "Don’t worry, I definitely won’t disappoint you!"

For the bickering-like conversation between Chen Xin and Mo Qingyan, Qin Lan just watched from the side, waiting until they finished before speaking, "If you two have finished chatting, shouldn’t we get the vegetables you promised us SWAT?"

Although Chen Xin was using Mo Qingyan’s name to trade supplies with Uncle Ding, both Uncle Ding and Chen Xin knew that the actual trade was between Chen Xin himself and the SWAT.

After all, although Uncle Ding is the SWAT team leader, he couldn’t personally come up with so many supplies, and so much vegetable supply couldn’t be consumed by one person alone, so collecting supplies under the SWAT name to exchange for vegetables to improve the SWAT’s meals was inevitable.

So Qin Lan’s comment was perfectly justified.

Facing Qin Lan’s prompting, Chen Xin smiled and led them to his planting greenhouse, "Don’t worry, the vegetables have already matured, and I was harvesting them before you arrived."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 55: Plantation Upgrade (4k - , Please Recommend and Bookmark)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

With the assistance of Mo Qingyan and Qin Lan, Chen Xin’s harvest of six hundred pounds of vegetables this month was quickly gathered from the fields, packed into the boxes Qin Lan brought, and placed in the car they drove over.

"Then we’ll be off, take care of yourself, Chen Xin." At the entrance of Chen Xin’s shelter, Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin, wanting to say something but finding it hard to start.

"Mm, you too, Qin Lan. The environment now is not like before. Your work is dangerous enough as it is, so be extra careful now." Seeing that Qin Lan was concerned about him, Chen Xin also carefully advised her.

Qin Lan nodded, said no more, and prepared to leave, but as she walked out of the shelter’s door, she turned back, frowned, and said, "Chen Xin, you should reinforce the door of your shelter and also create some camouflage outside. As you said, the environment is not like before, so... be careful."

"I know, I’ll take care of it later." Chen Xin nodded. He had already considered Qin Lan’s concerns, but hadn’t prioritized them.

Seeing that Chen Xin took her words to heart, Qin Lan nodded and got in the car, starting the engine to leave.

"Ingredients, remember to save a bit of the carrot harvest for me next month. I’ll trade you some Lafite wine!" As they left, Mo Qingyan didn’t forget to stick her head out of the car window, placing an order with Chen Xin.

"Got it!" Chen Xin replied, watching them drive away before turning back into the shelter and closing the door again.

Back inside the shelter, Chen Xin could hardly suppress his excitement and rushed directly into the greenhouse, preparing to upgrade it.

"Simple Greenhouse"

"Upgrade Plan One: Add drip irrigation system, add hydroponic planting trays, crop yield +10%, irrigation water consumption -10%, survival points required for upgrade 2000"

"Upgrade Plan Two: Increase planting area, improve supplementary lighting efficiency, crop yield +10%, lighting time -10%, survival points required for upgrade 3500"

"Provide steel, cement, supplementary lamps, micro vacuum pumps, PVC pipes, PVC hydroponic trays, and other materials for upgrade?"

"Materials for Plan One provided, reducing survival points required for upgrade by 90%, remaining survival points 200"

"Materials for Plan Two provided, reducing survival points required for upgrade by 90%, remaining survival points 350"

"Materials for both Plan One and Plan Two provided, reducing survival points required for upgrade by 150, remaining survival points 400, choose to upgrade?"

Faced with the system’s text, Chen Xin undoubtedly chose to upgrade, spending all the 400 survival points he had.

As Chen Xin chose to upgrade, the entire greenhouse was engulfed by the upgrade light he’d already witnessed twice before.

This time, not just the greenhouse but also various materials placed in the storage room and garage were covered by the light, turning into beams that converged into the greenhouse.

When the glow from the upgrade in the greenhouse faded, what appeared before Chen Xin was a greenhouse with an area doubled and a layer of hydroponic planting trays hanging from the ceiling, looking much more advanced than before.

The increase in planting area and the appearance of hydroponic trays were very direct changes, while the greenhouse becoming more advanced was a rather subjective feeling.

After all, without an increase in technological level, distinguishing whether something is advanced or outdated largely relies on one’s subjective judgment.

But looking at the upgraded clean and tidy greenhouse with soft and bright lighting from lamps upgraded from big lights to tubes, the orderly fields, even the crooked carrots Chen Xin previously planted were now lined up straight, Chen Xin truly felt a sense of "advanced."

The sight before him was no longer a typical shelter greenhouse Chen Xin had casually set up, but more like a plant cultivation laboratory.

Even so, the purpose of this greenhouse was still to cultivate crops and obtain food.

So no matter how good it looked, for Chen Xin, the utility of the upgraded greenhouse was the most crucial.

Originally, the floor of Chen Xin’s greenhouse was just soil, used to segment the land with furrows he left himself, without any plan.

But now, in the two hundred square meters of planting area, the land was evenly divided into eight rectangular plots, looking much more organized.

Standing between the furrows, Chen Xin reached down to grab a handful of soil from the ground, rubbing it in his hand to check the soil quality.

It seemed that even the soil was affected by the upgrade, as it also seemed to have been upgraded.

The soil originally in Chen Xin’s greenhouse was obtained from nearby farmer’s gardens, already cultivated soil, suitable for crop farming.

But compared to the soil in the greenhouse now, the previous soil was noticeably more ashen, making the contrast very apparent.

The soil in the greenhouse now was dark and glossy, delicate and fluffy. As you squeezed it, it felt as though it might even exude oil, giving an impression of fertility at first glance.

Although Chen Xin didn’t know much about soil, he could roughly guess that this should be the black soil from northern Flame Country, renowned for its fertility.

This was far better than the soil he used before. It’s a natural treasure bestowed upon humanity, perfect for growing crops, with only three patches of such black soil on the entire Blue Star.

The change in soil gave Chen Xin a deeper understanding of the system’s upgrade power, and he hadn’t expected that upgrading the greenhouse would upgrade the soil as well, especially to this black soil most suitable for crop growth.

Aside from the change in soil quality, the most noticeable transformation in the greenhouse was the hydroponic planting trays hanging from the ceiling.

Chen Xin had exchanged for a set of hydroponic planting equipment before, including hydroponic trays, a nutrient solution circulation system, and a micro vacuum pump, but these exchanged pieces of equipment clearly could not compare to the equipment provided by the system upgrade.

The hydroponic trays, although called trays, are not actually dishes but consist of a nutrient trough and a planting board.

The hydroponic trays Chen Xin exchanged for were common plastic trays, with a grid-like planting board that looked ordinary, and felt roughly made to the touch.

But after the system upgrade, the tray became an integrated sink, and the planting board turned into a more professional planting board with slots, all in white color by the system, feeling smooth and rounded to the touch, and therefore looking exceptionally clean and tidy.

There are pipes for circulating nutrient solution installed on the brackets, as well as supplementary lights to provide illumination for the lower plants, so that the lower crops are not affected by the blockage of the hydroponic planting trays.

Chen Xin reached out and held the bracket hanging from the ceiling, trying to shake it, but found that it was remarkably stable with no wobbling at all. It seemed that the quality of things after the system upgrade was also rather good.

After examining the upgraded planting greenhouse, Chen Xin began to prepare for the use of the hydroponic planting system. Although hydroponic cultivation involves hydroponics, it’s not as simple as just water alone.

While plants can grow with only water, they won’t grow well, and the harvest would be rather disappointing.

Therefore, in normal hydroponic cultivation, it is necessary to add a formulated nutrient solution to the water to provide various trace elements needed for plant growth.

Chen Xin was naturally aware of this, so when he asked Mo Qingyan to help him exchange resources previously, besides acquiring the necessary equipment for hydroponic cultivation, he also got a large bottle of concentrated nutrient solution.

This nutrient solution is a general-purpose nutrient for plant cultivation and is a highly concentrated model. According to the usage instructions on the package, only 30 milliliters are needed to dilute four liters of nutrient solution.

And this bottle of highly concentrated nutrient solution has a capacity of 1.8 liters, which, given Chen Xin’s current greenhouse area, is enough for him to use for several years.

After checking everything, feeling very satisfied with the upgraded system in the planting greenhouse, Chen Xin prepared the nutrient solution and started preparing for seed breeding.

Hydroponic planting differs from soil planting. In soil planting, seeds can be directly sown into the ground, and the crops will sprout, root, and grow on their own.

However, for hydroponic planting, even with a planting board, pre-seeding is necessary, and once the roots and leaves have grown out, they can be moved to the nutrient solution slot for planting.

Fortunately, Chen Xin is planting cabbage and lettuce. The seed breeding for these two crops is not complicated. Simply remove the planting board, place seeds on the sponge used to secure the plants in each slot, and then water it with the prepared nutrient solution to keep the sponge moist until the seeds germinate.

When taking the planting board off the bracket, Chen Xin also noticed there were still some issues with the installation height of the hydroponic planting tray.

The ceiling height of Chen Xin’s planting greenhouse is 2.5 meters, which, although a bit low, didn’t cause any problems during previous use.

But with the addition of the hydroponic planting tray after the upgrade, which is positioned at about 1.5 meters high from the ground, it resulted in Chen Xin having trouble straightening up when handling crops on the ground level.

Although farming usually involves bending over, if one can’t straighten up at all, it can be very uncomfortable. After all, even farmers usually stand up straight to rest occasionally while tilling, rather than being bent over constantly.

Fortunately, Chen Xin’s greenhouse is not large, and the area to be cultivated is just two hundred square meters, so it won’t be too exhausting.

However, after realizing this issue, Chen Xin remembered the small agricultural machine he planned to get earlier.

The resources exchanged with Uncle Ding this time did not include the small agricultural machine. It wasn’t that Chen Xin didn’t mention it, but rather Uncle Ding didn’t send the small agricultural machine he needed together, indicating they would only send it after Chen Xin paid off the balance of this transaction.

Clearly, Uncle Ding also maintains some caution towards Chen Xin.

This isn’t about trust or distrust. Even before the disaster, when applying for a loan from a bank or borrowing from friends, some kind of security was needed. Chen Xin’s installment payment method naturally made the other side a bit uneasy, so keeping some reservation is normal.

Chen Xin didn’t resent or object to this; it just meant this month’s work would inevitably be a bit more strenuous for him.

Fortunately, along with the system upgrade, the soil on the ground was also upgraded, so he didn’t need to fertilize or till it anymore, only scatter the drought-resistant rice seeds.

——————————————————

After finishing the work in the planting greenhouse, the time had already reached evening.

Although there was no longer a change in light, and evening could no longer be called "evening," the routine remained the same.

After having dinner, Chen Xin slumped onto the living room sofa, feeling a bit aimlessly playing a game console.
