Though if someone with darker intentions might think of taking over Chen Xin’s little shelter and enjoying it themselves, for the SWAT team, the one or two thousand pounds of vegetables a month wasn’t worth such action.

No matter what, the government institutions are well-structured, the country and military remain intact, and the municipal government still relies heavily on the SWAT team to maintain order in the city. But the SWAT team is just a part of the police department; they haven’t reached the point where they can do whatever they please.

"Sister Lan, do you think Ah Xin will accept the exchange conditions my dad and they proposed?" Sitting in the passenger seat, Ding Ning, now donned in a police uniform again, asked Qin Lan, who was driving.

Under Uncle Ding’s firm stance, Ding Ning was taken back to the police shelter, dressed in a police uniform again, but she was transferred from her original unit to the SWAT team, responsible for assisting Qin Lan, nominally as a reserve observer.

This time, she came with Qin Lan to deliver supplies to Chen Xin and incidentally bring back this month’s exchanged vegetables.

Focusing on driving, Qin Lan, upon hearing Ding Ning’s question, thought for a moment and said, "Based on what I know about Ah Xin, he should accept exchanging living supplies for vegetables, but for other materials, it will depend on our negotiation with him."

This was also Qin Lan’s main task this time: to discuss the specific proportions of the supplies-for-vegetables exchange with Chen Xin.

Currently, the SWAT team still has a decent stock of supplies, and they can receive what could be considered ample provisions under the current circumstances from the city administration. Offering a small portion to exchange for fresh vegetables to improve meals is something the SWAT team can currently accept.

Even though it’s indeed the apocalypse, as long as the supply is guaranteed, one or two thousand pounds of vegetables really just serve to improve meals for the SWAT team; after all, vegetables aren’t the staple food and can’t be eaten as a meal.

Upon hearing Qin Lan’s response, Ding Ning, indifferent, pulled out her pistol from the holster at her waist and toyed with it in her hand, grumbling somewhat discontentedly, "Ah Xin is still too unguarded. Even if it’s because of my connections, he shouldn’t trust my dad so much. If someone wants to rob his shelter, they can settle it with just a gun."

Qin Lan naturally agreed with Ding Ning’s words, as, in her view, Chen Xin’s shelter indeed lacked sufficient security measures.

Two or three fortified doors might suffice against natural disasters, but if someone really had ulterior motives, the extent to which Chen Xin’s two doors in his shelter could serve as obstacles is hard to say.

The two drove the truck to Chen Xin’s shelter, still using the shortwave radio to request the door be opened.

"Little Ding? From the look of you, have you returned to the police station? Made up with Uncle Ding?" Chen Xin looked at Ding Ning in her police tactical gear and full single police equipment and couldn’t help but ask.

"My dad sent Sister Lan to bring me back. What else could I say? At least I get to wear the police uniform again." Ding Ning explained to Chen Xin, also explaining with a certain helplessness, "Originally, my dad agreed to let Sister Yan and Little Sister Xin move to the municipal police shelter, but Sister Yan didn’t want to and still lives at her own home."

Saying this, Ding Ning also showed concern for the Mo Family sisters on her face.

Chen Xin naturally understood the worry from Ding Ning’s words. He also couldn’t help but frown at the matter.

However, as Mo Qingyan’s choice was not something he could interfere with, all he could do was shake his head and say, "I’ll talk to Madam Long later. If worse comes to worst, I’ll help her make an oxygen generator."

"Ah Xin, you can make an oxygen generator?" Ding Ning and Qin Lan were obviously surprised by what Chen Xin said.

Seeing their astonished expressions, Chen Xin couldn’t help but find it a bit strange, so he said, "A simple oxygen generator is easy to make, you can modify a fish tank or a plant box yourself, or even without modification, just directly use the plant box as a replacement, though the oxygen output will be very low."

"Oh, oh, I see!" Ding Ning quickly nodded, indicating her understanding, as she initially thought Chen Xin was talking about oxygen-generation equipment like those used in official shelters and didn’t associate it with such simple oxygen generators.

Chen Xin didn’t mind Ding Ning’s attitude and asked Qin Lan, "Ah Lan, did you bring what I wanted last time?"

"Don’t worry, the small agricultural machines you asked for last time are all on the truck, and this time I brought you something else too." Qin Lan assured as she moved to the back of the truck to open the compartment.

————————————————————

"Sit wherever you like, I’ll get you some water." After storing the items Qin Lan brought in the storeroom, Chen Xin invited the two to sit in the living room while he searched for cups to pour them water.

However, as Chen Xin lived alone in the shelter, he hadn’t prepared extra cups, and after searching for a while, he couldn’t find any to pour water for Qin Lan and Ding Ning.

Seeing this, Qin Lan quickly said to Chen Xin, "No need to look, Ah Xin, there’s water on the truck, you don’t have to trouble yourself. Sit down first; I have some matters to discuss with you."

Hearing Qin Lan’s words, Chen Xin had no choice but to abandon the effort to find cups and sat down on the sofa, waiting for Qin Lan to speak.

Taking out a piece of paper from her person and handing it to Chen Xin, Qin Lan then said, "The supplies-for-vegetables trade you mentioned before with our SWAT team, the captain and commissar have already agreed after discussion. This is a list of supply quotations we’ve put together. See if you can accept it, and if there are any modifications or dissatisfaction, we can negotiate."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 79 - 78: Guns and Food
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Accepting the quote handed over by Qin Lan, Chen Xin nodded and began to read it carefully.

It listed various materials that the SWAT team could provide, including common building materials like steel and cement, as well as food, salt, sugar, toiletries, and even fuels like gasoline and diesel.

However, the most striking item on this list was the QSZ92G semi-automatic pistol and its matching 9mm bullets, specially marked in red.

"Even guns can be traded! Is there no problem with such things?" Of course, Chen Xin noticed the specially marked section and looked at Qin Lan in surprise.

Qin Lan nodded and explained to Chen Xin, "Ah Xin, living alone in a place like the suburbs, it’s safer to have a gun in these times. So, I pleaded with the captain to get a gun for you."

"Ah Xin, you only have two iron doors here. If someone tricks you into opening the door, you won’t even have a chance to resist." Ding Ning couldn’t help but interject, criticizing the security of Chen Xin’s shelter.

Chen Xin naturally knew that the security of his shelter was indeed worrying. Although the two protective doors could handle disasters, they weren’t enough to fend off someone with intent.

Thus, he was planning to renovate the door of the shelter to enhance its safety and protective features.

However, regarding the pistol, Chen Xin didn’t have an urgent need. After all, he could make a gun himself and didn’t need to trade one with the SWAT team.

But after thinking for less than a second, Chen Xin still decided to trade for this gun.

It’s not that the SWAT team’s gun was better than the one Chen Xin could make, but rather, making firearms on one’s own was already considered illegal in Flame Country.

Even though this referred to the pre-apocalyptic world, being the country with the strictest gun control, Chen Xin didn’t think the country would allow private gun ownership even after the apocalypse.

Being able to obtain a gun from the SWAT team provided a legitimate reason to use firearms, which was much better than making one himself and then having to use it surreptitiously.

Moreover, it was Qin Lan’s heartfelt gesture.

"Thanks, but what about the bullets?" Chen Xin looked at Qin Lan and asked her.

"You can trade for bullets too, but you can’t get too many at once, up to 50 rounds maximum," Qin Lan explained to Chen Xin, seemingly afraid he might overthink it: "It’s not that we won’t give you more bullets; it’s just for safety reasons."

Hearing Qin Lan’s explanation, Chen Xin nodded to express his understanding: "Don’t worry, I understand, given that Flame Country prohibits guns."

Seeing that Chen Xin had no objections, Qin Lan was relieved and had Ding Ning push a black box they brought to Chen Xin’s front.

Seeing the letters "QSZ92G" printed on this box, Chen Xin knew that this was the gun Qin Lan provided him.

Reaching out to open the box, sure enough, a completely black pistol lay inside.

Besides the gun, there were also two magazines and a box of golden bullets inside the box.

Chen Xin picked up a magazine, saw it wasn’t loaded with bullets, then took the gun from the box and inserted the empty magazine directly, pulled the slide, and held the gun with both hands in a standard shooting stance.

The QSZ92G semi-automatic pistol is the latest generation of police pistols in Flame Country, derived from the military’s QSZ92-style pistol improvement.

The biggest difference between the police and military versions is the caliber. The military model is 5.8mm caliber with a 20-round magazine, while the police version is 9mm caliber with 10 and 15-round options. Qin Lan brought Chen Xin a police version with two 15-round magazines.

For the potential dangers Chen Xin might face, two magazines with thirty bullets were already more than sufficient.

"Ah Xin, have you used guns before?" Qin Lan watched Chen Xin’s skilled gun-holding posture and couldn’t help frowning. As Chen Xin’s ex-girlfriend, she didn’t know he could use guns at all.

Hearing Qin Lan’s words, Chen Xin couldn’t help but chuckle: "Ah Lan, you’re underestimating boys, right? Which boy hasn’t played with toy guns as a kid? Even though toy guns aren’t real, you can still mimic the posture."

Of course, Chen Xin wouldn’t tell Qin Lan that these days, he’d been playing with the gun he made himself, trying out all kinds of gun-holding postures he’d seen on TV.

Although this didn’t have much practical value, it did somewhat cultivate the feeling of holding a gun.

Qin Lan was skeptical of Chen Xin’s explanation, but since she’d already given him the gun, it was better if he knew how to use it. So, she didn’t say more and had Ding Ning place another box in front of Chen Xin.

This was a cardboard box, much bigger than the pistol box, and felt heavy because it was full of stuff.

Chen Xin used a small knife to cut open the box, the top half contained various daily necessities, and the bottom half was a layer of plastic boxes.

"This is the monthly allowance of daily supplies for each person in the shelter, including toothbrushes, toothpaste, towels, soap, and a month’s rations for one person." Seeing Chen Xin going through the items in the box, Qin Lan provided him with an introduction and explanation.

The toothbrushes, toothpaste, and soap were placed in a plastic bag, somewhat similar to the toiletries kits Chen Xin had seen in some budget hotels before.

Besides these items, there was also a roll of toilet paper in the box.

Chen Xin took these out and put them on the table, leaving only a layer of plastic boxes in the box.

Qin Lan took out a box from the box and opened it to show Chen Xin: "This is the main food currently eaten in the shelter, containing two servings of rice cooked with rice, potatoes, and meat, and two energy bars.

Each month, shelters hand over the raw materials and used boxes from the previous month to the processing plant, and once processed, they are returned to each shelter, so they are basically fresh."

Looking at the two brick-like food portions and two energy bars that looked like compressed biscuits mixed with nuts, Chen Xin couldn’t help touching his eyebrow: "The food seems to be alright."

"It might look fine, but the meat is rat meat." Ding Ning couldn’t help but comment. In Mo Qingyan’s shelter, she could still eat luncheon meat and canned pork, but back in the police station shelter, the meat now was only rat meat.

Chen Xin didn’t mind this, nodding normally: "Rat meat actually tastes good. In these times, breeding pigs or cattle is unrealistic."

Regarding the box of so-called standard daily supplies for a shelter brought by Qin Lan, Chen Xin felt quite satisfied. The food and necessities were decent, not luxurious but definitely sufficient.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 80 - 79 Bargaining
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The list Qin Lan brought had quite a few supplies, but all of these needed to be exchanged with vegetables produced in Chen Xin’s shelter. The list also included the exchange ratios deemed suitable by the special police team after discussion.

Chen Xin had no objection to the resource exchange since it was beneficial for him as well. However, he did have different opinions on the exchange ratios.

"A hundred pounds of fresh vegetables for five bags of cement? This ratio is acceptable." Chen Xin checked each exchange ratio marked on the list: "But the one for rebar is a bit exaggerated, isn’t it? It takes twenty pounds of vegetables to get a single piece of rebar?"

Rebar was extremely useful for Chen Xin; needless to say, reinforcing the door of the shelter required rebar concrete.

"We’re providing standard φ12X12 rebar, plus you know well that there’s no steel mill in the city, so all of these rely on rail input. It’s understandable for the price to be a bit high." Qin Lan explained to Chen Xin.

Though they were once a couple, neither of them let past feelings influence their bargaining; they both held their ground tightly.

"But needing twenty pounds of vegetables for a single piece of rebar is still outrageous. I think ten pounds is an acceptable price," Chen Xin said, immediately cutting the exchange ratio in half, but he kept his eyes on Qin Lan, waiting for her counteroffer.

As expected, Qin Lan shook her head and directly refused: "Ten pounds is too little; the most I can do is eighteen pounds."

However, Chen Xin stood firm, still repeating: "Ten pounds, or else I’ll find someone else to trade with."

"That’s impossible. Even before the disaster, you couldn’t get a rebar for ten pounds of cabbage." Qin Lan shook her head vigorously but still compromised: "Fifteen pounds, no less."

Hearing Qin Lan say this, Chen Xin smiled and said to her, "Let’s meet halfway at twelve pounds, and first get me fifty pieces of rebar, it’s urgent."

"Urgent? What do you need so much rebar for?" Qin Lan was immediately curious when Chen Xin mentioned it was urgent.

Chen Xin straightforwardly stated, "It’s been three months since the apocalypse began, and the temperature is nearing freezing. Before it freezes over, I plan to reinforce the shelter’s door. So besides rebar, I need cement, sand, gravel, and steel plates. I can accept the exchange ratios for these construction materials. Bring them over quickly."

Hearing that Chen Xin needed them to reinforce the shelter’s door, Qin Lan nodded in understanding and agreed readily.

The official shelters are also undertaking large-scale expansion work, and all shelters have been allocated a large amount of construction materials. The amount that Chen Xin needed wasn’t much; the special police team could easily spare a little from their supplies to meet his needs.

After negotiating the demand for construction materials, Chen Xin proposed another item not on the list: "Ah Lan, could you get me some glass? Preferably large cylindrical glass tubes, or an aquarium in that shape would be even better. Also a vacuum pump."

"I might be able to get those, but what do you need them for?" Qin Lan asked curiously.

Chen Xin smiled and explained, "The aquarium can be converted into a makeshift algae oxygen generator. Staying in an underground shelter will definitely have oxygen issues. I need one for myself, and Long Niang needs one too. If I help her with one, I can trade it for her good liquor. In cold weather, a drink can really warm you up."

"You’re not Russian; what’s with drinking to keep warm!" Qin Lan joked, but she agreed to Chen Xin’s request and jotted down what he needed in her little notebook.

The two then cross-checked the exchange ratios of all the items on the list again, as well as the additional items Chen Xin had mentioned, before ending their negotiations.

Once the negotiation was over, the topic among the three lightened up. Chen Xin also asked about a topic he was interested in.

"So what’s the main food in the shelters now? It can’t really be something like rat, worm, and mushroom soup, right?" Chen Xin joked.

But rats, worms, and mushrooms are indeed suitable for raising in low-temperature, underground, lightless environments and were announced by the government as the main food sources for shelters before the disaster.

To this question, Ding Ning just shrugged, leaning on the sofa in Chen Xin’s living room: "I just returned to the shelter recently, not sure. But it’s mainly potatoes and mushrooms stewed with rat meat, although there’s enough rice, which is quite surprising."

"It’s not surprising to have rice. The country had stockpiled a lot before the disaster," Qin Lan knew more about the situation than Ding Ning, so she explained to Chen Xin: "As for mushrooms and potatoes, mushrooms are something every shelter is currently growing. The yield from the government-provided mushroom spawn packs is quite good; they can be harvested once a month. If it weren’t for not enough growing space, mushrooms alone could meet food needs."

At this point, Qin Lan paused before continuing: "Regarding potatoes, the country hasn’t promoted large-scale planting. Although they’re high-yield, they require fertile soil and a lot of fertilizer, unlike mushrooms which have more stable yields.

They also consume a lot of water and electricity, and most shelters don’t have the conditions for large-scale planting, so the city has a designated underground growing area near the power plant for potatoes, hence the low yield."

Chen Xin nodded in understanding upon hearing this from Qin Lan.

In fact, when he built the shelter, the first thing he thought of growing was potatoes.

Thanks to that movie, "The Martian," everyone knew growing potatoes could help them survive, so in the apocalypse, shouldn’t potatoes be planted?

Especially in all those time-travel novels, besides making glass and soap in ancient times, finding potatoes was almost a must.

Everyone seemed to think of it as a divine crop, with yieldization overwhelming traditional agriculture which perhaps couldn’t even reach a tenth of a potato’s yield.

Potatoes indeed have many advantages. Naturally, the first is high yield; thousands of pounds per acre seem common for potatoes.

Moreover, potatoes are well suited to grow in cold regions; Flame Country’s Mongolia and Siberia in Lucia are main planting areas. They manage to grow healthily in environments below 10 degrees, not needing much water irrigation to assure high yield.

Potatoes are also rich in nutrients and starch, meaning in colder climates, they offer a significant calorie and nutrient intake, making them essential for survival.

But potatoes aren’t flawless.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 81 - 80: Potatoes Are Poisonous
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

First, the requirement for soil when planting potatoes is very strict. Only soil with a deep layer, loose structure, good drainage and ventilation, and rich in organic matter is the most suitable environment for potato growth.

And that’s not all. The growth and development of potatoes require more than ten types of nutrients, and they have a considerable demand for fertilizers.

If a plot of land continues to grow potatoes, due to the absorption of soil fertility by the potatoes, the soil fertility will greatly decline. In the second year, the yield of continuous cultivation plots must be reduced, and the quality of tubers also declines. In particular, diseases, pests, and weed problems will occur more severely.

Even the protagonist of The Martian, when planting potatoes on Mars, reused the vacuum-dried feces stored by previous astronauts, fermented them into manure, and only then began to cultivate potatoes.

More crucially, the stems and leaves of potato plants are toxic, and even the potatoes themselves are toxic.

Potatoes mainly contain solanine and chaconine, which generally decompose when heated to 170°C.

Although artificially cultivated potatoes have low toxicity, if the consumption exceeds 1.4 kilograms, the accumulated toxicity in the body can still cause poisoning reactions.

As for solanine produced after potatoes germinate, it is a toxin that can cause severe poisoning if ingested between 0.2 to 0.4g.

Especially for pregnant women, if potatoes are consumed over a long period, the alkaloids accumulated in the body may very likely cause fetal malformations.

Before the disaster, everyone could of course eat potatoes and plant potatoes as they pleased, after all, potatoes are indeed plentiful and filling, and by paying attention to crop rotation and fertilization during planting, many problems that arise during cultivation can be avoided.

But the toxins in potatoes are an unavoidable issue; after all, the post-apocalyptic world is not like before the disaster, where people have a rich variety of food choices and can eat many different foods, and an abundant supply of resources allows people to always consume fresh potatoes, greatly reducing the risk of poisoning.

Yet in a post-apocalyptic world, such conditions no longer exist. The land resources underground are limited, and if staple foods are planted, there will basically only be one type.

And this leads to people consuming potatoes over a long period.

Moreover, the shelter certainly cannot plant and eat on demand, and it is foreseeable that the harvested potatoes will undergo mildew and germination during storage.

Before the disaster, these would definitely be discarded, and no one would eat sprouted potatoes.

But in the post-apocalypse, these are all important food reserves; rats, cockroaches, and earthworms are eaten, so a few bad potatoes? As long as they don’t kill, cook them thoroughly and they’re edible!

So in such a time, the toxins in potatoes are a problem that cannot be ignored.

Considering this from a national level, if you need to choose a doomsday staple food, potatoes are clearly not the best choice, especially since growing potatoes also requires a lot of sunlight.

Chen Xin initially chose drought-resistant rice as the main food source for his shelter due to these considerations.

——————————————————

After seeing off Qin Lan and Ding Ning, Chen Xin tidied up the place and sat down in front of the workbench.

While taking out the pistol he exchanged from Qin Lan and loading the bullets into the magazine, he was also considering the issue of making an oxygen generator for Mo Qingyan.

Although he chose a simple and crude method to create an oxygen generator for himself, later obtaining one through system upgrades, this approach was still somewhat too rough.

Moreover, the overly simplistic oxygen generator he previously made had nothing notable in terms of efficiency, and its utilization rate of light energy was very low.

However, after using a system upgrade, even the crude oxygen generator Chen Xin had now looked decent.

Chen Xin chose the second upgrade option, where the original square plastic box transformed into a cylindrical hollow tube, with supplementary lighting directly installed in the hollow part of the tube, illuminating from the inside to achieve the highest efficiency of light energy utilization.

Algae were directly cultivated within the tube for reproduction, with the produced oxygen being expelled from the top while carbon dioxide was injected through vents at the bottom.

And the settled algae corpses would be filtered and recycled during the water circulation process to produce biomass for generating methane to supply fuel cells for electricity.

This entire system, after the upgrade, turned into a green cylinder about two meters in height, with light faintly shining through from within, filled with algae.

Such a device can produce enough oxygen to sustain a normal person’s survival, and the electricity generated can maintain the entire equipment’s energy consumption, excluding the supplementary lighting, with a bit of surplus.

After all, normal algae oxygen generation and electricity technology rely on sunlight to promote photosynthesis in algae. Now, without sunlight, the energy consumption of the supplementary lights must be compensated by other energy sources.

Mo Qingyan’s shelter has power and water supply from the city. Although it is supplied only at certain times, it is still sufficient for maintaining equipment operation.

Having loaded all the bullets into two magazines, Chen Xin inserted one magazine back into the pistol.

Feeling the gun in his hand, he couldn’t help but also take out the pistol he made, placing both on the workbench for comparison.

Compared to the pistol Chen Xin made himself, the one given by Qin Lan had a noticeably larger caliber but was lighter in weight, as Flame Country used engineering plastics for the grip when manufacturing the QSZ92G semi-automatic pistol, significantly reducing its weight.

As for other performance differences, without actually going out and firing dozens of bullets, Chen Xin could not compare the pros and cons of the two guns, as that would require very professional knowledge and firearms usage experience.

After a little consideration, Chen Xin decided to use the pistol he got from Qin Lan as a regular self-defense weapon, while the handgun he made himself was placed in the bedside cabinet in his bedroom as a backup.

This is also a safety strategy; having weapons and equipment prepared in the place closest to oneself is a good way to enhance one’s ability to respond to danger.

Even though in Chen Xin’s view, as long as the main door of his shelter is securely closed, without heavy firepower, it’s very unlikely he would face any safety problems needing a gun to deal with.

But it’s better to be safe; if some problem does arise, having a gun in hand is very important.

After finishing these tasks, Chen Xin also took out his bow from the weapon cabinet.

Archery was previously just a hobby developed with Mo Qingyan, with no practical use.

In the apocalypse, even after obtaining guns, the role of a bow and arrow is greatly diminished, making the existence of this bow somewhat redundant.

Furthermore, with the external temperature dropping, maintenance and preservation of the bow and arrow itself would be a problem if taken out for use.

Therefore, Chen Xin planned to see if the bow could have other uses after upgrading. If possible, he intended to upgrade the bow into a crossbow and then install it at the upgraded door, using it as a defensive facility.

Perhaps it can’t be used as a ballista, but there’s no saying it can’t be used as a trap.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 82 - 81 Feeding Peanuts
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The supplies Chen Xin asked for, such as steel bars, cement, glass, and vacuum pumps, were delivered a week later.

However, this time, only Ding Ning came to deliver them; Qin Lan didn’t come today.

Wearing a gas mask, Ding Ning jumped off the truck and greeted Chen Xin, who had come out to meet her.

The weather today was really bad, because without rain, there were too many pollutants in the air, making it impossible to breathe outdoors without a gas mask.

"A Xin, the things you wanted have been delivered." Speaking through a gas mask made her voice somewhat muffled, but if she spoke louder, it was still clear: "Steel bars, cement, the glass, and vacuum pump you needed, but I couldn’t get the aquarium, the former bird and flower market in the city is just a pile of broken glass now."

Hearing Ding Ning say this, Chen Xin was not bothered, and nodded at her: "No worries, I can process it myself. Let’s get the stuff inside first."

After saying that, they began to move the supplies from the truck together.

Since there wasn’t much to unload, the two quickly moved everything into Chen Xin’s shelter.

"This weather is really hard to bear. I can’t imagine how one would live outside without a gas mask." After closing the three protective doors, Ding Ning finally took off the gas mask from her face, exhaling a long breath.

"Is the situation outside really that bad now?" Chen Xin looked at Ding Ning in confusion. He had no need to go out recently, so he hadn’t left his shelter and wasn’t aware of the changes outside, only feeling that the weather was particularly severe today.

Ding Ning patted the dust off her clothes and explained to Chen Xin: "Several chemical plants around the city have exploded, especially those tank areas of Flame Country Petrochemical. A Xin, your family used to run a chemical plant too, so you know what kind of effect those things exploding can have."

When Ding Ning mentioned the chemical plant tanks exploding, Chen Xin’s face paled a bit, and he nodded silently.

He knew about those things, as his parents used to work at a chemical plant and were very familiar with them.

In short, to put it simply, all those tanks are national-grade fire prevention units, and once they exploded, each tank was like a mushroom cloud.

"It was somewhat better before when it was constantly raining, as there wasn’t that much dust in the air. But the rain stopped these days, and the temperature has dropped to almost zero, so it’s extremely uncomfortable outside," Ding Ning continued, seeing Chen Xin nodding.

In fact, the outside environment could no longer be described simply as intolerable; it had turned into a place unsuitable for human survival.

Without professional protective gear and gas masks, exposure to the outside environment for even half an hour would result in severe physiological reactions due to inhaling too much toxic and hazardous gases and dust.

Moreover, with the temperature approaching zero degrees, even Ding Ning came fully armed—wearing a gas mask on her face and specially issued winter warm clothing.

"Really? Then does that mean there are no survivors outside now?" Chen Xin couldn’t help but remark, considering that if the environment was as harsh as Ding Ning described, even survivors might not last long.

However, Ding Ning shook her head, disagreeing with Chen Xin’s statement: "There are still some survivors. Earlier, Sister Lan and the SWAT were searching the city for possible survivors and actually found quite a few people."

"They’re really lucky to have survived!" Chen Xin exclaimed, marveling at their good fortune.

Surviving through this series of post-apocalyptic disasters without a sturdy shelter indeed required some luck.

Especially when they were rescued by the SWAT team as the weather turned increasingly harsh; their luck must have been great.

But earlier, Ding Ning mentioned Qin Lan, prompting Chen Xin to ask: "By the way, why didn’t Lan come today? Does she have a mission?"

"Sister Lan and her team went to clean out shelters in the city today. The second batch of national oxygen equipment has arrived, but it’s still not enough for all the shelters, so the city government decided to move some people from the small shelters to the medium-sized ones." This information wasn’t classified, so Ding Ning didn’t have to keep it secret.

Chen Xin understood immediately, but he worriedly asked: "If they’re merging shelters, can they accommodate so many people?"

"No problem. The six medium-sized shelters in the city were designed to accommodate 25,000 people, and after expanding, they can hold 30,000. They’re basically not full now," Ding Ning reassured him with a shake of her head and explained to Chen Xin: "Relocating people from the small shelters may be a bit crowded for a moment, but it’s still better than being outside without enough food, clothing, or even oxygen."

Chen Xin could understand the government’s approach; it was traditional for Flame Country’s government to never abandon its compatriots.

"It seems the country is prepared! It’s good to bring people together; it facilitates management and resource concentration." Chen Xin nodded, showing approval of the city’s approach.

Although the darkness within human hearts is deeper than we imagine, and gathering people can bring many problems, it’s impossible to just stand by and watch them die because of potential issues.

"That’s what my dad says too. Though more people lead to more problems, it’s better than having scattered shelters that can’t handle issues in time." Ding Ning didn’t tell Chen Xin about the unspeakable things that happened in some small shelters.

Those things were too challenging for human endurance, and even thinking about them made Ding Ning’s scalp tingle and back cold.

As for those who crossed the line of human morality, a single bullet was probably their best fate.

Ding Ning knew about these events, Qin Lan knew, and it was even Qin Lan who personally dealt with those people, but they didn’t tell Chen Xin.

The reason? Well, there isn’t always a "why." If forced to explain, it’s because they were police while Chen Xin was just a civilian.

Chen Xin naturally didn’t know, but he could imagine some things, so he told Ding Ning: "In times like these, you never know when something bad might happen. A Ning, spare some time to convince Long Niang to move in with you. In case anything happens there, she won’t even be easily rescued."

"I know, I already tried convincing Sister Yan, but she can’t bear to leave her place." Being Mo Qingyan’s good friend, Ding Ning certainly wouldn’t have missed advising her: "But the official shelter... honestly, it’s just safer and guarantees food and clothing. Compared with your private shelters, the living conditions are far inferior."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 83 - 82: Comparison of the Shelters
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Ding Ning had stayed at Mo Qingyan’s shelter, seen Chen Xin’s shelter, and was even taken back to the official shelter by her dad. During her days at the police station, she gained insight into various shelters around the city, allowing her to understand the living conditions in these shelters post-apocalypse and draw distinct comparisons.

In terms of comfort, Mo Qingyan’s shelter undoubtedly ranks first.

Although the shelter was built before the disaster, its design wasn’t intended for this catastrophe, the living facilities and conditions were well-equipped, and Mo Qingyan had taken years to operate it meticulously, making it naturally comfortable to live in.

However, Mo Qingyan initially built this underground shelter for short-term refuge, planning to rely on reserved supplies for about one to three years.

Thus, in terms of self-sufficiency, her shelter can be said to be almost non-existent.

As for abundant materials, it’s still the official shelters.

Even in the apocalypse, where supplies became scarce and precious, official shelters had national reserve supplies before the disaster, and post-disaster there were pre-planned responses, coupled with national resource allocation and planning, making the official shelters have almost everything at present.

It’s just that official shelters only consider how to keep people alive, as for comfort in living, quality of food, these are secondary, only considered when everyone survives and there’s extra capacity.

It’s like in Frostpunk, setting up tents to provide a place for people to stay first, then slowly upgrading houses to withstand severe cold.

Chen Xin’s shelter, in terms of comfort, is indeed not as good as Mo Qingyan’s; the container-style house built into the mountain as a shelter doesn’t particularly boast excellent accommodations.

However, in this post-apocalyptic situation, having a place like this to stay is already very fortunate. Some small shelters in urban areas don’t do well in cold protection, where the internal temperature may not be uninhabitable, but still requires wearing cotton clothing.

The most crucial point is Chen Xin’s shelter has a growing greenhouse that can produce food.

This is extremely important because only self-sufficient shelters can assure the survival of survivors amidst the adverse environments that might last for decades or even centuries.

Otherwise, relying solely on material support, without replenishment, how long can a pile of reserved supplies last?

"Xin, what do you use for power generation here? Gasoline? Is it enough? If not, when we exchange supplies, I’ll ask my dad to swap a bit more for you." Ding Ning inquired Chen Xin, the thought just crossed her mind: "Everything in your shelter is great, except for the energy issue. If the fuel runs out, you’ll face power outages."

Thinking of this, Ding Ning was intrigued, and thus said to Chen Xin: "How about I talk to my dad later and get you a diesel generator! Diesel is easier to store than gasoline, so Xin, you can use it for a longer time."

"Thanks for your kindness, Little Ding, but I have backup plans." Chen Xin didn’t tell Ding Ning he planned on algae oxygen production and biogas fuel cells.

All these would later be leveraged through system upgrades, it’s hard to explain before upgrades, and post-upgrade the system’s information state obfuscation makes explanation unnecessary, so Chen Xin chose not to mention anything.

As for Ding Ning offering to get him a diesel generator, while diesel generators are more efficient and energy-saving compared to gasoline ones, on one hand, this slight improvement means little to Chen Xin who possesses a system, and on the other hand, he really doesn’t have space for another diesel storage tank.

————————————————————

Since Ding Ning delivered the materials, Chen Xin can start making an algae oxygen machine for Mo Qingyan now.

Compared to the one he previously crafted himself, the oxygen machine Chen Xin is making for Mo Qingyan certainly has a surplus of materials.

At least he no longer needs to use mismatched pipes bound with tape for a hard connection, among the materials Ding Ning delivered, there are pipes and joints of all sizes.

Previously when he made the oxygen machine himself, due to simple materials plus there not needing intricate craftsmanship, Chen Xin just cobbled it together with the tools on the weapon workstation.

Now that he’s making an oxygen machine for Mo Qingyan, Chen Xin looked at the glass Ding Ning brought him and decided he needed to make a workstation.

After all, the tools on the weapon workstation suffice for simple use, but processing glass, connecting pipes, and welding circuits demand a more professional workstation.

"Luckily, Little Ding delivered quite a lot this time." Standing at the storeroom’s entrance, looking at all the various items Ding Ning brought over, Chen Xin smiled.

He found several planks in the storeroom, nailed them together to serve as a table and surface, then placed tools on top, thus assembling a simple workstation.

This type of workstation was something Chen Xin had seen workers make during home renovations and when previously building the shelter.

Three or two pieces of wood joined together form a table, which is also a work surface, extremely simple yet practically convenient.

Once equipped with tools and equipment, it becomes a very efficient tool station.

After completion, Chen Xin didn’t inspect too much, he just opened the system, eyed the upgrade materials, then spent 20 survival points to upgrade the workstation.

Post-upgrade, the workstation transformed immediately, from a wooden slat structure to a sturdier table, the surface covered in a green rubber pad, and behind it appeared a pegboard like the weapon workstation’s.

Unlike the weapon workstation’s pegboard, though, the workstation’s pegboard is laden with tools, while the surface is neat, leaving ample space.

Looking at the workstation before him, Chen Xin sat on the stool in front of it, testing the surface’s texture, the smooth and flat surface instantly gave him the urge to create something.

Even though the workstation was satisfactory, Chen Xin built it in the garage, which unsurprisingly made the already cramped space appear even narrower and crowded.

This vexed Chen Xin, upgrading the garage to expand its area or simply separating the workstation into a studio are certainly viable, but Chen Xin must consider resource consumption, particularly the input of survival points.

"For now, let’s focus on upgrading the door’s defense." Chen Xin muttered to himself, pulling out his notebook and checking the recorded information: "Maybe I can add a garage and defense room when upgrading the door, move the car and outbound gear there, kind of like preparing for departure in a game by the door..."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 84 - 83: Thanks to Xianya Xisha Ji for the reward of 100,000
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the bedroom, Chen Xin was leaning against the bed using CAD design software on his laptop to design the door of the shelter.

When upgrading the system items or the shelter, modifications can be made according to Chen Xin’s ideas, so Chen Xin decided to incorporate a bit of his own thinking into the design of the shelter door.

As the first gateway to resist external invasion and the most solid defense, the door of the shelter, in Chen Xin’s conception, is not just a door.

Back when the disaster was about to strike and the survival group was discussing how to build a doomsday shelter, many plans were proposed for this question.

The initial proposal came from the boss who made water purification equipment. He referenced the Federation’s strategy of converting abandoned missile silos into shelters and proposed buying an abandoned air raid shelter to convert into a small shelter where everyone could live together to form a small community.

In this plan, the shelter door was designed to meet the same protection shielding door standards as the state builds for civil defense projects, encased in more than three tons of reinforced concrete structure with steel plates, along with facilities like poison filtration and air purification.

Such a door structure, according to the standards, would be enough to withstand nuclear shock waves, biohazard weapons, and gas attacks during wartime, providing an unparalleled sense of security.

However, this plan was abandoned right from the proposal stage.

The reason being, Flame Country doesn’t sell abandoned civil defense facilities, especially not at such a critical moment when the doomsday is imminent.

So later on, everyone could only follow the construction plan proposed by Chen Xin, find a mountain ridge to dig a tunnel, and then bury specially customized container-style houses inside to build the shelter.

Of course, such a shelter isn’t as sturdy and reliable as an abandoned civil defense project, but considering everyone isn’t facing something as terrifying as nuclear war, this kind of shelter would suffice.

After all, what everyone mostly has to deal with is extreme cold and severe air pollution, which is much easier to cope with compared to nuclear radiation.

The door of the shelter thus changed to a door that everyone could purchase, three-defense doors, instead of the previously envisioned protective shielding doors.

Initially, Chen Xin didn’t have much thought about this either; the three-defense doors he installed are at least twenty centimeters thick, and if the two doors are deadlocked from the inside, it would be truly hard to break down with even a tank.

But now, with the system upgrade, Chen Xin thought he could still make his home’s door even sturdier and improve its defensiveness.

In Chen Xin’s conception, he would increase the current two three-defense doors to three and modify the corridor entering the shelter.

The first door would naturally be the outermost door of the shelter. Upon entry, there should be a purification room for cleaning and disinfecting personnel, vehicles, and equipment entering the shelter from the outside.

Although there isn’t something as deadly as nuclear radiation outside now, after knowing all chemical plants exploded, Chen Xin knew the air outside was no less deadly than a nuclear bomb detonating at the doorstep.

A purification room is absolutely necessary. Those toxic and hazardous substances now pervasive in the air might not be as lethal as nuclear radiation, but if accidentally contaminated, in this current era where medical conditions are far inferior to pre-doomsday times, the lethality might even surpass that of nuclear radiation.

Not to mention, if drenched in acid rain, given the current medical conditions, unless Chen Xin figured out a medical pod capable of repairing body tissue, one could be better off dead.

The current acid rain isn’t like pre-disaster times; before the disaster, it would at most turn the skin red and itchy, causing allergies or requiring treatment for lung disease.

But the acidity and alkalinity of current acid rain are much higher than pre-disaster times, and if drenched, it could truly corrode the skin.

After the purification room, Chen Xin plans to construct a preparation room.

The so-called preparation room is a place for preparing travel equipment.

Chen Xin intends to place the protective equipment that might be used for going out here and also move the garage up here.

In this way, one can be fully prepared here, depart from the preparation room with everything ready, and there’s no need to bring the protective gear inside the shelter.

This can free up some space in the currently cramped shelter and reduce the chance of harmful substances being brought inside the shelter.

Between the purification room and the preparation room, Chen Xin also plans to install an isolation door to block harmful substances from entering the shelter through the air, while also installing defense weapons between the preparation room and the purification room.

Chen Xin, although not intending to harm others, still needs the necessary precautions.

He’s not like official shelters with various defense facilities, nor does his outer door have the solidity of official shelters, so if someone manages to open the outer door, he needs a means of self-defense capable of harming the enemy.

The ideal way would, of course, be to create a laser corridor like in the "Biohazard" movie, where any intruder would die.

But this is just a thought for Chen Xin; actually creating such a thing isn’t impossible, just not practical.

Therefore, Chen Xin plans to set up a heavy machine gun directly facing the shelter door direction, to shoot immediately if any unidentified or enemy person enters, making it convenient and quick.

However, currently lacking a heavy machine gun, Chen Xin plans to use a modified crossbow to replace it.

In a sense, using a crossbow might even have more power and better effects than a heavy machine gun.

Even a modified repeating crossbow is lethal, with arrows that can be recycled and reused, making it efficient and environmentally friendly.

Thinking about this, Chen Xin’s design sketch was almost complete.

The first three-defense door serves as the main door; after entry is the purification room, connected to the preparation room by a short corridor, where two isolation doors are installed. In the corridor is a repeating heavy crossbow, ready to skewer any intruder.

And after passing through the two isolation doors and entering the preparation room, one still needs to go through another three-defense door to officially enter the corridor connecting the shelter.

The end of the corridor then is the final three-defense door.

With such protection, Chen Xin felt the safety of his shelter could be greatly improved.

Besides protection, Chen Xin also plans to move shortwave antennas and ventilation openings further inside the shelter to prevent enemies from cutting off his communication and throwing poison into the shelter through ventilation ducts.

Should he also modify the structure of the corridor? Make it a floor trap door to add another layer of defense?

Chen Xin considered this carefully, but ultimately abandoned the idea, not because it’s unfeasible, but unnecessary.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 85 - 84 Mo Qingyan’s Oxygen Generator
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

There’s really no need to make the passage into a floor door. After all, the first two doors have been breached, and the significance of making the passage a hidden door isn’t great. If someone has already come in, how could they miss a door?

Moreover, turning the door into a floor door and altering the passage structure seems to make it inconvenient for transporting supplies.

Since no other modifications are planned, this blueprint for the main door can be considered complete.

Looking at the finished CAD design on the computer, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel a bit sentimental. This was a skill he taught himself when he was seeking his first job just after graduating from college. Over the years, he hadn’t drawn any blueprints in a long time.

Originally, Chen Xin thought that after the apocalypse came, he wouldn’t need to draw blueprints anymore. Yet he didn’t expect to use this skill again so soon.

But reality is always more convoluted and complex than we imagine; you never know when fate will surprise you with an unexpected twist.

Chen Xin couldn’t tell whether this was a pleasant surprise or a shock, so he closed the laptop, placed it on the bedside table, checked that the pistol in the bedside cabinet was within easy reach, and then he turned off the bedroom light, covered himself with the blanket, and closed his eyes.

Tomorrow, he still has to upgrade the main door and make an oxygen machine for Mo Qingyan. There’s still a lot to do.

However, this feeling of being busy isn’t bad for Chen Xin.

While Chen Xin was sleeping, the bedroom still had a faint green glow. That was the algae oxygen machine in the corner working.

——————————————————

Waking up early, Chen Xin was already sitting at the workbench. He planned to make the oxygen machine for Mo Qingyan first.

The materials Ding Ning brought were fairly sufficient, enough for making the oxygen machine. The only troublesome part was that the glass she brought, though kept as intact as possible, still had many fragments that needed to be cut and pieced together.

Actually, if there could be a furnace to melt this glass and remake it into the required shapes, it would be the best way to utilize these glass fragments instead of just gluing them together.

But for Chen Xin, having glass as the raw material is enough. With the help of the system, he can easily process the glass into any shape he desires.

By the side of the workbench, the original broken glass has been reshaped into a tall annular cylinder, and Chen Xin is making the base.

The base of this microalgae oxygen machine is key. The glass cylinder is merely a container, and its exact shape doesn’t matter. But an annular cylinder is relatively more efficient in utilizing light.

On the base, pipes and a vacuum pump need to be installed to inject carbon dioxide into the annular cylinder, stimulating the algae to carry out photosynthesis.

Therefore, the base should be equipped with ring-shaped pipes, and an air pump should be used to pump carbon dioxide into the pipes, allowing it to enter the annular cylinder through small openings in the pipes.

However, extracting high-purity carbon dioxide is complicated and somewhat dangerous. So Chen Xin directly uses ordinary air here, letting the base draw in the surrounding air directly.

As for the supplementary light, it’s installed on the top cover because the annular cylinder is placed vertically. Dead algae would accumulate at the bottom, which may hamper light penetration.

With various tools on the workbench and the system-upgraded oxygen machine in the bedroom as a reference, making this oxygen machine for Mo Qingyan was quick.

After all, the structure itself is quite simple, and the technology is relatively uncomplicated. With the system’s assistance, Chen Xin quickly assembled everything together.

Compared to the system-upgraded oxygen machine in the bedroom, the one Chen Xin made manually looks rougher. But apart from that, there isn’t much difference, and it’s sufficient for Mo Qingyan’s use.

Therefore, after making the oxygen machine, Chen Xin did not spend survival points to upgrade this one and planned to give it to Mo Qingyan as is.

After placing the completed oxygen machine aside, Chen Xin checked the time and decided to have lunch first, take a break, and then upgrade the main door in the afternoon.

Although it was just about confirming the upgrade, Chen Xin didn’t intend to rush to finish it.

In the shelter, there isn’t much to do every day, and Chen Xin didn’t want to finish everything at once and then have nothing to do other than play games.

Of course, Chen Xin wasn’t truly without tasks. Daily greenhouse maintenance couldn’t be skipped.

However, as crops enter the growth phase, he only needs to ensure water and light supply to maintain the greenhouse’s operation.

As for common pest and disease control in typical greenhouses or planting sheds, Chen Xin naturally paid close attention.

But, whether it’s the quality of the seeds he bought or the system-upgraded greenhouse’s black technology, Chen Xin hasn’t encountered any crop diseases or pests so far—all the crops have been growing healthily.

The drought-resistant rice he planted has now entered the booting phase, developing from the young panicle stage, which will last about thirty days. According to the growth cycle, in a few days, these drought-resistant rice plants should enter the maturation phase and begin to flower.

Needless to say, Chen Xin had never farmed before. After he really started planting drought-resistant rice, he realized that the so-called two-crop-a-year system was merely because of environmental and climatic constraints.

If inside a planting greenhouse, not to mention two crops a year, he could even have three or four.

As a result, Chen Xin discovered that after expanding the greenhouse to its current 200 square meters, using half of the land for planting drought-resistant rice could yield about 600 jin of rice a year. Although it’s not much, it’s enough to sustain Chen Xin’s own consumption as a staple food.

If Chen Xin upgraded the greenhouse further, switching the current single-layer hydroponic setup to a multi-layer system and used all the space for vegetable cultivation, while reserving land for drought-resistant rice, his production could support one to two more people.

As for further upgrades, developing an aquaponic system or raising fish, chickens, or rabbits could support more people’s survival needs.

Moreover, Chen Xin could exchange vegetables for food with the SWAT team now.

Although the food Qin Lan brought from their shelter was lacking both in taste and texture, at least it was food that could be eaten, which is considered quite good in the current times.

Speaking of food, Chen Xin decided after some thought to eat the mushroom and rat meat Qin Lan brought for lunch today, and keep the canned meat from Mo Qingyan for later.

After all, rat meat is currently available, while pork’s next appearance is uncertain.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 86 - 85 Upgrading the Gate
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Regular shelter entrance"

"Upgrade Plan One: Increase the thickness of the protective door, enhance the defense of the shelter entrance, add a biochemical radiation decontamination room; upgrade requires 1500 survival points"

"Upgrade Plan Two: Add a shielding door, add a partition door, add a preparation room, upgrade the air intake of the shelter’s ventilation system; upgrade requires 1350 survival points"

Seeing these two upgrade plans, Chen Xin, who had already acquired sufficient materials from the SWAT team, didn’t consider which plan was more suitable and chose to upgrade everything.

For Chen Xin, who now earns 15 survival points a day, the cost of choosing both upgrade options, with ample materials reducing survival point consumption to 10%, was within his acceptable range.

Chen Xin was surprised that an oxygen generator, which barely consumed a few survival points to make, could provide him with two survival points every day, which exceeded his expectations.

However, this is not hard to understand since, after upgrading, his algae oxygen machine not only produces oxygen but also generates biogas through biomass fermentation for fuel cell power generation.

This means that this single piece of equipment functions as an oxygen machine, biomass cultivator, and fuel cell, greatly improving Chen Xin’s current living conditions, so gaining more survival points is understandable.

After choosing to upgrade everything, the shelter’s door was upgraded into the design Chen Xin had drawn on the blueprint under the system’s power.

A sturdy and heavy protective door, a decontamination room capable of handling radiation and biochemical exposure, a preparation room for parking vehicles and organizing equipment, a defensive corridor equipped with a heavy crossbow...

The current shelter finally looked like a true doomsday shelter, with a solid and reliable defense.

Chen Xin drove up the vehicle that was originally parked below and parked it in the preparation room, also bringing up equipment that he might need when going out.

He hung the heavy protective suit in the closet of the preparation room, neatly arranged all the equipment and supplies needed for outings, and felt a sense of satisfaction and pride looking at the orderly scene in front of him.

It’s like playing Minecraft and finally building a little house where one feels safe and secure, achieving that kind of accomplishment.

And, unlike the game, this is the real world; this is a real shelter that can truly protect Chen Xin’s safety.

By upgrading the shelter entrance, Chen Xin also moved the original ventilation outlet and shortwave antenna from the middle of the mountain to the top of the entrance, placing them in a small bunker made of reinforced concrete.

This bunker isn’t large, just a very small space located above the shelter’s decontamination room, somewhat resembling a machine gun bunker in war games.

However, this isn’t a machine gun bunker; although, in some sense, if Chen Xin sets up a machine gun here, it could indeed be used as one.

The actual function of this small bunker is to protect the relocated shortwave antenna and ventilation outlet, not for actually setting up a machine gun bunker here.

Even though this location may be upgraded into a defensive facility in the future, for now, it only serves as a bunker for the shortwave antenna, with a potential ventilation function at most.

The bunker is covered by the mountain’s soil, with only a palm-width gap, inaccessible from the outside and only reachable through the shelter’s internal passage.

Compared to the shortwave antenna originally just covered by a camouflaged cloth and left in the open air, the antenna is unquestionably much safer under the protection of such a small bunker. Even if discovered, it would be hard to damage when housed in such a solid bunker.

As for the impact of the bunker on the antenna signal, it’s not nonexistent but not severe. At least Chen Xin tested it and found he could receive outside signals smoothly and communicate with others.

The original ventilation outlet, moved inside the bunker, has also changed, adding protective covers and grille filters. After entering the shelter, the ventilation duct also acquired a pressure-relief automatic vent valve.

This is ventilation equipment used in civil defense engineering, using the pressure difference between inside and outside the shelter to create a seal, blocking toxic and harmful air from outside while allowing the shelter’s internal air to exhaust, ensuring ventilation cycle.

Although it sounds complex, its structure is straightforward. With it, the shelter doesn’t have to worry about external toxic and harmful substances entering through the ventilation duct.

────────────────

At an originally existing mall entrance in the city, Qin Lan and several special police officers were guarding with guns.

This was initially a large mall in the city, with an underground supermarket and parking lot, planned pre-disaster as a small shelter, housing about five thousand people from the surrounding area.

The planning of such a small shelter was originally intended to allow some citizens to avoid doomsday disasters there and, post-disaster, connect passages to other shelters, serving as an underground settlement.

But now, due to environmental deterioration and the temporary inability to supply oxygen equipment to small shelters, the city government had to transfer residents to other medium-sized shelters and temporarily store this small shelter.

They plan to reopen it later when passages connecting this place to medium-sized shelters are accessible and there’s surplus oxygen equipment.

Qin Lan and others were there to assist the residents with relocation, along with city government personnel overseeing the transfer.

The second batch of oxygen equipment arrived not long ago, but there were far fewer in this batch than the previous one, with only four small bio-oxygen machines.

Two of these naturally went to the Lakefront District and Polytechnic College, two medium-sized shelters that hadn’t received oxygen equipment before, and the remaining two went to the Armed Police Force’s shelter and a factory.

The city’s government decided to temporarily abandon all small shelters and relocate all the population to medium-sized shelters due to the insufficient oxygen equipment.

Although the population in small shelters isn’t large, only several thousand, transferring and accommodating them is no easy task. The city government staff is already overwhelmed, with various issues needing resolution.

Qin Lan stood on the shooting position on top of an armored vehicle, setting up her sniper rifle, constantly scanning the crowd, searching for potential threats.

In such situations, anything could happen, and Qin Lan and her team must prevent any possible criminal acts to avoid escalating into mass incidents.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 87 - 86 Population Relocation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Even before the disaster, it was an incredible task for a country or government to relocate thousands of people at once, requiring strong organizational and execution capabilities. The various potential safety issues were even more headache-inducing.

And now, it’s even more challenging than before. The dim environment relied solely on the spotlights of surrounding police cars for illumination. Under the stark white light, the faces of the people were filled with anxious fear of the unknown and worry for their own future.

This wasn’t like the past, where when the government mentioned relocation, everyone was willing to trust them because the image cultivated by the Flame Country’s government made the public willing to believe it.

But under the apocalypse, the threat of disaster already made everyone live in fear and anxiety, worrying daily about how they would survive. Now, having to move from a relatively stable shelter is nothing short of a major blow for these people.

If it weren’t for the city government’s staff tirelessly calming the crowd and with the special police standing by, it might have already descended into chaos.

However, even so, there was constant unrest within the crowd, uneasy whispers passing through.

This shelter housed just over five thousand people, and the city government organized about a dozen buses that could still run to transport them in batches. The group currently waiting were the third batch.

Each bus could carry about fifty people. A single trip would transport a tenth of the people, so the more than five hundred individuals waiting at the entrance were only this batch, with the rest still inside the shelter.

This was for safety’s sake. Managing five hundred people was far easier than five thousand, and it also prevented the remaining people from waiting indefinitely, becoming restless, or worse, suffering from frostbite.

The temperature was already extremely low, dipping below freezing. While it wouldn’t immediately freeze people to death, making them stand outside for a long time was very grueling.

Especially since the current air was particularly bad. Without the rain to bring them down in this cold, the toxic substances and dust in the air lingered. Engaging in outdoor activities without protective gear was simply impossible.

Even the special police, fully equipped with protective gear, had to rotate shifts after a while.

However, the people didn’t wait long. A dozen or so buses soon arrived and stopped in front of them.

Seeing the buses arrive, the waiting people appeared slightly relieved and reassured.

Though the future remains uncertain, at least they could now rest assured that the government hadn’t abandoned them.

Watching the waiting people board the buses one by one under the guidance of city government staff, a member of the special police in the armored vehicle where Qin Lan was stationed asked the team leader, Li: "Leader Li, is this the third batch? How many people have been sent off already?"

"Yes, with this batch, it should be over seventeen hundred," Li nodded.

Each time a batch left, the city government kept count and reported the numbers to prevent anyone from being missed.

Hearing Leader Li’s response, the special police officer couldn’t help but sigh: "Isn’t this the seventh shelter? These people are really pitiful."

"What else can be done? If they don’t move, they’ll suffocate!" Leader Li understood what the special police officer beside him was lamenting, but there was indeed no other solution.

Hearing their words, Qin Lan also couldn’t help but feel a twinge of empathy—indeed, if they didn’t move, they’d suffocate.

In fact, the city’s decision to relocate was out of desperation. With the temperature further dropping, issues of oxygen deficiency or carbon dioxide poisoning were arising in each shelter.

If the shelters hadn’t paid close attention to this issue and taken preventive measures in a timely manner, many people might have died from it.

Nevertheless, a number of people still died due to this, most of whom were elderly or children.

Thus, the city decided to relocate the small shelters’ populations to medium-sized shelters.

After all, the three large shelters and six medium-sized shelters were equipped with oxygen-generating devices, providing the most basic survival assurance for the residents.

While this relocation would bring about many troubles and issues, as long as more people could survive, the city wouldn’t choose to abandon the residents of these small shelters, letting them fend for themselves.

The topic didn’t continue, and after a sigh and a shake of his head, the special police officer who had spoken turned his attention to the buses filled with people and ready to leave. He couldn’t help but worry, "In this weather, these buses will likely need all their air filters replaced after this trip, won’t they?"

"It doesn’t really matter. After transporting these shelters’ people, the buses won’t be of any use anyway," Li was indifferent about it. Although car parts were difficult to obtain in the current situation, the buses wouldn’t have much use after transporting these shelters’ people.

There were a total of eleven small shelters in the city. The hospital, armed police units, underground planting bases, and factories wouldn’t be moved, so only seven small shelters needed relocation, and this was the last one.

Listening to the discussion between Li and the special police officer, Qin Lan naturally thought about this issue as well. Although the buses would be useless after this trip, that didn’t mean there wouldn’t be a need for vehicles anymore.

Not to mention, the special police’s daily patrols did wear down the vehicles, not to mention Qin Lan had to make a monthly trip to Chen Xin’s shelter.

Even though there was a stock of auto parts from before the disaster, without resupply, all vehicles would eventually break down.

Their special police vehicles had undergone protection measures, replacing the paper dry air filters with oil bath air filters more suitable for harsh environments and less prone to wear. Still, the consumption of air filters remained significant.

However, as the nation resumed industrial production, these supplies were gradually becoming available, albeit consumption still outpaced supply, necessitating the use of existing stockpiles.

This train of thought led Qin Lan to consider that Chen Xin’s car might not have undergone special protection, and its air filters might not be replaceable. Perhaps she could add this item to their exchange list?

This idea gave Qin Lan quite an inspiration. If automotive supplies could be added to the list, so could other items like generator repair parts, which could also be exchanged for vegetables with Chen Xin.

Needless to say, even though everyone might get by without vegetables, for the special police, getting fresh vegetables from Chen Xin each month had become the thing they most looked forward to, even if the vegetables only lasted for a few days each month.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 88 - 87: Delivery to Your Door
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the preparation room, Chen Xin put on the Protective Suit and fastened the protective mask on his face, organizing the equipment he planned to take.

Originally, Chen Xin intended for Qin Lan to bring the completed oxygen machine to Mo Qingyan when she came to exchange supplies with him. However, out of concern for Mo Qingyan, Chen Xin decided to deliver it to her himself.

The entire oxygen machine had already been stuffed into the SUV’s backseat compartment, with secure binding to ensure it wouldn’t roll due to its round shape.

After all, this thing was made of glass, and if it got bumped, it could be damaged.

The equipment Chen Xin brought included a flashlight and a folding hiking pole, the same gear he used on his last trip out, except this time he swapped his homemade gun for a handgun he obtained from Qin Lan.

Initially, Chen Xin had not planned to bring a gun, considering he was heading to the city. However, after hesitating for a while, he decided to carry the gun for his own safety.

In addition, Chen Xin brought along a bottle of oxygen.

This was a bottle of oxygen he had previously stored, with not many on hand, and once used up, there wouldn’t be more. But considering the current reduction in oxygen levels outside and the potential risk of hypoxia, he carried one bottle to prepare for the possibility of insufficient oxygen.

Having organized his equipment, Chen Xin opened the upgraded shelter door and drove the car out.

After securing all doors, Chen Xin set out on his journey to deliver the oxygen machine to Mo Qingyan.

In the rearview mirror, the shelter door looked much more proper than before, no longer resembling a gate buried into a dirt pit, but rather a reinforced solid door with cement surroundings.

In fact, it was all constructed from reinforced concrete, not just cement. Although it looked more conspicuous, there were advantages.

Sturdiness was, of course, the main one, and besides that, it facilitated drainage.

Although the major precipitation had turned into snow as the temperature dropped, a sturdy cement ground was still more drainage-friendly than ordinary mud ground and easier to maintain.

Driving towards the city, Chen Xin had to turn on the headlights this time instead of keeping them off as before when going out.

This was because the roads had deteriorated compared to when Chen Xin went out last time, and with the recent rainfalls and occasional meteor impacts, the road was now littered with more debris than during his last trip to the city.

This considerably slowed down Chen Xin’s progress since the car was carrying a glass-made oxygen machine. Too much jostling would easily damage it, so Chen Xin had to carefully navigate smoother paths to protect the oxygen machine.

Last time Chen Xin entered the city, most of the city’s buildings were merely fire-damaged, with their overall structure still intact.

But this time, upon entering the city, Chen Xin noticeably saw some buildings had collapsed.

Those were collapses caused by meteor impacts. Over time, the floating dust and meteor debris in the sky would gradually settle, with larger chunks falling first; now, the larger debris was among those falling.

In a sense, this was also a blessing. The continuous descent of debris meant the dust clouds in the sky wouldn’t be as thick, and after one to two years, the land would gradually regain sunlight.

For humans currently, transitioning from a lightless to a dimly lit environment in one or two years could significantly improve human survival conditions.

However, restoring normal lighting would still require prolonged periods; such dust-clouded years might last at least several decades, requiring at least two generations.

As for restoring Earth’s ecosystem to its original state, that would require an even longer period, possibly hundreds of years before the Earth’s ecosystem could recover.

To Earth, this might merely be a fleeting moment, an insignificant instant in Earth’s billions of years, and despite the significant damage caused to Earth’s ecosystem in this instant, it remains trivial in the broader scope.

However, for humanity, this is an apocalyptic catastrophe affecting the survival and development of civilization across generations. Despite humanity’s self-perceived dominance over Earth, humans are extremely small and fragile compared to Earth itself.

————————————————————

Arriving at Mo Qingyan’s doorstep, Chen Xin called her via radio as he did previously.

Compared to the last time Chen Xin visited, Mo Qingyan’s home had evidently undergone some fortification and modifications. A ring of barbed wire had been added to the wall, and the iron gate appeared reinforced. The storefront doors and windows facing the street had been nailed shut, and a simple barricade made of angle iron and wood was placed at the entrance of the back courtyard gate.

Though somewhat conspicuous, it indeed provided a measure of security. At least making it more difficult for anyone wanting to do something to Mo Qingyan’s house.

Receiving Chen Xin’s call, Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin opened the iron gate together, removed the barricade, and gestured for Chen Xin to drive in.

After Chen Xin drove the car in, the Mo sisters quickly moved the barricade back to its original position and closed the gate again.

Just like the last time, Chen Xin drove into the elevator and descended into the shelter with the Mo sisters before getting out of the car and removing the mask from his face.

"Hey, your Protective Suit looks great! Where did you get it? I didn’t see you wearing it last time," Mo Qingyan said, looking at Chen Xin’s Protective Suit with envy.

Although she had some protective gear, it was evidently nowhere near as professional as Chen Xin’s Protective Suit that covered him from head to toe.

When Mo Qingyan asked about his Protective Suit, Chen Xin lifted the cap off his head before responding, "I modified it myself. If you’re envious, Mo Qingyan, you can give me the materials, and I can modify one for you too."

"Oh! Did you make it yourself? It’s really well-done! What materials do you need? Make one for me and little Xin next time!" Mo Qingyan didn’t hesitate upon hearing that Chen Xin could make one for her, readily requesting his help.

Chen Xin nodded in agreement. It was just a Protective Suit, and even if it required survival points to upgrade, it wouldn’t cost many survival points with Mo Qingyan providing the materials.

"By the way, this is the oxygen machine I made for you. I mentioned the oxygen issue previously, and worried that you wouldn’t be able to cope, so I made one for you," Chen Xin didn’t forget the purpose of his visit, opening the car’s trunk and taking out the completed oxygen machine, "As long as there’s no power outage, this machine should be enough for you and little Xin."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 89 - 88 Sudden Distraction
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"You actually know how to make this out of ingredients! That’s amazing!" Mo Qingyan sincerely admired as she looked at the oxygen generator Chen Xin had unloaded from the car.

Earlier, when Chen Xin mentioned the need to pay attention to oxygen issues, Mo Qingyan also learned from Chen Xin how to make a simple oxygen generator by themselves. She even planned to make one with her sister, Mo Qingxin, but they soon realized that it wasn’t something that could be easily put together.

Faced with the oxygen generator Chen Xin took out, besides admiring his hands-on ability, Mo Qingyan also felt quite touched.

So she half-jokingly said to Chen Xin, "Ah Xin, how should I exchange something like this with you? If I barter with booze, wouldn’t you empty my wine cellar? Or should Qingxin and I just give you some fun time?"

Hearing Mo Qingyan say this, Chen Xin hadn’t responded yet, but Mo Qingxin’s face immediately turned red.

Although in the past, jokes like that between Chen Xin and Mo Qingyan wouldn’t be minded, saying it directly still made Mo Qingxin feel very shy and awkward.

"Nah, let’s forget the fun time; I don’t have that thought now." Chen Xin shook his head, declining Mo Qingyan’s proposal, "Just give me two bottles from your collection, two crates of booze, and two crates of canned food. As for wanting some fun time, wouldn’t it be better for you two to move into my place?"

Even though having something happen between him and Mo Qingyan wouldn’t affect their relationship, Chen Xin still wanted to stick to his principles.

"Damn, didn’t see that coming! You’re actually thinking of hiding beauties in your house?" Mo Qingyan, hearing Chen Xin’s rejection, didn’t quite know if she felt relieved or regretful but quickly and exaggeratedly teased Chen Xin, thus ending the topic.

Chen Xin didn’t continue with the conversation, instead helped Mo Qingyan set up the oxygen generator.
