After filling it with water, he said to her, "The operation of the oxygen generator requires continuous power. Without light, the algae inside won’t photosynthesize, so make sure it stays powered. Longniang, you have backup power here, right?"

"Yes, I designed a battery group when setting up the shelter. Fully charged, it lasts three days, and I also have a generator here." Mo Qingyan nodded, indicating that the power was no issue.

Hearing Mo Qingyan say this, Chen Xin nodded reassuringly. While pouring water into the oxygen generator, he said, "The water in the oxygen generator doesn’t need frequent changing, but remember to clean the algae that settle at the bottom to prevent clogging the spray nozzles at the base."

Listening to Chen Xin’s instructions on using the oxygen generator, Mo Qingyan nodded and had Mo Qingxin take careful notes.

While recording the points Chen Xin mentioned, Mo Qingxin asked with concern, "Ah Xin, I’ve noted everything you said, but where are we supposed to get algae now? Given the current situation outside, aren’t all the algae dead?"

"Algae aren’t that easy to completely die off; there should be some surviving." Chen Xin had naturally prepared for this, smiling at Mo Qingxin before taking out a bottle of green liquid from his car, "But you don’t need to go and find them; I’ve prepared this for you. It’s water from my oxygen generator, rich in algae, which will reproduce in yours once poured in."

"Self-reproducing? Doesn’t it need anything else?" Mo Qingxin curiously asked as she watched Chen Xin pour the green liquid into the tank of the oxygen generator.

"Under normal conditions, you should add nutrients to promote algae reproduction, but given the circumstances, we’ll have to make do," Chen Xin shook his head helplessly but also explained, "Even when just left alone, over time, the water becomes nutrient-rich and naturally produces algae, so you don’t need to worry about the algae proliferation."

"If the algae inside really aren’t growing well, Longniang, just go find A-Lan or Xiao Ding. I heard them mention that the official shelters use the same kind of algae-based oxygen generators and should have nutrient salts for algae. You can just get some from them."

Qin Lan indeed mentioned this matter when she delivered goods earlier. Since he was also using an algae-based oxygen generator, Chen Xin had naturally taken note of it.

Hearing Chen Xin say this, Mo Qingyan quickly nodded and said, "I got it. I’ll ask A-Ning later."

After connecting the power and getting the oxygen generator running, Chen Xin finally felt satisfied, clapped his hands, and said to Mo Qingyan, "Alright, in a day or two, it should turn green, and oxygen will be generated."

Though he said this, Chen Xin actually wasn’t sure if enough algae would form within a day or two.

After all, his oxygen generator was directly upgraded by the system, with the algae rapidly reproducing after the upgrade, turning the water dark green in just one day, far exceeding the normal algae reproduction rate.

However, algae are really easy to grow. If you leave a puddle untouched, it turns green in just a few days, so Chen Xin wasn’t worried about the oxygen generator at Mo Qingyan’s place functioning properly.

Seeing that Chen Xin had installed the oxygen generator, Mo Qingyan proactively said to him, "Thank you for your hard work, Ah Xin. Why don’t you stay for lunch before heading back!"

"Huh...? Oh! Okay!" For some reason, Chen Xin was momentarily stunned, and it wasn’t until Mo Qingyan called out that he came to his senses and accepted her invitation to stay for lunch.

Although Chen Xin agreed, he seemed somewhat absent-minded while waiting for Mo Qingyan to cook, as if lost in thought.

Noticing Chen Xin’s state, neither Mo Qingyan nor Mo Qingxin said much, merely asked with concern, and, seeing there wasn’t an issue with Chen Xin, didn’t dwell on it.

For lunch, Mo Qingyan brought out the emergency food stored before the disaster — three portions of self-heating food, a can of pork, and the cabbage exchanged from Chen Xin.

It’s undeniable that such items, which can be stored for years or even decades, are incredibly useful in the current environment.

Compared to storing rice, frozen meat, or dehydrated vegetables, emergency food not only lasts longer but also saves on fuel and water.

Since the emergency food nowadays is self-heating, just a sip of water can heat it up by itself, making it more convenient and reliable in a post-apocalyptic setting than raw ingredients like rice, which need water to cook.

As for raw ingredients like rice and frozen meat, they must be cooked before consumption, requiring precious drinking water and fuel, and their storage heavily depends on conditions.

Rice is slightly easier to handle, but meat like frozen meat needs to be kept in a low-temperature environment for longer storage, unlike emergency food that can be stored almost anywhere with better longevity.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 90 - 89: A New Way to Obtain Survival Points
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After having lunch at Mo Qingyan’s place, Chen Xin didn’t linger and drove back to his own shelter.

However, along the way, his emotions appeared somewhat excited, and his expression seemed a bit unusual.

Returning to the shelter, he parked the car in the maintenance room, closed the doors again, took off his gear, and quickly returned inside, sitting down on the sofa in the living room, calming his excitement from the unexpected gain.

"Helped someone improve their shelter’s survival conditions, gained 100 survival points."

"First time helping someone improve their shelter’s survival conditions, gained 100 survival points."

"Helped 2 people improve their survival conditions, gained +2 daily survival points."

The system, which usually didn’t seem to have much presence, suddenly threw three notifications in front of him, making it hard for Chen Xin not to notice them.

The sudden increase of two hundred survival points, and +2 survival points daily, was undoubtedly a huge surprise and a significant reward for Chen Xin.

He helped Mo Qingyan make an oxygen machine, which only cost a few survival points for material processing fees, yet the gains were equivalent to what he would earn in half a month.

Such a generous return made Chen Xin wonder if he should make more oxygen machines for others.

After all, apart from "first-time helping someone improve their shelter’s survival conditions," which is an achievement and cannot be repeated, the other two can be obtained repeatedly.

"Helping someone improve their shelter’s survival conditions" is a one-time income, but the system didn’t say it could only be done once.

Chen Xin isn’t stupid; if oxygen machines can improve survival conditions, then can greenhouses? Ventilation systems? Biogas fuel cells? More robust shelter doors?

Improving a shelter’s survival conditions, just like enhancing his own, is a project that can be continuously upgraded.

This means he can earn a lot of survival points as long as he is willing to help others improve their survival conditions.

And by improving others’ survival conditions, he can earn more daily survival points, significantly increasing his speed of gaining survival points.

Through these three prompts, Chen Xin seemed to see a sea of survival points rushing toward him, and the power armor and mecha that he hoped to get when the shelter upgraded seemed to be slowly approaching him, waving at him.

"Improve someone’s survival conditions, count by the head, and it’s one more point per person every day. If I could make the official shelter generate such a notification, ten thousand people would mean ten thousand points a day!" Chen Xin excitedly muttered to himself, though this was just his imagination, it didn’t stop him from anticipating a bright future.

However, this really has a great possibility; as long as Chen Xin can improve the survival conditions of a large shelter, according to the current system prompts, one person could mean an increase of one daily survival point, enough for him to earn a lot of survival points by headcount.

Of course, truly achieving this isn’t easy.

Large shelters are different from smaller ones, where any random upgrade can lead to improved survival conditions.

For large shelters, improving the overall survival conditions undoubtedly requires upgrading and renovating the entire shelter.

This apparently requires a lot more survival points than upgrading smaller shelters to achieve the goal.

Just like the official shelter’s oxygen machine, with thousands of bio-panels, it’s not something Chen Xin can currently produce.

Even using survival points for upgrades, the survival points consumed by such large devices would probably be enough to upgrade his entire shelter.

In short, helping large shelters improve their survival conditions can indeed bring substantial rewards, but it’s not something Chen Xin can afford right now.

However, this also gave Chen Xin another thought, if he can’t impact large shelters, there are still small shelters to take advantage of!

In the survival group he formed with Mo Qingyan, there are quite a few who built their own shelters. According to post-disaster statistics, there are more than twenty private shelters owned by the group members.

Although over the past few months, a small portion of group members, due to lack of preparation or initial plans to just survive the disaster, abandoned their shelters and moved with supplies to the official shelter, there are still nineteen private shelters remaining.

Five of them are in Lin City, two are in Three Towns, and excluding Chen Xin’s and Mo Qingyan’s, ten are in this city.

In previous routine communications, everyone’s situation was fairly good, without major issues, and the supplies were relatively sufficient. Besides exchanging what they each had, they didn’t even need to trade much.

But to Chen Xin now, these ten shelters are just brimming with survival points!

He doesn’t even need much; delivering an oxygen machine to each shelter could provide Chen Xin with a thousand survival points, and by gathering some himself, he could use materials to upgrade his entire shelter.

Thinking of this excited Chen Xin; he had long witnessed the powerful system upgrade function, and being able to upgrade the entire shelter would undoubtedly bring him better survival conditions, allowing him to survive this apocalypse better.

The temperature has already dropped to zero, and it will continue to fall. Without proper preparation, the impending extreme cold is enough to freeze those who aren’t sufficiently prepared.

As for why helping others improve their survival conditions can earn survival points, Chen Xin didn’t find it odd.

Humans are social creatures; helping others can arguably improve the overall social environment, and once the overall environment is better, individual survival conditions naturally improve as well.

After all, in this harsh post-apocalyptic environment, unity and cooperation are clearly more favorable for survival than going it alone.

It’s like those surviving shelters in the Ice-Punk Era; without mutual support and help, how could they survive better in the apocalypse?

If Chen Xin didn’t have the system, he might be eagerly exchanging supplies with others by now?

After all, even though everyone stocked supplies according to the same list, the supplies each person managed to purchase are different, and so are the shelters built.

Like him and Mo Qingyan, he has vegetables, and Mo Qingyan has wine and canned meat; their exchanges can achieve resource complementarity to some extent.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 91 - 90: Exchange Conditions
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The private shelters are the same; there are always things you have that I don’t, and things I have that you don’t. Trading and exchanging are the keys to everyone’s survival.

It’s just that Chen Xin has connections with the SWAT team, allowing him to trade for more and a greater variety of supplies, so he hasn’t needed to trade resources with other shelters.

But now, knowing that helping others improve their survival conditions can also earn survival points, Chen Xin naturally won’t miss the opportunity to communicate with other shelters.

During the evening’s routine communication time, Chen Xin first brought up the oxygen issue again. This was an issue he had informed Mo Qingyan about earlier, and he had simultaneously notified other group members, so as Chen Xin mentioned it again, others naturally responded.

"It’s a good thing you reminded us, Writer. I almost suffered from oxygen deficiency because I went out searching for supplies without an oxygen tank."

"But even knowing about it, there’s not much of a solution, right? At my home, I rely on the photosynthesis from the few plants in the greenhouse for oxygen, and I don’t even dare to turn off the grow lights now."

"I heard that in the official shelters, the state seems to have oxygen machines ready, so now the city hall is transferring people from smaller shelters to larger ones. Should we also...?"

The group member didn’t finish the sentence, but the meaning was clearly expressed.

If you can’t make it, go to the official shelters.

This isn’t an unacceptable or last-resort choice; several companions who couldn’t hold out had already chosen this path, so it was only natural for someone to suggest the same idea now.

For Chen Xin, this was exactly the effect he wanted, so he hurriedly spoke up: "I have a solution for the oxygen problem! I can make a simple oxygen machine, and I’ve already traded one to Long Mei. If anyone else needs one, I can help you make it."

"You can make an oxygen machine, Writer?!"

"Really? If you can make it, I’ll take one!"

"I need one too! What do you want in exchange, Writer?"

"With you saying that, from today, you’re my brother from another mother!"

"Writer, what materials do you need to make an oxygen machine?"

Chen Xin’s words immediately sparked a strong reaction, because for these survivors, getting an oxygen machine would undoubtedly help them survive better in this apocalypse.

Although going to the official shelters was an option, being able to stay comfortably in a private shelter was preferable. Who would want to struggle to survive in the official shelters?

Before the apocalypse, they already had some understanding of the conditions in the state-constructed shelters. While the conditions provided by the state weren’t bad, if given a choice, they wouldn’t want to live in the official shelters.

Even though no one said it out loud, thanks to pre-apocalypse disaster films, everyone had some understanding of the darkness of human nature in an apocalypse.

In a sense, the more people there are, the more likely problems will arise in this apocalypse.

At first glance, this seems to contradict the idea that only through unity and cooperation can you better survive, but in fact, the two do not conflict.

With fewer people, the main threat is from the external environment, while with more people, it primarily comes from internal conflicts.

Facing threats from the external environment, if there are too few survivors or only one or two people, there is very little they can do.

Not to mention, it’s hard for one person to build a sturdy shelter. If there are enough people, they can even gather resources while building the shelter.

But more people also bring more problems; everyone has their own ideas. With fewer people, you can discuss and agree.

However, with more people, there are naturally more ideas, and if you can’t get most people to focus on the same thing and work together, having more people is more dangerous than having fewer.

So, at the beginning of the apocalypse, when Chen Xin and the others discussed building a shelter, they actually concluded that the most reliable and suitable method to maximize the chances of survival was to establish a community-like existence, gathering everyone together with a small team model forreasonable division of labor, unity, and cooperation to face the threats brought by the apocalypse.

Unfortunately, Flame Country is not like the Federation, where you can sell abandoned missile silos to be converted into shelters by the wealthy.

Their plan was scrapped from the start, and everyone could only build private shelters on their own.

And private shelters, while more comfortable than large official shelters, obviously lack the basic infrastructure.

Chen Xin hadn’t decided what to exchange for the oxygen machines, because for him, giving an oxygen machine to others and earning survival points was his biggest gain.

But obviously, he couldn’t just tell others he needed nothing and give them an oxygen machine for free.

The story of "grains of gratitude, pecks of enmity" is ancient wisdom in Flame Country.

"To make an oxygen machine, I mainly need glass, a vacuum pump, and grow lights. I still have materials here, so don’t worry." Chen Xin didn’t plan to ask others to provide materials, but he didn’t intend to give them away for nothing either: "As for what to exchange it for, see what you have. If it’s useful to me, I’ll trade. I can’t just let everyone suffocate."

"Don’t worry, Writer, we won’t let you suffer. I have some fresh fish here. Do you want some fish?"

"Just a few fish for an oxygen machine? That’s too stingy! That SUV of yours was bought from my shop, right? With the environment so harsh now, I’ll do a full maintenance for you!"

"The weather’s getting colder; do you need a heater, Writer? I have a spare heater."

As soon as Chen Xin finished speaking, everyone began to talk about their exchange terms, eager to impress Chen Xin and get him to make them an oxygen machine first.

After all, even if Chen Xin could make oxygen machines, he’s only one person, so production efficiency is low. If everyone wants one, some will get theirs sooner, and some later.

No one wants to fall behind when it comes to life-and-death matters.

Chen Xin could only feel a mixture of helplessness and amusement about this, but it had been a long time since he saw such a lively scene.

After the apocalypse, Chen Xin had spent most of his time alone. The lively atmosphere gave him a sense of normalcy, making him feel he wasn’t living in isolation.

But among all the offers, Chen Xin heard something that caught his interest.

"I’ll trade a medical kit with you, Writer. It has all kinds of common medicines, medical supplies, and a set of basic surgical instruments that can handle most common illnesses and injuries."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 92 - 91 "Doctor
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

For Chen Xin, a medical kit stocked with a complete range of drugs and filled with various medical supplies is precisely what he needs.

Although Chen Xin himself also has a medical kit and a field first aid kit, these are just ordinary household medical kits and emergency kits for wilderness survival.

Even though Chen Xin tried to stock up with as many kinds as possible of medicines before the disaster, they were basically just some common household medicines like cold medicine and anti-inflammatory pills.

It certainly cannot compare to a professional medical kit, especially since the group member providing the medical kit specifically mentioned they would prepare enough drugs and a complete set of basic surgical tools inside.

This group member’s former nickname in the survival group was "Doctor," and he indeed works as a doctor, but not in a hospital; he opened his own clinic.

When the survival group was discussing what medical survival supplies to prepare, it was he who provided a lot of advice and help from a professional standpoint. Some medicines and medical supplies were also acquired through his connections.

Speaking of which, the medicines in Chen Xin’s medical kit were also organized with his help.

So when the "Doctor" mentioned he could trade a medical kit with various drugs and basic surgical equipment for an oxygen concentrator from Chen Xin, Chen Xin agreed after only a little consideration.

This was actually also related to Chen Xin’s desire to set up a medical room for himself.

He had tried to partition off a medical room using his medical kit and first aid kit when he first obtained the Survival System, but obviously, with such rudimentary tools, the system wouldn’t recognize it even if it were omnipotent.

Now the "Doctor" says he can provide more professional medical tools, which naturally caught Chen Xin’s attention and interest.

If he could use these medical tools to set up the medical room, it would be great news and an improvement in survival quality for Chen Xin.

————————————————

The "Doctor’s" refuge is located in the city’s old town, near Dongting Lake.

This area used to be old blocks, which were upgraded and transformed into Jiangnan-style gray-tiled, white-walled structures by the previous Mayor, redeveloping the area into a tourist and commercial street.

"Doctor" lived here before the disaster, and his clinic was also located here.

His home wasn’t a newly rebuilt house but an old one with a layer of exterior decoration added after renovation.

But even so, the original gray tiles and white walls were very nice-looking, though now they’re charred and blackened, looking like ruins.

When Chen Xin arrived, the "Doctor" was waiting eagerly.

"You, Writer, have finally come!" Seeing Chen Xin appear, the "Doctor" hurriedly greeted him.

"Long time no see, Doctor." Chen Xin greeted the "Doctor" and parked his vehicle at the front of his house.

The "Doctor’s" house wasn’t as spacious as Chen Xin and Mo Qingyan’s where you could drive the car into the refuge. It was just a small building along the street with a half-basement structure and an additional basement level dug out by him during the previous street renovation, forming the Doctor’s refuge.

After removing the oxygen concentrator from the car, the "Doctor" quickly handed over the medical kit he had prepared long ago.

Chen Xin opened the medical kit, checked the items inside, and nodded to the "Doctor," indicating there were no issues.

Seeing Chen Xin approve of the goods, the "Doctor" called out to his disfigured clinic, and an old man and a young woman came to haul the oxygen concentrator inside together.

Behind them, there was also an older woman watching them closely, full of vigilance and suspicion toward Chen Xin.

"Who are these people?" Chen Xin frowned slightly; the young woman he recognized, as she was a little nurse who worked at the "Doctor’s" clinic.

A very simple girl, but she was from out of town and stayed to work in the city after graduating from medical vocational school here.

As for the other elderly couple, Chen Xin had never seen them before, especially since both wore masks and scarves wrapped around their heads, making it hard to identify who they were.

The "Doctor," hearing Chen Xin’s inquiry, quickly introduced them: "These are my parents; they used to live in the countryside. I didn’t want them to squeeze into the official refugee centers, so I brought them here."

Hearing this, Chen Xin nodded without surprise. It’s only natural to think about one’s own parents and family when there is space and ability.

If Chen Xin’s parents were not living back in their hometown in another province, he would have let them stay with him.

Chen Xin didn’t worry about his parents, though; he had told them about building a refuge before the disaster and asked them to come back to stay.

However, Chen Xin’s parents found it too troublesome to move back and forth, and his relatives in his hometown were all from the same village. The local refuge was also built in that village, so they didn’t come back but stayed with their relatives.

Therefore, Chen Xin didn’t find anything strange about the "Doctor" bringing his parents over.

But seeing the young woman helping "Doctor’s" father carry the oxygen concentrator, Chen Xin was a little curious why she was there, casting a strange look at the "Doctor."

Perceiving Chen Xin’s gaze, the "Doctor" felt embarrassed and hurriedly explained: "Xie Chang is alone in this city, and after the disaster, she couldn’t return home, so I kept her here to avoid crowding in the official shelters."

"Is that so? Seems like you two plan to be together?" Chen Xin teased the "Doctor" upon seeing his embarrassed expression.

It’s not surprising if something developed between Xie Chang, the little nurse, and the "Doctor," especially in this environment where the "Doctor" took her in, and the two getting together wouldn’t be unusual.

However, just after Chen Xin said that, the "Doctor’s" mother, who was watching them, harshly commented, "My son will never be with a woman like her! Eating from us, living with us, and now she wants to be my daughter-in-law? No way! She’s just a hired help for our family!"

The ’Doctor’s’ mother’s words made Xie Chang freeze involuntarily while moving the stuff.

Chen Xin looked at the "Doctor’s" mother with a changed expression. Although her words seemed reasonable, her attitude and speech were really off-putting.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 93 - 92: Medical Room and Doomsday Menu
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Mom, go inside first, it’s cold outside!" The "doctor’s" face froze for a moment, and he hurriedly pushed his mother back indoors.

After pushing his mother away, the "doctor" said to Xie Chang, "Xiao Xie, my mom is just like that. She means no harm, don’t take it to heart!"

Clearly, the "doctor" did not treat Xie Chang as someone who just ate and lived in their house.

Faced with other people’s family matters, Chen Xin did not comment but simply picked up the medical kit the "doctor" exchanged with him. He explained the usage of the oxygen generator to the "doctor", glanced at Xie Chang, and then turned to his car to leave.

On the road home, Chen Xin reflected on the attitude of the "doctor’s" mother towards Xie Chang and felt inexplicably uncomfortable.

Even though the attitude of the "doctor’s" mother towards Xie Chang seemed to be a commonplace norm even before the apocalypse, Chen Xin felt it was wrong.

Because it lacked the most basic respect between people.

In the apocalypse, perhaps everyone struggles to survive; some may do selfish things to ensure they live, which is understandable.

But Chen Xin always felt that even while safeguarding one’s own survival at the expense of others’ interests or lives, one should maintain the most basic respect for others.

The reason why humans are called humans is because we have humanity and morals, which are also our biggest distinguishing traits from beasts.

If people cannot be called humans, then what difference is there between them and animals?

Moreover, even animals do not amuse themselves by bullying, abusing, or insulting their kind.

This is something only humans do after losing the moral baseline and the most basic respect for others.

Chen Xin did not intend to judge the "doctor’s" mother on her attitude towards Xie Chang, but he never hoped to encounter a person who did not understand respect for others.

Fortunately, the "doctor’s" mother lives in their own family’s shelter; otherwise, if it were an official shelter, her attitude would inevitably cause trouble.

In those post-apocalyptic scenarios depicted in movies, she might very well be among the first to die.

Chen Xin shook his head, as if realizing that cursing someone with death wasn’t good, so he stopped thinking about it and pressed the gas pedal, intending to quickly return home and try to see if the doctor’s medical kit could upgrade to a medical room.

——————————————————

The city roads seemed to have been cleared by someone, not all roads were clear, but at least the main roads were passable.

Chen Xin drove fast and soon returned to his shelter.

After a bit of tidying up, Chen Xin carried the exchanged medical kit back to the living room, found his medical box and first aid kit, and put all three together.

Looking at the three medical supplies he had gathered, Chen Xin thought for a moment and then decided to bring over a chair.

If upgraded to a medical room, the level of upgrade certainly wouldn’t be high right now, but there should at least be a place to sit down and deal with injuries or provide treatment.

Once everything was ready, Chen Xin hesitated a moment but still placed everything in a corner of the living room, planning to connect the medical room to the living room rather than moving it to the empty garage.

He planned to turn that space into a workshop for making equipment or other things.

After all, when it comes to building something like Power Armor or Mecha in the future, it would definitely require space, even though it’s still far-fetched for Chen Xin at the moment.

With everything prepared, Chen Xin called out the system upgrade interface.

"Simple Medical Treatment Room"

"Upgrade Plan 1: Expand medical treatment room space, add surgical chairs, upgrade surgical instruments, add sterile space, bacteria reproduction rate -10%, survival points required for upgrade 1000"

"Upgrade Plan 2: Expand medical treatment room space, add testing equipment, add a medicine storage cabinet, medicine storage time +10%, survival points required for upgrade 1100"

Seeing the upgrade plans provided by the system, Chen Xin finally felt at ease.

He had been worried that even with the medical kit provided by the "doctor," the system might not consider it enough to set up a medical room, and he wouldn’t know what to do.

If that were the case, he would have to go to the "doctor" again or get more medical supplies via Qin Lan.

Though it wouldn’t be very troublesome, Chen Xin didn’t like any kind of hassle.

Fortunately, the system now provided a satisfactory result; he just needed to complete the upgrade.

Chen Xin didn’t rush to upgrade, as upgrading the medical treatment room would require some materials in addition to merely using survival points.

There were some things he didn’t have yet, which he planned to exchange for with Qin Lan later.

Anyway, he already had the frequency of the communications channel from the special police unit so he could contact Qin Lan directly without needing Mo Qingyan to relay messages.

The possible outcome of upgrading the medical room made Chen Xin feel very pleased, and since it was lunchtime, he decided to eat something nice today.

He took out a can of canned pork from the storeroom, along with some shelter food given by Qin Lan. After thinking it over, he added a box of luncheon meat and a cabbage.

Since he decided to eat a bit better, adding another dish wouldn’t count as extravagant.

However, when leaving the storeroom, Chen Xin glanced at the beer and cola he had found from outside, hesitated a bit, but ultimately decided not to touch them.

In this post-apocalyptic situation, the production of wine and drinks might take a long time to recover, and what’s available is mostly what was stored before the disaster; using some means having less left.

Just like the cola and beer Chen Xin discovered, brewing beer requires wheat, and now to use wheat for brewing beer would be an extremely luxurious affair.

While cola might be produced again, it would probably become like a high-end luxury item.

So, the cola and beer he found are things Chen Xin wouldn’t easily indulge in before they expire.

Unless something happens that makes him happy enough, he might consider opening one to celebrate.

He opened the can of pork, poured the stewed, tender meat and congealed fat into a pot and placed it on the induction cooker to heat. Meanwhile, he cleaned the cabbage and chopped it together with the luncheon meat, planning to stir-fry them.

When the juices in the pot started to bubble, Chen Xin poured the meat and juices back into the can.

As for the leftover oil and a bit of juice in the pot, Chen Xin didn’t pour it out or clean it but instead put it back on the induction cooker, then added the cabbage and luncheon meat to the pot and started stirring.

Given the scarcity of resources in the apocalyptic world, things like oil and salt should be saved where possible, since even at Qin Lan’s place, it’s hard to exchange for seasoning like oil and salt.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 94 - 93 The Dilemma of Wind Turbines
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Help improve survival conditions for others at the shelter, earn 100 survival points."

"Help 4 people improve survival conditions, earn +4 survival points daily."

Unlike last time when handing over the oxygen generator to Mo Qingyan immediately delivered survival points, it took Chen Xin two days to receive the survival points from the ’doctor’.

Of course, this was because he didn’t help with on-site installation like he did for Mo Qingyan and also provided a bottle of algae seedlings.

Without the algae seedlings provided by Chen Xin, the ’doctor’ himself would need some time to acquire enough algae for the oxygen generator to start working properly.

But since the survival points have been credited, Chen Xin could naturally infer that the ’doctor’s’ oxygen generator had begun normal operation.

During these two days, Chen Xin wasn’t idle; he used the materials on hand to make two more oxygen generators, but he didn’t deliver them door-to-door again.

One must know that making a trip would consume quite a bit of gasoline; previously, it was just the gas money, but now gasoline is a critical resource. Even with the Special Police Squad connections, the amount of gasoline he can exchange for each month is just barely above his personal consumption.

This small amount of gasoline can be used for the occasional outing, but delivering goods to people over and over would mean he can’t even afford to run a generator.

Before the biogas fuel cell is created, Chen Xin still relies on gasoline to power the entire shelter, so gasoline must be conserved whenever possible.

While Chen Xin himself stayed indoors, others were free to come to pick up the goods.

The two oxygen generators he hurriedly made showed no signs of being unsellable. As soon as he announced on the radio that he had made two more oxygen generators, someone reserved them immediately.

Coincidentally, the ’doctor’s’ survival points arrived today, and someone came to pick up the two oxygen generators.

"Writer, I’m at your doorstep, can you open the door?" Chen Xin was checking the growth condition of drought-resistant rice in the greenhouse when a voice came through the radio hanging from his waist.

Chen Xin unhooked the radio from his waist, listened to the voice coming from it, and responded, "Got it, Old Farmer, coming to open the door for you now."

Seeing that someone had arrived, Chen Xin of course couldn’t just stay in the greenhouse and, after tidying up briefly, planned to open the door.

Of course, Chen Xin didn’t foolishly open the door directly; instead, he went to the bedroom first and took out a pistol from the weapon cabinet, tucking it into his clothes.

Even with the gun ready, Chen Xin did not open the door immediately but climbed through the passageway to a small bunker where a shortwave antenna was placed, using a gap to take a look at the entrance of the shelter.

Seeing that there was indeed only one vehicle and it didn’t look like there were other people in it, Chen Xin climbed back through the passageway before starting to open the door.

After opening three doors, Chen Xin closed the innermost protective door connecting the interior of the shelter with the prep room. Then he put on a gas mask, opened the remaining three doors, and walked out.

"Writer!" Seeing Chen Xin open the door and come out, the person in the car quickly jumped out and greeted Chen Xin.

The person picking up the oxygen generator was also a member of the survival group, nicknamed ’Old Farmer’; everyone usually called him Old Farmer.

His real name is Li Wendong (provided by reader group member Shihao), and he originally ran a hydroponic planting equipment business in the city.

When disaster struck and the group discussed how to prepare the shelters, it was he who suggested that everyone set up greenhouses to ensure food production after the disaster.

After communications were restored post-apocalypse, Chen Xin heard him mention the aquaponics system in his shelter, which he found enviable.

One must know that this disaster can be described as a catastrophe for the entire Earth’s ecosystem; not only were terrestrial creatures almost completely exterminated, but marine and freshwater ecosystems also collapsed catastrophically. Fish in Dongting Lake were massively caught even before the disaster.

This was partially to store more edible food, and partially to reduce losses.

However, this batch of fish was not immediately used as food by the city government but was canned and stored as part of the city’s strategic reserves.

As a result, the city now has no fish to eat anymore, and even if Chen Xin could trade for resources with the Special Police Squad, fish weren’t accessible.

But this is not a problem for Li Wendong.

His aquaponics system not only provides vegetables like a regular greenhouse but also offers him fresh fish.

This time, the price Li Wensheng paid to get an oxygen generator from Chen Xin was ten fresh fish.

Such a deal is quite rare in the post-apocalyptic world. In the three or four months since the apocalypse began, Chen Xin hadn’t tasted a bit of fresh meat, let alone fresh fish.

Receiving the cooler filled with fresh fish from Li Wensheng, even though he couldn’t smell them, Chen Xin smiled in satisfaction upon opening it and seeing the ten dressed-up crucian carp inside.

He invited Li Wensheng into the shelter and from the garage of the prep room, he brought out the ready-made oxygen generator and handed it to Li Wensheng.

"Thank you so much, Writer. Your oxygen generator is truly a godsend!" Li Wensheng expressed his gratitude to Chen Xin over and over after receiving the oxygen generator.

Chen Xin looked at him curiously and asked, puzzled, "Old Farmer, you have an aquaponics system and the oxygen produced by the greenhouse should be enough, right? You shouldn’t need an oxygen generator!"

"Writer, don’t remind me about aquaponics; that’s a long story!" When mentioning his aquaponics system, a wry smile appeared on Li Wensheng’s face. "Aquaponics is great, but it consumes a lot of electricity! You can’t imagine how power-hungry the whole aquaponics system is to operate!"

Upon hearing Li Wensheng’s complaints, Chen Xin couldn’t help but raise an eyebrow and asked him, "Old Farmer, didn’t you consider the energy consumption before building this system?"

"Of course I did!" Li Wensheng’s face showed even more bitterness, but he couldn’t avoid explaining to Chen Xin, "Initially, I thought there wouldn’t be much of a problem as long as the city didn’t cut off power, but in the end, my area had no power supply because there were no official shelters.

But I had anticipated this when I was building the shelter and had specially prepared three wind turbines."

"Wind turbines? They can’t cope with such harsh environments, can they?" As soon as Chen Xin heard, he knew what kind of situation Li Wensheng was dealing with.

At this, Li Wensheng was immediately filled with resentment: "Exactly, first acid rain seeped into the bearings, causing a power decrease and severe bearing wear. Then came freezing rain, directly freezing the bearings... Troubles are endless, and all three wind turbines were completely incapacitated in three months."

With the resentment in Li Wensheng’s words, Chen Xin suddenly understood the predicament he was currently facing.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 95 - 94 Aquaponics
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Obviously, Li Wensheng originally intended to use wind turbines for power, providing sufficient electricity for his shelter, but clearly the harsh environment has greatly shortened the lifespan of the three wind turbines.

Although they can’t be said to be completely broken down now, the paralyzed wind turbines are evidently not something Li Wensheng can repair on his own.

Therefore, he can only rely on the generator that was originally planned as a backup power source to supply electricity to his shelter, maintaining the operation of his fish-vegetable symbiosis system.

But this clearly has exceeded the consumption limit Li Wensheng can sustain, plunging him into a predicament of energy shortage.

"Then you surely can’t hold on for long?" Chen Xin couldn’t help but worry about Li Wensheng’s situation, especially since he just took an oxygen generator from Chen Xin, which, though not consuming much electricity, needs to run continuously.

"Who says not? I’ve already used more than half of my stored diesel. Without replenishment, I won’t last more than three months." Li Wensheng couldn’t help but shake his head, his expression filled with bittersweet helplessness: "I’ve now shut down half of the fish-vegetable symbiosis system, and temporarily processed and stored the harvested vegetables and fish to reduce consumption, hoping to last a few more months, or maybe repair the wind turbines.

If I really can’t hold out, I’ll have no choice but to bring my wife and children to the official shelter, because I can’t watch them starve to death!"

After hearing Li Wensheng say this, Chen Xin felt a bit uneasy and almost spoke up to say he could help repair the wind turbines, but he held back.

There are some things you can help with, and there are others you shouldn’t be overly enthusiastic about.

Holding back the urge to help, Chen Xin thought for a moment and still couldn’t bear to stand by and do nothing, so he said to Li Wensheng, "Old Farmer, if you have excess output, try to barter with the official shelter. Right now, I exchange the vegetables grown in the greenhouse for supplies from them, managing to barely get by."

Upon hearing Chen Xin say this, a glimmer of hope appeared on Li Wensheng’s face, and he quickly asked Chen Xin, "How did you barter, Writer? Can you help me exchange some resources too? I don’t need anything else, just diesel!"

However, before Li Wensheng finished speaking, he could tell he said the wrong thing just by looking at Chen Xin’s gaze.

Chen Xin didn’t say much to Li Wensheng, only reminded him, "The official shelter should be quite lacking in vegetables and fresh meat. Although they raise rats and grow mushrooms themselves, and the municipal government has an underground growing area at the power plant, fresh vegetables and meat are still relatively scarce. Old Farmer, you should be able to exchange for the gasoline you need."

"Okay, thank you, Writer! I’ll head back first!" Li Wensheng felt somewhat embarrassed because of what he had said earlier, thus carrying the oxygen generator given by Chen Xin, he left his shelter.

Chen Xin also closed the shelter door tightly, and carrying a box of fresh fish, he walked into the depths of the shelter.

It’s not that Chen Xin couldn’t help Li Wensheng exchange diesel, but he and Li Wensheng didn’t have the same depth of relationship as he did with Mo Qingyan, Ding Ning, and Qin Lan. Giving him advice was fine, but for Chen Xin to personally help him exchange gasoline seemed too eager.

Stories like "a gift of a measure of rice becomes animosity from a fight over two measures" have happened too often in history, so while Chen Xin isn’t stingy about helping others, it doesn’t mean he will selflessly assist them.

Ultimately, Chen Xin barters supplies with the SWAT team not only because his fresh vegetables are what they need, but the main reason they can achieve fair trade is due to his relation with Ding Ning and Qin Lan, leading Uncle Ding to offer relatively favorable exchanges as a nod to Ding Ning and Qin Lan.

If Li Wensheng really falls into hard times, Chen Xin might help him exchange diesel once or twice to address the emergency, but to expect Chen Xin to long-term help him exchange diesel to maintain his shelter is impossible.

This kind of thing can be done once or twice but not repeatedly over and over, after all, Chen Xin doesn’t have a reason to spend his resources on helping others.

However, the fish-vegetable symbiosis system mentioned by Li Wensheng actually gave Chen Xin great inspiration and temptation; he might also consider setting up a similar vertical agricultural system.

Should he try it when upgrading the shelter next time?

This thought crossed Chen Xin’s mind, causing him to seriously contemplate.

While putting the exchanged fresh fish into the freezer to freeze, Chen Xin began considering the feasibility of this idea.

The fish-vegetable symbiosis system combines fish farming and hydroponics into a cyclic composite cultivation system. Its primary principle is using bacteria to decompose ammonia nitrogen from fish waste into nitrite, which is then decomposed into nitrate by nitrifying bacteria, and nitrate can be directly absorbed and utilized by plants as nutrients.

The water absorbed by plants becomes relatively clean and can be reused to supply the fish, thereby realizing fish farming without changing water and vegetable planting without fertilization.

Consequently, fish, vegetables, and bacteria can form a complete cycle. Before the disaster, with sufficient sunlight, the system could even achieve a closed-loop cycle by feeding the fish with leftover vegetables, requiring only 2-3 cubic meters of water paired with 20-30 square meters of vegetable planting area to essentially meet the vegetable and fish consumption needs of a family of 3-5.

In the current apocalyptic environment, maintaining such a system requires supplementing with lamps to replace sunlight, and expanding the planting area to provide staple food output.

Chen Xin’s current greenhouse doesn’t have the conditions to establish this, although during the initial construction of the shelter he did consider setting up a fish-vegetable symbiosis system. However, realizing that the energy consumption of this setup far exceeds that of soil cultivation, he abandoned the idea due to energy concerns, opting for an ordinary planting greenhouse instead.

Yet now, with the system in place, Chen Xin feels he can reconsider upgrading his planting greenhouse to a fish-vegetable symbiosis system.

However, the energy consumption issue is indeed a critical concern, especially since Chen Xin can only barely maintain the shelter’s operation, with all electricity supply dependent on the monthly gasoline exchange with the SWAT team to power the generator.

Without upgrading the power system, he’s not planning to increase the shelter’s electrical load.

Thus, besides setting up a medical room, Chen Xin’s primary upgrade target is the power system.

Despite having prioritized power system upgrades from the start, step by step other upgrades were completed, leaving the power system untouched.

Though the current power system is still adequate, Chen Xin decided it’s best to upgrade it, at least installing biogas fuel cells to reduce the generator’s load.

Of course, these things can’t be rushed, for now...

Chen Xin looked at the fresh fish already placed in the freezer and couldn’t help but swallow; he hasn’t eaten fish for quite a while.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 96 - 95 Concerns About Heating
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin was unsure whether Li Wensheng had exchanged for diesel, but after making one more oxygen generator, his materials finally ran out. The remaining six group members who needed an oxygen generator would only be able to get one after he obtained new supplies from Qin Lan.

Knowing this, the other six group members naturally couldn’t hold back. In such a situation, waiting even one more day could increase their chances of encountering danger.

But gathering materials was not an easy task.

Among the materials Chen Xin needed to make the oxygen generator, glass was the easiest to obtain. After all, if one didn’t mind broken glass, it was everywhere now.

As for pipes, it wasn’t too difficult either. By dismantling apartments where no one lived, it was possible to gather enough of them.

However, vacuum pumps and grow lights were much harder to collect. Two group members had spare grow lights, but no one had extra vacuum pumps.

So despite the urgency, apart from the "doctor" who was quick to get an oxygen generator, Li Wensheng, and two other group members, the remaining six would still have to wait until next month.

After delivering four oxygen generators and with the benefits from Mo Qingyan’s place, Chen Xin gained a total of 600 survival points, and a daily increase of 16 survival points.

Currently, Chen Xin could earn 18+16 survival points each day, with monthly income already exceeding a thousand points.

This was a rapid increase compared to when Chen Xin first got the system, where his monthly income barely exceeded 300 points.

This also made Chen Xin understand the system’s method of acquiring daily survival points.

One method was the current one he used — improving his shelter’s survival conditions.

The other was helping others to improve their survival conditions.

Each method had its pros and cons; the latter wasn’t necessarily better than the former. However, one approach might bring survival points more slowly, while the other could be faster but riskier.

After all, amid the apocalyptic disaster, the darkness in people’s hearts could grow. Helping others might indeed aid them, but it could also harm oneself, and finding the right balance was crucial.

Therefore, appropriately helping others to gain more survival points, while also building a safer and more comfortable shelter for oneself, was a suitable approach as long as one’s own safety was ensured.

Finally having some surplus at hand, Chen Xin couldn’t hold back his desire to upgrade and upgraded the kitchen.

The kitchen was a place that Chen Xin had considered upgrading since he received the system. Originally a place for an induction cooker and cookware, after the upgrade by Chen Xin, it became a formal stove setup, with a row of cabinets added to the walls, finally looking like something proper.

However, upgrading the kitchen didn’t bring Chen Xin any new daily survival points.

This was not too hard to understand. After all, even if the kitchen was upgraded, Chen Xin still ate the same things every day.

Only if he could obtain more food ingredients and use the kitchen to cook elaborate and delicious meals would it count as an improvement in survival conditions.

As for the medical room, because it still lacked some materials, Chen Xin could only wait for Qin Lan to deliver the supplies and materials needed this month to upgrade it.

Otherwise, he would have to spend ten times the survival points to upgrade it.

While this wasn’t impossible, Chen Xin still adhered to the principle of saving wherever possible.

Besides upgrading the medical room, he also needed to upgrade the power system, which was a major aspect.

After all, the power system in Chen Xin’s plan also needed to connect to a biogas fuel cell, and to have biogas, he would first need to set up a biogas digester.

A biogas digester certainly couldn’t be dug on site; it could only be obtained by upgrading the algae oxygen generator along with the fuel cell. Chen Xin had to reserve this survival point for this purpose.

————————————————

Qin Lan’s delivery time was quite punctual. Although in the apocalypse, no one cared much about time and date anymore, Qin Lan still delivered the supplies Chen Xin needed regularly at the beginning of each month.

"Ah Xin, your front door is quite well done!" Qin Lan couldn’t help but praise the shelter’s door and entrance, which had completely changed since her last visit.

Hearing Qin Lan mention this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel a jolt of anxiety. This was the first time he had exposed something upgraded by the system in front of someone else, and it made him a bit nervous.

So he casually asked Qin Lan, "Ah Lan, do you think it’s good?"

"Of course it’s good. For you to manage this all by yourself, it’s already impressive. An average construction team might not achieve this," Qin Lan seemed unaware of any irregularity and instead praised Chen Xin’s craftsmanship: "I knew you were handy when we were together, but I didn’t expect you to have such skills."

"There never was a chance to show it before, right?" Chen Xin could only respond with a smile at this time.

Through Qin Lan’s reaction, Chen Xin seemed to understand what the system’s concept of information state blurriness meant.

For instance, regarding the shelter door, normally Qin Lan would immediately recognize that it wasn’t something Chen Xin could accomplish, but the information state concept blurriness from the system made Qin Lan believe it was Chen Xin’s work.

This information state concept blurriness was something Chen Xin found hard to comprehend in terms of technological tier, but it didn’t stop him from understanding its effects.

Simply put, it makes everything appear reasonable, not arousing suspicion.

Others would believe the shelter’s upgrades were Chen Xin’s own renovations, and new items would be seen as his own creations or something he bought; in short, it all seemed logical.

Chen Xin didn’t know if this qualified as a Halo of reduced intelligence. After all, the current degree only rationalized the emergence of various items without lowering intelligence.

Chen Xin didn’t ponder over the issue any further and quickly put it behind him.

After moving the supplies Qin Lan brought into the shelter, Chen Xin still invited her to sit down and have a drink.

Qin Lan didn’t refuse. After sitting on the sofa in the living room and taking a look around, she suddenly asked Chen Xin, who was pouring her water: "Ah Xin, do you have any heating equipment prepared?"

"Heating equipment? Yes, I have an electric heater and an electric blanket for sleeping at night, that should be enough." When Qin Lan asked about the heating equipment, Chen Xin was a bit surprised but still replied: "I’m inside the mountain. The shelter’s walls were insulated during construction, so the temperature doesn’t drop too low, and the electric heater is enough for regular use."

"Your shelter is so big, with only one electric heater, it can only warm one area, and the overall rooms are still cold, right? Also, you’re growing vegetables in the greenhouse; the temperature in the greenhouse can’t be too low!" Qin Lan was still somewhat concerned after hearing Chen Xin’s explanation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 97 - 96: Seeds in the Apocalypse
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As for Qin Lan’s worries, Chen Xin actually didn’t care much. With the system in hand, he had the confidence to solve any problem he faced, even if he was initially unprepared; a quick upgrade could resolve it.

Take the greenhouse heating issue Qin Lan mentioned, for instance. He could easily upgrade the greenhouse a level or two, enabling it to have heating functions to maintain a certain temperature.

According to Chen Xin’s plan, he wants to upgrade the entire shelter. A complete upgrade of the shelter could include a heating system, just like when he upgraded the main gate and added a washing and preparation room.

Plus, the mountain itself offers some insulation, so Chen Xin wasn’t overly concerned about it.

Though he couldn’t tell Qin Lan this directly, he still nodded toward her and said gratefully, "I understand, Lan, thank you for the reminder. I’ll pay attention to this issue."

"It’s good that you are aware, Xin. The city office shelter has heating equipment. If it’s not enough, I can talk to the captain to get you a set with higher power," Qin Lan proactively offered a solution to Chen Xin.

Although the two had broken up, Qin Lan still didn’t have a bad impression of Chen Xin. She cared for him like she used to, though now they weren’t as close, more like just friends.

Chen Xin felt the same about Qin Lan. Their personalities simply weren’t suited to being together.

But perhaps because caring for Qin Lan had become a habit, Chen Xin still showed concern, saying, "My side is not a big issue, but Lan, you need to be careful. You’re always performing tasks outside. Now, with the shortage of materials, and without the usual hand warmers and such, you must wear more when out on duty."

"I understand, I will," Qin Lan responded with a smile to Chen Xin.

Seeing the familiar yet long-lost smile, Chen Xin felt a stir in his heart and said to Qin Lan, "Stay for lunch before you go. I got some fish from a friend. I remember Lan liked chopped pepper fish head the best, though unfortunately I don’t have peppers. Otherwise, I’d make you one."

Hearing about having fish to eat, Qin Lan’s expression turned surprised: "Xin, you actually managed to get fish? Such a rarity now! I’ve heard the city government has a fish-rearing area in the underground planting zone, but the yield is so low we can’t even taste it.

The pre-disaster caught fish are all canned now, and the SWAT team did get a batch, but the captain said it’s reserve stock; no one is allowed to touch it."

"In that case, all the more reason for you to stay for a meal, Lan," Chen Xin replied with a smile.

Faced with Chen Xin’s invitation and the lure of fish long unseen, Qin Lan hesitated but ultimately chose to stay.

Seeing Qin Lan agree to stay for lunch, Chen Xin retrieved supplies from the storage room intending to treat her well.

Though the meal still consisted mainly of canned pork, luncheon meat, and rat meat, along with home-grown cabbage, lettuce, and carrots, the addition of fish made this meal exceptionally luxurious and rare in today’s post-apocalyptic setting.

From the supplies Qin Lan brought in this exchange, he found sugar, vinegar, and oil, planning to make sweet and sour fish.

Though the fish Li Wendong brought were all carp—a variety not ideal for sweet and sour—but given the current conditions, Chen Xin decided to go ahead with sweet and sour fish.

Chen Xin remembered the first meal Qin Lan ever cooked for him was his favorite: sweet and sour carp.

As he put the frozen fish into the kitchen sink to defrost, Chen Xin simultaneously placed various condiments in the cabinet above the stove.

"Lan, is the government still supplying salt, sugar, and soy sauce? Are they all out of production now?" Chen Xin asked casually while tidying up.

"It’s not so bad; while it’s quite scarce, there’s no complete shortage. The local government is growing beets and soybeans in the underground planting zone, even rice, so producing sugar, soy sauce, and vinegar isn’t a problem, just low yield." Responding to Chen Xin’s inquiry, Qin Lan naturally shared her information.

Some of this information was what Qin Lan heard while on duty, some she saw in internal reports.

The government has built an underground planting zone near the power plant, not for planting a single crop but creating a complete ecological agricultural system that can produce diverse crops, even for further processing.

While the yield isn’t high, the variety is quite complete.

Hearing Qin Lan’s response, Chen Xin raised an eyebrow with curiosity: "Do they have peppers there?"

"Of course! How could the people from Hunan not eat peppers?" Qin Lan said with a matter-of-fact expression.

Qin Lan’s words excited Chen Xin, prompting him to ask hurriedly, "Can you get me some pepper seeds? Soybean and beet seeds would be great too."

"Okay, I’ll tell the captain when I go back, it should be possible," Qin Lan nodded, agreeing.

If Chen Xin wanted the crops themselves, it might be difficult, but if he just wanted the seeds, it wasn’t too challenging.

Despite the seeds being precious, without farmland, having seeds is useless, so relatively, the value of seeds is less than that of grown crops.

Of course, not everyone could easily access them, but the SWAT team could still manage to get some.

Thinking of seeds, Chen Xin regretted the apocalypse seed pack he purchased before the disaster.

Considering post-disaster planting, Chen Xin bought a comprehensive seed pack online, intending to combat food shortages after the catastrophe.

The pack contained eighty different types of seeds, from common vegetables like cabbage and lettuce to watermelon, strawberries, even cherry radishes, cucumbers, scallions, and cilantro.

This seed pack promised food security and even the potential to build a small ecological park, utilizing aquaponics alongside dry farming to meet all his nutritional needs.

Despite not considering aquaponics, Chen Xin planned this way, but unfortunately, he bought the seed pack online. Even the most reputable SF Express couldn’t maintain deliveries under national lockdown.

So the pack never reached him, becoming an eternally unreceived package.

Consequently, post-disaster, he could only plant three types of vegetables—cabbage, lettuce, and carrots—and one crop—dry rice.

Luckily, Qin Lan said she could help get seeds for him, or he would have had to consider asking around to see if anyone had extra seeds to trade.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 98 - 97: Considerations for the Medical Room and Staffing
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After having lunch at Chen Xin’s shelter, Qin Lan didn’t stay long and left after helping Chen Xin clean up.

Once Qin Lan departed, Chen Xin turned his attention to the medical supplies placed to the side and chose to upgrade the medical disposal room.

There was still a flash of green light, but this green light didn’t construct a room on the spot. Instead, it opened a hole in the wall of the living room.

The wall was decomposed by the green light, which extended outwards into the mountain, quickly hollowing out a sufficiently large hole and constructing a new room.

When the green light receded, a medical room appeared before Chen Xin, small but fully functional, capable of performing surgeries.

With a bit of excitement, Chen Xin pushed open the door and stepped inside.

The medical disposal room wasn’t large—just a rectangular room with a surgery chair placed at the back. Next to it was a curtain that seemed to be drawable for partitions, intended for surgeries.

Apart from serving as a temporary surgery room in the latter part, the front of the room had a small table with some instrument-like devices on it.

Next to the table, against the wall, were two large cabinets that contained various medicines and surgical instruments.

The entire medical disposal room didn’t have any high-tech elements and looked quite ordinary—aside from being able to perform surgery, it wasn’t even as good as a school medical office.

Chen Xin was very satisfied with this; regardless of its advancement or completeness, the priority was to have something, then consider making it better.

After examining the entire medical disposal room, silently looking at various items, opening and checking the cabinets, and even lying on the surgery chair for a trial, Chen Xin felt that the current form of the medical disposal room was basically sufficient.

After all, in this apocalyptic world, this medical disposal room was completely adequate for general minor injuries and ailments. As for the diseases or injuries it couldn’t handle, Chen Xin didn’t believe he had the capability to deal with them anyway.

In the end, he was just an outdoor survival enthusiast who had learned first aid for a few days, not a professional doctor.

Mentioning professional doctors brought Chen Xin to think about Mo Qingyan’s sister, Mo Qingxin, a student at a renowned medical school in Xiang Province, who was supposed to graduate this year.

However, various events delayed the school’s start by half a year last year, turning her final year of clinical internship into a practical battle. This year, she was supposed to graduate smoothly, but due to the meteor incident, she didn’t graduate and probably won’t ever receive her diploma.

Fortunately, this didn’t affect the fact that Mo Qingxin had completed all necessary learning. Her final year was merely for clinical practice, and compared to formal doctors, Mo Qingxin only lacked experience.

Thinking about this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but wonder if he could have Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin move to his place, not only would he have two more people for work, but also gain a sufficiently professional doctor, despite the lack of experience.

However, this was just a consideration. Not mentioning how he would persuade the Mo sisters to move to his shelter, even if they did move, with two women and one man sharing a room, ignoring whether Chen Xin had any thoughts about Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin, if they had any thoughts about him, he’d have little ability to refuse or resist two against one.

So, not wanting these issues to trouble him, Chen Xin decided not to consider having others move into the shelter for now.

Of course, with the shelter’s upgrade, recruiting manpower would be inevitable.

After all, with the shelter’s upgrade, many tasks would require manpower, and there were times when Chen Xin couldn’t handle everything alone.

However, this matter required careful consideration, such as whom to invite to his shelter and how to extend the invitation—all these were questions.

Without clarifying his thoughts and plans and completing the shelter’s upgrade, Chen Xin wasn’t planning to think about this, merely considering whom he might invite.

As the shelter upgrade would only accommodate five people according to the system’s upgrade plan, excluding Chen Xin himself, that left only four slots.

Putting aside the thoughts about recruiting manpower, Chen Xin returned his focus to the daily maintenance of the shelter.

The current daily maintenance of the shelter was not light. Besides refueling the generator and regular cleaning, the maintenance of the planting greenhouse was Chen Xin’s most labor-intensive task each day.

Arriving at the planting greenhouse, the vegetables on the hydroponic growing trays had been harvested before; now they’re empty, waiting for Chen Xin to plant again.

The dry rice on the ground had started filling, and in a month it would be ready for harvest.

The carrots were replanted last month and were growing well. If Chen Xin wanted to eat them, pulling one out, washing, and eating raw was entirely possible.

The tender carrots, though not mature, still tasted nice even in this early stage.

However, Chen Xin didn’t do such things. He had quite a bit of carrots planted, and since he hadn’t finished last season’s harvest, there was no need to waste them.

After checking the cabbage and lettuce left for seed saving, seeing no issues with their growth, Chen Xin took down the fixing panels on the hydroponic trays, preparing for new seed planting.

Seed saving was a rather troublesome task because cabbage is a biennial plant, requiring about ten months for seed saving. Even with a consistent temperature in the greenhouse, the growth cycle still takes months.

Fortunately, lettuce grows relatively fast. As the first batch of planted lettuce, the seed-saving plant Chen Xin kept had already flowered and fruited.

However, the seeds weren’t mature yet, and thus were not ready for harvest.

Although picking only the outer leaves of lettuce while keeping the heart allows continued growth, there is a time limit for this. After one or two years, it stops growing, so Chen Xin opted for complete harvest and seed saving.

After cleaning the fixing panels, taking new seeds and placing them on the panels, spraying

to keep them moist, Chen Xin returned the panels to the hydroponic trough.

After finishing the tasks, he couldn’t help but tap his lower back, feeling somewhat sore.

Although it’s a workload for one person, if someone else were helping him, it would undoubtedly be much easier.

Seeing all the fixing panels placed back in the hydroponic trough without issues, Chen Xin confirmed once more before leaving the planting greenhouse.

Though the work in the planting greenhouse wasn’t much, Chen Xin looked at the time on his phone after leaving the greenhouse and found it was already dinner time.

Dinner wasn’t as luxurious as lunch. Chen Xin simply stir-fried a piece of lettuce and opened a portion of the shelter food Qin Lan sent today for dinner.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 99 - 98 Entertainment Methods
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

While having dinner, Chen Xin was also planning the work for tomorrow.

There are actually quite a number of things to do in the shelter every day, so Chen Xin has developed a habit of making a plan for himself to keep things more organized.

The seeds in the greenhouse have already started to germinate; in the coming days, it will only require checking the germination status. There’s no need to schedule work for the greenhouse tomorrow, just a quick look in the morning, noon, and evening will suffice.

Qin Lan delivered new materials; the oxygen machines promised to the six other group members need to be made as soon as possible. Chen Xin plans to make two machines in the morning.

Two oxygen machines equal two hundred survival points, and six machines equal six hundred points. Such a significant amount of survival points cannot be ignored by Chen Xin.

However, Qin Lan brought quite a lot of materials, and Chen Xin reserved some to make a few more oxygen machines for himself. He plans to make four more oxygen machines this month, then use five machines to upgrade into an oxygen machine group, enhancing the overall efficiency of oxygen production and biomass generation.

With sufficient biomass, the biogas fuel cell that Chen Xin was hoping for can also be put on the agenda.

Once the biogas fuel cell is online, it can effectively reduce the generator’s load. When Chen Xin upgrades to a new power generation method, the gasoline generator can retire with honor, and gasoline can be used specifically for vehicles to travel.

In fact, in Chen Xin’s plan, the biogas fuel cell is merely an auxiliary power source. Its main significance is to consume the biomass produced by the algae oxygen machine, while releasing heat in the reaction process to supply the planting greenhouse.

The low-temperature heating issue mentioned by Qin Lan is not a concern for Chen Xin, though he still needs to consider it.

After all, the shelter’s level is not high currently. Although system upgrades can solve many problems, there are still numerous issues that require Chen Xin to discover and come up with solutions before they can be solved by system upgrades.

With these thoughts in mind, Chen Xin is also pondering how to conduct the shelter’s first overall upgrade.

For survival points currently, as long as the six oxygen machines are delivered to the six group members, he can earn another six hundred points. Coupled with the saved ones, and at the current speed of acquiring monthly survival points, Chen Xin estimates that in about a month, he should be able to save enough survival points for the upgrade materials.

As for materials, it’s easier to say as it mainly involves steel and cement, which are materials currently provided by the SWAT team.

Similar to the game where players’ speed of upgrading is limited, and the store sells a limited amount of items each month, there is a limit to the supplies Chen Xin can get from the SWAT team each month. Still, the total amount of materials he needs is not large.

Considering the materials he has already received from Qin Lan this month and the ones he can exchange for next month, Chen Xin calculated it and it’s almost enough to meet the upgrade needs.

Being able to upgrade the shelter overall is something Chen Xin has been looking forward to ever since he got the system.

After finishing his meal and tidying everything up, Chen Xin turned on the TV and the game console, planning to play a couple of games to relax and even fetched some liquor from the bedroom.

But although he was drinking while playing games, Chen Xin did not intend to play for long.

He doesn’t play games often, even though it’s the only form of entertainment in the shelter currently, and it’s his way to reminisce about the pre-disaster times. However, he doesn’t indulge in it and strives to restrain his desire to play games.

On one hand, game consoles and TVs are known to consume a lot of power. Previously, it didn’t matter with electricity supplied—at most, it just resulted in slightly higher electricity bills at month’s end.

But now, it’s a matter of life and death; Chen Xin can’t waste too much energy playing games. The electricity could better serve the greenhouse.

On the other hand, Chen Xin doesn’t want to get too caught up in games, reminiscing about the past all day long.

Such behavior would demoralize him and negatively impact his mindset, which does not contribute to surviving in this apocalypse.

Playing some games in moderation can adjust one’s mood, relieve various pressures brought by the apocalypse, and ease tense nerves—as it did before the disaster.

But being overly indulged in games would have the same adverse effects as addiction before the disaster.

While fiddling with the game controller, Chen Xin suddenly thought about what people in the shelter could entertain themselves with since he had games to play.

Entertainment is necessary at any time; if there’s only work without relaxation, the negative emotions in people’s minds will quickly grow, affecting an individual’s mood and making one’s personality more repressed, even turning thoughts extreme.

If only one person becomes like this, it’s fine, but if many people become like this, various social problems would follow.

It’s like a string: tensioning it too much results in its snapping.

Even the ruffians in "Frostpunk" could enjoy drinking and boxing at minus seventy degrees to relax.

However, after briefly considering this issue, Chen Xin gave up thinking about it further. It’s not that he couldn’t come up with ideas, but because the solution was too simple.

The people of Flame Country have created countless entertainment methods since ancient times. Even before TV and all the dazzling electronic products, people’s entertainment life was diverse and colorful.

Traditional opera may seem outdated, but card games never go out of style; a deck of cards is enough to keep a group of people entertained all day.

And if you add in the traditional mahjong of Flame Country, there’s no need to say more—let’s play a few rounds first.

Of course, these activities could become breeding grounds for gambling, but Chen Xin doesn’t believe the managers of the shelter would allow such a vice to flourish.

And frankly, in a post-apocalyptic shelter, Chen Xin can’t think of anything worth gambling.

Although the government of Flame Country hasn’t collapsed and all departments are functioning normally, it’s clear that money has become useless in the apocalypse.

So, gambling for money? It would be more practical to gamble for a few mushrooms.

Things might still be purchased with money in the shelter. Still, seeing how Qin Lan always trades materials with Chen Xin through barter, never mentioning money, Chen Xin had gained a clear understanding of how much purchasing power pre-disaster currency retained.

Moreover, in the apocalypse, all individual allocations are distributed per capita, barely meeting survival needs. If someone is willing to take something scarce from their own "meager resources" to gamble, Chen Xin could only wish them early blessing and farewell from this world.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 100 - 99: Blizzard
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Beep beep beep... beep beep beep..." The alarm on the phone went off on time, waking Chen Xin, who was still sound asleep.

Chen Xin reached for his phone, glanced at the time, put it back on the bedside table, yawned, then rubbed his face before sitting up in bed.

Grabbing the clothes from the bedside, as soon as he put on a shirt, Chen Xin immediately felt a chill transmitting from the clothes to his body.

Shivering from the chill, Chen Xin hurriedly shrank back into the covers.

Wrapped in the blanket, letting the residual warmth warm the clothes on his body, Chen Xin finally poked his head out of the covers, pondering what was going on today that made it so cold.

This isn’t like before; if it was before the disaster and suddenly got this cold in the morning, it was probably a cold wave, and a temperature drop of over ten degrees overnight in autumn and winter wasn’t uncommon.

Moreover, ordinary houses in the south have limited insulation effectiveness, and if the external temperature drops by over ten degrees, the indoor temperature, without heating, would decrease by three to five degrees or even more, so feeling cold in the morning is quite normal.

But now it’s the apocalypse, and Chen Xin lives in a well-insulated shelter, so it logically shouldn’t be this cold.

Could it be a problem with the ventilation system’s insulation? This thought flashed through Chen Xin’s mind, but he quickly dismissed it.

Even though it feels cold now, that’s only because he’s not wearing much and just climbed out of a warm bed, donning clothes that were originally at a lower temperature. In reality, the temperature hasn’t dropped to an unbearable level; it’s still a few degrees warm.

If the ventilation duct’s insulation failed, causing the cold outside air to pour in directly, the temperature would have dropped below freezing.

If that were the case, what Chen Xin felt wouldn’t be cool but ice-cold.

Moreover, he only used a camel hair quilt last night, which, although offering decent warmth, wasn’t enough to withstand freezing temperatures with just one quilt.

Confronting this strange situation, Chen Xin didn’t overthink it. He quickly put on the clothes beside him piece by piece and crawled out of bed.

Once dressed, although the initially cold clothes made Chen Xin feel slightly numbingly cold, it wasn’t to an unbearable extent.

Especially after he took out the wool sweater and cotton pants from the wardrobe and put them on, the cold wasn’t noticeable anymore.

Previously, due to the shelter’s insulation capability, the temperature inside remained over ten degrees even if the external temperature fell below zero. As long as he dressed warmly and was active, Chen Xin didn’t feel cold.

But today’s sudden temperature drop caught him off guard.

If it were before the disaster, Chen Xin might have checked the weather forecast on his phone before getting out of bed, but now he could only go check the thermometer on the living room wall after brushing his teeth and washing his face.

"Seven degrees? The temperature dropped six degrees overnight, that’s not possible, right?" Chen Xin looked at the temperature, which had dropped to a single digit, feeling a burst of surprise.
