This is inside a shelter; not to mention the reinforced concrete walls and the insulation measures inside, the geothermal warmth from the two hundred meters of soil above shouldn’t cause the shelter’s temperature to drop this much in one night!

Feeling puzzled, Chen Xin skipped breakfast, left the living room, and headed to the public space to check the ventilation system.

Yet, seeing the ventilation system running smoothly, and the noticeable cool breeze blowing out from it, Chen Xin couldn’t help but touch his face; he thought he understood what was happening.

Chen Xin didn’t think much further about it; it’s not a problem with the ventilation system or the shelter’s insulation unless he upgrades the ventilation system again or the entire shelter; otherwise, this issue couldn’t be resolved.

Returning to the bedroom, he dug out an infrared thermometer, checked that it was still usable, then grabbed a large tool hammer from the workbench before heading to the prep room to change into a Protective Suit. After donning a protective mask, he took a flashlight and crawled through the corridor pathway to the small bunker where the communication antenna was installed.

As soon as he opened the door, a cold wind rushed in, instantly covering Chen Xin’s protective mask with a layer of frost.

Looking at the frost on his mask, Chen Xin tried to wipe it but couldn’t, so he left it alone as it didn’t significantly affect visibility.

With the flashlight on, Chen Xin scanned the interior of the small bunker. Unsurprisingly, as he had predicted, a layer of ice had formed near the vent, and the crack connecting the bunker to the outside was covered in thick ice, nearly sealing it shut.

With hammer in hand, Chen Xin approached the crack and delivered a strong blow.

Fortunately, the ice layer wasn’t frozen too thickly. Though solid, a few blows managed to break it.

As Chen Xin shattered the ice sealing the crack, more cold wind rushed in.

Chen Xin raised his infrared thermometer towards the crack where the cold wind was blowing in, revealing a temperature of -34°C.

"Impossible, isn’t it? Wasn’t it -4°C only yesterday? Has it dropped thirty degrees overnight?" Chen Xin found it hard to believe; this temperature drop was terrifyingly similar to the instantaneous freezing scenarios depicted in movies like "The Day After Tomorrow."

However, if it did indeed drop by thirty degrees overnight, it would indeed resemble the effects shown in "The Day After Tomorrow." After all, the Ice Age was only 8-12°C cooler than pre-disaster temperatures, not as dramatically cold as it is now.

Still, Chen Xin couldn’t help but worry about another issue.

Before the disaster, Blue Star’s climate had already shown abnormalities. Scientists claimed it was due to environmental destruction and the solar activity cycle entering a minimum, reducing the heat reaching Blue Star, leading it into a mini Ice Age.

But this was always just a theory, and the mini Ice Age scientists predicted had never arrived, despite their predictions falling within thirty years.

However, Blue Star’s situation now might mean it directly enters an Ice Age, or it might already be in one.

If we enter the Ice Age, the anticipated century-long polar night might extend even longer.

Humans are creatures that need a warm environment to survive. Although we’ve extended our activity range to frigid zones using buildings, clothing, and heating, it doesn’t mean humans can survive long-term in temperatures tens of degrees below zero.

Even in polar research, long-term outdoor activities are challenging; most of the time is spent inside research stations.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 101 - 100 Microalgae-Based Ethanol Production
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The sudden drop in temperature has made the warmth and heating issues in the shelter serious.

Although the external temperature is -34°C, the fact that the temperature inside the shelter is still 7°C is enough to prove the excellent thermal insulation and cold resistance capability of the shelter, but this does not hide the fact that the temperature inside the shelter is already quite low.

It’s important to note that there is also a greenhouse in the shelter, and if the temperature there is too low, it would affect the growth of crops.

While thinking about these issues and clearing ice from the small bunker, Chen Xin has to contemplate how to improve the heating and insulation of the shelter.

Compared to the heating problem, insulation is not as troubling for Chen Xin, after all, his shelter is protected by over 200 meters of soil, and even the entrance has a thick layer of reinforced concrete and insulation, which leaves nothing to be desired in terms of insulation performance, as evidenced by the indoor temperature remaining at seven degrees.

If he wants to improve the insulation, Chen Xin thinks he only needs to upgrade the insulation material of the walls once more.

Regarding the choice of upgrading insulation materials, Chen Xin doesn’t have a specific idea, but if possible, he wants to replace the wall insulation with carbon aerogel.

Under normal temperature and pressure, the thermal conductivity of carbon aerogel can be as low as 0.013w/m·K, which is currently the lowest thermal conductivity of solid materials on Blue Star.

Compared to traditional polyurethane materials, it is more efficient and can still provide excellent insulation even in extremely low temperatures.

However, the heating issue is more severe.

Although the indoor temperature of the shelter is seven degrees, which is manageable for Chen Xin even without a heater, the greenhouse cannot do without heating.

While low temperatures won’t kill the crops, they will lower crop activity, slow growth, and turn crops that originally could be harvested in a month into ones that require forty days to harvest.

Therefore, Chen Xin must find a way to maintain the temperature of the greenhouse at least around 20°C to ensure the good growth of the crops.

This undoubtedly requires a powerful heating system, and both electricity and gasoline consumption would be significant.

After all, the heat value of gasoline is fixed, and the conversion of heat energy to electricity itself involves losses. Furthermore, converting electricity back to heat leads to excessive energy loss, making it entirely unworthy.

Calculating everything, Chen Xin realizes that he is stuck with the energy issue, and without solving this problem, it would be difficult for the shelter to continue operating normally.

After clearing the ice in the small bunker and throwing the ice fragments out through the gaps, Chen Xin carried the tool hammer back to the shelter.

After removing the protective suit, he took a temperature gun to the greenhouse to measure the temperature and then consider the heating issue.

However, upon measuring, it was somewhat unexpected for Chen Xin to find that the greenhouse still had a temperature of 11°C.

Chen Xin was somewhat surprised to examine the greenhouse carefully, looking at the greenhouse walls and the supplementary light fixtures hanging on racks, and immediately understood.

These supplementary lights are used to simulate sunlight exposure, with an intensity equivalent to natural light exposure, so naturally, they also generate heat. Coupled with the greenhouse’s wall insulation, it’s not hard to understand why the temperature is higher there than in other parts of the shelter.

Nonetheless, Chen Xin still needs to consider raising the greenhouse’s temperature.

Although 11°C isn’t low, it would still affect crop growth.

Putting away the temperature gun in the bedroom cabinet, Chen Xin opened his computer and, while eating self-heating food for breakfast, planned to search for information.

While searching for information, Chen Xin also pondered what kind of heating method would be most suitable for him.

Chen Xin’s shelter is located by Dongting Lake, and this city serves as the northern gateway of Xiang Province. Regardless of whether looking south or north, there are numerous hot springs distributed, but unfortunately, this city itself doesn’t have any geothermal resources or hot springs.

If there were geothermal hot springs, Chen Xin felt he could give up further considerations and directly dig a large hot spring bath in the shelter to use geothermal heat for warmth, or even utilize it for electricity generation if geothermal resources were abundant.

However, Chen Xin has given up hope on geothermal hot springs. The city’s lack of geothermal resources is one reason, and during the previous upgrade of the water supply system, drilling down fifty meters only brought up ground water that wasn’t warm and actually a bit cool.

Therefore, geothermal hot springs are out of the question for Chen Xin.

While browsing the information, Chen Xin came across an interesting piece of information.

Although this information wasn’t directly related to heating, it still caught his attention.

This was a technology developed by an algal biofuel company in the Federation’s Florida, utilizing the photosynthesis in cyanobacteria cells to produce ethanol.

The ethanol produced by cyanobacteria can be excreted through their cell walls as a dilute ethanol/water solution, collected from closed photobioreactors, and purified into fuel-grade ethanol.

This was a new technology that was still experimental before the disaster, but its prospects are indeed promising as the technology itself is proven to be feasible.

According to the company’s data, they estimated setting up 430 bioreactors per hectare could produce about 56,000 liters of ethanol per hectare annually, which averages to about 130 liters of ethanol per bioreactor.

A photobioreactor contains about 4500 liters of culture medium, which is roughly 4.5 cubic meters in volume, leading Chen Xin to consider upgrading his algae oxygen generator to this mode, simultaneously producing ethanol while generating oxygen.

It’s important to know that ethanol is quite a decent fuel, and although 130 liters isn’t much, it can be used in various aspects.

Recording this information, Chen Xin continued his search until he found the information he wanted: the shallow geothermal energy utilization project for this city planned in Flame Country’s "Thirteenth Five-Year Plan."

Shallow geothermal energy, as defined, refers to geothermal resources contained within 0-200 meters depth of the earth’s surface, as opposed to deep geothermal resources.

Like geothermal resources, shallow geothermal energy is a type of geothermal energy, but it is defined as low-temperature geothermal energy below 25°C, and before the disaster, it was mainly used for heating.

Although the utilization technology of shallow geothermal energy isn’t particularly new, because the city’s three shallow geothermal energy utilization projects were only planned in recent years, none were fully completed and operational before the disaster.

Seeing the information and related data on geothermal energy, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel tempted. Should he try it? If successful, not only could he solve the current heating issue, but future upgrades might even allow him to tap into deeper geothermal resources.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 102 - 101 Upgrading the Generator
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Shallow geothermal energy is different from deep geothermal energy. Although both belong to geothermal resources, the use of shallow geothermal energy is much simpler than that of deep geothermal energy.

The utilization of deep geothermal energy requires drilling very deep wells, which is difficult to exploit and relatively challenging to use effectively.

However, the utilization of shallow geothermal energy is not so complex. It only requires burying pipes in the shallow ground surface, injecting a circulating working fluid into the pipes, and connecting it to a heat pump unit to continuously absorb geothermal energy and release heat. This effectively utilizes shallow geothermal energy.

Its principle is actually the same as the operating principle of air conditioners and refrigerators, which is to use the evaporation of the circulating working fluid to absorb heat and then compress and condense it to release heat.

The only difference is that the ground source heat pump, using shallow geothermal energy, absorbs heat from the ground layer and releases it indoors, unlike air conditioners and refrigerators.

In fact, this system can be used not only for heating but also for cooling, although cooling is completely unnecessary in the current environment.

Compared to other heating methods, the advantage of ground source heat pump technology is its low energy consumption. Although it also uses electricity, it only requires the electrical energy to operate the compressor, without consuming additional electricity to generate heat.

After all, whether it is a boiler or electric heating, the essence is to consume energy to convert it into heat. Even electric heating with air conditioning uses auxiliary electric heating, consuming a significant amount of electricity.

But ground source heat pump technology is different. Its heat comes from the ground itself, and the ground source heat pump unit is only used to compress, condense, and release the heat of the circulating working fluid, without additional energy consumption.

So, relatively speaking, the ground source heat pump is indeed much more energy-efficient.

Looking at the information and related principle descriptions, Chen Xin thought about installing such a system in his own refuge.

Overall, creating such a system is not complicated. All he needs are pipes, a compressor, a condenser, an evaporator, and an expansion valve, all of which can be obtained by dismantling an air conditioner.

And the installation is not difficult. Since even a primitive structure can be mistaken for an algae-derived oxygen machine, burying pipes in the soil of the growing greenhouse and installing other equipment to dissipate heat into the greenhouse should also be considered a ground source heat pump system, right?

Although this would increase Chen Xin’s electrical consumption, putting more strain on his already overburdened power system, as the necessary consumption for heating, Chen Xin had no choice but to accept this fact and chose to upgrade the generator, increasing fuel consumption while boosting total power generation.

"Simple Fossil Fuel Internal Combustion Generator"

"Upgrade Option 1: Improve generator operational efficiency, fuel utilization efficiency +5%, improve motor material, power generation +5%, energy loss -5%, upgrade requires 1700 survival points"

"Upgrade Option 2: Increase generator output power, fuel consumption +75%, power generation +90%, energy loss -5%, upgrade requires 2000 survival points"

This was already a very high consumption of survival points upgrade, besides upgrading an entire room, it was the first time Chen Xin upgraded a single piece of equipment by more than a thousand points.

Fortunately, besides Chen Xin’s spare generator, the required materials were mainly various metal materials. With Chen Xin providing a large amount of steel as a substitute, he completed the upgrade with only one-tenth of the survival point consumption.

The upgraded generator became larger, and two units turned into one, increasing fuel consumption but also greatly enhancing power generation.

Overall, energy consumption was still reduced because Chen Xin could now generate 1.9 times the electricity with 1.75 times the original fuel. Overall, it reduced consumption.

Moreover, after the upgrade, power generation efficiency improved, and the total electricity consumption did not change, effectively reducing fuel consumption.

Based on Chen Xin’s current fuel consumption, his stockpile and the gasoline he could exchange monthly with the special police force were sufficient to sustain his consumption.

Of course, this did not account for the impending increase in heating consumption.

For heating, Chen Xin was also troubled. Electric heating is the least efficient method but also the simplest and quickest. Chen Xin only needed to plug in his electric heater.

As for burning a boiler, it’s not impossible. Boiler heating with water is an ancient and mature technology, relatively safe and stable. The carbon dioxide produced by burning can supply the oxygen machine for oxygen recycling, making it quite a good choice.

The only constraint for Chen Xin to adopt this heating method is obtaining fuel.

To use this method for heating, Chen Xin might need to add coal to his monthly transactions with the special police force.

Exchanging for coal poses no problem for Chen Xin since coal is not gasoline, and Flame Country’s coal production is quite high.

However, doing so would inevitably push the style of Chen Xin’s refuge towards a steampunk aesthetic.

Although he personally likes steampunk-style art, it doesn’t mean he wants to live in that environment because steampunk also implies coal smoke pollution.

In this post-apocalyptic setting, no one would bother checking if your PM2.5 levels are off the charts because the entire atmosphere’s PM2.5 has already exploded.

So, it’s certainly doable, but how to do it is a big problem.

In his childhood, Chen Xin had seen those briquette stoves in his old home, a technology still widely used in Flame Country’s rural areas today.

If using briquettes for heating, Chen Xin thought this should be an acceptable and effective choice.

Besides, the earlier seen ground source heat pump system utilizing shallow geothermal energy is actually an excellent heating method, and it wouldn’t increase additional energy consumption, which is perfectly suitable for Chen Xin, who currently faces problems with energy acquisition.

Ground source heat pump technology has long been used on Blue Star, first proposed in 1912. At the time, energy procurement was relatively cheap, and technological limitations prevented large-scale application.

But since entering the new century, as the human energy problem has increasingly become acute, clean, efficient, and renewable energy has gradually become the focus of attention. Ground source heat pump technology’s development and widespread application for exploiting shallow geothermal energy naturally gained considerable traction.

In the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau region of Flame Country, this technology has already been applied for local residents’ heating, but the technology is not very mature. Although it is applied, it remains limited to simple heating.

However, the advantage of this technology lies in its relatively high heating efficiency.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 103 - 102 Ground Source Heat Pump Technology
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

According to the law of conservation of energy, generating heat with 1 kW·h of electricity cannot possibly exceed 1 kW·h, but if ground-source heat pump technology is used, it can bring about 4 kW·h of heat or cold with 1 kW·h of electricity.

At first glance, this seems almost magical, like some pseudoscience trickery, but in fact, this is a tangible technology, and devices that achieve this level of efficiency are ubiquitous in our lives, with air conditioners being the most representative.

In thermodynamics, the ratio of energy conversion efficiency is called the coefficient of performance (COP).

Among these, electric heaters, or directly electrically heating devices, have the lowest efficiency, roughly only about 60%, meaning they consume 1 kW·h of electrical energy to produce 0.6 kW·h of heat.

Apart from electric heating, the efficiency of energy boilers varies depending on the fuel, with natural gas and oil boilers having an efficiency of about 0.9, while coal only achieves 0.6.

In contrast, electric boilers have quite a high efficiency, reaching about 0.95, like using electricity directly in an oven, which is not as good as using a kettle to boil water.

Achieving an efficiency of 0.9 is already very high, as the law of conservation of energy limits it to never reaching 1.

However, the efficiency of an air conditioner in cooling mode can exceed 3.

This does not violate the law of conservation of energy because air conditioners use a refrigerant to transfer the indoor heat outside; the heat it moves is not generated by the air conditioner itself but is pre-existing, merely transported by the unit.

In fact, in Flame Country, air conditioners with an efficiency below 2.6~2.8 are not allowed to be sold, and only those above 3.2 can be considered true air conditioners.

Understanding this, it’s not surprising that ground-source heat pumps can achieve an efficiency of 4.

As for why air conditioners and ground-source heat pumps can achieve such high efficiency, it is because they include a series of equipment such as compressors, evaporators, condensers, and expansion valves, which are far more complex than devices like boilers.

But precisely because of this complex set of equipment, a substantial amount of energy is brought in.

Using 1 kW·h of electricity to produce 4 kW·h of heat is clearly more energy output than input.

Seeing this data, a thought involuntarily flashed in Chen Xin’s mind. He took out a piece of paper and a pencil, planning to think it over.

"Using 1 kW·h of electricity to bring about 4 kW·h of heat, even if only half of the heat is used for heating, it’s still more profitable than directly using an electric heater or boiler..." Chen Xin doodled on the paper, not intending to draw anything specific, just using it as a tool to aid his thoughts: "If half of the heat is used for heating and the remaining half is recovered for electricity generation..."

What was drawn on the paper wasn’t important; what mattered was what was swirling in Chen Xin’s mind, and as he pondered, a light bulb went off in his mind!

"Even if the efficiency is low, as long as it can reach a coefficient of performance of 0.6, this system can sustain itself!" Chen Xin slapped the table with excitement.

This is not some kind of mythical perpetual motion machine; it’s just a common industrial process of waste heat recovery for power generation, though Chen Xin’s method of recovering waste heat is abundant enough to make the system self-sustaining.

Achieving this isn’t difficult because once the refrigerant brings the heat from the layers of the earth, it needs to evaporate and release a large amount of heat. During this process, whether Chen Xin plans to use it for boiling water or generating electricity through thermal differences, it can produce enough electricity to keep the system running while still supplying enough heat for heating.

Regarding the need to drill into the ground to implement this system, Chen Xin is living in an underground shelter, with more than 200 meters of earth overhead, equivalent to being underground by 200 meters.

This can be seen from the fact that even when the external temperature is -34°C, the temperature in Chen Xin’s shelter remains at 7°C. If it weren’t for the ventilation system continuously causing some heat loss, the temperature in the shelter could be even higher.

So Chen Xin doesn’t need to go through much trouble utilizing geothermal heat; he just needs to bury the pipes in the ground, even if it’s directly in the soil of his greenhouse.

Although doing so may lower the ground temperature in the planting greenhouse, affecting crop growth.

Chen Xin can completely build the system in the ground layer of the greenhouse and then use system upgrades to re-plan, embedding the pipes deeper into the ground.

The entire shelter of Chen Xin is encased in thick reinforced concrete and insulated layers, and if Chen Xin wants to bury the pipes into the real ground, he needs to penetrate at least 40 centimeters of reinforced concrete.

If it weren’t for the system’s assistance, Chen Xin would simply abandon this idea.

But with the system, all Chen Xin needs to do is upgrade with it.

By now, Chen Xin fully understands the terror of this system; although it only has a simple upgrade function, lacking the variety of gimmicks and exchanges of other systems, just this simple feature, when used right, can unleash tremendous potential.

The system indeed cannot create something from nothing; there must be basic materials as a foundation, but the upgrades it performs are still very powerful.

Chen Xin is a novelist, and novelists are monsters with their heads in black holes. Plus, he enjoys pondering, so he once tried to analyze the principles behind system upgrades.

However, the conclusion was that the system’s upgrades are far from the imagined step-by-step unlocking of high technology.

Instead, it is a more terrifying concept upgrade.

To give the simplest example, if the system was about technological upgrades, if Chen Xin had a wooden stick, he could choose to process it into a spear, club, sword, or bow and arrow, and in subsequent upgrades, add new materials to replace and transform it into something more advanced.

All of this should have a traceable lineage and rules to follow, as technology must progress with prerequisite technologies.

However, even though the system’s upgrade appears similar on the surface, it is far more terrifying because according to the system-embedded information state concept blur function, Chen Xin concluded that the system’s so-called upgrade is enhancing an object conceptually and informationally.

Since this advanced civilization can modify the information contained in an object to make it seem reasonable, it can naturally also modify the information to add more concepts, and Chen Xin’s choice of upgrade plan is precisely the process of infusing these concepts.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 104 - 103: The Disaster of Wind and Snow
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The system’s capabilities were beyond Chen Xin’s understanding. The results he had analyzed were merely the product of a web novelist’s musings.

But this didn’t prevent Chen Xin from using the system and knowing how to utilize it to achieve his desired outcomes.

"But before that, let’s handle the business first!" Chen Xin took a deep breath, closed his laptop, cleaned up the remnants of his breakfast, and went to the radio.

The sudden temperature drop last night had a significant impact on even someone like him with a solid and reliable shelter, so it was hard to imagine what the city shelters had become.

Especially considering some friends in the city group, Chen Xin still owed them six oxygen generators! Until he collected those six hundred survival points, he didn’t want them to kick the bucket.

"Calling, calling, this is the writer, the temperature suddenly dropped last night, is everyone okay?" Although it wasn’t their scheduled communication time, Chen Xin called out as he turned on the radio.

"This is the old farmer, last night the city was hit by a sudden snowstorm, the temperature plummeted by thirty degrees, now it’s only -35°C. I woke up frozen in the night, half the vegetables in the greenhouse have been frosted, the losses are heavy!" Li Wensheng was the first to respond, his voice filled with distress, clearly the sudden drop had caused him great loss.

Through Li Wensheng’s words, Chen Xin understood what had happened last night, and also had a clear understanding of the impact of the sudden snowstorm.

Chen Xin’s situation, with the mountain as a barrier, was relatively better, while Li Wensheng’s shelter was directly built beneath his villa, which made its insulation and thermal performance considerably worse.

From Li Wensheng’s words, Chen Xin could deduce that the temperature in his shelter had at least dropped below freezing, otherwise, the plants in his greenhouse wouldn’t have been frosted.

Thinking about this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel a bit worried for them.

Private shelters, though built underground like the official ones, and wealthier people could afford the same insulation measures as official shelters, this undoubtedly increased the cost significantly.

Take Chen Xin’s shelter for example, he had added a layer of polyurethane insulation within the walls, which took up a large portion of his initial construction cost for the shelter.

But not everyone has enough funds to invest in that, like the "doctor" who, due to budget constraints, used foam plastic instead of polyurethane insulation.

Li Wensheng’s situation was slightly better than the "doctor’s", as he used ultra-fine glass wool, whereas Mo Qingyan’s family used the same polyurethane as Chen Xin.

Foam plastic and ultra-fine glass wool are both materials used for insulation in aircraft cabins and cockpits. While foam plastic has decent performance, it’s inferior to ultra-fine glass wool, let alone polyurethane.

As for the other group members’ shelters, although they all added insulation materials, most of them, like the "doctor’s" house, used foam plastic.

Despite the average performance of this relatively inexpensive material as an insulator, its affordability made it a good choice for those without substantial funds to build private shelters.

But facing this kind of one-night, thirty-degree drop in a snowstorm, how effective foam plastic can be is questionable.

Fortunately, with Li Wensheng speaking up, others also responded upon hearing the call. After all, the snowstorm brought about a thirty-degree drop overnight, and everyone was severely affected, so timely communication with others could potentially lead to help.

Listening to each person reporting their situation, Chen Xin also couldn’t help but feel a bit heavy-hearted.

Besides him and Mo Qingyan, among the ten city group members with shelters, two couldn’t hold out and sent out SOS signals to the official shelter.

Chen Xin knew the two group members who gave up; their shelters were converted from small garages. Though well-equipped and well-prepared with supplies, their shelters evidently lacked sufficient resilience against such intense cold.

Unless absolutely necessary, who would abandon a comfortable private shelter to squeeze into an official one?

Their decision to give up added a somber note to everyone’s mood, and Chen Xin especially felt compelled to help them.

Though Chen Xin had the system and could assist them, these two members who chose to abandon their shelters were already unable to resist the cold, left with only their reserve fuel to light bonfires for warmth as they awaited an official rescue. They couldn’t hold out much longer.

Unless Chen Xin could quickly create heating equipment or provide better insulation solutions, they would have no fate other than freezing to death.

But the snowstorm outside showed no sign of stopping, and in such weather, even if Chen Xin was eager to save people, he lacked the ability to do so. In such weather, there was no way Chen Xin could drive out into the storm.

However, Chen Xin did say on the communication channel, "Everyone, I am able to manufacture a ground-source heat pump device that uses geothermal energy for heating, but to create such equipment requires a compressor, evaporator, condenser, and expansion valve.

Simply put, you can think of it as an air conditioner. All the materials needed to make the device can be found in an air conditioner. As long as you can bring me these parts, I will be able to make the ground-source heat pump device for you."

"Are you kidding, Chen Xin? Can you really make a ground-source heat pump?" Mo Qingyan expressed her doubt towards Chen Xin. Being very familiar with Chen Xin, she had never heard of him having such skills.

Besides, if Chen Xin had this ability, why didn’t he mention that he could make a ground-source heat pump before the disaster?

Back then, collecting materials or installing equipment would have been much easier and more convenient due to the availability of more resources.

"I can indeed make it, but manufacturing such a device requires a lot of materials." Chen Xin naturally understood how suspicious his behavior seemed upon hearing Mo Qingyan’s doubt, but still explained, "I’ve been reading books on mechanical manufacturing lately, accumulated some experience, and making such a device is not a problem, as long as you provide the materials."

Maybe manufacturing the device isn’t a problem, but clearly, creating the device won’t conclude the matter.

Although Mo Qingyan believed that Chen Xin could indeed manufacture the ground-source heat pump as heating equipment, she still questioned, "But Chen Xin, the ground-source heat pump pipes you mentioned have to be buried underground, right? Even if we don’t need to dig hundreds of meters deep, we still need to dig several dozen meters deep. Now with the temperature at minus thirty-something degrees, the ground is frozen solid. How can we make holes in the ground?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 105 - 104
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Faced with the question posed by Mo Qingyan, Chen Xin was just about to say he could use a drill to help them bore holes, but he stopped himself.

He could certainly come up with a suitable drill; he only needed to upgrade the hand drill on his workbench. Not to mention just boring a hole a few dozen meters deep in the permafrost, even if it were for deep drilling, Chen Xin could provide the equipment through a system upgrade.

But is it really good for him to do so?

Chen Xin couldn’t help but shake his head.

These group members indeed faced difficulties, but they were far from the point of being unable to survive.

They still had shelters to live in and stockpiled supplies to sustain life. While the temperature was indeed low, it was a problem raised and discussed before the apocalypse, and everyone had made adequate preparations.

Therefore, although their shelters were indeed very cold now, it wasn’t to the point where they couldn’t survive. Using heating equipment could still maintain a sufficient temperature.

However, doing so would consume a lot of reserved fuel or increase energy consumption, shortening their theoretical survival time, but it wouldn’t truly make them unable to survive.

And even if it really came to that, they could, like the two who called for help from the authorities, send a distress signal and move to an official shelter, which is also a way to survive.

Helping them would only allow them to continue surviving in private shelters rather than help them survive a crisis or save their lives.

Since their lives were not in danger, Chen Xin did not need to rush to help them by using a large amount of survival points.

The system could indeed help him make various items or equipment reasonable, but this was limited to the items themselves. It wouldn’t make people oblivious to the things Chen Xin came up with or the actions he took.

Having others process materials or exchange for something was a normal thing, as everyone had needs or lacked something during the apocalypse.

But proactively helping others... While unity and cooperation in apocalypse survival are crucial, proactively helping others is not the correct posture of extending a helping hand.

If they were at the end of their tether, with no one to help them survive, Chen Xin would help them survive no matter what, but they were far from that point.

-34℃ is indeed very dangerous and deadly, but humans can survive in such an environment.

The former leader from the Sovia Alliance, Father Georgia, was once exiled to a small village near the Arctic Circle, a place not even found on maps, in Kuleika, where he spent five long years in the harsh Siberian winter, surrounded by temperatures of minus sixty degrees. Living in a rudimentary wooden hut, leading a life akin to a wild hunter, relying solely on hunting and fishing.

Yet even in such harsh conditions, the Iron Father survived tenaciously, forging an iron will.

Chen Xin bit his finger in silent contemplation. Helping the group members could earn him survival points, which was the greatest benefit for him. He could also use this opportunity to exchange resources he needed from them, despite the possibility that they didn’t have what he wanted.

But this could reveal some issues too, like where did Chen Xin’s equipment come from, or what were his motives for helping...

The human heart is deeper than we imagine. Chen Xin must consider the consequences of showing excessive enthusiasm and kindness.

He finally made a decision and pressed the radio’s talk button once more, speaking in a deliberately cold voice, "I can get a drill, and I can provide ground source heat pump equipment. I can even help you install it, but nothing is free; I need you to exchange materials with me."

After speaking, Chen Xin quickly released the talk button, exhaling a sigh of relief to calm his nerves, then waited for others to respond.

This was the countermeasure Chen Xin came up with, adopting an attitude of "I can help, but it costs." It allowed him to justifiably help others while avoiding being perceived as foolishly generous, thus preventing unnecessary thoughts.

With a bit of force deterrence, Chen Xin felt this should ensure his safety.

"Xin, can you really get a drill? You wouldn’t be thinking of finding Alan..." Mo Qingyan spoke first, yet her train of thought drifted in another direction, filling in some story on her own.

Chen Xin naturally understood her meaning upon hearing Mo Qingyan’s speculation and imagination, but this was precisely what he hoped to see.

So Chen Xin followed her lead and continued, "Yes, I plan to ask Special Forces through Alan to get a drill. After all, even if I don’t install it for you, I need a set of such equipment for heating myself, so I must get a drill. But using it for myself and using it for you are two different concepts."

Chen Xin’s response left Mo Qingyan silent. He didn’t know what Mo Qingyan intended to express, but others weren’t concerned about the peculiar atmosphere between the two of them and eagerly asked Chen Xin about the effectiveness of the ground source heat pump equipment, along with what installation conditions were required.

And most crucially, what kind of exchange terms would Chen Xin agree to for making and installing a set of such equipment?

Chen Xin gave a detailed explanation about the heating effectiveness of the ground source heat pump, installation conditions, and maintenance and consumption after installation.

However, when everyone asked about the cost, Mo Qingyan suddenly interrupted, halting everyone’s speeches, loudly declaring, "Food supplies, install the first one for me. I’ll give you ten boxes of wine, ten boxes of luncheon meat cans, five boxes of canned pork... and my sister!"

"Mo Qingyan, what are you doing?" Hearing the exchange terms proposed by Mo Qingyan, Chen Xin was utterly shocked, and it took him a while to regain his composure.

Yet Mo Qingyan did not want to explain to him. She continued using a very firm tone in the radio, emphasizing, "I feel cold, so I want to be the first to install heating! Anyone with objections, bring out more than I offer, and I’ll let you install first!"

Mo Qingyan’s attitude left everyone else speechless, forcing them to acquiesce to Chen Xin installing ground source heat pump equipment for her first.

This created a peculiar situation where if others wanted to install a ground source heat pump, the materials they offered in exchange couldn’t be much less than what Mo Qingyan offered, but such a large volume of materials was challenging for them to provide or something they were unwilling to give up.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 106 - 105: Explaining the Reason
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Because of Mo Qingyan’s previous statements, both Chen Xin and other group members found themselves in a predicament. Thus, after waiting three days for the blizzard to subside, Chen Xin hurriedly drove to Mo Qingyan’s place to inquire about her motives.

However, Mo Qingyan’s answer left Chen Xin unsure whether to thank her or complain.

Facing Chen Xin’s inquiry, Mo Qingyan nodded earnestly and seriously, criticizing Chen Xin with a disappointed tone, "You’re too kind with the resources! In these end times, how can you so casually reveal such capabilities? What if someone harbors ill intentions towards you?"

"Not necessarily, not necessarily!" Chen Xin burst into laughter but was very grateful to Mo Qingyan.

Even though Mo Qingyan didn’t know his plans, and her actions affected his strategy to collect survival points, Chen Xin was still thankful that Mo Qingyan was willing to help him this way.

Though Mo Qingyan was a good friend—almost like a brother to him—it didn’t mean she had to help him in such a way. After all, it’s the apocalypse now, and everyone must focus on their survival; no one would help others selflessly without considering the cost, as that would jeopardize their own survival.

Mo Qingyan’s willingness to help, albeit just verbally and not materially sacrificing resources while her sister exchanged the geothermal heat pump with him, was already quite commendable.

Chen Xin thought for a moment and decided to reveal his real plan to Mo Qingyan, so she wouldn’t be kept in the dark: "Actually, Long Niang, there’s really no need for you to do this. The reason I’m doing this is to collect resources from everyone, but your interference actually scared them away."

"Collect resources? How do you plan to collect resources? Aren’t you afraid they’ll come knocking, and when you open the door, they’ll kill you and take over your shelter?" Mo Qingyan’s expression was one of disdain, even slightly angry, feeling that Chen Xin had wasted her good intentions.

Yet, Chen Xin only watched Mo Qingyan’s angry expression, then smiled and took out the pistol Qin Lan had given him from his clothes.

"Is this...a gun? Where did you get it, Ah Xin?" Seeing the pistol Chen Xin took out, Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin were both shocked.

Even though it’s the apocalypse now, Flame Country isn’t like the Federation where elementary students go to school armed. Flame Country has always had the strictest gun control in the world, with only police and the military usually allowed to carry firearms, so Chen Xin suddenly having a gun surprised Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin immensely.

"Lan helped me get this. With it, I have at least some guarantee of safety." Chen Xin explained to Mo Qingyan about how he acquired the gun.

"Lan treats you so well? But even if she’s special police, how could she get a gun?" Mo Qingxin asked in bewilderment, full of questions: "When Ning was staying here, Uncle Ding didn’t give her a gun either!"

Mo Qingxin’s doubts were naturally Mo Qingyan’s as well. She knew Qin Lan treated Chen Xin well, so finding a way to get him a weapon was reasonable, but a gun was beyond Mo Qingyan’s expectations.

Even Flame Country police couldn’t casually get access to firearms; their use and management were strictly regulated. Each gun has a number, is registered, and even every fired bullet requires a report.

Under such strict control, it seemed impossible for Qin Lan to claim she lost her gun to give it to Chen Xin, leaving Mo Qingyan very curious about how Qin Lan managed to get the gun for him.

Naturally, Chen Xin could only fully explain, telling Mo Qingyan, "Every month, I have to trade at least two thousand pounds of vegetables with the special police team. Lan convinced Uncle Ding to give me a gun for my safety and to ensure this supply chain remains uninterrupted."

"So that’s how it is..." The impact brought by a gun was too profound, leaving Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin momentarily dazed.

If someone had said this before the disaster, they would have slapped him awake. If not for gender considerations, they might’ve given him a taste of bitter thug life.

Even in the current apocalypse, where everything has changed, obtaining a gun is still a nerve-wracking affair.

But with a gun in Chen Xin’s hand, he has an offensive edge over others. As long as he doesn’t take unnecessary risks or trust strangers too easily, his safety is assured.

After all, Chen Xin lives in his shelter, protected by a solid reliable door, making it difficult for anyone to harm him.

Understanding this, Mo Qingyan felt slightly embarrassed and awkwardly remarked, "Looks like I disrupted your plans..."

As she spoke, Mo Qingyan’s expression became more awkward, feeling remorseful for her impulsive actions earlier.

Chen Xin didn’t mind, putting the pistol away with a smile, "It’s not necessarily a bad thing; your stir actually raised the geothermal heat pump’s price. If they want to exchange, they’ll have to offer something at least close to your asking price, which they can’t muster immediately."

Hearing Chen Xin mention her previous offer, Mo Qingyan’s face turned red, her expression increasingly awkward. Having Mo Qingxin nearby didn’t help her discomfort.

After all, Mo Qingyan’s previous offer to Chen Xin included involving Mo Qingxin.

In truth, Mo Qingxin felt equally embarrassed; the situation was uncomfortable for both sisters.

Chen Xin noticed their awkwardness and quickly changed the topic, "Long Niang, your offer was too exaggerated. A geothermal heat pump set doesn’t require that much exchange. If you need it, I can accept five boxes of wine, five boxes of lunch meat cans, plus two boxes of canned pork as a fair trade."

This exchange rate was half of Mo Qingyan’s initial offer. Despite Chen Xin’s gratitude for Mo Qingyan’s good intentions, he didn’t say he’d give her a geothermal heat pump set for free.

However, Mo Qingyan shook her head, saying, "There’s no need; I have air conditioning, and the municipal government hasn’t stopped supplying power, so heating is not an issue. Earlier, I said that just to help you."

Mo Qingyan’s words made Chen Xin frown slightly, asking, "Then how long can your supplies last? A year? Two years?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 107 - 106: Exchanging Supplies
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"I have enough food here, so you don’t need to worry. With just Xiao Xin and me, we have enough for another three to five years." Mo Qingyan put on a very confident demeanor, expressing optimism: "In three to five years, the country will certainly have started recovering production. Even if we still have to live underground by then, food won’t be a problem."

Chen Xin saw through Mo Qingyan’s bravado despite her optimism, but friends should not force their goodwill on each other, so Chen Xin didn’t push further. He simply said to Mo Qingyan, "Since you say so, Longniang, I won’t insist. If you need any help, feel free to contact me at any time. Also, your place..."

Chen Xin hesitated for a moment but still said to Mo Qingyan, "Although your reserves should be enough for you, after all, this is the apocalypse, and no one knows what the future holds. I suggest you set up a growing greenhouse to have some degree of self-sustainability. This way, you can keep going for longer and also improve your life."

Mo Qingyan had considered Chen Xin’s suggestion before because even the most stocked supplies would eventually run out, and plans needed to be made for when that happens.

"Your idea of growing food is excellent, but the problem is I really don’t have the conditions to build a greenhouse here! I mentioned to Aran about growing mushrooms before, but when she brought someone to assess it, they said it wasn’t suitable for conversion, so I didn’t pursue it." Mo Qingyan knew she should grow something, but she really didn’t have the conditions to grow mushrooms.

Although Mo Qingyan’s shelter had good insulation and with air conditioning, it could ensure the temperature inside the shelter wouldn’t be too low, there were indeed quite a few conditions lacking for growing mushrooms.

"Can’t grow mushrooms? That’s..." Chen Xin frowned, thought for a moment, and said to Mo Qingyan: "Since mushrooms won’t work, Longniang, you should do hydroponic farming like I do."

At this point, Chen Xin ran through his ideas excitedly, explaining his thoughts to Mo Qingyan: "I can install a geothermal heat pump system for you, and then connect an aquaponic hydroponic system and a waste heat recovery power generation device to it.

This way, you can get energy from the geothermal heat pump, which can both generate electricity and provide heating, maintaining the whole system while continuously supplying you with food."

"Can it really be done?" Mo Qingyan was tempted by Chen Xin’s description.

The system Chen Xin described was indeed wonderful, as it could generate electricity, provide heating, and produce food. With such a setup, Mo Qingyan wouldn’t have to worry about heating and eating in her shelter.

Facing Mo Qingyan’s tempting question, Chen Xin nodded affirmatively: "The geothermal heat pump system can achieve an energy efficiency ratio of 4. By recovering part of the energy for power generation, it’s enough to keep it running and simultaneously there is enough heat output.

As for the aquaponic hydroponic farming, as long as you ensure the temperature, it’s easy to manage. I’m planning to build such a system myself, so rest assured, Longniang."

"If that’s the case..." Mo Qingyan nodded, seeming convinced by Chen Xin: "Let me think about it, as setting this up is quite a project, and you don’t even have a set of equipment yet, do you?"

Regarding Mo Qingyan’s question, Chen Xin just smiled and said, "If needed, the equipment can be ready anytime."

"Alright, let me consider it and I’ll get back to you." Mo Qingyan didn’t give a direct answer.

Chen Xin only nodded and didn’t say anything more.

——————————————————

Back at his shelter, Chen Xin contacted Qin Lan, requesting materials including a drill and a compressor.

"Axin, what do you need these things for?" Qin Lan didn’t immediately agree upon hearing Chen Xin’s request but asked about its purpose: "The special police team doesn’t have these items in stock. We might have to gather them first."

"I plan to use these things to assemble a geothermal heat pump by myself to heat my shelter." Chen Xin was straightforward with Qin Lan about this.

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s plan to assemble a geothermal heat pump himself, Qin Lan was puzzled, and only understood after Chen Xin patiently explained it to her.

"So that’s how it is. I’ll ask the captain then. Besides the drill, a lot of the things you need aren’t easy to come by now." Qin Lan couldn’t guarantee getting them for Chen Xin.

Although the things Chen Xin needed were commonly found in air conditioners and easily available before the apocalypse, they were hard to obtain now.

Although the country was restarting its industry, it was evident that all capacities were prioritized for the most needed areas. The first to resume production were the basic industrial facilities. Items like the compressor or evaporator that Chen Xin needed, even if produced, were for large industrial use.

Whether they could be used was another matter. Anything produced would directly go to the factories resuming production, not be sold individually.

Therefore, even the special police team could only gather these items bit by bit.

After ending the call with Chen Xin, Qin Lan naturally relayed the message to Uncle Ding first.

"Comrade Commissar, what do you think?" Uncle Ding looked at the Commissar as Qin Lan arrived while they were discussing things.

For decisions and evaluations like this, Uncle Ding habitually asked the Commissar’s opinion first.

"I have some knowledge about geothermal heat pump systems. It seems that during the 13th Five-Year Plan before the disaster, three heating projects using this technology were to be implemented in this city, but before completion, the meteorites fell." The Commissar was familiar with the technology and equipment, thought for a while, and replied: "If this comrade Chen Xin can indeed modify such a system, I think we could help, and also secure a few systems for us."

After hearing the Commissar’s opinion, Uncle Ding understood his old comrade’s intention: "Commissar, you’re suggesting we use it too?"

"A blizzard dropped the temperature by thirty degrees, and the future temperatures might be even lower. Although there’s power in the city, and the shelter has boilers, all coal is transported externally. What if rail transport is disrupted? We need a backup plan." The Commissar looked at Uncle Ding, eyes burning with intensity.

After considering the Commissar’s suggestion, Uncle Ding nodded and said to Qin Lan: "If that’s the case, Xiao Qin, inform Old Li to gather some materials."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 108 - 107: Gathering Materials
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In an abandoned district, Li Tou led his squad through a residential building, gathering supplies.

"Li Tou, there’s still one air conditioner unit that hasn’t been burned out!" a member of the SWAT team spotted a still intact outdoor unit and quickly reported it to Li Tou.

"Where is it? Take it down and bring it along!" Hearing his subordinate’s report, Li Tou quickly looked over, not forgetting to issue a command.

The previous blizzard had caused quite significant damage, aside from the snow accumulation, the strong winds damaged many things, and even caused some buildings to collapse, like a typhoon passing through.

Without the surface vegetation to slow down the wind, the hazards caused by strong winds escalated dramatically.

Some structures in the downtown area, previously damaged by fires, had collapsed under the blizzard, and those still standing were severely damaged.

Items like air conditioning units and windows were mostly blown away, and what remained in many houses was literally swept clean, creating significant challenges for Li Tou and his team as they collected materials.

Fortunately, this time they came to a district with relatively decent construction quality, and most buildings weren’t too heavily damaged, only the glass had been blown away.

Nevertheless, the search work was still filled with difficulties.

Most air conditioner units were already damaged, and the units were installed on exterior walls, so even if they were intact, dismantling them was a troublesome task.

With the current freezing weather, the ground snow could bury a person, and after a whole morning, Li Tou and his team had only found a few intact air conditioning units.

Although the blizzard had stopped, the temperature hadn’t significantly risen, and with temperatures of minus twenty degrees, even breathing caused their masks to freeze.

In such an environment, these SWAT team members still had to climb up the buildings’ exterior to dismantle the air conditioners, which was indeed not an easy task, making the inefficiency somewhat unavoidable.

"Li Tou, our search is too inefficient this way, how about we take down the ones not too severely damaged as well? There might be some useful parts inside." One SWAT team member suggested beside Li Tou.

Faced with the suggestion, Li Tou merely furrowed his brow, thought for a moment, then asked, "How many units have we dismantled?"

"A total of eight, three in perfect condition, four somewhat damaged but still usable, and one without a casing but everything inside is there," replied another SWAT team member who had been keeping count of their gains.

Having received the answer, Li Tou couldn’t help but grit his teeth in frustration.

Although eight units weren’t too few, the commissar had specifically instructed him before they came, to find more, and eight units hardly met the mission requirements.

After considering for a moment, Li Tou hesitated but still told the SWAT member beside him, "In the afternoon, have the men take down all the ones that aren’t too severely damaged; these won’t be enough to report back."

"Yes, Li Tou!" The SWAT team member responded, relaying Li Tou’s orders through the radio.

Watching his subordinates busy working, Li Tou also turned on the radio and said, "After finishing this unit, everyone can eat first, and after eating, let’s try to find more in the afternoon."

Hearing they could eat, the team members couldn’t help but perk up a bit and quickened their actions, clearly eager to start their meal sooner.

Perhaps motivated by lunch, everyone’s actions became somewhat faster, and the relatively intact units they found were quickly dismantled. The SWAT team members returned to the police cars, preparing to eat.

"Li Tou, why are we collecting these old air conditioners? We’re not planning to fix them for heating the shelter, are we?" A SWAT team member, holding a self-heating meal in his hand while also warming his hands, asked Li Tou about the purpose of collecting air conditioner units.

"If you ask me, a boiler is more reliable. If the electricity goes out, wouldn’t everyone freeze?" said another SWAT team member, placing the self-heating meal, now steaming, on his lap to warm his knees, adding, "But this weather is really strange. Our city hadn’t seen snow in winter for over a decade, it’s just these two years there’s been a bit more snow."

"And you thought it was before? The sun is gone; what’s wrong with a bit of snow?" Li Tou, also holding a bag of self-heating food, waiting for it to be ready, commented after hearing their discussion.

"Hey, I wonder when this life will have an end," mumbled the SWAT team member, now feeling a bit dejected as he fiddled with his self-heating food.

It had been nearly half a year since the meteor impact, yet over the past months, life continued to deteriorate, living such hopeless and futureless lives; who wouldn’t feel miserable?

Hearing the SWAT team member’s depressed words, Qin Lan, holding her meal, couldn’t help but recall what Chen Xin had once told her before the catastrophe.

"The meteor impact is just the beginning. The dust will shroud the entire sky, and the century-long darkness will not only freeze the land but also cause ecological extinction. Maybe in one to three years after the impact, some dust in the sky will settle, allowing some faint light through, but it’d take at least a hundred years to return to the pre-disaster state."

Unbeknownst to her, Qin Lan had recited those words of Chen Xin aloud.

"A hundred years? Does that mean we won’t see the sun in our lifetime?" Qin Lan’s words instantly silenced the SWAT members inside the vehicle as they ate their meals.

For Earth, a hundred years is but a brief moment; this apocalyptic disaster, in its extensive 4.6 billion-year history, has likely happened countless times, so trivial it’s not worth recording.

Only ancient paleontologists, studying prehistoric fossils, could trace back such insignificant traces.

But for humans, a hundred years is an immensely long time!

The adage "seventy years is rare" means living through a century, even in peaceful times, is challenging. How about now, when everyone struggles to survive in this apocalyptic world?

"Why are you all looking so downcast? Now, you have food, drinks, and a place to live; what’s there to be dissatisfied with?" Li Tou shouted at his team members, seeing their low morale.

"Li Tou, there’s no hope in sight for this life!" One SWAT member responded with a bitter smile, looking up at him, "Are we really going to live underground for the rest of our lives? We don’t mind, but surely the next generation shouldn’t live in such darkness as well?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 109 - 108: Switching Wine
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Listening to the slightly questioning tone of his subordinates, Leader Li frowned immediately and said to them, "Do you not know what the current environment is like? Are you hoping that seeing the sun could send you to the sky, or make the black clouds fall from it? Instead of wasting time on pointless thoughts, why not think about how to gather more resources in the afternoon to complete the task."

Leader Li certainly understood why these team members were so dejected. In this kind of environment, if they were just living in a shelter, everyone could still endure the harsh conditions.

But now, with the arrival of the blizzard, the temperature has further dropped, and even the official shelters have become harsh environments for survival.

The low temperatures bring more than just cold—the accompanying problems are manifold, some of which the shelter managers have to face, while others each survivor has to bear.

Originally, everyone was already overburdened after the end times, and now with additional pressure, some people with weaker psychological endurance are starting to crumble.

In the past six months or so, although the shelters have been trying various ways to relieve stress and reduce the psychological burden, there are still people who can’t bear the pressure and choose to end their own lives. Some have even developed antisocial tendencies, causing violent incidents in the shelters.

If it weren’t for the timely handling by the grassroots police and SWAT teams in the shelters, such incidents could have escalated into group events.

Now, it’s considered normal for the members of the SWAT team to be only feeling depressed and low-spirited.

Although Leader Li understood this, he didn’t have any good way to boost morale, and he still had to keep these people motivated to complete the afternoon’s work.

Being reprimanded by Leader Li, everyone could only eat their meal in even more silence, and the atmosphere in the vehicle became even lower.

Seeing this situation, Qin Lan hesitated for a moment, then came to Leader Li’s side and whispered, "Leader Li, this isn’t a solution! If everyone stays this down, there will be problems."

Leader Li knew very well that problems would arise, but if he had any solutions, he would have used them long ago. Why else would the whole team be in such low spirits?

Seeing Leader Li remain silent, Qin Lan thought for a moment and said, "How about we gather some things to trade for alcohol?"

"Alcohol? Xiaoqin, do you know where to get alcohol? Just smelling it would be nice!"

"Don’t even mention alcohol! It’s been so long since I’ve had a drink that I’ve almost forgotten what it is. Now that you mention it, my alcohol craving is already at my throat!"

"Lan Jie, seriously? I haven’t even smelled alcohol in half a year! If I could have some, I’d be willing to donate my rations and chew mushrooms for half a month!"

As soon as Qin Lan mentioned the word alcohol, everyone became excited, bombarding Qin Lan with questions on how to get alcohol. A few people even started gathering their own belongings to trade for it.

Leader Li also paid attention to Qin Lan’s suggestion to trade for alcohol. If they could get hold of a couple of bottles, it would be very beneficial for boosting morale.

You could tell just by the way their ’alcohol cravings’ were raging.

In the current situation, food is already extremely valuable, and brewing alcohol is clearly no longer possible.

Although all the shelters have organized scavenging teams to go out and collect resources from the city’s ruins, alcohol remains hard to find.

Finding an undamaged bottle in the ruins is as rare as getting two six-star operators in a ten-shot draw.

Even with a black market, trying to buy alcohol there isn’t just about the sky-high prices deterring SWAT members; the alcohol itself is a rare commodity with prices above what the market can offer.

"Xiaoqin, can you really get alcohol? How much do we need to trade for it? You’re not going to the black market, are you?" Leader Li seriously looked at Qin Lan, calculating within himself how much he could spare to trade for alcohol.

To be honest, Leader Li himself had an alcohol craving and used to enjoy a couple of drinks when he had nothing else to do, but now...

Not only has he not smelled the scent of alcohol for half a year, but it was also exposed in one shelter that someone had stolen medical alcohol from the infirmary to dilute and sell as fake alcohol.

"I know a friend who owned a bar before the disaster; she has a lot of alcohol in her house. We can trade with her." Qin Lan knew Mo Qingyan had alcohol and many good brands but never thought of buying from Mo Qingyan.

The reasons were simple: first, it was too expensive, and she didn’t want to buy it; secondly, though Qin Lan drank, she didn’t have a craving and didn’t need it.

Hearing Qin Lan say she knew someone with alcohol at home lit up everyone’s eyes, and given the dim lighting in the vehicle, Qin Lan even thought she saw a pack of wolves with green eyes.

"Xiaoqin, ask your friend what it would take to trade for two bottles. If the price is right, we could gather up some stuff, and you could help us get it. Does that work for you?" Leader Li looked at his subordinates, who were almost morphing into werewolves, helplessly shook his head, swallowed his saliva, and half-consultatively asked Qin Lan.

Qin Lan didn’t refuse Leader Li’s request. She nodded and said, "I’ll ask my friend when we get back. It shouldn’t be too expensive. A-Xin previously traded two hundred pounds of vegetables for a box of wine from her."

Hearing this, everyone in the vehicle felt much more at ease. Trading two hundred pounds of vegetables for a box of wine—even if it’s six bottles in a box—isn’t too expensive. They could gather enough materials to trade for two bottles among themselves.

With everyone agreeing to gather some things to trade for alcohol, the atmosphere in the vehicle warmed up quite a bit, and people even started smiling, no longer wearing their earlier worried expressions.

Seeing everyone’s spirits seemingly lifted, Qin Lan felt slightly happy herself. These were comrades she entrusted with her life and death. She didn’t want to see them looking dejected and dispirited.

Thanks to this plan and a hearty lunch, everyone was filled with enthusiasm for their work, and the speed of resource gathering in the afternoon significantly increased.

Moreover, these SWAT team members learned from Leader Li why they were collecting these items. Upon discovering they were to be used to make heating equipment, they paid much more attention to the remains of burnt-out appliances, giving them more than just a cursory glance.

By the time they called it a day, they had found thirteen complete air conditioning units and about twenty more severely damaged ones from which useful parts could be salvaged.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 110 - 109 Li Wensheng’s actions
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

While the special police unit was helping Chen Xin gather raw materials to manufacture ground-source heat pump equipment, the members of the survival group didn’t remain idle either.

Although Mo Qingyan’s previous offer had dissuaded many people, a set that can continuously provide heating in such an environment, without high consumption, was still something everyone was tempted by.

Their hesitation and doubt were merely because they worried they couldn’t provide enough resources for a trade with Chen Xin, or find the materials needed for the equipment.

However, since these group members could build their own private shelters instead of cramming into official ones, they naturally had some means.

Moreover, they had a keen eye for opportunities and enjoyed outdoor survival, so most of them had amassed a considerable amount of resources.

If they really grit their teeth, they could meet the price Mo Qingyan previously laid out, but whether it was worth it required some consideration.

The real limitation stopping them from trading with Chen Xin was the issue of materials for manufacturing the equipment.

If even the special police unit needed to specially collect resources to gather materials, it goes to show the situation for these private shelters.

Among these people, Li Wensheng was the most advantaged, as his shelter was built beneath his original villa, and the surrounding area was a villa district, so finding air conditioning units was much more convenient for him.

In fact, the villa Li Wensheng lived in had a central air conditioning system installed, and the materials needed by Chen Xin for the ground-source heat pump equipment could be directly stripped from it.

Although Li Wensheng thought this way, unfortunately, not only in his home but most of the air conditioning units in the villa area were badly damaged. After searching the entire community, he only found two relatively intact air conditioning units.

After loading these two air conditioning units into his car, Li Wensheng stretched his freezing cold limbs and said to his son, "Alright Xiaohai, go to the storage room and bring another case of compressed biscuits."

"Okay, Dad." Li Wensheng’s son Xiaohai nodded, turned around, went back to the basement of their villa, and brought another case of compressed biscuits.

Taking the box from his son’s hand, he placed it in the trunk of his car. Looking at the loaded goods, Li Wensheng said to his son, "Xiaohai, you head back inside, it’s cold out."

"Be careful on the road, Dad!" Xiaohai handed a flashlight to Li Wensheng, watched him get into the car, and only then went into the basement of their villa, securely closing the door.

Seeing his son close the door well, Li Wensheng finally felt assured, started the car, and drove towards Chen Xin’s residence outside the city.

"Luckily Xiaohai can help now, otherwise..." As he drove, Li Wensheng mused to himself.

Li Wensheng was a farmer in his youth, but he was quick-witted and didn’t persist around his small plot of land. He went out to work along the coastal cities, broadened his horizons, and after making some money, returned to lease land in his hometown to start greenhouse farming, becoming one of the first well-off families in his village.

Li Wensheng was not the type to settle. When hydroponic technology arose, he got into it, but this time he wasn’t farming himself; he began selling the technology.

It must be said that those who dared to venture in those years had distinctive insights and boldness, and with a bit of skill, success wasn’t hard to come by.

Through running hydroponics, Li Wensheng quickly earned a substantial amount, moved into a villa, drove a luxury car, and his life appeared affluent at first glance.

The usual saying that men grow bad when rich didn’t seem to apply to him. His wife was his childhood sweetheart, and they married before he ventured out. Over the years, they had their share of hardships, but their partnership endured the test.

Xiaohai was his eldest son, and he had a younger sister who was under nine. The children were quite young, with Xiaohai only sixteen and just in high school.

Due to his early experiences of venturing out, Li Wensheng was restless. After acquiring wealth, he became fond of outdoor sports, naturally meeting people like Chen Xin and Mo Qingyan.

Maybe it was the crisis awareness from his early ventures, but when he first heard in the group about a potential meteorite impact disaster, he went against his wife’s objections and emptied their savings to build a shelter beneath their villa, stocking it with supplies, fully prepared.

Initially, during the partial success of the interception of the meteorite, his wife complained about him spending all their money like that. But when the meteorite really fell, both Li Wensheng and his wife were grateful they had built that shelter.

Thanks to his foresight, Li Wensheng’s shelter, in terms of preparation, was more thorough than Chen Xin’s, with heating equipment naturally equipped.

If it weren’t for all three of his wind turbines breaking down, Li Wensheng wouldn’t worry about heating at all.

But now, without solving the heating issue, his reserved diesel was really not much, only enough for three more months at most, and that didn’t even account for the heating consumption of his shelter.

Thus, when Chen Xin mentioned being able to manufacture the ground-source heat pump equipment, Li Wensheng felt a compelling urgency.

The sudden drop in temperature brought various impacts, one being the snow accumulation after the blizzards. Although Li Wensheng wound tire chains on his car, in waist-deep snow, even chained tires struggled to move.

Fortunately, the weather was so cold and the blizzards had poured for several days, causing the snow to freeze solid, eliminating much of the risk of falling into snow pits.

Moreover, the snow levelled the originally bumpy roads, making them considerably easier to traverse.

Despite everything, after quite some struggle, Li Wensheng drove to the entrance of Chen Xin’s shelter.

The snow cover buried nearly half of the once-prominent shelter door, and if Li Wensheng hadn’t been here once and had a good sense of direction, finding the entrance in the pitch-black snow field would have been challenging.

Through the shortwave radio, Li Wensheng contacted Chen Xin.

The door of the shelter opened a crack amidst Li Wensheng’s anticipation.

The snow provided camouflage for the shelter door but also added to the difficulty in opening it.

Luckily, it wasn’t necessary to open it entirely; just enough of a gap for someone to step out was sufficient.

"Writer, I’ve brought what you asked for. Can you help me make a set of ground-source heat pump equipment?" Li Wensheng said to the figure coming out of the shelter, "The exchange goods, I don’t have as much as the boss lady, but I’ve brought a case of compressed biscuits as a deposit, and another case as the final payment. Additionally, I can give you ten fish. Is that enough?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 111 - 110: Reaching a Deal
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After stating his offer, Li Wensheng felt somewhat nervous as he looked at Chen Xin, who remained silent. What he could offer certainly couldn’t compare to the prices Mo Qingyan had quoted earlier.

Nowadays, a fixed black market has formed in the city, a place for private exchanges among several shelters within the city.

The authorities are aware of the black market’s existence, yet they haven’t cracked down on it. Instead, they’ve tacitly allowed its presence.

The most valuable goods on the black market are various non-renewable resources produced before the disaster; alcohol can be considered the most popular commodity.

Even a regular bottle of liquor can be exchanged on the black market for enough rations to feed a person for a week, not to mention more valuable spirits, some of which can exchange for a month’s worth of food and still have extra.

The box of compressed biscuits Li Wensheng brought is also a hot commodity on the black market, but it pales in comparison to Mo Qingyan’s offer of ten boxes of alcohol and ten boxes of canned meat, not even reaching one percent.

Not to mention that Mo Qingyan’s offer also included her sister.

However, Li Wensheng’s offer wasn’t cheap either. The valuable part was those ten fish.

If alcohol was scarce, then meat was truly rare. Fresh meat couldn’t even be found on the black market; there were only pre-disaster preserved canned meats.

Most of these were pork or lunch meat, which could hardly be called meat. Fresh fish, needless to say, was completely extinct, except maybe in the underground cultivation areas managed by the city government, or with people like Li Wensheng engaged in aquaponics.

In response to Li Wensheng’s offer, Chen Xin merely furrowed his brows and asked, "How many fish did you exchange for the oxygen machine with me last time, Old Farmer?"

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s words, Li Wensheng’s face turned bright red, akin to constipation. If he weren’t wearing a protective mask and if the dim light around didn’t obscure his expression, he might have felt ashamed enough to bury his head in the snow.

"Then I’ll add a few more fish..." Li Wensheng reluctantly increased his offer, which felt like sacrificing a crucial part of himself.

Although he had a fair number of fish, he had shut down half of his aquaponics systems to save electricity, so he didn’t have many left. Offering a dozen fish in exchange with Chen Xin was already pushing his limits.

Fish aren’t like vegetables in the ground, where seeds can be harvested in a month and even perpetually. Rearing a fish from egg to mature fish takes at least a year, and it’s not uncommon for them to be two or three years old.

Seeing Li Wensheng’s predicament, Chen Xin shook his head and asked him, "Old Farmer, you should have surplus aquaponics equipment, right?"

Hearing Chen Xin’s question, Li Wensheng couldn’t quite grasp his intentions, but he nodded and said, "At home, I only have spare parts, not extras. However, my previously stockpiled warehouse wasn’t badly burned. When I checked, I could probably gather three to five sets of equipment."

Chen Xin nodded in satisfaction when he heard this, "In that case, bring two sets of equipment along with six male and six female live fish for breeding, and I’ll give you a geothermal heat pump system and help you install it."

"This..." Hearing Chen Xin’s offer, Li Wensheng hesitated for a moment.

It wasn’t that he couldn’t afford two sets of aquaponics equipment or thought the price wasn’t worth it. Rather, the fact that Chen Xin wanted aquaponics equipment seemed to be for his own use.

This meant that Chen Xin would also have the capability of producing fish resources, which naturally meant that in the future, Li Wensheng wouldn’t be able to exchange fresh fish, a renewable resource for him, with Chen Xin or sell it at a high price.

Despite this, Li Wensheng only hesitated briefly before gritting his teeth and agreeing, "Alright, two sets of aquaponics equipment and twelve breeding fish. The box of compressed biscuits I brought will be the deposit."

Seeing Li Wensheng so cooperative, Chen Xin had a newfound respect for him and nodded, saying, "Now that we’ve settled on the price, where’s the material? Once the equipment is ready, I’ll come and install it for you."

"The stuff is in the car." Having settled the price, Li Wensheng didn’t hide anything and led Chen Xin to the back of the car, pointing at the items, "Two outdoor units of central air conditioners. The parts you need are all there. I’ve selected units that are relatively intact, so everything should be usable."

Chen Xin nodded. Damage wasn’t a problem for him. He could always repair them on his workbench if needed.

After clearing some of the accumulated snow off the doorway with Li Wensheng’s help, Chen Xin opened the door and moved the two old air conditioner outdoor units from Li Wensheng’s vehicle inside.

This also served as a reminder for Chen Xin, indicating that he would have to clear ice not only at the hideaway where the shortwave antenna was placed but also at the entrance of the shelter.

Unless Chen Xin later modified the door at the entrance to be electric and hydraulic with automatic de-icing and defrosting features, ice and snow removal would be unavoidable.

After all, although Chen Xin went out infrequently, he still needed to leave the shelter occasionally.
