Watching Chen Xin move the items into the shelter and handing over the box of compressed biscuits as a deposit, Li Wensheng bid his farewell and left.

Although he felt uneasy about whether Chen Xin would provide him with the equipment after taking his goods, knowing the location of Chen Xin’s shelter reassured him, as he could always come to claim his debt if needed.

After seeing Li Wensheng off, Chen Xin shut the shelter door once more.

Although the blizzard had stopped, snowflakes continued to drift from the sky, a lingering effect of the meteor impact.

Of the impact sites, three struck the Pacific Ocean and one the Federation. The massive amounts of seawater thrown into the sky, combined with evaporation from the widespread fires, filled the entire atmosphere with moisture.

For the past few months, since Chen Xin had been staying indoors and his shelter was built within a mountain, there was no risk of flooding at the entrance, so he hadn’t noticed.

However, outside, it had been raining continuously for two to three months, and this recent blizzard was caused by an abrupt temperature drop due to excessive local rainfall.

The meteor impact not only blocked out the sky but also led to the collapse of the weather system after the loss of sunlight. Without the self-regulation of the weather system, extreme weather events only multiplied.

Nevertheless, living underground, Chen Xin remained largely unaffected, as no weather condition could impact two hundred meters of mountain above him.

Yet, as Chen Xin pushed the two air conditioner outdoor units further into the shelter, the ground beneath him suddenly shook, causing him to lose balance and stabilize himself by leaning against the wall.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 112 - 111: Upgrades After the Earthquake
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

It’s an earthquake! Almost without thinking, Chen Xin had already figured out what happened.

However, the intensity of the earthquake was not high, and it didn’t last long, stopping after a brief shake.

Even so, Chen Xin didn’t dare to be complacent. He quickly pushed the two air conditioning outdoor units back inside the shelter and began checking the facilities inside for any damage.

Although the shelter was built to the highest standards and theoretically shouldn’t collapse even in a magnitude six earthquake, Chen Xin couldn’t discern the magnitude of the earthquake that just occurred.

Moreover, it’s better to be safe than sorry. If something went wrong and he didn’t notice, it could indeed be a life-threatening situation.

Fortunately, after a round of inspections, the quality of the shelter’s construction was indeed up to standard, with no cracks or other damages. Even when Chen Xin activated the system view, he didn’t find any areas needing repair.

"Woohoo, looks like the construction team wasn’t exaggerating; it really can withstand a magnitude six earthquake." As Chen Xin tidied up items that had been displaced or fallen, he also praised the construction team that helped build his shelter.

It didn’t take Chen Xin long to tidy up. After reorganizing everything, he had neither the mood nor the energy to fuss over the two air conditioning outdoor units he had just brought back.

However, when he stacked the two air conditioning outdoor units in the original garage space, Chen Xin glanced at the workshop he had converted from the garage and suddenly found it a bit small.

This was initially a space designed by Chen Xin for parking. Although he had now moved the car out, the space left was about one and a half car spaces.

While it’s more than enough for a workbench, adding more items would make it a bit cramped, especially since Chen Xin had just dragged two air conditioning outdoor units back, making the place feel crowded.

Looking at the cramped space, Chen Xin didn’t have the intention to upgrade the workshop but instead planned to upgrade the storage room.

Even if the workshop is upgraded, it’s towards improving manufacturing and processing capabilities. The space and area will increase, but not by much.

But what Chen Xin needed now was precisely a place to store things, from this perspective, upgrading the storage room was much more appropriate.

Especially since these two air conditioning outdoor units were only appetizers; he also requested supplies from the SWAT team.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin naturally went to the entrance of his storage room.

Chen Xin’s storage room was actually not large, with only ten square meters of space left during the design.

Against the walls of the room were six simple iron shelves filled with various foods Chen Xin initially stored, as well as supplies exchanged from the SWAT team and Mo Qingyan over the past few months.

Aside from these, the most space-consuming item was the freezer he got from Mo Qingyan’s house, holding the vegetables harvested this month and uneaten fish.

This place initially existed only as a food storage room, naturally not requiring a large space, just enough to hold all the food.

But after Chen Xin got the system, the storage room’s function was no longer limited to storing food. A considerable portion of the supplies he exchanged through Qin Lan from the SWAT team were also piled on the floor of the storage room, leaving him barely any room to step in.

This was with the cement piled outside in the public space’s corner.

"Looks like I need to do some categorization, one room for food, one for materials." Looking at the packed storage room, Chen Xin scratched his head.

Although he wasn’t the type who can’t bear to throw things away and loves to hoard like a hamster, he still felt a sense of pride and security seeing the packed storage room.

However, storing materials like this was not the best way, so Chen Xin planned to separate food and other materials and categorize the supplies.

But the prerequisite for this was expanding the storage room to at least separate it into two spaces.

"Cramped Storage Room"

"Upgrade Plan One: Expand storage space, add wall-mounted storage racks, extend food shelf life, requires 800 survival points for upgrade"

"Upgrade Plan Two: Expand storage space, separate material storage rooms, reduce aging probability for stored items, requires 1000 survival points for upgrade"

The upgrade cost wasn’t high, and as Chen Xin looked, both upgrade plans could expand the current storage room’s area by 50%. However, Plan One remains a single storage room, with added shelves on the walls for placing items, effectively increasing space utilization.

As for the second plan, it separates the original storage room into two rooms, one large and one small, with the smaller one inside for food storage and the larger one outside, for material storage.

Seeing that both plans were good and the material consumption wasn’t much, Chen Xin didn’t hesitate and simultaneously chose to upgrade both plans.

As supplies were consumed and green light flashed, the storage room in front of him didn’t look much different, but there was an additional door on the innermost wall, and a few rows of wall-mounted racks made with long boards appeared. The iron shelves previously against the wall had been moved to the middle of the room, neatly arranged, with two additional new shelves.

But this wasn’t the biggest change in the storage room. The food and freezer previously here had disappeared, replaced by the cement and other materials originally placed in the public space outside, all neatly stacked, while the reinforcement materials previously piled on the floor were now categorized and placed on the shelves.

"Does the upgrade include organizing the warehouse? That’s really saved me a lot of trouble." Seeing the organized supplies, Chen Xin nodded in satisfaction.

This indeed saved him a lot of work. After all, if he were to organize the warehouse alone, it would definitely be a huge project, not to mention the difficulty of just moving the dozen bags of cement and that heap of reinforcement materials.

Walking through the shelves to the innermost part of the storage room, he opened the newly added door, revealing a newly expanded space to Chen Xin.

The area was only about half the size of the outside material storage room, where Chen Xin had moved the freezer and food previously placed outside.

Feeling the temperature briefly, the food storage room was slightly cooler than the outside material storage room, but the lower temperature was more favorable for food storage, which Chen Xin was quite satisfied with.

After checking once to see all the food categorized and placed on shelves, he contentedly closed the door and exited the storage room.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 113 - 112 Making of the Ground Source Heat Pump
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The additional storage space from the storeroom upgrade wasn’t much larger, only expanding by five square meters for the food storage room.

However, this was much more spacious compared to the original ten-square-meter space that had to accommodate both food and supplies. At least Chen Xin no longer had to deal with stepping on scattered supplies everywhere.

Especially with the newly added shelves on the walls, after placing the steel bars and some small parts, the floor of the storage room became much cleaner. Apart from cement, there was nothing left that needed to be placed on the floor.

Thus, a significant amount of space was freed up.

At least Chen Xin could drag the two air conditioning units piled beside the workbench into the supply storeroom rather than leaving them there, getting in the way.

After dragging the two air conditioning units into the storage room, Chen Xin returned to the workbench and opened the system interface again.

The workbench produced by the system not only can manufacture various equipment and process various parts with the help of the system, but it can also be used to disassemble various things.

Of course, this is a feature that the workbench inherently possesses; it’s just that with the power of the system, Chen Xin can make disassembly work easier and recycle resources more efficiently.

For example, as he sat in front of the workbench, the system interface showed the two air conditioning units that had been stuffed into the storage room, marked as ready for disassembly and resource recycling.

Evidently, under the might of the system, Chen Xin could sit in front of the workbench and directly disassemble items in the storeroom.

Seeing the air conditioning units displayed as ready for disassembly, Chen Xin hesitated slightly before clicking disassemble and broke one of the units into parts.

The parts did not appear on the workbench but remained in the storage room.

However, Chen Xin, having clicked to disassemble, was still curious and returned to the storeroom to see what was left of the air conditioning unit after the system’s disassembly.

At this moment, in the storeroom, the air conditioning unit that Chen Xin had originally stacked against the wall was already missing one unit, and the previously empty space on the nearby shelf now contained many disassembled parts.

Chen Xin walked to the shelf and picked up the disassembled parts, recognizing that it was one of the most critical components of the air conditioning unit—the compressor.

This was also a critical component needed for Chen Xin to manufacture a geothermal heat pump unit. Even though the system could assist Chen Xin in creating a geothermal heat pump, he couldn’t make these parts due to the inadequate level of the workbench.

Faced with the situation similar to a game where certain parts can’t be created and can only be collected in the wild or obtained through tasks, Chen Xin was not surprised.

With existing parts, crafting a usable geothermal heat pump unit through pipeline modification and parts assembled wasn’t too difficult. In fact, as long as the parts were intact, Chen Xin could assemble a geothermal heat pump unit without relying on the system.

However, trying to create a compressor with the current tools on the workbench was as fantastical as a fairy tale.

Although the compressor used in air conditioners seemed like merely a large and a small long canister connected, its internal structure was quite complex.

Before becoming a full-time writer and starting to write online novels, Chen Xin had worked in other fields and had some exposure to mechanical manufacturing due to work-related matters.

He knew clearly that manufacturing a compressor couldn’t be completed by a manual workshop. Even dismantling a compressor into parts and giving them to him, he might not be able to reassemble them entirely back to their original form.

Or rather, even if he could reassemble it, Chen Xin couldn’t be sure if the compressor would still work.

Because when a compressor is working, its internals is in a high-pressure state. Under such circumstances, even the slightest leak or improper assembly could turn a compressor into a bomb, causing serious consequences.

So manufacturing and processing such a device require true industrial-grade processing and manufacturing capabilities, not a garage workshop.

About such a situation, Chen Xin wasn’t actually bothered. His demand for such equipment parts wasn’t high. The supplies he could obtain from the special police team were already sufficient to meet his needs.

Moreover, with the system, if he couldn’t obtain them, Chen Xin could continuously upgrade the workbench and the workplace, ultimately enabling the production of the parts and equipment he needed.

Having lost interest in working, Chen Xin, upon seeing the disassembled parts, somehow became intrigued and returned to the workbench, intending to manufacture the first geothermal heat pump.

The geothermal heat pump unit, unlike the system blueprints for firearms on the gun cabinet workbench, didn’t have system blueprints for reference, relying on the system’s powerful features for foolproof operation.

The geothermal heat pump unit without blueprints or references required Chen Xin to assemble it piece by piece himself, with the system only providing some assistance.

Just like when Chen Xin previously made the oxygen generator, the system could only help process materials, enhancing parts precision and quality, but couldn’t replace him in directly processing materials into parts.

But this wasn’t a problem for Chen Xin. Although the structure of the geothermal heat pump system was somewhat complex, Chen Xin had relevant structural flow diagrams and schematic diagrams in his notebook, which allowed him to assemble the entire unit with reference to the diagrams.

However, even if he could assemble it, the one assembled by Chen Xin was still rough.

There was one thing Chen Xin hadn’t thought of before: in the data he saw, the refrigerant used in geothermal heat pump units was typically water, unlike the chemical refrigerant used in household air conditioners.

Water is the medium with the highest latent heat of evaporation in nature, and it carries the most heat when transitioning from a liquid to a gaseous state. However, for small household air conditioners, using water as a cooling cycle medium is unsuitable.

The refrigerants used in household air conditioners are small in volume, quick in effect, capable of vaporizing at room temperature and condensing when compressed.

As for using water as a cooling cycle medium, this exists in large central air conditioning systems or in applications like geothermal heat pumps where the cooling cycle medium needs to carry more heat, which is where water’s advantage comes into play.

This is somewhat like the difference between small trucks and large trucks. Small trucks carry less but are fast and flexible, suitable for small-scale use; large trucks carry more but are big and slow, suitable for large-scale use.

However, Chen Xin did not consider directly replacing the remaining cooling medium in these devices with water but intended to assemble the equipment first.

After all, this was just the outdoor unit of a household central air conditioner. The cooling cycle medium it used was still a chemical medium, which resulted in its circulation pipes being quite small. The evaporator and condenser were both designed for chemical media and not suitable for water.

If it weren’t for the system, even if Chen Xin wanted to make such changes, he wouldn’t succeed. But with the system, modifying the cooling medium wouldn’t be too troublesome.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 114 - 113: The Yearning for Sweet and Sour Ribs
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Simple Geothermal Heat Pump"

"Upgrade Plan 1: Replace the circulating medium to improve the thermal exchange efficiency of the device medium. Survival points needed for the upgrade: 300"

"Upgrade Plan 2: Replace the circulating medium to enhance the circulation efficiency of the device medium. Survival points needed for the upgrade: 500"

The geothermal heat pump device assembled by Chen Xin was still an ugly thing, but compared to the jumble of parts used when first constructing the oxygen machine, this geothermal heat pump device at least looked like it was supposed to, not too rudimentary and shoddy.

Upon seeing the upgrade plans displayed by the system, Chen Xin merely raised an eyebrow before confirming the upgrade, applying both upgrade plans to the geothermal heat pump device.

A green light flashed once again, and the entire geothermal heat pump device seemed to have been reborn, with both its volume and structure changing.

Overall, it simply meant the thing got bigger, the pipes became thicker, and the structure more complex.

Or it could be said that what Chen Xin had just made was only something that could barely be called a geothermal heat pump, whereas the upgraded version was a true geothermal heat pump device.

However, looking at the massive pile of pipes on the ground, Chen Xin couldn’t help but scratch his head. Qin Lan’s drill hadn’t been delivered, so how was he supposed to install this thing into the ground himself?

The piping component of the geothermal heat pump was an enormous structure, and since the device was still uninstalled, many of the pipes were simply stacked together.

But looking at that huge pile of pipes, Chen Xin still felt a headache. It was somewhat different from his expectations; assembling these pipes was like handling a colossal radiator, which couldn’t just be shoved into a hole in the ground!

Just as Chen Xin was feeling troubled, the system once again emphasized its presence.

"Would you like to spend 50 survival points to install the geothermal heat pump?"

Looking at the system prompt that appeared in front of him, Chen Xin scratched his chin, his beard a bit prickly from not being shaved for days, but that wasn’t the main point.

"Is the system really this powerful? You really can do whatever you want with survival points!" Chen Xin massaged his chin, and although he already knew of the system’s capabilities, seeing this still made him feel like he hadn’t fully utilized all of the system’s features.

Previously, he had considered using the system for device installation, planning to put the assembled geothermal heat pump into the planting greenhouse, pretending it was installed and then using the system’s upgrade power to position it in place.

Instead of what was happening now, where the device was right here, just one system click away from being installed.

But since such a convenient function was available, Chen Xin didn’t trouble himself and chose to use survival points for the system to handle the installation.

As for the installation location of this set of equipment, Chen Xin chose to place it beside the wall near the planting greenhouse in the public space, and also connected a radiator into the planting greenhouse.

The system installation interface resembled placement interfaces in certain management and construction games, a green grid-like virtual interface.

Chen Xin tried it out and found that within this interface, he could freely move or adjust the structure of the shelter and the placement of items, all controlled by his mind without any additional operations.

Through this interface, Chen Xin was able to adjust the architectural structure and item placement within the shelter that he found unsatisfactory or inconvenient, even enlarging or shrinking room areas, creating functional divisions, and it was extremely convenient and powerful.

Of course, such a convenient and useful function naturally depended on the consumption of survival points.

Chen Xin had merely adjusted and moved a few rooms, changed the placement of some devices, and the survival points required for these adjustments as indicated by the system exceeded a thousand points.

Seeing the number displayed by the system, Chen Xin could only obediently abandon his thoughts of adjusting the shelter’s building structure and device placement, opting to just have the geothermal heat pump installed and then closing the interface.

Clearly, this interface wasn’t something Chen Xin could afford to play with at the moment.

After installing the geothermal heat pump device, Chen Xin felt that he might need to upgrade the entire shelter as soon as possible or save enough survival points to adjust the architectural structure and equipment layout.

Otherwise, he felt that his shelter would only become more cramped.

After installing the geothermal heat pump, he connected the power to get it running. Chen Xin quickly felt the heat radiating from its radiator.

On the control panel of the geothermal heat pump, he set the temperature to 20°C, checked everything, and after confirming that the device was functioning properly, Chen Xin then took out today’s dinner from the newly added food storage room, ready to eat.

Chen Xin’s dinner was quite simple today, just a portion of shelter rations and a can of luncheon meat.

Chen Xin simply pan-fried the luncheon meat, heated the shelter rations in a pot, then sat on the sofa holding the pot to eat.

Despite the system saving him a lot of effort, Chen Xin still felt exhausted from such a busy day.

Eating the somewhat coarse food, Chen Xin was unexpectedly reminded of his mother’s sweet and sour spare ribs.

That was his favorite dish since childhood, tender ribs cut into sections, blanched and then fried, cooked with a sweet and sour sauce, thickened with starch before serving, and finally topped with chopped scallions...

When Chen Xin was little, even if there was no meat and only a gloopy sauce over rice, he could finish a bowl of rice.

As he reminisced, tears unwittingly flowed from the corners of his eyes, dripping into his food.

His mother was far away in their hometown, over six hundred kilometers away, across the four provinces of Hunan, Hubei, Anhui, and Jiangxi. Without the existence of the system, seeing his mother again in this lifetime would likely be a luxury for Chen Xin.

Although Chen Xin forced himself not to think about these things, the tears continued to flow, and the longing for his mother surged uncontrollably.

His parents were too far away. Even with a radio, Chen Xin couldn’t communicate with them, and his hope of seeing them again in this lifetime was already slim.

The spoon in his hand felt as heavy as a thousand pounds, and he couldn’t lift it to bring the food so close to his mouth.

At this moment, what occupied his thoughts was no longer eating, but his parents, his hometown more than six hundred kilometers, over a thousand two hundred li away, wondering if they were well.

Overwhelmed by longing, Chen Xin simply put down his spoon, grabbed a tissue to roughly wipe away the tears on his face, went to his bedroom, and took out the photo album originally locked in a box.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 115 - 114: Photos in the Old Album
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

With slightly trembling fingers, he flipped open the album, revealing yellowed old photographs.

Most of these old photos were of Chen Xin’s parents when they were young, since later on, when Chen Xin went to college and digital cameras became popular, keeping photographs had gone out of fashion.

Gently stroking these photos, looking at his once high-spirited, handsome parents and himself as just a little kid, more tears streamed down Chen Xin’s face.

Everything in the photos had long become memories, and even the places where these photos were taken no longer exist, becoming hard to trace. He had grown to the age his parents were in the photos, while his parents were already covered with white hair, their looks aged.

The greatest regret and sorrow in life is nothing more than when you want to care for your parents but they are no longer around.

Fortunately, Chen Xin still had the chance to see his parents again, as long as he could cross the thousands of miles blocking his path.

Wiping away the tears on his face, he looked through the old photos and, looking at his parents in them, Chen Xin bit his lip and firmly decided to go back to his hometown to find his parents.

Tearing a piece of paper from his notebook, he wrote down the four characters "I want to go home," and after sticking a family portrait on the paper, Chen Xin placed it in the most noticeable spot in the living room.

With a determined goal, looking at the parents in the photo, the sadness in Chen Xin’s eyes had vanished, leaving only resolve.

————————————————

Certainly, wanting to travel the 600 kilometers distance now, crossing four provinces to go back home to bring his parents back, is not an easy task.

However, it doesn’t mean there’s no way to think of. Even though traveling 1,200 kilometers round trip, nearly 3,000 miles in total, even in pre-disaster times, would take a day and a night to drive and at least eight to nine hours by train, it doesn’t mean that the current situation is completely devoid of transportation options.

According to the information Chen Xin heard from Qin Lan, despite the severe impact on the national railway and highway networks, the railway mainlines connecting the provinces are still currently operational.

This city, as the northern gateway of Xiang Province, is an important land and water transportation hub connecting Xiang Province and Hubei Province. Even in the current situation, a train loaded with supplies travels from Hubei Province every week to provide supplies for this city as well as the neighboring provincial capital of Xiang Province and surrounding counties.

Although the traffic situation between Hubei Province and Anhui Province is unknown, there might still be functional rail lines between the two, given that the railway between Xiang Province and Hubei Province is operational.

If Chen Xin was merely returning home to bring someone back, without considering systematic constraints, finding a way to get on a train heading to Hubei Province would be his best and only choice.

As for traveling by car on the highway... Not to mention whether the highways are clear, merely driving more than six hundred kilometers in subzero weather and back is a perilous task.

Any carelessness could be fatal, leading to a wrecked car and loss of life.

Plus, whether cars can even pass smoothly on this route is an unknown, not to mention the pitch-black visibility outside that makes it impossible to see one’s own hand.

Even if Chen Xin opts for the road, he would need to transform his car into an all-terrain vehicle using the system’s capabilities before considering choosing the road route.

Although roads are hard to travel, if Chen Xin wants to successfully return home to fetch his parents, he probably indeed can only choose the road route.

Under the current circumstances and environment, it’s obvious that the railway can’t provide passenger services, and in the post-apocalyptic setting, ordinary people can barely find time to escape to shelters, so who would take the train for long-distance travel?

Therefore, even if Chen Xin could get train tickets through Qin Lan or the special police team, he most likely wouldn’t choose to travel by train and instead would drive on the road back to his hometown.

The SUV currently in his garage certainly wouldn’t make the trip back home, even before the disaster. Even with a full tank of gas, starting from this city, he would definitely need to refuel once on the way to reach home.

Under the current circumstances, there are no operating gas stations along the road. It’s not feasible to carry oil in barrels and pour it into the fuel tank along the way, right?

In such a scenario, besides a full gas tank, Chen Xin would need to carry enough oil to fill up two more tanks to ensure his car makes the trip there and back.

Thus, to drive back, Chen Xin must upgrade his car, either by enlarging the gas tank or switching to another power source.

Enlarging the gas tank is one potential solution, although carrying more oil would increase the car’s payload, the more water brought requires more flour added, more flour needs more water, and it truly is one way to tackle the issue.

Switching to another power source, in this case, means using the system to upgrade to a long-lasting energy source, like a nuclear battery.

A nuclear battery isn’t something only found in science fiction. Its formal name should be a radioactive isotope battery, and as far back as 2012, a nuclear battery capable of operating for fourteen years was installed in the Federation’s Mars rover Curiosity.

This technology is also gaining widespread application in the aerospace field.

Chen Xin thinks if he could install such a nuclear battery in his car, it should be enough for him to travel from this city back to his hometown and return.

The Curiosity rover weighs 3893kg; since the nuclear battery can power it and has a lifespan of up to fourteen years, powering a car obviously wouldn’t be an issue.

Yet, humans have yet to truly apply this thing to cars, and the reason... the total investment in the Curiosity rover project was $2.6 billion.

Of course, for someone with a system like Chen Xin, he’s only concerned about how many survival points it would cost to upgrade his car to nuclear power.

Though his longing for his parents is intensely strong, Chen Xin has no intention to set off immediately.

Currently, his shelter conditions are not perfect. Bringing his parents back means having no place for them to stay, and the shelter isn’t capable of supporting the consumption of three individuals. He must at least perform an overall upgrade of the shelter, making it habitable for more people with the ability to produce more food, before considering bringing his parents back.

While he can manage his life sufficiently on his own now, bringing his parents back would triple the consumption compared to when Chen Xin is alone.

If he doesn’t properly prepare, bringing his parents back would only worsen his own living conditions, letting them endure hardship alongside him.

Hence, despite his firm resolve to bring his parents back, Chen Xin has decided to first complete all necessary preparations before proceeding with the plan to bring his parents back.

Writing these notes on the paper with the family portrait, Chen Xin looked at this family portrait, stroked it, further solidifying his determination to bring his parents back.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 116 - 113: The Longing for Sweet and Sour Pork Ribs
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Simple Ground Source Heat Pump"

"Upgrade Option 1: Replace the circulating medium, improve the heat exchange efficiency of the equipment medium, survival points needed for upgrade: 300"

"Upgrade Option 2: Replace the circulating medium, enhance the circulating efficiency of the equipment medium, survival points needed for upgrade: 500"

The ground source heat pump equipment assembled by Chen Xin was still an ugly thing, but compared to the clutter of items created for the initial oxygen machine, this ground source heat pump at least looked like something decent, not overly crude or shoddy.

Seeing the upgrade options displayed by the system, Chen Xin merely raised an eyebrow and clicked to confirm the upgrades, applying both upgrade options to the ground source heat pump.

A flash of green light passed, and the entire ground source heat pump seemed rejuvenated, with changes in its size and structure.

Overall, the equipment got larger, the pipes thickened, and the structure became more complex.

Or it could be said that what Chen Xin had just made was barely something that could be called a ground source heat pump, and it was only after the upgrade that it became a true ground source heat pump device.

However, looking at the large pile of pipes on the ground, Chen Xin scratched his head. Qin Lan’s drill hadn’t arrived yet. How was he going to install this thing into the ground?

The piping part of the ground source heat pump was an enormous structure, and although the equipment was still uninstalled, a large number of pipes were just stacked together.

But looking at that pile of pipes, Chen Xin still felt a headache. It was different from what he imagined; assembling these pipes was like dealing with a huge radiator! It wasn’t something that could be stuffed into a ground hole easily!

Just when Chen Xin felt troubled, the system once again made its presence known.

"Consume 50 survival points to install the ground source heat pump?"

Looking at the system prompt appearing in front of him, Chen Xin scratched his chin. The unshaven beard pricked his hand, but that wasn’t the focus.

"Is the system this powerful? Truly, with survival points, you can do whatever you want!" Chen Xin rubbed his chin. Although he was already aware of the system’s prowess, seeing this made him feel he hadn’t fully utilized all system functions.

Previously, he had considered using the system to install equipment, planning to put his assembled ground source heat pump into the planting greenhouse and pretend to install it, relying on the system’s upgrade power to set it up.

Not like now, where the equipment just lay there and a system click would install it completely.

Since the feature was so convenient, Chen Xin let himself relax and chose to consume survival points to have the system handle the installation.

As for the installation location of the equipment, Chen Xin chose a spot in the public space, near the wall close to the planting greenhouse, and connected a radiator to the greenhouse.

The system’s installation interface was somewhat like the placement interface in certain management and construction games, a green grid-shaped virtual screen.

Chen Xin tried it out; within this interface, he could freely move or adjust the shelter’s structure and item placement, all controlled by thought without any extra operation needed.

Through this interface, Chen Xin could adjust structures and item positions he found unsatisfactory or inconvenient within the shelter, even enlarging or shrinking room sizes and segmenting functionalities, which was remarkably convenient and powerful.

Of course, such a convenient feature was naturally based on the consumption of survival points.

Chen Xin only adjusted and moved a few rooms and changed some equipment placements, and achieving these changes required over a thousand survival points as shown by the system.

Seeing the numbers displayed by the system, Chen Xin could only obediently abandon the idea of wanting to adjust the shelter’s architectural structure and equipment placements, simply installing the ground source heat pump and closing the interface.

Evidently, this interface was not something Chen Xin at the moment could afford to tinker with.

After installing the ground source heat pump, looking at the heat pump taking up a considerable part of the public space, Chen Xin thought he perhaps should quickly upgrade the entire shelter, or save enough survival points to adjust the shelter’s building structure and equipment placements.

Otherwise, he felt the shelter would just get more cramped.

Once the ground source heat pump was in place, powering it up to make it operational, Chen Xin soon felt the heat being emitted from its radiator nearby.

Setting the temperature to 20℃ on the ground source heat pump control panel and after checking to see the equipment was functioning well, Chen Xin took out dinner from the newly added food storage room and prepared to eat.

Today, Chen Xin’s meal was simple, just a portion of shelter rations plus a can of luncheon meat.

Chen Xin simply pan-fried the luncheon meat, and heated the shelter rations in a pot, then sat on the sofa with the pot to eat.

Despite the system helping save a lot of effort, Chen Xin still felt exhausted from all the busyness.

As he ate the somewhat coarse food, out of nowhere, Chen Xin began to miss his mother’s sweet and sour pork ribs.

Those ribs, tender and succulent, were his favorite dish since childhood. After cutting the ribs and blanching them, they were fried, then cooked in sweet and sour sauce, finally thickened with slurry before serving, and topped with scallions...

Even when he was little and there was no meat, just a bowl of thick sauce over rice, Chen Xin could eat a whole bowl.

As he reminisced, tears started flowing from the corners of Chen Xin’s eyes, dropping into the food.

His mother was now far away in their hometown, over six hundred kilometers away, spanning four provinces: Hunan, Hubei, Anhui, and Jiangxi. If not for the system, Chen Xin feared that seeing his mother again in this life would be just a dream.

Though Chen Xin forced himself not to think about it, tears continued to flow, and the longing for his mother surged unchecked from his heart.

His parents were too far away. Despite having a radio, Chen Xin couldn’t get through to them. The hope of seeing them in this life was already dim.

The spoon in his hand felt as heavy as a thousand pounds, and Chen Xin just couldn’t lift his hand to put that close-by morsel into his mouth.

At this moment, what occupied his mind was not the meal, but thoughts of his parents, whether they were doing well in the hometown over six hundred kilometers, one thousand two hundred miles away.

Unable to contain his longing, Chen Xin threw down the spoon, grabbed a tissue to wipe his tears messily from his face, and went to the bedroom to pull out the photo album locked in the box.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 117 - 115 Emotional Breakdown (1/10)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Chen Xin is back, wash your hands and eat!" "Mom, I got first place in the class for the final exams!" In the haze, the call from the depths of memory is so familiar yet strange.

"My son is amazing! I’ll take you swimming this weekend!" Father always saw him as his life’s pride, but Chen Xin no longer remembers when his father stopped constantly praising him.

"Dad, I want to buy a telescope, I promise I’ll get four As this semester!" As a child, he always yearned for the skies, what was his dream back then? An astronomer? An astronaut? It seemed everyone thought similarly in those days.

"Son, take care of yourself out there, make more friends at school, if you need money, just tell me." He got into college, leaving home for the first time in his life, and his father, now with graying temples, slipped a bank card into Chen Xin’s pocket.

"Hello, I’m also in this dorm, from now on we’re dorm brothers." Chen Xin can’t remember who this was in the dorm, but it should be the oldest brother, right? The oldest always took care of everyone else.

"Chen Xin, look at that senior girl over there, she’s so cool! Don’t compete with me, I’m going to pursue her to be my girlfriend!" Senior girl? Chen Xin remembers her well; he didn’t compete with his brother, but the brother still ended up heartbroken.

"Who’s going to the cafeteria, bring me a meal! Quick, quick, quick! Defend point A!" A group of shirtless guys in the dorm were playing games; back then even laptops were rare, what were they playing? It seemed like CS.

"Lights out, don’t text your girlfriend!" Someone was checking the dorm from outside, Chen Xin almost forgot that after ten at night, they weren’t allowed to keep the lights on in college.

"Senior, do you have a girlfriend? If not, can I be your girlfriend?" How many junior girls have confessed to him now? Since sophomore year, Chen Xin had been responsible for welcoming new students each year, combined with his good looks, he couldn’t remember how many junior girls confessed to him.

"Junior Chen, do you want to start a club with senior sister?" Which senior sister was this? Why can’t he remember?

"Let’s all sing together! Celebrate the founding of our new club!" When did I form a club?

"What song? I can play the piano accompaniment, there’s a piano right here." A senior sister who can play piano? Why don’t I remember?

"Can you play that song ’Tianya’? It was really popular a few years ago." Why did I choose this song? Isn’t it more fitting to sing something more festive to celebrate the new club?

"That piece adapted from ’Song of Bamboo’? Yes, I learned the original Japanese version." The senior sister sat down by the piano, and the bright music began.

Interestingly, the senior sister sang the Japanese version, while Chen Xin sang the Chinese version.

But both songs used the same tune, so even though the lyrics were different, Chen Xin and the senior sister on the piano still harmonized well.

Just as they were singing together, the alarm clock suddenly rang, shattering the harmony.

Chen Xin woke up.

Reaching for the phone on the bedside table, he turned off the alarm, and then sat up in bed.

The newly installed geothermal heat pump had started working, and the temperature in the shelter had risen again, not as cold as it was a few days ago, so Chen Xin didn’t need to wrestle with the decision to get out of bed from under the covers each morning.

However, though he was awake, the memories that emerged from the dream left Chen Xin in melancholy, still immersed in those memories.

Bits and pieces of life with his parents when he was young, the friendships with classmates, brothers, and friends at school as he grew older, and those who appeared in his life as beloved ones...

Memories are beautiful but are also heavy.

Yet, among them, what Chen Xin was most concerned about besides his parents was the senior sister who sang with him at the end of the dream.

Strictly speaking, she wasn’t actually a senior sister, since she was in the same grade as Chen Xin, though half a year older, so after they met, she had him call her senior sister all along.

Recalling the high-spirited self from ten years ago, the memorable things done at school, and the memorable people, Chen Xin felt as if he was sucked into a massive whirlpool, his emotions continuously plummeting, unable to break free.

Brushed his teeth, washed his face, and unlike usual, Chen Xin did not prepare breakfast, instead, he sat on the couch with a sense of defeat and darkness.

It’s not surprising for Chen Xin to dream of past events, as dreams are connected to one’s thoughts and experiences, so it’s normal to dream of what occupies one’s mind during the day.

But...the people and events within memories, those places laden with memories, no longer exist and cannot be returned to.

Looking at the papers and goals posted on the wall last night, Chen Xin fell into deep self-doubt; can he truly achieve this goal? Can he really persist? Do his efforts have meaning?

Chen Xin isn’t someone easily discouraged or depressed, but at this moment, he was deeply trapped in self-doubt and denial.

He doubts his abilities, his motivation to persist in survival, and the purpose of his existence...

The apocalypse is inherently an environment that brings immense mental pressure, moreover, it’s a continuous, nearly endless burden.

Under such circumstances, the pressure of survival, the loneliness of solitude, the hopelessness of seeing no future...

These emotions continually accumulate, those with poor endurance might quickly have emotional breakdowns, even those with strong endurance struggle to remain optimistic and upbeat in such an environment.

Because there simply isn’t a reason to be optimistic, nor is there a future worth looking forward to that motivates one to be positive.

Previously, perhaps due to the existence of the system, Chen Xin felt like he had less pressure, believing life would only get better and maintaining some optimism.

But when he recalled past experiences and beauty, and compared them to the current isolation, loneliness, darkness, and cold, the pressure and negative emotions accumulated from the apocalypse surged forth, making Chen Xin unable to persist any longer.

He isn’t the indomitable Brooklyn kid, nor is he someone who has instantly transformed his mindset from an ordinary person to a conqueror of realms slaying gods and demons upon crossing into the Otherworld.

Chen Xin is just an ordinary person, no different from everyone else; he cries, fears, gets defeated, and despairs.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 118 - 116: Choosing Decadence (2/10)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin is just an ordinary person, indistinguishable from everyone else. He can cry, be afraid, feel dejected, and despair.

The only difference, perhaps, is that he got a bit luckier, having a system that can improve living conditions and serve as a cheat.

The system brought a chance for change, and change means a better future.

This is also why Chen Xin hasn’t had an emotional breakdown before, because the system is like a carrot dangling in front of a donkey, allowing him to keep moving forward.

But a carrot is just a carrot, a temptation that can be seen but not necessarily reached.

Perhaps in the distant future, when Chen Xin upgrades his shelter to a sufficient level, he will live well and comfortably. But for now, he is still struggling in this apocalypse like everyone else.

The future might be beautiful and bright. Maybe the donkey can finally eat the carrot that tempted it all the way, but along the way, it has to endure the constant temptation of that carrot.

Why can’t I stop and eat this carrot before moving on?

Humans shouldn’t be like donkeys, led by a dangling carrot, right?

Once this thought appeared in Chen Xin’s mind, it burst forth uncontrollably.

Relax a bit, enjoy yourself, make today comfortable, and why worry about how tomorrow should be? That’s something for tomorrow’s self to worry about.

Admittedly, such a thought is really... too tempting.

For most ordinary people, indulgence and enjoyment are just too tempting and too easy to obtain.

Under the heavy pressures of life, maintaining one’s resolve and living a frugal life by working hard every day is truly exhausting.

Compared to wild indulgence and enjoyment, it’s really hard to stick to one’s beliefs.

The willpower needed for this is not something everyone, or most people, possess. Besides, Chen Xin himself is not a monk-like person.

Once the thought arises, it grows uncontrollably like wild grass.

Chen Xin finally understood why, in Christian doctrine, sloth ranks second among the seven deadly sins.

Because for a person, laziness is the most irresistible and easily cultivated emotion.

Thinking about his struggle for survival over the past half a year, always having endless tasks, Chen Xin suddenly thought that maybe taking a day off wouldn’t be bad.

Forget it, let’s indulge a bit today.

Chen Xin abandoned his struggle and decided to give himself a break today.

After all, doing things tomorrow is just the same, why exert so much effort today?

Let work go to hell! I’m going to play games today!

Taking out the cola he’s been saving from the storeroom, Chen Xin couldn’t help but lament that if he had some chips at times like this, enjoying cola and chips while playing a thrilling shooter game with friends would be perfect.

Unfortunately, there’s only cola, no chips, and the shooter game could only be played in boring single-player mode.

But who cares! There are always some games worth playing ten, twenty, or even a hundred times without getting bored!

Besides, isn’t shooting games all about enjoying the thrill of spraying bullets?

——————————————————

"Sister Lan, how much longer until we arrive?" On the truck, the young special police officer accompanying Qin Lan for the first delivery looked out the window at the darkness, feeling a bit restless as he asked Qin Lan.

Due to the worsening conditions after the snowstorm, the special police team wasn’t comfortable with Qin Lan driving alone, so they specifically assigned someone to accompany her to prevent any dangerous or difficult situations on the road that Qin Lan couldn’t handle alone.

However, there hadn’t been any issues along the way, except the snow made the road a bit hard to navigate.

"We’re almost there, A-Xin lives just up the mountain." Qin Lan glanced at the road ahead, answering with certainty, while also looking at the young officer driving beside her, smiling, "Why, does it feel far already?"

"Not really, just..." The young officer tried to find the words but seemed unsure of what to say, suddenly appearing flustered.

"Focus on the road, drive carefully." Qin Lan reminded him but also understood his feelings: "In these road conditions, it’s easy to feel like you’ve been driving forever without arriving, but don’t worry, we’re almost there. Just turn ahead and head up the mountain."

"Up the mountain? Got it." Following Qin Lan’s directions, the young officer slowly drove up the barely recognizable mountain path.

That’s why he felt he’d been driving long; in these icy and dark conditions, they could only inch forward, and the road conditions didn’t support speeding up.

Fortunately, when Qin Lan said it wasn’t far, it really wasn’t. After driving less than five hundred meters up the mountain, he saw an iron door half-buried in snow illuminated by the headlights.

"Is this it?" The young officer, looking at the nearly snow-covered door, confirmed with Qin Lan beside him, simultaneously commenting on how inconspicuous the place was.

If not for knowing the location and having Qin Lan guide him, accurately finding it wouldn’t be easy, especially with snow everywhere and the darkness.

"This is it, I’ll call him to open the door." Qin Lan nodded, not intending to get out, but instead took out the shortwave radio to call Chen Xin.

Qin Lan’s call was quickly answered, but she found it strange that Chen Xin’s voice sounded a bit different than usual over the radio.

However, Qin Lan didn’t delve into it because, in such weather and environment, Chen Xin might be unwell or something else.

Not waiting long, the half-buried door opened.

A beam of light shone through the door crack, only then did Qin Lan and the young officer put on their protective masks and get out of the car.

"A-Xin, I’ve brought you the things you wanted, but there’s a lot, so I only brought a third today, totaling eight air conditioner units." Qin Lan greeted Chen Xin and began introducing the items she brought.

Peeking behind the door Chen Xin had only opened a crack, Qin Lan worriedly asked, "A-Xin, is your door blocked by snow? Do you need our help clearing it?"

"I just cleared it yesterday, but it seems blocked again overnight. If you could help, that’d be great." Chen Xin quickly nodded. Although he planned to take a day off today, with things at the doorstep, he couldn’t just ignore them and laze around.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 119 - 117 Black Snow (3/10)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Ah Xin, are you going to have to come out and clear the snow every day from now on? If the snow gets so heavy one day that it directly buries your door, wouldn’t you be stuck down there and unable to get out?" Qin Lan joked with Chen Xin while shoveling snow.

Though it was just a joke, her concern was not unfounded. A snowstorm had already left the snow on the ground waist-deep, and with the temperatures continuing to drop, it wasn’t going to melt. A few more snowfalls like this, and the door to Chen Xin’s house would likely be completely buried under the snow.

But for Chen Xin, especially the unusually dejected Chen Xin today, this wasn’t something worth worrying about.

"If it gets buried, so be it. I have food and drink in my shelter, so it doesn’t make much difference whether I go out or not." Although Chen Xin continued to shovel snow, his attitude was one of indifference.

Hearing Chen Xin’s response, Qin Lan couldn’t help frowning. As his ex-girlfriend, she noticed that something wasn’t quite right with him.

But just as she was about to say something to Chen Xin, a young special police officer, who was helping shovel snow alongside them, suddenly asked, "Sister Lan, Brother Chen, why is this snow all black? At first, I thought it was just dirty on the surface, but how come the snow underneath is also so dirty?"

Confused, the young officer looked at the snow they had dug up and asked Qin Lan and Chen Xin.

"Look up at the sky," Chen Xin, too lazy to explain, simply pointed upwards.

The young officer looked up, only to see nothing but darkness.

Seeing the officer’s puzzled look, Qin Lan couldn’t bear it and explained to him, "What Ah Xin means is that the dust in the sky is so thick that it blocks out even the sunlight, so when it snows, it’s carrying a lot of dust with it, making the snow naturally dirtier."

"I see! Thank you, Sister Lan, I understand now." The young officer thanked her and continued shoveling snow with effort.

After not much longer, Chen Xin stopped: "That’s enough, we’ve shoveled enough to open the door now."

Hearing Chen Xin, the other two stopped their work and looked at the doorway, where they had only cleared about a meter of snow. Qin Lan asked worryingly, "Is this really enough? Won’t your car get stuck, Ah Xin?"

"We’re not going anywhere today, just need to open the door." Chen Xin shook his head unconcernedly and turned to open the shelter door: "Bring the stuff in."

Watching Chen Xin, Qin Lan felt increasingly that there was something wrong with his state of mind today.

Still, she gestured to the young officer to help her move the six air-conditioning outdoor units they had brought to the car.

The three of them carried these eight air-conditioning outdoor units into Chen Xin’s shelter. Qin Lan looked at the heap of units and asked Chen Xin, "Do you want these taken down?"

Qin Lan had been to Chen Xin’s place quite often, so she knew that the space in his shelter wasn’t very large, and eight outdoor units would take up quite a bit of room if moved downstairs.

However, Chen Xin shook his head: "Take them down, I’ll still have to carry them down when I need them if they’re left up here."

"Alright, it’s no bother with the trolley." Qin Lan nodded slightly and said nothing more, then she and the young officer placed the units on the trolley and followed Chen Xin down into the shelter.

As they passed through the passage and entered the shelter, Qin Lan noticeably felt a change.

"Ah Xin, what..." Qin Lan took off her protective mask and gloves, reached out to feel the temperature inside, and looked at him in surprise: "Did you turn on a heater?"

"No, I just finished installing the ground-source heat pump for myself." Chen Xin pointed to the heat pump he had installed.

Surprised, Qin Lan followed his gesture to look at the ground-source heat pump, and she couldn’t help but ask with interest, "Can I have a look?"

"Feel free, just don’t touch it." Chen Xin shrugged, then directed the young officer to help him move the eight units into the storage room.

Qin Lan walked over to the heat pump, watching the unit in operation and listening to the hum of the compressor, then reached out to touch the pipes above.

"Ah!" Qin Lan quickly withdrew her fingers, surprised as she looked at the reddened skin: "Is it really that hot?"

"You touched the cooling pipe, which conducts heat to the adjacent greenhouse, so it’s hot." Chen Xin said from behind her. He walked over and took her hand to look: "It’s okay, not serious, probably doesn’t need burn ointment."

Hearing that, Qin Lan quickly pulled her hand back: "I’m not that delicate, it’s not like it was boiling water."

Seeing her reaction, Chen Xin raised his eyebrows helplessly.

This was, in fact, part of why they broke up in the first place. Chen Xin always treated Qin Lan like a fragile woman, worrying about her constantly; but Qin Lan felt she wasn’t weak at all, and that it was Chen Xin who lacked physical exercise and needed protection.

These differing perceptions led to conflicts in their daily lives and caused numerous arguments between them.

Chen Xin thought Qin Lan was too strong-willed, while Qin Lan considered Chen Xin overly macho.

Though they loved each other, they couldn’t accept each other’s contrasting perceptions.

"Take a seat before you leave, would you like a Coke?" Chen Xin asked Qin Lan, perhaps feeling lonely after being without company for too long.

Qin Lan glanced at the young officer, seeing no objection, the two of them followed Chen Xin to his living area.

"Ah Xin, is that..." Qin Lan saw the Coke placed on the coffee table in front of the sofa and paused video game footage, then her expression softened into concern as she looked at him: "Feeling down lately?"

"Not really, maybe I just need some sugar." Chen Xin denied, picking up the unfinished can of Coke on the coffee table, took a sip, and said: "I found it in an abandoned shop, there’s not much, just enough for you two to share a can, get a cup and pour it."

Upon hearing this, the young officer quickly shook his head: "I’ll pass, Sister Lan can have it, I’m not a fan of Coke!"

Seeing the officer’s sensible response, Chen Xin said nothing more, just nodded and tossed an unopened can of Coke to Qin Lan.

This chapter is updated by freew(e)bnovel.(c)om

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 120 - 118 Qin Lan Staying Overnight (4/10)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"I’ve told you so many times, cola isn’t for throwing!" Although Qin Lan caught the cola that Chen Xin threw over, he didn’t open it; he just put it back on the coffee table and sat down next to Chen Xin. "Where’s the other controller?"

"In the drawer below the TV, grab it if you want to play." Chen Xin glanced at Qin Lan, silently saved his current game and exited, then switched to a two-player fighting game.

The young special ops officer sitting nearby also sat down on the sofa, but he sensibly kept his distance from the long sofa where Chen Xin and Qin Lan were sitting, opting instead for the single-seater sofa.

Qin Lan pulled out the controller from the drawer, giving the TV screen a look to understand Chen Xin’s intention. "Is this the same controller I gave you back then? Still the same old one?"

"Choose your character; don’t say I’m bullying you, I’ll let you pick first." Chen Xin felt a bit nostalgic. Among the few shared hobbies he and Qin Lan had, playing fighting games was probably the one they did the most.

Soon, just like in the past, they picked their characters and started vigorously battling with their game controllers.

Perhaps games truly affect a person’s mood; they hadn’t played many rounds before shouting and exclaiming, and even the young special ops officer watching them seemed quite engrossed.

"KO! You lost again." Chen Xin took on an elder’s demeanor and said with a somewhat smug smile at Qin Lan, "Lan, your skills have regressed!"

"You think everyone has time to play games every day like you do?" Qin Lan didn’t seem to care about losing the match but still retorted in response to Chen Xin.

Setting the controller down, Qin Lan picked up the soda Chen Xin had given her earlier. Just as she was about to open it, she hesitated, put it back, and picked up Chen Xin’s opened can, pouring it into her mouth.

Chen Xin frowned at the sight. "Didn’t I give you one? Why do you have to drink mine?"

"What’s the big deal? It’s not like I haven’t drunk yours before, and we’re not even sharing the same spot on the can." Qin Lan put on a nonchalant look; when she and Chen Xin were dating, they’d been more intimate, so to her, this wasn’t anything to fuss over.

Her casual demeanor, however, seemed to the young special ops officer nearby like she was bantering with Chen Xin, making him feel awkward, like a third wheel.

Chen Xin glanced at the young officer, feeling a bit bad about leaving him out, so he handed his controller over. "Want to play a round?"

"No, no, watching you guys is fine." The young special ops officer quickly declined, though he loved playing games and was itching to play, he wisely turned down the offer.

For the young officer, if it weren’t for waiting to leave with Qin Lan, there was nowhere else to go in Chen Xin’s refuge. He truly wished he could escape somewhere else to avoid being in their way.

However, Qin Lan seemed to notice his embarrassment and said to him, "Xiao Wang, you can head back first. I have some things to discuss with Xin."

"Huh? You’re not going back, Lan? Then how are you getting back?" The young officer seemed a bit confused upon hearing Qin Lan wasn’t leaving.

"I’m staying at Xin’s for a couple of days because I’m off duty for the next two days." Qin Lan seemed to have planned this well, instructing the officer, "Besides, I need to discuss the ground source heat pump equipment for the team with Xin and supervise him in making it."

"Well..." The young officer wanted to say something but encountered Qin Lan’s warning gaze, leading him to answer firmly, "Alright, Lan, I understand."

Saying that, the young officer quickly stood up. "I’ll be off, Lan, enjoy your time here."

Watching the young officer prepare to leave, Chen Xin gave Qin Lan a meaningful look and said to her, "Alright, Lan, you see him out and remember to close the door."

"I know, I’ll be right back." Qin Lan nodded, standing up to see the young officer out.

As Qin Lan and the officer left, Chen Xin couldn’t help but stroke his chin, feeling something was off.

Though Qin Lan was his ex-girlfriend, even when they were dating, she had never actively stayed over at his place.

Suddenly exhibiting this behavior now, Chen Xin didn’t believe she was staying just to play games for two days.

However, Chen Xin didn’t want to delve into why Qin Lan stayed. Today, he felt particularly downcast and didn’t want to trouble himself with thinking things over; on the other hand, he trusted Qin Lan enough to believe she wouldn’t do anything against him.

Continuing to play the game alone, Chen Xin casually picked up the soda Qin Lan had just sipped from and glanced at the rim, smiled slightly, then drank from it directly.

Just like Qin Lan mentioned, she used to drink his soda too; back then, she would leave a lipstick mark on the can.

Qin Lan soon returned to the living room after seeing the young officer off and sat down beside Chen Xin, who was still gaming, picking up the controller again.

"Why did you stay?" As Chen Xin played the game, he raised the question.

Not wanting to think things through didn’t mean Chen Xin wouldn’t find out the cause.

"Xin, is your mood not good lately?" Qin Lan didn’t answer Chen Xin’s question; instead, she posed a new one. "You used to be like this when you felt bad or stressed—just soda, chips, and gaming all day, right?"

Qin Lan’s words made Chen Xin feel a bit embarrassed, also rather surprised; he hadn’t realized how predictable his behavior was or how observant Qin Lan was about his situation.

"Did I make it that obvious?" Though he chose to be downcast today, being directly pointed out made Chen Xin somewhat uncomfortable.

"You were once my boyfriend; I should at least notice when you’re unhappy." Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin with slight annoyance but also worried whether her words seemed too intimate.

Chen Xin didn’t mind Qin Lan’s remark, only sighed helplessly and said, "Last night I dreamed about past events, so I’m not in a good mood today."

Upon hearing Chen Xin, Qin Lan glanced at the paper stuck on Chen Xin’s wall, having some inkling about why he felt down. "Xin, are you missing your parents?"

"How could I not miss them?" Chen Xin’s face showed a bittersweet smile.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 121 - 119 Underground Water Getting Warmer (5/10)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin didn’t answer Qin Lan’s question because it wasn’t one that required his response.

"Have Uncle and Auntie gone back to their hometown? How are you going to bring them back, Xin?" Qin Lan put down the game controller and placed her hand on Chen Xin’s shoulder. "If you need my help, just say the word, Xin. I’ll help as much as I can."

Chen Xin believed Qin Lan when she offered help, but he knew her ability to help was quite limited. Nevertheless, he thanked her, "Thanks, Lan. I’ll let you know if I need anything."

Seeing Chen Xin looking listless, Qin Lan didn’t try to force him to perk up. She simply said, "If you’re feeling down, find something to do. The more you wallow, the harder it is to bounce back."

"Is that so? Then what do you think I should do, Lan?" Chen Xin looked at Qin Lan, not really wanting to work. "And you said you want to stay for two days? I don’t have an extra bed or toiletries. Where will you sleep? And you didn’t bring any clothes to change into. How will you take a shower?"

"Can’t I just wear yours?" Qin Lan blurted out, but regretted it immediately.

Back when they were dating, Qin Lan did wear Chen Xin’s clothes. But now that they had broken up, saying such a thing felt too awkward.

It was Chen Xin who broke the awkwardness by crushing the empty soda can, standing up and saying, "I was planning to take a day off today, but with you here, I suppose I can’t. Come help me then. I’ll start by dismantling the air conditioning units you brought."

Saying this, Chen Xin tossed the can and walked out of the living room, heading towards the storage room.

Qin Lan watched Chen Xin walk out and quickly followed him.

They lifted an air conditioning unit out of the storage room and placed it on the workbench.

Staring at the charred air conditioning unit, Qin Lan asked, "Xin, can this still be used? How do we take it apart?"

"It’s not too badly burnt; it should still be usable. Let’s start by removing the casing." Chen Xin sat down, reached for a screwdriver, and handed it to Qin Lan. "Help me loosen the screws."

With that, he picked up another screwdriver and began unscrewing the bolts on the air conditioning unit.

Thanks to Qin Lan’s assistance, they quickly unscrewed all the bolts, allowing Chen Xin to remove the outer shell and expose the inside.

Even with the casing as protection, the inside didn’t seem too badly burnt. All the parts remained intact, but they were covered in soot.

"Looks good. Everything should still be usable." Chen Xin nodded slightly, signaling Qin Lan to continue unscrewing the bolts.

Dismantling things is often easier than assembling them; even if you’re not familiar with it, unscrewing bolts is straightforward as long as you have hands.

Once all the bolts are loosened, anything detachable can naturally be taken apart.

With Qin Lan’s cooperation, Chen Xin soon disassembled the air conditioning unit into a pile of parts.

Chen Xin placed these parts one by one on the workbench, frowning slightly at the layer of soot on them. He said to Qin Lan, "Lan, could you get a basin of water? These need cleaning."

The parts were all charred black; even if they weren’t damaged, the layer of soot on them definitely needed cleaning.

Qin Lan nodded and was about to get the water, but she couldn’t find a basin. "Xin, where’s your basin?"

"Basin?" Chen Xin was a bit taken aback. He suddenly remembered he hadn’t used a basin in years, so he said to Qin Lan, "Lan, go to the storage room and get a large plastic container. I don’t seem to have a basin."

Seeing this, Qin Lan could only nod without saying much and went to the storage room to fetch a plastic container.

Watching Qin Lan enter the storage room, Chen Xin suddenly had an idea. He wanted to test the abstraction effect of the system’s information state concept.

He opened the system interface, seeing the air conditioning unit displaying disassembling, he clicked disassemble.

A green light flashed in the storage room, and the air conditioning unit Qin Lan brought today silently disassembled into parts on the shelf, unnoticed by Qin Lan.

Seeing this, Chen Xin was relieved. Now he didn’t have to worry about using the system’s functions in front of others, and after upgrading the entire shelter, he could consider recruiting others.

Qin Lan didn’t realize Chen Xin had done something just now. She came out of the storage room with a large plastic container and asked Chen Xin, "Xin, is this okay? Where’s your faucet?"

"The faucet is over there by the water tank; just fill it up." Chen Xin pointed to the water tank embedded in the wall next to the storage room.

After pointing out the water tank to Qin Lan, Chen Xin prepared to go to the storage room to get some parts to make another ground-source heat pump.

But before Chen Xin could stand up, Qin Lan exclaimed in surprise, "Xin, are you using hot water? Even if you have a heat source, you shouldn’t waste it like this!"

Chen Xin was puzzled. He stood up and walked over to Qin Lan, questioning, "I’ve always used cold water! I drilled a well for groundwater, not hot water!"

But when he put his hand under the faucet, feeling the water, he was also surprised. "What’s going on? Why is the water so hot!"

Surprised, Chen Xin pressed his hand against the side of the water tank, feeling the warmth. Clearly, the water in the tank was now hot.

"What’s happening? The water I’ve been drawing has always been cold!" Chen Xin was utterly confused.

The water in his tank had always been cold. Initially, it was regular tap water, but after upgrading, it was groundwater. The temperature had always been cool.

Now, it suddenly turned hot, leaving him utterly baffled.

While checking the water tank and supply system with the system function, Chen Xin pondered what had changed the water temperature in the tank.

Ground-source heat pump? That was unlikely. While a ground-source heat pump uses a water cycle, the connected pipes have valves and only open in one direction, eliminating the possibility of backflow.

As for rising temperatures causing the water to heat up, that still wouldn’t make the entire tank feel hot.

"Xin, you’re using groundwater? Could it be a hot spring?" Qin Lan suggested a possibility.

Hearing Qin Lan’s suggestion, Chen Xin thought it was possible, but something still didn’t add up.

"That’s odd. The water I’ve been drawing was always cold. There’s no reason for it to suddenly turn hot!" Chen Xin still found it strange and shook his head, "If I’d discovered earlier that it was hot spring water, I wouldn’t have needed a ground-source heat pump. I could simply pump up hot water and use it, completely eliminating the need for shallow geothermal heating."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 122 - 120 Hot Springs, Snickers (6/10)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"This isn’t right. I’ve always drawn up cold water before; it can’t suddenly become hot!" Chen Xin still found it very strange. He shook his head and said, "If I’d discovered earlier that there were hot springs below, there would have been no need to bother with a geothermal heat pump. I could have just drawn up hot water directly for use, no need to mess with shallow geothermal heating."

Qin Lan heard what Chen Xin said and nodded in agreement.

Though she agreed with Chen Xin, Qin Lan was still puzzled about the current situation. Why did the cold water suddenly turn into hot water?

"So, Xin, do you know what could cause the underground water temperature to change suddenly?" Qin Lan thought it over and still felt that the temperature change was due to something happening with the underground water.

"Change the underground water temperature? Unless there’s a geological change that causes the aquifer to break, allowing deep underground water to seep into the shallow underground water." Chen Xin had indeed studied this matter and understood the subject: "I researched related knowledge before when I wrote novels. Deep underground water is closer to the mantle, so its temperature is higher than that of shallow underground water. If geological activity occurs, causing the interval between the aquifers to break and deep underground water to seep upwards, it could affect the shallow underground water temperature. However, I didn’t have a..."

Chen Xin was about to say that no geological activity had occurred, but he suddenly remembered yesterday’s earthquake.

Qin Lan, listening to him, naturally thought of the same thing: "Was it yesterday’s earthquake?"

"It’s possible. If the earthquake affected the aquifers, it’s very likely to have caused hot water." Chen Xin nodded in agreement with what Qin Lan said.

"That’s wonderful, Xin, now you have hot spring water to use." Qin Lan couldn’t help feeling happy for Chen Xin.

However, Chen Xin didn’t seem to care much, just shook his head and said, "Hot spring water is just like that, and how long this hot water will last is still unknown. Geological activity might cause deep underground water to surge to the surface or seep into shallow underground water, but it could also be short-term, and it might be gone after a while.

To have a continuous supply of hot spring water, there needs to be a stable passage below, allowing hot water to constantly flow up.

In the past, such things were quite common: sudden murkiness, off-flavors, or heating of well water—all indicating that geological activity affected the water quality of underground water, but generally, these were short-term changes."

Facing Chen Xin’s explanation, Qin Lan just looked at him, then smiled somewhat dejectedly after he stopped talking, "Xin, you still know a lot as always!"

Chen Xin nonchalantly raised an eyebrow. Seeing the plastic box filled with enough water, he turned off the faucet and said to Qin Lan, "Let’s carry it over. Those parts need a good scrubbing."

Qin Lan nodded and helped him lift the water-filled plastic box together.

"Just place it on the ground." The plastic box itself was not heavy, but filled with water, it became weighty. Chen Xin didn’t plan to put it on the table and just placed it on the ground next to the workbench.

After setting down the water-filled plastic box, Qin Lan looked at the disassembled parts on the table and asked Chen Xin, "Xin, should I just soak these parts in the water?"

"You need to scrub them, clean off the surface grime." Chen Xin took two steel brushes from the hanging board, handing one to Qin Lan: "We should actually soak them in kerosene, which effectively dissolves the grime on these parts, but now we’ll just scrub them slowly."

Even though he could use the system to clean these things instantly without Qin Lan noticing, Chen Xin thought scrubbing them slowly was not a problem.

There was only one stool in front of the workbench. Chen Xin intended to bring a chair down from the bedroom, but Qin Lan just had him move aside a bit and sat down next to him.

Chen Xin didn’t say anything upon seeing this, and after indicating to Qin Lan that there were rubber gloves on the table, he put on a pair, picked up a burnt black part, and began scrubbing it with the steel brush in the water.

Qin Lan was doing the same thing, but with only one stool, the two were a bit cramped sitting there together.

"Xin, you’ve gotten thinner." As Qin Lan scrubbed a part with the steel brush, she chatted casually with Chen Xin.

"I work on the farm every day, unlike writing books before which required a high sugar and energy intake; it’s normal to slim down." Chen Xin was naturally aware of his weight loss.

At least when he showered every day, he could clearly see his middle-aged paunch fading away and his abs becoming more defined.

"Xin, do you still like eating sweets? Is it difficult for you not to have sweets now?" Qin Lan couldn’t help but worry about Chen Xin, as she had seen him in a fluster when he was too focused on writing and forgot to eat, searching for something to eat.

This is a common problem among those who do intensive mental work, where a lot of blood sugar is consumed due to the high-intensity mental labor and concentration, while energy consumption is not high.

This was why Chen Xin would always want to eat something when writing, felt easily hungry, and particularly liked sweets.

This also explains why Qin Lan said he had slimmed down. Chen Xin used to rely on high-sugar, high-energy foods, but while sugar would be consumed by mental labor, the energy wasn’t, thus building up as belly fat.

Chen Xin just smiled at Qin Lan’s worries and shook his head, saying, "It’s fine, I’m not using my brain as much now. Most of the work now is physical, so the energy consumption is quite large. It’s okay not to eat sweets."

Listening to Chen Xin, Qin Lan hesitated for a moment, then took off one glove, unzipped her jacket, and took out a Snickers bar from the pocket inside her clothes, handing it to Chen Xin.

"Snickers? Lan, how do you have this?" Chen Xin did not take it but was curious about how Qin Lan had it.

"The scavenger team found it in a supermarket, and I exchanged it from them." Qin Lan explained briefly without mentioning that this Snickers cost her three servings of the standard rations from the shelter.

Although it sounds like a big gap, what logic applies in a post-apocalyptic world? Especially now, sweets like chocolate are in short supply, let alone Snickers, which is great for emergency food.

Though Chen Xin didn’t know how Qin Lan traded for it, hearing that she exchanged it made him aware it wasn’t cheap, so he shook his head and refused, "Keep it for yourself. I’m not lacking food, and if I need something sweet, I can make malt candy."

Seeing that Chen Xin did not accept it, Qin Lan did not insist, but she didn’t put the Snickers away; instead, she placed it on the workbench.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 123 - 121: Large Equipment (7/10)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Even though each part needed scrubbing, with Qin Lan’s help, the two of them quickly managed to clean all the parts and left them soaking in water.

"Is it okay to just soak them like this? Xin, aren’t you going to add any detergent?" Qin Lan asked, as she watched Chen Xin soak all the parts in water, confirming with concern.

Chen Xin nodded, affirming, "Soaking them like this is fine. It’s a pity we don’t have kerosene; it would be the most effective."

"What are you doing now?" Qin Lan asked, removing her gloves and putting them aside, looking toward Chen Xin, waiting for his instructions.
