Feeling a little embarrassed under Qin Lan’s gaze, Chen Xin scratched his face with his finger and said, "What if I say I want to keep playing games, will you hit me?"

"..." Qin Lan silently looked at Chen Xin, then said after a while, "No, Xin, you can do whatever you like. But I still think you should find something to do; playing games won’t make you happier."

Qin Lan’s words made Chen Xin smile, but he didn’t say much and just nodded, "Since that’s the case, Lan, continue to help me assemble the geothermal heat pump. Uncle Ding sent over so many air conditioner units for you, hoping to exchange for geothermal heat pumps from me, right? How many do you need?"

"Xin, how much material does it take to make one geothermal heat pump? What’s the heating range?" Qin Lan didn’t state her demand immediately but instead asked Chen Xin about the relevant data for the geothermal heat pump.

"Materials from one air conditioner unit are enough to make one geothermal heat pump, with an effective heating range of about one hundred square meters. As for specific data, I don’t have the tools to measure it; that’s roughly it." Although he had a system, it didn’t have any data panels, so Chen Xin wasn’t very familiar with the equipment he made. "The thermal efficiency of a geothermal heat pump is approximately twice that of an air conditioner of the same power. Based on this calculation, you should roughly determine the heating effect of a geothermal heat pump. In an enclosed space, the effect should be quite good."

Through Chen Xin’s explanation, Qin Lan roughly understood the performance of the geothermal heat pump Chen Xin made. Having an idea in mind, she said to Chen Xin, "I brought eight air conditioner units this time. If possible, I hope you can make at least six devices. If one device is made using two air conditioner units, would it enhance the heating effect?"

Before coming, the political commissar specifically mentioned this issue to Qin Lan. The SWAT team didn’t intend to exchange eight units for eight devices.

Exchanging eight air conditioner units for six devices, leaving the other two units as Chen Xin’s labor fee, was the result of discussion between Uncle Ding and the political commissar.

They still had some doubts about the heating effect of geothermal heat pumps, and before Qin Lan came, the political commissar specifically instructed her to ask Chen Xin as many questions as possible to understand the specific heating effect of geothermal heat pumps.

However, after entering Chen Xin’s shelter, she no longer had any doubts. Although Chen Xin’s shelter was just a private one and not large, it still spanned two to three hundred square meters, and the heat provided by one geothermal heat pump could maintain the temperature at around 20℃, which was already quite good.

"Using two? Are you suggesting parallel equipment to enhance the heating effect of a single unit?" Chen Xin understood Qin Lan’s meaning immediately and nodded, "Of course, it’s doable. Parallelizing two devices can indeed improve the heating effect. Do you need high-power geothermal heat pumps?"

To this question, Qin Lan certainly gave an affirmative answer, "The SWAT team area is not small, and even providing heating to the main area requires high-power equipment to meet heating demands. Moreover, with high-power equipment, we can use pipes to distribute heat to more rooms, ensuring more people are kept warm."

"So you want to use geothermal heat pump equipment to create a heating system?" Chen Xin understood Qin Lan’s intention right away.

For official shelters where there is a large area heating demand, centrally using multiple devices to aggregate and distribute thermal energy to more areas through pipes is indeed more effective than each device providing heat independently.

When multiple devices work together, they can generate more heat, thus providing better heating effects than a single device.

"Xin, can you do it?" Seeing that Chen Xin understood her meaning, Qin Lan asked with concern.

What she was asking wasn’t whether Chen Xin could make the larger geothermal heat pump device that he already knew how to make, but whether he could create a heating system based on the geothermal heat pump equipment.

Chen Xin thought for a bit and after simulating the entire system in his mind, he said, "Of course, it can be done, but it would require more materials and parts. High-power devices aren’t simply a matter of connecting low-power devices together; it’s best to have matching components.

Additionally, to construct such a large-scale heating system, more than just geothermal heat pumps are needed. Pipes, radiators, pressure pumps... there are many things needed, and I haven’t attempted it before; perhaps professional help is needed."

Qin Lan wasn’t surprised by this; although Chen Xin had experience in mechanical equipment work and was proficient at DIYing a geothermal heat pump, he wasn’t a professional. To expect him to single-handedly build a heating system might be too demanding.

If it weren’t for the novelist-like endless knowledge base, knowing bits of everything, perhaps Chen Xin wouldn’t even be able to say what is needed to build a heating system.

"There’s no need to worry about professionals, Xin. Previously, the city had a project plan for centralized heating using geothermal heat pumps, and experts in this field are available. You just need to provide the equipment." Qin Lan reassured Chen Xin, which was part of the preliminary research done by the political commissar and Uncle Ding when they decided to implement a heating system in the SWAT team’s shelter area.

Without professional theoretical support, they wouldn’t dare undertake such large-scale renovations in the shelter.

Uncle Ding and the political commissar made the decision to use geothermal heat pump equipment to centralize heating for the SWAT team area after consulting relevant experts for theoretical validation.

"Since there are experts to guide the process, I won’t interfere further. Just report the power requirements to me and I’ll take care of it," Chen Xin replied, nodding after hearing Qin Lan say experts would provide technical support, thereby agreeing to the matter.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 124 - 122: Qin Lan’s Sweater (8/10)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Although that’s what was said, manufacturing high-power equipment is evidently much more challenging than making standard equipment.

The power of the compressor itself is a limitation, and the dimensions of other parts are not designed for high-power equipment. To manufacture a high-power ground source heat pump, unless Chen Xin uses the system to help modify the parts, genuine compatible high-power components are still needed.

Qin Lan was aware of this as well, so she didn’t insist on Chen Xin manufacturing high-power equipment. She simply said to him, "Since high-power equipment is hard to make, A Xin, you should just make a ground source heat pump like an air conditioner outdoor unit. Make six ground source heat pumps for our SWAT team. As for how to use them, I’ll hand them over to the experts; you don’t need to worry."

"Alright, since A Lan, you said so, let’s do it your way." Chen Xin did not object.

Using the system to help modify the parts also requires spending survival points. Although it doesn’t cost much, why not save a bit if he can?

With Qin Lan’s help, Chen Xin retrieved a pile of parts from the storage room that had been placed on the shelves, which were exactly the parts of an air conditioner outdoor unit he had just disassembled using the system in front of Qin Lan.

Of course, the items disassembled by the system didn’t have the layer of burnt grime that manually disassembled parts had and were clean as if they were new.

Perhaps thanks to the so-called blurred concept of the information state, Qin Lan didn’t find anything wrong with these parts looking new and, instead, assisted Chen Xin in assembling the parts one by one under his guidance.

"A Xin, can such a thin pipe be effective?" Qin Lan asked Chen Xin, holding a copper pipe. This copper pipe was only as thick as a finger and didn’t seem capable of effectively transmitting thermal energy.

Chen Xin, however, was unconcerned. Not to mention that he could improve the equipment’s efficiency with a system upgrade and alter the pipe’s diameter, the diameter of this copper pipe itself was not an issue.

Still, he explained to Qin Lan, "Don’t worry, this thickness is fine. The pipes used in air conditioners are this thick, and they can still achieve high-efficiency cooling in summer, right?"

"Can cooling and heating be the same?" Even though she knew the working principles of ground source heat pumps and air conditioners were the same, she still didn’t believe the two pieces of equipment were identical.

Smiling as he took the copper pipe from Qin Lan’s hand and connected it to the assembled equipment, Chen Xin laughed and said, "Why can’t they be? Aren’t they both about transferring heat? Plus, the thermal efficiency of a ground source heat pump is twice that of an air conditioner. You’ve seen this one’s heating efficiency yourself, so there’s no need to worry."

Qin Lan turned her head to look at the ground source heat pump installed by Chen Xin, hesitated for a moment, and then nodded in affirmation. She then unzipped her jacket and took it off, saying, "A Xin, I wouldn’t have noticed if you hadn’t mentioned it; it’s actually warm here."

"Why are you wearing something so thick indoors?" Chen Xin glanced at Qin Lan’s jacket, which unsurprisingly was thickened.

Qin Lan stretched with a yawn, her impressive figure wrapped in a gray thick sweater exuding a unique allure, causing Chen Xin to awkwardly avert his gaze, "Is that the sweater I gave you?"

"Yeah, it’s the one you bought with your first-month manuscript fee, right? Should be the first birthday gift you gave me?" Qin Lan looked down at the sweater and chuckled.

"In the blink of an eye, so many years have passed. I didn’t expect you’d still be wearing it." Chen Xin couldn’t help shaking his head, a bit sentimental.

Back then, he had just quit his original job and planned to become a full-time online writer, a decision his parents strongly opposed, but Qin Lan supported him wholeheartedly.

When Chen Xin had a fallout with his parents and moved out, he intended to rent a place on his own, but Qin Lan directly let him move in with her.

It was with Qin Lan’s support that Chen Xin was able to persist and become an online writer.

So after becoming a full-time online writer and earning his first manuscript fee, he bought Qin Lan this sweater as a birthday gift at that time.

Qin Lan loved this sweater and took great care of it; it was also a testament to their love.

Unfortunately, the two didn’t end up together and chose to break up instead.

"Yes, it’s been so many years already." Qin Lan couldn’t help but feel sentimental, and even said somewhat playfully, "I still remember how much you liked me wearing just this sweater back then! Why did you like it so much at that time?"

Faced with this question, Chen Xin awkwardly turned his head, rubbed his nose, and said, "Because back then, A Lan, you had a great figure!"

"It’s like implying I don’t have a good figure now." Qin Lan revealed a smile upon hearing Chen Xin’s answer.

However, she didn’t continue the topic and kept assembling the ground source heat pump with Chen Xin.

With Qin Lan’s help, the assembly of this ground source heat pump went very smoothly, much faster than when Chen Xin assembled the first one on his own.

However, this ground source heat pump was made from parts sourced from a standard air conditioner outdoor unit, whereas the first ground source heat pump Chen Xin assembled used parts from a central air conditioner outdoor unit. The power difference was significant, resulting in a notable size difference in the finished products.

"A Xin, is this considered complete?" Qin Lan asked Chen Xin as she looked at the assembled equipment, confirming with him.

Chen Xin nodded, stretched with a yawn, and stood up, "The assembly is complete, but it still needs debugging to check for any issues."

"Oh, then just leave it for now; there’s no rush for debugging." Qin Lan also stood up, wiping her hands with a rag.

After busying herself the whole day, she inevitably felt a bit hungry and thus asked Chen Xin, "A Xin, are you hungry? Should we eat first?"

"A Lan, are you hungry? Sure, then let’s eat first." Seeing Qin Lan indicating she was indeed hungry, Chen Xin nonchalantly nodded, "You go wash your hands first, and I’ll cook."

After saying this, Chen Xin wiped his hands and planned to go to the storage room to get some food, but Qin Lan stopped him.

"How about I cook today? A Xin, you’ve been busy all afternoon, you must be tired." Without waiting for Chen Xin to protest, she headed towards his storage room.

Watching Qin Lan like this, Chen Xin raised an eyebrow as he watched her back, crossing his arms and placing the second knuckle of his right index finger in his mouth.

This was a habit of Chen Xin’s when thinking about certain problems; whenever he couldn’t figure something out, he would do this to aid in his thought process.

Biting his finger, Chen Xin fell into confusion and thought, "A Lan... what are you trying to do? Reconcile? That’s just impossible for us now..."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 125 - 123 Eating (9/10)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

What exactly Qin Lan wants to do, Chen Xin doesn’t want to delve into. Emotionally speaking, he believes QIn Lan won’t harm him or have any ill intentions towards him.

With this premise, whatever Qin Lan wants to do is something Chen Xin feels he can accept.

If that’s the case, why should he care too much?

Originally, Chen Xin didn’t want to think about anything today; he wanted to give himself a day off. Yet, he was dragged by Qin Lan to work the entire afternoon, which left him quite frustrated and on the verge of exploding if he had to think about anything else.

Thus, giving up on thinking entirely, he just tidied up the workbench and, after cleaning it, went to wash his hands and waited to eat.

Though Qin Lan claimed she was cooking, in reality, there wasn’t much to make due to the lack of ingredients.

Chen Xin’s food storage indeed had quite a bit of food, but besides the carrots, cabbages, and lettuce he grew himself, there weren’t other ingredients.

There were only pre-stored self-heating foods from before the disaster and the standard shelter food obtained through exchanges with the Special Forces team from Qin Lan herself, as well as two kinds of canned food swapped from Mo Qingyan.

Perhaps considering saving food for Chen Xin, Qin Lan didn’t take much from his storage, only grabbing two portions of standard shelter food, one can of luncheon meat, one cabbage, and two carrots.

Returning to the living room with these, Qin Lan saw Chen Xin still playing games on the couch without a word, and she just headed to the kitchen to cook dinner.

With limited ingredients, Qin Lan didn’t plan to make anything too complicated, just washing the cabbage and carrots before chopping them and the luncheon meat to boil together.

While these things were boiling, she also heated the two portions of standard shelter food.

The standard shelter food she returned to Chen Xin was actually not bad if you judge solely from nutrition and calorie supplement perspectives.

Rice and potatoes as the main ingredients provide starch and calories as well as some nutrition, while mushrooms and rat meat offer another portion and also provide some animal protein and fat intake.

If ignoring these things being simply boiled with just oil and salt for seasoning, they’re pretty good as food.

Especially since it also comes with two energy bars made from mixed fried flour and nut crumbs, just one serving is enough to meet an adult’s daily nutrition and calorie needs.

For kids, it might even be too much to eat.

But the texture and taste of these foods... Qin Lan could only say you can’t ask for too much in the apocalypse.

Cooking the rice is certainly okay, and the potatoes are fine too. Just a bit of salt for flavor doesn’t make it taste bad; even if the texture is lacking, it’s still passable.

But boiling the mushrooms and rat meat in water... Qin Lan herself really dislikes the taste of boiled mushrooms.

As for the fried flour energy bar, it’s not the stir-fried noodles, but directly fried flour.

Mixing nut crumbs into the flour, adding a bit of salt, and frying, using the oil from the nut fragments to add some stickiness to the fried flour so it isn’t too loose, and finally pressing into shape with a mold, that’s how the energy bar of the standard shelter food is made.

Since this food is a model supplied to their special police team, it has two energy bars, but the model distributed to the general public in shelters only has one.

In official shelters, to save resources, only those doing physical labor or going out to work can get the model with two energy bars.

Eating this energy bar is straightforward, just break it up and add some water, mix it, and then you can eat it directly. If it feels cold and not to your taste, you can use hot water.

Seventy years ago, at the beginning of the founding of Flame Country, in the war that broke out on the peninsula with the Federation, this was what the volunteer army predecessors ate. Of course, their fried flour didn’t have nut fragments.

A bite of fried flour with a mouthful of snow, that fried flour is exactly this kind of fried flour.

With four energy bars in the two portions of standard shelter food, Qin Lan thought for a moment and put two of them into the boiling vegetable soup.

As she stirred, the soup that was originally clear turned thick and mushy, becoming vegetable mush.

Leaving the other two energy bars unopened, she just set them aside.

The vegetable mush was soon cooked, and the heated food was quickly warmed, releasing the aroma of food.

Setting the food on the coffee table, Qin Lan tapped Chen Xin on the leg, "Dinner time, play later."

Following her words, Chen Xin put down the game controller, went to wash his hands, and came back ready to eat.

"Lan, your cooking has improved! I remember you could only cook instant noodles before." Chen Xin looked at the well-heated food and the quite decently cooked vegetable mush, not holding back his praise.

In the past, because of her job, Qin Lan didn’t care about her quality of life, and the Special Forces often had missions, making meal times irregular. Many times, what she ate were cold takeouts.

Back then, even though Qin Lan looked beautiful and fit because of the high-intensity training, with the added aura of a female special forces sniper, making her seem perfect, her personal life was actually a mess.

Her home looked clean and tidy because she hired housekeeping every week; the kitchen was spotless because she didn’t set foot there for a month...

The only thing Qin Lan could make at that time was instant noodles.

So seeing the current Qin Lan able to cook up a pot of quite decently smelling vegetable mush, Chen Xin really felt her cooking skills had improved a lot.

Qin Lan wasn’t particularly pleased with Chen Xin’s compliment and instead pouted, "Aren’t you looking down on me too much? It’s not like I don’t have an intermediate cooking certificate, now you also can only make this food, even instant noodles can’t be made!"

"Yes, yes, I’m just a chef who can’t even make instant noodles now. Thank you, officer, for the great mercy of cooking for me." Chen Xin chuckled nonchalantly; Qin Lan’s little mood added to her charm in his eyes.

Watching Qin Lan puffing up her cheeks, Chen Xin struggled to resist the urge to pinch her cheeks.

Of course, Qin Lan could see what Chen Xin was intending to do. When they were together before, Chen Xin especially liked to tease her and then pinch her cheeks when she puffed them unhappily.

Recalling this, Qin Lan couldn’t help but let out a snicker, throwing a playful glance at Chen Xin, "Alright, hurry and eat before it gets cold."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 126 - 124 The Tigress (10/10)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

With limited food resources, there wasn’t much to say about this meal.

After eating, Qin Lan helped take the dishes to the kitchen, while Chen Xin cleaned the table as she washed the dishes.

After tidying the table, Chen Xin picked up the game controller again, but Qin Lan started exercising beside him after washing the dishes.

Watching Qin Lan stretch, Chen Xin couldn’t help but ask, "Ah Lan, since when did you develop the habit of exercising after meals?"

"This isn’t called post-meal exercise, just moderate activity to help digestion." Qin Lan continued to move her body while also advising Chen Xin, "Ah Xin, you should exercise too. Some moderate activity after eating can help with digestion."

"No, I want to play games!" Chen Xin refused directly and bluntly.

Seeing this, Qin Lan didn’t insist and continued to exercise on her own—something she had gotten used to after the apocalypse.

After all, the conditions in the official shelter couldn’t compare to Chen Xin’s place. Without games to play, the only way to kill time was exercise.

However, Qin Lan only did some stretching and shaping exercises, not deliberately trying to build muscle. She remembered Chen Xin once said he liked girls with muscle definition but didn’t want them to look like bodybuilders.

So Qin Lan exercised to maintain stamina and core strength, rarely focusing on building muscles to avoid becoming overly muscular.

After some activity, Qin Lan inevitably worked up a little sweat and, being dressed warmly to keep out the cold, decided she wanted a shower after the exercise.

If she were in the official shelter, she might have to hurry.

Because the shelter had periodic water supply, with no running water after nine at night, even the hot water only lasted until ten due to the tank. If she didn’t hurry, she wouldn’t finish her shower.

With the idea of taking a shower in mind, she began undressing.

To Qin Lan, it seemed like a perfectly normal action, but Chen Xin was a bit surprised and asked, "Ah Lan, why are you taking off your clothes?"

"Taking a shower, of course. I sweated, so I need to clean up!" Qin Lan took off her sweater, unbuttoning her shirt while asking, "Ah Xin, where’s your bathroom?"

Seeing Qin Lan’s matter-of-fact expression, Chen Xin could only look away and point to a nearby bathroom, saying, "There, the hot water’s on the left tap."

"Oh, I see. I didn’t bring a towel. Is it okay to use yours?" Qin Lan glanced at Chen Xin, having managed to take off her pants, revealing her stunningly well-toned legs.

Faced with such temptation from his ex-girlfriend, Chen Xin felt increasingly awkward, so he paused the game and headed to the bedroom, "I’ll get you a new one."

Qin Lan looked at what seemed like Chen Xin’s slightly embarrassed back and couldn’t help but chuckle. She felt like Chen Xin had become a lot shyer compared to before in some ways.

Soon, Chen Xin returned with a new towel and an unopened bar of soap.

Placing the towel and soap in Qin Lan’s hands and facing her now only in a vest, Chen Xin turned his face away, saying, "There’s shampoo in the bathroom."

"It’s not like you haven’t seen it before, why so shy?" Receiving the items from Chen Xin and seeing his reluctant glance, Qin Lan couldn’t resist teasing him and deliberately puffed out her chest.

"Alright, stop enticing me, go take your shower!" Seeing this, Chen Xin slapped Qin Lan on her shapely butt like he used to urge her to bathe while he pretended to act nonchalant, sitting back on the sofa to continue his game.

Touching where Chen Xin slapped her, Qin Lan laughed and then walked into the bathroom.

Seeing Qin Lan enter the bathroom, Chen Xin finally exhaled a sigh of relief, reaching up to touch his face, only to remember that this hand had just slapped Qin Lan’s butt.

"Feels even better than before." Chen Xin didn’t understand his mindset, but he instantly discerned the difference between the current and past feel.

However, he didn’t have any thoughts about Qin Lan, nor did he feel any particular allure about a beautiful woman showering less than ten meters away, behind just one door.

Even though Chen Xin knew that if he suggested showering together, Qin Lan wouldn’t refuse, he still had no such intention.

It’s not that Chen Xin was some kind of saint or didn’t like women. He and Qin Lan, when together, didn’t have any reservations in that regard. Both were energetic types, often spending weekends staying up all night.

But after breaking up, Chen Xin felt their relationship was no longer the same and that he shouldn’t have any entanglements with Qin Lan anymore. Breaking up meant breaking up.

Being broken up, there was no need to cling to someone.

This was also why Chen Xin hadn’t been in contact or interaction with Qin Lan since then, and Qin Lan understood Chen Xin’s thoughts.

Since both had broken up, they shouldn’t become a burden to each other. Starting anew would be better for both of them.

However, the apocalypse broke this mutual understanding, bringing them together again, and rekindled emotions that Qin Lan thought had been settled.

Listening to the sound of water in the bathroom and reminiscing about watching Qin Lan bath before, Chen Xin felt a certain agitation simmering inside him.

This caused his breathing to become somewhat heavy, so he put down the game controller, planning to go to the storage room to get a bottle of water to drink, hoping it might cool his emotional state.

Reaching the storage room, grabbing a bottle of stored purified water, Chen Xin unscrewed the cap and drank nearly half of it in one go, feeling his emotions settle down a bit.

He didn’t rush to go back, straining his ears to listen to the bathroom’s water sounds, planning to wait until Qin Lan finished showering to go back up, thus avoiding unnecessary embarrassment.

Perhaps it was due to her police habits, but Qin Lan didn’t take long in the shower, with the water sounds stopping after around ten minutes.

Estimating that Qin Lan should be dressed by now, Chen Xin took the water and returned to the living room.

However, what presented itself to him was a scene he rather wouldn’t have seen— Qin Lan was only wearing a sweater, leaning against the sofa, her long, porcelain legs stretching out, leading his gaze somewhere it shouldn’t.

Seeing Chen Xin enter, Qin Lan asked him, "What are you waiting for?"

"I think it’s better not to. We’re already broken up." Chen Xin swallowed his saliva, making the difficult choice to refuse.

But clearly, this wasn’t the answer Qin Lan wanted to hear.

So the tigress leapt up, grasped her prey, tossed him onto the sofa, and pounced on him...

(The next 39,078 words have been omitted.)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 127 - 125: Qin Lan’s Purpose
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When the tigress hunted, the prey fought back, the tigress subdued the prey again, and the prey still retaliated against the tigress, both Chen Xin and Qin Lan were exhausted.

"A Xin, how about we get back together?" Lying on Chen Xin’s back, Qin Lan rested her head on his shoulder and asked in a wistful tone.

"You were the one who proposed the breakup in the first place, and now you’re telling me to get back together?" Chen Xin lay on the sofa and turned his head to look at Qin Lan on his shoulder. "Just say what your purpose is, no need to use this as an excuse. Besides, we’ve been broken up for so many years, it’s hard for me not to suspect you have ulterior motives."

After Chen Xin said this, Qin Lan’s face immediately flushed, "Was I that obvious?"

"From the moment you decided to stay, I knew you had a purpose; it’s just that I was too lazy to think about it before." Saying this, Chen Xin rolled over and sat up, grabbed a water bottle from the table, and took a big gulp to replenish what he’d lost during the intense activity.

Qin Lan reached out and took the bottle from Chen Xin’s hand, took a drink herself before saying, "A Xin, come back to the official refuge with me! I really don’t feel at ease with you living here alone."

"What’s wrong with living here? I have food and drink, and the heating issue is resolved. I’m living comfortably." Hearing Qin Lan’s words, Chen Xin shook his head in refusal: "And you’re not my girlfriend anymore, in what capacity are you asking me to go back with you?"

Facing Chen Xin’s refusal, Qin Lan didn’t bring up what happened earlier. Though traditionally, girls might care about these things, she’s not that traditional, or she wouldn’t have become a SWAT officer.

Additionally, Qin Lan clearly knew that using these reasons on Chen Xin was meaningless.

"A Xin, living here alone, even if all your living conditions are excellent, you’re by yourself, with no one to talk to. Don’t you feel that’s a problem? Haven’t you noticed your emotional breakdown is due to too much psychological pressure?" Qin Lan said with concern to Chen Xin, reflecting her worries.

She had been concerned about Chen Xin’s mental state because, in this post-apocalyptic world, even in the official refuges with access to psychologists and other people to communicate with, many people still had mental problems.

Even the strictly trained SWAT members were not spared, making it more likely for Chen Xin living alone in a suburban private refuge with limited space and communication opportunities to have psychological issues.

However, during her previous visits, Chen Xin didn’t show any problems, and the situation wasn’t as severe back then. Chen Xin could still go out and communicate with people like Mo Qingyan, so Qin Lan didn’t bring up the issue.

Moreover, they had been broken up for several years, and both had tried to minimize contact. Qin Lan lacked the position and reason to care or interfere with Chen Xin.

But as Chen Xin’s interactions with the SWAT team increased and they met more often, their communication and interactions slowly returned to how they used to be, allowing Qin Lan to grow more concerned for Chen Xin.

Seeing Chen Xin’s unusual lethargy this time, as well as his habit of playing games with cola and chips only when he’s feeling down, made Qin Lan realize Chen Xin was having psychological problems, not just a bad mood.

As a sniper, high psychological resilience is required, so Qin Lan had read a lot of psychology books and was quite knowledgeable about judging someone’s psychological state.

Familiar with Chen Xin, Qin Lan could easily identify that his psychological issues stemmed from long-term lack of communication with others and excessive stress.

Qin Lan proactively offered to stay, hoping to help Chen Xin adjust his psychological problems.

Yet clearly, her current behavior and what she did with Chen Xin were somewhat different from her initial intentions.

As for why she wanted Chen Xin to go back to the official refuge, it was because Qin Lan didn’t want Chen Xin to continue surviving alone like this.

"Then why don’t you come live with me, A Lan?" Chen Xin looked at Qin Lan, meeting her eyes directly: "The living conditions here are obviously better than the official refuge. If you want to live with me, why not just move here with me? Once I get my parents back, this will be a complete home."

"A Xin! Haven’t you understood my meaning yet? I hope you can return to social life, you’re not Robinson; you don’t need to survive in the wilderness alone!" Qin Lan grabbed Chen Xin’s arm, earnestly persuading him.

In Qin Lan’s view, Chen Xin surviving alone outside could in no way compare to living a communal life at the official refuge.

"Just like this time, if we hadn’t come, you’d be trapped in your refuge with the door sealed by snow!" Qin Lan hoped Chen Xin could heed her advice, using the example of the recent snow-blocked door incident: "Had you been at the official refuge, despite its slightly worse conditions, you wouldn’t have to worry about these things, sparing you from bearing such pressure!"

Concerned that Chen Xin might worry about his parents, Qin Lan continued: "If you’re worried about your parents, A Xin, I’ll talk to the political commissar and the captain to help, along with you going back to your hometown to bring them back, okay?"

Seeing the affection in Qin Lan’s eyes, Chen Xin knew she genuinely cared for him and acknowledged the fact of his excessive psychological stress.

Compared to living alone in a confined space, returning to communal living in a larger space is indeed beneficial for mental health.

But for Chen Xin, that’s impossible, or rather it’s a path he wouldn’t choose.

Does Chen Xin know that living alone in such a cramped private refuge could lead to severe psychological issues?

Of course he knows. Much of Qin Lan’s psychological knowledge came from him; how could he not know that an oppressive, cramped environment leads to severe psychological issues?

But what could he do at the official refuge?

Work as a technician fixing equipment or be a laborer digging earth?

Though Chen Xin knows many things, he is not a highly skilled professional.

With his refuge’s support system, he can barely manage, but he truly doesn’t know what he’d do at the official refuge.

Most crucially, Chen Xin is unsure whether his system would still function if he went to the official refuge.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 128 - 126 The Sudden Disappearance of Chen Xin
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"No matter what you say, Lan, I’m not going to the official shelter." Chen Xin avoided Qin Lan’s gaze and still chose to refuse.

"Xin!" Qin Lan felt a surge of frustration. She was already familiar with Chen Xin’s stubborn nature, but she never expected that even in such matters, he would still be so obstinate: "Can’t you just listen to me for once? You’re all alone here, and if something happens, no one would even know if you died!"

As soon as the words left her mouth, Qin Lan immediately regretted them. They were unnecessarily hurtful.

However, just as she was about to remedy the situation, Chen Xin raised a hand to stop her: "Lan, there’s no need to say more. I understand your good intentions, but... I really don’t want to go to the official shelter.

Just assume I’m unwilling to part with the refuge I’ve painstakingly created. If I’m truly destined to die here, then let this place serve as my grave."

"Xin..." Qin Lan wanted to persuade him further, but Chen Xin clearly wasn’t interested in continuing the conversation.

Chen Xin shook his head, stood up, and picked up his clothes: "Lan, you sleep inside tonight. There’s an electric blanket on the bed, don’t catch a cold."

Saying that, he walked into the bedroom, brought out a quilt, and also took out the electric heater originally placed in the bedroom.

Seeing Qin Lan still sitting naked on the sofa, Chen Xin sighed at her: "Can we talk about this tomorrow? I’m really tired now. Can you let me sleep?"

Chen Xin’s words made Qin Lan feel a wave of irritation, but she contained her emotions, picked up her clothes from the sofa with a hint of anger, and walked into Chen Xin’s bedroom.

Watching the door slam shut with a "bang," Chen Xin scratched his cheek and felt utterly helpless.

After putting his clothes back on and pulling out the sofa to make it into a bed, only then did Chen Xin lie down again.

This sofa could be pulled out to turn into a single bed. Chen Xin hadn’t really expected it to fulfill this function, yet now it had genuinely become his bed.

Though he had just told Qin Lan he was going to sleep, in reality, Chen Xin wasn’t the least bit sleepy at this moment.

"Ah~!" He let out a long sigh, feeling that there truly was a discord between him and Qin Lan.

The reason they broke up back then was the same. Qin Lan believed she was doing what was best for Chen Xin, considering his interests. However, Chen Xin preferred to choose what he liked.

When neither could agree, it naturally led to quarrels. If only one of them were willing to take a step back, perhaps there wouldn’t be so many issues.

But neither Chen Xin nor Qin Lan were the type to back down. When two strong-willed people clash, it only leads to more conflicts, eventually resulting in a breakup.

Of course, Chen Xin understood Qin Lan was looking out for him, but sometimes, people’s choices aren’t necessarily what is best for them.

It’s like everyone knows drinking too much soda is bad for health, but who can resist the joy brought by soda and chips?

Moreover, for the current Chen Xin, staying in his self-made refuge was undoubtedly the best option, due to his unique circumstances. But this was something he couldn’t explain to Qin Lan.

Thus, it became inevitable for Chen Xin to refuse Qin Lan’s good intentions.

In this matter, as long as Qin Lan was willing to compromise and move in to live with Chen Xin, all problems could be resolved.

However, both knew that wasn’t possible, just as it was impossible for Chen Xin to move to the official shelter.

Turning over, Chen Xin’s feelings for Qin Lan and his attraction to her body made it difficult to let go of this woman who used to be his girlfriend, yet he couldn’t abandon everything he had for her.

The overwhelming emotions and thoughts caused Chen Xin to toss and turn, leading to complete insomnia.

————————————————

Outside the door, Chen Xin couldn’t sleep; inside, Qin Lan was no different.

If Chen Xin couldn’t let go of her, how could she let go of Chen Xin?

"Damn you, Chen Xin! Why can’t you just listen to me for once!" Qin Lan pounded her fist on the pillow, feeling utterly frustrated with Chen Xin’s stubbornness.

If she didn’t love him dearly, why would she go out of her way to come and persuade him like this?

But beneath her frustration, Qin Lan still couldn’t let go of her feelings for Chen Xin.

"Forget it. If you were so easily persuaded, you wouldn’t be you." Qin Lan shook her head helplessly, inhaled the scent of Chen Xin on the pillow and bedding, and buried her face in the pillow filled with his smell, deciding to persuade him again tomorrow.

A whole night passed without a word. The next morning, when Qin Lan opened her eyes, she had calmed down and reminded herself to control her temper and have a good talk with Chen Xin today.

However, when she got dressed and came out of the bedroom, the living room sofa was already devoid of Chen Xin’s presence.

The meticulously folded blanket and the breakfast on the coffee table indicated Chen Xin had gotten up early.

She walked over to the coffee table, touched the breakfast, and found that it was still warm, causing a warmth to flow through her heart.

"At least you remembered to make me breakfast. You do have a conscience," she snorted before heading to the bathroom to wash up.

Despite the limited supplies, Chen Xin had still placed a new toothbrush and towel on the washbasin.

Seeing that Chen Xin had prepared everything, a nostalgic look appeared in Qin Lan’s eyes. In the past, when she was with Chen Xin, he would always prepare everything for her, taking care of her meticulously without her needing to worry about her lifestyle.

But those days were gone. With a sigh, Qin Lan picked up the toothbrush and began her morning routine.

Yet, after finishing her wash and breakfast, she still didn’t see any sign of Chen Xin upon coming out of the living room.

This left Qin Lan extremely puzzled and also flooded her heart with concern, prompting her to search for Chen Xin’s presence.

However, he wasn’t in the storeroom, the greenhouse, or the utility room. Qin Lan searched every corner of Chen Xin’s refuge but couldn’t find him anywhere.

Growing anxious, she dashed towards the refuge entrance along the corridor, fearing that Chen Xin might have left to avoid seeing her.

Yet, the refuge’s main door was tightly shut, exactly as she left it yesterday. Even after she opened several doors and rushed outside the refuge, Chen Xin was nowhere to be seen, nor were there any footprints indicating his departure.

"Xin!" Qin Lan called out loudly while staring into the darkness outside, her heart sinking lower and lower.

Where could Chen Xin have gone? It was truly inexplicable and baffling to Qin Lan that he could disappear from his own refuge.

Without any footprints at the entrance, could Chen Xin have vanished into thin air?

Qin Lan weakly knelt at the entrance of Chen Xin’s refuge. The cold wind whipped across her body, chilling her to the bone and deepening the despair in her heart.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 129 - 127 Hot Water Bag
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Inside the small shelter, Chen Xin was in the midst of removing ice when he suddenly heard someone calling his name. He pressed against the slit where he had knocked off the accumulated ice to observe outside, and saw the door of his own refuge wide open with Qin Lan kneeling in the snow.

Chen Xin was a bit puzzled but still directed his flashlight towards Qin Lan.

"Why are you shouting? Running out like this early in the morning without wearing protective gear, are you trying to freeze to death? Get inside and close the door!" Chen Xin angrily shouted at Qin Lan.

It had just snowed a bit this morning, and the temperature was very low. Qin Lan, running out without any protective equipment, could become numb from the -30°C cold in just a short while. A bit longer and she could get frostbite or even freeze to death.

Seeing Qin Lan kneeling in the snow like that, it was no wonder Chen Xin was angry.

Hearing Chen Xin’s voice, Qin Lan quickly turned back, but the flashlight’s glare temporarily blinded her.

She could only shield her eyes with one hand, squinting up as she shouted, "A-Xin? How did you get up there? What are you doing?"

"I’m removing ice, hurry and get inside!" Chen Xin yelled even more irritably, his voice muffled through the face shield as he shouted at Qin Lan.

Being yelled at by Chen Xin, Qin Lan finally realized she was freezing all over. She stiffly got up from the snow, entered the refuge, and closed the door.

With the last bit of ice knocked off, Chen Xin carried his hammer back through the passage to the refuge. Seeing Qin Lan, arms shaking in the prep room, he removed his protective face mask and shouted angrily at her, "Early in the morning you lose your mind and run out like that? Weren’t you afraid of freezing to death?"

"I... I didn’t see you when I got up just now, I thought you... you went out..." Qin Lan lowered her head, feeling very aggrieved.

But seeing Chen Xin still looking angry, she rushed over and hugged him tightly: "I thought you were mad at me, that you ran out and weren’t going to talk to me..."

As she spoke, tears began to fall from Qin Lan’s eyes.

Seeing Qin Lan hugging him and crying, Chen Xin’s initial anger disappeared. He gently patted her back twice without saying anything further.

"Where did you go?" After crying in Chen Xin’s arms for a while, Qin Lan quickly collected herself. Yet, her tear-streaked face was vastly different from her usual competent and strong policewoman demeanor, making her seem a bit vulnerable.

"I went to remove ice from the vent. With the weather so cold, ice accumulates at the refuge’s vent every day. If it’s not cleared, it will get blocked," Chen Xin explained to Qin Lan, showing her the hammer in his hand.

Seeing the hammer, Qin Lan finally believed he had gone to remove ice, but she still puzzledly asked, "Then where did you go out from? Why didn’t I see you when I searched inside just now?"

To this question, Chen Xin merely shrugged helplessly, leading Qin Lan into the corridor and pointing to a passage on the wall.

"There’s actually a door here?" Qin Lan stared in surprise at the passageway, not having noticed there was a door there.

"There’s a communication antenna and vent above. I made a shelter with reinforced concrete. Due to the weather and the high humidity of the air expelled by the vent, it easily freezes and requires regular de-icing," Chen Xin explained before closing the door on the corridor wall again.

After Chen Xin closed the door, Qin Lan observed carefully and found the door to be quite inconspicuous. Though it wasn’t deliberately hidden, the corridor lighting was poor, and the door’s position was in the darkest spot between two lamps, making it easily overlooked.

This minor misunderstanding left both Qin Lan and Chen Xin feeling a bit embarrassed.

"Go inside and warm up first. I’ll take off the protective suit." Chen Xin touched Qin Lan’s hand, finding her fingers ice-cold, so he prompted her to return to the refuge: "Boil some hot water too; you might catch a cold in this cold wind."

At this moment, without saying much more, Qin Lan obediently nodded and returned to the refuge.

Chen Xin returned to the prep room, closing several doors and removing his bulky protective suit and face shield before heading back into the refuge.

Inside the refuge, Qin Lan had already put on the coat she hadn’t worn before and was warming herself by the electric heater, with water boiling on the kitchen stove nearby.

"Feeling better?" Chen Xin took the Snickers bar Qin Lan had left on the workbench yesterday and handed it to her: "This is what you brought yesterday, eat it to replenish some energy."

Chen Xin then went to the kitchen, noticing the water was nearly boiled, filled a mug from the stove, and brought it to Qin Lan.

Seeing her still somewhat pale face, Chen Xin shook his head, went into the bedroom, got a hot water bottle from the cabinet, filled it with hot water in the kitchen, and placed it inside Qin Lan’s clothes.

"What is this!" Startled by the sudden warmth stuffed against her, Qin Lan was taken aback, but soon the heat from the hot water bottle began to warm her up.

"Hot water bottle, didn’t you use one when you were little, A-Lan?" Chen Xin looked at Qin Lan, paused, then realized: "Oh, I forgot, given your age, you probably haven’t seen this kind of thing filled with hot water; you’ve likely only seen those electric heated gadgets."

Listening to Chen Xin, Qin Lan also reached into her bosom to take out the hot water bottle for a look, but Chen Xin directly held her down.

"Keep it there a little longer, warm up first." Chen Xin stopped her but quickly withdrew his hand, realizing where he’d pressed was her chest.

Seeing him retract his hand, Qin Lan was amused by his action, but she didn’t mind. Smiling, she asked, "A-Xin, why are you reluctant to go to the official refuge?"

"This question..." Chen Xin thought for a moment before answering: "Just like you wouldn’t give up your job as a special police officer to live with me here, everyone has something they hold on to. Plus, I’ve poured so much heart and effort here, and the living conditions are better than the official refuge. If you want me to move, how could I possibly agree?

If I couldn’t survive here, A-Lan, if you asked me to move now, I’d pack up and go with you without a word. But aside from having no one to talk to and lacking social interaction, I’m actually doing well. If you want me to move, how could I possibly move?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 130 - 128 Visiting Installation? (Reward Additional - )
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"But if I move in, there’s no place for me to sleep!" Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin. After this morning’s little incident, it seemed that Qin Lan was reassessing the place Chen Xin held in her heart.

Regarding Qin Lan’s question, Chen Xin just laughed and said, "If you really want to move here, of course I can find you a place to stay. And at worst, you can share the bed with me, can’t you? My bed may not be very big, but it’s perfectly fine for two people."

"What about your parents? Didn’t you say you wanted to bring uncle and aunt back?" Qin Lan asked Chen Xin a very crucial question.

The two of them talked about squeezing together in one bed. Clearly, if Chen Xin’s parents returned, it wouldn’t be possible for four people to squeeze together.

Not to mention, the bed wouldn’t fit.

"As for that problem, I can expand the shelter!" Chen Xin laughed.

With the system as support, Chen Xin truly didn’t have to worry about where others would sleep after the population increased.

Moreover, the question of whether others would notice any issues after he upgrades, if there were other people in the shelter, Chen Xin had already checked with the system last night.

The system provided the answer that others would reasonably assume it was your labor’s result.

In other words, if Qin Lan and Chen Xin both lived in the shelter, and Chen Xin upgrades the shelter or other things, Qin Lan would overlook everything just as she did during the system’s dismantle yesterday. As for the differences caused by the upgrade, she’d rationalize them as Chen Xin making improvements in a way she deemed reasonable.

As for when the population grows, the concept of informational state blurriness attached to the system upgrade would affect everyone’s memory, allowing them to "reach a consensus."

So now Chen Xin isn’t worried that his secret would be exposed after more people join the shelter.

After hearing Chen Xin say this, Qin Lan was somewhat tempted in her heart but bit her lip and gave up the idea.

Moving in to live with Chen Xin is certainly possible, but she still couldn’t convince herself to resign at this point.

"You don’t have to do this, I’ve been living alone, and it’s really fine." Chen Xin saw the hesitation on Qin Lan’s face but didn’t try to persuade her to move in together at the moment: "I’ve gotten used to living alone, but you’re different, A Lan, there’s really no need to move. And even if you do move, what about your work with the SWAT team? Can you really resign at this time?"

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s statement, Qin Lan could only lower her head.

However, she quickly lifted her head and asked Chen Xin, "So, is everything okay with you now?"

"Everything’s fine. Yesterday was just a special situation. After your treatment last night, I’m already fine." Chen Xin intentionally referred to what happened last night as a treatment and smiled at Qin Lan.

Upon hearing what Chen Xin said, Qin Lan couldn’t help but blush slightly.

After a moment of silence, Qin Lan seemed to make some decision and said to Chen Xin, "Since the treatment is effective, every month when I deliver things to you, I will ’treat’ you. Once a month; you need to consolidate the treatment."

Although Qin Lan’s words sounded quite normal, Chen Xin couldn’t help but grin mischievously.

"Once a month? Looks like I need to expand the bathroom and get a bathtub." Chen Xin touched his mouth, trying not to smile so sleazily: "That way, you can come for a hot spring bath, A Lan. I remember I invited you several times before for a hot spring bath, and now I can finally fulfill that wish."

Chen Xin’s words made Qin Lan recall the past, and she couldn’t help but feel sentimental: "At that time, I was too focused on work. Whenever you invited me, I agreed eagerly, but every time an assignment came up on short notice, that hot spring, I’ve been saying I would go for three years but haven’t managed."

"Now I have underground hot water here. Next time you come, I will get the bathtub ready so we can have a hot spring bath." Chen Xin looked at Qin Lan and made a promise.

Regarding Chen Xin’s promise, Qin Lan couldn’t help but laugh and say, "Didn’t you say this might just be temporary, A Xin? Aren’t you worried that once you prepare the bathtub, the underground water might turn cold again?"

"Then I’d just use it for a hot bath! After all, it’s the same underground water, only one is hot and the other is cold." Of course, a hot spring is not just simple underground water, but Chen Xin was too lazy to differentiate it.

Under the current circumstances, being able to bathe is already a luxury, not to mention a hot spring.

Naturally, Qin Lan wouldn’t insist on needing a hot spring, nodding to signify acceptance of the matter. fr\eewe.bn(o)v\el.c(o)m

Picking up the cup from the table, she drank some hot water and felt her body warming up. Only then did Qin Lan ask Chen Xin, "By the way, A Xin, are the geothermal heat pumps you installed easy to set up? You might need to visit the city."

"On-site installation?" Chen Xin was taken aback. He hadn’t anticipated Qin Lan mentioning this issue.

Concerning the installation of geothermal heat pumps, Chen Xin naturally understood that the main part of the equipment isn’t difficult to install; one simply needs to drill holes into the ground and secure it with bolts, not too different from an air conditioner’s external unit.

The critical issue is the piping. Besides the water piping, geothermal heat pump equipment includes underground intake pipes and heat exchanger parts.

Normally, these parts require underground construction.

The one in Chen Xin’s shelter is installed via system functionality. Can this functionality be used outside the shelter? fr\eewe.bn(o)v\el.c(o)m

Originally, Chen Xin planned to acquire a drill bit from the SWAT team through Qin Lan for others’ installation, using it to drill holes in the ground, fit the pipes, and then upgrade the equipment to realize the underground piping part.

But with the system now offering a more convenient installation method, is the drill still necessary?

While pondering these questions, the system emerged to assert its presence.

Looking at the words appearing before him, Chen Xin nod with reassurance and said to Qin Lan, "Since I sold the equipment, I’m definitely responsible for the after-sales installation."

"Mm, then you might need to stay a few days to ensure the equipment functions properly," Qin Lan’s first part was business, but the later part suddenly changed tone: "Then you can stay at my place; the City Bureau’s shelter is converted from an old air-raid shelter and might not have many rooms... But don’t worry, I have the room all to myself, and the bed is quite spacious for two..."

Before Qin Lan finished her sentence, Chen Xin grabbed her hand, watched her face blush, leaned closer, and whispered softly in her ear, "I’m only concerned about the soundproofing in your room. If someone hears you too loud, then you might really have to move in."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 131 - 129 Blade-Free Turbine
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The rekindling of old feelings between old lovers is ultimately just an interlude; survival is the theme of this apocalypse.

For Chen Xin, he no longer needs to worry about how to survive.

The emergence of hot spring water has greatly increased the heat energy available from the geothermal pump, to the extent that even if Chen Xin shuts down the equipment, the warming of the groundwater can maintain the shelter at a temperature of around 13~16℃.

If it weren’t for the need to maintain the greenhouse temperature, the geothermal pump Chen Xin just made might even be somewhat redundant.

However, this has given him a new idea, which is to use the heat from the geothermal pump to generate electricity, as he had thought before.

This is not difficult to achieve technically; it only requires attaching a heat recovery device to the heat dissipation pipe and using this heat to perform thermal energy conversion, converting it into electrical energy.

As for the means of converting thermal energy into electrical energy, a turbine commonly used in thermal power plants would suffice to complete the task.

Speaking of turbines, Chen Xin was once particularly fascinated by geek projects during his university days and had transformed a blower for an air mattress and discarded CDs into a Tesla turbine capable of powering a light bulb in his dorm room.

Although this thing had no practical significance, completely relying on the blower to drive the impeller to do work, overall, the device was indeed made, and the process of creating it was not complicated. Chen Xin still has the drawings he used back then on his computer.

The Tesla turbine is a turbine invented by the Federation’s legendary physicist Nikola Tesla.

This turbine’s efficiency is much higher than traditional blade-style turbines, and Nikola Tesla once dreamed of using it for geothermal power generation.

Of course, although this technology looks promising, it still faces numerous constraints in practical applications. Consequently, since Nikola Tesla invented it over a hundred years ago, its practical applications have remained few and far between.

The main reason restricting the promotion and application of this technology is that the rotor inside must be composed of discs that are thin enough and have smooth enough surfaces arranged closely to form a rotor large enough to allow the rotor to spin rapidly powered by the boundary layer effect of fluid flowing through it.

This undoubtedly imposes high requirements on the materials for making the discs, which must be both lightweight and sturdy.

Moreover, a bladeless turbine has a major issue where the efficiency decreases as the load increases, and to achieve a sufficiently high speed, the fluid flow through the rotor must be as small as possible.

However, none of this poses a problem for Chen Xin.

With the system’s help, making the rotor is not an issue, and all Chen Xin needs is a small generator to meet his shelter’s electricity needs, where neither the load nor the fluid flow will be too large.

——————————————

"Ah Xin, what are you making?" Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin continually processing a metal material on the workbench into thin circular metal discs, feeling very curious.

"I’m making a turbine. I plan to use the excess heat from the geothermal pump to generate electricity, which requires a turbine." Hearing Qin Lan’s inquiry, Chen Xin explained to her while not stopping his work.

Actually, Chen Xin is not the one processing these circular metal discs; but in Qin Lan’s eyes, Chen Xin appears to be doing the processing by himself.

With the system’s help, the metal material is processed into thin paper-like metal discs, assembled together, and then fitted with a main shaft in the middle, the rotor of the bladeless turbine is considered done.

Qin Lan watched Chen Xin work seriously and did not disturb him; she just leaned on the workbench next to him, quietly watching him work.

There’s a saying that men who work seriously are the most handsome, and Qin Lan’s gaze toward Chen Xin at the moment undoubtedly proves this saying.

But Chen Xin did not notice the admiration in Qin Lan’s eyes; he only placed the assembled rotor into the casing.

After assembling the outer shell of the whole turbine and checking it over to ensure there were no problems, Chen Xin then manually rotated the rotor to check its operation before connecting the casing to the gearbox.

Although the bladeless turbine’s speed is high, its torque is not large; to achieve sufficient output power, lowering the speed with a gearbox to increase the transmission shaft’s torque is necessary.

This is not a complicated process; a set of planetary gears can solve the problem.

As for the generator part, it’s even simpler, as the principle of cutting magnetic lines to generate electricity is understood even by middle school students.

Once all the parts are assembled and a generator connected to the bladeless turbine is completed, the next step is installation.

"Ah Lan, help me move it over there." The completed generator is quite heavy, so Chen Xin had to ask Qin Lan for help.

The two of them lifted the generator next to the geothermal pump and placed it down. Qin Lan curiously asked, "Where does this need to be installed?"

"Just connect the pipes to the condenser. The airflow in the pipes can absorb the heat emitted by the condenser as it passes through, causing the air to expand and form a high-speed airflow that drives the turbine’s rotor to turn." Chen Xin explained and then opened the system interface, choosing to consume survival points to install the equipment.

The system installation is much more convenient and faster than manual installation by Chen Xin, even directly routing the wiring and connecting the generator to the distribution room.

As Chen Xin adjusted the output power of the geothermal pump and simultaneously turned on the turbine’s switch, the generator started to operate.

"It actually started running!" Watching the turbine and generator connected to the condenser move as expected, Qin Lan couldn’t help but exclaim, "Ah Xin, you’re amazing!"

"Anyone who knows a bit of mechanical processing and physics can make this. It’s not a big deal." Chen Xin shook his head, not feeling proud of Qin Lan’s compliment: "If it weren’t for the appearance of hot springs underground causing the ground temperature to rise, this system’s power generation would barely be enough to keep the geothermal pump running itself.

But with the ground temperature rising now, the excess heat can convert to more electricity. If the generator’s power is a bit higher, it should be able to maintain my electricity consumption here.

I’m just worried that the increase in groundwater temperature is temporary. If the cracked groundwater layer seals up again and the temperature drops..."

Saying this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but show a little worried expression.

"Ah Xin, you’re too pessimistic. Think more positively; maybe this is permanent? You might even have a hot spring to enjoy in the future." Qin Lan laughed and encouraged Chen Xin, hoping he’d be more optimistic.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 132 - 130 Guests Are Coming
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After being comforted by Qin Lan, Chen Xin didn’t say anything more, but he still prepared for the worst-case scenario.

Optimism is necessary, but preparing for the worst is equally important.

In other words, if you’ve prepared for the worst, you can face any situation with optimism and positivity because it can’t get any worse.

And for Chen Xin, the worst scenario right now...

Is another geological movement occurring, causing the deep groundwater layer to lose pressure, and affecting the shallow groundwater layer, causing the shallow groundwater to flow into the deep layers, ultimately leaving him without any usable groundwater.

Although this is an extremely extreme situation, in geological terms, it is not impossible for groundwater layers to run dry due to geological activities.

In Chen Xin’s current situation, if a groundwater cutoff really happens, he would face the water scarcity crisis again.

While he can exchange greenhouse-grown vegetables for drinking water, the big challenge is that crop cultivation consumes a large amount of water resources.

Moreover, the vegetables contain a lot of water that does not enter the indoor circulation system of the shelter for recycling.

So, if this situation does occur, Chen Xin must find a new water source, and moving would be a last-resort option.

Fortunately, there is quite a thick layer of snow outside, and snowfalls still happen occasionally. Collecting snow and melting it for use is a good method.

However, the snow outside is very dirty, and the collected snow needs purification and filtration.

Besides, upgrading the water circulation system to drill the well deeper and increase the pumping height, so water can be drawn from deeper layers, is a one-time solution.

After going over the countermeasures in his mind, Chen Xin felt at ease with the possible worst-case scenario, as he had enough means to handle it.

This naturally made his mood much more optimistic, and he smiled at Qin Lan: "Alright, the generator is installed, and next we’ll continue with the geothermal heat pump. Your SWAT team only has one unit, and there are five more to rush out. I don’t know if we can finish them in two days."

"Xin, with your skills, you can definitely finish on time, and I’ll help out too." Having seen the "process" of Chen Xin making these mechanical devices, Qin Lan had full confidence in him.

"Alright then, Lan, help me bring the parts we cleaned yesterday..." Chen Xin was about to ask Qin Lan to dry the washed parts from yesterday when the radio in the living room suddenly sounded with a call.

Qin Lan and Chen Xin turned to look at the living room simultaneously and couldn’t help but be surprised: "Is someone calling you?"

"Yeah, I’ll go see what’s up. Lan, take out the parts we cleaned yesterday and dry them thoroughly." Chen Xin instructed Qin Lan, then walked towards the living room.

Picking up the transceiver of the shortwave radio, Chen Xin responded to the call, and after a conversation, he realized it was another fellow coming to exchange for the geothermal heat pump equipment.

After informing Qin Lan, Chen Xin picked up a gun and walked to the entrance of the shelter, while Qin Lan hurriedly put down her work and followed him.

In the preparation room, after putting on protective gear, Chen Xin still went to the small shelter first, looking outside through the observation port to see who it was.

However, he was somewhat surprised by the vehicle parked at his shelter’s entrance.

It was an eight-wheeled truck resembling a heavy-duty truck with one-meter-tall giant tires, making it ride smoothly over the thick snow.

But unlike a truck, it had a tough armored shell, and the window lights from the inside projected a cloud-patterned dragon emblem in black and red against the darkness.

No need for a close inspection, these noticeable features were enough for Chen Xin to recognize it as a modified RV and understand who it belonged to.

"Lan, don’t go outside for now." Chen Xin nodded to Qin Lan after coming down from the small shelter and then opened the shelter’s door.

Watching Chen Xin pushing open the shelter’s door and walking out, Qin Lan became tense and grabbed her waist gun, ready to rush out at any time.

Chen Xin stepped out of the shelter; the opponent hadn’t turned off the headlights, and the lights made him a bit dazzled.

So he raised his strong flashlight and shone it directly towards the driver’s seat of the vehicle, lighting up the driver’s eyes with his flashlight.

Apparently, the other party was also blinded by Chen Xin, quickly switching the truck’s headlights to low beams and jumping off the vehicle.

However, this car’s driver’s seat was quite high, and their legs plunged straight into the snow after jumping down.

"Brother Chen, why is the snow at your door so deep!" The person extracting themselves from the snow walked unsteadily and eventually reached Chen Xin.

Looking at the figure coming to his front, Chen Xin laughed, "Didn’t you feel the snow’s depth while driving here from downtown? And how many times have I told you, don’t always jump down from the driver’s seat, aren’t you afraid of breaking your legs?"

"It’s just a habit, and besides, by driving such a high-clearance vehicle, who’s got the patience to climb down!" Though masked, the person’s cheerful voice revealed his youthfulness.

Naturally, Chen Xin knew this person well. Otherwise, they would’ve addressed him as Mister Writer rather than using the familiar nickname "Brother Chen."

"Lil’ Long, why did you come to see me? Are you also looking to exchange for a geothermal heat pump?" Every time Chen Xin called this person Lil’ Long, he felt amused because of his surname Li: "Does your shelter really need these?"

"Why wouldn’t we need them?" The person urgently replied to Chen Xin, "My shelter uses air conditioning. It was fine when we had electricity, but now without a supply and relying on self-generated power, we can’t run the air conditioner!"

Hearing this, Chen Xin understood his point and nodded, "Alright, but you know the rules, materials and barter goods, have you brought both?"

"Brother Chen, don’t you know me? When have I ever been unreliable in doing things? As you said, I brought two outside air conditioning units as materials, and I also brought a cured ham and two boxes of single-use rations for you.

I originally wanted to bring Iberian ham, but my dad didn’t allow it, so I brought a Jinhua ham for you, Brother Chen, I hope you don’t mind."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 133 - 131: The Thoughts of a Rich Second Generation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After half a year since the disaster hit, only someone like Li Yulong, who is a wealthy heir, would dare to mention bringing out a leg of Iberian ham.

Li Yulong’s father is a somewhat reputable businessman in the city. He originally started in the restaurant business and later expanded into hotels, becoming one of the notable wealthy individuals in the city.

As the only son, Li Yulong naturally grew up spoiled by a wealthy upbringing. However, despite being a rich kid, he doesn’t have many bad habits and is quite easygoing.

After graduating from university, instead of idling and waiting to inherit the family fortune, he opened a 4S store in the city with his dad’s help. Chen Xin bought his car from him, and that’s how the two met.

Although Li Yulong exhibits some traits of a wealthy heir, he’s not a bad person, and he gets along quite well with Chen Xin.

Since he has money and likes to have fun, Chen Xin, who already knew Mo Qingyan at the time, took him to participate in outdoor activities organized by her a few times.

Li Yulong quickly fell in love with these activities that allowed him to escape the city and connect with nature. He bought quite a bit of equipment from Mo Qingyan, becoming a major customer of her outdoor store, and got himself the car he’s driving now.

The RV, built on the chassis of a Mercedes Arocs 8×8 heavy-duty truck, cost him more than ten million, with a luxurious interior that can accommodate 4 to 6 people—practically a movable villa.

Even though they get along fairly well, Chen Xin and Li Yulong are not really on the same wavelength. Even before the disaster, they only occasionally hung out without much involvement.

After the apocalypse, Chen Xin and Li Yulong only chatted a few times via shortwave radio, without much interaction.

As for Li Yulong’s family’s shelter, this has to do with his dad.

Back when his dad, one of the notable wealthy individuals in the city, shifted from the restaurant business to hotels, he initially started with small hotels. But as the business grew, he acquired and built several. According to Chen Xin, at least four hotels in the city are under Li Yulong’s father’s name.

Their shelter was built under their most luxurious hotel, constructed to nuclear war bunker standards, with independent water and electricity supply, and equipped with large cold storages capable of holding a considerable amount of food.

When the shelter was completed, Li Yulong invited Chen Xin to tour it, making Chen Xin naturally envious of this luxurious apocalypse bunker.

This also left a deep impression on Chen Xin about the well-equipped underground fortress.

Hence, it’s quite unbelievable to Chen Xin when Li Yulong now says his family’s shelter can hardly afford to use air conditioning and wants to trade for a geothermal heat pump.

"Brother Chen, can I come in and talk? It’s so cold standing at the door!" Li Yulong seemed to have noticed Chen Xin’s mistrust, but this rich heir appeared oblivious, naturally proposing to enter Chen Xin’s shelter.

Chen Xin glanced at the car he drove up in, discreetly felt the gun in his pocket, and finally asked, "Are you alone? No one else in the car?"

"There’s a bodyguard in the car, he’s my cousin, staying to watch the car." Li Yulong didn’t hide anything and his attitude was very sincere.

Seeing him like this, Chen Xin believed him for the time being and nodded, "Alright, come in and let’s talk."

Following his words, he moved aside, indicating for Li Yulong to enter.

Li Yulong didn’t hesitate and went straight through the door of Chen Xin’s shelter, with Chen Xin following him inside, then closing the door.

"You’re here too, Sister Qin? Have you and Brother Chen gotten back together?" Li Yulong definitely recognized Qin Lan, but he used to call her sister-in-law until she and Chen Xin broke up, after which he changed to calling her sister.

Qin Lan seemed very evasive about the topic, only asking Li Yulong, "I’m here for business with A-Xin, why are you here?"

"I’m here to trade for a geothermal heat pump, Sister Qin. You wouldn’t know, the air conditioning my dad installed in our shelter is almost too expensive to use now." Li Yulong repeated to Qin Lan the excuse he told Chen Xin, showing a helpless attitude towards his dad.

Yet neither Chen Xin nor Qin Lan believed it.

"Alright, no need to say that, we know the situation with your family’s shelter. If you say you can’t afford electricity, then probably no one in the city can." Chen Xin bluntly exposed Li Yulong and straightforwardly asked, "Come on, what are you really here for?"

Chen Xin didn’t let Li Yulong go further into the shelter and simply watched him with Qin Lan.

"Still not speaking the truth? Xiao-long, you must be ready for trouble, huh?" Qin Lan gave Li Yulong a threatening look. Though her face was partially obscured by a protective mask, her tone clearly indicated her stance.

After Qin Lan’s threat, Li Yulong had to surrender.

"Alright, I’ll talk. Sister Qin, you’re still as fierce as ever. Only Brother Chen can handle you." Li Yulong grumbled before explaining to Chen Xin, "I really am here to trade for a geothermal heat pump, but not for my family’s shelter. I’ve set up a base outside and need it for heating."

Hearing this, Chen Xin raised an eyebrow in surprise and said, "Are you just bored? Why leave a perfectly fine shelter to set up a base by yourself?"

"Haha, Brother Chen, you don’t understand." Li Yulong gave a confident smile and explained, "Living in my family’s shelter is comfortable, but it’s just for living. As you said, I was truly bored at home, doing nothing but eating and sleeping, almost becoming a pig!

Brother Chen, you know I’m a promising young man. How could I just sit idly in a shelter like my dad? So I decided to find something to do."

"Find something to do? So you decided to make a base? What’s the point of that? You already have a place to live." Chen Xin scoffed, thinking Li Yulong was simply looking for trouble.

Yet, Li Yulong was more delighted, saying confidently, "Brother Chen, you’ve got it wrong. I’m not setting up this base just to live in it. While I do plan to eventually move there, my aim in building this base is to create a trading market."

"Trading market?" Upon hearing, Chen Xin instantly understood his intention. "You mean a black market? You want to be the dealer?"

At this point, Chen Xin’s gaze towards Li Yulong had changed noticeably.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 134 - 132: How to Establish a Black Market
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Hehe, Little Brother Chen is indeed Little Brother Chen, he gets it right away, unlike my old man. It took me ages to explain what I wanted to do to him." Li Yulong complimented Chen Xin.

Qin Lan, who was beside them, understood what Li Yulong intended when she heard Chen Xin mention the black market. She frowned immediately, "Isn’t there already a black market in the city?"

Indeed, there already was a black market in the city, although it was formed spontaneously, it facilitated exchanges between various shelters.

The city government was aware of the black market but did not crack down on it.

From the city’s perspective, it was considered a channel for communication between shelters, allowing for mutual exchanges, so its existence was tacitly approved.

Li Yulong naturally knew this, but he laughed indifferently, "Sister Qin, what you’re talking about isn’t really a black market. That so-called black market in the city is just a place for a few shelters to swap things, far from a real black market. What I want to set up is a truly professional, standardized black market that can distribute goods and trade resources..."

"What you want to set up is essentially a trading platform under your control, right? Although you boast about it, I can see your intention at a glance," Chen Xin said. "You want to establish a base, meaning you need a venue that you control. You won’t participate directly in the transactions but will provide a platform for others to trade while charging a fee."

Being exposed by Chen Xin, Li Yulong couldn’t help but feel a bit embarrassed. "Look at what you’re saying, Brother Chen, I’m really just providing convenience for everyone while earning a little on the side..."

However, Chen Xin didn’t pay attention to him and continued, "To set up such a trading platform, you need significant capital and military protection, and you need to have enough credibility and prestige among those coming to trade. None of these would be an issue if your father steps in.

Additionally, for this trading platform to work, you can’t let people keep bartering, as you wouldn’t make money or provide services to others then.

For the traders, the existing black market suffices for barter, so you must offer valuations and equivalent items, meaning your real aim is to get hold of the currency issuance authority. Am I right?"

Chen Xin looked at Li Yulong, his eyes blazing with both intimidation and a little admiration for this rich second-generation.

To be able to think of these things at such times and plan to act on them, Li Yulong’s ambition and capability are noteworthy.

But Qin Lan, beside them, still frowned and confusedly asked Chen Xin, "If that’s the case, the city government won’t allow such a trading market to exist, as it completely escapes state control. Even if the city doesn’t intervene, what will he use as an equivalent medium, and why would anyone trust him? He can’t really use bottle caps like in the radioactive wasteland, can he?"

Faced with Qin Lan’s doubts, Chen Xin guessed Li Yulong’s thoughts but still shook his head and then turned to Li Yulong, "These questions, Sister Lan, you should ask him. But since Little Dragon came to me, I suppose someone advised him and a plan has been drafted?"

"No one is as wise as Brother Chen!" Li Yulong continued his playful compliment, but then explained seriously, "To start this black market or as Brother Chen referred to it, a trading platform, government approval is necessary, but there’s no need to worry about that as my dad will be able to persuade the city government."

Hearing Li Yulong mention his father, Chen Xin nodded in agreement, "Your father’s identity and connections should be enough to sway the city government. Although the city government is currently tolerating the black market, that doesn’t mean they won’t eventually manage it. Having someone establish a regulated market would also benefit the city.
