As for the equivalent medium, that’s easy. The country is still around, and money isn’t unusable; it’s just that there’s nothing to buy at the moment. If the city government gets involved and promises to accept money, it would remove the market trust issue with your own equivalent medium."

"Brother Chen, you’re really insightful. That’s the same thing my dad said after understanding my ideas." This time, Li Yulong was genuinely impressed with Chen Xin and did not offer empty compliments.

By now, Qin Lan had largely understood the situation and began to have some belief in the establishment of the black market but still had unresolved doubts: "If that’s the case, the black market can indeed be initiated, but the issue is that you’re setting it up to make money, and your way to earn money is through transaction fees. Why would people pay you?"

Before Li Yulong could explain, Chen Xin began laughing.

He looked at Li Yulong meaningfully and smiled at Qin Lan, "Perhaps this is why he came looking for me. Geothermal heat pumps, as a heating device, are a necessity under the current circumstances. If he is the only one able to provide them in the market, it will naturally attract others to trade at his location.

Whether bartering or paying with cash, it is more than enough for him to accumulate his initial resources and build a reputation. With initial resources and as people learn about his place, he can add more daily necessities and naturally have a functioning market."

"Hehe, that’s what my dad said too, so Brother Chen, what do you think..." Li Yulong smiled awkwardly, trying to propitiate Chen Xin.

However, Chen Xin did not respond to him at that time but turned to Qin Lan and asked her, "Lan, how does the official shelter compensate people for work now? Do they pay cash, tokens, or directly distribute living supplies?"

"The official shelter currently registers identities and monthly work hours. Those with 25 work hours can receive full rations and better living supplies at the end of the month," Qin Lan explained, though she didn’t understand why Chen Xin was asking. "Ordinary people receive 30 portions of shelter food monthly, enough to sustain one person for a day, and those who work receive the same but have two energy bars, while those not working only have one."

"Just like the ones I traded from your SWAT team." Chen Xin nodded, understanding the situation, "It seems there’s still a missing condition to make this work."

Since the official shelter is still directly distributing supplies, it’s evident that the so-called currency, or general equivalent, hasn’t formed yet.

Without the emergence of a general equivalent, market transactions can only remain at the barter level.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 135 - 133 Efficient Exploitation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Although he had confirmed Li Yulong’s plans and intentions, Chen Xin still did not allow him to enter his shelter.

He merely let Li Yulong enter the preparation room, where they could sit down and discuss the matter.

"Xiao Chen, you’re wrong to think I want the currency issuance rights, that would be such an injustice to me. The government is still around! How could such a thing be allowed for private individuals?" Despite his ambition, Li Yulong remained level-headed, not assuming that the end times allowed for any outrageous behavior: "What I want is simply the minting rights, not issuance rights, and whether this happens or not doesn’t really matter. The real core interest is that with a market, there is commercial flow."

Hearing this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but laugh: "You have a clear view; money is only money when it’s in circulation, when hoarded it can only be called production materials."

Nonetheless, while praising Li Yulong, Chen Xin remained curious and asked: "I understand you want to establish commercial flow, but the total amount of resources in the hands of survivors is limited. Without societal production, these people will eventually exhaust the wealth they control, and the market will soon run dry. How do you plan to solve this problem?"

"Xiao Chen, can you use your geothermal heat pump to build a greenhouse?" Li Yulong slyly asked Chen Yu, raising another question.

"A planting greenhouse? Of course, the geothermal heat pump provides a continual heat source, maintaining the planting greenhouse..." Chen Xin instinctively responded; halfway through speaking, he suddenly had an epiphany: "Ah, I see—plantation economy, exploiting surplus value."

Seeing Chen Xin understood his idea, Li Yulong scratched his head sheepishly: "Hehe, Xiao Chen, that’s harsh. I’m not a capitalist; I’m just trying to help others survive while gaining a little extra for myself."

Both Chen Xin and Li Yulong understood what the other was saying, but Qin Lan found it somewhat confusing.

She could comprehend what Chen Xin meant when he said the market would soon dry up—demand determines value, and what survivors need most now are various survival commodities, which are essential needs and the most valuable wealth.

However, the survival resources that each survivor possesses are limited and will gradually be consumed over time.

It’s similar to how everyone’s money is limited; if you can’t earn more, the existing money will eventually be spent.

While she understood what Chen Xin meant by market drying up, Qin Lan still couldn’t grasp what he and Li Yulong meant by the planting greenhouse, nor did she understand the plantation economy and surplus value exploitation Chen Xin mentioned—content she had only heard in high school politics class, leaving her bewildered.

"Ah Xin, what are you talking about?" Not understanding, Qin Lan directly asked Chen Xin about her confusion.

"Sister Qin, we’re not discussing much, just that I want to build a planting greenhouse and need Xiao Chen to provide a geothermal heat pump." Li Yulong hastily explained, but it was evident he was trying to mislead Qin Lan, hoping she wouldn’t understand the true motives.

However, Chen Xin glanced at him and began explaining to Qin Lan: "Ah Lan, you should understand that for a market to have commercial flow, there must be a constant supply of goods for trading, right?"

"Of course, if there are no goods for trading, there’s no commercial flow. I understand that principle." Qin Lan nodded, indicating her understanding.

Seeing Chen Xin begin to explain to Qin Lan, Li Yulong’s expression turned ugly, but he held back the urge to speak, merely keeping a sullen face as Chen Xin explained his intentions to Qin Lan.

"Since you can understand this, Ah Lan, you should realize that given the current environment, the resources in survivors’ hands are limited. Aside from the official capacity to produce new resources, other survivors practically lack production capacity right now," Chen Xin continued, perceiving Li Yulong’s change in demeanor: "Therefore, if you truly want to create a trade platform and establish commercial flow, the supply becomes very important.

Currently, for all survivors, the most valuable items are survival resources, particularly food and heating.

The geothermal heat pump I can make serves as heating equipment, and once Xiaolong gets this equipment from me, he can sell it to survivors in need, but that’s just a one-time deal, yielding limited profits.

To earn more, Xiaolong plans to use the geothermal heat pump to create a planting greenhouse, employ survivors to work for him, produce more food, and then sell this food to survivors, ensuring an endless source of profit."

"But in that case, why would everyone buy his food?" Qin Lan understood Chen Xin’s explanation but had a new question: "Can’t they also obtain food by working at the official shelters?"

"Good question!" Chen Xin praised her, resembling a skilled orator pleased with an engaged audience, as he stood up to explain: "If it’s simply about growing food to sell, then there’s no need for a trade platform; just selling the food suffices.

But only primitive and foolish slave owners would use such an inefficient method to make money. A smart capitalist employs more effective means to profit.

If I were Xiaolong, I would hire a group of people to work for me, and after growing food, I would distribute more food than they need as ration, and this part becomes excess production material, evidently usable in trade.

With tradable resources in workers’ hands, commercial flow starts, but that’s still insufficient, as all production resources only circulate in a closed loop—just from me to the workers, then back to me.

This way, I can’t gain profits, so I need to introduce another trader between me and the workers, namely other survivors."

Chen Xin continued to explain to Qin Lan, gesturing and pointing at Li Yulong: "Suppose Xiaolong is another survivor, and Ah Lan, you’re my worker. The food you grow each day far exceeds what you need to eat.

Even though I ’generously’ give you enough food for two people, the total food you produce still greatly exceeds the wages I give you, and the excess is the surplus value I exploit from you.

At this point, I can take this surplus value and trade it with other survivors like Xiaolong, exchanging food for the resources I need from him, thus enhancing market activities.

But that’s not all, as the resources I exchanged from Xiaolong far exceed my survival needs. At this point, I can sell these resources to you, Ah Lan.

However, this way is too inefficient. I can have Xiaolong sell these resources directly to you, allowing the food you have beyond your survival needs to participate in market flow.

Thus, a three-party trade market forms. By maintaining the planting greenhouse and trade platform’s existence, I can continuously achieve new profits. This is the most efficient exploitation method—the gains appear generous, but in reality, you’re left with nothing."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 136 - 134: Why Cooperate
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin’s explanation made Qin Lan’s eyes widen. Once she understood what Chen Xin was talking about, she finally realized what Li Yulong was up to, which made her glare angrily at Li Yulong, even tempted to pull out her gun and shoot him right away.

"Sister Qin, don’t look at me like that! Brother Chen is exaggerating; this is just normal business activity," Li Yulong felt a chill down his spine under Qin Lan’s gaze and hastily explained to her, "Besides, by doing this, I can help more people survive! You can’t just kill a businessman because he’s making money, can you?"

"A businessman earns money through labor, but what have you put into this endeavor? You can earn money lying on your back!" Qin Lan was somewhat angry; after all, Flame Country isn’t a Western-style capitalist society and has an instinctive aversion to such exploitation methods.

However, before Li Yulong could explain, Chen Xin patted Qin Lan’s shoulder to signal her to calm down.

"It’s not that extreme, Ah Lan, you’re overreacting." Chen Xin soothed Qin Lan’s feelings, then continued, "Although the essence of the matter is what it is, as Xiaolong said, by doing this he indeed can allow more people to live, and it’s not genuine exploitation."

"How do you explain that?" Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin, not understanding why he still supported Li Yulong even after seeing through the essence of what Li Yulong planned.

"It’s the apocalypse now, for survivors, having a job that allows them to earn rewards and ensures they have enough food to survive is superior to anything else." With a helpless expression on his face, Chen Xin tugged at his mouth to smile slightly before continuing, "Though it looks like Xiaolong hasn’t put much effort into it, once the entire model starts operating he can continuously derive profit from it. But, Ah Lan, think about it this way: if Xiaolong didn’t create this business circulation, how do you think other survivors would continue living?

The food in their hands is limited, after it’s consumed, they might seek help from the official refuge, hoping to get a meal there. But what if they can’t make it into the official refuge?

Once their food is gone and there’s no heating equipment, they’ll just freeze to death or starve.

Xiaolong’s plantation, while seeming exploitative, admittedly does allow more people to survive, and this can be considered normal business activity.

After all, the greenhouses are Xiaolong’s investment in production materials; you can’t say an entrepreneur who hires workers to produce products should not only pay wages but also distribute all the profits they earn to the workers, right?"

With Chen Xin’s explanation, Qin Lan finally realized she had been misled by Chen Xin.

Even though what he said was true and indeed the essence of the matter, from another perspective, Li Yulong hadn’t actually done anything wrong. Instead, he was earning money through his own skills.

Despite the appearance that Li Yulong was really exploiting others, without his so-called "exploitation," the whole business circulation cycle wouldn’t exist.

Without the output from the greenhouse, there wouldn’t be necessary food to sustain others’ lives; without the trading platform, people’s resources couldn’t be exchanged, and they would struggle to survive with limited supplies.

But instead of merely struggling for survival with limited resources, they could, once this entire model is operational, obtain necessary resources to sustain themselves and live better.

Moreover, just as no one assumes that a worker sees the profits from the business’s product sales as their own after receiving their wages, Li Yulong employs these workers for farming, and the greenhouse, crop seeds, and other inputs belong to Li Yulong’s production materials. The value generated should belong to him rather than to the workers who only provided labor.

Workers put in labor and receive remuneration according to their work, while the remaining profits rightfully belong to Li Yulong.

This would be true even if Flame Country weren’t a capitalist society, otherwise people like Erma and Wang wouldn’t have appeared.

"As expected of Brother Chen, always seeing things so clearly!" Li Yulong swiftly praised Chen Xin, his face again displaying a flattering smile, "So, Brother Chen, about this ground source heat pump, could you..."

Facing Li Yulong’s flattering demeanor, Chen Xin countered with a question, "Since I understand all this, why should I sell you the ground source heat pump?"

"Uh..." Li Yulong stuttered; he hadn’t anticipated Chen Xin would ask such a question.

"The ground source heat pump is mine, I can establish the greenhouse myself, and I can also collaborate with Ah Lan and the special police team to employ people from the refuge officially to plant crops and cooperatively establish a trading market with the authorities." Chen Xin looked at Li Yulong, grinning mischievously, "I possess the ground source heat pump, and you don’t; moreover, I understand the principles and operations behind it, so I can entirely build such a system bypassing you. Why should I partner with you?"

Hearing Chen Xin’s question, Qin Lan also looked at Li Yulong with interest, curious to see how he’d respond.

"Uh..." Li Yulong was momentarily at a loss for words; as Chen Xin said, he could indeed implement everything Li Yulong envisioned bypassing Li Yulong, but Li Yulong couldn’t bypass Chen Xin.

Yet, since Li Yulong could come up with such an idea, he was indeed clever and talented. After pondering for a moment, he told Chen Xin, "Brother Chen, you really know what to do, and you own the ground source heat pump as a resource, but you’re just one person. If you handle both the production of the ground source heat pump and establishing a trading market, you can’t manage everything alone.

You need to collaborate with someone, right? Instead of partnering with others, why not partner with me? I have my dad’s resources and consider myself quite capable as well. With my cooperation alongside my dad’s resources, the efficiency of establishing the entire business circulation would be much higher than if Brother Chen worked on it solo, wouldn’t it?"

As he continued explaining, Li Yulong also organized his thoughts clearly, and his persuasive words to Chen Xin flowed more smoothly, "The world has entered a new era; though it’s an apocalypse, it’s also a sign that everything old is destroyed, and new opportunities abound. At times like this, how does solo effort compare to unity and collaboration? So, Brother Chen, partner with me!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 137 - 135: The Next Step Plan
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Li Yulong’s original purpose was certainly not to seek cooperation with Chen Xin, but simply to exchange for a geothermal heat pump from him.

However, it was evident that his plans had been thoroughly discerned by Chen Xin, and Chen Xin’s counteroffer put him in a highly passive position.

Yet, even though Chen Xin saw through Li Yulong’s ideas and motives, after Li Yulong’s persuasion, he agreed to cooperate with him.

Leaving behind the goods he brought for exchange, Li Yulong departed contentedly. Even though Chen Xin did not immediately give him the geothermal heat pump equipment, gaining his promise to cooperate was worth giving these items for free as far as Li Yulong was concerned.

After Li Yulong left, Chen Xin and Qin Lan returned to the shelter to continue their previous work.

"Ah Xin, why did you agree to Xiao Long’s proposal and decide to cooperate with him?" Qin Lan asked curiously as she handed him a wrench: "As you said, you could completely work with the SWAT team and bypass him to do this. Why choose to cooperate with him?"

Chen Xin accepted the wrench and tightened a component before answering Qin Lan’s question: "Because I’m lazy and don’t want to be directly involved in this matter. Establishing a marketplace requires doing too many things; it’s tiring and laborious, and entirely unnecessary for me."

As he answered Qin Lan’s inquiry, Chen Xin picked up another component and began attaching it to the equipment being assembled.

This was a geothermal heat pump being assembled, as there wasn’t a system blueprint, Chen Xin could only assemble it manually. The system at this time could only provide component refurbishments and specification adjustments, enabling him to put these parts together without failing due to a mismatch in size.

"Also, each profession has its expertise. I don’t believe that I, a book writer, can manage a market better than Xiao Long, who comes from a merchant family and inherits his father’s trade." Chen Xin explained while keeping his hands busy: "As for cooperating with the SWAT team, do you truly think they’re suitable for handling this?"

Qin Lan wasn’t a fool. Chen Xin had spoken so clearly, of course, she could understand his meaning.

"Indeed, the SWAT team isn’t suitable for intervening in black market affairs. This must be done only through cooperation with the city government." Qin Lan nodded in agreement with Chen Xin’s assessment.

The interests related to the black market and the impact it generates are immense; it can even be said that it influences the city’s structural dynamics significantly.

If it were a post-apocalyptic scenario like portrayed in movies where the nation has collapsed, anyone who controls such a black market could even be said to indirectly manipulate the entire city.

But under the current circumstance, where the government has not collapsed and can ensure the municipal control over the city, this must be, and can only be, done by the city government, though the SWAT team can partake in some of the benefits.

However, if the SWAT team were to undertake this, it would genuinely become a showdown between the SWAT team and the city government.

Understanding these relationships, Qin Lan no longer pursued the inquiry. Although Chen Xin could indeed go through the city government with the SWAT team’s guarantee to accomplish this, partnering with others is the best approach if he chooses not to do it himself.

"Yes, that’s essentially the case. I am not familiar with the city government personnel. Even if I work with them through your SWAT team, I’d still be the weakest among the three forces, requiring enormous energy and effort to maintain my interests, and I don’t have time or energy for such matters." Seeing Qin Lan understood his strategy, Chen Xin revealed his upcoming plan to her: "My next intention is to go and bring my parents back; compared to this, all else is secondary."

"Ah Xin, you’re going to fetch Uncle and Aunt back? How are you planning to go? Do you need my assistance?" Qin Lan became concerned upon hearing Chen Xin mention fetching his parents.

"Can you leave the SWAT team for this?" Chen Xin looked up at Qin Lan, noting her determined expression, then said: "In theory, using the currently operational railway to travel by train across four provinces to Anhui Province is the most time and energy-efficient method, but it’s probably hard to get a train ticket now, let alone bringing my parents back from Anhui Province.

The critical point is that my parents and relatives are in their mountain hometown, where it would take about an hour driving even before the snow, most likely now blocked by snow. Even if I could take the train to Anhui Province, fetching my parents would be troublesome."

Understanding Chen Xin’s explanation, Qin Lan nodded, realizing that if even the access roads near Chen Xin in the suburban area became much less navigable due to snow, it would undoubtedly be immensely challenging to drive into his so-called hometown.

"Do you plan to drive yourself there? But your car..." Qin Lan wasn’t optimistic about Chen Xin’s SUV.

Chen Xin rightly nodded, confirming: "My car definitely won’t do; to drive across four provinces, it has to be something like Xiao Long’s vehicle at least."

"So you cooperated with Xiao Long intending to swap for his car?" Qin Lan inferred his motive from what Chen Xin stated.

"It’d be great if I managed to swap for it, but I prefer to refit my own vehicle. His is a luxury RV suited for enjoyment, not quite right for such environments." Chen Xin shook his head. Despite coveting Li Yulong’s vehicle, he didn’t necessarily aim to get it.

Li Yulong’s RV, converted from a truck chassis, was designed for luxury and comfort, offering delightful living facilities but lacking measures to counter today’s extreme harsh environments.

Driving it around the city is one thing, but for long-distance journeys, it becomes less appropriate.

Chen Xin favored modifying a similar vehicle with preparations and protection against severe conditions and then using such a vehicle to fetch his parents.

For such a plan, Chen Xin would inevitably require substantial resources, which he could trade through the SWAT team, albeit less efficiently and slowly.

Hence, another reason for collaborating with Li Yulong; he could gather resources via Li Yulong instead of laboriously building a marketplace to collect them gradually.

Also, before setting off to fetch his parents, Chen Xin must first upgrade the shelter to accommodate more people.

Otherwise, without accommodation for his parents after they arrive, it’d be extremely awkward.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 138 - 136: Entering Downtown
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Two days later, the truck from the city stopped steadily at the entrance of Chen Xin’s refuge.

With Qin Lan’s "help," a total of six geothermal heat pump units had been completed, so Chen Xin would go with Qin Lan to the city this time to help with the installation of the geothermal heat pumps.

"Sister Qin, we’re here to pick you up." Two young special police officers jumped off the truck and greeted Qin Lan, who stood at the entrance of the refuge to welcome them.

"Thank you for your hard work. The items are inside. Let’s load them onto the truck first." Qin Lan nodded, not bothering with pleasantries as she instructed the two officers to start loading the equipment. She didn’t forget to introduce: "This is Chen Xin. He will join us later to help install and debug this batch of equipment."

Upon hearing Qin Lan’s introduction, the two officers greeted Chen Xin before starting to load the equipment.

Six geothermal heat pump units were not easy to manage. Despite coming with a fairly large truck, fitting six units and pipeline parts onto the truck was not an easy task.

With the two special police officers carrying the equipment, along with Qin Lan and Chen Xin’s assistance, they made six trips back and forth before moving all the equipment into the truck.

"Sister Qin, there are only two seats in the truck. You and Mr. Chen can sit in the front, and I will watch the equipment in the back." Seeing all the equipment loaded, one of the officers volunteered to stay in the back.

This type of truck has a single row in the cabin. While squeezing in three people would be fine, four would be too tight.

Since someone volunteered to go into the back, Qin Lan and Chen Xin did not object. They nodded and climbed into the driver’s cabin, while the officer headed for the rear compartment.

Once everyone was seated, the special police officer responsible for driving started up the engine, turned the steering wheel, and drove the truck towards the city.

The last time Chen Xin went out was after the snowstorm to Mo Qingyan’s home, and it hadn’t been long since, but the road conditions were even worse than when he last went out.

Strong winds howled, with large snowflakes slamming directly onto the windshield, while everything by the roadside was covered in thick snow. Only the scorched remnants of buildings still stood, but they too were covered in snow.

Don’t get it wrong; what hit the windshield wasn’t new snowfall but snow blown up by the wind before it was firmly frozen.

Watching the grayish-black snowflakes hit the windshield making soft knocking sounds, quickly swept aside by the wipers, Chen Xin couldn’t help but remark, "The weather is getting worse now!"

"Yeah, the wind is getting stronger. We don’t dare drive fast when patrolling, fearing a slide caused by the wind." The officer driving echoed Chen Xin’s sentiment.

Hearing this, Chen Xin asked in confusion, "What about the city’s people going out usually? Surely not everyone stays inside the refuge, right?"

"People in the refuges generally don’t leave them. Only special police officers like us who need to go out for tasks and search teams looking for supplies frequently go out." The officer driving explained, with a hint of hardship in his tone: "It’s better for us on patrol with vehicles, at least we’re not exposed to the cold, but it’s tough enough. Those search teams really suffer, braving wind and snow to scavenge ruins just to find usable items."

Saying this, the officer couldn’t help but shake his head.

Next to him, Qin Lan and Chen Xin both felt the sympathy, with Qin Lan herself being one of the patrolling officers, naturally understanding the difficulties.

Despite discussing this, he quickly changed the topic: "As for the transportation issues between various refuges, it’s better now. Ever since the apocalypse hit, the city has been digging tunnels. Now the underground passage between the municipal bureau and city hall is open, and the one to the shopping center side has also been dug through. Currently undergoing final repairs, it should be completed soon.

With these underground passages, it’s unnecessary to brave the elements outside when traveling between refuges. Tunnels connecting other refuges are also being gradually built and opened, soon forming an underground transport network.

If the city had a subway, we’d have a ready-made transport network by relying on the subway lines."

Upon hearing this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but raise his eyebrows, showing a surprised expression: "Already dug through? It seems the city’s construction efficiency is high!"

"Oh, saying that! It’s the apocalypse, with plenty of idle workforce in the refuges, digging the tunnels gives them something to do, preventing trouble from idleness." The officer shook his head immediately, seemingly quite empathetic about this.

Next to him, Qin Lan chimed in, "There are too many people and too few tasks in the refuges now. If most people aren’t kept busy, idleness can easily breed various social issues."

Qin Lan spoke with emotion, having dealt with many such issues within their special police team over the last few months.

Of course, Chen Xin understood the problem: idle people cause trouble. That’s why street wanderers make mischief—they’re bored.

If put to work from dawn till dusk with no free time, each one would settle down.

The three continued chatting as they proceeded. Although the truck wasn’t fast, it didn’t take long to enter the city.

Chen Xin turned his gaze outside the window, and by chance, he suddenly glimpsed a light shining from one of the buildings by the road.

He surprisedly asked Qin Lan, "Are those survivors?"

"Yes, they should be independent survivors," Qin Lan nodded.

"Are there many like them?" Chen Xin found it rather incredible.

Although he knew there must be people in the city surviving outside official refuges, he originally thought those were few.

After all, even he and his survival group prepared well enough beforehand to survive.

Qin Lan merely shook her head, "There are quite a few. Before the temperature dropped, we transferred a batch of survivors across the city into the official refuge system, receiving many requests for help. But still, many are surviving in the city in family units or small groups."

At this point, Qin Lan couldn’t help but glance at Chen Xin, saying, "If Xiao Long really wants to set up a black market, he could certainly do so since there are quite a number of survivors exchanging supplies in the city."

Chen Xin nodded in understanding, and at this moment, the truck drove into the former courtyard of the city public security bureau.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 139 - 137: City Bureau Shelter
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

This wasn’t Chen Xin’s first time at the City Public Security Bureau, but the bureau now looked vastly different from how Chen Xin remembered it.

The glass curtain walls of the office building were all shattered, with only a few remnants left to hint at their original appearance.

However, the bottom few floors seemed to have undergone some maintenance; at least the broken windows were covered up, and there was a faint light visible, indicating people were still inside.

It was unclear whether these were survivors who couldn’t fit in the shelter below, or police officers still working.

Chen Xin didn’t ask any questions, and the car quickly drove into the underground parking garage.

Although the parking garage’s first level wasn’t part of the protective shelter according to its construction standards, and it didn’t have heavy protective doors installed, the fire curtains were still lowered to block the cold wind from outside. This reduced heat loss from the shelter below and prevented the intrusion of some harmful substances.

This setup made vehicle entry somewhat inconvenient, requiring the already frozen fire curtains to be lifted before accessing the garage.

After entering the garage, the SWAT driver didn’t drive further down. Instead, he parked the car in a parking space.

Chen Xin and Qin Lan got out of the car together, and the SWAT driver went to the back of the vehicle to open the compartment door, freeing another SWAT member who had been guarding the equipment inside.

"The whole journey was really a struggle." The SWAT member getting out of the compartment stretched his limbs, feeling somewhat stiff.

The compartment didn’t have proper seating like the driver’s cabin, making the journey tiring indeed.

As the four were getting out of the vehicle, Uncle Ding, who had received news of their return, came out of the nearby elevator with others.

"Captain! Commissar!" Seeing Uncle Ding approach, Qin Lan and the two SWAT members quickly greeted them.

"It’s been a tough trip. Did you bring back all the equipment?" Uncle Ding returned the salute and nodded to Chen Xin, then inquired about the equipment on the vehicle.

Qin Lan quickly nodded and affirmed, "We brought back six units of ground source heat pump equipment, along with all the piping components. They’re all on the vehicle."

The SWAT member who had been in the compartment added, "All six units are intact. I watched over them the whole way; no damage."

Hearing Qin Lan and the SWAT member’s responses, Uncle Ding and the commissar beside him glanced into the compartment, smiling upon seeing all six units inside.

"Xiao Qin, you’ve worked hard these days! Rest well now that you’re back." The commissar said kindly to Qin Lan, then turned to Chen Xin with an even wider and friendlier smile. "This must be Comrade Xiao Chen? We really owe you a lot, not only for providing the equipment but also for coming to install them yourself. I’m sorry for the trouble!"

"It’s nothing. You’ve been taking care of me with your SWAT team; I’m just here to help install something—no big deal." Chen Xin responded, a bit awkwardly to the commissar’s kindness, politely saying a few words before falling silent.

The commissar didn’t show any displeasure at Chen Xin’s reticence, still nodding with a smile. "Regardless, since you’re here as a guest, we ought to show you around. Why not tour the place with Xiao Qin, and then have lunch together? We don’t have much to offer here, except some vegetables we got from you in exchange. Hope you don’t mind."

"Not at all, not at all. In these times, just having something to eat is good enough." Chen Xin smiled and nodded, not taking the commissar’s politeness seriously, and stepped back behind Qin Lan.

Meanwhile, Uncle Ding already started calling over his people to unload the equipment from the car.

Seeing them start to unload, Chen Xin reminded Uncle Ding, "Uncle Ding, these pipes and equipment are numbered. Please ask everyone not to mix up the order when carrying them."

"Got it, I’ll make sure they pay attention," Uncle Ding nodded, turning to his team and saying, "Did you hear that? Be careful not to mess up the order of the items."

Watching Uncle Ding finish instructing the others, Chen Xin nodded reassuringly.

Qin Lan then said to Uncle Ding and the commissar, "Captain, Commissar, I’ll take Chen down first."

With that, Qin Lan led Chen Xin towards the nearby elevator, followed quickly by the two previous SWAT members, as they took the elevator into the interior of the City’s shelter.

While pressing the elevator button, Qin Lan introduced the place to Chen Xin: "The shelter in the bureau has five levels. The upper three are parking and storage, while the lower two are bunkers built in the last century.

The level we were on is the first underground level. It’s mainly for parking and storing supplies, not for housing.

The second underground level marks the start of the formal shelter area. It’s suitable temperature-wise due to the protective and retrofitted insulation layers, though no one lives here. The bureau’s administrative units are situated here for daily operations and city bureau maintenance.

Starting from the third underground level is where people actually reside. Our SWAT team’s living quarters are on the fourth underground level, in the old bunkers."

As Qin Lan spoke, the elevator reached the fourth underground level.

Following Qin Lan out of the elevator, they passed through a corridor clearly meant to be an elevator lobby, entering a fairly spacious hall.

"This was built as part of the Third Line strategy during the Cold War peak, designed to withstand nuclear attacks, and was sealed until this disaster made its reuse necessary," Qin Lan explained while leading Chen Xin through the hall with its period-specific style, followed by the two SWAT members into a passage beside the hall. "This central hall connects different areas of the bunker, serving as an auditorium in the past. Now it’s more of a transit hub."

"This bunker is huge!" Chen Xin remarked, glancing at the passage while following behind Qin Lan, unable to hide his amazement.

Qin Lan nodded, confirming, "The entire underground bunker was constructed intermittently from the ’70s to the early ’90s, stopping only then. The space is large enough to accommodate all the bureau’s officers and families. Our SWAT team only occupies one area, yet it’s almost like a full-sized shelter."

As Qin Lan spoke, they passed through the passage and entered the SWAT team’s designated area.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 140 - 138 Qin Lan’s Room
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Qin Lan spoke as she and Chen Xin passed through the corridor and entered the area where the special police unit was located.

"This is my room, the hallway outside is somewhat noisy, but it’s okay when the door is closed at night. I’m not here during the day, so it’s not much of a bother." Qin Lan let Chen Xin into her room, her face showing slight embarrassment.

Chen Xin didn’t mind much. On their way here, although the residents were all families of the special police unit, the noise from children running in the hall and the ropes stretched out to dry clothes were unavoidable.

Even in the apocalypse, the faces of children were marked with worry, their cries exceeding their laughter, so Chen Xin didn’t mind Qin Lan’s so-called "slightly noisy."

Before the apocalypse, Chen Xin would have disliked such a noisy environment due to his profession and personality, which made him especially averse to noise. Otherwise, he wouldn’t have moved from the city center to the suburbs.

But now it’s the apocalypse, everyone is crowded into shelters, and noise is unavoidable.

Moreover, Chen Xin had been cooped up in his own shelter for months, with no one to interact with or disturb him. After months, he found his shelter too quiet.

Listening to the sounds around him, Chen Xin felt a sense of life.

"It’s okay, given the current circumstances, having such conditions is already good." Chen Xin entered Qin Lan’s room and only looked around after she closed the door.

The room wasn’t big, just about ten square meters, roughly equivalent to a university dormitory.

There was a desk and a vertical single bed against the inner wall, with two chairs by the desk, one facing the desk and the other near the bed. At the foot of the bed were two double-door cabinets also against the wall.

Besides that, there was a folding round table and several folding stools in the room, all folded against the wall, making the room look fairly spacious.

Chen Xin was surprised to see a washstand that seemed outdated in the corner of the room, with two basins and two towels on top, and four lined-up thermos bottles nearby.

Qin Lan took off her coat and hung it on the hook behind the door, then took off her heavy military and police boots and put on slippers, offering a pair to Chen Xin as well: "Change into slippers, feel free to sit, even on the bed."

Chen Xin obligingly changed his shoes.

Having left his shelter, he had worn a pair of heavy boots. Outside they were fine, but indoors, with heating, they were a bit hot for his feet.

While Chen Xin was changing his slippers, Qin Lan picked up a thermos from the floor, poured some hot water into a basin, and said to him after he had changed shoes, "Come wash your face, did Ah Xin bring a face towel? If not, you can use mine."

Since deciding to stay for a few days, Chen Xin naturally wouldn’t make the mistake of not bringing a towel and toiletries. He took out his toiletries bag from his backpack and retrieved his face towel, smiling as he went to the washstand, "The last time I used this kind of washstand to wash my face was when I was in elementary school."

"Stop joking, wash your face and I’ll take you to eat." Qin Lan knocked Chen Xin lightly, urging him to hurry up.

Chen Xin smiled at Qin Lan, said nothing more, wet and wrung the towel, and wiped his face.

"Wash properly! Just wiping doesn’t count as washing your face!" Seeing Chen Xin merely wipe his face, Qin Lan was a bit displeased.

Faced with Qin Lan’s dissatisfaction, Chen Xin smiled and said, "You told me to wash my face, why aren’t you washing yours? Are you going to use my wash water? So why don’t you wash first; I just need a wipe, men aren’t that particular."

After speaking, Chen Xin put away the towel and tidied up his belongings.

Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin, slightly helpless but with a hint of pleasure, and said no more.

After tidying up, the two left Qin Lan’s room, wearing slippers, and headed to the canteen of the special police unit.

"Earlier, you said the city bureau’s shelter has five floors, but this is the fourth floor. What’s the fifth floor used for? Is it a greenhouse?" Entering the canteen, Chen Xin curiously asked Qin Lan.

Qin Lan nodded, admitting, "Yes, the fifth floor was excavated during the transformation of the shelter before the disaster. It didn’t exist before. The space is used for growing mushrooms, raising mice and earthworms, and housing oxygen machines. However, because these facilities are critical, access to the fifth floor is generally restricted."

"Oh, I see." Chen Xin showed a look of sudden realization but then had another question, "Didn’t you tell me that the official shelter distributes monthly rations? How is there food in the canteen?"

As Chen Xin asked this, he also looked at the food serving window in the canteen, where the aroma of food made him swallow a little.

"We do receive rations, but the shelter prohibits cooking or heating food in rooms to prevent unnecessary risks, so meals are heated and supplied centrally in the canteen during dining hours." Qin Lan explained to Chen Xin, seemingly worried he’d misunderstand, adding, "This isn’t an extra food allocation from the government; it’s just centralized supply.

However, each shelter’s greenhouse can retain part of its produce, which allows the canteen to offer additional dishes depending on what’s available from the shelter’s production."

Chen Xin raised his eyebrows indifferently, saying nothing further, and simply followed Qin Lan to the canteen window.

"Master, two meals, please." Qin Lan quietly spoke to the chef at the serving window, who then nodded and handed over two trays of heated meals.

Qin Lan handed one tray to Chen Xin, then joined him in carrying their trays to an empty table nearby, ready to eat.

Chen Xin looked at the contents of his tray, surprised.

The food wasn’t different in ingredients from the shelter’s standard foods, but the chef in the canteen had obviously reprocessed them, making them look much more appealing, and the side dish of mushroom stewed mouse meat made the meal seem more enticing.

A feeling of hunger creeping in, Chen Xin didn’t hesitate any longer, picked up his chopsticks, and began eating.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 141 - 139 On-site Investigation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When Chen Xin and Qin Lan were having a meal together, Uncle Ding and the political commissar also appeared in the cafeteria.

Noticing Chen Xin and Qin Lan, they both walked over together.

"Xiao Qin, Comrade Xiao Chen, are you having a meal?" The political commissar greeted them with a friendly smile: "May I sit here?"

"No one is here, please sit down, Commissar." Qin Lan quickly invited the political commissar to sit down, while Chen Xin nodded in greeting as well.

The political commissar sat down to accompany Chen Xin and Qin Lan, though it was mainly Chen Xin speaking, and Uncle Ding went to the cafeteria window to get two meals before he also sat down.

"Comrade Xiao Chen, will you start the equipment installation this afternoon?" Although the political commissar was very polite, he did not engage in too much unnecessary small talk and quickly steered the conversation to the main topic: "Is there anything that requires our cooperation? We’ll do our best to assist with whatever you need."

"In terms of cooperation, the main point is where do you plan to install the equipment? And how do you plan to install it?" Chen Xin didn’t hold back upon hearing the political commissar’s question: "Installing a ground-source heat pump is different from other equipment. The heat exchange pipes need to be deeply buried underground, which requires drilling operations and a suitable installation location.

As for pipeline layout and heating arrangements, that’s your own planning job. I’m only responsible for equipment installation and operational adjustments."

The political commissar nodded in understanding. Although Chen Xin was in charge of on-site installation, neither had there been communication about the actual situation on site, nor about the usage requirements. They only sent over several recycled old air conditioner outdoor units. Chen Xin brought six modified ground-source heat pumps. To say that Chen Xin knew exactly how to install them would be nonsense.

However, the political commissar was not unprepared for this issue.

The advantage the official shelter has over the "dead man’s shelter" lies in having numerous personnel, so no matter what needs to be done, they can find enough manpower and professionals.

Additionally, the city had already planned projects for utilizing shallow geothermal energy and has been in continuous construction. If it weren’t for the disaster, using shallow geothermal energy for heating might have been possible after a year or so. Therefore, finding a professional to plan for heating pipelines and equipment installation poses no issue.

"Regarding this, we’ve already consulted a professional designer to do project planning, and the design drawings are ready. The main thing missing is the specifications and model of Comrade Xiao Chen’s equipment and the pipeline diameter." The political commissar introduced the situation to Chen Xin with great joy, mentioning that the drawings were in the SWAT team’s meeting room, and Chen Xin could view them after his meal.

Chen Xin nodded in understanding and assuredly replied, "It’ll be much easier with the drawings. Let’s look at the drawings after we finish eating."

The political commissar had no objections to Chen Xin’s suggestion.

While the four of them were eating, Chen Xin seemed to remember something and asked Uncle Ding, "Uncle Ding, where is Xiao Ding? Didn’t she come to eat?"

"A Ning has a task today and is on duty outside. She’ll return in the evening." Uncle Ding did not find it strange that Chen Xin asked about Ding Ning and just explained, "The city bureau is short-staffed right now, so even my daughter isn’t treated specially. She must go on duty outside."

Although Chen Xin felt there was no problem with Uncle Ding securing some privileges for his daughter, it was not his place to comment on whether Ding Ning should go out on duty, as that was their family matter and an internal police team affair.

After finishing the meal, Chen Xin followed the political commissar and Uncle Ding to the meeting room to view the drawings, while Qin Lan returned to her room.

Though they were on good terms, and the political commissar told Qin Lan to take good care of Chen Xin, as a female SWAT member, she obviously couldn’t be doing hosting duties all the time. Qin Lan had her own duties as well.

Especially since she had been staying at Chen Xin’s place for the past couple of days due to taking leave, albeit with a justified reason, her work was delayed quite a bit.

Chen Xin did not feel anything was wrong with this arrangement. After following the political commissar and Uncle Ding to the meeting room, the political commissar quickly got the drawings for him.

"You’re planning to install the ground-source heat pump equipment on this floor and connect it to the heat exchange pipe on the lower floor through the pipeline?" Chen Xin spread out the drawings and looked at the pipeline layout design. He did not suggest any modifications and merely nodded: "This piping arrangement looks fine, but I didn’t prepare enough pipes for the equipment I brought. You’ll need to handle the piping yourselves, as well as the radiators, as these aren’t included with the ground-source heat pump equipment, and I’m not in charge of installing them."

"Of course, we have those prepared. We just need to confirm the pipeline diameter for the ground-source heat pump equipment Comrade Xiao Chen manufactured, and we can arrange the piping accordingly." The political commissar assured confidently.

Chen Xin didn’t say much more but mentioned to them, "In that case, I need to check the actual conditions of the installation site. Also, installing the heat exchange pipes requires drilling. I previously asked A Lan to tell you that I need a drill. Has one been prepared?"

"Rest assured, Comrade Xiao Chen, we’ve already prepared the drill. It’s a well-drilling bit that can reach several tens of meters deep without a problem." The political commissar confidently assured, thumping his chest.

Seeing the situation was clear, Chen Xin had no more to say and expressed his intent to inspect the installation site’s on-ground condition.

The political commissar had no reason to object and personally led Chen Xin to the planned machinery room installation site, also calling in an engineer.

It was a room specially set aside for the purpose, where the six ground-source heat pump units sent by Chen Xin were temporarily stored.

Chen Xin examined the room and took out a tape measure to meticulously measure the data.

Amidst his work, his curiosity was piqued by the two large cylindrical water tanks in the room, and he asked, "What are these two water tanks used for?"

"These are heat storage tanks. In private shelters, such items might not be used, but we need them here to collect and store energy for use in heating." The engineer explained on the side, seemingly holding an odd admiration for Chen Xin.

In a post-apocalyptic environment like this, being able to modify old air conditioners into ground-source heat pumps by hand is an impressive feat for those who understand the challenges of mechanical processing and manufacturing, justifying the admiration.

Nevertheless, Chen Xin nodded after listening to the engineer’s words, saying, "That’s not entirely the case. Having such a storage tank means that apart from heating, private shelters can also have 24-hour hot water. Even if the equipment stops, the stored hot water should last for a while."

Hearing this, the engineer became highly enthused and discussed many technical details about the ground-source heat pump equipment with Chen Xin.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 142 - 140 Exchange Wine
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When dinner time arrived, Chen Xin, who had been busy all afternoon, returned to Qin Lan’s room to find her and go to dinner together.

"Ah Lan, what were you up to this afternoon?" However, Qin Lan wasn’t in her room, and Chen Xin had to wait for a while before she returned, which naturally made him curious.

"I was clearing some work I’ve accumulated over the past few days." Upon seeing Chen Xin waiting, Qin Lan felt a bit embarrassed but quickly explained her afternoon work without going into details: "Ah Xin, have I kept you waiting? Let’s go eat."

"I haven’t waited long, just got back. Let’s go grab dinner together." Chen Xin wasn’t curious about what Qin Lan did in the afternoon and simply called her to head towards the cafeteria.

With the absence of the political commissar and Uncle Ding at dinner, Chen Xin and Qin Lan felt more at ease and chatted about more casual topics.

But as they were chatting, a SWAT team member suddenly joined in, eagerly saying to Qin Lan: "Sister Lan, you’re finally back! When can you help us trade for alcohol from your friend? The guys are dying for a drink!"

"Ah! I forgo... remembered! Have you got everything ready? If so, I’ll go tonight." Qin Lan had obviously forgotten about this, but quickly covered up her slip of tongue.

Qin Lan had promised her SWAT teammates to help them find Mo Qingyan to trade for alcohol, but the task was forgotten after getting delayed at Chen Xin’s place for two days.

Luckily, this wasn’t an urgent matter, and since her SWAT teammates hadn’t gathered all the exchange items yet, it didn’t matter that Qin Lan forgot.

Now that it was brought up, Qin Lan only needed to help trade for the alcohol, which wasn’t a big deal. Mo Qingyan’s house was near the municipal office, and it was walkable even in this weather.

"We’ve had everything ready for a while, just waiting for you to come back, Sister Lan!" The SWAT member, hearing Qin Lan’s promise, didn’t mind her almost forgetting: "Since you’re going to trade the alcohol tonight, I’ll deliver the things to your place now!"

With that, the SWAT member hurriedly left the cafeteria, not caring about dinner.

Seeing him leave, Chen Xin chuckled: "Ah Lan, is your SWAT team really short on alcohol?"

"Not short exactly; it’s just that no one has had a drink for months." Qin Lan helplessly explained what had happened earlier and added: "I was supposed to help them trade for alcohol before, but got delayed at your place for two days, hence the delay."

"Oh~ Now I get it!" Chen Xin grinned mischievously upon realizing the situation.

Seeing the mischievous grin on Chen Xin’s face, how could Qin Lan not know what he was thinking? Her cheeks flushed, she kicked him under the table.

"I’m sorry, I’m sorry!" Chen Xin immediately stopped teasing her after being kicked and changed the subject: "So, are you planning to go to Dragon’s place tonight?"

"Yes, let’s go after dinner. Qingyan’s place is not far from the municipal office; we can walk there." Qin Lan nodded in agreement.

Chen Xin didn’t oppose, but while agreeing to accompany Qin Lan to Mo Qingyan’s, he thought of something else: "Is alcohol really in such short supply now? Even your SWAT team doesn’t have any?"

"It’s not completely gone, but most people haven’t had a drink since the disaster, even if some was stored before, it was used up in the first couple of months." Qin Lan explained, recalling what her teammates had told her about alcohol.

Hearing this, Chen Xin nodded in understanding. Official shelters and private shelters differ in terms of resource reserves and consumption.

"According to you, Ah Lan, then Little Dragon’s black market could have great potential!" From Qin Lan’s description, Chen Xin saw a business opportunity: "Growing grain to brew alcohol and trading that for resources could work out well."

But Qin Lan furrowed her brows: "That wouldn’t work, right? Food is already scarce, where would there be extra grain for brewing?"

"That’s not certain. To some people, you could ask them to skip a meal and they’d happily save up their grain to brew alcohol." Chen Xin knows well about some people’s passion for alcohol.

Flame Country people may not go to extremes like West Asia people drinking antifreeze or detergent for alcohol, but skipping a meal for a drink is something some would do.

Hearing Chen Xin say this, and thinking about her teammates eager for alcohol, Qin Lan couldn’t help but shake her head without further comment.

After dinner, they returned to Qin Lan’s room to gather their things, since if they decided to go to Mo Qingyan’s, they needed to put on protective gear.

However, upon returning to the room, they saw a cardboard box at the door and two SWAT team members chuckling awkwardly nearby. Qin Lan couldn’t help but shake her head: "Really? Are you worried I’d pocket your stuff?"

"Come on, Sister Lan, if you get us alcohol, this box of stuff could be a tribute to you!" One of the SWAT members hastily fawned over Qin Lan.

Qin Lan shook her head with a sigh: "Alright, leave it there, we’ll change into our gear and help you trade for alcohol. You can come pick it up later."

"Thank you, Sister Lan! Thank you, Sister Lan!" The two SWAT members kept thanking her, even offering: "Sister Lan, want us to help carry it out? The box is a bit heavy."

"No need, Ah Lan and I can carry it ourselves. Just wait to drink tonight." Before Qin Lan could respond, Chen Xin declined their offer.

Seeing this, Qin Lan merely thanked them for their kindness, and she and Chen Xin carried the box inside and closed the door.

With that, the two SWAT members could only turn and leave.

Inside the room, Qin Lan curiously asked Chen Xin: "Ah Xin, are you worried about others finding out Qingyan’s location?"

"It’s always good to be cautious. With so much alcohol at Qingyan’s, I’m worried someone might have bad intentions." Chen Xin nodded directly: "After all, it’s just her and her sister there, and if someone with ill intentions decided on them, they might not handle it."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 143 - 141 Mo Qingyan’s Malicious Intentions (Extra Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin, who had put on his protective gear again, and Qin Lan together carried away the carton that two SWAT team members had delivered just now, leaving Qin Lan’s room, ready to find Mo Qingyan for a booze exchange.

But Qin Lan didn’t lead Chen Xin out the way they came in before. Instead, she took him through the hall, went around through a passage to another elevator room, and then took the elevator to the first basement level.

"The entrance we came in through before is specially for vehicle traffic; this one is the personnel entrance and exit." Carrying the box and walking out of the elevator with Chen Xin, Qin Lan introduced the situation: "Previously, you could take the elevator directly to the ground, but now, after cooling, you can only go to the first basement level and then walk out via stairs."

Chen Xin nodded in understanding, but he curiously pointed to a deep-looking passage aside, asking Qin Lan: "Is this the underground passage you mentioned earlier? Where does this lead to?"

"That’s the way to the city hall. From here, even walking can get you there quickly. As for over there, it’s the route to the shopping center, but it’s not finished yet, so it’s unusable. Otherwise, going that way to Qingyan’s house would be faster," Qin Lan explained, pointing to another passage.

"Is there another passage leading to the surface over there?" Chen Xin was intrigued. Even if that route led closer to Mo Qingyan’s house, it wouldn’t warrant a special path; you’d still need to go from the surface.

However, Qin Lan shook her head: "Not really. It’s just that if you go through that way and reach the shopping center’s shelter, then out from there toward Qingyan’s house, it’s closer. You forgot how before you’d take the bus to the shopping center then walk over to Qingyan’s."

Qin Lan’s words made Chen Xin knock his head, lamenting: "It’s just been a few months, and I’ve already forgotten that Long’s house is closer to the shopping center."

Upon hearing Chen Xin say that, Qin Lan couldn’t help but sigh: "Things before the disaster are probably something many people are unwilling to recall, right?"

Regarding Qin Lan’s sigh, Chen Xin didn’t know how to respond. He could only carry the box together with her, leaving the underground using the stairs leading to the surface.

Once the two reached the surface, they felt the impact of strong winds sweeping past, even as they carried a box. The wind caused them to sway before stabilizing, and the box they were holding quickly gathered a layer of snow.

Chen Xin pressed his protective mask, turned on the headlamp he was wearing specifically for this, wiped away the snow and ice on the surface, and looked at Qin Lan, gesturing for her to move forward.

Qin Lan nodded, didn’t speak, just raised her hand to make an OK sign, and gestured for Chen Xin to advance.

The two walked forward, stepping through the thick snow, sinking deeply with each step.

Due to the strong wind and Chen Xin not having a radio with him, neither of them talked. They solely communicated through gestures.

However, with Chen Xin’s headlamp illuminating their surroundings, there was no risk of getting lost.

Mo Qingyan’s house wasn’t far from the city bureau’s shelter. Despite the difficulty of walking in the snow, they managed to arrive at Mo Qingyan’s home with the box in half an hour.

"Ah Lan? Ah Xin? Why on earth did you both suddenly pop over?" Upon entering Mo Qingyan’s shelter and Chen Xin and Qin Lan removing their masks, Mo Qingyan was greatly surprised.

"I came to help the SWAT team install a geothermal pump. Ah Lan’s colleagues asked her to swap alcohol, so I tagged along," Chen Xin explained to Mo Qingyan why he was there, then pushed the box he and Qin Lan had brought toward Mo Qingyan.

Qin Lan took out a tactical knife, cutting open the box, and said to Mo Qingyan: "The team wants to drink, so they pooled together these things. I’m not sure what they gathered; Qingyan, you decide. Just grab a couple of bottles of booze for me to take back to them."

Only then did Mo Qingyan understand the situation and squatted down to see what was inside the box Qin Lan opened.

"Energy bars, standard rations... basically, it’s these. They must’ve brought over any rations they had left over," Qin Lan glanced inside the box and quickly concluded.

Even SWAT team members didn’t have much to barter with in this post-apocalyptic world; these rations were likely what they saved from their own mouths.

The abundance of energy bars made that clear enough—they probably took out all their own energy bars, added additional standard rations scrapped together, and cobbled together this box.

Seeing the box full of items, Mo Qingyan found it a bit laughable, yet after counting them, she told Qin Lan: "These things are okay. Ah Lan, what kind of booze does your team enjoy? I have foreign liquor, stronger ones like vodka and whiskey. Shall I give you one... No, two bottles of Red Label."

Upon Mo Qingyan changing her mind, Qin Lan shook her head directly, saying: "One bottle is enough; after all, you mix whiskey with water. One bottle of liquor is enough for them to mess around with. No need to think you owe me face and give as extra."

Seeing Qin Lan say so, Mo Qingyan didn’t argue, merely nodded and asked Mo Qingxin to fetch the liquor.

The three sat in the living room of Mo Qingyan’s house, and Chen Xin asked Mo Qingyan: "Long, about last time we talked about the geothermal pump, how’s your consideration?"

"Hmm, I’ve thought it through. Better to install one. Wait for the weather to get a bit better, and Xiaoxin and I will dismantle the AC’s outdoor unit and bring it over to you," Mo Qingyan had indeed given the idea serious thought after Chen Xin mentioned it last time, finally deciding to install a geothermal pump.

Upon hearing her say so, Chen Xin happily nodded: "Alright, when the farmer fulfills his promise of two sets of aquaponics systems, I’ll assemble everything and send it over."

"Thanks for the food in advance." Mo Qingyan nodded, smiling, but looking at Qin Lan sitting beside Chen Xin, she deliberately joked: "Since the deal is settled, I’ll send Xiaoxin over to you afterward."

Upon hearing this, Qin Lan felt a bit strange and quickly asked Mo Qingyan: "Qingyan, why are you sending Qingxin to Ah Xin’s place?"

"Eh? Didn’t Ah Xin tell you? Besides materials, there’s Xiaoxin in the exchange deal I made with him!" Mo Qingyan mischievously looked at Chen Xin and Qin Lan, sighed purposefully: "Sigh, wanting to swap with Ah Xin, you need something good, right? I can only add my sister to the count."

Hearing Mo Qingyan say that, Chen Xin’s face turned green all of a sudden.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 144 - 142: Fire a Gun
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Of course, Mo Qingyan only joked about giving Mo Qingxin to Chen Xin.

Even with the explanations from Chen Xin and Mo Qingyan, Qin Lan still looked grumpy and shot Chen Xin several glares, clearly planning to give him a piece of her mind when they got home.

The two didn’t stay long at Mo Qingyan’s house. After getting the wine they exchanged and chatting about random interests, Chen Xin and Qin Lan left Mo Qingyan’s house.

Before leaving, Chen Xin still expressed concerns about the safety of Mo Qingyan’s home.

"Longniang, it’s just you and Qingxin here, you need to pay more attention to safety." Chen Xin advised Mo Qingyan, pointing out the biggest safety hazard in her home: "When you’re not going out, lock the elevator on the ground floor. That way, even if someone climbs into your yard, as long as the stairwell door is locked, they can’t get in."

Listening to Chen Xin’s advice, Mo Qingyan, although feeling he was a bit overly worried, still took his words seriously, nodding and indicating she would heed the warning.

Seeing Mo Qingyan take note of his concerns, Chen Xin finally felt reassured.

The two left Mo Qingyan’s house, braving the wind and snow as they walked back. Due to the wind and snow, speaking was futile as they couldn’t hear each other, so neither of them tried to shout conversations in this environment.

The entire city was severely damaged, with the streetlights long destroyed, leaving the entire city in darkness.

There might be survivors living among the ruins of the city, but the light they could muster was insignificant compared to the city’s vast darkness.

Just like fragile human lives facing this terrifying apocalypse, the weak flames could be extinguished by the cold wind at any moment.

Since Chen Xin and Mo Qingyan didn’t need to carry supplies anymore, they walked a bit faster. To avoid getting lost in this dark, snowy environment, Chen Xin took the initiative to hold Qin Lan’s hand.

Under the headlamp’s light from Chen Xin, they silently followed their previous footprints, moving forward.

Distinguishing direction in this darkness wasn’t easy. Darkness obscured vision; snow buried roads. On their way here, they relied on slowly recognizing the outlines of building ruins, combined with old memories of the roads, to find the right path.

Going back wasn’t as troublesome; the footprints they left, though blurred by wind and snow, at least helped in determining direction, guiding the way.

However, just as they could vaguely see the city bureau office building radiating light, a cry for "help" suddenly came from within the wind and snow.

Chen Xin and Qin Lan abruptly stopped, exchanging glances. Even with protective masks between them, they could still see the astonishment in each other’s eyes.

Chen Xin hesitated, unsure of whether they should check out what was happening, but Qin Lan had already drawn her gun and darted toward the direction of the cry.

Seeing this, Chen Xin gritted his teeth, pulled his own gun from his pocket, turned off his headlamp, and followed.

Through the wind and snow, they pursued the direction of the cries and arrived at a narrow alley, immediately spotting three figures beating another one on the ground.

Among the three was someone with a flashlight, allowing Qin Lan and Chen Xin to see the figures’ silhouettes and the fallen person.

Just as Chen Xin and Qin Lan rushed over, Chen Xin noticed one of them raise something in the light of the flashlight, something that looked like red.

"Stop!" Qin Lan shouted, but the wind and snow muffled the sound, or perhaps failed to make them stop.

Chen Xin saw the figure with the red object still attempting to wield their weapon.

"Bang!" Qin Lan fired.

"Bang! Bang! Bang!" After one shot, three more gunshots echoed, but Chen Xin noticed that even though Qin Lan shot at three figures, none fell. They even noticed Qin Lan and him, with the one holding the flashlight now illuminating their direction.

"Bang! Bang! Bang! Bang! Clang!"

Four consecutive gunshots rang out, and the heads of the three standing figures burst into blooms of blood. Even the red weapon that fell from the hand was shot, flying away and landing aside, not injuring the figure on the ground.

Qin Lan turned to Chen Xin, panting while holding the gun, amazed at his marksmanship but not overthinking, and quickly ran to check on the person on the ground.

Meanwhile, Chen Xin’s hands were shaking, still reeling from the shock of having killed someone just now.

He had killed, and it was three headshots.

Chen Xin was very clear about the results he achieved. Even though the lighting was poor and the flashlight glare was in his eyes, he knew he had landed three headshots and had shot the falling fire axe away.

All of this was crystal clear through the tactical goggles he wore under his eyes.

As he rushed over, Chen Xin, although drawing his gun, was aware of his lack of shooting experience, so he had spent 400 survival points to upgrade his handgun twice, turning it into a smart handgun with auto-aim and a pair of tactical goggles.

With auto-aim as a cheat, getting headshots every time wasn’t difficult.

In games, headshots bring the thrill of kills, but in reality, they bring nothing but blood-soaked shock.

He had killed, and it was three.

No matter who they were or what heinous acts they committed, it was undeniable that he had ended three lives with such brutal means as headshots.

Although his kill count in games had long surpassed five digits, in reality, he had at most killed chickens or watched pigs being slaughtered. The impact of personally taking three human lives left his mind blank.

Having checked the scene and provided emergency care to the wounded on the ground, Qin Lan saw Chen Xin still standing with the gun in the snow and quickly ran over.

"Chen Xin, it’s over, it’s over!" Qin Lan yelled, unable to speak softly, and tried to get him to lower the gun.

"I... I killed someone?" Chen Xin lowered his arm under Qin Lan’s pressure, looking at her incredulously.

"It’s over, Chen Xin. They were just three thugs. You didn’t hit them; it was me that shot." Qin Lan saw Chen Xin’s state and immediately lied, not wanting him to bear this burden.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 145 - 143: Stress Response
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Qin Lan said he didn’t hit them, it was she who did, out of goodwill. After all, as a special police officer and sniper, this wasn’t the first time her hands were stained with blood. It was part of her job.

However, Chen Xin was different. He was not a soldier or a police officer. He was just an online writer, an ordinary civilian, and his hands had never been stained with blood.

But such lies meant nothing to Chen Xin.

Qin Lan might have thought that in the darkness, Chen Xin couldn’t see clearly what was happening, but in the upgraded tactical goggles’ night vision, everything was so clear. It wouldn’t be an exaggeration to describe it as vivid.

With the tactical goggles’ additional magnification, zoom, and focus functions, Chen Xin could almost say he saw the bullets he fired pierce into the heads of the three deceased, shattering them like smashing a watermelon, taking away most of their brain matter, and their skulls collapsed like deflated bags.

The bloody and shocking scene greatly shook Chen Xin to the point that it took him a while to come back to his senses.

Although he had seen headshot scenes in various films and played at least a thousand such kills in various games, neither movies nor video games could ever show the audience or players the most realistic headshot scenes because they were simply too gory.

So even though Chen Xin didn’t think he lacked endurance and had seen headshots before, he was still terrified by the three burst watermelons, trembling all over.

Fortunately, they weren’t far from the city bureau, and after Qin Lan called for reinforcements, a nearby patrolling police car soon arrived.

The arriving officers were also taken aback by the scene in front of them. Three headshot kills—this wasn’t a scene from a game. In reality, this city had never seen such a brutal and bloody scene except during wartime.

But after all, they were police officers, and they had sufficient mental resilience. After understanding the situation with Qin Lan, they quickly put the injured and Chen Xin into the car and headed to the city’s First People’s Hospital’s shelter, leaving two to handle the bodies.

And Qin Lan wasn’t reassured about Chen Xin, so she jumped in the car and went to the hospital with him.

In the car, Chen Xin still clutched his gun. Qin Lan tried to get him to put the gun down, but the usually weak-handed Chen Xin was gripping the gun so tightly that no one could take it from him.

Seeing this, Qin Lan didn’t dare to forcefully take it, fearing it might provoke Chen Xin into firing, so she quietly turned on the safety of the gun while he didn’t resist.

Though the norm would be for Qin Lan to take away Chen Xin’s gun, considering Chen Xin had just been greatly traumatized, she and the other officers in the car tried their best to avoid further shocking him.

After all, a person in a state of shock is extremely dangerous and irrational.

However, in fact, Chen Xin wasn’t as agitated as they thought.

Earlier, he had indeed been greatly shocked and was in a state of stress, but now sitting in the car with Qin Lan comforting him beside him, Chen Xin had relatively calmed down a bit.

The main reason he held onto the gun was because he didn’t want others to touch his newly upgraded gun.

But to alleviate concerns or being mistaken for a dangerous person by the officers, he let Qin Lan turn on the safety of the gun.

Though Chen Xin had been mentally prepared for the dangers and potential confrontations he would face since entering the end times, killing someone, especially using a headshot, was beyond what he had anticipated.

This made Chen Xin somewhat regret upgrading his gun to such a high level.

Initially just wanting to activate auto-aim, it turned into auto-headshot. Though both aim to kill, the magnitude was clearly different.

Chen Xin was scared, not only from the mental shock of his first kill but mainly from the unbearably gory headshot scene.

I must say, though the upgraded tactical goggles were powerful and a very useful tool for the user.

Yet when the tactical goggles’ focus and zoom functions brought the scene so close, coupled with night vision making the killing process vividly clear like a game headshot animation, it was a relief Chen Xin didn’t throw up, remembering he wore a protective mask, or he would have suffocated in his vomit.

After managing to calm down as best he could, making his hands stop trembling, Chen Xin then put away his gun.

Seeing him putting away the gun, the surrounding police officers visibly breathed a sigh of relief.

Facing a gunman who just shot three people in the head, even the police would be nervous.

"A Xin, are you okay?" Seeing Chen Xin put away his gun, Qin Lan hurriedly asked with concern, very worried about his current state.

Chen Xin nodded slightly, reached up to remove his protective mask, took a deep breath, and said to Qin Lan, "I feel a little breathless."

Before Qin Lan could speak, Chen Xin grabbed her arm, asking, "A Lan! Did I just kill someone? I saw I hit those three people, and their heads burst open!"

At this point, Chen Xin wasn’t exactly acting, but he was indeed taking advantage of Qin Lan’s concern because ultimately, the laws in Flame Country were strict before the disaster, and ordinary people couldn’t even own a decent knife, let alone a gun.

Shooting and killing, even if it was assisting Qin Lan, a police officer, and even if he killed three criminals, the nature and consequences were still serious.

"It’s okay, it’s okay! Those three weren’t shot by you, I killed them. Your shots missed." Qin Lan continued to emphasize that she killed the three people, looking at Chen Xin in this state, she absolutely did not dare to tell him the truth that he had killed.

In case of provoking something in Chen Xin, the consequences would be far more severe than her lying that he missed.

"But I saw their heads explode!" At this point, Chen Xin silently apologized to Qin Lan in his heart, but he still had to continue pretending.

Seeing this, Qin Lan quickly said, "I shot them. I’m a sniper, remember? At such close range, hitting three headshots is normal. In fact, I should be apologizing to you, A Xin, for letting you see such a scene. That’s my fault."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 146 - 144 Psychological Counseling
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The police car entered the shelter located in the First People’s Hospital through the original entrance of the hospital’s underground garage.

The injured were quickly taken for treatment, while Chen Xin was sent to a ward to rest, waiting for a specialized psychologist to come and provide him with psychological counseling.

"Ah Xin, don’t take this matter too seriously. Those three people I shot dead were thugs. They were robbing the injured at the time and even intended to attack her with a fire axe. Even if you were the one who shot and killed them, it would be considered self-defense and bravery, let alone that you just fired the shots, and I was the one who killed them," Qin Lan was accompanying Chen Xin, trying to comfort him.

However, at this moment, Chen Xin still appeared in a dazed state, as if he hadn’t fully come to his senses.

"Knock knock, excuse me, I’m the psychologist here to provide counseling. Officer, could you please step out for a moment?" A pleasant voice was heard at the door, and while it was sweet, it gave off a cool feeling.

Qin Lan turned to see a female doctor wearing glasses standing at the doorway, holding a patient’s file. Although she was in an ordinary white coat, it exuded a fashionable quality on her.

What particularly surprised Qin Lan was that despite the current extreme cold, this female doctor was still dressed in a shirt, short skirt, stockings, and high heels in an office lady style. Her long hair was pinned up with a hairpin, looking nothing like a doctor in a doomsday shelter, but more like a specialized doctor providing custom services in some private hospital before the disaster.

Qin Lan didn’t know why she thought this way, but she somehow felt there was an indescribable aura about her, not looking like a proper doctor.

However, she quickly dispelled this thought, said, "Thank you," to the doctor, and left the ward.

After watching Qin Lan leave, the female doctor finally closed the door, pulled the curtain, and sat by Chen Xin’s bed.

She took out a pen-shaped flashlight and flicked it twice at Chen Xin, checking his state. The expression on her face changed, and she reached up to remove the hairpin from her hair, letting her long hair fall.

The female doctor shook her hair while placing the patient’s file to one side, lazily saying to Chen Xin, "Alright, since you’ve already come around, stop pretending. Don’t you feel uncomfortable?"
