"How did you know I was pretending?" Seeing the female doctor act this way, Chen Xin also snapped out of his daze, looking at this distinctive doctor: "Dior’s Poison, even in this environment, is there still someone using perfume?"

This time, it was the female doctor’s turn to be a little surprised. She couldn’t help but raise an eyebrow: "You have quite a keen nose. A man who can recognize Poison, that’s impressive, even before the disaster."

"I just tried making it myself before, so I have researched the five poisons," Chen Xin brushed over the topic, looking at the doctor: "Although I wasn’t actually in a daze, I think I still need psychological counseling, so Doctor, can you start?"

The female doctor shrugged, picked up the patient file on the table, and pressed a button on the hairpin in her hand before she started writing: "I thought I could take it easy. Aren’t men supposed to quickly recover when seeing a sexy female doctor like me in the apocalypse? You say you need psychological counseling, maybe it’s just an excuse to look a bit longer?"

"If you’ve just seen three people’s heads burst open like smashed watermelons in front of you, I don’t think anyone would be in the mood to ogle pretty women at this time." Although the female doctor’s words were a bit biting, Chen Xin’s expression remained unfazed, replying with a sarcastic retort: "Of course, if someone uses sex to relieve psychological shock, then ignore what I said."

Hearing Chen Xin’s response, the female doctor raised an eyebrow: "Having just seen three men get shot in the head, and you can get over it and banter with me, it seems your mental resilience is quite strong!"

To this evaluation, Chen Xin indifferently said: "Perhaps that’s the residue from being a novelist. Having researched too much for writing novels, I’ve seen a lot of gory scenes and developed some resistance. It just took a moment to snap out of it. After all, witnessing a real human head exploding in front of you can’t be compared to videos or photos in terms of impact."

The female doctor nodded in agreement, continuing to write on the patient file with the pen that doubled as a hairpin, chatting with Chen Xin: "You have good emotional self-regulation. Being able to talk to me so clearly now is also a distraction strategy, so you don’t have to think about what you just saw, right?"

"So, you are a psychologist? Dressing like this and wearing perfume must be to deliberately create a nostalgic illusion, making the patients feel like they’re still in the pre-disaster world while they relax during psychological counseling, right?" Chen Xin found this female doctor intriguing, and indeed, he no longer dwelled on his recent shooting incident.

The laziness on the female doctor’s face finally disappeared entirely, and she became much more serious, devoid of the seductive demeanor that made Qin Lan think she wasn’t a proper doctor.

"No doubt you’re a novelist; your observation and analytical skills deserve praise." The female doctor smiled, complimenting Chen Xin’s perceptive nature, while also bringing the topic back to his current state: "Seeing others get shot in the head can be a significant psychological shock for ordinary people, and even police need psychological counseling after such actions. Your ability to self-regulate is a positive sign, showing good psychological control capability.

However, I still advise you to rest and relax your nerves. Don’t put too much pressure on yourself. In these end times, everyone is under great stress. I almost have a case of emotional breakdown here every day. You’re a rare patient with self-regulating ability that I’ve seen these days, so don’t collapse under the pressure."

Chen Xin took the female doctor’s advice to heart, nodding in agreement: "I did have a breakdown before, but fortunately, I’ve adjusted now. Thanks for your concern, Doctor. Could I trouble you for your name?"

"Since you know I’ve sprayed Poison, why not guess my name? Poison is the hint," the female doctor said with a mysterious smile.

Hearing the female doctor’s words, Chen Xin couldn’t help but begin to think and analyze, speculating about the connection between the female doctor’s name and the Poison perfume.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 147 - 145 Doctor Hong
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Red Poison, this is a fragrance from the Gaul fashion brand Dior, with the exact name being HYPNOTIC POISON. While a direct translation would be Hypnotic Poison.

Because all fragrances in this Dior series are named after poisons, and different versions have different colors, there came to be distinctions like Red Poison and Blue Poison.

As for why Chen Xin knows about these, it’s because back when he traveled to Thailand, he was asked by Mo Qingyan to scour Bangkok’s cosmetic stores to help her find the white version of this series, which is White Poison.

However, at that time, all the other versions were available except for the white poison.

Especially since there was a trend of making your perfumes for a while, Chen Xin also tried making his, so he’s very impressed with this brand’s perfumes.

But as for the lady doctor saying her name was related to Red Poison, Chen Xin gave up guessing after thinking for a while.

It’s not that he couldn’t think of the answer, but he had a more straightforward way to get it.

"So your name is Hong Rui, the last name Hong is indeed rare. Here’s a tip: next time you want someone to guess your name, remember to cover your name tag, or this doesn’t count as a guessing game." Chen Xin glanced at the name tag on the female doctor’s white coat pocket and accurately called her name.

Hearing Chen Xin utter her name correctly, Hong Rui looked down at the name tag on her chest and laughed openly: "Looks like it was my oversight, but Mr. Writer, you have sharp eyes!"

"Just good at observing, gathering material, that’s all." Not continuing casual talk with Hong Rui, Chen Xin moved on from the topic and asked: "Dr. Hong Rui, how is my mental state exactly?"

"Overall, there’s not much problem, except for too much stress, mental tension... and quite a severe stress response, not much else," Hong Rui flipped through the medical records in her hand, reciting a string of terms before seriously saying Chen Xin had no major issues.

But for Hong Rui’s answer, Chen Xin seriously nodded, agreeing that these indeed weren’t problems.

In the end times, whose nerves wouldn’t be tense, and who wouldn’t live without pressure?

From the scenes Chen Xin witnessed at the city’s refugee shelter, even kids were thoughtfully sharing household duties with their parents; no one could live carefree under such circumstances.

Feeling some mental stress? That’s entirely normal.

As for anything else, nothing extremely severe, with rest and relaxation, it could be adjusted.

After diagnosing Chen Xin, Hong Rui indicated he could take a good nap there, turning on a sound system to play white noise, adjusting the lights to a relaxing and comfortable level while also lighting a section of incense.

"This incense has a unique smell, is it made by you, Dr. Hong?" The unique scent drew Chen Xin’s attention.

"I made the blend myself with calming effects, but it’s difficult to make now as many materials are unavailable." At this mention, Hong Rui showed some regret: "I can only grow a little herbal medicine in the office now, which isn’t enough. Alright, let’s not dwell on this, just close your eyes and relax, and rest well."

Hearing Hong Rui say this, Chen Xin nodded and closed his eyes.

————————————————

While Chen Xin was receiving psychological consultation, the political commissar, informed of the matter, also rushed to the hospital.

Such things generally wouldn’t require the presence of a SWAT team’s political commissar, but since Chen Xin was involved with the ground source heat pump equipment installation, and considering he was invited by them, the political commissar still headed to the hospital immediately upon receiving the news.

After understanding the situation from Qin Lan, the political commissar let out a sigh of relief. Though it shouldn’t be said this way, the fact that three irrelevant thugs were killed was indeed less important than keeping Chen Xin safe.

At this time, Qin Lan still insisted it was her who killed those three, saying Chen Xin fired but didn’t effectively hit.

And the political commissar clearly accepted this explanation, thinking Chen Xin felt it was him who killed due to the mental impact of seeing the three thugs have their heads blown apart, even though he had fired shots.

This situation isn’t too hard to handle; proper psychological guidance could essentially prevent any leftover issues.

However, to be cautious, the political commissar still told Qin Lan: "This matter was handled suboptimally, little Qin, although the situation was urgent, I understand you shooting, but letting ordinary people witness such scenes can have negative effects.

I heard your relationship with him is good, so make sure you spend more time with Comrade Xiao Chen afterward to avoid him bearing any psychological burdens."

"I understand, Commissar, I’ll do a good job comforting A Xin in the follow-up work." Qin Lan nodded seriously. Knowing it was Chen Xin who actually shot and killed the three thugs, she was more worried about Chen Xin being left with psychological shadows than the political commissar.

Just as she was discussing this with the political commissar, Hong Rui, having made Chen Xin lie down, also came out of the ward.

"Dr. Hong, how is the patient’s condition?" The political commissar was familiar with Hong Rui, given much of the SWAT team’s psychological guidance was done by her before doomsday, so seeing Hong Rui come out, he immediately approached.

"The patient’s self-regulation ability is decent, with no severe psychological shadow, just temporarily stimulated. Rest and relaxation will sort things out." As a professional psychologist, Hong Rui didn’t delve into professional jargon, simply giving a brief description of Chen Xin’s situation.

Hearing Hong Rui’s words, both the political commissar and Qin Lan felt relieved, though Qin Lan still very concernedly asked Hong Rui: "How is he doing now?"

"Are you his..." Hong Rui measured up Qin Lan, revealing a knowing smile, then answered Qin Lan: "I’ve suggested he relax and rest. Let him sleep here tonight; if you want to accompany him, you can, but try not to wake him. There are extra beds in the ward, and you can rest here too."

Seeing Hong Rui say this, the political commissar smoothly added: "If that’s the case, little Qin, you stay with Comrade Xiao Chen for now, accompany him until he’s alright before returning."

Despite having some minor protests about Qin Lan, a sniper, being unable to carry out tasks for several days because of Chen Xin, the political commissar still graciously allowed Qin Lan to remain temporarily by Chen Xin’s side.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 148 - 146: Breakfast
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

For Chen Xin, this may have been the most comfortable sleep he had in a long time.

Soft white noise, warm-toned dim lights, and Hong Rui’s specially-made incense, all of these helped ease the tension he had been carrying for this period of time.

Once he relaxed, he naturally fell into a deep sleep and woke up naturally.

Opening his eyes, Chen Xin saw Qin Lan asleep at the bedside, showing a surprised expression, he moved slightly, waking Qin Lan up.

"Xin, you’re awake?" Qin Lan seemed not to be in a deep sleep, she quickly woke up and upon seeing Chen Xin awake, she hurriedly asked with concern: "How are you feeling, any better?"

"Much better. After sleeping, I’m fine now. Lan, why didn’t you sleep in the bed next to mine? Lying like that is uncomfortable!" Seeing the red mark on Qin Lan’s face from leaning for too long, Chen Xin felt a bit distressed.

However, Qin Lan just smiled indifferently and comforted Chen Xin: "Don’t worry about me, I am a special forces officer, trained professionally. This is nothing to me."

After saying this, Qin Lan stood up, stretched lazily, moved her muscles to show she was fine, then with some guilt, she said to Chen Xin: "Sorry for making you witness that scene last night. I should have handled it better, aiming for the torso."

If Chen Xin didn’t know anything, maybe he would believe Qin Lan’s words. But he saw with his own eyes his bullets leaving the gun, hitting the heads of those three criminals.

Yet, Chen Xin couldn’t say this aloud because he knew Qin Lan claimed responsibility to protect him.

So, Chen Xin could only apologize to Qin Lan: "It’s my fault. If I could handle things better, it would’ve been different."

Saying this, Chen Xin also sat up from the hospital bed and asked Qin Lan with concern: "Did my shooting cause any trouble for you? I wouldn’t have pulled out the gun if I’d known!"

Chen Xin’s guilt was genuine; he truly didn’t want to cause Qin Lan any inconvenience or trouble.

To this, Qin Lan simply shook her head and comforted Chen Xin: "Xin, you were anxious because you care! Did you forget the situation we’re in? Besides, I’m in special forces. The team already issued a policy; in such situations, shooting to kill is permissible.

As for you having a gun, that’s not an issue, the political officer knows you have a gun. Besides, what you did last night, even in the past, had you killed those three people, it would at most be considered excessive self-defense, not a crime."

Hearing Qin Lan say this, Chen Xin felt relieved a lot, nodded, and said no more.

Seeing Chen Xin no longer dwell on this matter, Qin Lan felt a sigh of relief inwardly, then asked him: "Xin, are you hungry? The hospital food here is quite good. I’ll get you some breakfast, while you can rinse your mouth or wash your face! There’s a sink over there, you can use it for the time being."

As she said this, Qin Lan pointed to the sink in a corner of the hospital room, while taking out a handkerchief from herself and handing it to Chen Xin.

Taking the handkerchief Qin Lan handed over, Chen Xin nodded, while Qin Lan left the room to get him some food.

The hospital food is indeed better than that of the special forces team, not because the hospital treatment is better, but because their needs differ.

The special forces team and city bureau require high-calorie food that can fill and sustain energy, whereas the hospital food offers various nutritional meals that speed up patient recovery, which results in hospital food being quite different and even better.

In some sense, the hospital’s food is currently the least changed thing compared to before the disaster.

In the hospital cafeteria, Qin Lan even got porridge, buns, and even soy milk.

This made Qin Lan swallow hard, trying to resist the temptation in her stomach while smelling the aroma from her breakfast, she then carried the meal towards the hospital room.

However, when Qin Lan returned to the room, she saw not only Chen Xin.

Hong Rui, responsible for treating Chen Xin, was checking Chen Xin’s condition.

"Good morning, Dr. Hong!" Qin Lan greeted Hong Rui, after placing down the breakfast, she asked: "How is Xin’s condition? Is he okay?"

Hong Rui nodded and answered affirmatively: "Recovering well, a bit more rest and he can go home. Once back, just rest and avoid stressing yourself too much."

"Is that so? Thank you so much, Dr. Hong!" Hearing Hong Rui said Chen Xin was fine, Qin Lan immediately thanked her with joy.

Hong Rui did not pay much attention to Qin Lan’s gratitude, just smiled then said to Chen Xin: "Regarding those herb seeds, I’ll give you some later, it’d be fantastic if more could be planted."

"Don’t worry, Dr. Hong, my planting greenhouse is quite large, planting some herbs won’t be a problem." Chen Xin assuredly responded to Hong Rui’s words.

After Hong Rui left, Qin Lan then asked Chen Xin: "Xin, what were you talking to Dr. Hong about?"

"We were discussing the incense." Chen Xin did not hide anything from Qin Lan, while picking up the breakfast to eat, he explained: "Last night, the incense Dr. Hong used was homemade. I was curious about her formula, so I asked. Dr. Hong said many herbs can’t be bought now, hence she has to grow them herself.

I said I could help her plant some, after all, my greenhouse is sizeable. With a few pots, I can help grow some herbs. Later, I could also make some incense to improve the air in the shelter. Relying solely on the ventilation system leaves an odor over time, anyway."

Upon hearing Chen Xin say this, Qin Lan suddenly became interested too and said with deep concern: "Could you give me some incense once it’s done, Xin? I feel my room could use some, too!"

Chen Xin was eating the long-missed buns while nodding enthusiastically, agreeing to Qin Lan’s request.

"I didn’t expect to find buns and soy milk at the hospital, their food here is really decent!" Chen Xin was eating buns, and passed the soy milk and another bun to Qin Lan, intending to have some porridge and a bun as breakfast himself.

Regarding this, Qin Lan accepted the bun Chen Xin shared, without showing reluctance, eating bun and drinking soy milk, she agreed: "The hospital’s supplies are directly provided by the plantation area, although not in large quantities, the variety is guaranteed."

"Then I should find a way to trade seeds with them!" Chen Xin nodded thoughtfully, now evidently eyeing their seeds.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 149 - 147 Under Construction
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After finishing breakfast, having received permission to leave, Chen Xin and Qin Lan naturally didn’t stay long at the hospital.

After getting the seeds from Hong Rui, the two left the shelter where the hospital was located.

Thanks to the gradually connecting tunnels between major shelters, returning to the police station from the hospital no longer requires traveling in the minus twenty to thirty degrees Celsius temperatures on the surface. Instead, they can travel through the somewhat warmer underground tunnels.

And doing this simply requires taking a detour through another shelter.

With Qin Lan’s Special Police Team identity, they caught a ride towards the Polytechnic University shelter.

"Driver, what goods are you delivering?" Sitting in the car, Chen Xin felt a bit bored, so he struck up a conversation with the driver.

"Some common medications and medical supplies. The health center over there is running out of some medical items, so I’m resupplying them with this truck." The driver replied to Chen Xin cheerfully, explaining his purpose.

Chen Xin casually chatted with the driver, and upon seeing the smile on the driver’s face, he couldn’t help but ask, "Driver, you seem quite happy! In these times, not many people can be as cheerful as you!"

"Oh, what’s this fuss about! When I used to drive long distances outside, I faced worse situations than this. Besides, life isn’t unlivable now. I’ve had hard times before, as long as we don’t starve, there’s nothing to worry about. Plus, whether you have a gloomy face or a smiling one, a day is still a day, so why not smile?" The driver had a carefree attitude and gave Chen Xin another big smile.

Listening to the driver, Chen Xin also laughed, "Driver, you’re quite open-minded!"

"It’s not really about being open-minded; I just think a bit more positively than others. As long as life is livable, there’s nothing worth being unhappy about." The smile on the driver’s face seemed infectious, and even Qin Lan, who sat silently beside him, couldn’t help but smile.

Since it was an underground tunnel, there were no crossroads or bends, so the car quickly arrived at the Polytechnic University.

After getting off at the Polytechnic University’s underground parking lot and thanking the driver, Chen Xin and Qin Lan looked around, planning to catch another ride to the city government to return to the police station from there.

However, as there weren’t many cars going from the Polytechnic University to the city government, they could only wait by the underground tunnel entrance for a car to come out.

Looking at the deep underground tunnel ahead, Chen Xin couldn’t help but marvel, "Our country’s infrastructure capabilities are truly impressive; it only took a few months to dig such a tunnel!"

"This is considered normal, right? This tunnel isn’t long, and with the current surplus labor force, digging and building simultaneously is naturally fast!" Qin Lan wasn’t impressed; instead, she found Chen Xin’s reaction a bit exaggerated. "Plus, there’s no such thing as unpaid contracts now; wages can be settled monthly or even daily. Workers rely on their work to eat, so it’s no surprise they work harder and faster, right?"

Regarding this issue, Chen Xin could only raise an eyebrow and say, "All I can say, Alan, is that you’re used to Flame Country’s infrastructure speed; other countries aren’t this fast."

Talking about this topic reminded Chen Xin of when he could still access the internet, and he often saw foreigners online wondering why Flame Country builds things so quickly while complaining about the slow progress in their countries.

When he first saw it, he couldn’t understand why some countries could take decades to build a road or why an airport proposal in 1959 wouldn’t break ground until 2002.

Until he learned more, Chen Xin realized that it wasn’t that other countries were slow, but that Flame Country was extraordinarily fast.

However, discussing this topic now is pointless because the existence of foreign countries is uncertain, and who is left to compete with Flame Country’s infrastructure speed?

Perhaps Lucia still exists; powerful European countries like Britain, Gaul, and Hans may or may not survive, as their governments’ control over their nations is bewildering.

As for the planet’s previously strongest nation, the Federation, all that can be said is that the heavens did not bless the Federation; under the meteorite, all are equal.

If luck were worse, a meteorite could hit Yellowstone National Park, causing Yellowstone Volcano to erupt, or land on a fault line—then all that would be left to wish is good luck to the Federation people.

Thinking about these things, Chen Xin and Qin Lan finally hailed a car carrying shift police officers back to the station, smoothly returning to the police station.

When they returned to the police station, Qin Lan, appointed by the political commissar to accompany Chen Xin these days, didn’t go on duty but stayed by his side.

Though Chen Xin had just come back from the hospital, he still went to check on the installation progress of the geothermal pumps in the station’s shelter.

Even without Chen Xin’s presence, having thoroughly discussed the relevant technical parameters and installation issues with the engineer the previous day, the drilling and doweling work progressed smoothly in his absence.

Wearing a mask and watching the workers drilling the floor, Chen Xin pulled aside the engineer in charge of on-site construction and loudly asked, "Is there any problem with the installation?"

"None at the moment. We’re currently following the dimensions you provided, Engineer Chen, to drill holes. Next, we’ll lay the base plates and level them before installing the equipment." The engineer explained to Chen Xin while opening the blueprints he was holding to further demonstrate. "You’ll need to handle the equipment debugging by then."

"It’s part of my job." Chen Xin had no objections. Since he didn’t need to personally handle the drilling and doweling, he was happy to relax and watch the workers busily at work.

Though Qin Lan didn’t understand what the workers were doing, her task now was to accompany Chen Xin, so she had no objections, standing by his side and watching the construction.

It wasn’t long before the workers completed the floor drilling, inserting expansion tubes, and began installing base plates on the floor.

Watching the workers’ construction, an idea occurred to Chen Xin: if he were to use the system function now, spending survival points to complete the installation of those six geothermal pumps, what would these workers perceive about the installation process?

However, the thought quickly faded from Chen Xin’s mind. Although he had plenty of survival points, and that was his original intention, since the workers were already on the job, it was better to conserve them and not waste survival points unnecessarily.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 150 - 148: Casual Chat
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The installation of the geothermal heat pump equipment went very smoothly. The workers responsible for the construction were evidently very professional. Whether it was laying the cement base or aligning and leveling the equipment, everything was done flawlessly.

This gave Chen Xin an inexplicable feeling of familiarity. From childhood to adulthood, he had seen and interacted with these kinds of mechanical equipment and on-site constructions way too often.

Whether it was his childhood experiences living in a chemical plant, watching from the sidelines of construction sites with his father, or his own personal experiences after starting to work, Chen Xin always felt a close connection to this kind of equipment installation and debugging work.

However, while Chen Xin felt a sense of closeness and familiarity, Qin Lan, who was watching from the side, inevitably found it a bit boring.

As a sniper, Qin Lan certainly didn’t lack patience, but that did not stop her from feeling bored.

Nevertheless, since accompanying Chen Xin was considered a task and something she wanted to do, she managed to endure the current boredom, although it was hard not to show it in her emotions.

Chen Xin seemed to notice Qin Lan’s mood, so he said to her, "Ah Lan, if there’s something you need to do, you can go ahead and do it. I can manage here by myself."

"Ah? No, it’s fine. I don’t have anything to do today. I’ll just keep you company," Qin Lan quickly shook her head. Although she was a bit bored and had nothing to do, she insisted on staying with Chen Xin.

Seeing this, Chen Xin didn’t insist anymore but still said to Qin Lan, "It’s up to you, but if you really have something to do, don’t let keeping me company delay you."

"Don’t worry, Chen Xin. The political commissar said yesterday that my task for the next couple of days is to take care of you." Qin Lan smiled slightly upon hearing Chen Xin’s words, "Chen Xin, you have no idea how important this geothermal heat pump system is to the political commissar and the captain! It’s almost become the team’s top priority, so I’ll stay with you."

This statement made Chen Xin feel flattered, but seeing Qin Lan’s determined attitude, he didn’t say anything more.

Besides, in his opinion, although Qin Lan might find accompanying him a bit boring, it was better than going out on missions against the wind and snow.

Despite it sounding a bit selfish, Chen Xin hoped that the people he knew would endure a little less hardship.

The workers’ construction went very smoothly. Considering that there weren’t too many holes to drill and ground anchors to lay for the six geothermal heat pumps, they had basically finished the work by noon.

The ground anchors needed to be reinforced with cement to secure the steel plates, so after the construction was completed, it needed some time to set before the geothermal heat pump could be officially installed.

"So, Chen Xin, what are you doing this afternoon?" During lunch, Qin Lan heard that they had to wait for the cement to set before continuing with the geothermal heat pump installation, so she naturally asked about the afternoon plans.

"In the afternoon, mainly drilling holes on the lower level to prepare for placing the heat exchange pipes." Chen Xin answered Qin Lan’s question, and perhaps thinking she might have plans in the afternoon, asked her, "Ah Lan, do you have something to do this afternoon?"

Qin Lan shook her head at this question, "I don’t have anything in the afternoon. I was mostly worried about you, Chen Xin. After going through what happened last night and having to work again today, I’m afraid you might not be able to take it."

"You worry too much. I’m just standing on the side, watching and occasionally giving some guidance. It won’t be tiring." Qin Lan’s concern made Chen Xin laugh. While feeling touched, he seemed to change the subject, asking, "By the way, Ah Lan, why were you so decisive in directly shooting those three people last night? Isn’t it usually a warning before shooting?"

Upon hearing Chen Xin bring up last night’s incident, Qin Lan inevitably tensed up a bit. But when she realized he was asking why she shot, she relaxed, adjusted her emotions, and replied, "I did shout ’Don’t move,’ but those three still raised the fireman’s axe, intending to attack the injured on the ground. In that situation, I could only shoot."

At this point, as if to reinforce the impression that she was the one who shot the three people, Qin Lan deliberately apologized to him, saying, "I’m sorry, Chen Xin. I shouldn’t have shot them in the head directly last night. If I had aimed at the torso, I probably wouldn’t have scared you."

Of course, Chen Xin understood the situation. He just shook his head indifferently at Qin Lan’s apology: "It’s nothing; it’s my lack of endurance. But why did this happen? There shouldn’t be circumstances where three people with a fireman’s axe would be attacking someone, right? This isn’t some apocalyptic drama from the West where cannibals appear just to depict human darkness."

"Chen Xin, you’re being too optimistic!" Qin Lan bitterly smiled, denying Chen Xin’s conjecture before explaining, "Human nature hasn’t turned so dark as to spawn cannibals, but some people weren’t much better.

Ever since the disaster struck, some with dark thoughts thought it was their opportunity, and various illegal and even harshly barbaric acts emerged.

They claim to aid other survivors, but in reality, they kill or enslave men, confine women for pleasure, and engage in brutal killings for fun... The list of evil acts goes on!

The municipal government had already issued an order, stating that addressing such heinous incidents was no longer about capture but suppression. Additionally, last night’s incident involved a woman trading for food, who was discovered by those three trying to rob her, and they even intended to kill!"

The information Qin Lan provided came from the injured, whom she and the supporting police accompanied to the hospital with Chen Xin.

The identities of the three criminals shot dead were not confirmed, but their assault on the injured and attempt to attack with a fireman’s axe were verified, aligning with the injured’s testimony.

Such incidents were not new to Qin Lan. At the beginning of the disaster, she might have been reluctant to shoot so decisively.

However, incidents of severe injury due to delayed shooting had occurred, even injuring police officers. Since then, the new unwritten rule was to shoot immediately if the other party showed aggression or violent tendencies.

After Qin Lan explained to Chen Xin, he nodded in understanding, "Indeed, that’s how it should be. The state could maintain strict order before, but now in times like these, who knows if seemingly harmless people won’t suddenly pull out a knife?"

For the three people she killed, Chen Xin felt not the slightest sympathy or regret. Even if they had been innocent, under last night’s circumstances, killing them would only count as unintentional manslaughter.

Hearing Chen Xin’s thoughts, Qin Lan finally smiled, "Chen Xin, I’m glad you see it that way!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 151 - 149 Going Home
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

With professional workers on site and engineers assisting, the six geothermal heat pumps provided by Chen Xin to the special police unit were quickly installed.

Even though they needed to wait for the cement to dry completely, it didn’t waste too much time, and three days later, the entire set of equipment could run smoothly.

However, the equipment was only in trial operation, as the special police unit had not yet connected it to the heating system of the area where the special police unit was located, so Chen Xin just started the equipment to test its operational condition.

"The equipment is running fine, but whether it can operate for long periods is still unknown. My suggestion is to connect the pipelines with the hot water pipes in the bathroom, so we can test if the heating and operation are stable," Chen Xin suggested to the engineer next to him.

Since the geothermal heat pump equipment hasn’t been connected to the heating pipelines, the heat obtained by the geothermal heat pumps is all stored as hot water in the energy storage tank.

But the energy storage tank’s capacity is limited, and with the continuous operation of the six geothermal heat pumps, this energy storage tank is already half full.

The engineer looked at the water level displayed on the energy storage tank, calculated the heat supply efficiency of the six geothermal heat pumps, and nodded with satisfaction: "Based on the current operating data, the six geothermal heat pumps can provide 2 tons of hot water in an hour, which is already quite efficient!"

Talking about this data, the engineer was visibly excited.

Although he knew the heat efficiency of geothermal heat pumps was high, seeing firsthand that the storage tank capable of holding 5 tons of water had already been filled by one-fifth in half an hour was still exciting, especially since he had participated in installing this equipment.

So watching the equipment’s operation was so outstanding, the engineer felt a great sense of achievement.

Sharing in the engineer’s excitement, Chen Xin smiled too, but he continued, "Since we’re only testing right now, I haven’t set the equipment’s operational power to the maximum. You should connect the energy storage tank to the hot water pipes in the bathroom. This way, while providing hot water for the bathroom, we can also test the equipment’s stability during prolonged operation at maximum power.

After all, this equipment was handmade by me, and I can’t guarantee its operational stability. Testing it would be best."

"Indeed, stable long-term operation of the equipment is key, and we will modify the pipeline for testing," the engineer agreed with Chen Xin’s words, nodded, and after making a note, he asked Chen Xin with concern, "Are you leaving now, Chen Engineer?"

Chen Xin didn’t deny it and just nodded: "The equipment is installed, and it’s running smoothly now, so tomorrow I’m leaving. My greenhouse at home has vegetables growing, and I’m a bit worried about not tending to them for a few days."

"Oh, better go back and take a look then," the engineer felt Chen Xin had no reason to stay.

Even though he thought Chen Xin should indeed return, the engineer still asked with concern, "If the equipment encounters issues in the future, will you come around again, Chen Engineer?"

"Rest assured, if any faults occur in the equipment operation, the special police unit knows where I live, and I can come by anytime." Chen Xin readily guaranteed on matters concerning after-sales issues.

After chatting with the engineer about equipment operations and maintenance, Chen Xin finally turned and left, while Qin Lan was already waiting for him.

"Are you leaving now, Ah Xin?" Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin beside her, unable to help but feel a bit reluctant.

Staying together again with Chen Xin over the past few days, Qin Lan felt as if she had fallen in love with him all over again through their close contact, making Chen Xin’s departure somewhat painful.

Chen Xin noticed Qin Lan’s emotions, but having no intention to reconcile with Qin Lan, it was inappropriate for him to express too much intimacy.

Although over these days, his relationship with Qin Lan had returned to the same level of closeness as before, Chen Xin knew even Qin Lan didn’t have the intention to reconcile; otherwise, she would’ve already spoken up.

So, although he noticed Qin Lan’s mood wasn’t quite right, he could only nod and say to her, "Hmm, it’s time to go back. No one’s tended to the greenhouse at home these past few days, and who knows what kind of mess it’s turned into. I need to go back and tidy things up."

Though he was not too worried about whether the greenhouse would face issues due to being away for so many days.

Before he left, he had already turned on both the geothermal heat pump and the generator, so there was no danger of a power outage.

As long as there’s no power outage, the water and heating won’t stop in the greenhouse, and a few days unsupervised shouldn’t cause any problems.

But growing vegetables still requires regular tending, especially since the first batch of upland rice he planted is due for harvest this month, and he needed to go back to harvest it.

Qin Lan knew all this too, though she felt a bit reluctant to see Chen Xin go, she nodded and said, "Then be careful, Ah Xin. I have duty today, so let Ah Ning take you back."

"Xiao Ding? Haven’t seen her these days. Has she returned then?" Chen Xin, although a bit surprised that Qin Lan wasn’t the one to take him back, understood her meaning and didn’t comment further, just curious about why he hadn’t seen Ding Ning these past few days.

"Ah Ning has been on duty at another shelter these past few days, she just came back on rotation today," Qin Lan explained why Chen Xin hadn’t seen Ding Ning, leading him back to the room to pack his luggage, but did not escort him out.

Watching Chen Xin’s departing back, Qin Lan felt a pang of discomfort in her heart, the kind of feeling she hadn’t had since her breakup with Chen Xin.

Chen Xin knew Qin Lan was watching him from behind, but he didn’t turn back, just sighed and left the shelter by the elevator.

Under the escort of Ding Ning and another member of the special police team, Chen Xin returned to his home after several days away.

The doorway was still covered with snow, and with Ding Ning and the other member’s help, he was able to shovel away the snow and finally entered the house.

Ding Ning and the other special police team member didn’t stay long, just escorted Chen Xin home before leaving, while Chen Xin began tidying up his shelter.

After being away for a few days, though everything was clean, and the floor needed little cleaning, there was still a bit of dust.

As for the water circulation system, it didn’t need cleaning since it wasn’t used while Chen Xin was away from home.

The situation in the planting greenhouse was also good, not only were the crops growing normally, but they thrived because Chen Xin hadn’t turned off the supplementary lighting these days, making the growth even better than before.

The only trouble for Chen Xin was the small bunker where he installed the antenna, as it was covered in thick ice, with the gap connecting to the outside world completely blocked, and breaking through this ice was an arduous job.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 152 - 150: Observation Room Upgrade
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Holding the hammer taken from the workbench, Chen Xin struck the ice accumulation in the shelter repeatedly, unable to avoid feeling exhausted and panting heavily.

Supporting his waist, Chen Xin leaned against the wall inside the shelter and took a deep breath. Although the oxygen content in the shelter was always high due to the oxygen generator and the planting greenhouse, this location was practically considered as external, making him breathless and panting after the ice removal session just now.

Reaching into his pocket for an oxygen bottle, he opened the protective mask on his face, took a few breaths through his mouth and nose, and felt much better before putting the protective mask back on and straightening up.

"Whew, how long will this ice removal take?" Chen Xin couldn’t help but sigh. The ice was so thick that the small hammer in his hand couldn’t effectively break it, only knocking off some ice fragments with great effort.

This inevitably made him feel somewhat frustrated, as ice removal was not an easy task. Unlike shoveling snow with a shovel, it’s hard to clean thoroughly.

But ice, especially frozen hard ice, is very difficult to clear. In many places, if it froze over, it wouldn’t thaw until spring arrived.

However, now Chen Xin couldn’t wait for the temperature to rise and the arrival of a warm spring for the ice to melt on its own; he had to find a way to remove the ice accumulation in front of him, which was at least thirty centimeters thick.

To be honest, he had anticipated ice accumulation and knew that leaving it unattended for a few days would lead to serious issues, but he hadn’t expected it to be frozen a foot thick already.

Such thick ice accumulation would be difficult to clear even with professional ice axes and iron bars, let alone with the small iron hammer in Chen Xin’s hand.

With the hammer, he struck the ice surface a few more times, but the effect was minimal. Apart from chipping some ice fragments, there wasn’t even a crack on the ice surface.

This made Chen Xin feel discouraged, lamenting in his heart, wishing there was a method to solve the ice accumulation issue in one go, and if it could further completely resolve the ventilation issue here, that would be ideal.

But solving the problem was not that easy, Chen Xin smiled bitterly and continued hammering the ice accumulation.

While hammering, Chen Xin was also thinking about how to solve the ice accumulation problem.

The main reason for the ice accumulation was that the air blown out from the shelter’s vent contained water vapor, which quickly condensed upon encountering cold air. Before it could escape, it froze into ice and accumulated here.

This was evident as Chen Xin had been hammering the ice blocks for a while, and there was ice forming on the surface of his Protective Suit.

Brushing off the ice on the surface of his Protective Suit, Chen Xin sighed. While he understood the principle, removing the moisture from the air blown out of the vent wasn’t simply a matter of upgrading the ventilation system.

Chen Xin knew the method to remove moisture from the air, which was through air drying, but this was mainly an industrial production technique. Using it in the ventilation of the shelter was a real waste and consumed a lot of resources.

So, in Chen Xin’s view, it was sufficient if ice didn’t form within the small shelter.

To address this, improving the ventilation form at the vent or adjusting the shelter’s structure to move the vent outside could solve the issue.

However, Chen Xin was somewhat reluctant to move the vent outside because then the air blown out through the vent would be significantly warmer than the ambient temperature. Although it’s not visible to the naked eye, with an infrared telescope, the vent would be as conspicuous as a searchlight in the night.

Moreover, although the vent won’t allow people to crawl in like in Western movies, if discovered, it would still cause many issues.

Putting it inside the small shelter also had a risk of being discovered, but the danger was considerably less.

Therefore, to solve the problem, the best approach was to find a way for water vapor not to freeze into ice inside the small shelter.

Chen Xin racked his brains and suddenly struck down with his hammer, a light bulb moment flashing in his mind.

"I’m just being stupid, why not use the system upgrade to fix this!" Chen Xin slapped his forehead hard, secretly annoyed at how the days spent at Qin Lan’s place had muddled his head, forgetting he could use system upgrades to solve the problem.

Chen Xin called up the system interface, glanced at the already four-digit survival points, and felt assured.

These survival points were prepared for an overall upgrade of the shelter; some points were consumed when building the geothermal heat pump, but during these days away from home, his daily survival points gained hadn’t diminished, with almost 40 points acquired each day, enabling him to easily have the survival points needed for upgrading the small shelter.

"Simple Observation Room"

"Upgrade Option One: Increase observation room area, improve building structure, enhance concealment and breathability, improve shortwave antenna signal reception capability, upgrade requires 700 survival points"

"Upgrade Option Two: Increase observation room defense capabilities, improve building strength, enhance thermal protection performance, protect observation port, upgrade requires 1100 survival points"

Neither upgrade option was inexpensive, each focusing on different aspects. However, due to the lack of building materials and considering the existing materials were prepared for the overall upgrade of the shelter, Chen Xin didn’t adopt a method of upgrading both options.

After comparing the two options, he chose to spend 700 points to upgrade the first option—enhancing the small shelter’s concealment and ventilation properties, while also improving the signal reception ability of the shortwave antenna installed here.

Although Chen Xin also desired the second option, he decided not to overly consume survival points. After hesitating for a moment, he chose to give up the upgrade option that would evolve the small shelter into a bunker.

After the upgrade, the area of the small shelter increased slightly, displaying a circular shape, but it wasn’t expanded too much; the observation port remained unchanged.

However, the original ventilation port in the room had turned into a vent pipe buried within the wall, which would conduct ventilation through the external wall surface of the shelter.

This perfectly solved the issue of ice accumulation at the vent inside the room. Moreover, since the vent pipe ran from the outer wall of the observation room, the air expelled from the vent would reduce temperature in the pipe before being released, making it less conspicuous in infrared visibility.

After completing the upgrade, Chen Xin came out from the shelter’s main door and used the infrared vision function of his tactical goggles to observe the location of the observation room. Indeed, he could barely see the traces of air expelled from the vent.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 153 - 151: Preparing to Harvest Rice
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Climbing up to the location of the observation room, Chen Xin wanted to thoroughly inspect the upgraded observation room.

From the outside, the upgraded observation room appeared like a flat oval canned box embedded in the mountain, covered with snow, inconspicuous in the dark environment.

Facing the shelter’s entrance was the observation port, still that narrow slit just enough to peer out from inside, making the entire observation room resemble a machine gun bunker commonly seen in war movies, lacking only the heavy machine gun that Chen Xin hadn’t placed inside.

The vent moved to the "bunker" exterior wall after the upgrade was a louvered design, where air discharged from the shelter flows through the pipes with lower temperature outside the observation room, cooling down significantly before being expelled from the vent on the vertical wall façade.

This structural design resolved Chen Xin’s concern over the problem of icing. Without humid air being expelled from the inside, the observation port would not be sealed by frozen ice chunks, and Chen Xin naturally was relieved from the heavy daily ice removal task, avoiding the daily chore of breaking ice with a small hammer.

Chen Xin felt satisfied with the upgraded observation room. But while satisfied with not having to do ice removal work in the future, he truly realized his physical inadequacies and the flaws in the Protective Suit.

His physical inadequacies were a result of lack of exercise. Although his physical condition improved significantly over the past few months due to farm work, reaching the normal level of an average person in terms of fitness, he was still not considered strong.

So exercising is an option to be included in the planning.

During the few days at the SWAT team’s shelter, Chen Xin, having nothing to do after dinner, was taken by Qin Lan to the SWAT team’s gym for a workout.

Besides feeling a bit muscular sore for a few days, it made Chen Xin realize that his shelter could perhaps add a gym.

After all, it would both serve the purpose of exercising and occupying the free time every evening without spending every night gaming.

Not that gaming is not feasible, but compared to gaming, exercising is more beneficial for health.

Furthermore, Chen Xin’s evening time was relatively ample, allowing him to exercise for an hour and then game for a while, eliminating the problem of choosing one over the other.

Adding a gym is not complex, nor necessarily requiring the division of a separate room, just add some fitness equipment in the living room and exercise after dinner.

This doesn’t even require Chen Xin to consume valuable survival points, just need to get some fitness equipment, perhaps even making some himself to make do.

Having decided, Chen Xin naturally included this in his plan, recording it in his list of upcoming tasks.

Besides this, upgrading and modifying the Protective Suit was another task recorded on Chen Xin’s plan list.

The performance of the Protective Suit itself was not problematic, its anti-corrosion insulation effect had always been excellent. However, as temperatures dropped, what the Protective Suit faced was not corrosive acid rain but the severe cold brought by low temperatures and strong winds, along with the low oxygen environment due to reduced oxygen content.

Although not adjusting would not pose a significant issue, Chen Xin still hoped to add an oxygen supply function to the Protective Suit, ensuring he wouldn’t feel breathless after a period of outdoor physical labor next time.

Also, the Protective Suit needed some adjustments. The previously long hem was good for rain protection and warmth, but he found during physical labor that a long coat was inconvenient for labor, with shorter styles being more appropriate.

Thus, adjusting and modifying the Protective Suit naturally became part of Chen Xin’s modification plan.

Besides these two tasks, fulfilling the promise of making the ground source heat pump for Li Wensheng and Mo Qingyan, and monthly harvesting of the drought-resistant rice in the greenhouse were also pressing tasks, alongside the dealings with Li Yulong...

Counting it down, Chen Xin found he had quite a few tasks to accomplish this month.

Fortunately, most tasks were not urgent, listing and planning them one by one enables Chen Xin to address them all following the planned progress.

The Protective Suit modification and gym were not urgent, doing them later is not an issue. However, Chen Xin needed to complete the promised ground source heat pump for Li Wensheng quickly for a trade to acquire the aquaponics device he wanted.

This matter was previously agreed upon with Li Wensheng, but Chen Xin’s recent stay at the official shelter delayed this work, probably prompting impatience from Li Wensheng.

Without Qin Lan around, Chen Xin’s manufacturing of ground source heat pumps sped up compared to when she was present.

Using the system to disassemble an air conditioner outdoor unit directly, and assembling the parts, then performing an upgrade, proficient Chen Xin took just over an hour to create a new ground source heat pump.

Of course, this ground source heat pump differed from those Chen Xin himself used, lacking the addition of a turbine generator.

Leaving the completed equipment beside the workbench, Chen Xin did not immediately start crafting a second device.

To date, besides the promised unit for Li Wensheng, he only needed to make the device for Mo Qingyan. Other acquaintances also wanted ground source heat pumps, but with Chen Xin absent for several days, no new orders came in, requiring no further production.

As for Mo Qingyan’s unit, Chen Xin planned to add a turbine generator and an aquaponics system, rendering him unable to start until Li Wensheng delivered the promised aquaponics system.

Thus, upon completing Li Wensheng’s ground source heat pump, Chen Xin found himself without further orders to execute.

Yet, this didn’t mean idleness ensued; the greenhouse unattended for several days required managing, and the matured drought-resistant rice awaited harvesting, requiring preparation for the harvest.

Though the drought-resistant rice quantity was modest, manual cutting prompted Chen Xin to think his efficiency over a day might not suffice, and rice cutting is immensely tiring work.

Luckily, having acquired a small farm machine earlier, Chen Xin could use a small harvester for cutting, rather than manually cutting rice.

Of course, before officially commencing the harvest, he also needed to review the unutilized small harvester, understanding its operation to avoid problems arising from improper use.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 154 - 152 Happy Harvest
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The small harvester looks like a hand-held weed eater. Though in terms of structure and principle, the two are almost identical, and a weed eater can be slightly modified into a harvester, there are still some differences between them.

Especially since this one by Chen Xin is a micro combine harvester with a threshing function, there are significant differences in appearance.

The principle of both is to use an engine to drive blades to cut plant stalks. However, a weed eater needs to adjust the blade height as needed to prevent scalping the lawn, while a harvester requires the blades to be a bit bigger and equipped with guide vanes to better cut crop stalks.

To operate, you just need to start the machine, align it with the crops to be harvested, and push it forward at a steady pace.

This small harvester has wheels and tracks at the bottom, so as long as you hold the handles steady, you can smoothly harvest crops without veering off course.

Compared to manual harvesting, mechanical harvesting has significant advantages in efficiency and cost, as humans tire, but machines do not.

This unit of Chen Xin’s is so small it requires manual operation, but slightly larger ones even have seats, allowing one to operate it while seated quite comfortably.

So when the dry rice in the field matured, Chen Xin had already refueled the small harvester and was ready to begin harvesting once it started up.

"Zzz! Zzz!" With the engine running, all Chen Xin needed to do was push the machine forward, and the sharp blades would swiftly sever the rice stalks.

And since Chen Xin’s machine is an integrated combine harvester, the cut rice is crushed and threshed directly by the mechanisms, turning into bagged husked grains with only threshed stalks left on the ground.

Compared to traditional manual harvesting, where one has to grab stalks by hand and use a sickle to cut them, this is much simpler.

After all, manually harvested rice comes down with stalks attached, requiring another round of threshing. Even with a threshing machine, it’s still a labor-intensive task.

But using a combine harvester, there are no such worries; harvested rice can be processed directly into husked grains.

So when a row of rice was harvested, and the grains were in the bag hanging beside the harvester, Chen Xin couldn’t help but whistle cheerfully.

Initially, before acquiring small farm machinery, he thought harvesting rice would be exhausting work, but he didn’t expect it to be this easy with machinery.

This naturally boosted his enthusiasm greatly, pushing the harvester to continue with a quick pace, aiming to finish his rice fields within an hour.

The part Chen Xin needed to harvest wasn’t much, totaling 200 square meters, with 50 square meters planted with carrots, so he just needed to harvest 150 square meters of rice fields.

However, even with a small harvester making the work easy, Chen Xin still had to bow his head, almost crouching over the harvester’s handlebars since the overhead hydroponic trays were rather low, preventing him from straightening up.

Of course, this was still much more comfortable than bending over to harvest rice.

So it didn’t take long for Chen Xin to finish harvesting the 150 square meters of dry rice.

The harvested rice was threshed by the combine harvester and placed in bags on the machine.

The bags weren’t sealed but were stuffed full, and when Chen Xin tried to lift them, he found them so heavy he could barely manage.

After harvesting, the 150 square meters of rice filled three large burlap sacks, with each averaging about fifty to sixty kilos.

Of course, these are husked grains, which still need drying before long-term storage.

While directly dehusking them into new rice is possible, there’s still too much moisture, which could break the grains.

This process wouldn’t usually be troublesome—find a flat area and spread the grains to dry, and any smooth cement surface could dry rice.

Chen Xin even remembers seeing farmers dry rice on roads, making the task mostly reliant on weather rather than location.

But now, without the sun as a convenient direct heat source, and with the grains unable to be placed outdoors in the snow, drying can only be done inside the shelter.

Considering the lack of space inside the shelter, Chen Xin decided to use mechanical drying to remove the grains’ moisture instead of natural air drying.

Though mechanically dried grains have a slightly poorer texture, it’s better than letting them mold or sprout.

As for how to perform mechanical drying, with a continuous heat source from a ground-source heat pump, solving this problem isn’t difficult.

Either using trays to bake or blow hot air with a fan, both can remove moisture from the grains.

After lugging the three sacks out of the growing greenhouse, Chen Xin didn’t place them in the storage room but left them open in the communal area.

Without a dryer ready, storing these grains immediately isn’t possible, leaving them open is the correct approach.

As for making a dryer, Chen Xin felt a bit puzzled, having neither built nor seen one before.

Even the materials on his computer had no related images or texts, which worried him.

How should he build it? Should he really make a big tray to bake?

However, this idea was quickly dismissed as impractical in his mind, for if baking fails to dry due to low temperature, it’s fine. But if the temperature’s too high and ends up cooking the grains, that would be awkward.

Yet after pondering hard, Chen Xin recalled having used a clothes dryer and his mother using a household dryer before.

Both operate on the principle of drying wet items with warm air in relatively sealed spaces, removing moisture.

Could he make something similar to dehydrate the grains?

Chen Xin thought it over and found the idea feasible, but he decided to draft plans first before proceeding.

Since he hadn’t tried it hands-on, any failure would waste materials, though with his system, he could disassemble and recycle them, but damaging the grains meant losing at least a third of the year’s harvest.

Thus, for caution, Chen Xin decided to draw up equipment plans first and meticulously examine its feasibility.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 155 - 153: Designing a Dryer
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When Chen Xin was pulling his hair out trying to design a drying box for grains, Li Wensheng delivered the two sets of aquaponics equipment he wanted, exchanging them for the ground source heat pump equipment that Chen Xin had promised him.

Initially, Chen Xin said he could install it on-site, but Li Wensheng did not get to enjoy Chen Xin’s on-site installation service.

Because when he came to pick up the goods from Chen Xin, he encountered Li Yulong, who was also there to see Chen Xin. This wealthy second-generation heir, upon learning about this matter, exchanged an Iberian ham and a box of canned tuna for the drilling equipment from Chen Xin, becoming the designated installer.

Originally, Chen Xin didn’t want to do this, but Li Yulong’s persuasion convinced him.

"I think, Brother Chen, you’re being too bold. Don’t get me wrong, but if someone plans to deceive you into leaving the shelter under the guise of installation and then tries to rob or even kill you, what would you do?" Although Li Yulong was using the worst-case scenario to assess others, he still felt that Chen Xin was being somewhat too generous.

After hearing Li Yulong’s advice, Chen Xin considered it and agreed to Li Yulong’s request to take over the installation of the ground source heat pump.

After all, if Chen Xin were to do the installation himself without spending survival points, it would still be a troublesome task.

Moreover, the key point is that Chen Xin also found Li Yulong’s reasoning sound. Although he had a gun, generally speaking, it wouldn’t lead to trouble. But if someone deceived him and launched a surprise attack, it could still prove fatal.

Perhaps Chen Xin had been unguarded before because those who came to see him were acquaintances, and people like Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan were indeed trustworthy, so he subconsciously overlooked this issue.

But now that Li Yulong mentioned it, Chen Xin felt he should keep this matter at heart and pay more attention to personal safety.

This incident was considered an episode; nevertheless, although Li Yulong helped Li Wensheng install the ground source heat pump, it still brought Chen Xin an income of four hundred survival points and an increase of four survival points per day.

As for Li Yulong, he successfully reached a partnership with Chen Xin.

It’s indisputable that he’s one of the city’s prominent businessmen. After convincing his father, during the days Chen Xin was with the SWAT team, Li Yulong’s father had already made connections with the city government to obtain a business permit.

The city government allowed Li Yulong to run market operations and farming ventures, but correspondingly, taxes still had to be paid as before, and regulatory personnel would be dispatched.

But having obtained the permit was enough for Li Yulong, as it meant he could carry out his business expansion plans.

And there’s no doubt that Chen Xin is a particularly crucial part of his business landscape, as both the market and farms require Chen Xin’s ground source heat pumps.

Although for Li Yulong, having connections with the city government made obtaining other heating equipment not difficult, as a wealthy second-generation heir, he always chooses the best.

After cross-comparing various options, Li Yulong confirmed that Chen Xin’s ground source heat pump is the most efficient heating equipment and can provide self-sufficiency.

This point is precisely the most important in the current era. Regardless of coal or fuel, the city relies on external inputs, transported by rail.

If the railway encounters a malfunction causing supplies not to arrive timely, it could easily impact market and farm operations.

Thus, a ground source heat pump capable of better self-maintenance became Li Yulong’s first choice.

It was precisely for this reason that he personally approached Chen Xin twice, bringing along what are now considered extremely valuable meats to make the transaction, aiming to secure the opportunity to collaborate with Chen Xin.

After comprehensive consideration, Chen Xin did not reject Li Yulong; instead, he agreed to invest with ground source heat pump equipment for a twenty percent stake.

For the whole market and farm project, Li Yulong himself took fifty percent of the shares, Chen Xin received twenty percent, and the remaining thirty percent was divided, with ten percent for taxes, ten percent to the city government, and ten percent allocated to the SWAT team.

Chen Xin had no objections to this distribution plan. Getting a twenty percent stake was sufficient. As for separating city government and taxes, it made Chen Xin admire Li Yulong’s business acumen.

The portion given to the SWAT team was also appropriate, since current law and order are maintained by the SWAT team after all.

So after the transaction intention was reached, Chen Xin accepted the resources Li Yulong had delivered without any hesitation.

Even before the disaster, Iberian ham was a rare item, and Chen Xin had only bought a small packet to taste the flavor. A whole ham cost at least tens of thousands, and that’s before the disaster.

In this post-apocalyptic world, such a piece of pork leg is a definite luxury and rarity, a hard currency for trading whatever one wishes.

However, Chen Xin had no thoughts of trading this and hung it along with the Jinhua ham he previously received, planning to savor them slowly himself.

These two hams, if eaten carefully, could last him a year or two.

After reaching the transaction intention with Li Yulong, Chen Xin also handed him a list of supplies he needed, explaining to him how his share dividends would be converted into these supplies.

Being aware of Li Yulong’s plans, Chen Xin guessed that he and the city government would likely introduce some certificates as general equivalents for market transactions. Yet, such certificates to Chen Xin were far less beneficial than actual supplies. If he wanted supplies, he’d have to exchange for them, so he directly asked for supplies.

As for how to procure these supplies, that was what Li Yulong needed to worry about, while Chen Xin kept his head down in designing his grain-drying oven.

Chen Xin had considered making it like his mother’s home dryer, resembling a round steamer with layered structures, but after sketching the design, he dismissed this idea and decided to create a big cabinet filled with layered trays.

Although the round structure was good, the segmented layered grid design was quite complex. A drawer-style large cabinet was comparatively simpler.

A large cabinet made of metal, with drawer-style trays forming layered intervals inside, this was Chen Xin’s envisioned drying machine.

Just by laying the grains on the trays and continually blowing hot air into the cabinet, he could dry the harvested grains and remove moisture.

Yet, Chen Xin still hesitated over the form of the trays. Although they would be drawer-style trays, they were undoubtedly large trays. The bottom of the trays—should they be made of impermeable metal sheets or breathable mesh?

Looking at his drafted design, Chen Xin fell into hesitation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 156 - 154: Choosing a Dryer (Seeking Monthly Votes)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin was still a bit hesitant about the tray design. Although it was supposed to be drawer-style, it would still essentially be a large tray. Should the bottom of the tray be made of airtight iron sheets, or should it be made of breathable mesh?

Chen Xin looked at the design drawing he had sketched and fell into hesitation.

The entire cabinet design wasn’t difficult; it could simply be made into a four-legged standing cabinet. As for the bottom, just placing a blower to blow hot air upward would already be a perfect design.

The challenge for Chen Xin was the design of the tray used for holding the rice grains inside the cabinet. He had difficulty deciding on what structure to use.

In the whole cabinet, Chen Xin planned to place about twelve layers of trays so that more rice grains could be dried at once while ensuring ventilation. With three bags of over two hundred pounds of rice, it would take about four or five batches to dry everything.

But the question was whether to use iron trays or mesh trays?

Iron trays have better load-bearing capacity and can hold more rice grains. With mesh, the load capacity isn’t as good, and it clearly would hold fewer rice grains, which might mean more work for him.

Moreover, the iron tray structure is quite simple and relatively easy to make; just cut a piece of iron sheet and fold the edges to finish.

With mesh, however, you first have to make the frame and then secure the mesh, not just by nailing a few nails, but by using a pressure strip to tightly press the four sides so it won’t fall apart.

The main issue is that the mesh isn’t very durable; even wire mesh can unravel if it frequently bears weight.

However, if you could get hold of old kitchen strainers or sieves, that would be ideal; they are much sturdier than wire mesh and have fine meshes yet still enough breathability, making them perfect for this job.

It’s just that such items are hard to find now, and even if available, they aren’t large enough. Making a fine metal mesh oneself, even with system assistance, is a demanding task.

After all, without blueprints, using the system’s power only processes the materials into Chen Xin’s desired shapes, while assembly of the components is still up to him.

So, while sketching design drawings and pulling his hair, Chen Xin also asked Li Yulong to help get a grain dryer or some metal mesh.

However, even Li Yulong, who wanted to work the trade market, found it a bit challenging, saying he would do his best to find them but couldn’t guarantee their availability.

Chen Xin also felt a bit helpless about it. If suitable meshes really couldn’t be found, he would have to consider abandoning the standing cabinet design and making it into a drum.

What’s known as a drum is essentially a horizontally placed rotating cylinder with baffles inside. After putting in the grains, it’s heated with electricity while rotating, using the heat to evaporate the moisture from the grains until dry.

Drum-style and upright cabinet-style are two common dryer types regardless of size; however, relatively speaking, drum-type electric heating undoubtedly consumes more electricity than dryers using blowers.

Precisely due to energy saving considerations, Chen Xin still hoped to use an upright blower dryer instead of a drum-type electric heater.

Fortunately, after the rice grains were harvested, they could be stored temporarily for a few days, and the dryer wasn’t immediately needed. Moreover, if worse comes to worst, Chen Xin could spread the grains on the shelter’s floor for natural air drying.

Of course, natural air drying is incredibly inefficient and takes up a lot of space, as over two hundred pounds of grain spread out would cover a large area. For Chen Xin, the only conceivable place to do this would be the passage connecting the shelter’s entrance to the interior.

The tens of meters long passage has a fully hardened cement surface, and is straight and flat. Spreading the grains there could indeed provide natural air drying.

However, doing so might need a week to truly dry the grains.

Moreover, considering that the internal air humidity of the shelter is relatively high is necessary. Although the ventilation system expels some moisture, and the water circulation system Chen Xin installed at the earliest stages of building the shelter recovers some moisture, the existence of a growing greenhouse means the relative humidity remains higher due to plant transpiration.

Thus, air drying like that can dehydrate the grains but cannot achieve the effect a dryer would, so there will still be some moisture remaining, affecting storage.

Yet if no other options are available, that’s the only solution.

Before addressing these issues, however, Chen Xin had one more important task to do.

That was... to cook rice!

Freshly harvested rice couldn’t just go without cooking a fragrant bowl of rice to taste the fruits of his labor, right?

Dehulling rice grains isn’t particularly troublesome; if the rice is already dried, even hand-rubbing could remove the husks, turning the grains into brown rice.

However, if insufficient moisture is removed, tools would be necessary for processing.

Before the disaster, even in rural areas, few people manually husked grains; rural areas typically had grain processing stations with machines that, when rice grains were poured in, would process them into white rice, and the husks would be collected as bran.

Bran is valuable; though just husks detached from rice grains, it does contain some nutrients, and even some scholars have discovered that rice bran oil inside can help lower cholesterol.

Moreover, bran itself is edible, although most bran now is used for feeding livestock, in ancient times, it wasn’t uncommon for impoverished people to eat bran.

The idiom "eat bran and swallow vegetables," or the northern dishes such as bran wotou or bran dough are all evidence of bran consumed in the past by less fortunate individuals.

Of course, Chen Xin wasn’t planning on eating bran but rather saving it to see if he could raise some livestock himself.

He certainly couldn’t raise pigs, but perhaps chickens or rabbits could be achievable for him.

The crucial issue is getting the right seed stock, which is hard, and even if things exist in the city’s underground planting areas, acquiring them would be difficult.

As for the rice grains after the bran is removed, they technically aren’t the same rice people ate before the disaster. Before the disaster, the rice was polished further, removing the layers and germ to make it white.

True rice with only the husk removed should be light brown or beige, which was what became popular before the disaster as brown rice, or whole grains.

Because it retains the layer and germ, it has relatively higher nutritional value.

For Chen Xin, he decided to dehull the rice grains through pounding.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 157 - 155: Pounding Rice
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Pounding rice is a very ancient and practical agricultural production activity, with its history tracing back to the early stages of human civilization.

It originated during the dawn of human civilization with the earliest agricultural practices like slash-and-burn cultivation. In Flame Country, it has been passed down for thousands of years, until modern mechanical processing gradually replaced this age-old agricultural activity.

However, even so, in the vast rural areas of Flame Country, this ancient skill can still be observed.

The word "pound," pronounced the same as "chong," means putting something into a stone mortar or pestle and using a rod to mash it.

This is an ancient pictograph, with the bottom being the character for mortar, and the upper part in ancient script and small seal script depicts two people holding a pestle, vividly and graphically expressing the meaning of the word.

Once you understand the meaning of this word, recreating this ancient agricultural production activity becomes simple.

Find a sufficiently sturdy container, put rice grains inside, and use a heavy object to continuously pound until all the rice husks are broken, revealing the grain, which completes the rice pounding process.

This container in the vast rural areas is usually made of stone mortar, with even more primitive and ancient ones being burned out of tree stumps.

As for the tools used for pounding, wood, stone, or metal can all be used as long as they are heavy enough to crush the rice husks.

Chen Xin adopted a method of using metal materials to make a large bowl and a metal rod, then putting rice grains into the bowl and pounding them with the metal rod.

Because the rice grains were not dried, and he did not pound all the rice, but just processed a little to cook, a large bowl was sufficient for pounding rice.

Putting the golden rice grains into the bowl and forcefully pounding them with the metal rod, watching the rice hulls gradually break and reveal the light brown grains inside, brought a significant sense of the joy of labor.

Especially since these rice grains were not dried, they emitted a faint fragrant scent, further uplifting the mood, making Chen Xin eager to taste the rice he had grown himself.

Since Chen Xin only pounded a bowl of rice, it did not take him long to finish pounding the rice.

But at this point, the rice and husk had not been separated, mingling together couldn’t go directly to cooking, needing another screening to separate the rice and husk.

The most traditional method Chen Xin knew was using a flat sieve to sift the pounded rice and husk mixture, with the heavier rice grains falling back into the sieve under the force of gravity, while the lightweight husk or empty grains would be blown away during the sifting process.

Dust mixed with rice grains would also fall through the gaps of the flat sieve, ultimately leaving behind clean rice grains.

However, at this point, Chen Xin did not have a flat sieve on hand, nor could he make one himself, as flat sieves are woven from bamboo strips, and where could bamboo be found in the post-apocalyptic world?

If using metal materials, that would purely be asking for trouble, as how much effort would it take to sift with an iron flat sieve?

Thus, at this point, some relatively modern methods are needed.

Sifting rice itself is a classic and simple physical knowledge application. Although the ancestors might not have understood the physics principles involved, it did not prevent them from doing it.

Knowing that the principle of sifting rice involves the weight difference between rice grains and husks, using wind to carry away the husk and empty grains, it becomes manageable.

Chen Xin just needed to make a blower and blow through the rice and husk mixture to remove the husk.

A blower is not hard to make, especially since separating husk from rice does not require much wind power; even an electric fan or a hand-cranked blower can do the job.

With the help of the system, making a hand-cranked blower on the workbench and installing a funnel at the blower’s outlet with an opening on one side to facilitate husk separation, Chen Xin could easily obtain clean rice grains.

The blown-out husk was carefully collected by him in a small bag, and the light brown rice grains placed in a bowl, making Chen Xin impatient to taste their flavor.

Wash the rice grains with water, place them in an iron pot, and add an appropriate amount of clear water to cook; the rest is just waiting for the rice to be cooked.

During the waiting time for the rice to cook, Chen Xin went to the food storeroom to cut some Jinhua ham and Iberian ham, took a cabbage, as well as canned pork and canned tuna, to celebrate the day’s harvest.

Iberian ham is raw ham, best eaten by slicing and eating directly.

Though it can also be used to make salads or paired with bread and fruits, or used to make soups and other hot dishes, the former is impractical, the latter too wasteful.

So Chen Xin just cut a few slices to pair with the rice.

Jinhua ham has a wider range of use; it can be used to stir-fry cabbage, simmer soup, or even lay on rice and cook together for a perfect delicacy.
