Therefore, regarding Jinhua ham, Chen Xin cut half of what he sliced into thin slices intending to place them on rice, and the other half into crumbs intending to stir-fry with cabbage.

As for canned pork and canned tuna, these are merely heat-and-serve side dishes.

Humming a cheerful tune while slicing cabbage, Chen Xin felt he had rediscovered the joy of cooking after such a long time.

Being able to take the chef certification exam without being a professional chef proves that Chen Xin truly loves cooking, but the post-apocalyptic lack of ingredients had suppressed his culinary skills, forcing him to eat ready-made food and stifling his desire to cook for far too long.

Though now there are only simple ingredients, he could manage to create one or two dishes.

Compared to simple seasoning shelter food, the meal he was preparing might be simple too, but the taste and sense of happiness and fulfillment differ vastly.

The former merely fills the stomach, whereas the latter offers a true enjoyment of food.

In this extremely cold and dark post-apocalyptic world, being able to savor real culinary joy is an extravagant act.

However, in many construction games, the rich food enhances the happiness of citizens and boosts the morale of warriors, proving this logic to be true and reasonable.

Eating well truly grants immense happiness and satisfaction.

This Chen Xin deeply realized as he added Jinhua ham to the rice, which emitted a rich aroma as the rice cooked, causing him to swallow his saliva.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 158 - 156 Thinking Pointlessly After Eating
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After finishing the first meal post-harvest, and the first proper rice meal since arriving at the refuge, Chen Xin patted his bulging stomach, feeling genuinely satisfied.

Having a full stomach had become a long-lost feeling since the apocalypse struck. After all, having food to eat and eating until your belly is full are two entirely different things.

The former is considered semi-full, while the latter is eating until you’re stuffed. The difference in food consumption between the two is significant; the former might be 0.8, and the latter reaches 1.5, enough to have another meal.

So Chen Xin felt extremely satisfied and very blissful.

After eating and drinking his fill, Chen Xin didn’t turn on the game console today. Instead, he planned to play some games on his laptop before continuing to work on the dryer blueprints.

Using the laptop, he could start work right after gaming without having to turn off the TV or game console, making things a bit more convenient.

However, after turning on the computer, Chen Xin inexplicably opened a refuge game.

It was a simple pixelated side-scrolling game. The main storyline was about a family building an underground refuge after a nuclear war to escape the apocalypse.

This game was likely downloaded by Chen Xin a long time ago because it was small and he never deleted it, leaving it on the desktop.

In this game, players need to control a game character to venture into urban wastelands to gather resources, expand their refuge, and ultimately collect parts to repair a car for escaping the apocalypse.

During the game, characters would face various crises and difficulties, encountering enemies, excessive radiation, illness or hunger due to lack of water and food, and even psychological issues from committing acts like shooting people...

All these issues needed to be handled by the players to keep the refuge running and the characters alive until they fixed the car to escape the apocalypse.

But as Chen Xin played, he discovered a fundamental problem that made him want to laugh.

Because he himself was in the apocalypse, playing this game gave him a vivid impression. Some issues he encountered in the game, while some he didn’t—but that was aside. However, the main character surviving solely by scavenging for food in the abandoned city was something Chen Xin couldn’t accept.

This might reflect the biggest difference between Eastern and Western civilizations. Westerners always think about looting resources to develop, while Easterners think more about farming.

For someone from the Flame Country, if they encounter an apocalypse, once the environment stabilizes, they will find means to plant something, even if it’s just a pot with tomatoes. Plant something they must.

This mindset difference also led to Chen Xin’s survival strategy differing vastly from the game’s.

Of course, it might be because he had a system and perks, making farming feasible. If he was like other refuges without conditions for a greenhouse, he wouldn’t be able to farm either.

However, the mindset of wanting to farm is deeply ingrained; given the means, people would still try to plant something.

In the view of the Flame Country people, relying on the sky, earth, or parents is nothing compared to relying on oneself.

Hoping to gather resources from abandoned cities, how’s that more stable than farming? Growing your own food lets you eat your own produce, and surplus can be traded with others. Isn’t this development model appealing?

Moreover, without taking the risk of venturing into city ruins to gather resources, doesn’t it greatly enhance safety?

If one could farm peacefully and have food, why risk life and limb battling others for a bit of food?

Of course, if truly without conditions, there’s no choice. But if conditions permit, Chen Xin believed no one would willingly risk life and limb for food rather than farm steadily.

Perhaps this highlights the difference in perspectives between the Flame Country and other countries. Western perspectives on the apocalypse resemble beasts in a dark forest, everyone cautious, hiding, wary of others, struggling to get a bite, and upon discovery, a vicious fight ensues— the defeated become the victor’s prey.

But for people from the Flame Country, they prefer to cultivate and farm together, then build a home, unite and strengthen to live better.

It’s because of such deep-rooted beliefs and sufficient conditions that Chen Xin took special care of his greenhouse, not only planting upland rice and vegetables to eat but also trading with Li Wensheng for aquaponics equipment, planning to implement aquaponics in his greenhouse, raising fish to diversify his diet.

However, although Li Wensheng delivered the aquaponics equipment, Chen Xin hadn’t started raising fish immediately. One reason was the equipment wasn’t installed, and Chen Xin had nowhere to raise fish. Another was Li Wensheng hadn’t delivered live fish.

These days, Chen Xin was occupied with harvesting rice, delaying the aquaponics equipment installation.

Now that the rice was harvested and he had tasted the new rice cooked meal, Chen Xin naturally remembered this matter.

For the aquaponics equipment, Chen Xin initially planned to integrate it into the greenhouse, combining it with hydroponics, cultivating vegetables while raising fish simultaneously.

It’s not hard to achieve since aquaponics was designed for hydroponics; integrating the equipment posed no conflict or unsuitability.

However, the aquaponics equipment Li Wensheng delivered is quite small. According to him, it’s a small household set, designed for a 30-square-meter hydroponic area and a 3-cubic-meter aquaculture water body.

For a household’s consumption, this was sufficient for 3-5 people, but for Chen Xin’s greenhouse, one set doesn’t cover even a fifth of the area.

If Chen Xin wanted to convert the hydroponic system in his greenhouse to an aquaponics system, he’d need either multiple setups or use the current equipment as upgrade materials to upgrade the entire greenhouse.

However, upgrading the greenhouse this time would require three times the previous survival points, and more crucially, Chen Xin lacked enough materials. The aquaponics equipment alone would only reduce the survival points consumption by 10%.

Fortunately, his greenhouse’s hydroponic planting trays were separated, not an integrated large trough. He could first connect part of the trays to the aquaponics system.

After all, he only traded with Li Wensheng for ten live fish, so he wouldn’t need a large-scale aquaculture water body immediately.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 159 - 157: Plantation Location Selection
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

With the system’s installation feature, Chen Xin just needed a thought to move a set of aquaponics equipment originally stored in the storage room to the greenhouse, connecting it to several hydroponic planting basin tanks.

The aquaponics system has relatively strict requirements for the oxygen content, pH value, total nitrogen, temperature, and alkalinity of the water, so after Chen Xin installed the equipment, even though he didn’t start running it, he still needed to use the monitoring instruments included in the equipment to check the water quality to ensure it met the requirements.

Fortunately, Chen Xin was using groundwater, and since he had only been adding nutrient solutions to grow plants, the water quality was still fine.

However, due to the recent increase in groundwater temperature, the water used in the shelter inevitably had a slightly higher temperature, which also caused the water temperature in the hydroponic planting basins in the greenhouse to be a bit high.

There’s no problem with growing plants, but if used for fish, unless they are tropical fish, the fish might feel uncomfortable.

So when actually using it, Chen Xin needs to consider controlling the water temperature to avoid it becoming too high and turning fish farming into making fish soup.

And this is actually easy to control because the groundwater Chen Xin uses is first pumped up and stored in water tanks and is only used when needed, then transported from the tanks to the water pipes in the shelter.

Therefore, as long as the groundwater pump draws up the warmer groundwater and it’s not used immediately but allowed to cool for a while before using it, there won’t be any problems.

As for the hot spring pool that Qin Lan wanted, it’s easy to manage, just add another insulated tank to keep the drawn hot water at a constant temperature, or even more simply, use the water immediately after drawing it.

After all, most so-called hot springs are either too hot or too cold, so true bathing-friendly hot springs are rare, hence most hot springs require heating or cooling, and some are just hot spring water brought to the pool and boiled.

With these thoughts and ideas swirling in his mind, Chen Xin looked at the blueprint of the dryer on his computer but had no inspiration, feeling irked, so he turned off the computer, took a shower, and went to bed.

————————————————————

Li Yulong was very efficient in his affairs, perhaps because even in the apocalyptic times, his father still had people working for him.

So, Chen Xin didn’t have to wait long; after two days, he came looking for him again.

"Xiaolong, have you got the dryer I asked for or the mesh net?" Invited to his luxurious RV and sitting on the sofa, seeing Li Yulong’s joyful expression, Chen Xin curiously asked.

Li Yulong didn’t keep him in suspense and said with a smile, "Brother Chen, you really are lucky. When you mentioned needing a dryer, I went to look for suppliers, and guess what? I really found one!"

"You actually found a dryer?" Hearing Li Yulong say so, Chen Xin couldn’t help but show a pleased expression and pursued, "What kind of dryer is it, how big is it, and how does it perform?"

"I found it. It’s a fruit and vegetable machine from my dad’s hotel kitchen, used for making dried fruits. It was quite big when we bought it back then, capable of drying about thirty to forty jin of fruit at once, and it features an LCD touchscreen and intelligent temperature control. It’s really good stuff." Li Yulong showed a look of seeking approval while also boasting about the item he found.

After hearing Li Yulong’s explanation, Chen Xin widened his eyes in surprise and said, "A fruit and vegetable machine?"

"Yes, a fruit and vegetable machine! Although it’s for drying fruits, I found the manual stating it’s also okay for grains. Plus, it’s practically brand new, bought only in the second half of last year, and hardly used." Li Yulong hadn’t expected to find such a thing either.

Li Yulong’s description moved Chen Xin’s heart, prompting him to ask, "What’s the power consumption like? Is it big?"

"The power consumption is just five hundred watts; this thing doesn’t use more electricity than an induction cooker." Having anticipated Chen Xin’s concerns, Li Yulong took out the already prepared manual and handed it to Chen Xin: "The item is a bit big because it’s used in hotels, but I’ve brought it over, right on my vehicle, ready for Brother Chen to take inside anytime."

Taking the manual Li Yulong handed over, Chen Xin seriously flipped through it.

This fruit and vegetable machine, as Li Yulong said, is a bit large, but not too big, under a meter tall. Although marked in the manual as weighing forty kilograms, it’s not overly cumbersome.

It might only dry thirty to forty jin of grains at a time, but having usable equipment is already excellent.

Chen Xin was very satisfied and nodded, saying, "It’s really nice, in such circumstances finding such a machine is already rare, help me bring it inside, please."

"Okay, as long as Brother Chen is satisfied!" Li Yulong, still smiling broadly, nodded and instructed the bodyguard who came with them to help move the equipment into the shelter for Chen Xin.

After setting up the fruit and vegetable machine in the public area, against the wall, and connecting the power, seeing it could start up normally, Chen Xin then moved a bag of rice originally stored in the corner over to try out the machine.

Seeing this, Li Yulong told the bodyguard to head back to the vehicle outside, while he stayed eagerly nearby, helping Chen Xin place the rice onto the machine’s mesh trays.

Since it was the first time using it, Chen Xin set it to a relatively low temperature and only set it for an hour, intending to first see how it worked.

Seeing Chen Xin trying out the machine, Li Yulong didn’t rush to leave but instead started discussing with Chen Xin about setting up a greenhouse after obtaining a geothermal pump from him.

"Xiaolong, you’re planning to set up a farm, with the geothermal pump from me solving the heating problem, but where do you plan to establish this farm?" Chen Xin looked at Li Yulong and asked him this crucial question.

This is an important question because under the current conditions, even with the geothermal pump provided by Chen Xin, it’s still impossible to place the farm structures aboveground, not only due to the harsh environment but also because of the severe cold affecting construction.

In the extreme cold where water freezes instantly, how do you mix cement and build walls?

Even if you use steel and glass to construct a greenhouse, how can such a fragile structure withstand environmental erosion?

Compared to expending massive resources on building a farm on the surface, finding a suitable underground space is a more sensible approach, even if it means manually digging tunnels is better than constructing a house aboveground.

Therefore, Chen Xin was very curious about where Li Yulong, who had the support of the city government and his father, would decide to set up the farm.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 160 - 158: Chen Xin’s Talent
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Regarding the location selection, the city government did provide an option. They opened up five small shelters, which had previously been paused due to supply issues, for me to choose from." Li Yulong was clearly considering the issue of location as well, explaining to Chen Xin while also mentioning the locations of the five small shelters.

Of course, there were more than five small shelters that had been closed in the city, but due to their locations or other reasons, the other small shelters were not among Li Yulong’s options to choose from.

The currently available small shelters for him to choose from are located around the city government, city bureau, train station, and shopping center, with one near the train station and one near the shopping center, leaving the remaining three near the city bureau and city government—one of which is situated right between the two major shelters of the city government and city bureau.

For these five small shelters being in such locations, both Chen Xin and Li Yulong were well aware that this was a restriction imposed by the city government on Li Yulong.

After all, even though the city government agreed to Li Yulong setting up a trade market and plantation, it’s evident they still wanted to regulate it.

Even with regulatory personnel in place, the trade market and plantation location had to be set somewhere visible to the city government’s monitoring.

This arrangement not only makes it convenient for oversight but also facilitates transactions between the city government and Li Yulong.

"These five places..." Upon hearing the five location options mentioned by Li Yulong, Chen Xin couldn’t help but touch his forehead and asked him, "Xiaolong, what are your thoughts?"

"The train station and shopping center, one focuses on material circulation and the other on material storage. Choosing these locations would mean requiring connections and collaborations with the major shelters at the train station or shopping center." For Chen Xin’s question, Li Yulong did not immediately share his thoughts; instead, he began analyzing the advantages of each location one by one.

Listening to Li Yulong’s analysis, Chen Xin nodded and continued, "The remaining three locations are all near the city government or city bureau. Since you’ve already established cooperation with the city government and the special police force, being close to these two major shelters doesn’t matter. So, in this sense, these three places have an advantage over the first two locations because you don’t need to add additional partners."

Li Yulong nodded affirmatively, agreeing with Chen Xin’s viewpoint: "That’s true. There are already enough people involved in the three-party cooperation, and bringing in another party to divide the pie would only annoy the city government and the special police force."

"So you’re inclined to choose one of the latter three locations?" Chen Xin understood Li Yulong’s implication: "Then, is it the city government or the city bureau?"

"I’ll choose the city bureau." Li Yulong gave the expected answer to Chen Xin.

Chen Xin nodded, not saying much more. Instead, turning to ask him about the plantation plans: "Since you’ve chosen the location, how do you plan to handle the plantation? Soil cultivation? Hydroponic farm? Or aquaponics?"

"I haven’t thought deeply about this. My father contacted an agriculture professor from the University of Engineering to help design it. Once the plantation is set up, the professor and his research team will work for me to manage the plantation." Li Yulong was indeed clueless about professional knowledge, but he knew how to leverage resources to achieve his goals.

This answer made Chen Xin pause for a moment, but he nodded understandingly.

"Initially, I plan to do as you do, Brother Yu, by planting some rice and vegetables to ensure food supply before considering other options. If conditions allow later, I’d also like to raise fish and plant fruit to diversify the product range." While Li Yulong didn’t know how to farm, he understood how to grasp market demand.

Chen Xin slightly nodded, not expressing any opinion on his plan. After considering for a moment, he asked Li Yulong: "Since you have agricultural experts planning for you, I suppose the plantation should be manageable. But have you considered raising poultry or livestock? I’m not sure about other places, but the city government probably has the stocks, right?"

"Yes, but no deal was reached." For this question, Li Yulong answered very definitively.

"Nothing was agreed upon, rather than being non-negotiable?" Chen Xin understood Li Yulong’s meaning, paused, and then continued: "Since that’s the case, let’s leave it for the future. But for now, Xiaolong, could you get me a batch of seeds? The quantity doesn’t need to be much, but the variety should be diversified. The seed package I ordered before the apocalypse didn’t arrive, so I’m really short on seeds right now."

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s request for seeds, Li Yulong hesitated for a bit but quickly agreed.

Once they finalized this matter, Li Yulong, feeling it was about time, was ready to take his leave, but before leaving, he confirmed the ground source heat pump’s supply issue with Chen Xin one last time.

To this question, Chen Xin stated that if there are materials, there will be supplies. For high-power or larger equipment, as long as the required materials are available, he could make it.

Satisfied with the response he wanted, Li Yulong left content.

Chen Xin then closed the shelter door and returned inside, attending to his drying grains.

————————————————

"Young Master, why do you value him so much? It’s just a ground source heat pump. Our boss could also find someone to do it; why do you specifically want to cooperate with him?" On the way home, Li Yulong’s bodyguard asked this question out of curiosity.

Faced with this inquiry from his bodyguard, Li Yulong simply glanced at him, then replied: "What I value is not just Brother Chen’s ability to make ground source heat pumps, but the person himself."

"Is this person that impressive? It’s rare to see you admire someone so much, Young Master." Li Yulong’s bodyguard, having followed him for many years, knew clearly how prideful his master was.

As a wealthy second-generation, Li Yulong rarely acknowledged anyone in the city, but obviously, Chen Xin was not just anyone in his eyes.

"When I talked about setting up a market and plantation, including with my dad, I explained it as intending to implement a slave system where people are caught to work for free, with maybe some wages at most. Only Brother Chen understood right away that I wanted to create an economic cycle. Don’t you think such a person deserves admiration?" Li Yulong looked at his bodyguard, seeing his enlightened expression before continuing: "Brother Chen may appear ordinary and not as skilled as professional experts, but he always manages to grasp the essence of things. That’s what I admire the most about him."

"Hearing you say that, this Mr. Chen Xin is indeed remarkable." The bodyguard complimented Li Yulong.

Li Yulong merely smiled, reminding the bodyguard, "Remember to have someone pick one of each type of seed we have at home and deliver them to Brother Chen."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 161 - 159 All-Terrain Vehicles
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Although Li Yulong brought Chen Xin a fruit and vegetable machine, it’s still not like those large drying machines. Chen Xin had to slowly dry the harvested rice batch by batch.

Fortunately, this machine can dry twenty trays of grain at a time, processing thirty to forty jin each time. A bag of grain can be dried in two rounds, so three bags take six rounds.

He occasionally reached in to toss the grain. Besides swapping the moist bottom layer to the top, Chen Xin also felt the dryness level with his hand.

Only when he confirmed the rice was completely dry, with no trace of moisture left, could he bag and store them.

More than two hundred jin of rice, after drying and husking, would yield about one hundred seventy jin of rice, enough to feed Chen Xin for most of the year.

If he could harvest twice a year, that would guarantee his food supply. In the greenhouse, without worrying about temperature and humidity changes, he could plant under ideal conditions, with ninety to one hundred ten-day cycles for three seasons a year.

Calculated this way, Chen Xin could harvest roughly six hundred, close to seven hundred jin of rice annually, resulting in more than five hundred jin of rice, plus over a hundred jin of husk.

He also harvested several hundred jin of vegetables monthly from his greenhouse, enough food to sustain two to three people.

"As long as the old farmer brings fish, and Xiaolong and Alan get new seeds... it’s finally worry-free eating and drinking." Chen Xin calculated the output of his greenhouse, feeling relieved.

There is an old saying in Flame Country, "With grain in hand, there’s no panic in mind," which is always pertinent.

After tallying the resources he had, ensuring the food supply for three people, Chen Xin finally put the plan to bring his parents home into action.

The reason for not acting immediately was because Chen Xin understood if he couldn’t guarantee adequate food supply, bringing his parents back was pointless.

Now that he could ensure a food supply, he had the necessary preconditions to officially consider bringing his parents back.

To bring his parents back, Chen Xin needed to cross four provinces, a journey of over six hundred kilometers.

Previously, it was possible to drive on the highways non-stop and complete the journey in half a day. But now, with everything frozen and snow piling almost one meter deep, crossing such a long distance would not be easy.

Therefore, Chen Xin first needed a vehicle that could cope with the current environment, could complete the journey and make a return trip.

Such vehicles exist and are usually called all-terrain vehicles. They can negotiate various complex terrains and conditions including jungle, mountains, desert, snow, beaches, swamps, and water networks.

Among the many types of all-terrain vehicles, Li Yulong’s modified luxury RV also belongs to this category, but compared to his luxury RV, Chen Xin preferred the "Robust" and "Gallop" models used by the Flame Country’s military.

The "Robust" and "Gallop" are articulated two-compartment tracked all-terrain vehicles, with a cabin in the front and a trailer in the rear.

The Robust is a heavy-duty vehicle, weighing thirty tons and capable of carrying thirty tons, making it a powerful and reliable transport vehicle.

The Gallop is slightly smaller, weighing about ten tons, with a top speed of sixty kilometers per hour and a range of five hundred kilometers. It can easily cross 1.5-meter wide ditches and climb over 0.6-meter vertical obstacles and ascend slopes over 30°.

Compared to Chen Xin’s SUV, the difference is as notable as between an elephant and a horse.

Chen Xin envisaged getting a Gallop and transforming the rear compartment into an RV-style living cabin to meet the needs of this journey.

Of course, having a Robust would be even better, as the larger, heavier body of the Robust means a larger compartment, capable of carrying more supplies and providing more living space.

It’s a pity that before the disaster, Chen Xin hadn’t thought of this, otherwise getting a Gallop wouldn’t have been out of the question.

However, now he could still upgrade his SUV to create an all-terrain vehicle.

Honestly, with the snow deepening, Chen Xin’s SUV was increasingly inadequate for travel needs.

Without Li Yulong’s RV’s one-meter high tires, driving on nearly meter-thick snow meant any careless move could get the wheels stuck in the snow.

After consideration, Chen Xin’s first thought was to replace his car’s tires with tracks.

In terms of speed, tires absolutely prevail, but in terms of traversal ability, the gap between tires and tracks is as significant as their speed difference.

Switching to tracks meant the engine needed alteration, since tracks have greater load demands than tires, requiring the engine to have stronger output power.

In this situation, a high-powered diesel engine is indeed more suitable than a gasoline one.

However, regarding the power system for the vehicle, Chen Xin preferred electric drive instead of fuel power.

Firstly, electric drives are cleaner and more efficient, and electric cars aren’t new. Besides battery and charging issues restricting their usage, this new form of vehicle has been accepted and recognized as the future automotive trend.

For Chen Xin, the constraints on electric vehicles don’t exist. With the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery for power and high-performance energy storage batteries as the battery pack, an electric vehicle could run for decades nonstop.

For others, this might be a distant laboratory concept, but for Chen Xin, it’s just a matter of upgrading with survival points.

He seriously planned to do this, intending to upgrade his car after completing overall upgrades to the shelter.

While considering the power system and mode of travel, Chen Xin also contemplated the design of the car’s living cabin. Should it be like the articulated cabin of the Robust and Gallop, or a unified design like Li Yulong’s car?

Both have their advantages, but they also come with their respective issues. Chen Xin stroked his beard, deep in thought.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 162 - 160 Live Fish (Additional Update 3/16)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Is the oxygen pump connected, Xiao Hai?" Inside the garage, Li Wensheng was checking his car, while his son Xiao Hai, who had just loaded the cargo into the trunk, was asked again by his somewhat uneasy father.

Xiao Hai nodded, "Yes, it’s connected, and I put the oxygen head into the water like you said, Dad."

Hearing his son say this, Li Wensheng nodded as well and said, "Alright, then you go back. Take good care of your mom and keep an eye on your sister. I’m going out to make a delivery."

After speaking, Li Wensheng was about to get in the car and prepare to drive out.

However, Xiao Hai didn’t obediently return to their family’s shelter, but instead said to Li Wensheng, "Dad, I want to go with you!"

"Go with me? No way, it’s too dangerous outside!" Li Wensheng’s brows immediately furrowed, and he refused without hesitation.

"Dad! Please let me go with you! I want to help you!" Xiao Hai pleaded with Li Wensheng, feeling that he had grown up and was eager to share some of the burden with his father.

But Li Wensheng still refused his son’s plea, got directly into the car, closed the car door, rolled down the window, and said to his son who was still standing there, "Go back, listen well and help me by taking good care of your mom and watching your sister, that’s the biggest help."

After saying this, Li Wensheng started the car and drove out of the garage.

"Close the garage door and go back quickly!" Li Wensheng stuck his head out of the car window and shouted to his son, who was still standing there, before driving away.

Xiao Hai looked disappointedly at his father driving away into the darkness before reluctantly closing the garage door and returning to their shelter.

Li Wensheng drove the car carefully. The road outside was no longer visible, so he relied on buildings that were not completely buried on both sides to determine the tracks of the road.

Driving in such conditions requires extra caution, as any oversight could lead to various accidents.

Tire skidding or wheels getting stuck in the snow are minor issues that Li Wensheng believed he could handle, but if he encountered an icy road and the whole car skidded, that would be very dangerous.

If he hit something, it could result in vehicle damage or it could simply crash.

In today’s world, if the car breaks down, you have to find a way to fix it yourself; you can no longer tow it to a repair shop like before, and there are no replacement parts for damaged components.

Li Wensheng drove to the bridge leading to the suburbs, which was the bridge that Chen Xin had to pass when entering the city before.

At the moment, Li Wensheng was filled with tension, and he reduced the speed to the slowest, driving the car carefully onto the bridge.

Li Wensheng had no choice but to be cautious. Although the bridge was built solidly and had six wide lanes in both directions, the bridge railing was less than a meter high, and now the bridge deck was already covered with nearly a meter of snow. Driving on it was like driving on snow without guardrails, and any carelessness could result in falling off.

Fortunately, before leaving home, Li Wensheng had put anti-skid chains on his tires, and he drove slowly enough, so he managed to cross the bridge safely.

Letting out a sigh of relief, Li Wensheng slightly increased the car’s speed and headed towards Chen Xin’s house.

Successfully reaching the destination, Li Wensheng took out a shortwave radio to contact Chen Xin, informing him that he had brought the fish.

In the pitch-black snowfield, visibility was extremely poor. Even if he turned on the high beams, it was hard to see anything a hundred meters away. Everything was pitch black, like some kind of monster or as if lurking danger was waiting to pounce or swallow him at any moment.

Although it was hard to admit, Li Wensheng was indeed afraid of the dark, which was related to his childhood experiences.

But he couldn’t tell anyone because he couldn’t let his wife and kids know he was afraid of the dark.

And as a man, he couldn’t expect his wife and kids to go out in such situations, could he?

Li Wensheng believed he couldn’t bring himself to do that.

Luckily, he didn’t have to wait long. Chen Xin quickly opened the gate of the shelter, adding a glimmer of light to the darkness.

"Writer, I’ve brought the ten fish you wanted. They’re all alive. I was worried they would die on the way, so I kept the oxygen pump running, and the car heater was on to ensure none froze to death." Li Wensheng said to Chen Xin somewhat eagerly and boastfully about the fish he had delivered.

This seemed like seeking credit, but Li Wensheng himself couldn’t tell if it was boasting or using the conversation with Chen Xin to dispel the fear of darkness he had just felt.

Chen Xin apparently saw nothing suspicious and asked Li Wensheng to open the trunk to check the fish he had brought. Confirming they were alive, he nodded with satisfaction and let him drive into the shelter.

Once the car was inside the shelter, Chen Xin closed the gate, keeping the cold and dark outside.

"Thank you, farmer. If convenient, please help me carry the fish down." Chen Xin politely addressed Li Wensheng while pulling over a pre-prepared trolley and a water-filled plastic box placed on it.

Li Wensheng, naturally, didn’t refuse to help. The two of them quickly transferred the fish from Li Wensheng’s trunk into Chen Xin’s plastic box, and sure enough, the fish were alive, as Li Wensheng had said. One even made a splash, getting both men wet.

After transferring all ten fish to the plastic box, Chen Xin covered the lid to prevent water from splashing everywhere.

Li Wensheng helped Chen Xin move the trolley and box into Chen Xin’s shelter. Although he had visited Chen Xin’s home several times, entering Chen Xin’s shelter was a first for Li Wensheng.

Looking around Chen Xin’s shelter, Li Wensheng couldn’t help but express envious admiration: "It’s impressive, writer, you’ve built a fantastic shelter."

Hearing this, Chen Xin raised an eyebrow and smiled, "Everyone uses the same basic design; I just added a few more containers, that’s all."

Saying this, Chen Xin opened the door to his growing greenhouse and pushed the trolley inside.

The aquaponics equipment obtained from Li Wensheng was already in place. While turning on the equipment, Chen Xin transferred the fish from the plastic box to the hydroponic tray’s water tank.

Fortunately, the water conditions had already been adjusted, so when the fish entered the water, they experienced no obstacles or discomfort.

At this moment, Li Wensheng curiously entered Chen Xin’s greenhouse, looking around and feeling full of envy, especially at the unharvested roots of drought-resistant rice in Chen Xin’s fields. Li Wensheng remarked with envy, "Writer, your rice looks great! Seems like a bumper crop!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 163 - 161: Seed Packet
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"It’s not really a bumper harvest; it’s just enough to feed one person," Chen Xin merely shook his head modestly in response to Li Wensheng’s expressed envy.

The rice in the fields, though yielding well, wasn’t truly abundant. Especially since he was growing dry rice, its yield was only about 60-70% of that of wet rice.

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s explanation, Li Wensheng nodded in understanding, agreeing with Chen Xin’s perspective. However, he also proposed an idea, "Can you convert your area here into a paddy field? Wet rice might yield better than dry rice, right?"

"A paddy field? Underground?" Chen Xin furrowed his brows, finding the idea somewhat impractical.

Yet Li Wensheng explained, "It’s not possible to create traditional irrigated paddy fields underground, but what if you used hydroponics? Growing rice on hydroponic trays should be feasible."

Li Wensheng’s suggestion greatly enlightened Chen Xin, who quickly asked, "Rice is an aquatic crop, isn’t it planted directly in water? What’s this hydroponic method you mentioned?"

"Although rice is grown directly in water, it still has requirements. Too much water will cause waterlogging," Li Wensheng, who had farmed before, naturally understood this: "Also, only the roots and stalks of rice are immersed in water; if the leaves are also submerged, they’ll surely get diseased, so the water amount can’t be excessive. The hydroponic method uses a planting tray to keep the rice fixed on the water’s surface, with the roots submerged but the other parts above water."

With Li Wensheng’s explanation, Chen Xin immediately saw the light and nodded, saying, "I understand now, but this method sounds simple; in practice, it should be quite challenging, right?"

"It’s not easy. I’ve seen people try it before and it’s not easy, but that was on a lake; you have a greenhouse, so it’s different," Li Wensheng acknowledged the technical difficulties in growing rice this way, but he didn’t say it was impossible.

Chen Xin nodded in understanding and said nothing further, and sensing the moment, Li Wensheng took his leave.

After seeing Li Wensheng off, Chen Xin returned to the refuge, still pondering about Li Wensheng’s suggestion of growing rice using the hydroponic method.

If rice could be grown hydroponically, Chen Xin could certainly calculate the potential increase in yield, a tempting prospect indeed.

The average yield of dry rice per acre is about 800-900 pounds per season, whereas the yield of wet rice is much higher. Not counting the super rice varieties in Flame Country, which yields a ton per acre, even ordinary wet rice yields about 1,000 to 1,200 pounds per acre, with high-yield varieties reaching up to 1,500 pounds.

In most parts of Flame Country, farmers can expect two or three harvests of wet rice per year, and with a little diligence and scientific techniques, producing one or two tons of grain per acre annually is achievable.

In comparison, to get over a ton of dry rice, Chen Xin would need to grow three crops per year in his greenhouse.

Despite high-yield rice’s reliance on hybrid seeds and fertilizers, the prospect of growing more food is a compelling temptation for Chen Xin.

Perhaps during the next upgrade of his greenhouse, he could modify the current planting model?

Chen Xin seriously considered the feasibility of this idea. With a system at his disposal, he wasn’t worried about realizing it, even if he were to transform the current land into a paddy field.

————————————————————

Along with the ten fish sent by Li Wensheng, Li Yulong soon delivered the promised seeds to Chen Xin.

Chili, eggplant, spinach, cilantro, tomato, white radish, lettuce, crown daisy, long beans, flat beans, red amaranth, water spinach...

Chen Xin counted the packets of seeds sent by Li Yulong, a genuinely happy smile spreading across his face.

The seed packets Chen Xin originally purchased included these common crops. According to his initial plan, he would plant half his greenhouse with grain, a quarter with vegetables, and a quarter with fruits.

This way, even if cut off from external food sources, he wouldn’t worry about sustenance in his underground refuge.

But no one could predict the hiccup with the seed delivery; he hadn’t received the seeds he purchased.

Chen Xin thought he’d have to sustain himself on cabbage, lettuce, and carrots in the apocalyptic aftermath.

However, Chen Xin no longer faced this dilemma. With the seeds sent by Li Yulong, he could now grow a variety of foods, significantly enriching his diet, ensuring that his culinary skills wouldn’t go to waste.

Moreover, something that brought Chen Xin particular joy and excitement were the fruit seeds—there were about a dozen varieties.

Mango, watermelon, strawberry, jujube, cantaloupe, kiwi, orange, lychee... despite some being difficult to cultivate, Chen Xin could grow strawberries, watermelons, and grapes in his greenhouse.

It’s been nearly half a year since he last ate fresh fruit.

Getting the seeds made Chen Xin eager to plant something immediately and satisfy his long-craved appetite.

Fortunately, Chen Xin restrained this impulse.

His greenhouse hydroponic trays were designated for vegetables, and until these vegetables were harvested, it was impossible to replace them with fruit.

Even though the fields were now empty, Chen Xin, after some deliberation, decided to continue planting rice there instead of converting them to grow fruit.

Of course, after harvesting the current batch of carrots, Chen Xin planned to reduce their planting area to save some space for strawberries or similar crops to improve his meals.

Additionally, Chen Xin also needed to consider what Li Yulong aimed to receive in return for offering such benefits.

Considering Li Yulong’s multiple visits and his generous gifts—excluding the not-so-valuable fruit and vegetable machine—like two hams and a large batch of seeds, all hard commodities in the post-apocalyptic world.

Chen Xin didn’t believe his relationship with Li Yulong was good enough for him to give so much without expecting something in return. In the pre-apocalyptic world, altruistic actions without a return were rare; post-apocalypse, even more so.

Chen Xin had only agreed to collaborate, and the collaboration hadn’t started, yet Li Yulong made such significant initial investments; his expectations must be equally substantial.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 164 - 162: Sealed Shelter
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Pushing open the originally sealed heavy protection door, the small shelter that had been closed was now open again.

Standing at the entrance, Chen Xin could still see some scattered debris on the ground, clearly leftovers from the previous evacuation.

"Looks like it needs a good cleaning." Even though they hadn’t entered yet, the first impression this small shelter gave Chen Xin and Li Yulong was that it was a bit dirty.

"After evacuating the residents originally living here due to oxygen supply issues, this shelter was temporarily sealed." The staff sent by the city government heard Chen Xin’s assessment and didn’t think there was anything wrong, just nodding while introducing the situation here: "This shelter was originally an underground parking lot, with two floors each 2000 square meters. Only the second underground floor met the civil defense standards, so only this floor was converted into a shelter, designed to accommodate a thousand people."

"A thousand people? Sounds pretty big!" As he listened to the city government staff’s introduction, Li Yulong walked inside while asking Chen Xin: "Xiao Chen, do you think we’ll need such a large space?"

To this question, Chen Xin just shook his head and replied, "Not enough. Two thousand square meters is just over three acres. If you’re running a plantation and growing vegetables and fruits, it’s fine; but growing rice isn’t enough to feed the workers."

"The consumption can’t be that high, right?" Hearing what Chen Xin said, Li Yulong frowned, thinking that two thousand square meters sounded quite large and not understanding why Chen Xin said it wasn’t enough.

"My shelter’s planting greenhouse is 200 square meters. Calculating an annual yield of 1200 jin per mu, growing rice you can harvest 1200 jin three times a year. It’s more than enough for three people, but not enough for four. Do you still think this space is big now?"

"Huh? Really? So, the food grown from a place of 2000 square meters isn’t enough for 50 people? Doesn’t our country have super rice? What good is this yield?" Li Yulong seemed like a bucket of cold water had been poured over him, instantly feeling dejected.

However, Chen Xin just laughed and said: "Super rice can indeed yield 2000 jin per mu, but it heavily relies on seeds and fertilizers. Plant it for a year, and you’d need to replace the seeds, otherwise, it will degrade significantly. Even if you could find someone to cultivate seeds for you, the operating costs would be very high, so if you’re running a greenhouse just to grow rice, it’s a waste of time and effort."

Although Chen Xin said these words with a smile, everyone around who followed them down could hear the sarcasm in his words.

Because this matter involves collaboration with the SWAT team, a team of SWAT officers will be stationed here, maintaining order when they’re not on patrol duty.

Chen Xin, a bit biased, persuaded Uncle Ding to send Qin Lan’s team; thus, Qin Lan also came along with them today to inspect the situation here, preparing for the SWAT team’s later stationing.

Hearing what Chen Xin said, Qin Lan furrowed her brow and asked, "But we can’t just not plant food! If we stop because it’s unprofitable, wouldn’t everyone starve to death?"

"That’s true, but Xiao Long is running a plantation, so economic value should be prioritized." Facing Qin Lan’s inquiry, Chen Xin gave her a reassuring look before continuing to explain: "Of course, all of this is under the premise of growing a single crop. In the current environment and with existing planting conditions, what’s needed is vertical farming, maximizing space use to expand planting areas indirectly.

With this approach, the usable planting area and the variety of crops that can be grown significantly increase. If an aquaponics system is used, it can also provide aquatic products, and the yield is more assured."

"Vertical farming, aquaponics? How would that work?" Hearing Chen Xin’s words, Li Yulong immediately became enthusiastic again, as if he had not been disheartened moments ago.

"It’s quite simple. Dig deep in the ground to make fish ponds, lay seeding boards on the surface to grow rice, then stack several layers of hydroponic cultivation layers for vegetables and fruits." Chen Xin briefly explained, surveying the empty shelter, asking Li Yulong: "Xiao Long, have you decided how to plan this place? It’s impossible to turn all 2000 square meters into farmland, right?"

"It’s not impossible to make all of it farmland, right?" Li Yulong joked, but quickly grew serious, saying: "I plan to allocate two-thirds of the area for cultivation, and transform the remaining third into a residential area."

"The place will be a bit cramped!" Chen Xin, hearing his arrangement, understood his intentions and said this with a smile, then said nothing more, following the city government staff to check the shelter’s condition.

Although this is just a small shelter, it was built according to standards back then, with all facilities well-equipped.

Following the city government staff for an inspection, Chen Xin found that the water and electricity facilities were still functioning well, which made him very satisfied.

After introducing the interior conditions of the shelter, this staff member spoke about other supporting facilities: "After the disaster, the upper floor of this shelter was renovated but only half of it was used, as a coal stockpile. Since the interior of the shelter uses boiler heating and the boiler is installed on the upper floor, the coal is also stored on the upper floor."

"Can this boiler be dismantled? We can install a geothermal heat pump; there’s no need to keep the boiler." Hearing Chen Xin and the city government staff talk about heating equipment, Li Yulong immediately didn’t want that old coal-fired boiler.

However, Chen Xin shook his head and said, "Keep it. In case something happens and the geothermal heat pump malfunctions, there’s a secondary contingency plan. Besides, the equipment won’t run forever; it needs shutdowns for maintenance."

"Then I’ll follow your advice, Xiao Chen, but having half the upper floor as a stockpile seems wasteful, right?" Li Yulong nodded in agreement with Chen Xin’s opinion and then raised his own doubts: "Xiao Chen, do you have any plans for that?"

Faced with Li Yulong’s question, Chen Xin just smiled and patted his shoulder: "You already have ideas, why ask me? You’re the boss, do as you think."

After saying this, Chen Xin walked over to Qin Lan, the two of them huddling together closely, seemingly discussing something private.

Li Yulong watched as Chen Xin and Qin Lan walked to the side to discuss their matters and shrugged nonchalantly, continuing to talk with the city staff: "If that’s the case, then we won’t dismantle the boiler. Can the city government still supply coal?"

"That won’t be a problem, though the city government won’t supply it for free." Although the city staff was a bit unclear about who actually called the shots between Chen Xin and Li Yulong, he wouldn’t neglect his own duties. Hearing Li Yulong mention coal supply, he immediately began discussing prices.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 165 - 163: News about the Car
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

While Li Yulong was bargaining with the city government staff, Chen Xin and Qin Lan used the original staircase of the shelter to go up to the underground floor.

"Axin, will you move here in the future?" Qin Lan and Chen Xin came to the boiler room. Seeing Chen Xin inspecting the boiler, Qin Lan asked this question for some unknown reason.

Chen Xin was examining the now frozen boiler and was taken aback by Qin Lan’s question. He looked back at her, surprised, but quickly replied, "No, this place will be used as a trading market and plantation in the future. Those who live here will be workers. I’ll come by occasionally, but I won’t move here."

"Oh, I was thinking if you moved here, I would too." Qin Lan lowered her head, biting her lip, feeling a bit disappointed.

"If you want to move here, you might as well move to my place. It’s much better in all aspects." Qin Lan’s meaning was clear, and Chen Xin responded smoothly.

However, Qin Lan didn’t respond, continuing to lower her head in thought.

Seeing Qin Lan’s attitude, Chen Xin didn’t say anything further and continued inspecting the boiler and pipes before him.

This is an important part of the shelter’s heating system. Even if Li Yulong plans to install a ground-source heat pump here, these pipes will still be useful.

However, when Chen Xin knocked on the obviously frozen pipes, the dull sound inside troubled him. There was still water frozen inside these pipes. To make it operational again, the ice would have to be melted bit by bit.

Starting the boiler directly or connecting it to a ground-source heat pump with the ice still in there could very well burst the pipes due to blockage.

But Chen Xin was not a professional engineer, so he just checked the boiler’s condition without inspecting the pipeline thoroughly.

This boiler is still usable, but clearly the entire set of pipes needs cleaning. When they evacuated, the person responsible for the boiler must have been lazy and didn’t drain the water from the pipes. Thus, under low temperatures, the pipes were surely blocked with ice.

"What’s so interesting about this boiler?" Qin Lan, seeing Chen Xin still focused on the boiler, couldn’t help feeling a bit jealous.

"This is the heating system of the shelter. If there’s a malfunction here, it would be a big problem." Chen Xin obviously detected Qin Lan’s jealousy and turned around to smile at her. "The pipes seem to be frozen. To restore heating, we need to solve the ice problem first. But that’s not my responsibility; I’ll let Xiaolong worry about it."

As Chen Xin spoke, he suddenly removed one of his gloves, reached into his clothes, and took out an insulated box. After opening it, he took out a rice sandwich and handed it to Qin Lan. "Try it. I made this in the morning. It’s a rice cake sandwich made with rice I grew myself, fried, and filled with lettuce and ham. The ham is Iberian ham that Xiaolong sent over. Although it shouldn’t be eaten with rice cakes, with limited ingredients now, we have to make do."

After saying that, Chen Xin handed the rice sandwich to Qin Lan.

Qin Lan, looking at the sandwich, hurriedly took off her gloves and face mask, grabbed the still warm rice cake sandwich, and quickly took a bite.

"It smells great! So delicious!" Qin Lan chewed with a smile on her face, looking delighted by the taste.

Seeing Qin Lan enjoying the meal, Chen Xin then extended the rest of the contents of the box to her with a smile.

The method of simply using a rice cake to sandwich ham can’t be considered a delicacy, and it’s more like just throwing ingredients together.

Even though neither of the components is particularly flavorful, Chen Xin had especially added lettuce to counteract greasiness, so it tasted not bad, but still quite distant from truly delicious.

Qin Lan’s enjoyment could simply be explained by the fact that she hadn’t had anything good to eat during this post-apocalyptic time. Even a plain-tasting rice cake sandwich could seem delightful to her.

As Qin Lan ate the sandwich, Chen Xin suddenly asked her, "Alan, do you know where in the city I can find a car?"

"A car? That would be very difficult." Qin Lan, who had just quickly finished one sandwich, responded to Chen Xin after swallowing the food. "Both the city government and our special police unit are using cars from before the disaster, but it’s not possible to spare one for you.

If you’re after parts, that might be negotiated. Materials transported every week by rail from San Zhen in Hubei Province and Jianning in this province include car parts. When they pass through the city, we can get a few. If your car breaks down, you can get parts for repairs."

Avoiding the food from getting cold, Qin Lan spoke while picking up the second sandwich and taking another bite.

"Is that so? That’s a bit troublesome!" Chen Xin couldn’t help frowning.

"What’s wrong? Axin, is your car broken? If you need repairs, I can let the mechanic in our team take a look at it for you." Qin Lan asked with concern, seeing Chen Xin’s troubled look.

Chen Xin shook his head and explained, "My car isn’t broken, but the problem is that regular SUVs can’t drive on these snowy roads anymore. I plan to modify it or switch to something that can travel in the snow."

"That’s true. Even without considering issues like engine oil freezing or diesel solidifying, just the tires slipping is troublesome enough. Just the day before yesterday, two vehicles from the team nearly crashed on a mission." Qin Lan understood Chen Xin’s difficulty but didn’t have a solution.

Although she couldn’t solve Chen Xin’s problem, Qin Lan offered some information, "I heard the city government is considering this issue and plans to replace the vehicles for themselves and our special police unit.

Perhaps a batch of cars will arrive from Star City next month. By then, you could ask the captain or let Xiaolong connect with city officials. You need vehicles for your plantation, so perhaps you could get one through Xiaolong’s connections."

"Really? But wouldn’t the cars coming from Star City be the same as the ones I’m driving now? Apart from maybe having anti-freeze measures." Chen Xin didn’t immediately grasp, thinking Qin Lan was talking about regular cars.

But Qin Lan laughed, closed the empty insulated box, and returned it to Chen Xin before explaining, "They would definitely replace them with snowmobiles and all-terrain vehicles that can travel on snow. The city contracted the far-off heavy industry company for tracked snowmobiles and all-terrain vehicles, which are much better than the current wheeled ones."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 166 - 164: Equipment for Mo Qingyan
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Regarding the renovation of small shelters, Chen Xin didn’t need to get involved or worry about it. If it weren’t for the fact that Li Yulong had given him so many good things, he wouldn’t have been dragged along for the on-site inspection.

As for the subsequent tasks, Li Yulong’s professionals would handle them, and there was no need for Chen Xin to intervene.

Moreover, compared to these tasks, the ground source heat pump with a fish-vegetable symbiosis system that he promised to give Mo Qingyan was the real thing he needed to focus on.

Chen Xin had already completed the manufacturing once Li Wensheng delivered the fish-vegetable symbiosis system. It was just that Li Yulong’s matters had held him up, preventing him from delivering the system to Mo Qingyan’s house immediately.

However, after dealing with Li Yulong’s issues, Chen Xin quickly notified Mo Qingyan to come and pick up the equipment.

It’s not that Chen Xin was unwilling to deliver it himself, but his SUV really couldn’t handle the entire set of equipment this time, so Mo Qingyan had to bring her pickup truck to haul it away.

"The roads to your place are getting harder and harder to navigate." As Mo Qingyan was welcomed into his shelter, her first act after jumping off the truck was to complain to Chen Xin about the difficult roads.

Chen Xin felt the same way. This was no longer before the disaster when frost and snow would be cleared by sanitation workers and municipal departments to ensure traffic flow.

In a situation where the snow depth was approaching one meter, maintaining road accessibility was extremely challenging, especially when the municipal government had neither the energy nor the manpower to clear the snow, and there was not much need either.

Aside from people like Chen Xin who were living outside of the official shelters and still using the old roads, the city was gradually opening underground passages between the official shelters.

Once these underground passages were opened, transportation between the official shelters could completely shift underground, making surface roads almost unnecessary to manage.

But for survivors like Chen Xin who hadn’t joined the official shelters, impassable roads meant travel was more difficult and resources were harder to obtain.

Mo Qingyan, being in the city, might find it acceptable. If the distance wasn’t too far, walking was feasible, and short drives could still be managed.

After all, she wasn’t far from the city hall, city bureau, and shopping center, which were all official shelters. Even using the underground passages would be okay with no trouble.

But for Chen Xin, it wasn’t as convenient. The distance from the city wasn’t really the issue; the main problem was that to get to him, one had to cross a big bridge. Otherwise, the journey would be three times longer to bypass a river that had now dried up.

In the current weather, both crossing the river and taking detours were risky. Deciding whether crossing was more dangerous or taking detours was really a tough call.

"The weather is getting colder, and roads harder to navigate, which is only natural." Chen Xin wasn’t significantly affected by Mo Qingyan’s complaints: "But since I can be self-sufficient with food and drink, I don’t worry about going out. If there’s anything, people come to me."

"Yeah, you’re living comfortably with food and drink, swapping resources with the SWAT team, and having Ah Lan deliver to your doorstep. Life must be really laid-back!" Hearing Chen Xin say this, Mo Qingyan couldn’t help but put on an "I’m not envious at all" expression.

Seeing Mo Qingyan’s "not envious" look, Chen Xin simply smiled, helped her load the prepped ground source heat pump-fish-vegetable symbiosis equipment onto her pickup, and said, "With this equipment, Long Niang, you can also be self-sufficient. You can exchange seeds and fish fry with the old farmer, or ask Ah Lan if you can get some from the city’s planting area.

I did some calculations, and the heat and electric energy generated by the ground source heat pump are enough to support the plant growth and fish farming in the fish-vegetable symbiosis system, ensuring there’ll be no problem meeting the needs of you and little sister Xin."

"Really? That’s great, thank you so much!" Hearing this from Chen Xin and seeing the equipment loaded onto her pickup, Mo Qingyan showed a contented and expectant look.

Even though she had a stockpile of supplies, after over six months, the total amount of supplies Mo Qingyan had was still gradually decreasing.

Although exchanging alcohol for supplies could help sustain, it wasn’t a long-term solution. The equipment Chen Xin provided was just what she urgently needed.

"You’re welcome, it’s just a piece of equipment. No need for thanks between us, right?" Chen Xin wasn’t too bothered by Mo Qingyan’s gratitude: "As for the installation, you can find Xiao Long. He took on the job of installing the ground source heat pump for me. You just need to contact him."

"Alright, got it." Mo Qingyan naturally knew Li Yulong and nodded to show she remembered.

"Xiao Long partnered with me to develop a planting area in a small shelter preserved by the city near the city bureau. A trading market will open later, and by then, Long Niang, if you have anything you want to exchange, you can go there," Chen Xin also mentioned Li Yulong’s planting area and trading market to Mo Qingyan. It was indeed not far from Mo Qingyan’s house, offering her a convenient trading channel, and Chen Xin added, "I might visit sometimes. If you need to find me, you can go there, and if I’m not around, you can let Xiao Long pass a message."

Knowing that there would be a new planting area and trading market near her home made Mo Qingyan even more excited.

For survivors in private shelters, their survival relies on pre-disaster supplies and post-disaster resource exchanges, or, like Chen Xin, self-maintenance and self-sufficiency.

Although the city hall has an underground planting area and goods shipped in by rail, these supplies are promptly distributed to official shelters and not meant for public sale.

The few resources reaching the market were resold by people in official shelters and ended up on the black market, where they were highly sought after and snatched up as soon as they appeared.

Those without connections and information channels found it hard to purchase them.

So, having Chen Xin help her with self-sufficient equipment was undoubtedly something Mo Qingyan felt grateful for.

However, just as Mo Qingyan was thinking about how to thank Chen Xin, he pulled out a bag and handed it to her: "This is the dry rice I grew myself, just harvested. I’m sharing some with you to taste, or you can keep it as rice seeds."

Receiving the bag, which weighed about four to five pounds, Mo Qingyan looked at Chen Xin and impulsively gave him a quick kiss on the cheek.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 167 - 165: Frozen Dead Body
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Mo Qingyan’s kiss did not bring any different changes to their relationship because both she and he knew it was just a gesture of gratitude.

Chen Xin didn’t take it to heart, and Mo Qingyan didn’t feel she would have any feelings for Chen Xin; they just saw each other as brothers.

So after kissing Chen Xin, Mo Qingyan went home with her equipment, looking for Li Yulong’s people to help install the equipment, while Chen Xin continued doing his own things step by step.

After the drought-resistant rice was harvested, it was necessary to reapply the base fertilizer and plow the land. He had promised to help Dr. Hong Rui plant herbs, which also needed a planting area to be planned. The vegetables that were about to mature needed harvesting as well, so Chen Xin actually had a lot on his plate.

For these tasks, he could only tackle them one at a time due to his limited capacity. After all, he was just one person, only able to do one thing at a time.

Applying the base fertilizer and plowing the land after harvesting the dry rice were relatively easy tasks. The small agricultural machinery Chen Xin had exchanged for was not just a combine harvester. The cultivator could integrate multiple functions such as tilling, weeding, trenching, and sowing.

All Chen Xin had to do was prepare the machine, then apply fertilizer and plow the land.

Since the soil hadn’t hardened over the winter, the soil in Chen Xin’s planting greenhouse was still relatively loose. Even without plowing, there wasn’t much of a problem. The main reason Chen Xin did it was to apply base fertilizer and enhance the soil’s fertility.

After all, the fertility in the soil was greatly depleted after a round of cultivation. Without fertilization, it would undoubtedly affect the growth and yield of the crops for the next season.

The high yield of seeds comes from not only the variety but also from their heavy reliance on fertilizers.

Since Chen Xin couldn’t prevent seed degradation or cultivate new seeds himself, all he could do was ensure the soil’s fertility as much as possible so that the crops he planted could grow better, allowing for a better harvest.

With so much to do, naturally, Chen Xin had to get up early every day, otherwise, there might not be enough time.

So upon being woken by the alarm clock, Chen Xin immediately got dressed, brushed his teeth, washed his face, ate breakfast, and then prepared to start his day’s work.

It must be said that since he had rice, Chen Xin’s breakfast had become much more sumptuous.

Previously, he could only eat shelter food or his emergency reserves. But now, he could cook a pot of congee in the evening, setting it aside for the morning. With white rice porridge, ham slices, and two energy bars, it was enough to make him full and comfortable.

After filling his stomach, Chen Xin didn’t immediately start working in the greenhouse. Instead, he planned to clear the snow accumulating outside the shelter door.

Although, like he said, he could go a long time without going out, too much snow at the entrance would still be a bit bothersome.

After changing into a two-piece short Protective Suit, putting on a respirator, Chen Xin opened the shelter door.

Because Chen Xin had already cleared the snow once when Mo Qingyan came before, there wasn’t much snow blocking the door, preventing the door from opening.

Chen Xin had considered changing the outward-opening door to an inward-opening one, which would be much more convenient. However, after careful consideration, Chen Xin decided not to modify the door’s structural design for now.

This wasn’t because of anything else but the better protective performance of an outward-opening door compared to an inward-opening one. Although using this protective feature might be irrelevant by now.

Today, though, the door’s opening feel puzzled Chen Xin a bit. The wheel used to unlock the door seemed jammed, causing him to exert some force, even needing a wrench to turn the wheel, and there was some resistance when pushing open the door, making it not open very smoothly.

"Strange, is it frozen outside?" Chen Xin couldn’t help but frown, as it was the first time he encountered such an odd situation.

Even when heavy snow nearly buried the door before, Chen Xin could open the door smoothly. This kind of trouble in opening the door happened for the first time.

This caused Chen Xin to be more alert, and only after grabbing a gun and flashlight did he walk out of the shelter to check what might have gone wrong with his door.

However, when the flashlight illuminated the scene outside the door, Chen Xin was stunned.

A man holding a little girl in his arms, two frozen corpses lay on the door, and the thing obstructing the wheel movement was nothing else but the man’s arm frozen to the wheel.

Because Chen Xin had tried to open the door earlier, the arm, initially frozen, was now broken.

Looking at the two bodies, Chen Xin immediately felt a pang of sadness but could only shake his head in helplessness.

He could almost deduce what had happened without thinking.

A father, protecting his young daughter, for some reason had left the shelter they lived in, traversed the snow, finally found Chen Xin’s shelter, and thought they could be saved. However, the thick protective door not only blocked the cold but also their cries for help outside.

A 20-centimeter-thick protective door surrounded by steel and reinforced concrete, even hitting it with a large iron hammer wouldn’t make much noise, let alone transmit that sound tens of meters underground to wake Chen Xin from sleep.

Unable to open the shelter door, this father and daughter pair could only despairingly freeze into an ice sculpture in the cold night of wind and snow.

Staring at these two bodies, Chen Xin felt his heart freezing over as well. His warm breakfast seemed to have turned into ice, making his intestines and stomach feel a painful freeze.

This made him regret not having installed a doorbell at the entrance. If there had been a doorbell last night, hearing someone outside, he would have at least checked to see what was going on, allowing the father and daughter a chance to seek refuge.

Even though it wouldn’t have let them into the shelter’s interior, letting them sleep in the decontamination room just a door away wouldn’t have been a problem, and they wouldn’t have frozen to death at his doorstep.

But unfortunately, the people were already dead, and even if Chen Xin wanted to change something, it was already too late.

Sighing, Chen Xin didn’t disturb the two bodies but went back into the shelter and contacted Qin Lan to inform her of the two bodies at his doorstep needing handling.

After making the report, Chen Xin took a pot of hot water outside the door, melting the broken hand frozen on the wheel with hot water, placing it beside the man.

Seeing the father still tightly holding his daughter in his arms, shielding her from the wind and snow as much as he could, Chen Xin was genuinely saddened.

Life was so fragile, and yet death came so easily. Just a single cold night took two lives from this world, one of whom was so young and hadn’t even experienced the beauty of this world.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 168 - 166: Qin Lan Executes the Mission
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Upon receiving the message from Chen Xin, Qin Lan informed the relevant departments of the situation.

Chen Xin didn’t have to wait long before a vehicle brought people to handle the situation right to his doorstep.

Qin Lan did not come along; it was a SWAT officer who had previously been to Chen Xin’s place, accompanied by two staff members.

Seeing the father and daughter frozen into sculptures in the snow, everyone couldn’t help but sigh.

"It’s so pitiful, why would they come out in such cold weather!" Looking at the deceased little girl, the SWAT officer, possibly because of his youth, couldn’t help but express his lament.

"Without professional protective equipment and sufficient warm clothing, in weather like this, people can freeze to death within a few hours." The staff member tasked with collecting the bodies was somewhat accustomed to it but still felt regret upon seeing the frozen young girl.

Listening to them, Chen Xin couldn’t help but ask, "Are many people freezing to death? Aren’t there official shelters?"

"The official shelters only accommodate eighty percent of the population!" The staff member smiled wryly and sighed before explaining, "Although the vast majority managed to enter shelters promptly before the disaster, a small portion did not. They used various means to survive the first wave of disasters from the meteor impact, but they are short on clothes and food.

When the weather wasn’t so cold, they could barely make do, but as it gets colder, they find it increasingly difficult. The city government has been working hard on rescue missions, but many still live outside. You often find frozen bodies in the city, not even knowing when they froze to death."

While speaking, the two staff members tried to separate the father and daughter’s corpses to fit them into body bags. However, the frozen corpses were not easy to separate, especially with the father holding the daughter tightly. To separate them, they had to break the father’s arms.

Faced with this situation, the expressions on the two staff members’ faces grew more complex. Unable to succeed, they sighed and had to place both bodies into one body bag.

Looking at the bodies in the bag, both Chen Xin and the young SWAT officer appeared very upset.

"Brother Chen, if there’s nothing else, I’ll take my leave." Seeing the bodies collected, the young SWAT officer didn’t want to stay longer and bid farewell to Chen Xin, preparing to leave.

Seeing the young SWAT officer about to leave, Chen Xin didn’t try to keep him, only asking, "Does Lan have a mission today?"

"Yes, Sister Lan’s team went to the county shelter. I heard there’s trouble over there." The young SWAT officer nodded, informing Chen Xin of Qin Lan’s whereabouts before turning around and leaving with the two staff members.

Watching them go, Chen Xin turned back to look at the bloodstains in front of his shelter’s door that had stayed from a previously frozen hand, he couldn’t help but sigh again.

But the deceased are gone; Chen Xin didn’t continue to grieve. He returned inside the shelter and closed the door. He had already been delayed a lot today and had many things to do.

————————————————

On the other side, Qin Lan and her teammates were sorting out their equipment, preparing to carry out their mission.

"Last night, the county government of Baling County received a distress call from Baling County Shelter No. 2, where a group of criminals intends to seize control of the entire shelter for their criminal objectives." The team was still led by Captain Li, who gravely reported the mission: "After receiving the distress call, the county government of Baling took this matter very seriously and immediately contacted the county’s SWAT and armed police. They rushed to the scene to deal with the situation.

However, the criminals have seized the shelter and blocked the gates. The county’s SWAT and armed police lack heavy weapons and cannot forcibly enter the shelter, so they sought assistance from the city government.

The city’s leadership also took this matter seriously, and besides our team and Old Zhang’s team, the city’s armed police dispatched an armored vehicle and a squadron of soldiers as reinforcements.

Upon arrival, a negotiation expert would attempt to negotiate. If the criminals could be persuaded to surrender peacefully, that would be the best outcome. But if they resist stubbornly, the armed police will use the armored vehicle to forcibly breach the shelter’s gate, and we’ll assault inside with them.

The armed police will focus on incapacitating the resisting criminals, while we’ll focus on rescuing any potential hostages. Additionally, if the criminals agree to negotiate, we’ll be responsible for protecting the negotiation expert as they proceed inside."

After introducing the mission, Captain Li looked at his team seated inside the armored vehicle and sternly asked, "Did everyone understand?"

"Understood!" everyone shouted in unison, then fell silent, focusing on adjusting their state and tightening their grip on their weapons.

As a sniper, Qin Lan repeatedly loaded and unloaded bullets from her beloved sniper rifle, one by one, into the magazine and back out again.

This was her ritual whenever she undertook a significant mission, merely to calm her mind and maintain focus, allowing her to execute the mission with the utmost calm and concentration.

A sniper doesn’t get a second shot; that’s what Qin Lan’s instructor told her during her initial sniper training.

The instructor emphasized that on the battlefield, snipers must remain absolutely calm and have absolute confidence in each shot because there’s no opportunity for a second follow-up shot—the sniper must hit with the first shot.

Thus, before firing, one must calm down and concentrate all attention.

Qin Lan’s repetitive bullet handling was her way of making a small gesture to focus her attention.

Her teammates were already familiar with her habits and did not disturb her. Once they arrived at the mission site and others disembarked to prepare for combat, Qin Lan finally loaded the last bullet into the magazine, inserted the magazine into the gun, stood up, and took position in the vehicle’s roof hatch, setting up her rifle.

As it was a forced assault mission, while sniper cover was needed, no additional shooting position was necessary; the shooting hatch on the roof was the ideal spot.

Qin Lan didn’t like prone shooting. On one hand, lying down always gave her a pressing feeling around her chest, which was uncomfortable. Another reason was that with the current weather, lying prone on the roof would quickly result in her and the gun freezing.

Relatively speaking, standing inside the vehicle and merely extending the gun and upper body allowed her to maintain the best shooting condition.

Outside, the SWAT and armed police were already in position, with multiple police car headlights illuminating the gate of Baling County Shelter No. 2. On the police armored vehicle brought by the armed police, a 12.7mm rotary cannon was already aimed at the shelter’s gate, capable of shredding it with its terrifying rate of 2,000 rounds per minute and the penetrative power of its large-caliber bullets.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 169 - 167: Ploughing the Land
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The five armored vehicles of the SWAT and paramilitary police stopped at the gate of Shelter No. 2 in Baling County, poised for an assault.

Even though only one of the paramilitary police armored vehicles was equipped with heavy weapons on its roof, and the other four were just light troop carriers, just one rotary heavy machine gun was enough to breach the shelter’s gate.

The machine gun did not fire, even though the gunner had already placed his finger on the trigger, and the crosshair in the sight was aimed right at the shelter’s gate before them.

"Is there still no response?" asked Team Leader Li, looking at the shelter’s gate illuminated by the headlights of the five armored vehicles, turning to another SWAT team leader beside him.
