Team Leader Li, this SWAT officer, the officer from the paramilitary unit in charge of the squad, along with the Baling County SWAT and paramilitary commanders, formed a temporary command team on site. Decisions on which strategies to adopt to resolve the incident, and whether to use force, were all determined by this temporary command group of five.

"The phone’s been connected, and we’ve tried calling over the radio, but there’s been no answer from inside." The other SWAT team leader, surnamed Zhang, known as Old Zhang, was rather impatient, "If you ask me, we should just launch a forced assault. We don’t need the heavy machine gun—planting explosives on the four hinges of the gate could take down this protective door."

The gate of Shelter No. 2 in Baling County was merely an ordinary civil defense door, and to an explosives expert like Old Zhang, there were many methods to open it.

Actually, Team Leader Li supported Old Zhang’s idea of launching an assault. It’s so cold letting everyone stay in a state of readiness like this. If any of his men caught some frostbite, it wouldn’t be worth peeling the skin off those criminals.

Things aren’t like before. Back then, a standoff just meant a test of patience, but now, it’s also about whether the team can withstand the severe cold of minus several tens of degrees.

It makes no sense that the criminals could comfortably waste time inside the shelter while the police were left to freeze outside in the icy wind and snow.

The paramilitary officer in charge was similarly inclined towards a direct attack. Although the soldiers could still hold on, such a standoff would unlikely break anytime soon, and he didn’t want his soldiers to suffer the cold any longer.

"Issue the final ultimatum, wait ten more minutes, and if they don’t open the door, we’ll force our way in!" Finally, the officer made a decision.

Upon receiving the order, someone immediately proceeded to execute it, while Team Leader Li, Old Zhang, and the other two commanders also instructed their troops to prepare for the assault.

The ten minutes passed in a flash, and seeing the door still tightly shut with no sign of opening, the paramilitary officer in charge issued the command to storm.

However, just as they were about to dismantle the gate of Shelter No. 2 in Baling County by explosives, the wheel on the door moved slightly.

The once tightly closed gate of Shelter No. 2 in Baling County finally opened.

————————————————

Pushing the small cultivator and watching the blades chop the soil and the remaining rice roots together, Chen Xin hummed a cheerful tune.

Having completed the harvest of this month’s vegetables, he also began tilling the fields, preparing to plant the second crop of rice for the year.

Although intending to convert the dry rice to water rice, the current conditions were not yet suitable, so this second crop would still be dry rice.

Currently, the rice seeds had already begun to sprout, and he needed to finish tilling the soil before the seedlings grew out.

Chen Xin wore a protective mask because the freshly fertilized greenhouse smelled really strong, and without the mask, the odor would be intolerable.

These fertilizers came from his water treatment reaction tank, containing the fermented products of processed toilet wastewater, which are traditional organic fertilizers.

In the past, these fertilizers might have been insufficient, but with the harvest of the first crop and the biomass produced by the oxygen machine, the composting materials were temporarily adequate.

Chen Xin crushed these rice stalks and fed them into the upgraded reaction tank. Although a few days of fermentation wouldn’t turn these stalks into fertilizer, the previously stored portion and the increased fermentation efficiency brought about by the system upgrade provided Chen Xin with sufficient fertilizer.

The fertilizer was sprinkled in the field and mixed into the soil during tilling. This method, called base fertilization, could influence the entire season’s crop growth.

Before the advent of chemical fertilizers, base fertilization and plowing were critical parts of traditional agricultural production activities. Even with the modern advancements in agriculture with machinery and fertilizers, they remained indispensable agricultural practices.

Pushing the cultivator, the small field was soon fully tilled.

Looking at the tilled field, Chen Xin smiled, his heart filled with hope for a good harvest this season.

"Grow up quickly!" After tidying up the cultivator, Chen Xin looked at the seedlings being nurtured and earnestly wished for them to grow faster.

However, no matter how fervently Chen Xin wished for the seedlings’ growth, plants have to adhere to the natural objective laws, and they cannot grow instantaneously.

So, even as Chen Xin stared longingly at the seedlings, wishing for rapid growth, they still appeared as tender sprouts emerging from the soil, with no noticeable change.

Nevertheless, Chen Xin happily gazed at the seedlings, almost imagining them already bearing the weight of a bountiful harvest.

Despite placing profound hopes on the seedlings, Chen Xin eventually turned to other tasks after watching them for a while, given that aside from them, there were other important matters that warranted his attention.

The herbs promised to Doctor Hong Rui had already been sown. Since he hadn’t planted such things before, Chen Xin felt unsure and only planted a third of the seeds. He followed Doctor Hong Rui’s instructions on planting and sowed them on a specially reserved planting board.

The current situation seemed favorable; most of the seeds had germinated. However, whether they would grow and eventually yield a harvest, Chen Xin was still uncertain.

Given that encountering issues during plant growth is utterly normal and despite the fact that his greenhouse has not seen any crop diseases in the past half year, possibly due to the system upgrade, Chen Xin believed he couldn’t be careless.

After thoroughly inspecting all the crops and ensuring there were no issues, he finally left the greenhouse.

As per his routine, Qin Lan would deliver this month’s supplies today, and Chen Xin needed to organize the harvested vegetables and prepare for the exchange.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 170 - 168: Accident
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As usual, the special police team’s vehicle arrived punctually at the entrance of Chen Xin’s refuge, bringing the supplies for exchange while also preparing to transport the vegetables Chen Xin had produced this month.

What’s strange to Chen Xin was that it was not Qin Lan accompanying the car today, but the young officer who had come before along with another officer he didn’t recognize.

"Why are you guys here today, isn’t Lan coming?" Chen Xin asked the two officers retrieving goods, furrowing his brow.

Qin Lan had previously agreed with him to come and stay for two days every month when fetching the goods. She wasn’t someone who would break her word; unless something had delayed her, Qin Lan would never miss the appointment.

Sure enough, asked about why Qin Lan hadn’t come, the two officers exchanged glances, with expressions of hesitation and unease appearing on their faces.

This made Chen Xin increasingly worried, he asked the two nervously, "What’s happened to Lan? Did something happen?"

"This... Chen, don’t be nervous, Lan didn’t come because she got injured and is currently in the hospital!" The young officer, who had come before, hurriedly tried to soothe Chen Xin’s emotions, knowing that his relationship with Qin Lan was not ordinary, he quickly explained the situation: "Yesterday, Lan went on a mission, and an accident occurred, she got shot by a stray bullet in the shoulder and is now receiving treatment in the hospital."

Perhaps to assuage Chen Xin’s worries, the young officer added reassuringly: "Chen, don’t worry too much, Lan was only grazed by the bullet, the situation isn’t serious, the doctor is just worried about her wound getting infected, that’s why she’s hospitalized, she really is fine!"

Listening to the young officer’s explanation, Chen Xin’s face completely darkened, he hadn’t imagined Qin Lan would encounter such an incident.

Though the young officer seemed to downplay the severity, saying it was just a graze, anyone could think and deduce that being grazed by a bullet implies there had been a gunfight - where else would the stray bullet come from?

Qin Lan being a sniper, a supposedly most concealed unit on the battlefield, yet even the sniper got injured, how fierce must the battle have been?

Chen Xin couldn’t contain his concern anymore, wanting to rush to the hospital to see Qin Lan right away.

But he had to solve the matter at hand first before he could go to the hospital.

Thus Chen Xin could only suppress his tension and worries, helping the two officers to count this month’s vegetables for trade, not even checking the supplies they delivered, he let them pile them haphazardly in the prep room by the main entrance and prepared to drive downtown to visit Qin Lan.

Seeing Chen Xin so agitated, the two officers hurriedly stopped him.

"Chen, calm down, if you drive to the hospital like this, it’s easy to get into trouble on the road, it’s better to go in our car!" The young officer didn’t stop Chen Xin from going to the hospital, but prevented him from driving himself: "The road conditions are bad now, Lan is fine there, don’t get into trouble yourself, Chen!"

Listening to the young officer, Chen Xin hesitated for a moment but quickly abandoned the thought of driving himself downtown, nodded, secured the refuge’s door, and went to the city with them by car.

In the city hospital, Qin Lan was lying with her arm hanging by the bedside, a look of self-reproach and fear on her face.

Her jacket and clothes were undone, exposing her left arm and shoulder, which were now wrapped in thick bandages hanging from her neck.

Being hit by a stray bullet was something Qin Lan hadn’t anticipated at all, she hadn’t imagined that after the criminals were subdued, she could still be hit by a stray bullet.

However, recalling yesterday’s mission, Qin Lan couldn’t help but feel her heartbeat quicken, feeling fearful of those criminals’ viciousness.

Back then, after the temporary command team delivered the final ultimatum to the criminals occupying the refuge, those criminals chose to open the door, everyone thought the mission was over.

The criminals coming out were subdued, handcuffed, ready to be transported back to the city, but no one expected one of them had hidden explosives and detonated the fuse.

Two officers nearby were killed instantly in the explosion, and a dozen officers and armed soldiers were injured to varying degrees.

After the explosion, another prepared group of criminals stormed out from the refuge, seized guns from the injured special police and armed soldiers, intending to resist till the end, even planning a counterattack.

The sides broke into a fierce firefight, even the rotary cannon that wasn’t initially intended to be used roared in a sweep against the criminals.

Qin Lan, responsible as the sniper for support, naturally joined the battle without hesitation, in fact, she should have been the first to notice the anomaly, as she saw through the sniper scope the criminal detonating the fuse and immediately pulled the trigger intending to kill the criminal.

But as she pulled the trigger, it was already too late, though the criminal was killed, the fuse was already ignited.

Despite the firefight not lasting too long, and at the refuge gate lacking cover, the criminals soon were killed, during the conflict, a stray bullet still hit Qin Lan’s shoulder.

After the battle ended, all wounded were immediately sent to the city hospital for emergency treatment, but three needed urgent care due to severe injuries and could not be rescued successfully, while five severely injured are still undergoing treatment.

Among everyone, it was Qin Lan, hit by the stray bullet, who had the lightest injury.

This made her feel both relieved and fearful, yet couldn’t help but blame herself, if she had shot even one second earlier, perhaps such a tragic outcome wouldn’t have occurred, a total of five dead, five severely injured, and over a dozen slightly wounded, it could be said to be the city’s heaviest loss in the police system since the founding of the nation.

Thinking about comrades she lived alongside now sacrificed and injured because she shot too late, Qin Lan felt unbearable anguish, and the shoulder wound pain equally affected her so much that she hadn’t closed her eyes from last night after surgery to remove the bullet until now.

She just stared blankly at the ceiling lamp, eyes bloodshot, her mind endlessly replaying yesterday’s scenes.

If only she could have shot one second earlier, if only she could have detected the anomaly sooner, then so many wouldn’t have been sacrificed or injured!

This thought lingered in Qin Lan’s mind, making her repeatedly revisit yesterday’s scene, attempting to find evidence to convince herself that she wasn’t late in shooting, also searching for overlooked "evidence" of anomalies.

But that didn’t help at all, rather it exacerbated her self-blame and regret, almost consuming her until Chen Xin burst into the ward, pulling her out from the endless cycle of recollections.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 171 - 169 Crying Qin Lan
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"A-Lan, how are you? Are you okay?" Seeing Qin Lan lying on the bed with a lost look and red eyes, wrapped in thick bandages, Chen Xin hurriedly asked her with concern.

But seeing Qin Lan just lying there with her eyes open, Chen Xin let out a sigh of relief, finally able to relax the worry he had been holding onto.

After all, Qin Lan didn’t seem to have any missing limbs or other significant injuries except for the bandages on her shoulder, and she was not unconscious.

She just appeared to be in poor spirits, which wasn’t considered too serious a condition and could be recovered with proper rest.

"A-Xin..." Seeing Chen Xin appear, Qin Lan called his name, but her voice was particularly hoarse and dry.

Looking at Qin Lan’s almost cracked lips and hearing her hoarse voice, Chen Xin didn’t need to think to know she hadn’t drunk water for a long time, so he hurriedly picked up the cup on the bedside table and poured a glass of water from the kettle for Qin Lan.

Carefully supporting Qin Lan’s neck, he fed the water to her mouth, watching her parched state, Chen Xin continually comforted her, "Drink slowly, no rush, there’s plenty here."

However, after finishing the water in the cup, Qin Lan shook her head, stopping Chen Xin from giving her more water.

Seeing this, Chen Xin put down the cup and sat by Qin Lan’s bedside, looking at her haggard appearance, he reached out to touch her forehead and asked with concern, "What exactly happened? How did you end up like this? This is not like you at all!"

"A-Xin..." Feeling the gentle soothing from Chen Xin, the emotions Qin Lan had been holding back since yesterday suddenly erupted. She called Chen Xin’s name and then burst into tears.

Wiping the tears from Qin Lan’s face, seeing her cry so sorrowfully, Chen Xin didn’t stop her but gently stroked her forehead, comforting her, "It’s okay, it’s okay, it’s good to cry it out."

Qin Lan cried for a long time, as if she couldn’t stop the tears.

Until Chen Xin felt that her continued crying would harm her body did he stop her, coaxing her while trying to understand what had happened.

After a bout of crying, Qin Lan’s emotions seemed to have been released, and she finally managed to gather herself, sobbing intermittently as she told Chen Xin what had happened.

"It’s all my fault. If I had fired the shot a little earlier, if I had been a little more decisive, those comrades wouldn’t have sacrificed themselves!" After explaining what had happened to Chen Xin, Qin Lan was on the verge of breaking down again, constantly blaming herself, regretting her mistakes, believing she was the cause of it all.

Listening to Qin Lan’s words, Chen Xin didn’t know where to start comforting her, so he silently wiped away her tears and said to Qin Lan, "Don’t be like this, A-Lan, this isn’t what you wanted either. Who could have expected things to turn out this way? Even if you had fired in time, those maniacs would still have had other means. You can’t blame yourself for this."

"How can I not blame myself? If I had fired sooner, I could have stopped that criminal from detonating the explosives, and those comrades standing beside him wouldn’t have sacrificed!" Qin Lan shouted at Chen Xin, venting her dissatisfaction with herself.

"How can this be your fault? You couldn’t have known in advance that the criminal had explosives and detonators on him! You already reacted in time. It’s the criminals who were too vicious; they are to blame, not you." Facing the emotionally unstable Qin Lan, Chen Xin could only try hard to comfort her, "And even those who were sacrificed, they wouldn’t want to see you like this, blaming yourself. If you feel sorry for them, shouldn’t you be more determined to bring those criminals to justice?"

Listening to Chen Xin’s comforting words, Qin Lan’s emotions finally calmed down somewhat.

Although she still felt guilty, she no longer shouted or cried.

As her emotions eased, a deep fatigue washed over her, and she soon fell asleep with her eyes closed.

Watching Qin Lan’s tight-frowned look with her eyes closed, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel a pang of heartache, but he didn’t do anything more, just covered her with a blanket, intending to keep watch over her from the chair by the bed.

However, just as Chen Xin was keeping watch over Qin Lan, a hand suddenly pressed down on his shoulder.

Chen Xin quickly turned his head and saw Hong Rui standing behind him, holding up a finger, making a "shh" gesture.

Seeing it was Dr. Hong Rui, Chen Xin didn’t call out in surprise, only quietly asked, "Dr. Hong, when did you arrive?"

"I’ve been here for a while, since you told her not to cry." Dr. Hong Rui explained with a smile to Chen Xin, "I was actually supposed to help her with psychological counseling, but seeing you were doing a good job, I didn’t step in. Thank you, you saved me a lot of work."

"Dr. Hong, you’re too kind. Regardless of everything, A-Lan is my..." Chen Xin paused here, smiled and shook his head before continuing, "with her going through such an ordeal, I can’t just stand by and do nothing, as long as it’s within my capability."

Saying this, Chen Xin glanced again at Qin Lan on the hospital bed and couldn’t help but sigh.

Who would have thought that someone so strong-willed as her could have such a vulnerable moment? Clearly, not long ago, she was the one comforting me, telling me not to break down emotionally.

Thinking about this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but sigh again.

Hearing his continuous sighs, Hong Rui also shook her head, patting Chen Xin on the shoulder and quietly saying, "Let’s talk outside, let her rest well here."

Hearing Hong Rui say this, Chen Xin nodded, following her out of the hospital room.

Out in the hallway, after closing the door to the ward, Hong Rui and Chen Xin finally spoke in normal voices.

"Didn’t expect to meet you again, Dr. Hong, I still haven’t thanked you for last time!" Chen Xin politely said to Hong Rui, while also inquiring about Qin Lan’s condition, "How is A-Lan’s condition? Is it very serious?"

"Not seriously, but the psychological trauma might be heavy, and it might take a while to heal, she needs time to slowly come through it on her own." Hong Rui shook her head, not using any complex medical terms, "Faced with something like this, it’s surely hard on her mentally too; during this time, make sure to show her lots of care and companionship, that’s what she needs most now from family and friends."

"I understand, I will take good care of her." Chen Xin nodded solemnly.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 172 - 170: Noticing the Abnormality
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After a deep sleep due to exhaustion, Qin Lan woke up because of the pain in her shoulder wound.

"A Lan, you’re awake? Are you hungry? There’s porridge here. I borrowed a thermos on purpose, so it’s still warm," Chen Xin immediately expressed his concern upon seeing Qin Lan open her eyes.

Qin Lan glanced at Chen Xin, seeming to want to get up, but with one hand in a sling, it was really inconvenient. Besides, moving clearly tugged at the wound, making Qin Lan’s face show a pained expression.

"Don’t move, don’t move! If you want to get up, I’ll help you!" Seeing this, Chen Xin quickly and carefully held her down, then raised the bed so Qin Lan could sit leaning back on it.

Chen Xin served a bowl of still-warm porridge and sat by the bedside. He scooped a spoonful, gently blew on it, and brought it to Qin Lan’s mouth, "Eat something. I asked the doctor; they said you haven’t eaten since you were brought in yesterday. You must be starving."

Looking at the hot porridge brought to her, Qin Lan indeed felt a bit hungry after what Chen Xin said, so she opened her mouth and let Chen Xin feed her the hot porridge.

However, as soon as the porridge entered her mouth, Qin Lan tasted something different and asked Chen Xin, "Did you cook this, Xin? You’re the only one who likes to add salt even to plain rice porridge."

"It’s all about flavor!" Seeing that Qin Lan still had the energy to discuss the taste of porridge with him, Chen Xin laughed and scooped another spoon of porridge to feed her.

As she ate the porridge Chen Xin fed her, Qin Lan couldn’t help but curiously ask, "Xin, where did you get the rice to make porridge? It’s hard to buy rice now, isn’t it?"

"Did you forget about the dry rice I grew in the greenhouse? It’s already been harvested; this is the rice I grew myself." Chen Xin explained to Qin Lan, seemingly happy to see she wasn’t brooding anymore, and continued the topic, "After husking, it wasn’t thoroughly polished, just roughly processed. It can be considered germ rice. A Lan, you’re injured now, so you need to eat something nutritious."

Hearing Chen Xin say this, Qin Lan’s expression darkened immediately. She glanced at her shoulder, and her mind returned to the comrades who had previously sacrificed themselves.

However, after a good sleep, Qin Lan’s spirits had improved significantly. She was no longer lost in blame and regret as before. Although she still felt heavy-hearted, she had recovered from the previous shock and breakdown.

But the lingering resentment in her heart over the matter wasn’t so easy to eliminate.

Naturally, Chen Xin noticed this. While continuing to feed her porridge, he asked the question that lingered in his mind while she was sleeping, "A Lan, you told me earlier that the criminals triggered the explosives on themselves, causing such heavy casualties, but that doesn’t make sense!"

"Doesn’t make sense? How so?" Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin, frowning immediately, not understanding what he meant.

"You said criminals took over the shelter, which I believe. In such apocalyptic times, there are always people who impulsively want to cause trouble, thinking the state and military can’t control them, allowing them to start their ventures. But these are merely insignificant schemers or socially awkward dreamers. Where do they get the charisma and execution to convince someone to conduct a suicide attack with explosives?" Chen Xin had previously failed to understand this point and thus asked Qin Lan his doubts.

Suicide attacks are not rare; during World War II, Japan’s Kamikaze tactics and Kikusui operations, the battlefield warriors aware of imminent death yet undeterred, religious extremists radicalized for acts of terror...

These actions involved self-sacrifice, but were all backed by strong ideological pillars. Rightly or wrongly, only individuals with the strongest resolve could launch such attacks, using their lives to inflict damage on enemies and opponents.

However, a strong resolve requires ideological guidance. Without systematic, almost brainwashing-level training, it’s difficult to establish adequately firm beliefs.

Where could these criminals get such firm thoughts and resolve?

Knowing they would certainly die, with no remains left behind, they still resolutely chose to attack, detonating the explosives strapped to their bodies — so resolute that even Qin Lan, a sniper, couldn’t react in time. Imagine their determination when carrying out this act.

They are neither soldiers indoctrinated with militaristic values and samurai spirit nor religious fanatics turned lunatics. Merely criminals from a third-tier city in Flame Country, whose usual tendency would be clinging to life. Where did this recklessness come from?

"This..." Being questioned by Chen Xin, Qin Lan also realized something was off. The criminals occupying the shelter behaved too abnormally.

Besides the criminal who detonated the explosives, those who later emerged from the shelter intending a counter-attack were also decisive and well-trained, even demonstrating skilled firearm seizure and counterattack actions. If not for the barrage of machine gun fire, they might have inflicted further damage.

These actions are not something ordinary rogue criminals could manage; only those who are well-trained and mentally conditioned could achieve this.

"Are you saying they’re terrorists?" Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin, understanding his implication but was equally puzzled, "But this is the central region of Flame Country, not the war-torn Middle East or South America! where would terrorists come from?"

"I don’t know; this should be something for your police to investigate, isn’t it? But you must admit, all this is exceedingly abnormal," Chen Xin laughed, continuing to feed Qin Lan porridge.

He realized Qin Lan’s attention had been diverted from remorse and self-blame to the abnormality in the situation, thereby achieving his intended effect.

"Since you noticed this point, Xin, didn’t you tell anyone else?" Under Chen Xin’s guidance, Qin Lan’s professional skills began to emerge, "Having realized the problem, I should quickly report to the captain!"

Saying this, Qin Lan was about to get up and report to Uncle Ding about the matter.

Chen Xin quickly held her down, stopping her impulsive action, "Leave this matter, A Lan. If I could notice the problem, I also think Uncle Ding can’t miss it. Besides, you’re injured and need to heal first. Even if messages need to be passed, I can help convey them."

"Then, Xin, quickly make a call to the captain! The hospital has a line connecting to the SWAT team!" Upon hearing Chen Xin, Qin Lan hurriedly urged him.

Just as Chen Xin comforted Qin Lan and prepared to make the call, Ding Ning’s voice suddenly came from the door, "What’s the urgent need to call my dad?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 173 - 171 Sudden Incident
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Xiao Ding!" Chen Xin greeted Ding Ning: "Why did you come over?"

"I heard Sister Lan got injured, so I came to check on her. What were you guys talking about just now? What’s the matter that needs to be urgently told to my dad?" While greeting Chen Xin and Qin Lan, Ding Ning was also concerned about their previous conversation.

Chen Xin explained to Ding Ning and then followed up with his earlier speculation: "A group of ordinary criminals, when faced with special forces and armed police, at most would resist desperately and only fight back when there is no way out. Most would act like a cornered dog.

But these criminals are clearly not like that. They obviously launched a premeditated suicide attack, and even their action of occupying the shelter itself is suspicious. They couldn’t possibly be unaware that taking over the shelter would attract the police, armed police, or even a military crackdown."

"It’s also possible they were so ignorant that they didn’t realize it," Ding Ning listened to Chen Xin’s analysis and added a possibility.

Chen Xin nodded, affirming Ding Ning’s supplement but added, "But people that ignorant couldn’t execute a suicide attack or a death charge unless they’re fools who don’t even realize what death means."

At this point, Chen Xin turned to Qin Lan and asked her, "Lan, do you think those people are fools?"

Qin Lan earnestly recalled the faces of those criminals she saw through the scope, shaking her head as she said, "They don’t look like fools; rather, they seem like..."

It’s undeniable that the faces deeply carved into her memory were full of ferocity and menace, with not a hint of foolishness. If anything, they seemed extremely fanatical.

"Their targets were very clear, and their actions decisive. Even in the heat of battle, they weren’t affected by the deaths of their comrades. Their fighting spirit was very tenacious. This doesn’t seem like a group of misled fools or typical criminals." At calm moments, Qin Lan’s mind as a sniper was very sharp, quickly analyzing the critical elements.

Listening to Chen Xin and Qin Lan’s analysis, Ding Ning’s expression also grew serious, nodding as she said, "If it really is as you both analyzed, then this matter urgently needs to be reported to my dad."

Being a police officer herself, Ding Ning understood the seriousness of the situation. With such a major incident happening yesterday, resulting in over twenty casualties, she would inevitably know what happened.

Since Ding Ning and Qin Lan both said so, Chen Xin did not insist on his view, acknowledging that this matter indeed needed to be reported to Uncle Ding.

The hospital had a phone; although mobile phones were unusable due to satellite and base station disconnection, traditional landline phones were still functional.

The call was quickly connected, and after listening to Chen Xin’s analysis, Uncle Ding also took the matter seriously.

After all, more than twenty casualties was a first in the city’s history. Both the city bureau and the government, as well as the armed police, were angered by it. The city government specifically ordered a thorough investigation.

A special investigation team was formed to get to the bottom of why such a heinous act of terrorism occurred. As a SWAT team captain, Uncle Ding was naturally a member of the investigation team.

Chen Xin’s analysis came timely; although the investigation team had already gathered many clues, Chen Xin’s analysis provided a new perspective.

After expressing gratitude to Chen Xin, Uncle Ding also asked him to convey to Qin Lan via phone: she should focus on resting and not worry. The team granted her a month-long leave and sent Ding Ning to take care of her.

After hanging up and returning to the ward, Chen Xin couldn’t help but find it strange to give Qin Lan a month’s leave and have Ding Ning take care of her at a time like this. Was that okay?

With such doubts, Chen Xin relayed the situation to Qin Lan and Ding Ning, both of whom looked somewhat puzzled after hearing it.

"Indeed, it’s odd. Taking a leave to recover from an injury isn’t a problem, but why would my dad want me to take care of Sister Lan?" That was Ding Ning’s most perplexing issue; what was the real intention behind arranging her to care for the injured Qin Lan?

Qin Lan also found it odd. Though the injury to her bones required a hundred days’ recuperation, being shot and given leave to recover wasn’t unusual, but granting a month-long leave during such a turbulent time was overly generous.

She had the lightest injury among everyone. Despite being hit by a stray bullet in the shoulder, after removing the bullet, it was expected to recover in about a week according to doctors.

Even if concerns about her mental state or related investigations were a factor, a month’s leave seemed too excessive given the current need for personnel, especially since Uncle Ding, the SWAT captain, sent Ding Ning to take care of her, making things even more suspicious.

Although Ding Ning could manage care, if someone were to look after Qin Lan, there were idle people in the shelter, and hiring someone would be more reliable than Ding Ning.

"Don’t overthink it. Since Uncle Ding said to give you a break, Lan, focus on recuperating first." Chen Xin couldn’t quite figure it out himself and advised Qin Lan to rest easy.

Since Ding Ning was temporarily assigned to take care of Qin Lan, Chen Xin didn’t have to stay by her side and could return to his shelter to manage things.

After all, the vegetables in Chen Xin’s shelter needed replanting, and the rice seedlings being nurtured needed timely transfer to the fields. There were plenty of tasks to handle.

Leaving the hospital to return to his shelter, Chen Xin didn’t forget to thank Li Yulong, who brought him back: "Thanks a lot this time. If not for you lending your car, Xiaolong, my car couldn’t possibly handle such frequent city trips in this weather."

The porridge in the hospital earlier was made by Chen Xin, who utilized the time while Qin Lan slept to fetch the rice he’d grown. He’d been able to make quick trips to the city thanks to Li Yulong lending his modified RV, even making porridge in the well-equipped kitchen of the RV.

The convenience of the RV made Chen Xin envious and appreciative. He thought if he were to modify a vehicle, regardless of the style, the living compartment must be fully equipped, and a kitchen was essential.

"Brother Chen, you’re too polite. I’m also worried about Sister Qin; lending the car is no big deal." Although Li Yulong was a businessman, in this matter, he acted like a true brother, showing no reluctance or stinginess.

As Chen Xin thanked him again, during his expression of gratitude, a long-inactive system suddenly displayed a message: "Personnel death at others’ shelters, survival points daily -2."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 174 - 172: Speculation About the Enemy
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Someone died? Looking at this prompt, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel very surprised.

"Personnel death in another shelter" this clearly referred to the shelter that had received the oxygen generator and ground source heat pump equipment he provided, earning him survival points.

Given that the official shelter accounting wasn’t settled yet, this range was actually very narrow.

The doctor, Li Wensheng, Mo Qingyan... were all friends in the survival group.

So whose shelter had someone died? So far, Chen Xin had traded out eight oxygen generators and two ground source heat pumps to friends, gaining a total of 3400 one-off survival points and +34 points per day.

But now this number had changed to +32.

Does the increase from previously gained points decrease with personnel deaths? While Chen Xin noted this down to himself, he was more concerned about who had someone die in their shelter.

He couldn’t openly ask about it, after all, he couldn’t just turn on the radio and shout, "Who lost two people in their shelter?" right?

Thinking it over, Chen Xin could only note this down, planning to ask which friend’s place had an incident during the routine communication contact.

Besides this unexpected event, nothing else disrupted Chen Xin’s plans.

After cleaning the empty planting panels in the greenhouse and putting in new seeds, and checking the rice seedling status and the fish in the tanks, Chen Xin finally finished his chores.

Rubbing his already somewhat hungry stomach, Chen Xin also began preparing dinner. Tonight, he planned to visit Qin Lan in the city, and bring her dinner.

Although the hospital’s meals weren’t bad, with porridge and other vegetables, the rice was clearly aged rice stored in warehouses, and it was polished white rice, nutritionally inferior to germ rice or brown rice.

It’s not that Chen Xin insisted on arguing that germ rice is better than white rice, but in this current widespread situation, the hospital’s meals are roughly considered good compared to the standard food in other shelters. Though not bad, it’s just ordinary standard before the disaster.

For an injured person like Qin Lan, being able to eat something more nutritious would also aid in her recovery.

Taking some rice from the food storage room, cutting a piece of Jinhua ham, taking a piece of previously frozen fish from the fridge, and some stored carrots, Chen Xin pondered a bit then took a can of tuna before leaving the storage room to prepare dinner.

He hulled the rice, blew away the rice bran, washed it clean, then took it to make porridge. Only then did Chen Xin start handling other ingredients.

The frozen fish was obtained in a trade with Li Wensheng, and since Chen Xin wasn’t eating quickly, there was still some left. The live fish in the greenhouse were for breeding, and he wouldn’t touch them for now, so only the frozen fish was available.

Fortunately, the fish had been cleaned long ago, and Chen Xin had cut them into sections, so they just needed to be thawed before cooking.

Chen Xin planned to stew a pot of fish soup, as fish meat is a high-protein food, beneficial for wound recovery.

"It’s a pity there’s no ginger or garlic, so I’ll have to use wine to cover the fishy taste." Chen Xin sighed. Fish naturally has a fishy odor, and normally, ginger and garlic would be used to remove it. But with none of those available now, he’d have to use some wine for the job.

Fortunately, since it’s stewing soup, Chen Xin wasn’t worried about the wine affecting Qin Lan’s wound recovery.

After frying the thawed fish meat in a pot, he added a splash of rum he hadn’t finished near the pot edge, and finding the smell okay, Chen Xin felt reassured putting the fish into the stew pot, adding water to start stewing.

As for the Jinhua ham and carrots, they were to be stewed together in the soup.

Jinhua ham would enhance the flavor, and carrots are nutritious, making them great complementary ingredients for the fish soup.

While thinking about this, Chen Xin opened the can of tuna, checked the smell, and added it to the boiling porridge.

White rice porridge doesn’t have much taste, so adding a little tuna counted as flavoring.

After everything was prepared, Chen Xin heated up a portion of shelter food for himself, serving as his dinner.

Finishing quickly and taking a simple shower, the soup and porridge were also ready. Chen Xin packed the soup and porridge separately in the thermos he had used for Qin Lan before. The remaining portion was stored in sealed containers, and Chen Xin carried the thermos to his car, heading towards the city.

With the roads being tricky and lacking Li Yulong’s modified RV, after some hesitation, Chen Xin decided to upgrade his SUV.

However, since it was a spontaneous decision and due to material and survival point restrictions on hand, Chen Xin only spent 500 points to modify the tires, enhancing the vehicle’s mobility, without undergoing major modifications.

With the upgraded tires improving passage, the SUV drove more smoothly, and even when encountering crosswinds on the bridge to the city, Chen Xin’s car only swayed slightly without any skidding.

Driving steadily to the hospital, Chen Xin stepped into the ward with the thermos, finding Ding Ning chatting with Qin Lan.

Their conversation was undoubtedly the content Chen Xin analyzed before, as they continued discussing who those people, deemed as terrorists, really were.

Ding Ning speculated that they might be members of some terrorist organization, but Qin Lan didn’t think any such organization could exist in the heart of the Flame Country and cause such a commotion. Instead, she believed it might be some cult.

After all, only faith can make people fearless, but some faiths inspire greatness, while others lead to blind fervor.

In today’s post-apocalyptic environment, with shelters isolated, it’s a fertile ground for cults. If not for strict control by the city and each shelter, doomsday cults would likely have spread widely already.

Even so, Qin Lan had already witnessed various absurd, foolish, and even evil and cruel cults during previous duties.

However, she didn’t believe these cults, cropping up like mushrooms after rain, could possess such organizational and execution capabilities. Inspiring followers with fervent faith is the basic skill of cults, but achieving well-trained, fearless suicidal attacks isn’t something ordinary cults can do.

Moreover, cultivating followers akin to Death Warriors isn’t something accomplished overnight.

So Qin Lan felt it was likely an old cult that had long existed, merely hidden below the surface until the apocalypse exposed their ambition.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 175 - 173 Subsequent Impact
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Although Qin Lan had many guesses about the identities of those criminals, as she was on leave, she couldn’t do anything about it. Now, all she could do was focus on recovering in the hospital.

Fortunately, Ding Ning was there to keep her company, so she wasn’t left with idle thoughts running wild.

Meanwhile, Chen Xin continued to bring her food every day. Although there weren’t many ingredients available, Chen Xin tried his best to vary the dishes to supplement her nutrition.

This incident might only have such a limited impact on Qin Lan and Chen Xin, but it stirred a huge commotion throughout the entire city and its subordinate counties. It even alarmed the provincial government, prompting the dispatch of an emergency investigation team to the city government to conduct a joint investigation with the local team. They also screened all city shelters.

Regardless of size, whether official or private, all shelters had to undergo inspections by the joint investigation team. As a result, not only did the city’s armed police and special police deploy comprehensively, but the provincial investigation team also brought in a military unit from the provincial capital.

Though it was just two companies of soldiers, they were true combat troops, different from the internal security forces like the armed police and special police. They were meant for external military operations, signaling a completely different significance to the event.

If it was just armed police and special police involved, the incident could have still been seen as an internal conflict among the people, not as foreign enemies.

But once the military was involved, it indicated that the criminals behind this incident were no longer considered part of the people but enemies outside of them.

This was akin to the riots in Hong Kong before the disaster. Despite Flame Country having stationed troops locally and an armed police force in a neighboring city, the incident was still handled by Hong Kong’s local police, not the stationed troops.

This wasn’t just a political consideration due to the threat of foreign hostile forces. More importantly, those people were still considered part of the people, not deemed as enemies of the state.

In Flame Country, the military is for external threats, and the police for internal affairs, which has always been the national policy.

Although during major disasters the military is also deployed for rescue and relief or to assist the people, military operations are only against foreign enemies. Never in Flame Country’s history has the military been aimed at its people.

But now that the provincial investigation team had called in the military, the nature of the incident undoubtedly became vastly different.

At the joint investigation team’s request, all shelters underwent inspections. Personnel identities and backgrounds within the shelters were screened by specialists, suspicious individuals interrogated, and relevant personnel were stressed about the seriousness of the matter.

For a time, the entire city was in a state of high alert, enveloped in a tense and serious atmosphere.

Under such an atmosphere, the spontaneously formed black market in the city naturally couldn’t continue. It was the first to be raided by the special police, banning any further private transactions to prevent providing resources for potential criminals.

Li Yulong’s plantation was also suspended after a raid on a minor shelter. Although with the city government’s backing, Li Yulong wasn’t treated as a suspect, the project was still put on hold until the investigation concluded, unable to resume until then.

This was undoubtedly a huge blow for Li Yulong, who was determined to achieve something great. The setback to his fledgling business, though not a failure, left him extremely frustrated.

"Brother Chen, what’s going on here? Who did I offend?" Li Yulong complained to Chen Xin, as Chen was the only one he could vent to over this issue.

"This is just a disaster without cause; you were merely affected by it." Listening to Li Yulong’s grievances, Chen Xin was also in a foul mood: "Even my shelter was searched by the special police. You should feel grateful that your project was only suspended, not canceled."

Hearing this, Li Yulong felt even more depressed: "You’re right, but it’s really frustrating!"

"If you’re dissatisfied, investigate what’s going on and find those people." Chen Xin looked earnestly at Li Yulong: "Your family has connections across all facets in this city; can’t you uncover the truth? If you can identify who’s behind it and present the clues to the joint investigation team, I’m sure they’ll restart your project soon."

Given his father’s status as one of the city’s wealthiest merchants before the disaster, and his involvement in the restaurant and hotel industry, Li Yulong naturally had substantial connections to probe into certain matters.

But hearing Chen Xin’s suggestion only made Li Yulong’s expression more gloomy.

"Don’t mention it. I asked my dad to use his contacts to investigate, but he told me not to get involved and to let the project remain halted." That’s what frustrated Li Yulong the most.

His business being shut down, while his father not helping nor coming up with ideas, and instead telling him to stay out, left Li Yulong’s enthusiasm wasted, like punching into thin air.

Upon hearing this, Chen Xin frowned; this was indeed interesting.

With his son’s business affected, for Li Yulong’s father not to find a solution but to tell him to stay out, either he thinks the matter is too deep and wants to avoid trouble, or he knows something and doesn’t want his son involved.

So, which thought does Li Yulong’s father have?

Thinking the matter too deep is a possibility. While Li Yulong’s plantation project was affected, it wasn’t too entangled; as long as they stayed out, it wasn’t a big deal.

However, Chen Xin felt it wasn’t that simple.

Yet, this was a family matter between them, and Chen Xin didn’t want to meddle, so he shifted the conversation to other topics.

"Brother Chen, did you know? The ’Doctor’s’ family had an accident. Due to a lack of heating equipment, his parents froze to death a few days ago." Shifting the conversation, Li Yulong naturally didn’t bring up the issue again, changing the topic to events among their survival group members.

"Hmm? The ’Doctor’s’ parents died?" Hearing this, Chen Xin immediately recalled a previous system notification he had received, which must have been when it happened.

But why did the ’Doctor’s’ parents die? In this weather, even though their shelter was simple, the temperature had been dropping for some time. Why would they freeze to death now?

Recalling the behavior of the ’Doctor’s’ parents when he last visited, Chen Xin shook his head, not wishing to delve deeper into the reasons.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 176 - 174 Nuclear Battery
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The way others live doesn’t really concern Chen Xin; what he needs to worry about now is his own life.

His original plan to upgrade the shelter was also impacted by this incident. The plan to collect supplies through Li Yulong’s trading market was temporarily shattered as the trading market and the plantation were halted, and the special police team’s supply trading wouldn’t have the energy to spare in the short term.

After all, with the trading market project suspended and the special police team needing to invest their forces into investigating this incident, the material exchanges with Chen Xin can only be maintained at the level of basic living supplies.

This makes it impossible for Chen Xin’s original shelter upgrade plan to continue in the short term.

Although after the geothermal pump unit started running smoothly at the special police team, the survival points were cleared, bringing Chen Xin’s reserve of survival points close to five figures, and the daily acquisition of survival points broke through a hundred, allowing him to upgrade the shelter using survival points after saving for some time, Chen Xin still didn’t want to upgrade in such a manner.

On one hand, the cost is too high; on the other, a single upgrade plan cannot meet Chen Xin’s needs.

When calculating survival points at the special police team, Chen Xin also discovered that according to the rules set by the system, the maximum survival points he could obtain at once from a single shelter or settlement was 5,000 points, and the daily survival points acquisition limit increased by 50 points at a time.

This clearly restricted Chen Xin’s idea of quickly acquiring a large number of survival points in a short time to directly upgrade the shelter using survival points.

Perhaps he could choose one upgrade plan first and make adjustments later, but if possible, Chen Xin still hopes to upgrade the shelter as a whole in one go.

So, due to the shortage of supplies, Chen Xin had to temporarily shelve his shelter upgrade plan and focus his energy on other things.

For example, upgrading and modifying his vehicle.

These days, due to frequently entering the city to deliver food to Qin Lan, the demand for the vehicle has increased, and Chen Xin has more requirements for his car, making the vehicle upgrade and modification become his current priority.

However, this is not considered a major impact. After all, vehicle modification was already among the prioritized tasks in his list, now it’s just prioritizing this over the overall shelter upgrade.

Previously, to go out, Chen Xin upgraded the wheels, improving the tires’ ability to cope with snowy roads.

But that’s only the most basic upgrade and cannot transform the vehicle’s performance significantly.

Though this is sufficient in the current situation, Chen Xin wants more.

The previous tire modification merely enhanced the grip of ordinary snow tires, adjusted the chassis height and suspension system, but if he wants to further improve performance and ride comfort, more components must be upgraded.

For instance, the chassis structure.

Currently, Chen Xin’s vehicle has a wheeled chassis, offering advantages like low fuel consumption, lightweight, and good maneuverability, but the passability is inferior to a tracked chassis.

Tracked chassis is heavier and has higher fuel consumption, but it has stronger carrying capacity and can tow a heavier car body.

Additionally, the tracked chassis has greater traction and lower ground pressure, thus exhibiting good off-road performance and stability, greater climbing ability, and is more suitable for complex road conditions with elevation changes.

Especially when driving on snow, wheeled chassis can easily encounter tires getting stuck in snow, while tracks don’t have such issues, enabling vehicles to travel stably across the snow surface without wheels getting trapped.

For Chen Xin, he prefers to replace the SUV’s chassis with a tracked one, upgrading it to an ATV all-terrain vehicle.

Yet, despite various benefits of the tracked chassis, energy consumption is a crucial issue Chen Xin cannot avoid, as without a powerful engine he cannot drive the tracked chassis, and high power also means high fuel consumption.

"So it still comes back to nuclear power, huh!" Chen Xin looked at the design sketch he drew on the computer and thought he still needs nuclear power for his car.

Considering his calculations, only nuclear power can meet his usage needs, as switching to a tracked chassis would lead to severe fuel consumption.

As for nuclear power, it’s not really considered high-tech; although many associate nuclear with radiation and find it dangerous, it must be said that with humanity’s current technological level, we can cleanly and efficiently utilize nuclear energy.

Moreover, the sustainability and reliability of such things are quite dependable. The nuclear-powered lighthouse built during the pre-Sovia era is still running today, though radiation leaks occurred due to long-term lack of maintenance and aging equipment.

Thus historically, those people who resorted to nuclear solutions not only built nuclear-powered tanks with four tracks but also used nuclear bombs to build reservoirs and extinguish oil reservoir fires.

Implementing nuclear power in vehicles is not complicated, just a radioactive isotope thermoelectric generator is needed.

The principle of this device is utilizing the heat-emitting rays released during the decay of radioactive substances, converting heat energy into electric energy within thermoelectric elements.

Radioactive substances are stable throughout the decay process, with released energy size and speed unaffected by external environmental temperatures, chemical reactions, pressure, or electromagnetic fields, hence radioactive isotope thermoelectric generators are known for strong interference resistance and accurate reliable operation.

Another feature is the long transformation time, dictating that nuclear batteries can be used for a long time.

Chen Xin certainly doesn’t have a nuclear battery at hand, nor does he have a place to buy it, but having a system means he can upgrade on his own.

Creating a nuclear battery requires making isotopes with long half-lives into fuel, wrapping them with thermoelectric elements, and for safety perhaps coating them with a layer of lead to prevent radiation leaks.

But upgrading through the system is not complicated; Chen Xin just needs to prepare isotopic material with long half-life, thermoelectric elements, and lead to upgrade his SUV’s car battery to a nuclear battery.

Needless to say, upgrading using survival points without these materials is also fine.

After carefully considering the pros and cons, Chen Xin decided to first directly use survival points to upgrade to a nuclear battery, then consider acquiring isotopic materials.

Considering isotopes are regulated materials in Flame Country, unlike thermoelectric elements and lead which are easily obtainable, he certainly won’t be able to acquire isotopes directly.

But having a nuclear battery as a sample, claiming he can manufacture nuclear batteries, Chen Xin thinks there’s some hope of acquiring isotopic materials.

Thinking this, Chen Xin picked up a wrench and screwdriver from the workbench, deciding to first remove his car’s battery and upgrade it to a nuclear battery.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 177 - 175 Vehicle Modification Plan
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Upgrading the car battery to a Nuclear Battery is a simple task for Chen Xin, who is now well-acquainted with how to utilize system upgrades.

He removes the car battery from his SUV, opens the system interface, invests survival points, and clicks upgrade.

1,000 survival points disappear, a green light flashes, and the originally black-and-white plastic car battery transforms into a cylindrical Nuclear Battery with a metallic shell emitting a green glow.

Seeing this familiar shape, Chen Xin can’t help but press his forehead. Isn’t this the Titan’s battery from "Titanfall"?

In the game, this thing powers the energy for Titans, those gigantic humanoid combat Mechas. Just one can activate the Titan, and three can allow it to dominate the battlefield with formidable combat power.

If it really is the kind of Nuclear Battery from the game, using this thing on a vehicle would be perfect.

The Titan not only fires artillery and moves, but also supports the Shield and unleashes various high-tech skills, yet the Nuclear Battery can handle all that, let alone just a car.

However, even though the Nuclear Battery is created, Chen Xin still needs to conduct inspections on it.

At the very least, measuring voltage, current, and output power is necessary to ensure there are no issues in usage.

After all, if the current is too large and burns out the circuit, that would be problematic.

Fortunately, after a round of inspections, Chen Xin confirms that this Nuclear Battery can continuously output 300A of current at 500V, with an actual power of 150KW, which converts to approximately 200 horsepower.

This power is already quite significant; before the disaster, the batteries used in popular new energy vehicles had about the same power, but those batteries could only store around a few dozen kWh, which is a crucial factor limiting the range of new energy vehicles.

But this Nuclear Battery is different. Using long half-life isotopes as its heat source, its theoretical lifespan exceeds ten years.

Of course, if Chen Xin were to use it as in "Titanfall," mounted on Titans for battle, a single Nuclear Battery wouldn’t last long, but clearly Chen Xin doesn’t need to use it that violently, just as a power source for a car.

With the Nuclear Battery’s output power of 200 horsepower, it’s naturally no problem powering an all-terrain vehicle. Before the disaster, the classic Swedish Bv206 all-terrain carrier’s engine power was only 136 horsepower, just two-thirds of this Nuclear Battery, and the Bv206’s weight was 4.34 tons with a maximum load capacity of two tons.

Most ordinary cars weigh about one and a half tons, making this battery’s output power more than sufficient for Chen Xin.

With the biggest limitation, the energy problem solved, the rest is much simpler.

For the chassis, Chen Xin prefers a tracked design, but he wants the body not in a hinged two-section design like the Bv206 but rather a monocoque body.

The advantage of the hinged two-section design is the ability to conveniently swap out rear compartments to change function modules, and the hinge connection can eliminate torsional loads on the frame, thereby enhancing the entire structure’s reliability, and greatly reducing the vehicle suspension system’s demands when traversing rugged terrain.

However, Chen Xin doesn’t want a transport vehicle or a truck but a more comfortable caravan that can provide a relatively comfortable driving and living environment while on the move, rather than bouncing around inside the cabin like a roller coaster.

Thus, this naturally places high demands on the vehicle’s suspension and shock absorption.

Moreover, the key point is Chen Xin plans to modify this vehicle to pick up his parents back home, and for a long-distance drive spanning several hundred kilometers, Chen Xin must consider stopping for rest.

If he chooses a hinged two-section cabin, when stopping for rest and wanting to use the rear cabin, he must get out from the front driver’s cab to enter the rear cabin from outside the vehicle, which significantly increases risk in the current environment.

An integrated body cabin naturally avoids such troubles and dangers, allowing him to directly enter the rear living cabin from the driver’s cab inside the cabin.

Even if surrounded or needing to escape while resting, he can directly enter the driver’s cab from the living cabin and drive away, without needing to get out and circle around to start the vehicle.

After considering his own needs, Chen Xin decides the modified vehicle will be a caravan with a tracked chassis and an integrated body.

Without relying on the system, it would clearly be impossible for Chen Xin to transform his SUV into such a vehicle. The wheeled chassis cannot simply be turned into a tracked one, and the transmission structure and power system need complete redesigning, not to mention converting the SUV’s small body into a living cabin.

But with the system, all that is not a problem.

However, to complete the modification, besides the investment of survival points, a large amount of materials is also required. Without material investment, the cost in survival points would be too high.

The materials needed for the modifications are substantial, even more than what Chen Xin needed for the overall upgrade of the shelter.

Of course, this refers to the variety, not the quantity of a single material, as the total amount of materials needed to modify a vehicle can’t compare to the materials for a shelter.

Seeing the diverse system-listed modification materials, Chen Xin can’t help but scratch his head.

The variety of modification materials mainly consists of car parts, alongside materials for cold-proofing and modifying the living cabin. Among the materials list, Chen Xin even sees items like water circulation systems and shortwave communication radios.

"To obtain these things..." Chen Xin copies the materials list onto a sheet of paper, pondering how to acquire them.

It’s not like before the disaster when these items could simply be purchased with money. In fact, many of these are no longer obtainable with "cash," and some even require connections.

Car parts are relatively easy since Li Yulong used to work at a 4S dealership. Chen Xin believes he can still get those from him.

As for cold-proof materials and some items needed for modifying the living cabin, Chen Xin retrieves the list previously used for trading with the SWAT team and sees many materials he needs.

Yet, for items like water circulation systems and shortwave communication radios... while Chen Xin has them, they are important facilities installed in the shelter. Is he expected to dismantle the shelter’s equipment for a car modification?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 178 - 176 Making a Water Purifier
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin didn’t plan to dismantle the equipment in his shelter, so modifying the vehicle meant he had to collect resources anew or make parts by himself.

Fortunately, he had a workbench, and he could process some of the materials needed for the vehicle modification himself. What he truly needed to trade for and collect were mainly raw materials and those that were more complicated to process and more conveniently exchanged.

These included car parts, cold-resistant materials, and various types of steel needed for vehicle modifications, which Chen Xin had to obtain through exchanges with Li Yulong and the SWAT team.

However, materials like shock springs, track parts, and water circulation devices could be fully manufactured by himself, consuming only a minimal amount of survival points and raw materials.

Most of the raw materials Chen Xin had reserved were prepared for the planned comprehensive upgrade of the shelter, including mainly construction materials like cement and rebar, with the rest being mostly insulating boards and similar items.

But he didn’t have much high-performance steel used for making shock springs and track parts, with only a few steel plates intended to reinforce the shelter’s structural integrity.

"So, I have to collect a batch of steel!" Chen Xin sighed helplessly as he looked at the list of materials.

Even though there was a system, allowing even the simplest metals to serve as raw materials, or even non-metal materials, considering cost and investment, as well as subsequent use, Chen Xin felt it more appropriate to get a batch of steel.

Fortunately, aside from parts requiring metal materials, items like the water circulation device did not require a large consumption of metal. Although metal performed better, other materials could substitute.

So Chen Xin decided to first create everything he could with the materials at hand and see if he could make some lower-grade substitutes for what he couldn’t, then compensate by upgrading them with survival points.

Although the system’s upgrade function was powerful, it consumed a significant amount of survival points. Even though he currently had nearly five digits of survival points, if he used the system functions without reservation to make these things, he’d use up more than one-fifth of them.

So, saving where possible was necessary.

Making a water circulation device wasn’t too difficult and didn’t consume too many materials; the key aspect was the recycling and purification of wastewater for reuse.

No need to worry about recycling the wastewater, as the water pipelines would handle waste recovery when the water circulation device was installed in the vehicle. The real focus was on purification.

Simple wastewater purification was not complicated; using activated carbon, gravel, and such materials could sediment and filter the wastewater.

However, this kind of simple filtration was ineffective, not separating potential chemical contaminants and heavy metal ions.

Chen Xin had purchased portable filters essential for wilderness survival and had studied their structures.

Apart from basic gravel and activated carbon filtration, the key component was the filter membrane, which effectively filtered out bacteria and other harmful substances, making the dirty water drinkable again.

In his wilderness survival experiences, Chen Xin used such portable water filters, handy for obtaining clean drinking water when clean sources were hard to find or boiling was not feasible.

Using this type of filter as part of the water circulation device was more than adequate.

However, Chen Xin didn’t use his portable filter directly as a component nor dismantle it for parts but planned to create one himself.

He sawed a segment of PVC pipe for the main body, cut additional piping for auxiliary materials, used the system’s processing functions to modify structures, transforming the auxiliary pipes into endcaps, and filled the main body with filter materials.

The filter membrane had specific materials he didn’t have. Though possible to fabricate with system functions, Chen Xin found it unnecessary.

Since using system functions was involved anyway, why not upgrade directly?

After filling the newly made filter body with sand and charcoal, Chen Xin compared the consumption of making a filter membrane himself versus directly upgrading the filter and decisively opted for an upgrade.

After all, it was a small component, costing 10 survival points to make a filter membrane, but a simple upgrade required only a bit of additional material and 15 survival points. Weighing both, Chen Xin could afford it.

Once the homemade filter reached the level of a purchased portable one, Chen Xin installed it as the water purification component in the self-made water circulation device.

Thus, the water circulation device, a crucial part of vehicle modification, was completed.

Marking a tick on the checklist, Chen Xin looked over the listed materials and components, contemplating his next move.

Just as he was deep in thought, his radio suddenly buzzed.

————————————————

At this time, outside Chen Xin’s shelter, a police armored vehicle was parked at his doorstep.

"Chen Xin said he’d come to open the door right away." On the vehicle, a SWAT team member put down the radio and informed Li Head and an official-looking person beside him, not wearing a uniform.

Both nodded and said nothing further.

In the previous operation, although Li Head’s team had members like Qin Lan injured and hospitalized, and a few others also treated in hospitals, even lacking manpower, amidst city-wide mobilization and sweeping searches, they were still dispatched to conduct non-official shelter inspections with the Joint Investigation Group.

"A citizen cooperating with our SWAT team lives here, mainly providing us with fresh vegetables in exchange for survival supplies. We’ve checked before; there’s no suspicion." Li Head explained to the official, clearly speaking favorably on Chen Xin’s behalf, hoping to avoid suspicion.

"The Municipal Bureau’s work is, of course, trustworthy. However, the Joint Investigation Group decided on a second search; it’s safer to check again." The official nodded slightly without visibly reacting to Li Head’s explanation.

But seeing his demeanor, Li Head understood the official had considered his words.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 179 - 177: Investigating the Situation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Old Li? Why are you here again? Didn’t we already have an inspection before?" Chen Xin opened the door to the refuge, allowing the SWAT team and investigation team members to enter, and asked Li Tou his question.

"We did come before, but this time the joint investigation team requested a re-inspection. Comrade Xiao Chen, we hope you understand and cooperate with our work." Li Tou was relatively courteous to Chen Xin, knowing that Chen Xin had good relations with Qin Lan and Ding Ning, without appearing too stern.

Chen Xin nodded in understanding and firmly stated, "I will cooperate as much as possible, but what is it you need to inspect again this time? You’ve already familiarized yourself with the situation."

"The issues are still pretty much the same. Comrade, you just need to answer briefly." The staff member from the joint investigation team spoke up on the side.

Hearing this, Chen Xin glanced at Li Tou. Seeing no reaction from Li Tou, he nodded, "Alright, let’s talk inside. It’s quite cold out here."

With that said, he led Li Tou, the joint investigation staff member, and two other SWAT officers into his refuge, leaving the outermost door open to avoid any suspicion.

Passing through the sanitation room at the entrance, as the group walked through the corridor, the joint investigation staff noticed the Four-Armed Heavy Crossbow mounted at the top of the passage. In surprise, they asked, "Comrade, what’s this thing for, mounted here?"

After systemic upgrades, the original recurve bow had become a Four-Armed Heavy Crossbow, equipped with a unique gear and reloading mechanism, turning it into a rapid-fire weapon. It looked especially menacing from the outside.

Particularly since Chen Xin mounted the heavy crossbow at the top of the passage, directly aimed at the entrance, it gave anyone entering the passage the feeling of having the crossbow pointed at their head.

"Oh, this..." Hearing the staff member ask about his crossbow, seeing Li Tou still showed no sign, Chen Xin explained, "It’s a kind of defense measure, I suppose. Since I live alone, if someone breaks in, this serves as a defensive mechanism, at least it can fend off dangerous individuals."

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s explanation, the staff member could understand his reasoning. However, looking at the finger-thick bolts, they couldn’t help but say, "Having safety measures is indeed necessary, but the crossbow is a bit... exaggerated, isn’t it?"

Chen Xin couldn’t say much regarding this evaluation, so he just smiled and led them down into the interior of the refuge.

After inviting the four to sit down in the living room, Chen Xin addressed them, "I’m not sure what you need to ask this time. Feel free to ask anything you need me to answer."

"Comrade, there’s no need to be nervous. We’re just here to understand the basic situation. You just need to tell me some basic information, like how many people live here and the basic personal information of each." The joint investigation team member pulled out a questionnaire, asking Chen Xin questions while filling in the form.

Since there wasn’t much content to ask, and since Chen Xin was indeed living alone, the investigation was concluded quickly.

After the staff member collected the form, Li Tou said to Chen Xin, "Comrade Xiao Chen, could you show us around? It’s just a routine procedure, and we ask for your understanding."

"No problem. After all, you’ve seen this place last time as well." Chen Xin didn’t oppose the request. Li Tou only wanted a brief tour, rather than a search, so Chen Xin didn’t ask him for a search warrant.

Thus, the group went on to inspect Chen Xin’s refuge.

Chen Xin’s place wasn’t big, so it didn’t take long to look around every corner.

Having been there before, Li Tou and the others weren’t surprised by the state of Chen Xin’s place, but for the joint investigation staff, the rich supplies in Chen Xin’s storage room and the sprouting crops in the greenhouse were nothing short of astonishing.

"This is truly... stunning!" The staff member’s eyes widened. Since the apocalypse began, it’s been difficult to see this much greenery, even in official refuges.

"It’s alright; most of it was prepared before the disaster," Chen Xin humbly commented, adding to Li Tou on the side, "I recently got a batch of seeds, including cucumbers, tomatoes, and such, which I’ve already planted. If all goes well, I should be able to provide more varieties of vegetables in the future. I hope you, from the SWAT team, could exchange more supplies with me."

Hearing this, Li Tou hastily agreed, delighted.

Having access to more vegetables was something everyone dreamed of, and Li Tou couldn’t and wouldn’t refuse such an opportunity.

After checking out Chen Xin’s greenhouse, they’d essentially inspected the entire refuge. Finding nothing suspicious, Li Tou and the others prepared to leave.

As they were about to depart, Li Tou noticed the water purifier and scattered blueprints on Chen Xin’s workbench and curiously asked, "Comrade Xiao Chen, what are you up to? It looks like a vehicle?"

"I’m planning to modify my car. Given the current weather and road conditions, regular cars can’t get around. Not driving is too inconvenient, so I’ll have to modify the car." Chen Xin didn’t hide his intentions and explained to Li Tou, mentioning, "However, I’m still missing some materials, so I might have to find someone to trade."

At first, Li Tou found Chen Xin’s words a bit strange, but noticing the staff member from the joint investigation team standing nearby, he realized Chen Xin’s concern. So, without saying anything further, he nodded to Chen Xin and left with his team.

After seeing Li Tou and the team off and closing the refuge door, Chen Xin let out a small sigh of relief.

Even though he did nothing wrong and had nothing to fear, having the SWAT come twice for inspections still made Chen Xin a bit tense.

If it were just the SWAT team, it wouldn’t be a big deal. They had a mutual interest relationship, and they knew him well enough. But with the joint investigation team member present, Chen Xin was genuinely worried they’d discover he had a gun.

In the current environment of screening for dangerous elements, getting caught with a gun — even if it was traded from the SWAT team — would undoubtedly cause trouble for Chen Xin, potentially implicating Qin Lan, Uncle Ding, and the SWAT members.

After all, when Chen Xin shot and killed three people last time, it was Qin Lan who took responsibility, claiming she fired the shots, while Chen Xin only witnessed the deaths, thus concealing the fact that he had a gun.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 180 - 178: Anxious and Frustrated
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After using the materials on hand to make all the parts that could be made, and even making half a track as an extra, Chen Xin’s materials were exhausted.

Although there were still quite a few parts in the storage room, those parts couldn’t be used for vehicle modification unless Chen Xin recycled them into raw materials for reprocessing.

To avoid such unnecessary waste, Chen Xin decided to go find someone to get more resources.

The special police team was probably not an option for the time being, as they were also trying to avoid suspicion in this sensitive situation, so Chen Xin could only rely on his survival group friends and Li Yulong.

The resources available for exchange from the survival group friends were not many, and were mainly survival supplies. They would also need to collect the parts required for vehicle modification.

So, after mentioning this during the daily information exchange with his group, Chen Xin didn’t hope they could provide him with much material and set off to the city to find Li Yulong.

Li Yulong had now moved to a small shelter where he planned to run a plantation and trading market. However, because this project had been temporarily suspended by the city government, even though the shelter was up and running again and relevant construction and modifications were underway, it still couldn’t be used.

When Chen Xin saw Li Yulong, he was standing in the area originally designated as the plantation, scolding his subordinates.

"What’s wrong, Xiaolong? Why are you so angry?" Chen Xin asked curiously when he was brought to Li Yulong’s side and saw his infuriated look.

"I’m so pissed off! Xiao Chen, you’re just in time!" Although Li Yulong adjusted his expression upon Chen Xin’s arrival, he was still fuming: "You know the situation. You be the judge; now that the city government has stopped this project, shouldn’t the planting greenhouse be temporarily sealed? Even if not sealed, shouldn’t we plant something that’s easy to store?"

"You make a valid point, so what’s the issue?" Chen Xin replied, turning his attention to the plantation in front of him.

Planting had already begun in the plantation, but just as Li Yulong said, when the project was temporarily halted by the city government, they shouldn’t have continued planting crops in the plantation.

Because the plantation was planned for crops of higher economic value, such as fresh vegetables and fruits, but these things had a very obvious characteristic: they weren’t easy to store.

Freezing them wasn’t out of the question, but they’d basically end up ruined once thawed. They could only be kept in a cold storage, which still had a time limitation.

If made into freeze-dried or dehydrated vegetables, while the nutritional value wouldn’t be a problem, there would undoubtedly be a huge difference in taste from fresh vegetables and fruits. There would also be a significant price difference.

Although Chen Xin wasn’t sure what Li Yulong’s subordinates had planted, from the leaves, it seemed very likely to be some kind of fruit.

"I don’t know if these guys did it to piss me off or just didn’t get what I was saying; they planted strawberries and grapes!" Li Yulong felt particularly aggrieved when he mentioned this, clearly very dissatisfied with the matter.

Seeing him so upset, Chen Xin couldn’t help but chuckle.

Li Yulong’s subordinates planting strawberries and grapes wasn’t exactly a mistake, but given the current situation, their actions were indeed a bit untimely.

If everything had gone according to plan, planting strawberries and grapes would have been a great choice, as fresh strawberries and grapes would undoubtedly become the hottest items once the trading market opened.

Even if it didn’t turn a huge profit, it would still fill Li Yulong’s pockets well.
