However, now that the project was halted and it was unknown when it could restart, the question of when the trading market would operate raised a big question mark, and whether these fruits, which were hard to store, could be sold in time was an even bigger question.

If they couldn’t be sold in time, the investment in planting this batch of fruit would be completely wasted.

This was enough to bankrupt a grower even before the disaster, let alone in the current post-apocalyptic world where every resource couldn’t be wasted.

"Don’t get too worked up. Think about how to fix this." Chen Xin could only advise Li Yulong to calm down for now.

"Fix it? How? The seeds have already sprouted; we can’t just pull them all up, can we?" Li Yulong still felt a wave of frustration. Though they had stockpiled a lot of crop seeds before the disaster, they couldn’t afford such wastage.

As for how to fix it, Chen Xin did have a bit of an idea, and since this plantation was partly his investment too, he suggested, "Strawberries and grapes take time to mature, so there’s no rush. Maybe during this period, the city government will have results. By then, your operation could resume and these crops could be sold."

"Xiao Chen, that’s the most ideal scenario, but considering the huge fuss the city government is making, how could there be results in a short time?" Li Yulong knew what Chen Xin suggested was possible, but it was too idealistic, and he wouldn’t pin his hopes on such an ideal situation.

"I know that’s the most ideal situation, but you can’t say it’s impossible, right?" Chen Xin laughed, seeing that Li Yulong was less angry, he patted his shoulder and continued, "And if worst comes worst, we could make strawberry jam or wine; making them isn’t complicated, and they’re more storable. Don’t worry too much; maybe jam and wine will sell even better?"

Li Yulong’s mood only calmed after Chen Xin said this. After cursing at his subordinates a few more times, he walked with Chen Xin to a cleared-out lounge.

After sitting in the lounge, Li Yulong poured water for Chen Xin and asked, "Xiao Chen, what brings you here today?"

"I’m planning to modify my car, so I wanted to ask if you have any extra car parts. If you do, I’ll trade for a batch." Chen Xin didn’t stand on ceremony with Li Yulong and directly stated his intention.

Hearing Chen Xin’s intention, Li Yulong hesitated for a moment before saying, "Xiao Chen, how do you plan to modify it? I remember your car is an SUV, right? Do you have a list of the parts you need? I can’t guarantee we have everything in storage, but I can try to gather them for you."

"It’s enough that you said that. I basically need some general parts." Chen Xin smiled at Li Yulong.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 181 - 179 Strange Aspects
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin and Li Yulong had negotiated the car parts issue, and after handing over his list to him, Chen Xin prepared to leave.

He was planning to visit Qin Lan at the hospital today, so he didn’t stay long at Li Yulong’s place.

However, upon leaving, Chen Xin unexpectedly saw someone who shouldn’t be there.

"Doctor? Why are you here?" Chen Xin looked at the "doctor" standing in front of him, surprised to see him here, especially since he didn’t appear to have come from outside—without a protective suit, he looked as if he lived there.

"Writer? The heating in my family’s shelter is really poor, and Xie Chang and I were planning to go to the official shelter. But then Mr. Li said he needed people here, so Xie Chang and I moved in." The "doctor" seemed equally surprised to see Chen Xin but politely greeted him, though with a worn-out expression, likely due to the heavy blow of his parents’ passing.

Listening to the "doctor" speak, Chen Xin’s eyes slightly narrowed—when did the doctor start calling Xie Chang by his full name instead of Xiao Xie?

However, Chen Xin didn’t delve into it further, nodded at the doctor, and left.

Driving towards the hospital, Chen Xin was quite pleased about the doctor moving to Li Yulong’s place.

After all, the doctor had a medical license and was running a small clinic before; moving to this future trading market would be beneficial for attracting customers.

Of course, hospitals can provide comprehensive medical services, but sometimes people have a preference for small clinics.

Chen Xin soon arrived at the hospital and found Hong Rui checking on Qin Lan when he got to the ward.

"Hello, Dr. Hong, why are you doing a check-up for Ah Lan?" Chen Xin greeted Hong Rui.

"As Officer Qin’s psychiatrist, it’s just a routine check-up." Hong Rui was still in her familiar short skirt, stockings, and white coat, strongly contrasting Qin Lan’s simple hospital gown beside her. "But Chen, you’ve been visiting the hospital quite often lately! Could you be harboring some ulterior motives for Officer Qin?"

Hong Rui teased Chen Xin and said "You guys talk" before leaving the room.

Watching Hong Rui leave, Chen Xin rubbed his nose and said, "She didn’t wear perfume today."

"Sit down, Ah Xin. You come here every day to bring me meals, I really am troubling you! Thank you!" Seeing Chen Xin carrying a thermal container, Qin Lan knew he had brought her food again, which made her grateful yet felt she was troubling him a bit.

"It’s just a meal delivery, no trouble at all. You took care of me when I was hospitalized before, didn’t you?" Chen Xin smiled at Qin Lan, placed the thermal container on the bedside cabinet, and started taking out the meals inside. "By the way, where’s Xiao Ding?"

"I asked her to go fetch some clothes for me, I’ve been in the hospital for a few days, and it’s time to change." Qin Lan answered while looking at the food Chen Xin had taken out. "Ah Xin, you’ve been cooking so much for me every day, isn’t that too extravagant? I feel like I’ve gained weight these past few days!"

Hearing Qin Lan say this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but laugh, "Why would that be? You’re a patient now, need to eat more to recover faster."

Saying this, Chen Xin put the food in front of Qin Lan and began feeding her.

Though Qin Lan had only injured one hand and could technically feed herself, to avoid aggravating the wound, Chen Xin insisted on feeding her.

While feeding Qin Lan, Chen Xin also mentioned Li Tou’s investigation at his house earlier.

This made Qin Lan feel very strange: "You say Li Tou went to your house twice? That’s a bit odd. One investigation should be completely sufficient, the consecutive investigations are strange!"

"Strange? Why would you say that, Ah Lan?" Chen Xin was curious upon hearing Qin Lan say this.

"Generally speaking, a large-scale investigation usually requires only one round because it’s heavy in workload, and currently, with little personnel movement between shelters, a single investigation suffices to cover a certain area. There’s no need for such intensive consecutive investigations."

Qin Lan became increasingly puzzled, wishing she could immediately discharge and find out what was going on.

However, she was injured and given leave by Uncle Ding, so despite her doubts, she couldn’t participate in the investigation, left to ponder it alone.

Listening to Qin Lan’s puzzled words, Chen Xin didn’t find it odd as she did, casually saying, "Old Li said the first investigation was arranged by the municipal government, and the latter by a joint investigation team. Different orders might explain the two investigations, right?"

"Two investigations within a short period is still highly abnormal unless..." Qin Lan stopped mid-sentence, suddenly thinking of something. Her gaze sharpened on Chen Xin, sitting up excitedly, almost knocking over the spoon in his hand.

Seeing Qin Lan’s agitation, Chen Xin quickly put down the spoon and gently laid her back on the bed, "Lie down, lie down! Your wound hasn’t healed yet, why are you so worked up!"

"Ah Xin, you don’t understand! If a short-term double investigation occurs, it might mean the first investigation identified the suspect’s vicinity, with the second aiming to pinpoint the suspect by narrowing down the area!" Qin Lan excitedly looked at Chen Xin, speaking quickly: "So, unless there’s another reason, it likely means the first investigation found clues, identified the suspect’s area, and your house is within that zone, hence the consecutive investigations!"

Hearing this, Chen Xin raised an eyebrow but shook his head, saying, "Whether they found the suspect doesn’t involve you. Your job now is to recover properly; even if something is happening, wait until you’ve healed to address it. In your current condition, can you even fire a gun?"

Chen Xin’s words left Qin Lan with no argument; she was now in a state with one arm hanging, barely able to wield a handgun, let alone sniper rifles, and even after the wound heals, returning to normal also requires time.

But Qin Lan stubbornly added, "But in that case, there would be danger at your place, Ah Xin! What if those terrorists target you? Your place is an excellent hideout!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 182 - 180 Safety Issues
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Hiding at my house? How could they be so brazen?" Qin Lan’s words made Chen Xin chuckle: "Apart from mentioning that your special police team comes to exchange supplies once a month, I and Longniang, plus the survival group friends like Little Long, have regular contact every day. If I don’t report for a day or two, nothing might happen, but if I go silent for three to five days, others might not react, but Longniang will definitely sense something amiss and report it."

"But the problem is, even if Qingyan or someone else notices something is wrong and comes to check, by that time, something bad might have already happened to you! Those people are terrorists, and it’s the apocalypse now; if they want to seize your place, how could they be polite about it?" Qin Lan was well aware that Chen Xin was stating the truth, but she was still deeply worried about him.

Because all these actions are just remedial measures after something goes wrong, if something actually happens to Chen Xin, it’s highly likely that those terrorists or other criminals seizing his shelter will kill him at the first opportunity, leaving no chance for others to rescue him.

Though it may not be appropriate to say so, in such situations, the survival rate of males is significantly lower than females.

In most cases, males are perceived as more threatening and would be killed sooner, while females are not, because many subconsciously view women as much weaker, thus sparing the less threatening females for enslavement or lust, rather than killing them right away, has become a subconscious choice for many people.

As for enslaving males, if it were ordinary criminals, there might be a possibility, but with the fearless terrorist types they previously encountered, Qin Lan didn’t think they would be so lenient.

Another possibility... Qin Lan glanced at Chen Xin’s face and immediately dismissed the thought from her mind that affected her appetite.

Chen Xin didn’t know what Qin Lan had just thought about, but regarding what she’d said, he thought for a while and then replied: "My safe house is still secure. Without heavy weapons or explosives, getting into my safe house is practically impossible."

"Besides, I do have the ability to resist. At the very least, I have the capability to hold them off for a while and send a distress signal to call for help."

Upon hearing Chen Xin say this, Qin Lan had to admit he was right, but she still said worriedly: "Axin, your safe house is indeed secure, but you go out every day. What if someone targets you and attacks you when you’re outside? Why don’t you stop bringing me meals for now and just stay at your safe house instead of going anywhere!"

In Qin Lan’s view, while Chen Xin wasn’t completely defenseless, when facing real criminals or more dangerous terrorists, his resistance abilities seemed negligible.

But Chen Xin thought otherwise, mainly because of the upgraded, self-aiming smart gun he had, along with its matching tactical goggles.

Imagine in this dark environment where everyone else has poor visibility, but Chen Xin, equipped with infrared-vision tactical goggles, perceives the battlefield almost transparently on his terms.

Combined with the smart gun that can automatically lock on and aim at the head, as soon as Chen Xin sees an enemy, he can take them down with a single shot.

Under these circumstances, Chen Xin didn’t feel particularly threatened by small groups of criminals or enemies, especially since gun control in Flame Country was incredibly strict. Even in the apocalypse, acquiring guns wasn’t easy.

So Chen Xin didn’t have much concern for his personal safety.

Nevertheless, understanding Qin Lan’s concern, Chen Xin responded, "Aran, you make a good point. When you’re discharged, I won’t bring you meals anymore."

Upon hearing Chen Xin say this, Qin Lan wanted to persuade him further, but after hesitating, she didn’t continue, given her wound was healing quite fast, she would be discharged shortly. Furthermore, Chen Xin wasn’t one to easily heed persuasion; his willingness to concede was already commendable.

Despite this, Qin Lan still cautioned Chen Xin, "Then Axin, be extra careful when you go out these days. Those terrorists we encountered before are extremely dangerous. Make sure you carry the gun with you, and if there’s any danger, don’t hesitate to shoot!"

As a police officer, she shouldn’t have advised Chen Xin this way, but out of concern for his safety and considering the previous incident, Qin Lan thought Chen Xin might be apprehensive about firing, so she specially reminded him.

More than anything, Qin Lan hoped Chen Xin would remain safe and sound.

Naturally, Chen Xin agreed compliantly, acknowledging that above all else, preserving his own life was most important.

Especially in these apocalyptic times, one must survive to deal with everything else; death would leave nothing.

After feeding Qin Lan and chatting with her for a while, Chen Xin stayed until Ding Ning arrived at the hospital with Qin Lan’s change of clothes before he prepared to leave.

Seeing Chen Xin about to leave, Ding Ning followed him out of the room under the pretense of sending him off and stopped him in the corridor to ask, "Axin, is there room for two more where you are?"

"At my place? If we squeeze a bit, there’s space. Why?" Chen Xin asked, thinking for a moment before nodding.

"When Lan-jie is discharged, I’ll take her to stay at your place for a while." Ding Ning’s expression was somewhat serious, and she spoke with a sincere tone.

Upon hearing this, Chen Xin found it a bit odd. He recalled the string of events happening recently, and couldn’t help but feel wary as he asked, "Are you both moving in? Is something wrong?"

Ding Ning gave Chen Xin a look that said, "You guessed it," but she didn’t explain. Instead, she deliberately raised her voice and said, "What could be wrong! You mean to say that Lan-jie and I moving in isn’t welcome? Hurry back and tidy up your place! Don’t let us come and see your place in chaos!"

With that, Ding Ning pushed Chen Xin outwards, seemingly slipping something into his pocket during the process.

Catching on to Ding Ning’s hint, Chen Xin played dumb and let himself be pushed out of the hospital by her.

After leaving the hospital and getting into his car, Chen Xin drove home, and just before exiting the city, he fished out the item Ding Ning had slipped into his pocket. It turned out to be a small slip of paper.

On it, written in tiny characters, were the words "Coordinate action, protect Qin and Ding, burn after reading."

Seeing these words, Chen Xin immediately understood. He lit the cigarette lighter in the car to burn the paper, and once it was charred beyond recognition, he tossed the remnants out the car window, watching them disappear without a trace.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 183 - 181 Upgrading the Bedroom
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The content on the note inevitably made Chen Xin feel a bit nervous and wary. He understood that this was clearly a big move by the police, and he had inadvertently been drawn into the situation.

The previous questions about why Qin Lan and Ding Ning were given a month’s leave and why his shelter was inspected twice were now explained.

Chen Xin didn’t mind cooperating with the police, and having Qin Lan and Ding Ning come to protect him was indeed a good thing.

However, having two girls move in to live with him, even for the sake of protection, still posed a certain level of trouble for Chen Xin.

After all, they were two girls; figuring out where they would sleep was an issue.

If it were only Qin Lan, that would be easier to address, since they had lived together before, making it less of an issue.

But Ding Ning and Chen Xin were just ordinary friends, not particularly close, which obviously caused Chen Xin some distress, requiring him to consider where to arrange accommodations for them both.

At the very least, Ding Ning needed to be given a separate room.

While pondering this issue, Chen Xin returned to his shelter.

Now that he knew from Ding Ning that he might encounter danger, he naturally had to pay close attention to the safety of his shelter.

After locking the main door, Chen Xin planned to enhance some defenses, such as upgrading the main door to improve its protective capabilities while also adding some defensive weapons.

However, this could be postponed for a while, as Chen Xin needed to first solve the accommodation issue for when Ding Ning and Qin Lan arrived.

Actually, this problem wasn’t too difficult to solve. Aside from the bedroom and living room, there was also a medical room, where they could sleep on the multifunctional surgical chair if they didn’t mind. After all, when unfolded, it served as a temporary operating table, about the size of a train sleeper berth.

However, that was indeed too simplistic. If Chen Xin had no other options, he might consider doing so, but with other potential solutions in mind, it wasn’t his plan.

Sitting down at the workbench, he opened the system interface and browsed the various materials in the warehouse, considering making a bunk bed.

Creating a bunk bed wasn’t all that complicated, the materials required were simple, and if it were like the kind in Chen Xin’s university dormitory, it could be made with just a few steel pipes.

Chen Xin looked at the materials in his warehouse; there was still a batch of steel pipes, a few pieces of angle iron, channel steel, and I-beams remaining. Even without a system, provided he knew welding, he could use these materials to create a bunk bed himself.

So, without hesitation, Chen Xin dragged two steel pipes from the storage room, cut them into four pillars, then used four pieces of angle iron to create two rectangular frames, attaching the four pillars to the top corners of the frames to complete the bunk bed frame.

He welded a few horizontal beams inside the rectangular frame and laid down a plank, making it suitable for sleeping. The bunk beds in Chen Xin’s dorm during university were just like this, and its quality might not even have been as solid as the one Chen Xin made himself. He remembered that during their batch, there was a classmate whose weight broke the bed.

However, even though he made the bunk bed, Chen Xin hesitated to let Qin Lan and Ding Ning sleep on it due to its simple and lackluster design, even missing the bed plank.

Therefore, after constructing the steel frame, Chen Xin found a composite board and a sheet of iron from the warehouse to enhance the bed, investing 20 survival points into upgrading it.

Once upgraded, the bunk bed finally looked a bit more presentable, with considerably thickened pillars, added planks on top, and a ladder on the side for ascent and descent, giving it a more believable appearance.

With the bed now ready, the remaining issues were easier to handle.

Leaving the workbench, Chen Xin went to the bedroom and opened the system interface once more.

"Container-Style Modular House - Bedroom Module"

"Upgrade Option One: Upgrade shell material, improve insulation, enhance temperature regulation and air circulation, life support level +1, requires 3000 survival points."

"Upgrade Option Two: Upgrade interior furnishings material, add smart home features, enhance living comfort level +1, overall tech level +1, requires 4500 survival points."

These were the options displayed by the system when Chen Xin previously checked the bedroom upgrade options. However, after he figured out how to use the system, he became able to control the upgrade directions.

Thus, when he opened the bedroom upgrade interface again, the displayed options were:

"Container-Style Modular House - Bedroom Module"

"Upgrade Option One: Expand living area, capacity +3 persons, upgrade shell material, improve insulation, enhance temperature regulation and air circulation, life support level +1, requires 5000 survival points."

"Upgrade Option Two: Increase living space +1 (each space accommodates 2 people), improve interior furnishings material, add smart home features, enhance living comfort level +1, overall tech level +1, requires 6500 survival points."

The prices increased, but the upgrade options clearly included more features.

Seeing the system upgrade options change according to his will made Chen Xin nod in satisfaction, and regarding the upgrade costs, the required materials for both upgrade options were available in his storage, overlapping to a significant extent with the materials he had prepared for the overall shelter upgrade, so Chen Xin could readily provide the necessary materials for both options.

But in doing so, he would deplete about one-fifth of his stockpiled materials, which would undoubtedly impact his goal to upgrade the entire shelter.

However, given that he hadn’t yet assembled all the materials needed for the comprehensive shelter upgrade, temporarily diverting some for the urgently needed bedroom upgrade seemed feasible. After all, he was already planning to acquire another batch of materials, so reallocating some now only meant acquiring a bit more later.

After considering this, Chen Xin chose only to implement Option Two.

He invested the materials, utilized survival points, and confirmed the upgrade. Just like refreshing a computer display with a different theme, it was as simple as a flicker of green light; a mere refresh transformed everything.

The bunk bed Chen Xin had just made disappeared from beside the workbench, while a new door appeared beside the original bedroom door in the living room.

Upon opening the new door, he saw a bedroom with a high-tech and somewhat futuristic feel. Its minimalist style, white color scheme, and warm lighting made it look nothing like an apocalyptic shelter but rather reminiscent of a future dwelling in a sci-fi movie.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 184 - 182: Choice of Defensive Weapons (Bonus Update 4/16)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

This bedroom is an expansion from the upgraded room, and it is just as big as Chen Xin’s original bedroom. A white bunk bed leans against the wall, with a clean white sheet and a brown blanket on it, looking very tidy, making one reluctant to mess it up.

This is an upgraded version of the bunk bed Chen Xin made earlier; the material is noticeably no longer steel pipes but something with a hint of metallic texture when touched, and the design also resembles something out of a sci-fi work.

Beyond the bed, there is a desk and a small round table in the room, with the desk and bed at one end of the room, each against a wall corner, while the small round table is at the other end of the room, next to which is an oxygen-producing box for planting greenery.

This is a device that appeared after adding a spare oxygen generator from the warehouse as upgrading material, allowing for planting greenery in the box to provide more oxygen to the bedroom.

However, the oxygen-producing box is currently empty, clearly requiring additional seeds.

Looking at this empty planting box, Chen Xin plans to move some of the herbs he promised Hong Rui to plant over here, increasing oxygen while utilizing the herbs’ natural fragrance to enhance the calming effect.

Besides these, there is also a white radiator beneath the wall where the desk is, connected to a ground-source heat pump, providing sufficient warmth to the bedroom, even though its thermal insulation performance is already sufficient to ensure it is very comfortable.

The light in the room comes from two light strips on the ceiling, with soft warm lighting adding a touch of warmth to the room, making the bedroom more comfortable.

As for the two wardrobes at the foot of the bed and another wall corner, they seem quite ordinary; they’re just regular wardrobes.

However, the wall material caught Chen Xin’s attention; he reached out and felt it, resembling plast-steel, with a matte texture not overly reflective and feeling quite comfortable to the touch.

After inspecting the room and testing the intelligent home and temperature control features mentioned in the system upgrade plan, Chen Xin understood how the technology level +1 and life support level +1 were reflected.

Near the door position in the room, there is a device resembling a touch screen on the wall, which opens to set up the room, and it intelligently adjusts the room’s light, temperature, ventilation according to the settings, monitoring indoor environmental data such as air humidity and comfort level, even being able to play music.

There are several chairs in the room, one being a chair with a backrest in front of the desk, and the other three being low sofas placed next to the small round table, all feeling comfortable upon sitting.

Experiencing the comfort of the room after upgrade, Chen Xin felt immensely satisfied, sincerely thinking that the survival points and resources spent were not wasted, significantly improving his quality of life.

However, after inspecting this bedroom designed as a guest room, Chen Xin needed to check his own bedroom.

After all, Chen Xin has placed many things in his bedroom, and the technology level +1 entry makes him quite eager; he is anxious to see if the things in the bedroom are also able to be covered by this attribute.

However, upon returning to his room, he couldn’t help showing a disappointed expression; the weapon cabinet and laptop, the two items he anticipated most, did not upgrade.

The entire room is like a replica of the next room, except that there is an extra weapon cabinet and workbench beside the desk.

All of Chen Xin’s personal items remain in their original arrangements, even the wine under the bed is put into the upgraded under-bed drawer.

Looking at all this, Chen Xin felt somewhat disappointed but not dissatisfied, as this was his hoped-for upgrade, but these items strictly speaking do not fall within the scope of the bedroom.

No longer dwelling on this, Chen Xin sat at the weapons workbench, deciding to craft several defensive weapons to add to the upgraded door, increasing the door’s defensive capability.

Crossbows were ruled out from the selection of defensive weapons by Chen Xin.

Not that crossbows aren’t useful; the heavy crossbow installed in the corridor is extremely powerful, if someone stands at the door and gets shot, its power wouldn’t be less than that of a large caliber anti-materiel sniper rifle shot at close range.

The key issue is that the materials required for the crossbow arms and strings are very high, and their lifespan will be severely affected by low-temperature cold environments.

Although the weapon cabinet’s attributes include the ability to maintain and preserve weapons, until now, Chen Xin has not used this function, uncertain whether damage to crossbows in cold environments can be repaired using this feature.

Hence, Chen Xin planned to make guns.

Though the system blueprint on the workbench only contained one pistol blueprint, in terms of structural principles, guns are similar in nature, so it’s not impossible to modify a pistol to create more powerful firearms.

Moreover, as a last resort, crafting a pistol and then upgrading to a submachine gun isn’t out of the question.

Besides, Chen Xin’s computer contains various documents, including firearm blueprints, from the oldest flintlock guns to Mauser rifles and Lee-Enfield rifles that saw action in World War I and World War II. Though these weapons were invented decades or even a century ago, no one said old weapons can’t kill; swords and knives have been used by humans for thousands of years, and even today they remain lethal.

Originally, Chen Xin collected these blueprints just to accumulate writing material, also as a personal interest, but undeniable, these blueprints have become a valuable asset to him now, also offering a great help.

Chen Xin did not plan to make rifles, and considering there is a system blueprint for pistols, he is too lazy to search further; what he wants to make is a machine gun.

After all, the old Maxim machine gun, although it was the world’s first truly successful powder automatic weapon, killed sixty thousand people in a day during the Battle of the Somme in World War I. Even up until the 1970s, Flame Country was using this old type of firearm, though only equipped for militia units.

If a Maxim machine gun capable of covering the entire doorway is mounted in the observation room above the shelter door, Chen Xin believes that even if a force were to attack his shelter, without heavy weapons, they wouldn’t be able to break in.

As for the Maxim machine gun blueprint, this weapon blueprint that’s been used for over a hundred years is quite easy to find, and its derivative models’ blueprints are numerous. Back when there was an internet, a quick search would bring them up; Chen Xin’s computer contains several different models’ blueprints.

Now all he lacks is to make it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 185 - 183: Twin Maxim
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Manufacturing a Maxim machine gun is not difficult; models of this antique can even be found on Taobao. Its unique knee-joint recoil mechanism is not structurally complex.

With reference to the blueprints, Chen Xin retrieved materials from the storeroom with the help of the system.

Although manufacturing a machine gun requires high-performance steel, for the system, ordinary steel is sufficient. The rest can be confidently entrusted to the system by Chen Xin.

Using the workbench’s processing capabilities to manufacture Maxim’s parts and assembling those parts bit by bit, a Maxim quickly appeared on Chen Xin’s workbench.

Even though the entire assembly is not completed and Chen Xin has only finished the barrel and bolt parts, lacking the crucial support bracket, the unpainted gun body retains its original metal color, appearing stark white. Yet it does not disguise the awe-inspiring power emanating from this fearsome killing machine.

The Maxim heavy machine gun was the world’s first truly successful automatic weapon powered by gunpowder gas, becoming the most efficient killing machine in human history upon its application.

During the war between the Japanese and Lucia, the army god Nogi Maresuke, extolled by the Japanese, led sixty thousand men to attack Lucia’s positions, employing a very mindless brutal charge tactic, resulting in five thousand deaths in just three hours.

Though eventually defeating Lucia, by the time the attack concluded, over thirty thousand of Nogi Maresuke’s sixty thousand soldiers were casualties.

And during the First World War, the Maxim’s terrifying efficiency earned it the nickname "widow maker."

In the Battle of the Somme, a deadly firepower web comprised of hundreds of Maxims resulted in over sixty thousand casualties in one day, nearly exterminating an entire generation in Europe during World War I.

This fearsome killing machine uses recoil generated by bullet shots for automatic firing, far more efficient than the Namo Gatling Bodhisattva, invented by Federation doctors to reduce soldier casualties, and simpler and more convenient to use and maintain. Thus, it quickly replaced the Gatling upon introduction.

However, the Maxim’s high rate of fire, at six hundred rounds per minute, generates substantial heat during shooting; the barrel turns red from firing in just a few minutes.

Hence, the Maxim’s barrel features a large circular sleeve, into which cooling water is injected during use to cool the barrel. As long as there’s water in the sleeve, the barrel temperature won’t exceed one hundred degrees Celsius, allowing continued firing.

Some might believe this weapon has a huge advantage in cold weather, because if the environment is cold enough, the cooling effect is surely excellent.

But actually, there are many inconveniences when using the Maxim in cold regions.

Since the Maxim uses water-cooled barrels, water instantly freezes in cold regions, requiring ice or snow to be melted into water before adding to the barrel, often causing the cooling water to freeze, hindering use.

To use in cold regions, antifreeze must be added to the cooling water, and at the time, alcohol was the widely used antifreeze.

This led to stories of Lucia soldiers drinking antifreeze, because the cooling water with added alcohol was a delicious beverage for Lucia soldiers, referred to as "grey beasts" at the time. Taking sips casually, who cares if the gun fires?

Later, to solve this problem, the Sovia learned from the Finnish and improved the fill port on the gun’s cylindrical sleeve, transforming it into a large flip cover for directly loading ice and snow, effectively solving the problem.

Of course, for Chen Xin, he wouldn’t use such cumbersome methods to cool the barrel, as he is just one person; even with Qin Lan and Ding Ning, they’re only three. Ideally, he would design it fully automatic to control remotely from inside the shelter.

As for the cooling issue, while it might have been a difficult problem for people in the Maxim’s era, for modern military engineering, using recyclable coolant in a circular system could address this problem.

Besides, the military has long replaced water-cooled machine guns with air-cooled ones, so there’s no need to struggle with water cooling.

For Chen Xin, he doesn’t need to worry much about coolant issues; simply maintaining a low firing rate and limiting prolonged use, the modern manufactured Maxim doesn’t require substantial cooling and heat dissipation, as manufacturing techniques and materials are completely different now.

Once the last part was fitted onto the gun body, and gun oil lubrication was added between the gun components, a Maxim without a support frame was completed.

Looking at this iron contraption resembling a cylindrical drum, Chen Xin reached out and tapped it, attempting to lift it with both hands.

Though somewhat heavy, it’s within Chen Xin’s ability to lift; however, it remains a weapon needing a support frame for stabilization—it’s impossible to shoot it like a light machine gun featured in films.

Thus, if Chen Xin wants to use this machine gun, he still needs to craft a support frame for it.

But when Chen Xin was preparing to make the stand, he suddenly remembered a picture he had seen before, and after thinking for a moment, he decided to make another one.

Since making the Maxim only required steel, soon the second Maxim also appeared on the workbench.

Looking at the two identical Maxim heavy machine guns laid on his workbench, Chen Xin nodded in satisfaction and began to make the support frame.

Compared to the Maxim machine gun itself, its stand is actually more complex in structure, because aside from serving as support, the Maxim’s stand has many different functions.

Some have added shields to stop stray bullets and shrapnel splashes; some have installed seats, allowing one to sit on the stand to shoot; some directly integrated spare barrels into the stand, which can be detached and replaced when necessary...

What Chen Xin made, however, was a parallel stand that could mount two Maxims together, transforming an original single Maxim into a double-linked setup.

Because he had the original stand structure as a reference, making the double-linked stand wasn’t difficult. Chen Xin only spent a little time to make a stand that could mount two Maxims.

Although the structure was somewhat unreasonable and a bit rudimentary, almost unable to adjust shooting angles and trajectories, it was still a complete stand.

Mounting the two Maxims onto it changed their name on the system interface from Maxim Water-cooled Machine Gun to Double-linked Maxim Water-cooled Machine Gun, and only then did Chen Xin press upgrade.

The familiar green light, consuming a hundred survival points and some steel materials, made the originally rough and rudimentary double-linked Maxim instantly different as the green light swept over it.

The originally stark white metallic outer color turned black, parts of the structure were replaced by engineering plastics, the thick water-cooling sleeve diameter was narrowed, but the length was stretched, where the longer sleeve and longer barrel improved heat dissipation while also enhancing the power of bullets fired from the chamber.

The rudimentary support frame was also upgraded from originally simple three supporting legs to a complete supporting structure. The active connection structure that Chen Xin originally hadn’t made was also added. Upon removing the fixation, the gun body could rotate within a certain range, achieving multi-angle shooting.

The trigger section was also modified, changing from two triggers for two guns to one trigger for both guns, merging the structure while reducing operational complexity.

As for the loading port, it was adjusted from originally facing the same side to one on each side, and according to Chen Xin’s design, a large-capacity magazine was installed at the loading port.

This large-capacity magazine still used a belt feed, or to simply put, the magazine was just a bullet container, but due to system upgrades, an ammunition feed mechanism was added onto the magazine, which, once mounted onto the machine gun, could directly feed bullets into the loading port.

Having completed the machine gun production, as long as this double-linked Maxim is mounted at the shelter’s entrance, Chen Xin believed that without heavy vehicles or heavy weapons, no one could break in forcefully.

However, a single machine gun had shooting angle limitations. After considering for a while, Chen Xin decided it would be more appropriate to mount two.

Thus, he made another identical double-linked Maxim machine gun, and then prepared to install them onto the entrance of his shelter.

The two machine guns were separately installed by Chen Xin on the left and right sides of the door. Since the terrain outside the shelter door presented an inverted L-shape, although snow accumulated around, the terrain was still flat. To enter Chen Xin’s shelter, one must stand in front of the door, which was within the coverage of the firepower of the two double-linked Maxim guns.

In this range, without sufficient protection, however many people would just become sieve-like under the gunfire.

Furthermore, Chen Xin mounted these two machine guns inside the wall, only opening a very small firing port, making it exceedingly difficult to destroy these two guns without heavy firepower. Even using human wave attacks would merely be a futile waste of Chen Xin’s ammunition.

And the large-capacity magazine held two hundred rounds, two double-linked guns, equating to eight hundred bullets.

Even in a sweep fire mode, a single machine gun firing two hundred rounds was enough for roughly a minute. If in a short burst fire, it might take several hundred human lives to exhaust these bullets.

Especially since, after installing the machine guns, Chen Xin harshly invested a thousand survival points and numerous materials to upgrade, reinforcing the door’s strength, changing manual push-pull opening to electric hydraulic lift opening, and also added automatic aiming functions for the two machine guns, along with infrared night vision monitor and motion sensors installed on the door.

The infrared night vision monitor and motion sensors were directly connected to the computer in Chen Xin’s bedroom, also linked with the two machine guns, allowing Chen Xin to operate them from inside his bedroom.

As soon as suspicious individuals appear and trigger the motion sensors, Chen Xin would be awakened by the alarm sound in his bedroom and could observe the situation outside the door through the computer screen.

If someone harbors malicious intent... the crossfire of four curving streams, even on the battlefield, would be a nightmare.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 186 - 184 Analysis of the Situation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The two twin-mounted Maxim machine guns have been installed, and after the upgrade, the gate looks thicker and sturdier.

Especially since the gate’s structure was changed from sliding to lifting, the entire gate is now neatly embedded in the wall. To open it without lowering the gate, one would have to blow up the entire wall.

The original gate, made of ordinary steel-plated reinforced concrete, was upgraded to solid heat-treated homogeneous nickel-chromium alloy steel plates with a thickness of 30 cm.

During World War II, Japan used this type of steel as the main armor for the Yamato-class battleships and stacked its thickness to an insane 650 mm.

At that time, the Yamato-class main gun armor was 650 mm thick, and no cannon could penetrate it in the immune zone.

In fact, the Federation found a piece of this kind of steel after the war. To test its performance, they used the 410mm main gun of a Missouri-class battleship to perform a zero-angle bombardment at a distance of 300 meters to pierce such a steel plate.

However, nearly a century-old technology has turned nickel alloy steel into a common material easily available at steel markets, and it is relatively cheap.

Chen Xin’s previously collected materials included a batch of nickel alloy steel columns, sold as raw materials for metal processing.

In Chen Xin’s original plan, these materials were used not only to reinforce the gate but also to strengthen the internal structure of the shelter.

Of course, Chen Xin’s original plan did not intend to make the gate into a 30 cm thick solid metal door. A hollow structure with a few centimeters of steel plate and reinforced concrete inside would have sufficed.

But after Ding Ning gave him that note, an inexplicable sense of crisis made Chen Xin choose to make the gate solid metal, even though this doubled the survival points spent on the upgrade.

Now, although Chen Xin’s gate isn’t as ridiculously thick as the Yamato-class battleship’s main gun armor, his enemies also don’t have battleship main guns.

As for using explosives, they couldn’t affect such thick armor. Otherwise, there would be no need for armor-piercing bullets in warfare to effectively penetrate battleship armor.

Looking at his gate, which could only be described as exceptionally strong, Chen Xin felt reassured but also somewhat puzzled. Now that the gate’s defense is enough, the wall seems less sturdy.

However, this issue doesn’t require much concern. After all, his shelter is underground, with more than two hundred meters of mountain above. The hardened soil layers won’t be much softer than metal once frozen, making excavation by human power impossible.

So, as long as Chen Xin doesn’t open the door, no one can enter his shelter.

The hydraulic lifting structure of the gate guarantees its reliability, but if there’s no power, opening it could be a headache.

Fortunately, the hydraulic mechanism of the gate’s lifting columns has an emergency opening device. In necessary situations, the liquid inside can be drained directly, allowing the lifting columns to drop instantaneously and open the gate.

However, this is a single-use emergency measure and should be used only in dire circumstances or when absolutely necessary, as the gate would be challenging to close after opening this way.

With such a solid and reliable gate equipped with Maxim machine guns for defense, Chen Xin felt entirely secure about his safety now.

Returning to the interior of the shelter and leaning back on the living room sofa, Chen Xin finally had enough space to think through the entire situation.

Initially, the beginning of the incident should be the attack on Shelter No. 2 in Baling County, which led to Qin Lan’s injury during that mission.

Because the terrorists who attacked the shelter used a suicide attack, causing more than twenty casualties, it drew significant attention from the city government, leading to the formation of an investigation team. The first sweep encountered by Chen Xin was at that time.

Subsequently, the provincial government, notified by the city, was equally shocked by this incident, prompting a special investigation team from the provincial capital, Star City, to join the city’s investigation team, forming a joint investigation team, followed by a second sweep.

According to Qin Lan, it is very likely that the city government obtained critical leads during the first sweep, determining that the terrorists responsible for the attack were within the region where Chen Xin’s home is located, hence including Chen Xin’s home in the second sweep.

But there is a discrepancy here because the incident involving Qin Lan’s injury and Ding Ning being sent to care for her, along with their one-month leave, occurred after the attack and before the city government formed the investigation team for the sweep.

"Did Uncle Ding and the SWAT team know beforehand that this matter was related to me?" Chen Xin rubbed his chin, feeling something was amiss here.

Logically, even if Uncle Ding wanted Ding Ning and Qin Lan to protect him, it should have been after the first sweep, confirming that the terrorists were within his area before making such a decision.

But Uncle Ding gave Qin Lan and Ding Ning a month off from the beginning, as if he knew in advance what would happen and deliberately found an excuse to send Ding Ning and Qin Lan away.

As for the note Ding Ning previously gave him, Chen Xin thought it quite normal, as this was already after the second sweep ended. If any action were to be arranged, there should be enough leads and intelligence.

It’s just that Uncle Ding always cared deeply for Ding Ning. Previously, to keep Ding Ning safe, he gave her leave before the disaster and sent her to Mo Qingyan’s place...

Thinking about the leave, Chen Xin suddenly felt inspired, connecting several things together.

Before the attack, Uncle Ding, as the SWAT team leader, likely received intelligence or leads from certain channels, but he wasn’t aware of the attack’s timing and location, or the extent of the casualties it would cause.

Therefore, at this time, Ding Ning and Qin Lan continued their normal work.

As the attack occurred, with Qin Lan injured and over twenty police officers from SWAT and armed police either wounded or killed, the situation worsened immediately.

Thus, Uncle Ding sent Ding Ning to take care of Qin Lan using her injury as a reason and gave them a month’s leave, likely intending to let them stay in the relatively safe hospital.

This explains why Ding Ning didn’t initially give him the note or mention moving in with Qin Lan temporarily.

But as the two sweeps unfolded, evidently something happened that Chen Xin wasn’t aware of, causing changes in the situation.

Ding Ning’s note was given to him during this phase, clearly indicating a shift in Uncle Ding’s original arrangement.

This might be a sign of some major action planned by the joint investigation team. Otherwise, Uncle Ding wouldn’t place his daughter in his place, which could be within the terrorist’s activity range - the shelter.

However, this isn’t certain. Chen Xin tapped his fingers on his knee, with clearer thoughts and new judgments after sorting out the situation.

What he just analyzed were the thoughts from the police and joint investigation team. But if he considered the perspective of those terrorists, Chen Xin felt they might not come to his shelter.

Because although terrorists are crazed, they’re not insane. If they can sacrifice dozens of lives to attack an official shelter and organize a suicide attack, they must have a target.

Regardless of their target, the suicide attack might only be the beginning, as for a terrorist organization, a dozen well-trained personnel daring to carry out a suicide attack are highly valuable and wouldn’t be recklessly wasted.

Therefore, the first attack they orchestrated might very well be to attract the attention of the police and city government.

Assuming their goal is achieved, with the city government and police’s focus drawn to Baling County, it also means their attention in the urban area has waned. In such a situation, if they were to launch another attack, where would they choose?

Chen Xin felt the answer was quite obvious.

Thinking of this, he worried about his friends in the city, especially Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin, who lacked solid defenses and weren’t particularly resilient. If something really happened, they wouldn’t have much capability to respond.

How about letting the two of them temporarily come to his place to avoid potential threats?

Chen Xin considered this idea but hesitated.

On the one hand, the Mo sisters might not be willing to come; on the other hand, living with five people, four of whom are women, would be very awkward for him.

However, considering possible dangers, Chen Xin thought he should discuss with Qin Lan and Ding Ning after they arrived before making a decision.

Just then, the living space...

Chen Xin scratched his head. After finally upgrading the bedroom, does he still have to sleep on the sofa?

Nonetheless, that’s a matter for later. For now, Chen Xin felt he should prepare for Ding Ning and Qin Lan’s arrival, as certain things need to be arranged for the two girls.

This inevitably made Chen Xin feel troublesome, but Ding Ning and Qin Lan’s presence could bring benefits, as they could help share many of the shelter tasks, even if temporarily.

At least several matters in the planting greenhouse would now have assistance, freeing Chen Xin from working from dawn till dusk.

Moreover, having other people in the shelter could bring much liveliness, someone to chat with, and gaming companions during leisure time.

Honestly, playing games alone is fun, but having companions is certainly more joyful.

Thinking about this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but look forward to Qin Lan and Ding Ning’s arrival.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 187 - 185: Origin
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Qin Lan and Ding Ning’s arrival was slightly later than Chen Xin had anticipated.

Nevertheless, they came in a car specifically sent for them, and arrived at Chen Xin’s doorstep.

Using the remote control to open the newly upgraded gate for the first time, as the heavy metal door slowly descended and the cold wind rushed in, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel an indescribable sensation inside.

He felt as if he were some kind of big boss or villain in a bunker, nothing like a protagonist’s entrance.

After all, in movies, when doors open like this, the person appearing behind them is rarely the protagonist or any positive character.

But this was just a random thought; as the gate fully lowered and seeing Qin Lan and Ding Ning step out of the car, Chen Xin took a few steps forward to greet them.

However, the snow at the entrance was rather deep, and the half-meter thick snow made it difficult for Ding Ning and Qin Lan to get out directly.

"This gate is really cool!" Ding Ning exclaimed, as she jumped down from the snow piled at the entrance, then turned to help Qin Lan down, and only then did she ask Chen Xin, "But last time I came, wasn’t it different?"

"I made some renovations. The previous sliding doors would be stuck by snow accumulation outside, so I switched to the current lift-style gate, which is much better." Chen Xin explained to Ding Ning, and also looked at Qin Lan on the side, "You’re here, Lan?"

Qin Lan nodded slightly to Chen Xin, and replied softly, "Yes, sorry for any inconvenience this might cause."

"Inconvenience? I’m thrilled you could come," Chen Xin replied with a smile, then glanced outside at the person who had brought them, though it was a young special police officer he didn’t know, he still greeted him, "Comrade, thank you for bringing them over, come in for a rest and have a drink."

But the officer shook his head and quickly said, "No, thank you. I have other matters to attend to and need to return to the city immediately."

After speaking, the officer handed over a black duffel bag and a long rectangular box, said goodbye to Ding Ning and Qin Lan, and turned to leave in the car.

Watching the officer leave, Chen Xin didn’t pay much heed, and proceeded to close the gate once more.

Once the gate was closed, he looked over at Ding Ning, asking, "Xiao Ding, what did your note mean? What’s going on here?"

"There’s a major operation that requires your cooperation, A-Xin. Sister Lan and I are here to protect you," Ding Ning quickly explained, glancing at Qin Lan, signaling her to continue the explanation, while she herself hurriedly opened the bag she brought to check its contents.

Chen Xin could only turn to Qin Lan, waiting for her explanation.

"A-Xin, do you remember the father and daughter who froze to death at your doorstep before?" However, Qin Lan didn’t explain directly, instead asking a seemingly unrelated question.

"The father and daughter who froze to death at my doorstep?" Chen Xin was a bit surprised, but still nodded, since such an incident was indeed hard to forget.

Seeing Chen Xin acknowledge that he remembered, Qin Lan continued, "That father and daughter escaped from a private shelter nearby. That place was originally built as a shelter for them and other relatives before the disaster, but it was occupied by the terrorists who attacked Shelter No. 2 in Baling County. They escaped to seek help, but unfortunately..."

Chen Xin understood what Qin Lan regretted, for the father and daughter had frozen to death at his doorstep.

However, Chen Xin still felt something was amiss, and asked Qin Lan, "How did the police find out about this?"

"We found a phone on the little girl, and upon turning it on, discovered a pre-recorded video," Qin Lan’s tone was low, whether due to just being discharged from the hospital or thinking of fallen comrades, but she continued, "The video was secretly recorded, explaining the origins of the terrorists occupying their shelter, and their planned attacks on the city’s major facilities."

At this point, Qin Lan paused noticeably, adjusting her emotions before continuing, "After the terrorists took over the shelter, they didn’t kill the survivors, only controlled them. But the little girl’s parents kept looking for ways to escape. With the mother’s sacrifice and other relatives’ cooperation, they managed to escape, believing this place, A-Xin, to be an official shelter, which is why they came knocking, only to... freeze to death at your doorstep."

"This... is really unfortunate." Chen Xin sighed; if only he had installed a doorbell at the time.

Perhaps finding this topic heavy, Qin Lan quickly moved past it, continuing to introduce the situation: "Afterwards, when the attacks occurred, while I was injured in the hospital, the municipal department had already launched an operation, sending people to that shelter, but... the terrorists had already left, and all the original people in the shelter had been killed."

Listening to Qin Lan’s account made Chen Xin’s scalp crawl; hearing about ruthless terrorists in games or news doesn’t have much reality, but when such things are happening in real life, close to oneself, it inevitably makes one feel terrified and horrified.

"Who are they really? Why would they do this?" Chen Xin calmed himself down and raised the critical question to Qin Lan.

However, Qin Lan did not answer. Instead, she looked at Ding Ning, who was still checking the equipment: "A-Ning, you explain, you know the situation better than I do."

This time, Qin Lan and Ding Ning brought a full set of special police combat gear, including helmets, bulletproof vests, and other equipment, and the long rectangular box contained Qin Lan’s sniper rifle.

These pieces of equipment were evidently disguised as Qin Lan and Ding Ning’s luggage, which Ding Ning had been counting and organizing.

Hearing her name called, Ding Ning stood up and explained to Chen Xin, "A-Xin, you know this city is located at the northern end of Xiang Province, bordering Gan Province and E Province, right?"

"Of course, I know. Also, in the past, this city was a transportation hub connecting the three provinces due to its location at their junction. However, because the land route was mountainous and the water route difficult, the junction area was practically a no-man’s land until developments after the establishment of the country." Chen Xin recalled information about the city he had researched for writing novels, allowing him to explain smoothly.

Although the city is a key water and land transport hub, connecting not only the three provinces but also north and south of the Yangtze River, the area is already hilly, rugged terrain, and the meandering Yangtze River creates nine twists and eighteen bends, added with hidden reefs and shoals, making it difficult to navigate in ancient times.

The river’s 240-kilometer route covers only a straight distance of 80 kilometers, twisting sixteen great bends, known as the "Nine Twists Intestine." Even historically, this stretch of the Yangtze River in the city’s jurisdiction is known as "The Mighty Yangtze, danger lies in Jingjiang."

After the country’s founding, a rectification project was intentionally undertaken to straighten the river and remove underwater reefs, finally making this stretch of the Yangtze River navigable.

As for the land, due to recent efforts from the Flame Country in promoting national infrastructure, numerous highways have been constructed, so Chen Xin had little impressions about the land routes.

But recalling driving past the junction of the three provinces, with its endless mountains, hills, and spans of bridges and tunnels, one can imagine how difficult travel must have been in the past.

"Since you know about these, A-Xin, it makes explanation easier," Ding Ning acknowledged and appreciated Chen Xin’s knowledge, before continuing, "Because this city used to be a no-man’s land, although not overrun with bandits like Xiangxi, the area wasn’t exactly peaceful either. Some historical issues have lingering effects even after all these years, so in more remote villages of Baling County, beliefs associated with the White Lotus Sect still persist."

"What the F**k!" Chen Xin was stunned by Ding Ning’s revelation, involuntarily blurting out the expletive in English.

However, both Ding Ning and Qin Lan understood why Chen Xin reacted so strongly, because the White Lotus Sect is incredibly ancient, famous enough to be mentioned in novels or folklore, making it an excellent villain trope among writers like Chen Xin.

Yet, unexpectedly, the White Lotus Sect became a real villain in reality, which was beyond Chen Xin’s imagination, especially since the sect still has activities even today, leaving one dumbfounded.

"We didn’t believe it at first, either, but according to collected clues, these people are indeed related to the White Lotus Sect, a surviving branch from the Tartar Dynasty era, long present in Baling County’s remote villages," Qin Lan said, giving Chen Xin a bitter smile. She hadn’t expected to hit the nail on the head; those terrorists truly were part of a cult.

Chen Xin pinched the bridge of his nose, calming his emotions before continuing, "But if they have existed since the late Qing Dynasty, wouldn’t the municipal government have known? Didn’t they eradicate them during movements like ’Destroy the Four Olds’ and fighting feudal superstitions in the past?"

"The municipal government certainly cracked down on them, but that only drove them underground. With beliefs rooted for a century or two, they are deeply entrenched," Ding Ning shook her head helplessly, expressing the same reaction she had when hearing about this from the political commissar, "Moreover, they generally abide by laws, and the White Lotus Sect worships Maitreya Buddha, making it difficult to eliminate them because of their subtlety. Thus, as long as they didn’t cause significant disturbances, the municipal government chose to overlook them."

"Yet no one expected the advent of the apocalypse to become an opportunity for them, leading to such a major incident," Chen Xin concluded, revealing a bitter smile.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 188 - 186: Two Women Settled Down
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"No matter how they got in, why are they causing trouble in this city? This is the heart of Flame Country, not somewhere like Southeast Asia. Aren’t they afraid of getting crushed by an iron fist?" Chen Xin asked, carrying Qin Lan’s things into the shelter.

This was Chen Xin’s biggest question. Flame Country wasn’t like other nations — not only were firearms strictly controlled, but social order was also rigidly enforced. Before the disaster, the streets were filled with surveillance cameras, and strict household registration systems could track everyone’s movements.

This changed only because the original social order collapsed after the disaster, and the harsh environment weakened the government’s control over the nation. Otherwise, these people would have been identified, crushed with force, and brought to justice long ago.

Therefore, Chen Xin was puzzled as to why a group of mercenaries who operated in Southeast Asia had come all the way to Xiang Province, deep in Flame Country.

"Because a few core members of these mercenaries are from this city," Ding Ning explained to Chen Xin.

Qin Lan, having been injured and in the hospital, didn’t know much about this matter, but Ding Ning, having participated in this mission, understood the situation in more detail.

"Based on current information, the leader of the mercenaries is from this city, and several core members are companions who ventured into the Southern Ocean with him back then," Ding Ning continued to explain what the police currently know to Chen Xin. "Before the disaster, they were active in Southeast Asia. While not exactly committing every atrocity, they had no qualms about killing and arson. They were primarily hired by local warlords to fight and train armies.

When the disaster struck, the entire Southeast Asia region’s order collapsed. A large number of refugees poured towards the borders, intending to seek refuge in our country. Some had crossed into our borders at that time, likely including these people.

As for why they came back to this city, it probably has to do with their origins. They might have relatives or friends here. However, we haven’t fully figured it out yet. It’s no longer like before; even extensive investigations can only cover official shelters or privately known shelters. If someone truly wants to hide, it’s hard for us to find them."

At this point, both Ding Ning’s and the atmosphere became a bit gloomy. If it were before the disaster, it would have been much easier for the police to locate these mercenaries.

Even without considering the surveillance systems spread across the streets, the grassroots-level community and village officials of Flame Country could be very familiar with their respective areas, down to every house. Mobilizing them would have been enough to identify these mercenaries.

Chen Xin understood this logic but said nothing more. He simply led Qin Lan and Ding Ning to the bedroom prepared for them, put down their luggage, and introduced, "This room is specially cleaned out for you two. The conditions are limited, so if there’s anything inconvenient, do let me know."

"Chen Xin, you’re too kind. The conditions here are already excellent. Even before the disaster, it would be difficult to find a room this comfortable," Qin Lan responded to Chen Xin’s polite words promptly, and with some apology, said, "We’re adding trouble for you by coming here."

She had been to Chen Xin’s place before and understood the situation somewhat.

The clean and tidy room they now saw wasn’t there last time, so obviously Chen Xin had prepared it specially for them.

Qin Lan couldn’t help but feel that she and Ding Ning coming over had added trouble for Chen Xin.

Chen Xin shook his head with a smile, saying, "It’s nothing. You are here to protect me, so preparing a room for you is only right. Place your things and rest a bit. Qin Lan, you’ve been here before. Make yourselves at home. I’ll be in the greenhouse. Call me if you need anything."

With that, Chen Xin turned and left the room.

After Chen Xin left, Qin Lan and Ding Ning started unpacking their bags. Although they had brought equipment, they also packed spare clothes and personal items.

"Sister Lan, you’re still injured. You should take the lower bunk. I’ll take the top," Ding Ning said straightforwardly, tossing her belongings onto the top bunk.

Seeing this, Qin Lan could only nod without saying much.

Though her shoulder wound had healed, it wasn’t completely recovered, and climbing up and down would be difficult for her.

Despite thinking it wouldn’t greatly affect her movement, Ding Ning insisted she avoid any exertion.

Once they had unpacked, Ding Ning sat with Qin Lan to discuss their mission.

"Sister Lan, how should we go about executing this mission to protect Chen Xin?" Though Ding Ning understood the mission well, she was previously just an ordinary police officer and was unsure how to begin this protection task.

Ding Ning didn’t know what to do, and neither did Qin Lan have experience in this area.

Though she was a SWAT officer, she had never carried out a protection mission and could only say based on experience, "First, get familiar with the surroundings... but there’s not much to check around here. As long as Chen Xin stays inside, I don’t think those terrorists can get in."

She had noticed the heavy, metal-like door at Chen Xin’s entrance earlier, feeling it’s so sturdy that even if the entire SWAT team was mobilized with all equipment, they might not be able to break it down.

It’s not something that could be handled with light weapons.

"The main thing is to understand Chen Xin’s basic situation and see if there are any defensive gaps," Qin Lan proposed. "As for if he goes out, we’ll accompany him, but it’s best to avoid leaving here as much as possible."

Listening to Qin Lan, Ding Ning could only nod in agreement. Truly, without breaching that heavy, sturdy door, there was no way into Chen Xin’s shelter.

Despite this, Ding Ning’s mind was more agile, and she brought up another issue, "What if those terrorists show up at our doorstep? How do we defend? We can’t just shut the door and hide in the shelter, calling for reinforcements? That’d be too pathetic."

"Wouldn’t it be nice if we could complete the mission simply?" Qin Lan smiled at Ding Ning but thought for a moment and added, "Chen Xin built an observation room above his door. You can enter via a hidden door. It’s like a small bunker, where you can set up a fire point. If attacked, it’s a means of counterattack."

Hearing this, Ding Ning was pleased, "No wonder you brought the sniper rifle, Sister Lan. With a bunker and you as a top sniper, they’d all be sitting ducks!"

Ding Ning’s remark didn’t cheer Qin Lan up. She just shook her head, touching her injured shoulder, where a slight pain reminded her she wasn’t fully recovered.

Her injury was on the left shoulder, and she was accustomed to shooting left-handed, with the sniper rifle butt resting on that shoulder.

The injury would affect her shooting. Though if necessary, she could endure it.

Observing Qin Lan’s actions, Ding Ning quickly asked with concern, "Does your shoulder hurt again, Sister Lan?"

"No, just bumped it," Qin Lan shook her head, telling Ding Ning, "Why don’t you go see if Chen Xin needs help? We’ll be here for a month, so let’s help him with whatever we can. We can’t just stay here doing nothing, right?"

"Okay, I’ll go find him. You get some rest, Sister Lan." With that, Ding Ning left the room.

Once Ding Ning was gone, Qin Lan stood up and placed a long case on the table.

She sat in a chair by the table and opened the case to reveal her beloved sniper rifle. She couldn’t help but gently stroke it.

The cold touch under her fingertips provided reassurance but also a trace of dissatisfaction.

This was her weapon, and last time she failed to use it to stop those terrorists from committing a suicide attack. She wouldn’t let it happen again!

With this determination, Qin Lan endured the stinging pain in her shoulder and began assembling the sniper rifle.

Though slower than before her injury, when she rested the rifle on her right shoulder and practiced aiming, Qin Lan found it manageable.

Shooting left-handed might be affected, so she resolved to switch sides to mitigate this. Since she was here to protect Chen Xin, she needed to quickly restore her combat capability and ensure his safety.

While Qin Lan was fiddling with the rifle, Ding Ning reached the greenhouse where Chen Xin was watering the dry rice seedlings.

Seeing Chen Xin at work, Ding Ning greeted him and asked, "Sister Lan sent me to help. Is there anything I can do?"

"Helping? There’s nothing in particular right now, just watering, which I can handle alone," Chen Xin shook his head but told Ding Ning, "However, there is something you could help me with."

"What is it? Just say it, I’ll handle it," Ding Ning promised eagerly amidst the greenhouse’s greenery, eager to act.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 189 - 187 Breakfast and Work
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The arrival of Qin Lan and Ding Ning brought some life to Chen Xin’s refuge.

After all, he had been the only person there. If he didn’t speak or make any noise, the entire refuge would become eerily quiet.

So even though the generator and ground source heat pump produced quite a bit of noise, Chen Xin never considered eliminating the noise or soundproofing because the sound reminded him he wasn’t living in a tomb and prevented him from feeling too claustrophobic from the silence.

However, the arrival of Qin Lan and Ding Ning brought other changes to Chen Xin’s life; at least now Chen Xin had to make breakfast for three people.

"Xin, is this what you eat for breakfast?" Ding Ning asked with a strange expression, watching Chen Xin prepare breakfast.

It wasn’t that Ding Ning thought Chen Xin’s breakfast was bad; rather, she thought it was too extravagant.

Fresh rice porridge could be attributed to Chen Xin growing his own rice, so it didn’t matter how it was eaten.

But the thinly sliced Iberian ham he had set aside was something Ding Ning found hard to accept. Even before the disaster, not many people would eat this for breakfast; it seemed excessively luxurious.

"You mean the Iberian ham? This was a gift from Xiao Long earlier. Things like ham degrade in quality if stored too long, and since I’ve already opened it, I can’t leave it for too long," Chen Xin explained, noticing that the porridge in the pot was nearly done. He turned off the stove and brought the prepared breakfast to the table.

After setting the table for breakfast, Ding Ning also brought over the bowls and chopsticks, and the three of them sat down to eat together.

"Smells great!" Though Ding Ning had mentioned the extravagance, when she tasted a slice of ham, she couldn’t help but exclaim in delight.

Qin Lan, however, seemed much quieter. No matter what, when she was hospitalized, Chen Xin would bring these items daily, so while she still found them delicious, she wasn’t as audibly expressive as Ding Ning.

But as they ate breakfast, Qin Lan asked Chen Xin, "Xin, is there anything you need us to help with? Although we’re here to protect you, we don’t want to just freeload, so feel free to ask if there’s anything you need help with."

Ding Ning quickly nodded and chimed in, "Yes, yes, let us know if there’s anything we can do. Don’t be like yesterday, asking formally only to end up having me water the plants."

"There are some things, but the relatively tedious and heavy work I already finished the other day, like plowing, sowing, and transplanting rice seedlings. If you had come a few days earlier, there would have been an endless amount of work." Hearing their willingness to help, Chen Xin thought for a moment before explaining, "Things are relatively light these days. The rice in the field just needs watering, and the vegetables in the hydroponic trays mostly take care of themselves."

"But we can’t just do nothing, can we? It feels like we’re here like freeloaders," Qin Lan still wanted to help Chen Xin with whatever she could.

Seeing Qin Lan so insistent, Chen Xin thought for a moment and said, "In that case, there is indeed something you can help with, Lan."

"What is it?" Qin Lan asked expectantly.

Chen Xin smiled, tapped her bowl with his chopsticks, and said, "First, eat your breakfast. We’ll talk afterward."

——————————————

Breakfast was quickly finished, and after Ding Ning took the initiative to help clean up the dishes, Chen Xin led the two of them to the oxygen machine in the refuge.

There were five oxygen machines in Chen Xin’s refuge; apart from the one in the living room, the other four were placed next to the generator.

"This is the algae oxygen machine in the refuge, primarily used to provide oxygen here. Because it’s a biological oxygen machine, the algae leave a lot of sediment," Chen Xin explained, tapping the green sediment at the bottom of the tank, introducing it to Ding Ning and Qin Lan, "These sediments are pure algae, similar to those spirulina health foods popular before the disaster. What I need your help with is scooping out the sediment from the tanks and adding it as raw material to the biogas reaction tank."

After Chen Xin explained, Ding Ning and Qin Lan understood what he wanted them to do.

Chen Xin picked up a bucket, placed it next to a valve at the base of the oxygen machine, and opened the valve while explaining, "This valve is used for extracting these sediments. Just open the valve and the sediments will be sucked out, and you just need to catch them with the bucket."

As the water flow from the valve carried out the sediment from the base of the oxygen machine, the bucket quickly filled with a large amount of dark green liquid.

And next to it, the sediment that had been a deep color was significantly reduced, appearing much lighter in color.

Chen Xin closed the valve, poured the green liquid from the bucket into another large container, then placed the bucket back under the valve and opened the valve again.

"Since the valve is mainly for draining water, it can’t thoroughly remove all the sediment in one go. It usually takes about three rounds for one oxygen machine." While explaining his method, Chen Xin tapped the large container next to him, "There’s a lot of sediment in the drained water, but it’s mainly water, so you’ll need to filter it in this large bucket to fish out all the sediment."

Saying that Chen Xin picked up a filter net hanging nearby and dipped it into the bucket, quickly gathering a layer of green algae on the net.

"Of course, you can also place the filter net over the bucket, which saves some work, but be careful not to spill water everywhere," Chen Xin said as he scraped the algae from the net into another bucket while handing the filter net to Qin Lan, "Put the filtered algae into this bucket, and the biogas reaction tank is over there with a label. Just open the lid and pour these into it, but it’s best to wear a mask when adding the materials due to the strong odor."

"Sounds rather tedious," Ding Ning couldn’t help but remark.

"It is a bit, which is why I’m considering how to modify it to make it less tedious, but I don’t have any ideas yet," Chen Xin nodded, acknowledging how cumbersome the process was, though he wasn’t in a hurry to improve it.

Qin Lan placed the filter net over the bucket, quickly filtering out quite a lot of green algae.

Pouring it into another bucket nearby, she said, "Leave this to me. All I need to do is filter out the algae sediment, right?"

Chen Xin nodded, pointing to the remaining sediment at the base of the oxygen machine, "Just scoop out these sediments. Once the bucket is full, take it to the biogas reaction tank to add the material, and you can work together."

Qin Lan and Ding Ning both nodded in agreement. The work seemed complicated but wasn’t really difficult or exhausting, and working together, they could finish quickly.

"Once you’ve scooped out the sediment, just open the intake valve next to it to refill the oxygen machine with water. It doesn’t need to be filled too much; there’s a scale on the tank, so just fill to that level." Having explained, Chen Xin turned and headed towards the growing greenhouse, leaving Ding Ning and Qin Lan to start extracting the sediment from the oxygen machines.

————————————————

While Chen Xin, Ding Ning, and Qin Lan were occupied with their respective tasks, the joint investigation team in the city convened a meeting to plan their next steps.

"Based on the clues we have, the next target of these terrorists is a refuge in the city, but we can’t yet determine which specific refuge. Therefore, all refuges must be well-defended." A high-ranking official spoke in the meeting room, his appearance conveying authority, and those seated at the meeting table listened attentively to his speech.

After the official’s speech, a city bureau leader in police uniform addressed the room, "Our bureau is taking this operation seriously, and we’ve deployed elite forces to collaborate with paramilitary forces, setting up joint defenses and control at each refuge. Checkpoints and defensive installations have been established at every refuge entrance to ensure that everyone entering and leaving the refuges is checked and no dangerous items are brought inside."

"Our paramilitary forces will also coordinate with the bureau to ensure the security of every refuge." A military officer in a paramilitary uniform, seated next to the city bureau leader, also pledged their support.

The high-ranking official hosting the meeting nodded in satisfaction, though his face showed no expression. These were basic requirements, necessary to avoid job losses or worse consequences during such tense times.
