Though it’s been more than half a year since the apocalypse hit, she’s gradually gotten used to the current lifestyle. But she’s always been living in their family’s private refuge with Mo Qingyan, where the living conditions aren’t bad, even barely experiencing the hardships of post-apocalyptic life.

But now, due to the power outage, their refuge’s original electrical appliances were mostly unusable. They could only rely on the ground-source heat pump that Chen Xin installed for heating and lighting, significantly lowering her quality of life.

Undoubtedly, this made Mo Qingxin quite uncomfortable.

This is actually one of the effects of having a cozy private refuge because they made sufficient preparations before the apocalypse, so they could maintain a relatively high living standard post-apocalypse.

Under such conditions, people living in private refuges actually don’t feel the struggle for survival during the apocalypse.

Because they had ample preparations, ensuring food, water, and various supplies, it’s as if nothing changed compared to pre-apocalypse, as long as they don’t leave home.

Of course, if preparations weren’t thorough, like Mo Qingyan’s case without a generator and well, relying on external power and water supply, once cut off, it would be a tremendous blow to her private refuge.

Their quality of life would quickly decline, making them truly experience the hardships of post-apocalyptic survival.

However, because of the ground-source heat pump and aquaponics system Chen Xin delivered to Mo Qingyan, their survival conditions weren’t too bad.

This entire setup could provide various necessary survival conditions like heating, food, and lighting. And if they don’t mind, it could even provide drinking water, so maintaining survival for two people isn’t a problem at all.

It was just that they had been quite comfortable before, so adjusting to this situation was a bit difficult for Mo Qingxin, still hoping to live a little more comfortably.

"Better get used to it quickly, the days ahead...who can say for sure? It might get tougher and tougher." Mo Qingyan shook her head and poured the last bit of broth from the can into her mouth, "Although they say this is a temporary power limit, who knows what might happen in the future? If it happens again later, Xiaoxin, you’ll need to adapt to surviving in harsh conditions."

Hearing this, Mo Qingxin lowered her head as well. She knew she had to adapt to the current living conditions, or even worse survival conditions and environments, but she couldn’t adjust quickly.

After the meal, due to the lack of electricity, the computer and game console that the sisters used to play were temporarily out of use. So, they had no choice but to sit together under the additional lighting from the aquaponics system and chat.

"Sis, what do you think caused this power outage?" Mo Qingxin was still very curious about this sudden power outage and wanted to know the root cause of it.

Even if she didn’t know the exact situation, it wasn’t hard for Mo Qingyan to guess: "Power plant restrictions can only mean the generators are damaged or there’s a coal shortage, and these are controlled by the country. Pre-disaster, they must have had plans in place, so for the past six months, there hasn’t been any issue. Now there’s suddenly a problem, either someone is sabotaging, or someone is causing trouble somewhere."

"Huh? How insane would someone have to be to cause trouble at a time like this?" Mo Qingxin clearly hadn’t considered such a thing, finding it hard to comprehend why anyone would want to stir trouble and sabotage in an apocalyptic environment: "Even if they succeed, what good would it do? Not to mention being wiped out by the military, if the country blocks external supplies, everyone would starve!"

For issues that even her own sister could think of, Mo Qingyan just shrugged and casually said, "If they could think of those things, they wouldn’t cause trouble. Honestly, for those people, they don’t care about others’ lives; they’re only interested in achieving their unspeakable goals.

Besides, don’t expect too much from those people’s intelligence. Remember when you interned at the hospital, those people who came to you with self-diagnoses from online searches? Last year there were people in the Federation who believed the so-called national leader’s claim that injecting disinfectant could kill the virus. Do you think those people are smart?"

"But, but... this is Flame Country! Causing trouble and sabotaging here, do they think the military won’t act quickly enough?" Mo Qingxin still couldn’t understand, even if some people weren’t particularly bright or were blinded by self-interest, shouldn’t they at least see the stark difference in force?

Mo Qingyan couldn’t help but smile sarcastically at this question: "Before, when writing a novel, Shicai had Alan help him research how many people declared themselves emperors since the country’s founding, can you guess the number?"

"Are you serious? Sis, are you saying after founding the country?" Mo Qingxin looked deeply shocked, unable to imagine that there’d still be people attempting to restore the monarchy after the founding of Flame Country.

"Yes, after the country’s founding, according to Alan’s research, from the country’s founding to now, there have been all sorts of self-declared emperors, at least dozens, from the early days of the country’s founding to the 90s." Mo Qingyan said, noticing the disbelief on her sister’s face and laughing as she elaborated, "Some of them were just ignorant peasants wanting to play emperor, stirring up a few relatives and friends. Others actually rallied hundreds of people to cause trouble in county towns and even brought out the army to suppress them. There were some that lasted several years without being dealt with because of isolated transportation...

Counting them all, even those with detailed online records total more than a dozen. The most recent one was just last year, declaring the establishment of some federation in a foreign hotel, electing himself as the supreme leader... Once people have ambition, their minds don’t work the same."

Mo Qingyan told it like a joke, but Mo Qingxin felt her worldview was being overturned.

Even though over seventy years have passed since Flame Country’s establishment and the last emperor has been dead for over half a century, she couldn’t believe there were still people with thoughts of restoring the monarchy.

This made it hard to imagine what these people could be thinking.

But since individuals like that exist, it’s not surprising there are people looking to exploit the apocalypse for chaos and destruction.

And frankly speaking, with current transportation difficulties, blowing up railways effectively cuts off transportation, blocking external supplies while simultaneously isolating cities from external contact.

Excluding external influences, if a small place like a county wants to cause trouble or sabotage, their chances of success aren’t small.

Thinking of this, Mo Qingxin couldn’t help but agree with her sister’s judgment, believing that someone was indeed sabotaging.

The sisters didn’t continue discussing the topic, once again lapsing into silence.

Mo Qingyan stood up to clean the empty cans from their meal with some water, putting them aside. These metal cans are quite useful in the apocalypse, whether for storing things or crafting something, they’re good options.

However, Mo Qingyan didn’t have Chen Xin’s hands-on skills nor systematic assistance, so for her, these cans were just accumulated supplies, perhaps to be traded for something else.

While Mo Qingyan was thinking about what to do with the cans, Mo Qingxin suddenly asked her, "Sis, if mom and dad were still around, what would they say seeing us like this?"

"Dad would definitely be worried about whether we’re eating and living well..." With the mention of their parents, Mo Qingyan’s expression shifted subtly: "As for mom... she’d probably still complain that we’re both bad investments for not being married yet, right?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 199 - 197: Azu’s Ambition
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The power outage in the city had a significant impact, but the culprits behind it were not affected at all.

Although Azu had previously met with his subordinates and Secretary Li in the city, in reality, the stronghold where he and his men were based was outside the city.

The stronghold controlled by Azu was quite a distance from the city. Although it was in the same region as Chen Xin’s shelter, it was even farther from the city, just a small village-level shelter.

Shelters like these were not rare. Even though they were all built within three months before the disaster struck, considering the small population in villages and towns, the construction of such temporary shelters was not particularly massive. Therefore, at that time, the government of Flame Country built quite a few of these small shelters in various villages and towns.

These shelters were not large, nor were they built deeply. Most were simply large pits dug about ten meters deep, with small bunkers built inside that could accommodate a few hundred people, followed by refilling the soil to form a layer about twenty meters thick.

Although simply constructed, they were sufficient for three to five hundred people to take refuge for a short time.

After all, these shelters were only intended to withstand the flames, low temperatures, and other secondary disasters brought by the apocalypse, not to resist nuclear or conventional missile strikes. They did not need to reach the high defense levels of military fortifications.

However, such small shelters naturally had their disadvantages. Due to the limited space, they couldn’t have dedicated greenhouses or comprehensive living facilities like larger shelters. Additionally, because of the constrained area, the living conditions for survivors inside were far from comfortable.

This shelter was not one that Azu and his men had seized; it was the shelter of the village where their families and relatives originally resided.

Azu’s group did not just appear out of nowhere, nor were they mindless lunatics.

Azu and his closest brothers were locals, but in their youth, they were hotheaded and reckless, known in the local dialect as "zao (cao) zi," individuals who did not study well and hung around aimlessly.

Azu’s parents died when he was young. Although he was raised by his second uncle, who was already a civil servant in an agency under city administration and had little time for him, he ended up becoming a street thug.

However, Secretary Li, who at the time had a certain status and connections, arranged for Azu to join the army for Azu’s own good.

Even in the military, some of Azu’s bad habits persisted, and after two years of service, he returned to the city and remained idle without a proper job.

Unwilling to see Azu continue down this path, Secretary Li introduced Azu to a friend doing business in Yunnan Province, hoping Azu would learn some skills.

Thus, Azu followed Secretary Li’s friend to Yunnan Province.

As for how Azu became a mercenary and made a name for himself with a group of brothers in Southeast Asia, it was simply due to individual encounters and opportunities.

Originally, Azu and his brothers were doing quite well in Southeast Asia, helping local warlords with battles, training troops, and living a carefree life. They never even regarded the regular armies of several local countries seriously.

Though they couldn’t defeat them, the regular armies couldn’t do anything to them, either.

Living such a life, although it offered no future prospects, seemed acceptable to them, considering they were mercenaries who might die at any time and had little interest in the future.

But, due to a chance encounter, Azu came into contact with a Doomsday Sect.

The core doctrine of this sect wasn’t complicated, emphasizing freedom and happiness. At first glance, there was nothing wrong with it, but their interpretation of freedom was quite twisted.

According to the sect’s doctrine, true freedom couldn’t be bound by anything; all morals, laws, and rulers were just shackles oppressing freedom. Only with the advent of the apocalypse, breaking all these restraints, could true freedom be attained.

To any sane person, such talk was utter nonsense. Absolute freedom doesn’t exist in this world because living in this world entails being influenced and constrained by other factors, making complete freedom impossible.

Moreover, if you act on your whims, what about others?

For example, if you want to roll freely on the street, and someone else wants to freely speed down the street, without rules, someone is bound to run you over.

Thus, the existence of rules and constraints is essential. Unrestrained freedom would inevitably lead to destruction.

Yet to mercenaries, who are inherently lawless, this kind of talk resonated deeply with them.

As a result, Azu and his brothers began to believe in this Doomsday Sect, becoming increasingly reckless and disregarding even the regular armies in Southeast Asia.

Everything could have continued this way without incident, whether Azu and his men were eventually eliminated by the regular armies or decided to retire after earning enough money, or were lured by the Doomsday Sect into becoming cultists.

However, the arrival of a meteor disrupted everything. Under the threat of apocalyptic disaster, no country in Southeast Asia could control the situation, and everything descended into chaos.

With the collapse of order, it should have been the era of freedom proclaimed by the Doomsday Sect. However, the sect was merely a tool for the sect’s leadership to accumulate wealth, and under the true threat of the apocalypse, even the sect’s leaders fell into collapse.

Azu was obviously greatly disappointed by this situation and, after killing the sect’s collapsed leadership, found himself at a loss for what to do next.

Eventually, an old brother suggested returning home, giving Azu and his group a sense of purpose and direction. Using the money and connections they had accumulated over the years, they crossed the border into Flame Country through Yunnan Province and, after several twists and turns, returned to their hometown in Xiang Province.

Because Azu and his group had registered citizenship in Flame Country, and had not used their real identities in Southeast Asia, they presented as normal citizens returning from there, encountering no issues regarding their identities.

Additionally, having entered through official channels without carrying dangerous items, their entry into the country caused no trouble or waves.

Back in their hometown in Xiang Province, even these lawless mercenaries became much more disciplined before the apocalyptic disaster, creating no trouble or unrest and smoothly entering the small hometown shelter.

As previously mentioned, the living conditions in such small shelters were far from good. Initially, due to the threat of disaster, this group was able to endure, but as external conditions stabilized and living conditions worsened, certain thoughts began to grow in these mercenaries’ minds.

Using methods learned from the Doomsday Sect, Azu and his group started spreading the sect’s teachings within the small shelter. Through familial connections, they quickly turned the hundreds of people in the shelter into followers and spread their beliefs to several nearby village-level small shelters.

For these mercenaries, who had long operated in Southeast Asia, the chaotic world of doomsday was an ideal environment. Combined with the teachings of the Doomsday Sect, they easily gained a large number of loyal followers, with the elite being strictly trained and mentally controlled, becoming fearless elite "cannon fodder."

This is why, during the attack on Baling County’s Shelter No. 2 and City Hall Shelter, they were able to employ tactics like suicide attacks, for they had "death warriors" under their command.

However, cultivating these "death warriors" wasn’t easy, and if not for realizing his plans, Azu wouldn’t exhaust his subordinates like this.

Now that his basic objective was achieved, it was time to consider the next move.

"Old Four, how’s the situation with that shelter I’ve asked you to monitor?" Azu looked at one of his brothers and asked.

Old Four, responsible for intelligence and surveillance in their mercenary unit, replied to Azu’s inquiry about that shelter: "It’s pretty much the same as before, no significant changes. As I mentioned earlier, two female police officers live there, and they haven’t left much during this period.

Only a few days ago, when we made our move, a group came by. Later, they drove a modified vehicle that could run normally on snow. This technology is highly useful, and we should have it under our control."

After hearing Old Four’s report, Azu raised an eyebrow: "A vehicle that can run normally on snow? It seems that the people living in that shelter are quite remarkable! Our enterprise needs that person."

As mercenaries, even without understanding the technological intricacies of snow-driving, they knew what mobility meant in military operations.

"But that shelter isn’t easy to breach. I’ve seen its gates, and even with rocket launchers, it’s hard to get in. As long as they don’t open the door, we can’t touch them." Old Four certainly understood the significance of the shelter they were discussing, but the problem was it was indeed a tough bone to chew. Perhaps fearing Azu didn’t grasp it, he added, "We’ve been observing that shelter for months. They can produce a truckload of vegetables each month, proving they’re not worried about food and water. If we can’t breach the doors, they can outlast us."

"So, we must strike swiftly, seizing the opportunity when they open the door or go out, quickly attack, and control the people inside the shelter." Of course, Azu listened to Old Four’s analysis. "Even if they don’t know about the technical aspects of snow driving, they can’t possibly deal with my brothers and a group of trained death warriors."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 200 - 198 Vehicle Modification Completed
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Regarding Azus and others’ intention to seize his refuge, and even control him personally, Chen Xin was naturally unaware and did not even know that his refuge was being monitored.

But even if he knew, Chen Xin would probably not consider it worth his attention.

With the current thickness of 300mm nickel-chrome alloy armor steel door at the main entrance, even a modern army would need heavy firepower to blast the door open.

Such heavy firepower doesn’t refer to heavy machine guns or rocket launchers, and mortars are inadequate as well—it would require genuine heavy firepower, at least armor-piercing bullets fired from the main cannon of a main battle tank.

Don’t think it’s exaggerated; during WWII, the front armor thickness of the main turret on Bismarck battleships was 360mm, the Hood battlecruiser turret was 381mm, and the Missouri battleship turret was 495mm...

You could say Chen Xin’s door is equivalent to battleship-level defense during WWII, where battleship turrets had the thickest armor on the ship, and only other battleship main cannons could penetrate them.

Even with the advancement of modern military technology greatly improving cannon penetration, penetrating 300mm homogeneous nickel-chrome alloy armor steel is not achievable by ordinary caliber cannons.

Moreover, such large-caliber heavy artillery is only equipped by frontline main combat forces in major military powers, like the top five in Blue Star. Even second-line forces would likely lack such heavy firepower, let alone special police or armed police.

Therefore, a few terrorists don’t concern Chen Xin in the slightest.

Even if they could initiate suicide bomb assaults, tying explosives to fearless infantry and blowing up the door, the two twin Maxim at Chen Xin’s entrance are not for decoration.

Though the ammunition supply isn’t vast, with assistance of automatic targeting, taking down three to five hundred people isn’t a problem.

To put it bluntly, considering the current defense capability of Chen Xin’s refuge, even combined efforts of city district special police and armed police couldn’t succeed in storming it.

The best result they could achieve might be leaving a few bullet marks on Chen Xin’s refuge door with heavy machine guns.

With such defense, Chen Xin naturally wouldn’t worry about his safety issues, giving him time to devote to transforming his vehicle.

After Li Yulong delivered the car parts and other related materials, Chen Xin’s vehicle transformation plan smoothly proceeded.

This vehicle now no longer resembles the former SUV and could be said to be completely two different vehicles.

If not for the system-provided information-state concept obfuscation technology, no one would think this bus-sized vehicle was transformed from an SUV, as they have no resemblance at all.

The 12-meter-long body rests on a segmented track chassis, with a dual-row, triple-proof driver’s cabin atop a four-meter-long track, while the back living cabin has a six-meter-long track underneath.

Originally, Chen Xin planned for a single continuous track, but after upgrades, it transformed into such a segmented setup, which he accepted, as he wasn’t a professional and couldn’t distinguish whether a single track or segmented track performed better.

The vehicle’s overall skin uses 9mm thick steel plates with armored protective plates on key parts. The chassis can withstand attacks from landmines or grenades, and only large-caliber anti-material sniper rifles could penetrate the frontal driver’s cabin glass.

Snow camouflage coated on the surface serves as the best disguise in the dark snowy terrain, and the vehicle’s propulsion from triple 200KW output power isotope thermoelectric batteries makes it exceedingly difficult to detect.

The infrared night vision device in the driver’s cabin makes it possible to drive without headlights, effectively preventing the vehicle from being spotted.

Additionally, the car is equipped with a radar unit that Chen Xin couldn’t identify, capable of detecting all moving objects within a radius of 5 kilometers, along with four military drones, two of which come with machine guns as weapons.

A hidden 12.7mm caliber tri-barrel rotary machine gun tops the vehicle, equipped with high-explosive incendiary armor-piercing bullets, against which nothing but main battle tanks can withstand such firepower.

As for the interior, the living cabin follows a luxury RV setup, including a fully functional kitchenette with an induction cooker, microwave, sink, washing machine, dishwasher, and cabinets for storing ample kitchenware.

The vehicle’s separate wet and dry restroom is like typical home restrooms, only sans bathtub.

Carefully designed two 2.1m×1.8m double beds and two 0.8m×1.8m single beds can comfortably accommodate at least six adults, and it wouldn’t be an issue squeezing in eight people.

The carriage has a complete water circulation system, complemented by a water tank that can hold five tons of water and a water treatment filtration system, sufficient to provide adequate living water for six to eight passengers.

To ensure the vehicle could meet long-term survival needs in the wild, Chen Xin even set up a planting box with five layers of 0.2m×0.6m, capable of providing ample vegetables, along with a super large trunk at the rear, enough to hold food for six people for a month without any pressure.

And it was with this vehicle that Chen Xin finally had the confidence to return home and bring back his parents.

He believed that with this vehicle, he could handle the journey across four provinces, over more than six hundred kilometers, and successfully bring back his parents.

Of course, the cost of modifying such a luxury, high-performance armored RV was exceedingly astonishing.

If Chen Xin had not gathered a substantial amount of raw materials through Li Yulong and personally modified and processed a large batch of components, the survival points needed to upgrade such a vehicle would have been enough to upgrade one and a half shelters.

Even with sufficient materials, the two thousand five hundred survival points still left Chen Xin feeling extremely pained.

Yet he gritted his teeth and invested those survival points into it.

After all, this vehicle was to bring back his parents, and he now feared whether there was any flaw or shortcoming that might prevent him from successfully returning home.

Besides, no matter how much resources and survival points were consumed, Chen Xin was willing to spend them.

Now, with the successful upgrade and modification of the vehicle, Chen Xin felt it was worth the value.

"Finally, it’s completed! Ah Xin, your hands-on ability is really strong!" Ding Ning looked at the armored RV in front of her, full of admiration.

To her and Qin Lan, this vehicle was transformed little by little with their cooperation with Chen Xin, so seeing the upgraded armored RV, both Ding Ning and Qin Lan couldn’t help but feel a bit excited.

However, to Chen Xin, this seemed somewhat discordant, but to avoid revealing his biggest secret, Chen Xin still said to Ding Ning and Qin Lan, "Thank you both for your help. Without your assistance, it would have taken me unknown years and months to complete the modification of this vehicle by myself."

"Ah Xin, you’re too polite. Ah Ning and I haven’t done much, just helped a bit here and there; it really doesn’t count for much." Qin Lan shook her head, although from her perspective, she and Ding Ning indeed helped a lot, her personality made her feel like she hadn’t done much.

Chen Xin didn’t mind this, opened a door on the side of the vehicle, lowered the boarding ladder, and said to the two, "Would the ladies like to go up and take a look?"

Facing Chen Xin’s invitation and this mighty, powerful, and yet understated heavyweight armored RV, Qin Lan and Ding Ning exchanged a glance and couldn’t help but smile at each other before they both boarded the vehicle.

Because they entered from the middle of the vehicle, after boarding, what presented before them was a table surrounded by a circle of U-shaped sofas.

Chen Xin also boarded the vehicle at this moment and introduced warmly to them, "This is the living area where you can sit normally to eat or chat. Ahead is the driver’s cabin, and behind is the bedroom, kitchen, and bathroom."

While speaking, Chen Xin also lowered the table in the middle of the sofas, smilingly explaining to the two, "This table can be raised and lowered; once lowered, it becomes a double bed."

"A double bed?" Ding Ning looked at the table that was now flush with the adjacent sofa and said to Chen Xin, "It looks quite spacious; is it comfortable to sleep on at night?"

"I’m not sure about that. You’d have to try sleeping on it to know." Chen Xin could only smile, expressing his uncertainty about the question.

Compared to Ding Ning, who was more concerned about the living area, Qin Lan actively walked forward, passed a channel less than half a meter long, and entered the driver’s cabin.

Certainly, Chen Xin followed her, seeing Qin Lan curiously survey the various equipment around the driver’s seat, so he volunteered to introduce to her one by one.

Listening to Chen Xin’s introduction of the radar, drones, and night vision equipment equipped in this vehicle, Qin Lan couldn’t help but exclaim, "Really, Ah Xin, you’re impressive, this vehicle is already stronger than the armored cars used by our team."

"No choice! After all, I have to cross six provinces and over six hundred kilometers to bring back my parents. Without complete functionality on the vehicle, it’s impossible to finish such a long journey and return." Chen Xin could only explain it as a practical necessity.

However, after hearing Chen Xin’s words, Qin Lan only looked at him and asked him a question, "So Ah Xin, when do you plan to set off to bring your parents back home?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 201 - 199 Preparations Before Departure
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Returning home to pick up his parents is Chen Xin’s most urgent task right now.

The lack of a suitable vehicle was initially the biggest obstacle preventing him from doing this, but now that the car has been modified, this task has been scheduled and is something Chen Xin must prepare for immediately.

No matter how urgent his feelings are, this is still not a trip that can be undertaken on a whim.

Although there’s a mode of transport now, the journey is long, and water and food must be prepared adequately; other related supplies are also essential because he doesn’t know what the situation is like with his parents.

The last message Chen Xin received before the disaster was that his parents and relatives had already entered the village’s shelter.

Everyone in that village is a relative, and since the construction of the shelter began, Chen Xin has been advising them, telling them to prepare what he has prepared regarding various precautions and survival facilities.

Therefore, the survival conditions of that mountain village shelter are not bad, hardly different from Chen Xin’s shelter before its system upgrade, and because of its large size, it might be even more comfortable to live in.

Thus, Chen Xin is not worried that his parents and relatives are in any danger or trouble; the village is miles away from the nearest town and an hour’s drive from the county seat, so no one would be interested in such a remote and poor mountain village.

However, despite saying this, no one can predict what might happen; the mountain’s isolated environment, free from external interference, also brings significant inconvenience for the mountain village shelter’s communication with the outside world.

After all, the nearest town is miles from the village, and the mountain road is hard to travel; considering the situation now with heavy snow sealing the mountain, it’s no easy task to enter the mountain, and it’s also difficult for mountain dwellers to come out.

If anything happens in the mountains, whether it’s seeking aid or sending help, it’s extremely difficult.

Although Chen Xin doesn’t believe there will be major issues at the mountain village shelter, he cannot guarantee that nothing will happen there.

To handle potential situations, Chen Xin must make sufficient preparations before setting out.

This journey is more than six hundred kilometers, and no one knows what situations might arise; it’s difficult to say whether shelters will be encountered along the way, and Chen Xin must prepare for the worst.

The six hundred kilometers points to pre-disaster expressways, where there were service areas for rest and supplies, but now it’s very likely that even shelters may not be encountered unless Chen Xin specifically enters cities.

If he can’t resupply or find shelters along the way, he must prepare enough supplies on the vehicle to last the journey.

Thinking about supplies, Chen Xin couldn’t help but ask Qin Lan, "Lan, considering all that’s happening in the city, can the SWAT team still come to exchange supplies this month?"

"It should be possible, though things are indeed hectic recently; the supplies exchange itself shouldn’t be heavily affected, but the exchanged supplies might be less favorable," Qin Lan thought and concluded that this wouldn’t obstruct normal exchanges.

Chen Xin nodded to show understanding, but after considering, he said to Qin Lan, "Please contact Uncle Ding for me, and tell him I’ll use the next two months’ supplies as collateral to advance some supplies. I need to prepare adequate supplies to go home to pick up my parents."

"This might be a bit difficult," Qin Lan frowned upon hearing Chen Xin’s request, "While the supplies exchange can continue normally, the SWAT team is likely fully engaged in handling recent attacks and lacks extra manpower to gather supplies.

Exchanging food and such should be no problem, but other supplies might be tricky; I’ll try to speak to the captain, but I can’t guarantee what can be exchanged."

"Alright, I understand the SWAT team’s situation now. I’ll make a list for you later; primarily, I’ll need construction materials and steel, so exchange for whatever you can." Chen Xin understood the SWAT team’s current situation and knew Qin Lan’s limitations.

But he intends to complete a comprehensive upgrade of his shelter before going to pick up his parents, plus he needs to prepare traveling supplies, so materials and supplies are indispensable.

"Okay, give me the list later, and I’ll ask the captain." Qin Lan nodded and agreed to the matter.

————————————————

Since the car has been modified, Chen Xin now begins loading various supplies into the vehicle.

Water and food are naturally the first items to be loaded.

Food isn’t lacking for Chen Xin; his storage room currently has enough food to last him, as an individual, for nearly six months.

From the storage room, he took two unboxed shelter food containers and placed them in the car. Chen Xin estimated that the journey is more than six hundred kilometers one way and, considering both ways, would be between thirteen hundred to fourteen hundred kilometers.

If the road presents any issues requiring detours, it could extend to fifteen hundred kilometers.

Chen Xin conservatively calculates making fifty kilometers per day, which means the round trip would take about a month.

Two boxes of shelter food are meant to ensure his own basic rations during this month-long journey; if there’s no resupply along the way, this food serves as a fallback.

In addition to these two boxes of shelter food, Chen Xin also plans to plant lettuce in a cultivation box on the vehicle and carry a packet of seeds.

This lettuce serves as food and can be exchanged for resupply at shelters or points encountered along the way; although it’s possible not to encounter any resupply points, passing through four provinces and several cities makes it feasible to access shelters in these cities if necessary.

Of course, these are provisions for unexpected situations; Chen Xin’s actual plan for meals involves a box of luncheon meat and a box of self-heating food.

Moreover, he plans to prepare a hundred pounds of dehydrated vegetables and a hundred pounds of rice, and once this month’s supplies exchange is completed, he’ll prepare two more boxes of shelter food—as such, Chen Xin feels that even if he manages to pick up his parents, he’ll have enough food for the return journey.

As for living water along the way, Chen Xin is not worried about this at all. Considering he has a five-ton water tank, this amount of water, as long as it’s not wasted, is already sufficient.

Previously, just by himself, Chen Xin used only three tons of water per month; he also equipped the vehicle with a purification filter, so if necessary, roadside snow can be used as a water source.

Connecting a washing hose from the washing room next to the garage, while filling the tank with water, Chen Xin thought about whether there was anything else he hadn’t prepared.

He doesn’t need gasoline; the car has three Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries—one for the engine, one for the cabin, and one as a backup or to boost the engine when necessary.

Each Isotope Thermoelectric Battery has a lifespan of over ten years and a rated output of 200KW, so there’s no concern of "running out of fuel" on this journey.

Food has already filled the trunk; a hundred pounds of rice, a hundred pounds of dehydrated vegetables paired with a box of self-heating food and a box of canned goods are the main meals, while the four boxes of shelter food serve as emergency reserves, more than enough to handle any unexpected situations.

Water is being filled now; the outlet is connected to a purification filter, so if needed, roadside snow can be shoveled in as a supplementary water source, with no worry about potential contamination of the tank.

Other things to prepare...

As Chen Xin was calculating what else he needed to prepare, Qin Lan, having contacted the SWAT team, came to him and said, "I’ve spoken to the captain about the supplies exchange; the captain said your request is fine, and he’s willing to advance the supplies for two months, but there’s a condition."

"Condition? What condition?" Chen Xin looked at Qin Lan; Uncle Ding mentioning a condition for the supplies exchange didn’t surprise him or seem unacceptable.

"The city plans to open a previously closed small shelter, intending to use the ground-source heat pump technology you provided to the team as the heating and power supply," Qin Lan explained to Chen Xin, who appeared calm, "The small shelter is similar in size to the one allocated to Li Yulong by the city, so the captain wants to know how many ground-source heat pumps are needed to meet the heating and electrical needs for such a shelter, how long it’ll take you to deliver, and what materials are required."

After hearing Qin Lan’s explanation, Chen Xin raised an eyebrow, feeling surprised yet recognized its rationale, "Is the city planning to adopt a new model?"

"Perhaps influenced by the recent coal shortage, attempting to mitigate some risk," Qin Lan speculated, unaware of the city’s reasoning but saw nothing unusual in their action, "Are you able to solve this problem, Xin?"

"Meeting the heating needs of a small shelter isn’t difficult; as for electricity..." Chen Xin rubbed his chin, contemplating, "If we’re not considering setting up a greenhouse like Xiaolong, fifty ground-source heat pumps should be enough to cover heating and basic lighting needs, although this figure is just my estimate and I can’t guarantee sufficient supply. Delivery may require about two weeks, with materials the same as the previous transaction."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 202 - 200: The City Government’s Order
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The city government wants to install geothermal heat pumps in small shelters, which in Chen Xin’s view should only be an experiment for large-scale application of geothermal heat pumps.

The destruction of the railway this time led to a tension in coal usage in the urban area, triggering an electricity shortage and possibly a heating crisis, all of which has alarmed the city government.

Indeed, various emergency plans had already been prepared before the disaster. In this event, the city government relied on pre-stored emergency coal reserves to sustain for several days, waiting for the provincial government to allocate coal from other places, to solve the coal shortage problem.

But all this is obviously not foolproof.

This time, the railway was only destroyed, delaying rail transport for about a week. As long as the railway is restored, the problem can be solved. But what if this crisis isn’t solved within a week, and the provincial government can’t timely allocate coal from elsewhere, or the railway for coal transport into the city gets blocked again?

The city government must consider the possibility of various contingencies, and at this time the batch of geothermal heat pumps from the special police department naturally became one of the options considered by the city government.

This batch of geothermal heat pumps had long been activated, and the survival points had already been settled. At that time, it brought Chen Xin five thousand survival points and a daily gain of +50 survival points, letting Chen Xin know the current technology level allows a shelter to gain this many points on one improvement, dispelling his idea of massively farming survival points.

Of course, despite this, five thousand survival points and a daily income of +50 points is still quite a generous income for Chen Xin.

It was precisely because of this income that Chen Xin dared to lavishly renovate the bedroom and upgrade the vehicle.

Now the city government proposed to use geothermal heat pumps for heating and electricity generation in a small shelter, which naturally represents another opportunity for Chen Xin to earn a large amount of survival points.

Although he still had several thousand survival points on hand, and his daily survival point income exceeded a hundred points, as an important resource in the system, Chen Xin would never mind having more survival points.

But Chen Xin now had to go pick up his parents, and he didn’t have time to slowly work on geothermal heat pumps in the shelter.

However, the city government clearly has no intention of waiting for him to bring his parents back before proceeding with this project, so can he use the city’s connections to bring his parents over?

Bringing his parents from the hometown through the city government, instead of going himself, was certainly part of Chen Xin’s previous consideration and planning.

However, after consulting Qin Lan and asking Uncle Ding, Chen Xin learned that the current railway transport network was not open for civilian or passenger transport, nor was there a demand for it.

Except for material transportation, only in emergencies would railway trains carry people, and even then, there are no special trains, only additional carriages attached to material transport cars.

Normally, even official personnel requiring to go to other cities also need various certifications explaining the reasons, before they are allowed to hitch a ride on the material transport train.

For example, during the city’s attack event, people from the provincial government came from Star City to the city by attaching a carriage to a northward-moving material transport car through emergency allocation.

Hoping to use the city’s connections to bring his parents over by train is almost impossible for Chen Xin.

Within the province, relying on the city’s connections might adjust things, but Chen Xin’s journey needed to cross four provinces, which obviously exceeded the city’s scope of authority, and even the provincial government might not be able to clear the path.

After all, crossing four provinces is too far, with too many checkpoints along the way.

Moreover, even if the city government could clear the path to bring Chen Xin’s parents over, why would they do him this favor?

So taking the train to pick them up naturally was ruled out by Chen Xin, and he could only drive himself to pick them up.

Yet now the city government proposed to conduct trials with geothermal heat pump equipment in a small shelter, and Chen Xin clearly did not want to pass on this opportunity.

Though it’s just a trial in a small shelter, once proven effective, it would naturally be promoted, and Chen Xin’s journey isn’t to leave forever—at most, he’ll be back in a month or two, so it wouldn’t make sense to refuse the proposal.

But this conflicted with his plans to pick up his parents, how should he handle this?

Delaying his departure by two weeks to complete a batch of geothermal heat pumps for the city government is feasible, but to avoid complications, Chen Xin preferred to leave quickly and not delay by two weeks.

Completing this order quickly and trading with the city government, while being able to set off to pick up his parents as planned, is certainly the ideal solution.

But without system blueprints for geothermal heat pumps, even utilizing the workbench to conveniently process parts, he could only make two to three geothermal heat pumps a day at most, and he quoted the city government fifty units which would take at least two weeks to complete.

The only option before Chen Xin seemed to be upgrading the workbench to enhance its efficiency in manufacturing geothermal heat pumps.

Fortunately, this choice was not hard to accept for Chen Xin.

——————————————————

"Azu, received word from Luu that there will be a batch of materials delivered to that shelter in a few days by the city government," Old Four found Azu and relayed the recently obtained information: "This is a great opportunity to take that shelter!"

"Is the information certain?" Azu didn’t get as excited as Old Four but sought confirmation.

"It’s information from Luu, there shouldn’t be any problem," Old Four was evidently confident in Luu’s capabilities and didn’t doubt the accuracy of the intelligence.

Upon hearing Old Four’s words, Azu nodded, but he clearly considered more: "How does that party connect with the city government? By what means does the government deliver supplies? Have these been clarified?"

"Luu has already inquired; the shelter communicates with the city government using shortwave radios, and a truck will transport the supplies," none of these details were overlooked by Luu, and Old Four naturally informed Azu.

After Old Four’s explanation, Azu thought it through carefully and said to Old Four: "Prepare two plans—get a truck to disguise as the supply delivery vehicle and deceive the shelter to open the door, rush inside, and take control of the people inside.

If this plan fails, then when the city’s people deliver supplies, organize a wave of attack when they open the door, forcibly seize the shelter.

The person inside the shelter who can modify cars to run in snow is important, do your best to control him and have him join us, so avoid killing if possible."

"I understand, Azu, Third Brother is the best at this kind of thing!" Old Four was skilled with intelligence but wasn’t adept at disguised attacks, but among Azu’s brothers, there were people who were.

Despite not having high-level disguise skills, Old Four felt that this operation wouldn’t require such high-level disguises.

Of course, Azu knew the strengths of his brothers, so he nodded and said: "I trust Third Brother’s skills, but to be safe, let Sixth Brother go with him. Sixth Brother is clever; if anything unexpected happens, he’s more capable of handling it than Third Brother."

"Got it, I’ll go find Third Brother and Sixth Brother now," Old Four nodded.

————————————————————

"A Xin, why do you still want to renovate the shelter? Isn’t your shelter nice enough to live in?" Qin Lan helped Chen Xin confirm matters with the city government regarding geothermal heat pumps, but she couldn’t understand why Chen Xin wanted to exchange so many materials to renovate his shelter.

Although the vague concept provided by the system auto-rationalized Qin Lan accepting Chen Xin’s shelter upgrading as just a renovation, the motive behind the renovation couldn’t rationalize, and explaining remained Chen Xin’s duty.

"I want to bring my parents to live, so I have to make the place more comfortable, right? Living alone in such a large place is already a bit too snug, let alone adding my parents, some expansion is necessary." The system already made the most illogical parts logical, concocting a believable reason and excuse was not difficult for a former online writer.

After hearing Chen Xin’s reason, Qin Lan understood, nodded, and asked: "Then you’ll complete renovations before you set off?"

"Yes, I intend to complete the shelter’s renovation and the city’s geothermal heat pump order before heading off to fetch my parents," Chen Xin nodded and affirmed Qin Lan’s question: "Running this errand may take one or two months; if the shelter is renovated before I leave, I’ll feel more at ease."

One question answered, Qin Lan then had another question: "I understand your reasons, but when you’re away for one or two months, what about the next two months’ supplies you foresaw from the captain? Who will manage them if you’re not around?"

"Let Longniang manage them for me. Previously, she wasn’t willing to come live here with her sister, presumably fearing owing me a favor. Now, asking her to look after my house plus farm, assuming I’m doing her a favor, should be fine." Of course, Chen Xin had considered this question earlier and explained his arrangements to Qin Lan while also stating the reason: "I initially planned for Little Long to send someone to help manage, but thinking twice, I trust Little Long, yet not necessarily his men. If my stuff gets ruined or broken, it would be troublesome, so better to ask Longniang instead."

"Hmm, Qingyan and Qingxin are indeed more reliable than Little Long’s men," Qin Lan also agreed with Chen Xin’s choice.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 203 - 201: Preparations by All Sides
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As the city government controlling the resources, providing Chen Xin with the supplies he needs was quick.

If it weren’t for the transportation difficulties in this weather, they could even deliver it to Chen Xin’s doorstep in a day.

Nevertheless, the city government quickly prepared the items Chen Xin required.

Chen Xin contacted Mo Qingyan, explained the situation to her, and asked her to help look after the shelter while he was away.

After a brief thought, Mo Qingyan agreed to Chen Xin’s request.

However, she raised an issue that she and Mo Qingxin might not manage the shelter well, especially with the crops in the greenhouse, as they didn’t know how to care for them.

Previously, Chen Xin had given them an aquaponics system, which Mo Qingyan barely managed, just following the basic care instructions from Li Wensheng, like watering daily and ensuring the fish didn’t die.

So if they were to manage Chen Xin’s greenhouse, all they could do was water the plants on time and possibly just pull out the whole plant when harvesting.

Moreover, their shelter needed attention too, so if both sisters moved over, their own place would be a problem.

Running between both places wasn’t feasible under the current circumstances.

However, these issues were not hard to solve, as Ding Ning said he could help with Mo Qingyan’s shelter, having lived there before, which would not be a problem.

Meanwhile, Chen Xin stated he would modify the shelter before leaving, adding a monitoring system to the greenhouse that would automatically prompt what needed to be done, so Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin wouldn’t have to worry too much.

This kind of smart monitoring program wasn’t new; it was popular before the disaster, with vegetable growing boxes having such features to monitor the plant’s growth and environment and suggest watering, fertilization, and light adjustments.

Even pre-disaster greenhouses had similar functions, only at higher costs.

For Chen Xin, implementing such a function was just an option added during an upgrade, easily solved with survival points.

Once these matters were settled, Mo Qingyan agreed to move over before Chen Xin left. All he had to do was await the city’s delivery and then implement the long-awaited upgrade to the shelter.

——————————————————

While the city was preparing supplies, Luu, who was infiltrating with Secretary Li, relayed the information obtained from Secretary Li to Azu.

Receiving the news, Azu instructed Lao Si to prepare with Lao San and Lao Liu by disguising themselves as city supply trucks and taking the chance to seize Chen Xin’s shelter before the official trucks arrive.

To achieve this, besides having Lao San and Lao Liu disguised as delivery personnel, Lao Si also selected over twenty capable hands to hide in the truck as a backup for unexpected incidents.

However, Lao San regretted that they only had a few homemade shotguns and handguns post-disaster, lacking decent weapons.

After all, this was Flame Country, with the strictest gun control globally. Even acquiring these guns was thanks to finding a craftsman who could make shotguns post-disaster.

Lao San couldn’t help but reminisce about the past when they never worried about weapons, as money could buy anything needed.

Unlike now, where money was worthless paper, and weapons and food were the most valuable commodities.

They struggled not only to acquire weapons but also barely managed to have enough food.

Dissatisfied with this situation, Azu and his followers aspired to take over the city.

If they controlled a city, food and weapons would no longer be a concern. They would have people and guns, plenty to eat, making a name for themselves, just as Azu planned!

Thinking this, Lao San became more invested in seizing Chen Xin’s shelter.

After all, that was a shelter that exchanged fresh vegetables with the city supplies every month! Fresh vegetables were truly scarce resources in the current times!

Besides, the modified vehicle they saw could drive on snow when monitoring that shelter, which was enough to attract Lao San.

That signified a mobility advantage and indicated the shelter had machinery and at least one person with mechanical skills.

That was incredibly important for them!

Because with machinery and skilled personnel, it meant they could manufacture guns!

The craftsman they found who could make shotguns only made them with a few simple tools. Lao San believed that with professional machinery, decently crafted firearms could be made, beyond just pellet, and BB gun shotguns.

"Lao San, Lao Liu, you already know the details, so I won’t say much more," said Lao Si, instructing his brothers: "Once you’re in the shelter, subdue the people first. Try not to kill if possible. The people there could greatly help our cause, and we need such talents."

"Don’t worry, Lao Si, Lao Liu and I know our limits, we won’t mess up," Lao San merely smiled at Lao Si’s instructions, unconcerned.

After all, they were fearless, battle-seasoned mercenaries. Though they might not match active military in military skills, they were experienced veterans.

Lao San didn’t think the people in the shelter could do anything to them. Ultimately, it was just a civilian and two female cops. Against such opponents, even if they had guns, it wouldn’t be much trouble for him and Lao Liu.

Of course, Lao Si trusted his brothers’ skills, but he emphasized, "Controlling the people is crucial. Also, capture the incoming city supplies without detection; you must disguise well! This shelter is to be our stronghold; no escape incidents like last time!"

Upon mentioning the previous escape, Lao Liu’s face turned awkward and grim.

Lao Si referred to the incident with the father and daughter who escaped and froze outside Chen Xin’s shelter. Lao Liu was on guard duty when they escaped but got distracted by the young girl’s mother, granting them leniency, which led to their escape.

He got a severe scolding from Azu for that, and ended many lives at that shelter himself, which rendered it unusable as a stronghold.

Though Lao Si brought up these past events only to remind against repeating mistakes, Lao Liu still felt embarrassed, his face souring and remained silent.

Lao San eased the mood, "Don’t worry, Lao Si. With me around, no issues will arise. And you, Lao Liu, I know you like having fun. Once this is over, I’ll talk to Azu to let you have those two female cops, have fun as you wish, but don’t neglect the task now."

"Got it, Third Brother." Lao Liu reluctantly nodded at Lao San’s words.

——————————————————

Since Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin agreed to come over to help manage his shelter, Chen Xin made some adjustments for their arrival.

Qin Lan naturally moved into his room, and Ding Ning planned to move to Mo Qingyan’s shelter after Chen Xin left, leaving the room for the Mo sisters.

As for Qin Lan, since she was still on leave, she would stay at Chen Xin’s for a while before returning to the SWAT team.

"Ah Xin, your room’s just like before!" Qin Lan laughed, seeing the unmade bed in Chen Xin’s room.

She had known during their relationship that Chen Xin didn’t make his bed. Seeing his messy bed now made her feel nostalgic.

Chen Xin didn’t find such matters embarrassing anymore. They had long grown accustomed to each other’s quirks in life.

"I’ll sleep on the top bunk, you can take the bottom bunk, A Lan. I’ll tidy up later, just change the sheets, blankets, and pillows," Chen Xin still accommodated Qin Lan with the bottom bunk.

Qin Lan didn’t mind. After staying at Chen Xin’s for several days, her shoulder was much better. Though it still hurt occasionally, it didn’t hinder her movement.

Seeing Chen Xin place her belongings in the room cabinet, Qin Lan noticed his weapon cabinet and workbench. Approaching the workbench, she asked, "Isn’t the workbench outside enough? Why have another in the room?"

"The outside one’s for other stuff; this one’s for weapons," Chen Xin didn’t hide the workbench’s true purpose from Qin Lan, as she’d seen it during a night spent in his room. "Besides regular weapons, I can make guns. Is there something you like or are used to? I can make it for you."

"You can make guns?" Qin Lan gave Chen Xin a surprised look, though not shocked, thinking for a moment before replying, "No need for other things, just make me a pair of brass knuckles. That’s simple."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 204 - 202: Commencement of Material Transport
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the early morning, although there is no longer a distinction between day and night nowadays, the old methods of keeping time still remain.

The municipal staff had prepared the supplies, and the truck for loading the goods was ready to depart.

"The supplies are loaded and ready to go at any time." Secretary Li reported to the leader beside him, who was responsible for the city’s small shelter geothermal pump renovation project this time, and was also Secretary Li’s direct superior.

As the secretary of the city office, Secretary Li had been put in charge of this logistics-related exchange of supplies due to both the inherent jurisdiction of the office and his own efforts to take on the responsibility.

The leader did not respond to Secretary Li’s report but merely glanced at his own watch before nodding at him. "Have you contacted the other side?"

"We have already informed them that we are transporting supplies over, and they are aware that we are coming." Secretary Li affirmed with a nod to the leader. In fact, he had not only contacted Chen Xin but also reached out to his nephew Azu through Luu, informing him of the city’s supply truck departure time.

The leader was unaware of Secretary Li’s machinations, simply nodding in satisfaction and saying, "It’s about time, let’s get moving when you’re ready; thank you for making this trip, Xiao Li."

"It’s my duty, my duty!" Secretary Li nodded eagerly at the leader, with a hint of flattery. "Any other instructions, sir? If not, then I’ll set off."

"Go ahead, and be safe on the road." The leader gave Secretary Li a kindly smile.

Upon receiving the leader’s permission, Secretary Li boarded the supply truck and signaled the driver to set off.

Watching the departing supply truck, the leader’s formerly friendly smile disappeared as he turned to the person next to him and said, "Notify the city bureau colleagues—the bait has set off."

——————————————————

While the city’s supply truck was setting off, Azu also received a message from Luu. He turned to Lao San and Lao Liu, who were ready, saying, "Let’s go. The city’s truck has set off, and my second uncle is on the truck. He’ll help buy you some time; you have half an hour."

"Don’t worry, Brother Azu. Something like this shouldn’t take more than ten minutes; we’ll definitely get it done in half an hour." Lao San assured Azu, while Lao Liu gave him a secretive look from the side.

Remembering what he promised Lao Liu, Lao San was annoyed at his brother’s indiscretion yet bound by his promise, he said to Azu, "Brother Azu, you know about Lao Liu’s habit—the two policewomen at the shelter... can we give them to him afterward?"

"Lao Liu can’t control himself again?" Azu glanced at Lao Liu standing beside Lao San but didn’t get angry.

However, after considering it for a moment, he shook his head. "We need to recruit people from that shelter; these two policewomen might be connected to them. I can’t let you waste them."

"What? Brother Azu!" Lao Liu’s expression turned dissatisfied upon hearing this and he hastily looked at Lao San: "Big brother, you promised me! You can’t go back on your word!"

Put on the spot by Lao Liu’s outburst, Lao San looked troubled as he turned to Azu, "Brother Azu, it’s Lao Liu’s only hobby, and I did promise him. What do you think..."

Yet Azu kept shaking his head, though perhaps considering that if Lao Liu wasn’t placated, it might affect the plan, he conceded, "You can’t touch those two policewomen right now. Once we’ve taken control of the city, I’ll find you two other women."

Azu’s concession was already significant, and Lao Liu, knowing Azu never went back on his word, decided to let it be, despite some dissatisfaction. He joined Lao San in getting into their prepared truck and set off.

Besides Lao San and Lao Liu, there were about twenty men hidden in the carriage. If the plan went awry, they would ensure the shelter’s capture by force.

Watching the truck drive away, Azu felt a vague sense of dread, though he shook his head, dismissing it as unnecessary worry.

Seeing Azu’s movement, Lao Si, who was standing by, comforted him, "Azu, don’t worry. Even if Lao Liu’s unreliable, isn’t Lao San there? They’ll make it work."

Azu gave Lao Si a smile, nodding, "I trust Lao San, but Lao Si, make sure to advance the overall plan swiftly. If the city calls in resources from elsewhere, it could disrupt everything we’ve planned."

"I’ll handle it, don’t worry!" Lao Si assured Azu.

——————————————————

After receiving the communication from the city, Chen Xin was also preparing for the arrival of supplies and the overall upgrade of the shelter.

He cleared out quite a bit of space in the storage room to store the supplies and also conducted a thorough inspection of all parts of the shelter to make sure there were no issues or unforeseen troubles when the upgrade commenced.

The overall upgrade of the shelter was something Chen Xin had never attempted before, so he needed to ensure every aspect of the shelter was precisely arranged in his mind. Lest, in a moment of enthusiasm, he went for some flashy function that would end up being a huge hassle.

While Chen Xin was busy, Ding Ning and Qin Lan weren’t idle either.

They knew the city wanted to use the situation as a lure, so they geared up as soon as they got the city’s notice.

Qin Lan, in particular, took her sniper rifle to the newly upgraded observation room and placed it at a narrow observation slit.

The view from here was excellent. With her night vision and infrared scope, she could monitor everything approaching the shelter’s front, even without a spotter.

The open slope ahead gave her an unobstructed view; there wasn’t a single mound of dirt in sight. It was the least challenging terrain for a sniper, as no one could evade a sniper’s aim on such ground.

Qin Lan had spent the previous night in Chen Xin’s room, and he had naturally not hidden the fact that there were two dual Maxim guns at the front entrance from her.

Even though she was surprised that Chen Xin could make Maxims, Qin Lan hadn’t planned to tell anyone, nor to expose them to others.

This was why she braved the nearly -40℃ cold in the observation room, ready for a potential sniper opportunity.

Qin Lan hoped to use her sniper rifle to deal with the enemies this time rather than relying on the widowmakers Chen Xin had set up at the entrance.

In the peaceful times before the disaster, Qin Lan wouldn’t have helped Chen Xin hide it. But now, understanding him, she knew he only had these things for safety, not for doing unfit things armed with such weapons.

Therefore, Qin Lan didn’t mind helping Chen Xin keep a secret, leaving him with a trump card against threats.

Since neither the city people nor terrorists had appeared yet, Qin Lan had the leisure to ponder these matters.

As she thought, she took the pair of brass knuckles off her waist and toyed with them.

These were weapons she had Chen Xin make her last night, a pair of brass knuckles that looked as beautiful as butterflies.

Brass knuckles are rather awkward as controlled weapons. If a policeman determines possession, it’s considered armed, yet its lethality is minimal. It might be less effective than a randomly picked-up stone from the roadside.

In the hands of someone unskilled, they could even fracture their own bones with brass knuckles.

But in the skilled hands of someone proficient in combat or martial arts, they could be highly effective.

Though not as deadly as a knife, they can significantly enhance punching power, and with the use of edges to increase harm, they can cause quite grotesque injuries albeit non-lethal.

However, the pair in Qin Lan’s hands were delicately beautiful and lacked aggressive edges or spikes, resembling more of a stylish metal artwork than a weapon.

But knowing what the brass knuckles were and their functions, Qin Lan would never mistake them for mere ornamental pieces.

Because they were a pair of electric brass knuckles, complete with high-voltage shock capability similar to a police baton, capable of paralyzing someone instantaneously.

Electric brass knuckles do exist, but Qin Lan had seen one before, just a plastic trinket with two electrodes on it. Its practicality was low; a stun flashlight would be more straightforward and practical.

Yet the pair made by Chen Xin wasn’t just flashy gadgets.

Full metal construction ensured that even without the stun function, they performed their basic function well, boosting punch impact and protecting fingers.

Additionally, while Qin Lan hadn’t tested the electric capability, she knew that if she used them, a single punch could instantly incapacitate a target.

Truly a practical non-lethal weapon.

While contemplating when to test her brass knuckles, a flash of light caught her attention outside.

Qin Lan pressed her eye against her sniper rifle’s infrared scope and saw a truck heading in her direction. She became alert, communicating through her mic, "Someone’s coming!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 205 - 203: Seizing the Gate
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Old Six, be alert later, don’t keep staring at women." Sitting in the car, looking at the heavy metal gate gradually descending ahead, Old Three kept reminding Old Six.

"Don’t worry, Third Brother, I know the importance of things, I won’t mess up in a situation like this." Old Six knew his own faults and didn’t find Old Three annoying when he reminded him, just nodded seriously.

Although both of them were seasoned veterans, they somehow felt a bit nervous at this moment. Their sixth sense, honed through years of life-and-death experiences, made them subconsciously feel that there was some danger present.

"Be careful." Old Three wasn’t careless. He reminded Old Six once, and after raising his own vigilance, he got out of the car together with Old Six.

The gate of the shelter in front of them had already opened. Chen Xin and Ding Ning stood together at the entrance, but Chen Xin hadn’t lowered the gate completely, only about two-thirds, leaving a third outside, resembling a chest-high wall.

This was a special use of this gate. Lowering two-thirds, leaving one-third outside, could serve as a necessary cover. It allowed people in the shelter to face those outside, without losing protection.

"Are you the people from the municipal government delivering supplies?" Chen Xin had no intention of opening the gate completely or coming out from the shelter, just standing behind the gate like a chest wall, asking Old Three and Old Six.

"Yes, comrade, we’ve come to deliver things to you. Can you open the gate and let us in?" Seeing the vigilant posture Chen Xin had adopted, Old Three felt an inexplicable sense of danger but still greeted Chen Xin, trying to get him to open the gate completely.

Yet Chen Xin remained unmoved, just asking them, "What materials have you brought? Are the things I asked for ready?"

"Building materials, steel, all the supplies you asked for are ready. Can you open the gate so we can drive the car in to unload? It’s quite cold outside." Old Three put on a simple and honest tone, speaking to Chen Xin, the sole purpose was to deceive him into opening the gate.

Although after the metal gate descended two-thirds, for mercenaries like him and Old Six, it was just a matter of a sprint to go over, the portion of the metal gate exposed above ground was still chest-high, and the car obviously couldn’t drive in anyway.

Moreover, only two people appeared in front of them, and there was an unknown female police officer somewhere. If they rushed in, others might already be holding guns ready to ambush them, wouldn’t they be rushing to their deaths?

The safest way was still to drive the car in directly, let the over twenty subordinates on the car pounce and subdue these three. If they couldn’t, lowering this "chest wall" in front of them would still allow their subordinates to rush in better.

However, Chen Xin remained unmoved, just shaking his head and saying, "Sorry, this gate seems to be frozen, it can’t go down. You’ll have to unload the car this way."

Saying this, Chen Xin even deliberately craned his head out to look, seemingly giving Old Three advice, "You can back the car up a bit, align the tailgate here, and just unload the cargo directly here, we can take it from here. If you come in to unload, you’ll have to help carry it, unloading like this might save you some effort."

Hearing this, Old Three felt like cursing, is this saving some effort?

I don’t need you to save effort, I just need you to open the gate! If you really want to save me some trouble, just open the gate and surrender, that would save the most trouble!

Despite his mind being filled with curses, Old Three still spoke to Chen Xin in a flattering manner, "There are many things on the car, unloading like this might be hard to move, and we can lend a hand too, can’t we? Where is the gate frozen? We have a hammer in the car, we can help de-ice it."

Saying this, Old Three really made a gesture of wanting to help de-ice, walking towards Chen Xin as if wanting to check where exactly the gate hadn’t descended due to frost.

Old Three walked towards them, while Old Six walked towards the rear of the delivery truck as if going to get tools.

However, seeing their actions, Chen Xin squinted his eyes slightly, a smile curling up on his lips, as if everything was going as he had predicted.

He didn’t stop Old Three’s actions, just signaled to Ding Ning beside him with a glance and walked towards the gate as well, but he walked to the side of the gate, looking like he was going to check the gate switch.

Old Three didn’t suspect anything, while Old Six was at the truck tail calling for the subordinates to get off, thinking they had succeeded, that Chen Xin had taken the bait, not realizing their every move was under the watch of Qin Lan in the observation room above.

"Ah Xin, be careful! There are more people on the opponents’ vehicle!" While warning Chen Xin, Qin Lan had already brought her eyes to the scope, aiming at the people getting off the truck, ready to fire at any moment.

Hearing Qin Lan’s warning through the earpiece, a look of ’as expected’ flashed in Chen Xin’s eyes, but he showed no expression, just standing by the gate.

Although it appeared that he was checking the gate switch, the remote control for the gate was always in his pocket and he could close the gate at any time.

Old Three, unaware they had been exposed, still acted enthusiastic, pretending to inspect the gate and said to Chen Xin, "Comrade, there doesn’t seem to be any problem outside, why don’t you try to open it again? Or maybe I can come in and help you check, maybe it’s frozen on the inside?"

As he said this, Old Three intended to climb the "chest wall" and get into the shelter.

Chen Xin simply smiled and said, "Alright, I’ll see if I can open it."

While speaking, he pressed the remote control in his hand, raising the gate again.

Seeing the gate rising, the third brother panicked and shouted loudly while climbing over the wall, "Old Six!"

Hearing the third brother’s shout, Old Six realized something had changed and shouted to the people in the carriage, "Charge with me!" Then he rushed toward the gate of the shelter.

The third brother was quite agile, and even though the gate was rising, he quickly climbed over it. But just as he wanted to rush over to subdue Chen Xin, Ding Ning had already pulled out her pistol and aimed at him, shouting, "Don’t move! Raise your hands!"

At this moment, the shelter gate behind him had already risen halfway. Chen Xin had also opened the hidden door beside the gate and grabbed the handle of a double-barreled Maxim machine gun.

The third brother had no idea what Chen Xin was doing. Due to the angle, he couldn’t see the machine gun hidden in the wall. He thought it was just the gate control switch. But he didn’t hesitate and directly crouched down, lunging towards Ding Ning, trying to snatch the pistol from her, subdue her first, and then subdue Chen Xin to lower the gate again.

"Bang!" A gunshot rang out.

——————————————————

Meanwhile, Secretary Li was trying to delay the municipal supply truck’s speed.

"Driver, the snow is so deep on this road, drive a bit slower, be careful. We’re not in a hurry anyway, it doesn’t matter if we’re a little late." Secretary Li told the driver, appearing concerned about safety, but in reality, just stalling for time.

The driver seemed unconcerned, just nodding and saying, "Don’t worry, boss, I’ve been driving for over ten years, I’m steady! There won’t be any problems."

Facing the driver’s attitude, Secretary Li couldn’t say much more and just awkwardly kept quiet.

Delaying the truck’s speed was just one of Secretary Li’s tactics. The real plan to gain time was to have Ah Zu’s men block the truck’s route ahead of time, forcing it to detour.

They didn’t plan on stopping the truck on the road, as that would be too obvious.

So what Ah Zu asked Secretary Li to do was simply reroute and drive a bit slower, trying to give the third and sixth brothers more time to capture Chen Xin’s shelter.

Secretary Li had been involved in planning the supply truck’s route, so he knew there was a critical point on the route. Once blocked, it would require a large detour, taking at least twenty minutes even before the disaster. In this current environment, a detour would take over half an hour.

As for roadblocks, Secretary Li didn’t need to worry about them. Luu, who got the route from him, would inform Ah Zu’s men to handle it.

Sure enough, when the supply truck reached the bridge leading out of town, there was a car sideways on the bridge, seemingly stuck in the snow.

"Boss, there seems to be a situation ahead." The driver naturally noticed the situation ahead and asked Secretary Li how to handle it.

"Ask about the situation first. If we can help, let’s lend a hand." Secretary Li feigned kindness, showing no particular concern.

He, of course, knew that the car ahead was prepared by Ah Zu’s men. He could choose to detour or let the supply truck help drag the car stuck in the snow, both taking about the same time, though towing would take slightly less time.

However, Secretary Li found it strange. The footprints around the car stuck in the snow were messy, making the ground chaotic.

This inevitably annoyed him, thinking Ah Zu’s men were sloppy with their work.

The driver slowly stopped the truck, seemed to inquire for a while, and quickly came back to tell Secretary Li, "Boss, their car is stuck in the snow, and they want us to help tow it out. Do you think we should lend a hand?"

"Will it delay us?" Secretary Li pretended to look at his watch before saying to the driver, "If it doesn’t hold us up, then lend a hand."

"Alright! It’s just a ten-minute job, it’ll be done soon, they’re not too stuck." The driver cheerfully took tools from the vehicle’s toolbox, got off, and started working, while Secretary Li watched the driver busy at work, feeling slightly reassured.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 206 - 204: Setting the Hook
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Contrary to Secretary Li’s expectations, with the help of the driver, the car was quickly pulled out of the snow trap, taking less than five minutes in total.

Watching all this, Secretary Li vaguely felt something was amiss. Passing through the roadblock so easily was clearly not what Azu had arranged, but he couldn’t directly ask the driver why it was done so quickly.

Secretary Li also couldn’t ask the people next to the car why there was no delay. He could only, after the driver packed his tools and got back in, subtly inquire, "Driver, what’s with that car?"

"Oh, nothing much. Their front wheel got stuck in a pit, and the wheel was spinning helplessly. I just gave them a tow, and they sorted it out themselves," the driver cheerfully answered Secretary Li’s question but then suddenly, somewhat suggestively, asked Secretary Li, "Sir, I didn’t waste any time, did I?"

"Oh... oh! No, no, continue on!" Secretary Li, startled by the driver’s question, hurried to indicate for him to keep going.

The supply truck continued on, but the car that had been freed from the snow pit did not. Once the taillights of the supply truck disappeared out of sight, the people inside finally started the car. However, instead of heading out of town, they turned around and headed back into the city.

Of course, Secretary Li didn’t know what happened with the car behind him. At this moment, he sat nervously on the supply truck, watching it get closer to the target. He was increasingly worried that Azu’s men hadn’t yet captured Chen Xin’s hideout, racking his brains for any possible ways to delay.

"Driver, can you pull over? I need to use the restroom," Secretary Li held it in for a long time and finally thought of a way to stall for a bit.

However, the driver gave him a surprised look, reached over, and handed him an empty plastic bottle: "In this weather, you want to go out and use the toilet? Aren’t you afraid you’ll freeze before you’re done? Use this, just go in the car, I’ll open the window to let the smell out easily."

Looking at the empty plastic bottle handed over by the driver, Secretary Li was at a loss whether to take it or not, frozen in embarrassment.

Noticing Secretary Li’s reaction, the driver finally laughed, but the laugh held an inexplicable sternness: "What, can’t go? We’re all men here, nothing to be shy about."

At this moment, Secretary Li also realized something was wrong and quickly said, "No, no, I’ll just hold it for a while."

"Alright then, just hold it in, don’t wet yourself!" The driver was no longer courteous with Secretary Li, tossed the plastic bottle to him, and then stepped on the gas, picking up speed.

At this time, Secretary Li dared not say anything more, only hoping that Azu’s side had already finished their task and would not expose his identity.

The driver no longer paid attention to Secretary Li, confident that he wouldn’t dare to open the door and try to escape.

Let alone if he would fall to his death, in this cold weather, even if he jumped out, where could he run to? He’d freeze to death in the snow.
