Besides, although this was a truck, that doesn’t mean if someone jumped out, they couldn’t be caught up with.

As he drove, the driver no longer feigned simplicity, directly asked Secretary Li, who had guessed his identity, "What’s the real name of that woman in your house? When did she appear in your home?"

"She..." Upon hearing the driver’s question, Secretary Li’s heart sank half a beat. He knew he was exposed but still wanted to struggle: "Driver, who are you talking about? I only have a distant niece at home..."

"Heh, quite the actor, huh? Unfortunately, we’ve already thoroughly investigated. If you confess now, you can still strive for leniency. But if you refuse to confess, it’ll be harsh treatment for resistance!" The driver instantly changed his attitude.

The driver’s sudden stern words shattered the last hope in Secretary Li’s heart, but he stubbornly asked, "Who... who exactly are you?"

"Hmph, Captain Li Yu of the First Battalion, Second Squad of the City’s Special Police Unit! Did you really think we didn’t know you were passing information to your nephew under the guise of smoking, disguised as a cleaning terrorist?" The driver revealed his identity, being Qin Lan’s old superior, Captain Li: "Think it over, come clean about everything to save yourself trouble when you get to the interrogation room, and save us some trouble too."

Hearing Captain Li reveal his identity, Secretary Li collapsed into his seat, and the plastic bottle previously thrown at him by Captain Li fell from his hand, hitting the truck’s cabin floor.

A pair of shiny silver handcuffs was promptly handed to him: "Put them on yourself."

——————————————————

When Captain Li drove to Chen Xin’s hideout, he was taken aback by the scene of vivid red before him.

In the range illuminated by the car headlights, at least seven or eight bodies were lying face down on the ground, facing the direction of the refuge, with blood flowing all over, dyeing the snow beneath them a crimson red.

Meanwhile, near the entrance of the refuge, a truck was parked in place, surrounded by a dozen or so bodies sprawled out in various positions, as if they attempted to rush into the refuge and were all shot dead.

Seeing this scene, not only did Secretary Li almost wet his pants from fright, but even Li Yu felt a chill in his heart. He quickly took out the walkie-talkie to contact Qin Lan, fearing that a stray bullet from nowhere might suddenly snipe him as well.

"These two girls, could they have gone on a killing spree?" Li Yu muttered while calling Qin Lan, "Xiao Qin, this is Old Li, can you hear me?"

"I hear you, Li Yu, you’re finally here." Qin Lan’s voice came through the walkie-talkie, with a hint of tired exhaustion, "I can see you, I’ll have Chen Xin open the door for you."

After speaking, Qin Lan hung up the communication, leaving all the questions Li Yu wanted to ask unsaid, he could only anxiously wait for the refuge door to open and try to look around to find Qin Lan’s position.

However, his anxiety did not last long, as the refuge door soon descended once again.

Li Yu didn’t dare to drive over, afraid of driving over the bodies, so he turned the steering wheel and parked the car to the side, telling Secretary Li, "Get out of the car, don’t try anything funny, there are snipers."

Seeing Secretary Li obediently get out of the car, Li Yu then got out himself, although he wore a mask, the bloody smell almost made him want to vomit.

Soon Ding Ning came out from the refuge, greeting Li Yu, "Uncle Li, you finally made it, let’s talk inside."

As she spoke, Ding Ning glanced at Secretary Li, asking Li Yu, "Is this the city administration’s mole?"

Li Yu nodded, not saying much, while urging Secretary Li to walk into the refuge and asking Ding Ning, "What’s with all the bodies at the door? Did you use a machine gun to mow them down? Did your dad prepare a submachine gun for you?"

"My dad isn’t that generous. It was Chen Xin who did it, he made a submachine gun himself." Ding Ning obviously knew what happened, but she deliberately helped Chen Xin hide the situation, "This guy is very handy, he made a Chicago typewriter based on plans he found online."

"Chicago typewriter?" Li Yu glanced at the bodies on the ground and couldn’t help shaking his head. Apparently, he didn’t think a Chicago typewriter could cause such horrifying damage, even though it was also a very famous firearm.

But Li Yu didn’t expose the "lie" Ding Ning told, he just led Secretary Li and Ding Ning into the refuge.

Inside the refuge, Chen Xin was holding a Chicago typewriter, while the third and sixth were both lying on the ground, their hands cuffed, and the sixth’s leg was wrapped in bandages, blood oozing out.

"Li Yu, you sure came late! We’ve already wrapped up the fight." Chen Xin joked with Li Yu, though his face was terribly pale.

Li Yu glanced at the Chicago typewriter in his hand, having roughly grasped the situation, and laughed without much humor, "You dare to say that, I hurried over to help, only for you three to leave just two alive!"

"I really can’t be blamed for this, they all swarmed to rush into my house. I got scared and didn’t hold back." Chen Xin purposely said with an embarrassed tone the line he had discussed with Qin Lan and Ding Ning.

Qin Lan didn’t want the fact that Chen Xin had a heavy machine gun to be exposed, and Ding Ning naturally had no intention of revealing his secret, so they came up with this ruse.

As for the Chicago typewriter Chen Xin was holding, it was made by Chen Xin, and the reason for using this gun as an excuse was nothing more than because the Chicago typewriter’s caliber was the same as the Maxim.

Listening to Chen Xin’s explanation, Li Yu had no intention to investigate the matter further. For Chen Xin, this was nothing more than excessive self-defense and illegal possession of firearms, but in this post-apocalyptic environment, no one would delve into these matters.

So he just nodded and said, "Alright, no hostages saved us a lot of trouble, just a bit of hassle to dispose of the bodies, what’s the situation with these two?"

"These two should be the main figures of a terrorist group, they pretended to be delivery drivers earlier, trying to rush in and open the refuge door, but both were taken down by Ding Ning. Hired mercenaries! What a poor level." Chen Xin explained the situation to Li Yu, not forgetting to disdainfully criticize the two captives on the ground.

Hearing his words, the third and sixth felt indignant; their skills weren’t poor at all. The reason they were taken down by one person, Ding Ning, was not only because she had a gun, but also because she had an electric weapon made by Chen Xin, which took them both down.

After all, no one can remain standing after being hit by a fist wrapped in tens of thousands of volts of high voltage.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 207 - 205: Capture
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The third and sixth were escorted onto the vehicle by Leader Li, awaiting the fair judgment that was to come.

As for the nearly unrecognizable corpse on the ground, it was cleaned up by a professional body retrieval team. However, the crimson bloodstains on the snow and some remaining body fragments were hard to clear. They didn’t bother, just shoveled up the frozen red ice chunks on the ground into body bags, leaving nothing behind but a patch of dark red bloodstains.

"Although the caliber of the Chicago typewriter is about the same as those bullet holes, a submachine gun can’t create that kind of effect, right?" Watching the dragged corpse, Leader Li whispered to Qin Lan, who had just come down from the observation room.

Qin Lan, of course, knew she couldn’t hide it from Leader Li, but she simply whispered back, "We’re not the ones writing the report, as long as those above don’t know, it’s fine."

Left speechless by Qin Lan’s reasoning, Leader Li could only shake his head, "Alright, but private possession of heavy weapons is quite serious. If it’s found out..."

"Xin isn’t the type to mess around. He’s just holding it as a trump card for self-defense. No one will investigate this," Qin Lan didn’t think Chen Xin would misuse heavy weapons, nor was she worried about the situation being discovered.

After all, times have changed. In this post-apocalyptic environment, if something like this were truly pursued, there’d still be those who would, but with a reasonable cover-up, no one would stubbornly cling to it, demanding that the matter be exposed.

Leader Li thought about it and realized it was true. Bringing these bodies back for autopsy would merely result in a report stating they died from gunshot wounds. Whether it was a heavy machine gun or a submachine gun, only professionals could discern the difference.

Even if the coroner wrote it down truthfully, the superiors above wouldn’t scrutinize it, nor would they even look at the autopsy report. They’d only want to know how many criminals were shot dead.

As long as no one deliberately mentioned it, just as Qin Lan said, once it’s covered up, no one would be bored enough to check whether these terrorists were gunned down by a heavy machine gun or a submachine gun.

After all, it’s the post-apocalyptic era. Even if this group of terrorists were alive, they’d face at least ten years of hard labor or simply a bullet to end it all.

Thinking of this, Leader Li didn’t say more, just nodded, "I’ll give a heads up to the body retrieval team."

Seeing Leader Li willing to help, Qin Lan thanked him and handed him a bottle filled with "water": "Thanks for driving over, have some water to quench your thirst."

"This... I am a bit thirsty," said Leader Li, smiling as he accepted the bottle Qin Lan handed him.

While Leader Li was taking sips of "water" to quench his thirst, Chen Xin was busy inventorying the supplies Leader Li brought over.

Although this was a baiting plan, the supplies transported were real. The city officials were indeed preparing to activate a small shelter, attempting to install the geothermal heat pump designed by Chen Xin to provide heating to the shelter.

If this plan could succeed, the city government would gradually reactivate previously closed small shelters, redistributing the population from the large shelter back to them.

The reason small shelter populations were initially concentrated into large shelters was primarily due to the oxygen issue. After all, the oxygen machines only covered large and medium shelters, while small shelters lacked oxygen equipment.

Another major factor was the heating problem that was hard to resolve.

Now, with Chen Xin’s geothermal heat pump, this issue is no longer a constraint, and with heating solved, setting up growing greenhouses in small shelters to use plant photosynthesis to produce oxygen could also resolve the oxygen issue. This way, living conditions in small shelters can be improved, allowing them to accommodate survivors again.

Ever since knowing the SWAT team had a more energy-efficient heating solution than coal furnaces in the form of geothermal heat pumps, the city government has placed great emphasis on this matter, even when arranging for the capture of terrorists, not forgetting to push this forward.

Not only did they prepare enough supplies for Chen Xin, but they also gathered the raw materials needed for geothermal heat pump manufacturing. If it weren’t for concerns that Chen Xin might not have enough space, they might have brought it all in one go.

Even so, the transportation truck still brought enough materials, according to Chen Xin’s list, to make ten geothermal heat pumps.

Originally, Secretary Li was supposed to handle this batch of supplies and discuss geothermal heat pump issues with Chen Xin, but since he’s been arrested as an accomplice to the terrorists, someone else will naturally have to discuss this with Chen Xin.

And the one to discuss this with Chen Xin was, in fact, Secretary Li’s superior from earlier.

"Sorry, Comrade Xiao Chen, it’s our fault for not doing our job well. This matter must have startled you," the leader arrived at Chen Xin’s shelter shortly after the body retrieval team left, greeting him very cordially.

Chen Xin didn’t really take the leader’s politeness seriously, just returned the greeting with a slight smile and similarly cordial words, "No need to be so polite, leader. Cooperating with the city to combat terrorists is also to protect my own safety. It’s something I should do. It’s cold here, should we go inside to talk?"

Chen Xin spoke, then invited the leader to join him in the shelter to discuss the details of the geothermal heat pump.

————————————————————

While Chen Xin was negotiating with the city government’s leader about the geothermal heat pump, Azu and his brothers were being besieged by SWAT and armed police.

"What the hell is going on! How did these cops find us?" Old Four, while holding back their forces from resisting, loudly advised Azu, "Azu, let’s run! If we keep this up, all the brothers will get caught here!"

"I don’t believe it! How could they have possibly found us here!" Azu was in a frantic state at this moment, full of unwillingness and disbelief.

It was no wonder Azu felt unwilling. Before the police blocked the door, he was still plotting how to take over Chen Xin’s shelter, how to utilize its production and resources to grow his power during the city’s resource shortage, and eventually control the entire city.

But as soon as he turned around, the police came to the door, not only blocking off the shelter but also killing many of his subordinates in the resistance just now, with even three of his brothers dying under police gunfire.

Azu had a total of six brothers; Old Three and Old Six went to Chen Xin, and now they were likely captured by the police, leaving only Old Four, who was still organizing resistance.

As for the subordinates he brought back from Southeast Asia, several were already lying lifeless on the ground, with only a few remaining, simultaneously resisting under Old Four’s commands and cursing in Vietnamese, urging Azu to flee.

The shelter where they were trapped was just a regular town shelter, designed with only the main entrance as the exit. However, Azu’s men had dug an escape tunnel for emergencies, and now was the time for this tunnel to come into play.

Yet Azu was extremely reluctant; he was unwilling to see his brothers die like this, unwilling for his grand ambitions to end this way, and unwilling to crawl like a dog through a tunnel to escape.

He simply couldn’t fathom how the police managed to find them and how he got exposed.

"Azu! The police are everywhere outside, let’s go!" Old Four pleaded with Azu, not wanting both himself and Azu to go down with three brothers already dead, and Old Three and Old Six likely in dire straits.

They only had a few inferior pistols and some homemade guns, no match for the well-equipped SWAT and armed police in a shootout.

Their current resistance was only possible due to their strategic position and the police’s reluctance to force a direct assault, which could cause extensive casualties, especially considering their previous two suicide attacks demonstrated their use of homemade bombs and their willingness to deploy human bombs.

However, Azu and the others no longer had any homemade bombs; their stock was depleted after the three explosions earlier.

Their resistance now stemmed from the knowledge that capture would mean death anyway, and because an escape tunnel still offered a chance for survival.

"But does this mean Old Two, Old Five, and Old Seven died in vain?" Azu still couldn’t bring himself to just run away, despite knowing further resistance would only lead to capture or death.

"Run, Azu! Surviving is the only way to avenge them! If we die here, everything is lost!" Old Four urged Azu, nearly dragging him along to leave.

Watching another subordinate fall to a bullet through the head, Azu gritted his teeth, finally letting Old Four drag him toward the secret passageway, albeit unwillingly.

Seeing Azu and Old Four begin to flee, the men they brought back from Southeast Asia quickly followed, retreating while firing, with only the pawns they used as cannon fodder remaining to resist.

But as Azu’s core men withdrew, their resistance waned, and it wasn’t long before a team of SWAT officers stormed in.

The bodies of the slain terrorists drew little attention from these SWAT officers. After checking if there were any survivors or bombs on them, they moved on, swiftly searching the entire shelter to capture Azu and his leaders.

The SWAT officers quickly discovered the escape tunnel Azu was using, reporting the situation to Uncle Ding, who was commanding outside, as they pursued the fugitives.

"Escaped? They do know how to save their skins, don’t they? But can they really escape?" Uncle Ding, upon hearing the report, showed no expression, but his heart was filled with rage.

For the sake of the SWAT officers who had sacrificed themselves earlier, he had long resolved to capture Azu and his gang.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 208 - 206: Persuading the Other Party
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Under the pursuit of the special police, whether Azu and Old Four and his group of mercenaries can escape is an unquestionable matter.

Even if they escape this time, where can they flee to in the icy, snowy wilderness? Flame Country is not a small Southeast Asian country; even in this post-apocalyptic world, capturing a group of fugitives is not difficult.

However, this is not a problem for Chen Xin to worry about. After sending off the city government leaders who came to discuss matters, Chen Xin finally had time to go to his storeroom to check the materials sent by the city government this time.

At this point, Chen Xin’s storeroom was already packed to the brim. Various building materials and metals were stacked so that he had no room left. The materials previously consumed to upgrade the bedroom and vehicle had been replenished.

This also meant that the materials needed for the overall upgrade of the shelter were fully prepared; all that was missing was the upgrade itself.

Chen Xin planned to postpone the overall upgrade until tomorrow, even though he had enough materials and survival points now. Today, they just gunned down more than twenty terrorists at the doorstep, leaving it covered in a sheet of crimson. Although it signifies prosperity, it still felt somewhat ominous.

It was as if twenty-odd terrorists were slain on the spot, which still somewhat concerned Chen Xin, so he intended to conduct this critical upgrade the next morning.

After all, the overall upgrade of the shelter was also a very significant matter for Chen Xin.

"A-Xin, what are you doing here? Checking the materials?" Qin Lan, did not return to the city with the others and still remained at Chen Xin’s place during her holiday.

"Yeah, counting the materials to see if they are enough for remodeling. I plan to start renovating the shelter tomorrow. Once I’m done, I’ll set out to pick up my parents." Chen Xin looked back at Qin Lan, who was still in her tactical police gear, looking valiant and spirited.

However, there wasn’t an ounce of femininity in Qin Lan today. Having just sniped some terrorists trying to escape, she carried an undeniable aura of ferocity.

Yet today, Chen Xin was the most ruthless one. Using a heavy machine gun to sweep the terrorists, although he only killed a dozen people and the sweep lasted less than ten seconds, the effects were horrifically terrifying.

The effect of a machine gun sweep is nothing like the TV scenes where a gunshot wound appears on the body and the person still staggers back or remains unfazed and continues fighting.

In the early Maxim heavy machine guns had a caliber of 11.45mm, later reduced, with the M1910 having a caliber of 7.62mm, but the power...

The M1910 used 7.62×54mm M1908 R rimmed cartridges, with an initial velocity of 860 meters per second. The caliber isn’t large, but upon hitting the human body, it leaves a small entry wound but a massive exit wound the size of a bowl of ramen.

Those hit by the Maxim heavy machine gun, especially the twin-mounted ones, were lucky if there was anything left of their bodies.

In fact, the few terrorists shot by Qin Lan were relatively lucky, having just one bullet hole; but most of the terrorists gunned down by Chen Xin were left in tatters, some who charged forward even shattered into pieces, causing the cleanup crew a major headache, needing shovels to clean up properly.

Yet Chen Xin didn’t carry any supposed aura of ferocity. When he pulled the trigger earlier, he didn’t think much, but after all was settled and he opened the door for the cleanup crew, the sight of red, green, yellow everywhere nearly made him throw up.

That’s why when Chief Li arrived, Chen Xin appeared pale.

Luckily, the incident had passed, and it’s not Chen Xin’s first time shooting someone; he quickly recovered, feeling much better and was in the mood to chat with Qin Lan.

"Speaking of which, I remember the first time I met you, you were also in a training suit, right? Back then, I was wondering what kind of woman would show up to a first date in a training suit." Looking at Qin Lan in her combat gear, Chen Xin started joking with her.

Hearing Chen Xin mention the past, Qin Lan couldn’t help but smile: "Speaking of that, you weren’t much better, were you? People usually dress up for a date, at least clean themselves up a bit. You didn’t even shave that day, A-Xin."

"Haha, that was because I didn’t even know it was a blind date that day. Xiao Ding just told me we were going to play games, I’m used to being casual, and Xiao Ding had seen me scruffy before, so I just tidied up a bit and went." Recalling the past, Chen Xin found it amusing. It wasn’t just him, Qin Lan was also tricked into a meeting under the pretense of playing games, which is why she showed up in a training suit.

At this, the two of them exchanged a glance and smiled. It’s remarkable how they ended up together, despite not leaving good first impressions, showing the uncanny nature of fate, even though they eventually broke up due to some personality differences.

"Let’s not dwell on that, A-Xin, are you planning to leave after finishing these things?" Qin Lan brought up the serious matter, asking him, "Are you planning to travel alone?"

Chen Xin nodded, affirming: "I initially planned to have you accompany me, but then I thought it over and decided not to. It’s a long journey, and it would be too hard on you to travel with me. Besides, this is a personal matter; there’s no need to make you come along."

"You’re going to pick up your parents; there’s no issue with me accompanying you, is there? Your parents have always been nice to me; going with you to fetch them is the least I could do." Qin Lan replied, determined to accompany him.

Qin Lan already had reasons prepared, thinking she might convince Chen Xin to change his mind, despite never succeeding in such matters before: "Besides, as you said, the round trip is over two thousand kilometers. How long will it take for you to drive alone? If I’m with you, at least we can take turns driving, I also..."

"You could also stay to help watch over this place." Chen Xin gazed at Qin Lan, with sincerity and seriousness: "I know, Lan, you want to come with me, but the fact is, I don’t want you to make this journey. This is my matter, not yours."

"But I..." Qin Lan wanted to explain, but Chen Xin raised a hand to her.

"I understand your point, but Lan..." Chen Xin placed both hands on Qin Lan’s shoulders, speaking seriously: "I am going to get my parents; it’s my duty as a son. There’s no need for you to take this risk with me.

This journey back and forth is over a thousand kilometers, and I don’t even know if I can make it back alive. There’s no need for you to come! If you truly wish to help me, look after this place, so I won’t return to an empty home."

"Didn’t you ask Qingyan and Qingxin to help take care of this place?" Qin Lan was not convinced by Chen Xin, just as she couldn’t persuade him.

Chen Xin chuckled, responding to Qin Lan: "Long Girl and Little Sister Xin will be staying here long-term, but with such a large place, do you really think the two of them can hold it down? Especially without anyone supervising?"

Hearing Chen Xin’s words, Qin Lan began to seriously consider the issue.

Is Chen Xin’s shelter good? Initially, it was average, suitable as a personal hideout, but not something that would attract much attention.

However, as Chen Xin gradually remodeled and upgraded it with the system, leaving aside the rest, just his thirty-centimeter thick metal door was better than those at official shelters!

Moreover, he had an aquaponic greenhouse, and having stayed there for some time, Qin Lan could confidently say there wasn’t a more comfortable or better-equipped place in the city.

If Chen Xin stayed here, it would be fine. His fearsome dual-mounted Maxim machine guns at the entrance, along with his dealings with the special police team and city government, would deter newcomers from harboring intentions.

But now, Chen Xin was about to leave, travelling over four provinces and more than six hundred kilometers back to his hometown to fetch his parents. Describing this journey as fraught with uncertainty and danger would not be an overstatement.

In such circumstances, asking Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin to watch over his home is theoretically viable, but it immensely increases the chances of someone harboring bad intentions, especially with Chen Xin planning further renovations before leaving. This would make the shelter even more enticing.

In case anyone with motives were to force Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin back to their own homes and take over the place, claiming to watch over it for Chen Xin, even if Chen Xin successfully returned, reclaiming his shelter wouldn’t be easy.

Furthermore, how much of the things inside his shelter would remain intact by then remains unknown.

Though Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin are capable, and maintain good relations with Qin Lan and Ding Ning, they are not Chen Xin. Maintaining the monthly material exchanges with the special police team would already be a stretch. They couldn’t continue the ground-source heat pump cooperation with the city, and naturally, they wouldn’t benefit from the protection these relationships afford.

So solely relying on Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin to guard Chen Xin’s shelter isn’t impractical, but it could pose certain problems.

Qin Lan’s presence provides an essential layer of security, symbolizing the special police team’s protection over Chen Xin’s shelter, given her connections.

"But if you’re going that far alone, driving all the way there and back, aren’t you?" Qin Lan asked seriously, considering the question posed by Chen Xin.

All over the city, given what Chen Xin had achieved through systematic modifications and upgrades, she could assert without reservations that there wasn’t a comparable setup elsewhere. Just the pair of dual-mounted Maxim heavy machine guns at the entrance was menacing enough, compounded by his collaboration with the special police and government, making it challenging for outsiders to entertain any designs.

"""

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 209 - 207: Overall Upgrade!
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin ultimately convinced Qin Lan to stay and take care of his shelter, rather than accompany him on a thousand-kilometer journey back to his hometown to pick up people.

As for how Chen Xin convinced Qin Lan, it was naturally by showing her the various weapons and equipment he had prepared on the tracked armored motorhome.

Seeing the radar, drones, and heavy machine guns installed on the vehicle, Qin Lan knew these things were enough for Chen Xin to protect himself on the journey. As long as he didn’t clash with the military, ordinary bandits wouldn’t be able to do anything to his armored vehicle.

The only concern Qin Lan had was that Chen Xin was alone, and if something happened on the way, he might not be able to handle it in time.

However, this point wasn’t much of an issue, and Qin Lan didn’t insist on accompanying Chen Xin on this trip, so after seeing the weapons on the vehicle, she let go of her worries.

Moreover, Chen Xin made a good point—it might take him one or two months, and she needed to help Chen Xin take care of the house.

With Qin Lan’s help in looking after the house and Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin taking care of the management, Chen Xin no longer had any worries about his home.

Now, it was just a matter of upgrading the entire shelter and finishing the geothermal heat pump that the city needed before he could set off.

To prepare for the upgrade, Chen Xin took a special bath that night and went to bed early to ensure he was in good condition for the overall upgrade of the shelter the next morning.

Qin Lan had already moved into his room, but tonight neither of them had the idea of doing anything intimate. Chen Xin climbed up to the top bunk and quickly fell asleep.

Meanwhile, Qin Lan lay on the bottom bunk, listening to Chen Xin’s steady breathing above her and soon fell asleep with a contented smile on her face.

——————————————————

Early the next morning, Chen Xin woke up very early, even earlier than Qin Lan, who was usually an early riser.

After washing up and making breakfast, Chen Xin finally woke Qin Lan.

Ding Ning had left yesterday to report to the city bureau, so she went with Li and the others, leaving Qin Lan behind because she was still on medical leave recovering.

So the breakfast this morning was just the two of them.

After eating the porridge with ham pieces that Chen Xin had specially prepared, Qin Lan asked, "Axin, you mentioned upgrading your home today. How do you plan to start? Is there anything I can help with?"

"Help? There’s really nothing you need to help with. I’ve completed most of the upgrades already. It was just a matter of not having enough materials before," Chen Xin said, considering Qin Lan’s offer to help but declined her kindness. "Now that the city has provided materials, I’m enough on my own, so Alian, you should rest well today."

After speaking, Chen Xin cleaned up the table and went to the storage room alone.

Hearing that Chen Xin didn’t need her help, Qin Lan didn’t insist and instead turned on his gaming console, planning to follow Chen Xin’s advice and take a good rest today.

Chen Xin, meanwhile, entered the storage room, looking at the supplies-filled room, ready to begin the overall upgrade of the shelter.

Opening the system interface, he focused his thoughts on the entire shelter, and the system interface displayed upgrade options for the shelter.

"Simple Shelter"

"Upgrade Plan 1: Upgrade the shelter’s protection level, enhance disaster resistance, improve living comfort, overall shelter technology +1, survival points required 12,000"

"Upgrade Plan 2: Expand the shelter’s living space, accommodate more people, improve resource support, shelter capacity +5, survival points required 15,000"

As with previous upgrade plans, Chen Xin made no adjustments, but he had detailed ideas for some aspects of the overall upgrade of the shelter.

Without much hesitation, he directly invested various materials from the storage room into the upgrade, choosing to simultaneously upgrade both plans, reducing the survival points consumption to one-tenth, which still required nearly 3,000 points, two-thirds of Chen Xin’s remaining survival points. Most of the materials stored in the storage room were consumed in this upgrade, leaving only a few scraps.

It could be said that Chen Xin’s accumulation over the past six months was all consumed in this upgrade.

But as the emerald light spread to every inch of his surroundings, Chen Xin closed his eyes with a smile of joy and anticipation, eager to see a brand-new shelter when he opened them again.

Counting down silently in his heart, three, two, one, when he reopened his eyes, the green light had faded, and the shelter had changed.

He was still standing in the storage room, but this storage room had now turned into a pure food storage room, with shelves neatly categorizing and storing various foods.

Chen Xin looked at the food storage room that was the same size as the original storage room. He also checked the cold storage that was upgraded from the previous food storage room and refrigerators, only then did Chen Xin nod in satisfaction and leave the storage room.

After stepping out of the storage room, what Chen Xin faced should have been the original public space, but it had now been upgraded to a hall.

This was one of the changes made after the overall upgrade of the shelter, as Chen Xin turned the original shelter into two floors, with the public space serving as a hall connecting the upper and lower levels.

The rooms on the first floor included a storage room, a workshop, and a medical room, as well as a power generation area composed of a ground source heat pump, an algae oxygen machine, and an electrical distribution room, with a passage connecting to the planting greenhouse also arranged on this level.

The second floor primarily consisted of a semi-open living room and kitchen. Since the public space connected the two floors, one side of the living room had a balcony-like structure, allowing a direct view of the first floor.

Surrounding the living room were five double rooms, each with its own bathroom, also featuring bathing facilities. However, Chen Xin still constructed an independent bathroom on the second floor to fulfill his promise to Qin Lan, creating a hot spring bath.

Chen Xin did not go up to the second floor, although now he only needed to look up to see Qin Lan sitting in the living room playing games, seemingly unaware of any changes.

Turning around, he walked through the passage leading to the planting greenhouse, where there had been earth-shaking changes.

The originally square planting greenhouse had transformed into a gigantic cylindrical structure with a diameter of fifteen meters and a height of twenty meters, and the biggest change wasn’t the space of the planting greenhouse, but the planting area inside.

The bottom of the planting greenhouse was turned into a one-meter deep large water pool, with several fish happily swimming around, seemingly finding relief from their cramped conditions in the previous water troughs, finally having enough space to move around.

In addition, the original single-layered hydroponic trays and soil planting areas were also transformed, turning into six layers of one-hectare square hydroponic trays, stacked one above another over the water pool, with irrigation and planting done through the greenhouse’s internal circulation system.

The bottom two layers of planting trays were planted with the previous drought-resistant rice, which seemed not to have changed much, but the base roots were no longer embedded in soil, instead relying on planting trays and nutrient tanks.

The upper four layers consisted of a quarter-layer of carrots, a quarter-layer of cabbage, a quarter-layer of lettuce, and the remaining quarter-layer with strawberries, with the other three layers vacant.

These were the crops Chen Xin had previously planted, clearly indicating that the system upgrade simply changed the greenhouse’s structure without affecting these crops.

The entire planting greenhouse had now entered mechanical automation, with a console at the entrance implementing fully automated planting, maintenance, and harvesting, which, compared to the previous manual work Chen Xin had to do like tilling and harvesting, was undoubtedly much more advanced.

This clearly demonstrated the technological level +1 in the overall upgrade.

After inspecting the planting greenhouse, Chen Xin proceeded to the power generation area, considered the core power supply of the entire shelter, essentially the heart area. Especially after the upgrade, Chen Xin connected the shelter’s ventilation system here, making the power generation area even more crucial.

The power generation area was also a large room with four ground source heat pumps and their associated generators on the floor, while against the wall were four enormous green glass vats, which were the algae oxygen machines.

However, after their upgrade, not only had their volume increased, but they also allowed automatic extraction of the algae sediments for harvest, and even the algae inside had undergone some changes, now capable of producing ethanol.

Although the production wasn’t high, only providing Chen Xin with a hundred liters of ethanol per month, it was still very good news, as it added a new product to the shelter’s production.

The algae oxygen machines were now linked with the ventilation system, with the oxygen they generated being directly delivered through air ducts to every corner of the shelter.

As for the electrical distribution room, it remained a combination of distribution cabinets and batteries, only now with three additional battery installation slots nearby, allowing for the installation of Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries, like the ones Chen Xin used in his armored vehicle.

Although Chen Xin had already manufactured Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries, commonly known as Nuclear Batteries, he didn’t directly convert the shelter to nuclear battery or reactor power supply, instead preparing three Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries in the distribution room as backup power.

"This is really great! This way, there’s no need to worry about power outages or oxygen shortages anymore." Chen Xin nodded in satisfaction, though in reality, he had long ceased to worry about oxygen and power issues.

After checking out the power generation area, Chen Xin took a look at the medical room and the work area; neither had major changes. The work area merely saw the workbench become more advanced, adding various small lathes and equipment, while the medical room was separated into a sterile surgery room and a ward, making it resemble more of a small medical station rather than just a medical room.

These constituted the main parts of the shelter’s overall upgrade, but Chen Xin knew that besides these, there were also transformative changes in the entrance area near the main door, as he moved the supply storage room upstairs, turning it into a supply warehouse.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 210 - 208: Lettuce Salad
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The original connecting passage between the main part of Chen Xin’s bunker and the entrance was a fifty-meter-long upward-sloping tunnel. It was built for the convenience of vehicles parked inside the bunker to easily enter and exit, but in practice, the tunnel was not as convenient as initially thought.

With a length of only fifty meters and a height difference of nearly twenty meters from the entrance to the interior of the bunker, this slope was quite steep, making it inconvenient for vehicles to go in and out, or even for transporting materials with a cart.

At the time the bunker was built, Chen Xin only considered making the bunker deeper to avoid potential impact from meteor strikes, using the more than two hundred meters of soil above as protection, without paying much attention to the details.

After all, it was just a steep slope, which was inconvenient but not completely unusable.

Now that upgrades are possible with the system, Chen Xin naturally made adjustments here, changing the originally sloping tunnel into two horizontal passages connected by an elevator.

This is one of the reasons why Chen Xin waited for the municipal government’s materials before starting the overall upgrade of the bunker; the elevator materials relied on the city’s help to gather, which Chen Xin originally planned to process himself.

The upgraded elevator can accommodate a standard family car, with a cylindrical cab seven meters in diameter and three and a half meters high. The bottom of the cab can rotate, solving the problem of vehicles being unable to turn around in narrow passages.

Riding the elevator to the upper level, passing through the passage, it’s still the original preparation room, but on the other side of the preparation room, a materials storage warehouse has been added.

This adjustment was made by Chen Xin due to the previous inconvenience of transporting materials, placing the storage warehouse on the upper level where the entrance is located, with only a food storage room retained inside the bunker.

This way, materials can be brought in from the entrance and stored directly without the need to transport them further down.

For Chen Xin, it doesn’t matter much where the materials are stored; he simply needs to open the system interface to use the materials without physically retrieving them.

Passing through the preparation room remains the passage equipped with a heavy crossbow.

Chen Xin did not make major modifications here as it is just a passage; the crossbow already has sufficient attack power.

The only adjustment he made was changing the crossbow’s activation from the original flip switch to infrared sensing automatic firing and setting up a hidden mechanism to conceal the entire crossbow in the ceiling.

Anyone passing through the corridor with the alarm activated will be directly faced with a barrage of ninety crossbow bolts per minute, with power comparable to the heavy machine gun at the entrance.

As for the alarm activation method, Chen Xin set it to be activated from the inside through facial recognition to trigger the attack.

In other words, it can be turned on from the inside when coming out, enabling facial recognition, allowing access to the interior of the bunker when recognition is successful.

If a stranger enters without passing facial recognition, the crossbow concealed in the ceiling will activate, providing a thrilling experience of dodging a crossbow barrage.

Regarding the area past the safety passage, the decontamination room has not been changed, but its equipment has been upgraded.

Outside the decontamination room remains the garage, which can be considered a foyer or entrance hall.

This is an L-shaped space, with the short side connected to the entrance and decontamination room, and the long side extended to accommodate Chen Xin’s modified armored RV.

There’s nothing much to say about the garage and foyer; the garage just added vehicle repair functionality and provided some repair tools, while the foyer is merely an empty space.

The real highlight is the bunker’s door.

It is still the previous lift-type metal armor door, but this time Chen Xin changed it from homogeneous armor to multi-layer composite armor, increasing the thickness from 30cm to 35cm. Although the thickness only increased by 5cm, the defense capability is equivalent to 120cm of homogeneous metal armor.

The original twin Maxim machine guns at the entrance have been replaced with twin 12.7mm triple barrel Gatling guns, the same type as mounted on Chen Xin’s armored RV, still computer-controlled for automatic aiming.

Of course, it can still be manually operated like Chen Xin did yesterday, offering the experience of "3600 rotations per breath, the compassion of saving the world."

After inspecting the entire bunker, Chen Xin felt very satisfied with the overall upgrade. Besides enhancing the overall layout and technology level, even the bunker’s originally gray concrete walls have been covered with paint and added with metal supporting frames, making it look much cleaner, tidier, and brighter.

Even the original light bulb lighting has been replaced with LED tubes, which soften the interior lighting and incorporate Chen Xin’s long-desired lighting control, allowing adjustments to lighting according to time changes so that people inside the bunker can also feel the passage of time.

As for the observation room originally set above the main entrance, it was also adjusted and upgraded this time, transforming from a simple antenna room into a formal bunker with added living facilities inside and double-layer vacuum glass on the observation windows to block cold air.

Of course, in order to retain the observation room’s defensive function, the glass of the observation port can be opened, reverting it back to a shooting aperture.

As for the ventilation ducts connected to the exterior walls, Chen Xin didn’t make major changes; he just added exhaust fans and air filters inside the ducts to improve ventilation efficiency.

Returning inside the shelter, Qin Lan was still sitting on the sofa playing games, seemingly unaffected by Chen Xin’s earlier actions. Even though the living room had completely transformed, her game console upgraded from a PS4 to a PS5, and even the television’s size had significantly increased.

"Done with your work?" Qin Lan glanced at Chen Xin as he picked up another controller placed nearby and joined the game. She then pressed pause and switched the game to dual-player mode.

Chen Xin sat down, nodded, and said, "There wasn’t much work left in the first place; once the materials were ready, it was quick to finish, and now it’s all done."

"Glad it’s done, come play a round with me. You lost last time, so here’s a chance to defeat me!" Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin, purposely provoking him.

Chen Xin chuckled freely, gripping the controller and said, "I let you win!"

"Heh, who’s letting who is still up for debate!" Qin Lan was clearly unconvinced and challenged Chen Xin, "Wanna bet on it?"

"Sure, what are we betting?" Chen Xin accepted the challenge, though he’d not tried the latest model PS5 before the disaster: "No backing out if you lose!"

Qin Lan bit her lip, thought for a moment, and then decided, "The loser has to be on the bottom tonight!"

"On the bottom? Don’t you sleep on the lower bunk? What’s on the bot..." Chen Xin realized, and his expression became suggestive, "Going big on this bet? Then I’ll have to give it my all!"

Qin Lan was already blushing but insisted defiantly, "Hmph! I won’t lose no matter what, let’s see how you perish!"

With that, she threw a challenging glance at Chen Xin and started the game right away.

Chen Xin laughed a little and got serious too; matters of a man’s dignity can’t be lost. Even though, for him, losing wouldn’t be the worst outcome.

——————————————

Win or lose aside, how they ultimately settled the bet is not for outsiders to know. Regardless, the next morning, when Chen Xin woke up, Qin Lan was nestled in his arms.

Cautiously withdrawing his arm, Chen Xin got up from bed, quietly got dressed, and to avoid waking Qin Lan, went to the bathroom in another room to freshen up before heading to the newly upgraded kitchen to start breakfast preparations.

However, Qin Lan wasn’t in a deep sleep, and missing the person beside her soon woke her up.

Wrapped in a blanket, Qin Lan walked into the living room; watching Chen Xin busy in the kitchen, she didn’t disturb him but leaned against the kitchen doorway.

The upgraded heating system provided enough warmth inside the shelter, so even with only a blanket, Qin Lan didn’t feel cold. She was cheerfully watching Chen Xin bustle around.

"Awake already, why not sleep a bit more?" Adding a person at the kitchen door was naturally noticeable to Chen Xin. He glanced at Qin Lan and continued chopping the boiled lettuce.

"I can’t sleep without you." Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin and suddenly spoke in an almost coquettish tone, "What’s for breakfast?"

"Can’t sleep without me?" Chen Xin chuckled, looked at her with a hint of tender affection, and said, "I made some lettuce salad and porridge. Though there’s no salad dressing, I’m not sure if you’ll like it? Go brush your teeth; it’ll be ready soon."

"Lettuce salad? It’s great we can have fresh vegetables now, not many choices available." Qin Lan smiled too, wrapped in the blanket, walking back to the room, "I’ll go brush my teeth then."

As Qin Lan went to brush her teeth, Chen Xin finished chopping the lettuce salad, needing only to season it with salt and other condiments before serving.

When Qin Lan returned from the bathroom, wearing just a shirt and sat down at the dining table, bowls and steaming porridge were already set.

"It’s always nice here with you, Ah Xin, everything’s provided, even breakfast is this comfy!" While eating, Qin Lan asked Chen Xin, "I have to go to the city bureau today, what about you, Ah Xin? Any plans for today?"

"I plan to work on the geothermal heat pump the city needs, the sooner I finish, the earlier I can head out." Chen Xin replied to Qin Lan’s inquiry and asked her in return, "Shall I give you a ride back?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 211 - 209 Large-Scale Equipment
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After upgrading the shelter overall, Chen Xin’s workbench also improved with a +1 technology level enhancement, adding many new processing equipment and making the already convenient workbench even more handy.

Especially after the upgrade, the newly added blueprint recording function on the workbench was a feature Chen Xin had long been looking forward to, allowing him to save a lot of effort.

For the workbench upgraded by the system, having blueprints means that the system can automatically produce items according to the blueprints, without the need for Chen Xin to process each part and assemble them manually.

Chen Xin can completely, like when he made a pistol before, use the system’s processing function to turn parts and raw materials into the components needed by the blueprints, and then assemble them through the system to create a finished product, saving a lot of time and effort.

All he needs to do is enter the blueprints into the workbench’s blueprint library, which he must do himself, either by drawing it himself or by manufacturing a piece of equipment once so that the system records the blueprint, or he can input already-made blueprints through a computer.

By inputting the blueprints stored in his computer into the workbench using his laptop, Chen Xin also nostalgically picked up a pencil and laid out white paper on the workbench to draw a geothermal heat pump blueprint.

Of course, Chen Xin knew mechanical drawing; he learned this skill in high school, although he couldn’t draw a complete blueprint with just one pencil like his father could, mechanical drawing itself wasn’t a problem for him.

However, after later learning CAD drawing at work, this skill had long been unused, and now picking up the pencil again was like returning to an old profession.

It’s not that Chen Xin couldn’t use a computer CAD to draw blueprints, it’s just that his computer is just a laptop. Even though he specifically made an effort to upgrade it during the shelter upgrade, it does not change the fact that it is just a laptop, unable to handle CAD drawing while transferring large data files simultaneously.

This is not something that can be done with a single upgrade, and for Chen Xin, there is no need for it. The time it takes him to draw by hand is only slightly more than doing it on a computer.

After all, it’s just a simple assembly drawing and doesn’t require Chen Xin to detail every part.

Humming a little tune, with several pencils in rotation, a not-so-complex geothermal heat pump assembly blueprint was drawn by Chen Xin.

Submitting the blueprint through the system, as the newly drawn blueprint was scanned by a green light on the workbench, a new blueprint appeared in the system interface.

Seeing the blueprint uploaded successfully, Chen Xin quickly opened this blueprint, intending to try if he could use this blueprint to make a geothermal heat pump.

This is also the main reason why Chen Xin was drawing the blueprint; if he could use the blueprint for manufacturing, then the fifty geothermal heat pumps that the city government wanted to order from him could be completed in just a few days, and he could set off earlier.

The system remained as reliable as ever, not disappointing Chen Xin’s expectations. As he opened the blueprint, the options for required materials and components manufacturing appeared before him.

However, just as Chen Xin was about to start manufacturing, he suddenly stopped as an idea popped into his mind: Since objects can be upgraded, can blueprints be upgraded using survival points as well?

If they can be upgraded, does this mean he could upgrade the existing blueprints to create better equipment?

With this thought of Chen Xin’s, a new option appeared on the system interface as well.

Chen Xin could use survival points to improve the blueprints under the current technology level.

"It’s not an upgrade?" Chen Xin looked at the options shown by the system and couldn’t help but feel a little surprised.

The system provided not an upgrade option for blueprints but an improvement option, which surprised Chen Xin, who had been used to the system being able to upgrade everything, also finding it a bit novel.

But after carefully reviewing the system’s prompts, he understood it.

Blueprint upgrading can only upgrade the "blueprint" itself, for example, upgrading from paper to another material such as the flexible touch electronic ink blueprints seen in movies, allowing them to be zoomed in and out, adjusted, and modified, etc.

However, the content carried by the blueprint cannot be upgraded; it can only be improved.

Also, due to the workbench’s limitations, the improvement of the blueprint content cannot exceed the technology level of the workbench itself.

This means Chen Xin must improve the blueprints within the technological level range of the current workbench.

This isn’t a particularly useful function for Chen Xin at the moment but cannot be said to be useless either.

At least now he can make some modifications to the geothermal heat pump blueprint, enhancing each pump’s power output or transforming the existing blueprint into a larger power output device.

On first hearing, it might sound like these two options are the same—both improving the power of the geothermal heat pump—but they are actually quite different.

Enhancing a single geothermal heat pump’s power output refers to optimizing the equipment structure without changing its size and material consumption, thereby improving the equipment’s efficiency to achieve higher output power.

This kind of optimization has a limited upper bound and falls mainly within the range of a general product update.

The latter option, however, involves consolidating the materials consumed by multiple devices into one device to create an equipment of higher power output and size.

The geothermal heat pumps Chen Xin can currently manufacture belong to the category of small equipment designed for home use, sufficient for personal shelter spaces that aren’t too large.

But for official shelters, even small ones, such small equipment can’t meet the requirements.

This is why when the city government talked to Chen Xin about trialing geothermal heat pumps in small shelters, Chen Xin said it took fifty pumps to meet the demand for a small shelter.

Because he couldn’t manufacture higher-powered large equipment, he could only meet the demand by utilizing a multi-unit parallel approach.

But with this blueprint improvement function, Chen Xin can leverage it to manufacture larger, higher-powered geothermal heat pumps that meet the heating demand with just a few devices instead of fifty.

After making up his mind, Chen Xin didn’t hesitate, spending one hundred survival points to improve the blueprint he just uploaded into a large equipment route.

After improvement, the system interface indeed displayed a new blueprint for a large geothermal heat pump.

However, to Chen Xin’s surprise, the original geothermal heat pump blueprint didn’t disappear from the system interface but continued to exist.

"It seems improving blueprints doesn’t consume the original ones!" Chen Xin raised his eyebrows slightly but didn’t dwell on it, instead turning his attention to reviewing the large geothermal heat pump blueprint, eager to see how much material it would consume for producing a large geothermal heat pump and what the output power of a device would be.

In the system interface, the materials needed for a large geothermal heat pump are detailed, and the performance of the device is also displayed in a data list format, allowing Chen Xin to clearly compare the differences between the large and small geothermal pump devices.

The small geothermal heat pump devices Chen Xin manufactured earlier had an output power of about 10KW, requiring the consumption of parts from a standard household air conditioning unit for manufacturing.

Of course, using a standard household air conditioning outdoor unit as a material unit was because the parts included in a household air conditioner were enough to meet the manufacturing needs of a small geothermal heat pump.

But after upgrading the workbench with the system, the required material lists on the blueprints detail a series of parts such as compressors, evaporators, and condensers.

Chen Xin can obtain parts by disassembling air conditioning units sent by the city government or directly input parts as materials for production, and even manufacture parts using raw materials according to the blueprints.

This is certainly good news for Chen Xin, as it means he doesn’t have to worry about being unable to produce geothermal heat pumps if air conditioning units are unavailable.

However, compared to small geothermal heat pump devices, large geothermal heat pump devices have an increased output power to 50KW, but also require the material consumption of three standard household air conditioner units’ parts, along with some additional raw materials to manufacture.

This is more material-saving compared to originally consuming five air conditioner units’ parts to manufacture five 10KW small geothermal pumps, and it also saves installation space.

Although a large geothermal heat pump device is much bulkier than a small geothermal heat pump device, it’s not as big as five of them, which is a crucial consideration for underground shelters with limited underground space.

After reviewing the large geothermal heat pump device blueprint, Chen Xin decided to use materials from the ten small geothermal pumps sent by the city government to make three large geothermal pumps first.

When discussing the geothermal pumps with city leaders, they had requested larger geothermal pump devices, and Chen Xin had initially explained the material inadequacy as the reason.

And for the city, larger equipment is undoubtedly better than smaller ones. Chen Xin being able to manufacture larger geothermal heat pump devices would surely be met with applause instead of any objections to previous arrangements.

So, without hesitation, Chen Xin started the workbench, commencing the manufacture of the first large geothermal heat pump device.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 212 - 210: Insufficiently Prepared Medication
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Compared to manufacturing ten 10KW small devices, three 50KW large devices are clearly stronger in terms of total output power.

Moreover, a large device has an advantage in that even though its output power is the same as five small devices, under the same output power, its electricity consumption is evidently lower than that of small devices.

The manufactured equipment did not appear on Chen Xin’s workbench but was directly delivered to the upper-level material warehouse.

This is undoubtedly one of the new features acquired after the upgrade, as Chen Xin can choose to have the manufactured items appear on the workbench or choose to place them directly in the material warehouse to avoid some items occupying the internal space of the main shelter.

The manufacturing time for three large geothermal heat pump devices was much shorter than Chen Xin originally anticipated for ten small geothermal heat pumps.

Based on the time Chen Xin took to help the SWAT team last time, he could complete about three small geothermal heat pumps a day, but now he finished manufacturing three large geothermal heat pump devices in just about a midday and afternoon.

This is undoubtedly a major boon for Chen Xin.

Originally, he needed to manufacture fifty small geothermal heat pump devices. Now, with large geothermal heat pump devices and keeping total output power unchanged, Chen Xin has used one-fifth of the original materials and less than one-fifteenth of the original work period to complete nearly one-third of the task requirements.

It should be noted that Chen Xin’s original calculation was that ten small devices could be completed within two to three days after the upgrade, but now it’s all done in just one day. Moreover, there was a bit of material left over, which saved Chen Xin a lot of time, and he could embark on his journey a few days earlier than planned.

Upon reporting this result to the city government via shortwave radio, the reaction was as expected; the city government was very pleased and extremely interested upon receiving the news.

After all, between ten devices and three devices, the city government people know clearly which is easier to maintain and use.

Furthermore, Chen Xin being able to manufacture large geothermal heat pump devices is a significant boon for the city’s plan to reactivate small shelters.

If it weren’t for the city government’s initial plan to conduct a pilot project by using geothermal heat pump heating in a small shelter and then decide whether to activate more small shelters based on the results, the leader in charge might have already planned to order more devices from Chen Xin to reactivate all the originally stored small shelters in the urban area.

But anyway, as a leader, he doesn’t act impulsively. Moreover, Chen Xin mentioned that he needs to return home to pick up his parents, so the city government did not change the plan and insist on keeping Chen Xin.

Therefore, the leader in charge of this matter only arranged for the previously agreed manufacturing materials to be delivered to Chen Xin’s shelter while accepting the three large geothermal heat pump devices to be sent to the small shelter ready for activation.

As for the installation work of the geothermal heat pumps, neither Chen Xin nor the leaders need to worry as the city government’s construction team will handle such matters. Chen Xin only needs to provide the equipment, and the leaders only care about the results.

With things going smoothly, Chen Xin naturally didn’t worry further about this matter. He just needed to complete the production of the geothermal heat pumps with the materials provided by the city government according to their request.

Besides this, what Chen Xin needed to worry about was how to hand over his shelter to Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin sisters for care before his departure.

Concerning this, Chen Xin had already negotiated with Mo Qingyan, and she agreed to come back to take care of Chen Xin’s shelter, though she and Mo Qingxin haven’t moved in yet.

However, Mo Qingyan agreed to move in two days before Chen Xin’s departure to have enough time to familiarize herself with Chen Xin’s shelter, preventing any unnecessary troubles due to unfamiliarity with the equipment after Chen Xin leaves.

Besides these matters, the vegetables Chen Xin planted in the greenhouse were almost ready for harvest, which also meant that the monthly resource exchange with the SWAT team was approaching.

Although the SWAT team’s resources had already been delivered alongside the materials sent by the city government earlier, Chen Xin’s vegetables had not yet reached harvesting time. Therefore, this resource exchange would only involve Chen Xin handing over the harvested vegetables to the SWAT team.

Since Chen Xin had previously advanced the exchanging shares for the next two months, he would need another two exchanges before he could resume regular exchanges.

According to Chen Xin’s own estimated time, if everything goes smoothly, by then he would already have picked up his parents from his hometown.

After some consideration, Chen Xin decided to let Mo Qingyan handle this and the next two resource exchanges, allowing him to embark on his journey home a few days earlier.

Luckily, his planting greenhouse is now fully mechanized and automated; whether planting or harvesting, it could be completed by machines. Mo Qingyan would only need to operate the console, without having to laboriously farm like Chen Xin used to.

While thinking about these matters, Chen Xin also arrived at the planting greenhouse, checking the growth condition of the crops in the greenhouse through the monitor screen at the console.

The console that appeared after the upgrade of the planting greenhouse proved to be a very convenient tool. Through the monitor screen on the console, Chen Xin could see the growth condition of every plant on the six layers of hydroponic growing plates above him without climbing up to inspect visually.

While examining the crops about to mature, Chen Xin suddenly noticed a small patch of plants on one of the hydroponic growing plates that looked quite different from the others, resembling a bunch of weeds.

Pulling the surveillance camera closer, he realized that these were the herbs he promised to plant for Doctor Hong Rui.

Even though Chen Xin had been taking care of them since planting, evidently, with all the recent happenings, he had forgotten about these herbs.

The herbs were growing quite well; however, as medicinal plants, their growth wasn’t as fast as the nearby vegetables. They still required some time of cultivation before they could be harvested.

Having checked the herbs, Chen Xin didn’t pay too much attention to them since their growth seemed fine, and it was still early for harvesting, so he didn’t give them much thought.

However, looking at these herbs reminded him of something he had overlooked.

Although he had prepared first aid kits in the armored RV, he hadn’t prepared enough medicine.

It’s not that Chen Xin forgot to prepare medicine. He had taken various medicines from the medical room and put them in the armored RV’s medicine cabinet.

Besides medicine, Chen Xin even prepared two simple surgical kits to prevent any unexpected incidents along the way.

But these medicines were prepared before the disaster, limited to some common drugs, and since Chen Xin needed to leave some behind in the shelter, although not insufficient, they might not be enough in some unexpected situations.

"It seems I need to figure out how to get more medicine!" Chen Xin rubbed his chin, contemplating which medicines he still needed to prepare: "It’s a pity Doctor Hong Rui had some problems at home, otherwise, his place would have been a good spot to get medicine, but now he’s at Xiao Long’s, I don’t feel right asking him for medicine."

It’s not that Chen Xin thought there would be a problem asking Li Yulong for medicines, but going to Doctor Hong Rui would definitely let Li Yulong know, and if Li Yulong knew about this, given his character, the medicines wouldn’t be exchanged but simply given.

Although Li Yulong had requests for Chen Xin and Chen Xin did help him with some things, overall, Chen Xin felt he owed Li Yulong and couldn’t just keep taking advantage of him.

Moreover, personal debts are easy to incur and hard to repay; too many of such matters might affect the relationship between Chen Xin and Li Yulong in the future.

Thus, unless necessary, Chen Xin preferred to exchange for some necessary drugs from the hospital through the city government on his own accord.

Besides, there was another benefit to this approach, which was that hospital doctors were obviously more professional, and the medicines prepared could better handle various situations.

——————————————————

Having realized the inadequacies, one must immediately make up for them.

Chen Xin did not delay. When the city government staff came to deliver the second batch of materials and take away the three large geothermal heat pump devices, he mentioned his need for a batch of medicine to the person in charge of delivering materials.

This person in charge had replaced Secretary Li and was mainly responsible for the exchange of materials and equipment between Chen Xin and the city.

After hearing Chen Xin’s requests, he agreed to help Chen Xin contact the hospital and ask them to prepare a batch of medicines for him. He even proactively invited Chen Xin to return to the city with him to get the medicines directly from the hospital.

Certainly, Chen Xin had no objections, so after completing the exchange of materials and equipment, he went back to the city with the person in charge in the city government’s car.

Upon reaching the hospital, the person in charge sought out hospital personnel to help Chen Xin allocate the medicines, while Chen Xin sat idly in the hospital lobby, waiting.

The lobby of the hospital wasn’t very different from before. Although it’s after the apocalypse and the hospital was converted from a shelter, it was still bustling with patients as it was pre-disaster.

Only now Chen Xin couldn’t take out his mobile phone to play games as he used to and could only sit bored, watching "Tom and Jerry" playing on the TV hanging in the lobby.

Just as Chen Xin was feeling bored to the point of yawning, suddenly from behind, there came a surprised voice: "Junior Chen?"

Chen Xin was a bit surprised too, as only one person would call him that.

As he turned his head and stood up from the chair, the person appeared before him, the very person who would call him like that: "Senior?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 213 - 211: Senior from the Past
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"How come you’re here, Senior?" Chen Xin looked surprised at the person appearing in front of him, as he never imagined seeing his senior again ten years after graduation.

"I’m here on a business trip. Xiao Chen junior, this is... Oh, I remember now, your home is here. During the club activities back then, you even brought lotus pods." The senior seemed equally surprised to meet Chen Xin here and greeted him while also sizing up this old classmate whom she hadn’t seen in a decade.

Due to being out, Chen Xin was wearing his own Protective Suit. Although he had taken off the protective mask indoors, one could still tell at a glance that it was an excellent Protective Suit, not easily available for purchase.

Compared to the ordinary warm clothing of people around him, Chen Xin’s attire clearly made him stand out.

Moreover, after not seeing each other for many years, the senior’s memory of Chen Xin still lingered in their past student days. Back then, Chen Xin didn’t possess the steadiness and the temperament honed by time like now; he was spirited, confident yet somewhat naive, which was how the senior remembered him.

But after many years, the current Chen Xin is no longer that naive young man, and the senior is no longer the gentle woman with long hair and a light-colored long dress in Chen Xin’s memory.

Looking at the senior now with her neat short hair and a black winter coat, Chen Xin greeted her with a hint of nostalgia, "Really long time no see. Since you graduated, it’s been ten years, right?"

"From my graduation, it’s been over ten years." The senior gazed at Chen Xin, similarly feeling nostalgic. "It’s been ten years in a blink of an eye! How are you doing now, Xiao Chen junior?"

"Not so bad. In these times, no one can claim to be doing well; just managing to survive." Chen Xin shook his head slightly, not dwelling on the topic. "And you, Senior? I remember after you graduated, you seemed to have taken the civil service exam, right?"

The senior nodded and showed Chen Xin the national emblem pinned to her collar. "The Provincial Discipline Inspection Commission, it’s alright. Since there was such a big incident in your city, I was sent by the province to investigate some matters."

The senior didn’t elaborate on what exactly she was investigating, as that was confidential. But with people from the Provincial Discipline Inspection Commission appearing in the city after such a major event, it was clear what their purpose might be.

So when the senior said she was investigating matters, it wasn’t considered leakage.

The name of the Discipline Inspection Commission made Chen Xin feel a chill, but he couldn’t help but laugh. Even if the senior came wielding the imperial sword to slay traitors, what concern was it to him, an ordinary citizen?

If that were true, he should be applauding and calling her a wise and just official.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but smile, "Such a choice is typical of you, Senior. Back then, when I heard you were taking the civil service exam, I thought you’d go to another agency!"

"My family wanted me to go to the Tax Bureau, but I chose my current job to fulfill my original ideal." The senior said this with curiosity about Chen Xin’s path, "What about you, Xiao Chen junior? I remember you said you wanted to ’write the history of spring and autumn, and depict life’s injustices’."

Recalling his slightly idealistic, youthful aspirations, a trace of embarrassment appeared on Chen Xin’s face. Although at the time he didn’t think of himself as naive, looking back now, there was some self-consciousness in retrospect.

However, he still jokingly replied to the senior, "I later chose to write online novels, fulfilling the first half of my dream at least. But as for life’s injustices, even if I wanted to write, I couldn’t publish them!"

Hearing Chen Xin’s words, the senior couldn’t help but laugh, "In school, we all thought we should set our hearts for the world, establish a destiny for the people, continue the learning of the past sages, and bring peace for all generations. But when you really do it, you realize it’s much harder than the ideal.

Knowing what to do is easy, doing it is hard. It’s already not easy, Xiao Chen junior, for you to be doing what you want to do. We’ve all been changed by time..."

The senior seemed to want to say more, but the person in charge of preparing the medicines for Chen Xin had already returned.

Seeing the senior standing beside Chen Xin, he was surprised and quickly greeted her, "Leader Tang, what brings you here? Do you know Comrade Xiao Chen?"

"He’s my university junior; we even set up a club together back then." The senior briefly explained her relationship with Chen Xin but seemed to emphasize that they hadn’t been in contact since university to avoid causing any unnecessary trouble for Chen Xin. "Unfortunately, we lost touch after graduation, so to meet again over a decade later is really something."

"Oh, so Comrade Xiao Chen is Leader Tang’s junior? That’s certainly unexpected!" The person in charge, hearing this, looked at Chen Xin with a different gaze, a tone growing more amiable, "By the way, Comrade Xiao Chen, here are the medications you need. They’re mainly for preventing frostbite and common illnesses caused by cold, along with some common drugs; hope they help you."

"Thank you so much; with these medicines, I feel more at ease." Chen Xin accepted the medical box offered by the person in charge, expressing his thanks.

"Xiao Chen junior, this is..." The senior noticed the considerable medicine box in Chen Xin’s hand and couldn’t help showing concern, clearly not for regular use.

"I’m planning to go to Anhui Province; that’s where my family is, and my parents were stranded there before the disaster. I’m planning to bring them back now." Chen Xin explained to the senior.

However, Chen Xin’s explanation startled the senior. She widened her eyes at Chen Xin in disbelief, "Are you really going to Anhui Province, Xiao Chen junior? How will you get there? In this environment, planes can’t fly, and the railway isn’t open for civilian use. How are you going to go?"

"I’ll drive myself." Chen Xin didn’t find the senior’s shock surprising; he just showed her the medical box in his hand. "That’s why I need to prepare more medicines for the journey, in case of emergencies along the way we can handle it."

Hearing this, the senior found herself speechless. She had never expected that their reunion after ten years would lead to Chen Xin heading out on what seemed to her an almost reckless endeavor.

"Is your car ready? Driving yourself, a civilian car would freeze overnight; you wouldn’t be able to drive at all!" The senior still found Chen Xin’s plan unrealistic and wanted to dissuade him.

Below freezing temperatures can be quite harsh for cars, making them difficult to start unless the engine remains in operation; otherwise, the cold could freeze the vehicle and damage parts and equipment not built for such temperatures.

Chen Xin had naturally prepared for this, smiling at the senior, "You don’t have to worry about that, Senior. When have you ever seen me dive into something unprepared? I’ve readied an RV and made modifications suitable for the current environment."

"That does sound like you, very thorough as always, Xiao Chen junior. I suppose I was overthinking." The senior shook her head, laughing at her needless worry, deciding against persuading Chen Xin otherwise, and extended her hand, "All I can do then is wish you a safe journey, Xiao Chen junior! If you have some time upon your return, you can find me in Star City easily."

"Thank you, Senior, for your blessing. I’ll take my leave first and visit you once I’m back." Chen Xin grinned at her and then turned away, medication box in hand.

However, before Chen Xin got too far, the person in charge called out to him, arranging for a car to send him back.

————————————————

Back at his shelter, Chen Xin sorted the medical supplies he’d brought back, transferring them to his armored RV.

While organizing the medicines, Chen Xin couldn’t help but reflect on unexpectedly seeing his senior again.

Though he called her Senior, she was only half a year older than Chen Xin, and they were actually the same class year. But as they grew more familiar, she playfully used her age as a reason for Chen Xin to always call her Senior, while she called him Xiao Chen junior.

Back then, although the senior appeared gentle, she was quite strong-willed, joining the student council in her first year and even dragging Chen Xin along to set up a club, making her quite a notable figure at school.

Chen Xin remembered their second year; she successfully became the student council’s vice president. For issues like cafeteria food quality, she organized students to co-sign a letter to the school, pushing for improvements...

During her two years as vice president, she accomplished quite a lot, thanks to which she smoothly became the student council president in her last year on campus.

But since everyone was nearing graduation, with internships and little energy to focus on student council work, her presidency didn’t leave much of an impact on Chen Xin. Rather, he remembered more about her tenure as vice president.
