The warmth of the bed calmed Chen Xin’s emotions and gave him the mind to think about what had just happened.

In fact, the goals of the relative he wasn’t familiar with weren’t hard to guess. Although they seemed evasive, if Chen Xin recalled their words, it was clear that their aim was to build connections with him.

But why would a relative suddenly come to build connections with him, a completely unfamiliar relative?

This left Chen Xin puzzled, not understanding the situation.

To Chen Xin, the village relatives weren’t particularly close, even the familiar elders like his second uncle. He would only see them once or twice during the year when visiting with his parents.

Without parental connections, Chen Xin might be closer to some acquaintances than to these relatives.

People don’t offer kindness without reason, so this unknown relative approaching to build connections and purposely choosing a time close to bedtime was obviously to avoid others’ attention, which puzzled Chen Xin.

Building connections isn’t something shameful, so why not do it during the day? Why choose a time when I’m about to sleep?

Moreover, building connections wasn’t the relative’s main purpose; it was clear they wanted something from him, but the distant relationship made it hard to say.

So what did they want? Chen Xin pondered this question in his mind until he fell asleep.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 257 - 255: The Relatives’ Intentions
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin thought that the relatives coming to him to pull connections were just an isolated case, but it turned out it was not an isolated incident.

Early the next morning, Chen Xin got to his car, had breakfast with his parents, and mentioned the plan to let his cousin’s husband take his car to the county to exchange for supplies. His parents then told him that not only had relatives come to seek him out last night, but some had also reached out to them.

Chen Xin’s parents were naturally easier to deal with than him. After living here for half a year, any relatives, no matter how unfamiliar they were at first, had become familiar over this time.

"So what do they want?" Chen Xin felt very puzzled and asked his parents.

These relatives were neither close nor distant, and by detailed analysis, they weren’t even within the fifth degree of kinship. This made Chen Xin very curious about what these people wanted.

As for Chen Xin’s confusion, his parents were much more perceptive. Chen Xin’s father laughed and said, "Have you ever heard the saying, when you’re poor no one cares in the bustling city, but when you’re rich you have distant relatives in remote mountains? These people are your mom’s distant relatives, not even yours technically, but now that it seems things are going well for you, naturally, they want to come and catch some of your good fortune."

"What’s so great about this good fortune? Look at the world we live in now; it’s already a feat to take care of myself and a few close relatives and friends, where do I find the time to care for distant relatives!" Chen Xin couldn’t help but complain.

As he said this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but scoff, "And even if when you’re rich, distant relatives come, what can they gain from me? Once I help Uncle with the equipment upgrades, I’m returning to Xiang Province. Do they plan to follow me to Xiang Province?"

However, as he spoke, Chen Xin noticed the look in his parents’ eyes, and he suddenly understood while also finding it hard to believe.

He looked at his parents and asked, "Do they really think like that? Are they crazy?"

"Crazy? They’re smart!" Chen Xin’s mom gave him an annoyed look without explaining further or saying any good words for those relatives of distant relation. It seemed she left everything to Chen Xin to think about and judge.

Yet Chen Xin’s father, seeing Chen Xin still puzzled, offered him a hint: "Do you think your current life is bad?"

Hearing his father’s reminder, Chen Xin immediately realized.

Is his own life bad? Certainly not!

If Chen Xin said his life was bad, in the current apocalypse, probably no one would dare say theirs was good.

In a private shelter, not worrying about food and drink with a stable supply of resources. As long as there’s no world-destroying catastrophe or a disaster severe enough to change terrain, with his current resources, even with complete disconnection from the outside, Chen Xin could survive in his shelter.

Moreover, he had the capacity to travel across four provinces and cover more than six hundred kilometers to bring his parents back home—something those struggling on the survival line could only dream of.

Especially the supplies Chen Xin took out after reaching his hometown shelter. Other foods aside, the dried meat was genuinely scarce now—it’s something ordinary folks could only dream of just smelling.

But Chen Xin could directly take out a box of dried meat to give away, indicating that the resources in his hand were abundant enough to do such things.

This inevitably made Chen Xin a bit annoyed with his recklessness, focusing only on bringing some gifts for relatives and forgetting the saying not to reveal one’s wealth.

Reflecting on this, Chen Xin also pondered whether these people really had it so bad that they wanted to follow him to Xiang Province.

However, after some consideration, he found that compared to him, these people’s lives weren’t going well.

Before Chen Xin arrived, they could still get by but with resource scarcity and heating shortages, their living conditions were nowhere near good, just slightly better than the nearby village shelters where people might starve to death.

As for the town and county situation, perhaps it wasn’t so bad, but considering the high number of people with hypothermia in town and frostbite cases in the county, one could imagine their lives weren’t very ideal either.

Through such comparisons, Chen Xin realized that if it were him, he might make the same choice to go with a relative who was clearly doing well rather than staying to suffer hunger and cold.

It only required letting go of a bit of pride to connect with distant relatives, surely a worthwhile deal.

Whether Chen Xin was willing to take them along, for them it was naturally a gamble, but just a bit of face was at stake. If they won, and Chen Xin couldn’t bear not to take them, then even if it was just taking them to the city where Chen Xin stayed in Xiang Province, it would be better than freezing in the village, right?

Bear in mind, the city Chen Xin resides in is at least a prefecture-level city, four levels higher than the village, the Flame Country’s lowest administrative unit, and its resource supplies and living conditions could match those in the upper administrative unit Anqing City, where the county is located.

For people in the village, even starving and freezing in the city is better than staying in the village.

In facing such a scenario, all Chen Xin could do was shake his head with helplessness.

No matter how you put it, these people could still be considered relatives, albeit distant ones, Chen Xin wouldn’t truly take them along, nor did he need to show a harsh face, after all, you don’t hit a smiling face.

Moreover, to be honest, Chen Xin did consider taking a few people back, with the idea of increasing the manpower in his shelter.

His shelter could currently accommodate at least ten people, but with just him and his parents, there was some waste involved.

Furthermore, such a large shelter with only Chen Xin and his parents meant many things would be too busy to handle, and with his bone fracture, even back home, he wouldn’t be able to perform heavy labor for at least two to three months. His parents were also aged, and the workforce issue indeed needed consideration.

But even if he had to bring people back, Chen Xin would consider his real relatives, not those beyond five degrees of kinship.

This also reminded Chen Xin that perhaps he should mention this to Uncle, to see if any suitable relatives would be willing to go to his place. Chen Xin thought that if there were such relatives, he wouldn’t mind taking them.

As for exactly what kind of people to bring back, Chen Xin thought that it required careful consideration because in such an apocalypse, personal abilities are secondary—the key is trustworthy character, otherwise, it would be akin to inviting a wolf into the house.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 258 - 256: The True Nature of the System
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After lending his car to his cousin’s husband, who was responsible for driving, and some relatives going to the county for resource exchange, Chen Xin returned to the shelter to find his second uncle, intending to see what could be done with the materials exchanged yesterday.

The cable needed for circuit modification wasn’t exchanged at the town yesterday, but he did manage to get car batteries for the power upgrade. With these, Chen Xin can first create an Isotope Thermoelectric Battery to assemble a power supply battery group for the shelter.

The second uncle, knowing Chen Xin’s agenda for the day, brought him to the warehouse. After taking out the materials exchanged yesterday, he asked Chen Xin, "Axin, is there anything you need help with today? Should I call two people to give you a hand?"

Upon hearing the second uncle’s words, Chen Xin shook his head: "Unless they study nuclear physics, they won’t be of much help, and there isn’t much physical work today. Just help me move things, that’ll do."

Seeing Chen Xin’s response, the second uncle did not insist. However, after helping Chen Xin deliver two car batteries and some materials to his room, the second uncle still called his nephew to assist Chen Xin.

Seeing the nephew whom the second uncle called, who ought to be addressed as a cousin, looking eager, Chen Xin shook his head, understanding the second uncle’s intention.

"Cousin, you help move the things inside first; I have something to discuss with Uncle," Chen Xin instructed his cousin nearby before pulling his second uncle aside to ask, "Uncle, is there something you want to say to me?"

"It’s nothing..." The second uncle understood that Chen Xin had seen through his intention, but failing to save face, he still wanted to downplay the situation with laughter.

However, Chen Xin shook his head and said to his uncle, "The people who came to me last night, and those who went to find my parents... Uncle, you should know what they want to do, right? Some things, even if I have to do them, I’ll definitely find trustworthy relatives, definitely not those unrelated by eight poles."

Uncertain of the second uncle’s intentions, Chen Xin didn’t directly expose the aim of those relatives. Still, he brought some matters to light, believing his uncle could understand his meaning.

Sure enough, the second uncle nodded to Chen Xin, smiling, "Rest assured, the people I recommend to you are definitely trustworthy relatives who won’t cause you trouble."

Of course, Chen Xin had enough trust in his uncle, but he wasn’t sure about the so-called trustworthy relatives he mentioned.

However, he also didn’t want to directly refute his uncle’s face, so Chen Xin thought for a moment and said, "The people you recommend are definitely trustworthy, but I still need to test them where it matters. Whether a person is trustworthy, ability is one thing, but character is more important."

"Well said, you test them as you see fit. If there are any problems, come to me, and I’ll resolve them." His uncle reassured Chen Xin by patting his chest.

"Having your assurance, uncle, I’m relieved. When there are suitable candidates, you can let them come to find me." Chen Xin was satisfied with this outcome, exchanged a few more words with his uncle, then returned to his room.

————————————————

Upgrading ordinary car batteries to Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries is something that’s not worth describing for Chen Xin anymore.

But for those who have never seen it, it’s still something beyond a normal person’s understanding.

After experiencing the incident where those researchers in the three towns recorded his upgrade process, Chen Xin pondered what this process was about, but what he could liken it to were only those indescribable horrors in the Cthulhu mythos.

Because what was seen and recorded has already surpassed ordinary understanding, it’s incomprehensible and undecipherable, and even if observed and heard, what is obtained is merely meaningless or already distorted information.

But for those with enough accumulated knowledge or a keen interest in exploring, they can instead extract fragments of the original information from this meaningless and distorted data, comprehending bits and pieces.

Even compared to the true information originally contained, these pieces are like a drop in the ocean, but it’s within the range a normal person can analyze and comprehend, even if these fragments might drive one insane.

Perhaps this is why those who pursue and explore the indescribable beings in the Cthulhu mythos went insane; they weren’t lunatics, they merely absorbed too much knowledge beyond normal comprehension.

Of course, the system Chen Xin holds might only share the same level of information as those indescribable beings, not that the system itself is an indescribable entity, nor that the system possesses such distorting and corrosive powers.

However, this is something Chen Xin himself dares not confirm, for the concept of information state is vague, and the system’s inherent capabilities in disguise and confusion also, in a certain sense, is a kind of distortion and erosion. Compared to indescribable horrors that distort and twist only the external form of material, this ability to warp and alter the internal concept and essence of a substance is much more terrifying.

The so-called vagueness of concept requires some imagination, maybe even a creative leap, to understand.

To illustrate, suppose the world we reside in now is a virtual network world, where everything is composed of data and information, and each thing has its attributes. Then, the system’s information state vagueness is akin to directly modifying an item’s data from the background, altering its attributes, and making everyone who sees it not find any strangeness in the change of data and attributes.

This is extremely terrifying and horrifying; to put it simply, it’s like a hacker who can arbitrarily alter world data, where the entire real world, in the system’s eyes, is merely a virtual game where data can be freely modified.

It’s just that now this hacker’s capabilities are being restricted and constrained. The permissions Chen Xin holds control the hacker’s powers, while survival points are the resources or energy required to use this capability.

And various constraints on Chen Xin’s usage of the system’s function are just some trivial rules to prevent Chen Xin from breaking the game.

No wonder this system’s initial description claimed it to be an entertainment system. For the high civilization that could create it, a material world, planet, or civilization on the verge of destruction might indeed just be a game.

Ultimately, to make such a capability an entertainment project for this higher civilization, to rewind destroyed worlds, planets, and civilizations, may be as simple as reloading a save file, akin to a game’s load state.

As for why this system appeared before him, and whether there is some conspiracy behind it, Chen Xin couldn’t care less, for pondering on it holds no meaning.

After all, does a high civilization capable of producing such a level of technology need any conspiracies to invade a world like Earth?

Directly modifying a "datum" of a robotic vacuum, transforming a household cleaning robot into Emperor Titan, Rampaging First Unit, Getter Emperor, Demon God Z...conquering Earth is merely a matter of altering a couple more data sets.

No one truly believes that the higher civilization responsible for this system needs to expend survival points to "upgrade" something, right?

Even if they needed Chen Xin to accomplish something, can Chen Xin ensure his memory hasn’t been tampered with? Altering memory isn’t a difficult task; in a way, even humanity on Earth can achieve it.

So thinking about whether there’s any conspiracy behind it is merely creating trouble and worry for oneself, because even if there were conspiracies, it wouldn’t be something a regular person like Chen Xin could change or prevent.

Instead of creating problems for himself, it’s better to utilize the system’s functions well, to improve life for himself and his family, allowing them to live better in this post-apocalyptic world.

——————————————————

In the town hospital, Zeng Liu finished her day’s work, greeted the colleague taking over her shift, completed the handover, and then left the pharmacy, heading towards the dormitory.

After being sent to this small town by Chen Xin, and having a pharmacist’s practicing qualification, Zeng Liu was naturally assigned to work in the pharmacy.

Though other medical departments needed staff more urgently, pharmacists with professional qualifications were hard to find in the town. With the town’s population now soaring—even when a whole village migrated over, bringing staff including a town clinic—the pharmacy still couldn’t keep up with demand due to a shortage of pharmacists.

Zeng Liu’s timely arrival allowed the pharmacy to evolve from two shifts to three shifts, reducing each pharmacist’s work time from 12 hours to a normal 8-hour shift.

The town hospital was also a complete shelter, with the dormitory naturally within the shelter’s scope. However, as Zeng Liu returned to the dormitory area, she found Old Tang and Sister Ning standing at her room door waiting for her.

"Zeng Liu, can we have a chat?" Sister Ning asked her purpose to Zeng Liu after greeting her when she saw her return.

"Of course, what do you want to talk about, Sister Ning?" Zeng Liu looked at Sister Ning and Old Tang, cheerfully inviting them into her room: "Come in, the dormitory assigned to pharmacists. My roommate just swapped shifts with me, so I’m the only one here now."

Once she brought Sister Ning and Old Tang into her room and poured them both a glass of water, Zeng Liu sat down, eager to know why they sought her out.

"Zeng Liu, do you still keep in touch with Mr. Chen Xin who brought us here?" Sister Ning asked about her and Old Tang’s purpose for visiting Zeng Liu.

"Mr. Chen Xin? Do you need to find him?" Zeng Liu looked at Sister Ning, not understanding.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 259 - 257: Young People Who Understand Finance
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Everyone wants to live a good life, and this is something no one can avoid.

If someone deliberately seeks to live a hard life, they either have great ambitions and are honing their will, or they have a strong faith and are purifying their spirit through suffering.

Apart from these two kinds of people, the rest of the ordinary people still hope and yearn for a better life.

Before Chen Xin returned to the village, the villagers’ lives were somewhat better than those in nearby villages, but it was still very limited and couldn’t really be called good.

After Chen Xin came back, the villagers’ lives noticeably improved.

The restoration of heating was the biggest change impacting the shelter where the village was located, and this was only the beginning.

The plan that Chen Xin had to upgrade the heating equipment and expand the greenhouse wasn’t a secret, and Uncle was not hiding it from the villagers. Influenced by this information, the villagers naturally thought that Chen Xin could bring them a better life.

If it were just this, the villagers wouldn’t be flocking around Chen Xin.

But Chen Xin came back only to fetch his parents. He is still staying in the village because he has some family ties in the village and wants to do something for them.

Once Chen Xin finishes these matters, he will still leave.

Ordinary villagers might simply think that Chen Xin’s presence made their lives better than before, but for those with a bit more thought, they naturally equate "Chen Xin = good life," understanding that following Chen Xin would lead to a better life.

This isn’t a difficult concept to understand. Most people in the village, though mainly farmers, have only a small portion who have worked elsewhere. This might mean they have less experience, but it does not mean they are stupid.

After realizing that following Chen Xin could lead to a better life, the first group to put aside their pride sought out Chen Xin or his parents the night before. Those close to Chen Xin’s family also began to approach Uncle, who is the village head, showcasing their skills and beginning to take action.

For those who reacted slightly slower, found it harder to swallow their pride, and were not very close to Chen Xin’s family, they also wanted to live a good life through Chen Xin, but there was relatively less they could do.

There were still some who felt they had decent abilities, recommending themselves directly to Chen Xin, straightforwardly hoping for him to take them to live a "good life."

Seeing the young man in front of him, wearing glasses and a suit, clearly dressed up for the occasion, Chen Xin’s expression was a bit strange. Nonetheless, he stroked his chin and asked, "You hope I will take you with me when I leave? That’s not a problem, but give me a reason to take you."

Speaking of which, ever since quitting his job to become a full-time online writer, Chen Xin hadn’t faced such interview-like or recruitment-like situations for a long time, although he had participated in a few interviews and hosted two recruitment sessions previously.

However, the current situation could count as a form of recruitment. Chen Xin wasn’t recruiting company employees but rather members for his own team.

This also inspired Chen Xin. In this apocalypse, he indeed needed to form a team. Whether in games or films, the protagonist usually has a group or companions. The situation of fighting alone is rare.

Just like in online games, where a complete team of specific roles is needed to tackle instances, in surviving the apocalypse, a diverse team is definitely more capable of facing crises and problems than going solo.

So, what kind of companions does he need?

While pondering this question, Chen Xin looked at the guy in front, who clearly regarded this as an interview, listening to his self-introduction.

"I graduated from one of the prestigious 985 universities domestically, with a major in finance. Previously I worked at an investment company in the provincial capital, and I have handled..." The guy in front of Chen Xin seemed to treat this as an interview and naturally recited his resume, but when he spoke about his work experience, Chen Xin directly interrupted him.

Seeing the man’s confusion, Chen Xin casually shook his hand and told him, "It’s already the apocalypse now. What you did before is not important. Tell me what you can do now."

"I am skilled in financial analysis, market investment, stock market predictions..." The young man spoke again, explaining his professional skills that he was quite proud of, but he was once again interrupted by Chen Xin.

Facing the man’s extremely puzzled and bewildered eyes, Chen Xin helplessly asked him, "Besides financial sector skills that aren’t applicable, do you know anything else?"

"How can the finance industry be useless! It’s clearly the ultimate solution to all conflicts!" The young man was clearly disgruntled with Chen Xin for deeming his abilities as pointless, arguing back quite unhappily.

However, upon hearing his words, Chen Xin began to chuckle, even to the point of having a slight bellyache: "Finance is the ultimate solution to all conflicts? Hahaha, indeed, allowing so-called financiers to plunder everything through financial investments and destroy everything does solve all conflicts."

"On what grounds can you say this? Financial investments clearly bring vitality and the necessary funds for development to entrepreneurs and industries lacking capital!" The young man, despite initially trying to ingratiate himself with Chen Xin in hopes of being taken along when Chen Xin leaves, began to argue.

Faced with the young man’s rebuttal, Chen Xin calmly said to him, "Finance itself doesn’t create any wealth or value. It’s simply an economic behavior where market participants transfer funds from the surplus side to the deficit side through various financial tools. This behavior is not problematic in itself, and indeed it’s a normal economic activity that fosters societal progression.

But financiers have exploited this economic behavior, turning ordinary monetary exchanges into plundering and destruction. No matter how those in the financial realm try to beautify this act, they can’t cover up the fact that bankers and investors use financial behaviors to commit acts of plunder."

Chen Xin continued, seemingly noticing the young man’s dissatisfied expression, and said with a smile, "You mentioned that financial investment brings vitality and development to entrepreneurs and industries lacking funds. I don’t deny that.

But how many entrepreneurs were ousted from the companies they founded due to so-called financial investments? And how many industries crashed from promising sunrise industries to neglected sunset industries because of capital greed?"

"But, but..." The young man wanted to argue but couldn’t refute these truths.

"If all these don’t convince you, I’ll give you another example. Isn’t the famous Asian financial crisis of the ’90s initially caused by the Federation’s financial investors’ speculation on Siam’s currency?" As an online writer, Chen Xin had researched substantially on the financial crisis of those years. Although many factors affected it, the speculation on Siam’s currency was indeed a catalyst for the crisis, making it a suitable case to persuade the young man in front of him.

Sure enough, Chen Xin’s words left the young man speechless. The financier who orchestrated the attack on Siam’s currency was revered as a god in the international finance community, and his most successful and glorious case was this one, frequently used and elaborated upon in many finance textbooks.

Of course, the young man had learned about this financial case and was well-acquainted with its consequences.

Because of the Asian financial crisis sparked by the financier’s speculation on Siam’s currency, the entire Asia region experienced economic decline, stagnation over the ensuing two decades, from rapid economic development.

The four Asian Tigers, once recorded in textbooks, nearly fell into economic stagnation for the next twenty years, with only Flame Country avoiding the financial crisis’s impact due to blocking the financier’s actions at the time.

"But in your view, isn’t there a beneficial side to finance? After all, financial investment is permitted under national policies, and finance is a legitimate university course!" The young man clearly didn’t want the degree he obtained after years of study and his professional achievements dismissed in a single sentence, striving to argue with Chen Xin.

Chen Xin merely shook his head, stating nonchalantly, "Financial actions aren’t wrong inherently. If used well, they indeed promote economic development significantly and drive social progress. But unregulated and uncontrolled financial acts cause societal destruction and disaster.

Honestly, I don’t think financial actions have much use in this apocalypse. The country is already fully controlling resources; all production materials are controlled by the state. Currency basically is akin to waste paper now. In such a scenario, practical skills in tangible industries are what actually matter.

In this apocalyptic state and in the coming decades, I really don’t think something like the financial industry, entirely based on the monetary system and producing no intrinsic value, has much importance.

So if you don’t have other skills and only know these things, then I’m sorry, you’re of no use to me because I have no money to invest."

Upon hearing Chen Xin, the young man appeared quite dejected. However, he didn’t leave but after some contemplation, he asked Chen Xin, "I’ve been working at a financial investment company for years, and I’m fairly articulate and good with people. Could this be useful to you?"

"That’s not too bad. If I truly can’t find enough people, or there’s a spot available in the vehicle, it’s not impossible to bring you along." Chen Xin thought for a moment and decided not to outright reject him.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 260 - 258: Income After Upgrading
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

There was more than one relative who came to find Chen Xin, each with a variety of skills.

Besides the young man who initially studied finance, among the other relatives who came to find Chen Xin, some knew masonry, having worked in construction teams before; some were merely village farmers who knew only farming; others had worked as electricians and understood some electrical technology...

The appearance of these various people somewhat annoyed Chen Xin, but he also got to see the skills of these relatives.

It must be said that the village really had all kinds of people, including even a Taoist priest who usually conducted funeral ceremonies from various villages and towns. This gave Chen Xin a fresh feeling, yet he couldn’t help but feel amused.

Among these people, some were undoubtedly very useful to Chen Xin. Even farmers who only knew how to farm could be extremely valuable in this apocalypse.

Even the one who studied finance before—Chen Xin thought he could put him in the black market that Li Yulong was preparing to open, to work as a salesperson there, making good use of his talents.

However, some people really left Chen Xin unsure about what they could do, such as the profession of a Taoist priest. Although in the past, this profession did indeed have market demand in rural areas, needing someone to play music and chant scriptures when the elderly passed away, now in this apocalypse, the dead are directly taken away to be cremated. Who has the mind to hold a funeral?

Nonetheless, Chen Xin earnestly made a paper record of the relatives who had come to him, intending to discuss with his parents seriously before deciding whether to take some of them back with him.

While Chen Xin was busy dealing with relatives trying to connect with him, his cousin-in-law and second uncle also finally got hold of the supplies he needed, with the list that Chen Xin had reorganized.

"Now it’s better, with these supplies, the related transformations can be carried out." At the warehouse door of the shelter, watching as the second uncle had people move the supplies into the warehouse, Chen Xin nodded in satisfaction and happily told the second uncle.

The second uncle was also very pleased. As the village chief, the pressure on him had actually been very high since the disaster began, as the lives of more than a hundred people in the village were all on his shoulders.

Now that Chen Xin said he could improve the villagers’ living conditions, it finally allowed him to breathe a sigh of relief, feeling much more relaxed.

"So when can you start the renovations, Xin?" This was the question the second uncle was most concerned about.

"Let’s start tomorrow. First, upgrade the circuits, and by the way, I’ll set up the workbench to process these raw materials, so the greenhouse can be transformed next." Chen Xin explained his plan to the second uncle and detailed: "The greenhouse transformation will increase power consumption. After upgrading the heating system, the power consumption will also rise, so we need to upgrade the circuits first, to avoid overload issues in the future."

The second uncle had no objections to Chen Xin’s arrangements, only nodding and continuously agreeing: "You go ahead, Xin, do whatever you think is right. If you need anything, don’t hesitate to ask. If there’s not enough manpower, just use people from the village! If anyone has any issues, let them come to me."

"Don’t worry, second uncle, I know what to do." Chen Xin nodded, indicating that there would be no problems, seeing how supportive the second uncle was.

————————————————

With enough materials, upgrading the circuits was no longer as troublesome as before, when they had to find an electrician to connect the batteries to the circuit.

Chen Xin simply had someone move his two newly made Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries to the distribution room, along with a bundle of cables brought by his cousin-in-law and the others, and directly opened the system interface to upgrade them.

While he connected three Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries to the circuit, Chen Xin didn’t allow all three batteries to supply power to the circuit simultaneously.

After all, the total power of the three Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries was as high as 450KW, and this was merely a village-level shelter that didn’t require such a high amount of electricity.

Even if Chen Xin completed the upgrades for the heating system and greenhouse, and combined them with daily household electricity consumption, two batteries would be sufficient. The third battery would only be used as a backup.

Though the setup seemed somewhat wasteful, for Chen Xin, it wasn’t a major investment. Perhaps because he used to favor two-on-one backup plans in past projects, he did it this way naturally.

Upon completing the circuit upgrades, Chen Xin promptly received feedback from the system:

"Improved survival conditions for others’ shelter, earned 5000 survival points."

"Improved survival conditions for others, acquiring +50 survival points daily."

Looking at the system’s prompts, Chen Xin nodded in satisfaction and recalculated how many survival points he could earn if he completed all three upgrade projects.

Before setting out, due to the overall upgrade of his shelter, Chen Xin had almost depleted his survival points. During nearly a month’s journey, gaining over a hundred survival points each day, he managed to accumulate back around three thousand points.

Previously, by helping the village’s shelter restore heating and now upgrading the circuits, Chen Xin had altogether gained a total of ten thousand survival points and a daily increase of 100 survival points.

If he could obtain the maximum survival points after completing the greenhouse and heating system upgrades, Chen Xin could earn a total of twenty thousand points from this hometown shelter, with an additional 200 daily survival points, which would be quite a substantial income.

Calculated in this way, Chen Xin estimated that he would reach a stockpile of around twenty-three thousand survival points, increasing by over three hundred points daily.

This exceeded the total survival points that Chen Xin had accumulated over half a year, transforming his own shelter before.

It made Chen Xin deeply understand that working alone like the Ten-mile Slope Sword God had no future. Actively cooperating with others and helping to improve living conditions in other shelters was the correct way to earn large amounts of survival points.

If he had initially chosen to go and upgrade official shelters, his total survival points could have perhaps exceeded six figures by now.

After all, many projects could be undertaken for shelter upgrades, with electricity and heating projects as unavoidable necessities. Chen Xin could have adopted this strategy in each shelter, earning six figures of survival points after handling seven or eight shelters.

With so many survival points, Chen Xin developed some new ideas about how to use them. Perhaps some original ideas and plans, which had been shelved due to a lack of survival points, could be considered again.

With these thoughts in mind, Chen Xin first set his sights on his vehicle. He had many ideas still unimplemented on this car, and now, with enough survival points, he might allow himself some luxury.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 261 - 259: Vehicle Second Upgrade
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A relative in the village who used to be a carpenter made him a workbench, and after spending a few hundred points for a casual upgrade, Chen Xin processed all the raw materials intended for upgrading the heating system and the greenhouse into corresponding components.

With these components, the upgrade of the heating system and the modification of the greenhouse were also smoothly completed, earning Chen Xin a substantial income of survival points.

After finishing these tasks, Chen Xin was also preparing to take his parents back home.

This immediately alarmed many who wanted to leave this impoverished mountain village through his connections, hoping to lead a better life, and they began approaching him more frequently, hoping to catch his eye and leave with him.

To achieve this, these people, aside from volunteering themselves and showcasing their talents, also resorted to various underhanded methods and diverse tricks.

Making familial connections and leveraging relationships were considered basic strategies since the whole village was somewhat related, with a significant number being within Chen Xin’s three generations. If one really played the family card, only those with closer ties to Chen Xin’s family could be considered intimate.

So other methods were expected, such as inviting Chen Xin for meals or giving gifts.

Although there wasn’t much of value in the shelter, the village was nearby, and before moving into the shelter, villagers had enough time to hide their valuable items or store them properly.

However, once-valuable items in peaceful times were no longer worth much in this apocalyptic era, as antiques of prosperity and gold in chaos lost their value.

Jewelry couldn’t be eaten or worn for warmth, and even if sold on the black market, they were often worth less than a single carrot.

For Chen Xin, these supposedly valuable items from relatives held no appeal; partly, he honestly didn’t care for them, and partly to avoid trouble, he rejected them one by one.

To avoid pestering relatives and make himself more comfortable, Chen Xin simply moved back to his vehicle.

The conditions in the vehicle were much better than those in the shelter. His parents had already stayed on the vehicle for a few days, and now Chen Xin finally couldn’t resist moving back himself.

Once moved back, however, Chen Xin found there were still some inconveniences.

Although there were other people on the vehicle when they arrived, they were strangers; allowing them a place to sleep was generous enough, and Chen Xin himself could sleep on the most comfortable bed in the vehicle without any inconvenience.

But with his parents on board, Chen Xin had to give them the most comfortable double bed at the rear, while he either slept on the sofa or the double bed on the roof, which was inconvenient with his fractured hand and uncomfortable on the sofa.

Adding to the mix, being over thirty and staying with his parents brought its own lifestyle inconveniences, reinforcing Chen Xin’s determination to modify the vehicle further.

——————————————————

After a rough night on the rooftop bed, Chen Xin could no longer bother saving survival points by gathering materials; with over twenty-thousand survival points in reserve and more than three hundred points in daily income, he confidently planned to spend them on upgrading his vehicle.

This upgrade, Chen Xin didn’t intend to alter the car’s main structure, only aiming to upgrade the living cabin and adjust some functional areas to make living more comfortable and spacious.

In Chen Xin’s plan, he didn’t intend to modify the front of the vehicle, only to expand and raise the rear living cabin.

The expansion and raising of the living cabin would affect tracks, power systems, and circuitry, but fortunately, the omnipotent system could handle these. Chen Xin only needed to conceptualize his modifications.

Now, Chen Xin didn’t plan to touch the sofa area in the living cabin but wanted to move the kitchenette from the cabin’s mid-section to the front end, aligning it with the sofa.

The door originally at the cabin’s front was moved backward, repositioned to the vehicle’s mid-section, shifting from directly opposite the sofa to the sofa’s rear side.

This adjustment made the cabin space slightly more compact while freeing up rear space for Chen Xin’s next modification step.

According to Chen Xin’s concept, the living cabin wouldn’t have open double beds but individual closed spaces inspired by space capsule beds.

The double bed at the rear position would be retained, and after adjustment, Chen Xin planned to make it a semi-reclining space for two, equipped with mirrors, air-conditioning, shelves, hat hooks, a small cabinet, and lighting.

Due to the height increase, there was another chamber of the same design above this double chamber.

Additionally, Chen Xin reserved a total of four single chambers with the same structure and interior design, but each accommodating only one person.

Two single chambers were placed next to the door, against the cabin wall, opposite the bathroom, and connected at the bottom to the double chamber.

The other two were adjacent to the double chamber, effectively transforming the cabin’s rear, which originally only held a double bed for two, into space for six in four chambers after expansion and elevation.

This chamber design was inspired by space capsule beds Chen Xin had seen, but his vehicle’s chambers were more comfortable and advanced than the wooden and ABS materials of typical space capsules.

The sofa in the living cabin, capable of sleeping a person, remained functional, while the twin bed on the roof was altered considerably.

Instead of a traditional bed, it became a semi-reclining sofa, transformed from a double bed into a multifunctional lounge area, with added bookshelves and cabinets on the walls, enhancing living functionality.

Apart from these, Chen Xin made no major adjustments to the vehicle’s body, merely proportionally enlarging the power system to accommodate the increased load and expanding the living cabin’s water tank to hold more water.

After completing the design, Chen Xin checked with the system; without using materials to reduce survival point investment, the entire upgrade would cost twelve thousand points.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 262 - 260: Two Brothers
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The vehicle’s new upgrade didn’t bring any significant changes, except for making it more comfortable to live in than before, and the body becoming larger and taller.

For Chen Xin, after finishing the three upgrade improvements to the village’s shelter, his vehicle has also been upgraded again, and he can now take his parents and leave.

So naturally, Chen Xin began to prepare to leave the village and return to Xiang Province.

This seemed quite normal to Chen Xin and his parents, but it wasn’t good news for those in the village who wanted to hitch a ride with them to live a good life in Xiang Province.

All these relatives started to panic, trying every means to cling to Chen Xin, hoping to squeeze into his vehicle returning to Xiang Province.

Faced with this situation, even if Chen Xin wanted to take some people along, it was impossible to take all these people, especially since most of them were of no use, weren’t particularly close relatives, and didn’t have much skill to offer.

"Ah Xin, are you ready to leave?" Second Uncle found Chen Xin storing water in the tank before departure and asked him.

Chen Xin looked at Second Uncle and the two young men behind him with a slightly uneasy expression, instantly understanding Second Uncle’s intention.

Chen Xin wasn’t annoyed by Second Uncle bringing people to see him.

He did indeed need to recruit some people to form his team, and if the people Uncle recommended were suitable, Chen Xin wouldn’t mind taking them along.

But whether these two were truly suitable, Chen Xin needed to assess them, so he asked Second Uncle, "I’ve done everything I should, and I’m almost ready. If there are no surprises, I’m planning to take my parents back in the next couple of days. Second Uncle, you brought these two over — do you intend to recommend them to me?"

"Yes, these two are my nephews, my sister’s sons, so you should call her Aunt," Second Uncle introduced the two young men behind him and explained their abilities to Chen Xin: "This one is Han Ze. You’ve met him once before. You can call him Xiao Han or Xiao Ze. He used to be an electrician in Anqing, and he’s certified, quite skilled, and also very honest.

The other is Han Ping, Xiao Ze’s elder brother. He used to be in the army, a driver, an automobile soldier with good skills. Both brothers have the same good character; they are honest, dependable people, and very hardworking too. You can reassure yourself when using them, Ah Xin."

"Hearing you say that, Second Uncle, they seem quite good. However, I’d like to ask, are both really willing to follow me to Xiang Province? What about their family?" Chen Xin, considering Second Uncle’s words, wanted to play it safe and thus queried.

To this question, Second Uncle could only look at the brothers helplessly, answering somewhat sadly, "These two brothers were unlucky. When their village snowed, the shelter collapsed, allowing only about thirty out of a hundred people to survive, so they had nowhere else to go but to find me.

Now with you needing people, Ah Xin, I thought of these two brothers. You can use them without worry; they are honest folks who won’t cause trouble or harbor any bad intentions.

If they disappoint you in their tasks, you have the freedom to reprimand or even expel them. If they can’t seize this opportunity, let such ungrateful ones fend for themselves."

Second Uncle’s words were a bit harsh, yet Chen Xin knew it was likely said in fear he wouldn’t accept the brothers.

Although Chen Xin noted the brothers showed no objection or displeasure towards Second Uncle’s words, seeming indeed quite honest and good-natured.

Thinking it over, Chen Xin said to Second Uncle, "If only the two brothers are involved, keeping them isn’t a problem. But if others come seeking you afterwards, what would you do, Second Uncle?"

"What’s the worry? I know the village’s situation better than you. Most people don’t have much skill, except these two brothers whose abilities and character are decent. I’m recommending these two with clear conscience; any objections from others, you ignore, I’ll handle it," said Second Uncle with his village chief’s authority.

Having been village chief most of his life, Second Uncle’s prestige was indeed high; hearing this, Chen Xin let go of his concerns and agreed to keep the brothers.

Seeing Chen Xin agree to keep the brothers, Second Uncle promptly turned and left, leaving the brothers with Chen Xin.

Chen Xin didn’t say anything to the brothers, just watching the display screen on the car, monitoring the water tank’s fill level, letting the brothers stand there in the snow.

After the water tank was filled, he closed the faucet, packed away the hose, and invited the brothers into the car.

By then, Chen Xin had formed an initial impression of the brothers. During the rather lengthy time he spent filling the tank, the brothers showed no dissatisfaction, standing quietly without questioning, demonstrating indeed they were quite honest.

After letting the brothers in and giving them each a cup of hot water, Chen Xin finally said, "Introduce yourselves first. Second Uncle said a bit, but it’s better if you both tell me about yourselves to help me get familiar."

The brothers exchanged glances before Han Ze, whom Chen Xin had seen once, took the lead, "I’m Han Ze, 25 years old. I was previously working in Anqing City at a telecom company, as an electrician focusing primarily on power maintenance for telecom stations. I studied at a tech school, specializing in this field. Additionally, due to personal interest, I know a little about radio."

Since Chen Xin already met Han Ze before, he didn’t ask much more and after listening to the self-introduction, nodded, turning to the other brother.

"I’m Han Ping, two years older than my brother, meaning I’m 27 now. I didn’t attend university, enlisted straight after high school," Han Ping, noticing Chen Xin looking at him, directly introduced himself, "I served in the army for four years as a driver in the automobile corps. I didn’t have many other skills, not like my brother."

After hearing their self-introductions, Chen Xin didn’t feel their abilities were lacking, nodded slightly and continued to ask Han Ping, "Having been in the army, do you know how to shoot?"

The question surprised Han Ping initially, but having been in the military for four years, he couldn’t claim he didn’t know how to shoot, so he nodded affirmatively.

Seeing Han Ping’s response, Chen Xin didn’t say much, just stood up and fetched from the back cabinet of the living compartment a handgun left by Zhang Chengguang and others previously, and handed it to Han Ping: "Disassemble, reassemble, how long will it take?"

Han Ping was obviously surprised at Chen Xin having a gun but said nothing, only after thinking answered, "In the army, my best time was 19 seconds."

"19 seconds? Give it a try," Chen Xin said, pulling out his phone, unable to call, set the timer on the table.

Han Ping upon seeing this checked the gun on the table, then placed it back, signaling his readiness.

Chen Xin tapped his finger on the phone and called out, "Start!"

Instantly, Han Ping picked up the gun on the table, dismantling it in a few moves scattered across the table, then swiftly reassembled it.

Once he reinserted the magazine into the body, completing the assembly, Chen Xin tapped the phone again, looking at the time, which showed "21 seconds".

At the moment, Han Ping held the gun, his expression seemed a bit conflicted, yet he asked Chen Xin, "Mr. Chen, where did you get this gun?"

"I previously rescued three astronauts, this is their self-defense handgun, they gifted it to me for protection upon leaving," Chen Xin explained, taking the gun back from Han Ping, placing it on the table.

He wasn’t worried about Han Ping threatening him; having dared to bring out the gun, naturally, he had removed the bullets.

Should Han Ping attempt to use force, Chen Xin had another gun in his pocket, that one loaded.

Han Ping upon hearing Chen Xin’s explanation accepted it quickly; after all, the gun was clearly a standard issue handgun, despite his several years away from military service, he was still familiar with standard issue firearms, such a seemingly unused and nearly barrel-unworn new gun wasn’t just anywhere available.

Chen Xin was satisfied with Han Ping’s attitude, he showed some sense of responsibility at least.

In Flame Country, unlike other nations, suddenly encountering a gun either meant official personnel or an outlaw criminal, the extremely dangerous type.

Han Ping raised a pertinent question; under current circumstances, his performance was commendable.

Afterwards, Chen Xin asked the brothers several more questions, gaining a clearer understanding of their circumstances before finally letting them go back to pack, preparing to move onto the vehicle.

As for the arrangement for the brothers, Chen Xin figured Han Ping could be a driver and his bodyguard, contributing to security in his forthcoming team.

While Han Ze, knowing electrical work and radio, could exist as a technician, handling radio tasks as a communicator.

Additionally, being rural-bred, both brothers weren’t unfamiliar with farming, making Second Uncle’s recommendation apt, delivering suitable manpower to Chen Xin, leaving him very satisfied.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 263 - 261: Zeng Liu’s Request
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The matter of Chen Xin recruiting the two nephews of his second uncle spread quickly, becoming known throughout the entire village.

Regarding this matter, the relatives naturally did not dare to express any grievances against Chen Xin. Even if they had complaints, they dared not show it, as Chen Xin was their only hope of leaving the mountainous village; resentment towards him could not be directly expressed.

However, there were no kind words for the second uncle among the relatives.

Evidently, everyone felt that the second uncle was exploiting his position for personal gain. Relying on his status as the village chief and his close relationship with Chen Xin, he deliberately sent his own two nephews to Chen Xin first.

For a while, the shelter was filled with gossip about the second uncle, and the things being said were quite unpleasant.

Regarding this situation, the second uncle naturally had anticipated it. Since he had made such a decision, he was also prepared to face the subsequent effects that would arise from it.

Having been village chief for many years, the second uncle was experienced in handling such matters.

Although the villagers were all relatives, humans naturally have varying degrees of closeness, and even amongst relatives, personal interests are put first.

When personal interests are at stake, not only harsh words but even pointing fingers and cursing are light acts.

The second uncle had experienced many such things in his youth and was well-versed in handling them. After all, having been the village chief for so many years, he didn’t rely on being a peacemaker who urged retreat.

There were instances where people pointed fingers and threatened to smash pots and pans at home, yet back then, the second uncle was able to handle those people, and of course, he wouldn’t be intimidated by mere gossip now.

Therefore, although the situation caused some ripples, it didn’t have a significant impact overall.

The matters in the village naturally were suppressed by the second uncle, who couldn’t affect Chen Xin. After completing all the preparatory work, Chen Xin was ready to set off and head back.

The original recruitment plan didn’t continue beyond the two brothers recommended by the second uncle to Chen Xin.

Initially, Chen Xin did agree to take the young finance student with him, but unfortunately, the young man wanted to bring along his parents and an aunt, which was beyond what Chen Xin could accommodate, so he had to refuse.

As for the brothers Han Ping and Han Ze, they didn’t have such troubles. In their previous conversations with Chen Xin, they had already explained their situation. Their father had died when they were young, and they were solely raised by their mother, who also supported them through schooling.

This was why the elder brother Han Ping did not go to college but joined the army, while the younger brother, Han Ze, attended a technical school. It wasn’t because they couldn’t get into university but because they genuinely couldn’t afford it.

Even Han Ze’s technical school education was funded by Han Ping’s savings from his service in the army.

Although the brothers didn’t have high educational achievements, they worked diligently. Han Ping consistently excelled in the army each year, and if not for worrying about their aging mother, he could have had a better development in the military instead of retiring to take care of his mother and farm.

Meanwhile, Han Ze, although not returning home to care for their mother like his brother, continuously worked hard in the city, sending money back home for his brother to take care of their mother, while he lived frugally.

However, unfortunately, their original shelter collapsed. The fit and strong young brothers managed to escape with their mother, but she could not hold on against age, harsh cold conditions, and passed away before rescue arrived, leaving the brothers to seek help from their second uncle.

Some of this information came from the brothers themselves, while some parts Chen Xin learned by asking village members through his parents.

In Chen Xin’s eyes, these brothers were very filial and reliable, and that was the main reason he agreed to take them along; the second uncle’s recommendation merely gave him a reason to consider giving them a chance.

So, in the end, although many wanted to leave with Chen Xin to Xiang Province, he only planned to take the two brothers.

He asked Han Ping to inspect the vehicle thoroughly, confirming everything was in good condition and the preparatory work complete, only then did Chen Xin plan to set off.

As Chen Xin got Han Ping into the car, seated in the front passenger seat, and prepared to drive off, intending to teach Han Ping how to operate the vehicle, Han Ping noticed a truck coming to the shelter.

"Mr. Chen, it’s a truck delivering supplies to the shelter," Han Ping informed Chen Xin upon noticing he was also watching the truck.

"It has nothing to do with us, ignore it. Now, let me teach you how to drive this vehicle." Chen Xin just took a glance and then disregarded it, focusing instead on teaching Han Ping how to drive: "This vehicle is voice-activated, so you need to record your voice into the control panel to gain access privileges."

Han Ping listened attentively to Chen Xin’s instructions, cooperatively recording his voice in the control panel.

However, even though Chen Xin ignored the delivery truck, the person getting off it walked straight towards them and knocked on the car door beside the driver’s seat.

Chen Xin rolled down the window and looked down, discovering that it was Zeng Liu who knocked on the door.

"Zeng Liu? What are you doing here?" Chen Xin asked curiously, leaning out towards Zeng Liu.

"I came with the supply truck, I have some matters to discuss with you, Mr. Chen Xin." Zeng Liu explained, noticing the vehicle was already started, she hastily asked, "Mr. Chen Xin, are you preparing to leave?"

"Yes, I’ve already picked up my parents; I’ve nearly completed everything here. But Miss Zeng Liu, you mentioned you had something to discuss with me, what is it?" Chen Xin was slightly puzzled by Zeng Liu’s sudden visit, unsure of what her purpose was.

Zeng Liu hesitated, biting her lip. Indeed, she had a matter to discuss with Chen Xin, one entrusted to her by Lao Tang and Sister Ning, but now that Chen Xin was ready to leave, it obviously disrupted her plans, leaving her unsure how to achieve her objective.

Yet Chen Xin’s inquiry offered Zeng Liu a glimmer of hope. Despite her plain appearance, this woman knew how to seize opportunities at crucial moments. Thus, she asked Chen Xin, "Mr. Chen Xin, could I come up and speak?"

"Come up? Well..." Chen Xin mused for a moment before nodding, "Come on up."

After saying this, Chen Xin motioned for Zeng Liu to enter through the living cabin’s door, while he closed the window and said to Han Ping on the side, "Han Ping, familiarize yourself with the console and equipment, I’ve given you access rights, let the console’s intelligent system teach you how to use the equipment, I’ll go meet the guest."

"Okay, Mr. Chen," Han Ping nodded earnestly.

Leaving Han Ping at the driver’s seat to familiarize himself with the equipment, Chen Xin turned back to the living cabin, waited for Zeng Liu to come on board, then closed the door and invited her to sit on the sofa.

"Miss Zeng Liu, what brings you suddenly to me? Is there something going on?" Chen Xin asked politely though a bit unexpectedly, trying to understand the reason for her sudden visit.

Zeng Liu gritted her teeth before saying, "Mr. Chen Xin, I wonder if you’re still short of manpower?"

"Manpower?" Chen Xin arched an eyebrow, watching Zeng Liu biting her lip with a look of anticipation, unconsciously touching his chin, "Manpower isn’t something I’d say I lack, but having a few more people wouldn’t be bad. Are you thinking of joining me?"

Chen Xin wasn’t surprised at Zeng Liu wanting to join him, but he wasn’t fond of the idea.

He had saved her life and taken her and her companions with him, believing that he had fulfilled his duty thoroughly. Admittedly, he had initially helped her for his own benefit in tending his fracture, but ultimately, it was a transaction. Chen Xin felt he had already paid his dues, delivering them to the town’s shelter for a new life.

Zeng Liu’s persistent entanglement felt excessive to Chen Xin, as he didn’t owe her any further obligation; if she thought she could demand anything based on past goodwill, Chen Xin considered her deluded.

However, things took an unexpected turn for Chen Xin. After receiving his response, Zeng Liu directly shook her head and explained, "It’s not me, it’s Lao Tang and Sister Ning. They’re struggling to adapt to life here and believe that following Mr. Chen Xin would lead to a better life. Hence, they asked me to find you and see if you’d consider taking them when you return."

After hearing Zeng Liu’s words, Chen Xin was surprised but didn’t reveal it. He continued touching his chin and asked, "What about you? Do you want to follow me?"

Chen Xin’s question posed a dilemma for Zeng Liu, though she eventually shook her head, honestly saying, "If there were such an opportunity, surely I’d want a better life, but Mr. Chen Xin has already helped me enough by rescuing me from that doomed city and bringing us to this town to survive. That’s already a huge favor, and I shouldn’t ask for more."

"But your two companions don’t seem to think the same." Chen Xin noted her response with some satisfaction, making her companions appear somewhat uncompromising by contrast.

Even so, Chen Xin was not swayed, as Zeng Liu’s account was only one side of the story, leaving him none the wiser about its veracity.

"Sister Ning and Lao Tang... they only want to live a better life..." Zeng Liu tried to defend them but found no words to clearly express it. Yet, recalling how they had taken care of her and their sincere request, Zeng Liu continued, "Sister Ning and Lao Tang simply want to ride on your car to San Town, Mr. Chen Xin. They are hopeful you would let them come along if they can pay for the journey."

"Hmm, paying for the ride? Sounds fair enough." Chen Xin nodded after hearing Zeng Liu, "However, I must refuse."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 264 - 262: Embarking on the Return Journey
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

For the four people he rescued from the abandoned city, Chen Xin actually didn’t care much.

From the perspective of recruiting partners for the team, among the four, both Lao Tang and Ning Jie’s roles were just ordinary grassroots auxiliary police officers. In terms of professional abilities or their own qualities, they were quite average, incomparable to veterans like Han Ping or specialized tactical police like Qin Lan.

They themselves didn’t possess any more professional talents, which resulted in their lack of military power if they were to be combat personnel, and the lack of irreplaceable professional knowledge if they were to be professional personnel in a team.

Therefore, for Chen Xin, he didn’t want to recruit these two people, especially since he had already helped them once and they were still pushing their luck by coming to him for more help.

In contrast, Zeng Liu was more valuable for recruitment. After all, she was a licensed pharmacist and knew some other medical skills, making her an asset as a doctor or healer in a team.

However, on one hand, Zeng Liu herself had no intention of joining Chen Xin’s team, and on the other hand, Chen Xin also had better options.

In Xiang Province, Mo Qingyan’s sister, Mo Qingxin, although not officially graduated, had completed all the systematic knowledge learning of medical school, and was already considered a qualified doctor.

Moreover, Chen Xin also knew a "doctor" who could independently open a clinic. His professional capability was more trustworthy, and he even came with a little nurse, making it a great deal with one purchase and a bonus.

If compared to a game, Zeng Liu and the four others were like the clear blue sky frequently seen in the card pool, usable only when there were no other options. But around Chen Xin, he already had rare purple allies like Qin Lan and Mo Qingxin, naturally not eyeing the ordinary options.

Of course, if Zeng Liu and the others could display golden legend-like qualifications and abilities, Chen Xin wouldn’t mind looking at them differently.

But unfortunately, among the people Chen Xin had encountered so far, those worthy of golden legend status were not non-existent, but only Zhang Chengguang and the three astronauts. Besides them, no one else had reached the level he deemed worthy of golden legend status.

Therefore, refusing Zeng Liu’s request became inevitable for Chen Xin.

However, he still felt perhaps he could give Zeng Liu a chance, so he asked her, "They want to hitch a ride with me to the Three Towns, so what about you, Miss Zeng Liu? Do you want to go to the Three Towns?"

For this question, Zeng Liu did not hide her true thoughts. She nodded and said, "If I could go to the Three Towns, that would naturally be good, but Mr. Chen Xin, you have already helped us once, and we have no reason to ask you for more help. Even if we paid you, Mr. Chen Xin, you are not a taxi driver and have no necessity to agree to such a thing, do you?"

"Also, I now have my work and have restarted a new life. Just leaving like this is somewhat irresponsible. So if I want to go to the Three Towns, I would prefer to achieve this goal through my own efforts rather than asking you for help, Mr. Chen Xin."

"Hmm, if that’s the case, then I can only wish you success." Chen Xin was quite satisfied with Zeng Liu’s attitude, and after expressing his approval, he gave up the idea of recruiting Zeng Liu.

After all, since she intended to improve herself through her own efforts, Chen Xin had no reason to stop her from striving for her future.

After exchanging a few more pleasantries, Chen Xin saw Zeng Liu off the vehicle and returned to the driver’s seat, where he spoke to Han Ping, who was studying the control panel: "Alright, we can prepare to depart now, watch how I do it."

"Okay, Mr. Chen." Han Ping quickly responded and readied himself.

"System, cruise mode, forward." Chen Xin gave the command via voice while steering the vehicle with one hand, directing the whole vehicle into motion.

——————————————

As Chen Xin embarked on his return journey, in a confidential laboratory at the Chuanchong Aerospace Launch Center, a group of researchers clad in heavy protective suits had already disassembled the isotope thermoelectric battery provided by Chen Xin and placed it on the laboratory bench.

"The radiation index is quite high, considerably higher than what would be emitted from normal isotope decay. It seems more radiation energy is being released by accelerating decay to increase the battery’s output power." One researcher, holding a Geiger counter, pointed it towards the disassembled battery materials, staring intently at the nearly maxed-out reading as he spoke seriously.

Another researcher studied the battery’s core on the table, shaking his head, "It’s not only about increasing the isotope decay speed; the heat energy conversion efficiency of the battery itself is quite high. We’ve tested this core’s efficiency in converting radiation heat to electricity; it’s at least twice as efficient as the Federation’s similar products. Such high conversion efficiency is simply astonishing!"

Apart from these two researchers, others were also evaluating the disassembled isotope thermoelectric battery in their specialized domains. Despite extensive testing done on its performance, and although they were well aware of the battery’s superior performance, they were still shocked by the technology contained within it.

To the uninformed, it’s nothing more than an exceptionally impressive battery, a technological marvel felt but not fully understood. They couldn’t truly grasp how remarkable it was.

But for knowledgeable researchers like them, they were able to clearly sense how extraordinary such an isotope thermoelectric battery was, and how challenging it would be to replicate similar feats themselves.

"The precision of these components is too high! Even domestically we don’t reach this level; only a few cutting-edge labs in Lucia and the Federation could produce similar products even before the disaster, but these components were manually crafted by someone—it’s utterly unbelievable..." A researcher observed a component in his hand with a magnifying glass, astonished by its processing precision.

Leading the researchers was the chief engineer of the Space Launch Center, an elderly man with white hair, also wearing a heavy protective suit. With admiration, he commented, "High-precision manufacturing capabilities are certainly praiseworthy, but they are not the key focus. The most important aspect of this isotope thermoelectric battery is the technology! The technology to convert ordinary car batteries into high-performance isotope thermoelectric batteries!"

The old chief engineer emphasized this point, surveying the group of researchers standing before the bench, speaking with heartfelt sincerity: "Although Mr. Chen Xin claims to suffer from the acquired savant syndrome, not knowing why he possesses such abilities and knowledge, nor how he can achieve what he does, we cannot use this as an excuse to abandon research and exploration!

As researchers, our job is to explore the unknown! If we don’t understand it, we must strive to understand it! If we don’t comprehend it, we must work hard to decipher it! We must do everything possible to analyze the technology contained in this battery!

Even if we cannot reverse-engineer all the technology within it, we must at least create a replica to improve our products’ performance, reduce costs, and deploy them quickly!

This concerns national reconstruction after the disaster and whether humankind can survive this current catastrophe! Such a high-performance battery could herald a new energy revolution! Therefore, comrades, we must go all out, overcome all obstacles, and complete the reverse analysis of this battery! Do we have confidence, comrades?"

"Yes!" All the researchers faced the elderly chief engineer, responding affirmatively with vigor.

"Good, that’s the spirit we need! Let’s get to work!" The elder engineer nodded with satisfaction, giving the command, and all the researchers immediately began working.

Although age was catching up with the chief engineer, seeing the work proceed orderly, he left the laboratory.

After removing the heavy protective suit, he returned to his office, sitting on the sofa and feeling his body’s fatigue, inevitably reflecting on how he was indeed growing old.

But after a brief rest, the chief engineer revitalized himself and returned to the desk to resume work.

As the chief engineer responsible for overseeing all aspects of the Space Launch Center’s activities, even though his subordinates tried their best to complete what they could to ease the old man’s burden, he still had a significant workload each day that required his attention.

Especially in these post-apocalyptic times, the tasks for him to handle only increased.

"Chief Engineer, here’s the proposal we drafted for collaborating with Mr. Chen Xin. Please review it." The door to the old man’s office was knocked, and a staff member presented a plan on his desk.

After completing the performance test of the isotope thermoelectric battery, the nation paid significant attention to it and set up a special team to manage the collaboration with Chen Xin. The staff member before him was part of this team.

Raising his head, the old engineer looked at the staff member in front of his desk, not immediately reviewing the proposal delivered, but nodding slightly and saying, "Leave it there, I’ll read it shortly."

"Yes, Chief Engineer." The young staff nodded, preparing to leave the office, but he could not restrain himself from advising the old engineer, seeing his fatigue: "Chief Engineer, please take care of yourself and don’t overwork."

"Thank you for your concern, but this old man can still hold on, it’s not time to rest yet!" The chief engineer wasn’t ready to yield to age, merely smiled, and continued working unperturbed.

Seeing this, the staff simply had no further words, as for the entire Space Launch Center, the chief engineer was a pillar of stability, controlling too many important affairs. The staff could only advise the old engineer to maintain his health.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 265 - 263 Injured Qin Lan
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In Chen Xin’s refuge, Mo Qingxin watched as her sister came out of the room carrying a prepared meal, and asked worriedly, "Sister, is Sister Lan still refusing to eat?"

"Yes, she still doesn’t want to eat." Mo Qingyan nodded at her sister with a worried expression and took the meal back to the kitchen.

Faced with this situation, Mo Qingxin couldn’t help feeling anxious and said to Mo Qingyan, "But if she doesn’t replenish her nutrition, it won’t be conducive to her wound healing! In the current situation, how can Sister Lan not eat?"

"Ah, try to understand Lan. After all, such a thing happened." Mo Qingyan sighed, feeling not only worried but also heartbroken for Qin Lan.

Hearing her sister’s words, Mo Qingxin could only lower her head regretfully and sighed, unable to help but say, "It would be great if Chen was here, he would definitely have a way to persuade Lan."

"Yes, he would definitely find a way with the food." Mo Qingyan nodded, agreeing with her sister’s words, "So we need to take good care of Lan right now and think of a way to guide her when Chen returns, to help her walk out of the shadow of this incident."

"I understand, sister, but if Sister Lan keeps refusing to eat, her body won’t be able to take it! We must find a way to make her eat!" As a doctor, Mo Qingxin knew that for an injured person, timely replenishment of nutrition was crucial for faster recovery and maintaining the energy needed for healing.

If she doesn’t eat, her body will consume more nutrients than usual, and she won’t be able to endure it for long.

"Think of another way, for now, leave the food here. Later, Xiaoxin, you can warm it and try giving it to Lan again." Mo Qingyan, having no better solution, could only let her sister try once more.

Mo Qingxin nodded and agreed, "Leave it to me, sister. You should rest for a while. You haven’t been resting well these days while taking care of Sister Lan."

"I’ll leave it to you, then. I’m going to take a shower and rest for a bit." Mo Qingyan gave her sister a tired smile before heading toward her room to take a shower and get some rest.

Seeing her sister’s exhausted state, Mo Qingxin couldn’t help but sigh again, thinking it would be great if Chen Xin were here, hoping he could return soon.

Meanwhile, in the room, Qin Lan lay lifelessly on the bed, her expression deathly still, as if she had completely lost her vitality, leaving only an empty shell behind.

Clearly, one only becomes like this after experiencing a major blow, turning into despair with a heart ashen with grief.

As for why Qin Lan became like this, one only needs to look at her right hand to see, for now, Qin Lan had already lost her right hand, the entire arm gone from the shoulder.

This happened two weeks ago, when Qin Lan encountered an accident during a mission, being buried under collapsed bricks and soil while saving someone.

Although she was rescued in time, her right hand was too severely injured and had to be amputated.

Such a thing is an unbearable blow, not only to a SWAT sniper but even to an ordinary person.

For a SWAT sniper like Qin Lan, losing a hand meant losing everything she once had, the dreams she had been striving for, the work she had devoted countless efforts and sweat into...

All were lost when she went from being a whole person to being handicapped with a missing hand.

She could no longer hold her beloved sniper rifle, join her comrades on the battlefield, or embrace her loved ones with both arms. All of that was impossible now.

This left Qin Lan utterly devastated, even after spending two weeks in the hospital where other injuries were treated, allowing her to be discharged, but her hand could not be restored, nor could the trauma in her heart be healed.

If Chen Xin were here, perhaps there would be a way to comfort Qin Lan, but with Chen Xin absent, Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin, the two sisters, truly did not know how to console her.

All they could do was bring Qin Lan from the hospital to Chen Xin’s place, so she could be cared for at any time.

However, even with Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin’s care, Qin Lan visibly wasted away, filled with lifelessness, lacking any of her past vitality and vigor, as if life had completely lost its hope.

Qin Lan certainly knew it was no good not eating, but she had neither the appetite nor the desire to eat.

For her, losing her right hand and becoming disabled was worse than dying.

If it hadn’t been for someone stopping her in time, Qin Lan might have already taken her own life.

After all, such a blow was too great for a young woman to bear.

In Qin Lan’s perspective, her future offered no hope at all.

Before the disaster, she might have relied on prosthetics to regain basic self-care ability, avoiding complete despair and being able to start a new life.

But in the current post-apocalyptic environment, even with prosthetics, she had lost her ability to labor, becoming completely useless. Who would go to such great lengths to equip her with prosthetics and take in someone who had lost the ability to work?

Chen Xin? He would surely take her in, but Qin Lan did not want to be a burden to Chen Xin.

She was sure that upon learning she had lost a hand, Chen Xin would do everything possible to get her a prosthetic, to restore her as much as possible to her former self. If Qin Lan had even a hint of selfishness, she could just cling to Chen Xin, knowing he wouldn’t drive her away but rather take good care of her.

But Qin Lan didn’t want that. She didn’t want to be a burden to Chen Xin. That was not the life she wanted. If she did that, she’d feel shameless, finding death more agreeable.

The reason she’s still lingering here is partly due to the care from Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin, who prevented her from ending her life.

The other reason is her last wish, to bid farewell properly to Chen Xin.

Even if she wants to end her life, Qin Lan couldn’t bear not seeing Chen Xin one last time.

"Sister Lan, may I come in?" As Qin Lan was lost in thought, Mo Qingxin pushed the door open and walked in, carrying the food that Mo Qingyan had brought before.

Placing the food on the bedside table, Mo Qingxin checked over Qin Lan’s body before persuading her, "Sister Lan, please eat something! You’ll harm your body like this!
