"You can try, but I need to remind you, the Power Armor uses a force feedback type assistance method, meaning the armor only assists when you make a move, so don’t make overly large movements to avoid accidents." Chen Xin didn’t stop Qin Lan from wanting to try it. After signaling her to remove the Power Glove from her right prosthetic arm, he indicated she could go in.

With a feeling of apprehension, Qin Lan stood behind the Power Armor as prompted by Chen Xin. Looking at the humanoid groove in the open back of the Power Armor, she impulsively asked, "Do I need to take off my shoes?"

"No need, the space inside the armor is adaptive; it won’t be tight or loose, ensuring comfort." Chen Xin smiled, directing her to place her feet into the Power Armor’s foot area and to first insert her head into the helmet.

Once Qin Lan felt her body fit snugly into the armor, her arms naturally slipped into the Power Armor’s arms as well.

"And then?" Qin Lan, though a bit nervous, was filled with anticipation for the Power Armor after taking a deep breath.

"And then? Say ’start’." With a smile, Chen Xin stepped into the adjacent Power Armor and said, "Start!"

With Chen Xin’s voice, the back of the Power Armor he entered automatically closed, the power system connected, and the locking mechanism resealed everything previously opened, vacuum valves expelled excess air, sealing the Power Armor and isolating it from outside, activating the life support system.

Soon after, the inside of the helmet lit up, the installed operating system of the armor began self-checking, and displayed the status of the armor on the helmet, allowing Chen Xin to see the entire suit’s status without moving his gaze.

Qin Lan also completed the process according to Chen Xin’s instructions, though she didn’t dare move too much due to the previous warning.

"Okay, once the armor is started, the wireless communication will automatically open, and you can talk to me through the built-in communicator in the helmet. For now, it defaults to internal communication mode, this is a squad channel." Chen Xin walked to Qin Lan’s front, visible to her, before pressing a button on the side of his helmet: "This button controls switching; press once for internal communication, twice for broad channel broadcast, and hold for external shouting."

Qin Lan heard Chen Xin’s explanation and wanted to lift her hand to try, but controlled her urge due to unfamiliarity with the Power Armor operation. She only spoke through the helmet’s communicator, "I understand, how should I operate this armor? I’m afraid to move now."

"It’s actually quite simple. Try gently raising your arm. If worried about your right hand, use the left, and slowly lift it normally." Chen Xin, though not very familiar with operating Power Armor himself, could grasp it quickly as he built it, and because of previous experience with similarly operated exoskeletons, he could teach Qin Lan.

Following Chen Xin’s instructions, Qin Lan controlled her left hand to slightly raise. She could clearly feel the burden but it wasn’t heavy.

However, because she didn’t slip her hand into the power armor’s hand portion, this hand-raising move seemed somewhat like a puppet being made to raise its hand.

"The control of the hand comes from the control stick held by your fingers. Use force on the finger you want to move, now try moving your fingers a little." Seeing Qin Lan successfully lift her arm, Chen Xin continued with the next step of teaching.

As Chen Xin and Qin Lan engaged in Power Armor lessons, Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin also gathered around, enviously eyeing Qin Lan as the first to try the Power Armor.

Mo Qingxin might have been merely envious, but Mo Qingyan felt a strange jealousy towards Qin Lan, unable to resist saying to Chen Xin, who was teaching Qin Lan how to walk in Power Armor, "Foodie, this thing is nice, how about making one for me too?"

"Dragongirl, you want it too? Sure, but it will take a few days. I plan to head to the city with Alan first, and when we return, I’ll make one for you and one for little sis Xin too." Chen Xin glanced at nearby Mo Qingxin and made a promise.

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s words, Mo Qingyan nodded, "Alright, but I don’t want mine in this dull black, can you give it a paint job?"

"Paint job? That’s up to you. Black is just me being lazy to paint it. If you want it to look nicer, you can choose any color you like," Chen Xin easily agreed to Mo Qingyan’s request to paint it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 287 - 285: Traversing the Blizzard
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After a day of practice, Qin Lan had basically mastered the use of Power Armor.

Of course, this mastery merely allowed her to walk while wearing the Power Armor, far from fully familiarizing with its performance and unleashing its full potential.

Applying a game concept, she had learned the skill and could use it, but her proficiency was practically zero, lacking any power.

Overall, Qin Lan had stopped face-planting while walking in Power Armor, wouldn’t hit her helmet from overly swinging her arm, and got familiar with using the armor’s arms to pick up objects.

Under such circumstances, she could accompany Chen Xin to the city while wearing Power Armor.

Just as they prepared to depart, Qin Lan asked Chen Xin with concern: "Aren’t we preparing weapons? Although the main issue in the city is the snowstorm, wouldn’t it be dangerous if we encounter hostile elements without weapons?"

"Hostile elements? What kind of hostile elements could oppose a Power Armor?" Chen Xin laughed at Qin Lan, merely questioning her back.

Though he knew Qin Lan had a point and indeed he could arm the Power Armor, it was unnecessary.

First of all, this was Power Armor, wrapped in at least 3mm steel plates, with internal composite cushioning defense structures. All key parts were thickened and reinforced, and there’s an outer armor layer over 5mm thick. The surface underwent a carbon coating, making it extraordinarily hard while the arc-shaped structure easily deflects bullets.

With its defense capacity, even being shot at close range by a pistol or an automatic rifle would cause no harm, at most scratching off a layer of paint.

To destroy such a Power Armor, unless a rocket is directly fired at it or a grenade thrown at its feet, it would take little to no damage.

The Power Armor itself has powerful output, able to easily lift 2.5 tons, and allows a running speed of 40KM/h. Chen Xin really didn’t know of any hostile elements in Flame Country capable of challenging such Power Armor, or any necessity for him to use weapons.

Moreover, its internal Life Support System provides oxygen for 40 hours in a vacuum, the air filter ensures survival in hazardous environments for over a week, and it includes a water filter and a three-day supply of emergency compressed biscuits. Even if trapped, they could send a rescue signal, holding out until rescue arrives.

Furthermore, they weren’t venturing into a warzone, merely crossing a snowstorm into the city.

Qin Lan realized she had overthought, pondering what her former SWAT team could do against such Power Armor, and concluding that without heavy weaponry, even unarmed Power Armor would be nearly impossible for a SWAT squad to effectively harm, unless a sniper rifle’s Armor-Piercing Bullet pierced the visor for a headshot.

Especially since Chen Xin demonstrated the armor’s defense by letting her shoot at it with his police pistol at close range, which left no mark, only scraping off a bit of paint.

Their Power Armor had no weapons, but each had a cylindrical bag about 20 liters in size on their back, filled with emergency supplies, planning to deliver them to Ding Ning once they entered the city. Besides this, Qin Lan insisted Chen Xin carry a pistol inside the Power Armor.

Checking on Ding Ning was one of the main objectives for Chen Xin and Qin Lan entering the city. More importantly, Chen Xin wanted to connect with the branch of the research institution stationed in the city, to learn more and see what he could do.

After packing, they opened the shelter’s door, using the Power Armor’s strength to force a path through the snow piled to the door’s top, leaving the shelter.

Watching the shelter door close behind, Chen Xin and Qin Lan turned on the lights on their Power Armor shoulders, moving one after another through waist-deep snow towards the city.

"The snow is so dense, you can’t see the direction at all! Ah Xin, how do we navigate?" Qin Lan asked, staring at the continually blinding snowflakes hitting her visor.

The lights on the Power Armor shoulders were like two flashlights, illuminating only a few meters ahead in this blizzard. Even with sunlight, visibility couldn’t confirm directions well, not to mention during this pitch-black night.

Yet Chen Xin had considered this issue and said to Qin Lan, "Try saying ’open navigation’ aloud. I’ve uploaded the map and route to your Power Armor system. The navigation system includes a compass, mini-map, and positioning, so even if lost in the snowstorm, you can locate my position."

Hearing this, Qin Lan activated the navigation system, seeing a mini-map similar to those in games appear in her upper right field of vision, with a yellow line for the route and a red dot indicating Chen Xin, finally feeling assured to follow him through the snowstorm.

Advancing in the storm, the only thing Qin Lan and Chen Xin could see besides snowflakes lit by their lights was endless darkness, hearing only dim wind and snow noise obstructed by their helmets and each other’s voices.

If not for knowing the shelter was behind, companion beside, and destination city ahead, in such dark, solitary conditions, it would be easy to lose track of time and orientation, eventually freezing to death in the ice and snow.

This wasn’t uncommon, even with modern technology, encountering a snowstorm outdoors was hazardous, with fatalities due to freezing every year.

Fortunately, Qin Lan and Chen Xin didn’t have far to go, aided by Power Armor, reaching a bridge connecting the city in just over an hour.

The bridge in the storm was completely unrecognizable, only faint traces suggesting its original position.

Through the Power Armor’s light, snow nearly buried the bridge, engulfing it in ice and snow.

"Looks like the Power Armor’s performance is decent. Crossing the river, we can enter the city." Chen Xin praised his Power Armor creation, leading Qin Lan across the river underneath the bridge into the city.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 288 - 286: Entering the City Bureau
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the darkness and endless blizzard, Chen Xin and Qin Lan finally managed to cross the streets completely buried in snow and arrived at the entrance of the city police station.

The original guard post was now completely buried under the snow, its roof no longer visible, and not a trace of the main gate was left.

The imposing police station building seemed slightly better compared to Chen Xin’s last visit. In the light from his Power Armor, Chen Xin could faintly see the glass that had been shattered due to various disasters post-meteor strike had been repaired.

However, the ground floor of the building was completely buried under snow, thick ice and snow had even piled up to the second-floor windowsill.

"With this much snow, will the entrance to the shelter be buried?" Chen Xin, looking at the snow-filled ground before him, somewhat hesitantly asked Qin Lan.

The city police station shelter was the nearest medium-sized official shelter to Chen Xin’s home shelter. Hence, after leaving his home shelter and entering the city, Chen Xin and Qin Lan had arrived here.

However, looking at the snow-covered ground, Chen Xin was clearly worried about being unable to enter the shelter due to the entrance being blocked by snow.

Qin Lan was also somewhat uncertain about this but still led Chen Xin toward the police station’s garage based on memory: "Let’s check the garage first, it’s one of the main entrances and exits of the underground shelter, and it connects to the underground highway, so it shouldn’t be buried."

Hearing Qin Lan say this, Chen Xin nodded in understanding and walked with her towards the garage.

Yet, the situation was worse than they anticipated; the snow had already buried the garage, and its doors were completely covered by snow.

"Looks like we need to find another route." Chen Xin scratched his head, looking at the thick snow before them.

After all, another reason they came to the city was to find Ding Ning, who stayed at Mo Qingyan’s house. Now, with the snow this thick, Mo Qingyan’s home was likely buried beneath the snow, and that elevator entrance was probably unusable.

This made Chen Xin worried about the condition of Mo Qingyan’s house; it might become abandoned, frozen into an ice cellar.

Although such a situation was almost unavoidable given the weather conditions, it was still Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin’s home. Even if they were currently staying at Chen Xin’s place, what if they wanted to return to their home one day?

They couldn’t very well find a way to dig their own house out of the snow first, could they?

If possible, Chen Xin still hoped to find a way to protect Mo Qingyan’s house.

However, before that, he and Qin Lan first needed to find a way to enter the city police station shelter so they could know where Ding Ning was now.

"Let’s try finding a window to get into the building and see if there’s a connected entrance to the garage that’s not shut," Qin Lan suggested to Chen Xin while trying to recall the layout of the passages leading to the garage within the police station building.

Chen Xin certainly wasn’t as familiar with the police station as Qin Lan was, so he could only follow her proposal.

The two of them came to the front of the building, and Chen Xin asked Qin Lan, "How do we get in? Break a window and climb in?"

"That’s about our only choice now. Find a window close to the corridor and head directly in," Qin Lan replied decisively and led Chen Xin to a window.

As a SWAT officer, although Qin Lan had undergone window-breaking entry training many times, doing so at her own police station building was a first.

Standing in front of the window, Qin Lan hesitated for just a moment before taking a deep breath, raised her fist, then controlled her Power Armor to punch and shatter the window glass.

The entire pane shattered into shards immediately upon Qin Lan’s punch, and although she wasn’t entirely skilled with operating the Power Armor yet, her muscle memory for tactical movements allowed her to nimbly climb inside.

Seeing this, Chen Xin climbed through the window shattered by Qin Lan.

Standing in the corridor, the light from their shoulders illuminated the way, making it clear that there wasn’t much snow in the corridor. However, the walls and floor were already covered in frost.

Chen Xin asked Qin Lan, "Should we head down? Or does this not lead to the garage, and we need to go into the building?"

"This staircase only goes to the ground floor; we need to enter the police station proper and use another staircase to get to the garage," Qin Lan replied after carefully identifying their surroundings.

Chen Xin merely nodded to this, then followed Qin Lan up the stairs, through a door, and into the interior of the police station building.

There was a slight hiccup as the feet of the Power Armor were evidently much larger than a regular footsteps in shoes, and the staircases in the police building were designed for regular foot traffic, giving Qin Lan and Chen Xin a bit of trouble as they ascended.

Fortunately, the Power Armor performed well, with an internal gyroscope for balance working correctly, so the two managed to ascend the stairs with some effort.

The interior of the police station was pitch-black, relying entirely on the light from their Power Armor shoulders for illumination. This environment made Chen Xin feel like he was playing some kind of adventure game, albeit one of those rated 18+ horror games.

Of course, reality wasn’t a horror game, and although there was a shelter underneath the police station, it didn’t have bald guys wearing green military coats, or disgusting monsters, nor would a skinned frog with a tongue that could whip out eight meters appear from the ceiling. Chen Xin himself wasn’t a vehicle killer, and Qin Lan wasn’t wearing a red cheongsam either.

However, thinking of this, a thought couldn’t help but arise in Chen Xin’s mind—perhaps someday he could try asking Qin Lan to wear a cheongsam?

Stuffing the thought away for later, Chen Xin followed Qin Lan as they quickly traversed the long-abandoned corridors and still showing some signs of use office spaces, heading to the stairs leading to the underground garage.

The police station building had been repaired and the lower floors had been put back into use, but now with the ravaging blizzards, those above-ground floors had evidently been abandoned again, with only traces of a hasty evacuation remaining.

Evidently, when the blizzards hit, those who had been working here hadn’t been notified or prepared and had been hurriedly evacuated to the underground shelter.

Chen Xin and Qin Lan descended into the underground garage by the stairwell, finding that the door here was indeed sealed shut.

"Should we knock, or force it open?" Chen Xin asked, standing before a tightly closed fire door leading to the underground garage, unable to pull it open, looking to Qin Lan.

Qin Lan answered with action rather than words, directly ramming the door open.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 289 - 287 Places Where Help Can Be Provided
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The moment Chen Xin and Qin Lan slammed the door open, it immediately startled everyone in the underground parking lot, and a few quick-reacting police officers hurriedly drew their service pistols, hiding behind cover for aiming.

Yet, two pitch-black steel monsters walked out from behind the slammed door, leaving them momentarily stunned and unsure whether they should fire.

They wanted to shoot because these two steel monsters looked quite terrifying—entirely black, breaking in with a chilling air, at first glance not resembling anything benign.

But considering the small pistols in their hands and looking at the thick armor on the opponents, they also didn’t believe their small guns could inflict harm, and rashly firing might even anger them.

Luckily, after breaking the door, Qin Lan and Chen Xin didn’t show any signs of aggression. Qin Lan quickly activated the external speaker to identify themselves to the officers.

Upon hearing Qin Lan’s identification, these officers finally relaxed, putting away their guns and emerging from behind cover to approach Qin Lan and Chen Xin.

"Qin Lan?" A police officer who knew Qin Lan was somewhat incredulous, as seeing Qin Lan in the Power Armor was beyond belief.

"It’s me, how’s the situation at the headquarters?" Qin Lan opened her helmet’s visor, revealing her face and inquiring about the condition from the officers in front.

Seeing it was indeed Qin Lan, the officer sighed in relief and spoke to her: "You scared me; I thought some alien monsters broke in! Your suits really are amazing!"

"The situation at headquarters isn’t very optimistic; it’s too cold, many people are suffering from hypothermia, and there are a lot of frostbite cases. But the situation is similar everywhere in the city; headquarters is relatively better. At least your Special Forces team can take hot showers, which help treat frostbite." Another officer told Qin Lan, with a casual tone yet conveying a sense of bitterness: "Other places are tough, with many not receiving timely and effective treatment for frostbite, leaving hospitals overwhelmed. If it continues, things might really go wrong."

Hearing the officer’s words, Chen Xin and Qin Lan both looked grim; they hadn’t expected the city’s situation to reach this point, though they anticipated poor conditions.

Especially as this was only the situation described by the officer, in reality, it might be worse.

Faced with such circumstances, Qin Lan quickly inquired about Ding Ning’s status from the officers: "Do you know how Ding Ning is doing? How is she now?"

"Ding Ning is fine; she’s maintaining order at the Special Forces cafeteria, no problems there," the officer responded to Qin Lan and then, showing concern, asked: "Qin Lan, what are you here for?"

"We were on the outskirts and saw the blizzard; we were worried about the city’s situation, so we came to see if there’s anything we can help with." Qin Lan explained to the officers the reason for her and Chen Xin’s visit.

Hearing Qin Lan say they came from the outskirts, the two officers looked incredulous but then believed them upon seeing the Power Armor on Chen Xin and Qin Lan, as such armor indeed allowed for breakthroughs through the blizzard.

But this wasn’t the main point; upon learning Qin Lan and Chen Xin came to help, the officer thought for a moment and said: "We’re not sure what help is needed; you should ask the person in charge downstairs."

The officers didn’t stop Qin Lan and Chen Xin from going down, partly because they couldn’t and partly because Qin Lan was still a Special Forces officer despite being on disability leave; her status hadn’t changed.

So, after Qin Lan said they came to help, the officers directly chose to believe them, notified the refuge below, and let them in.

Chen Xin and Qin Lan, wearing Power Armor, descended into the refuge, reaching the floor where the Special Forces were.

As the two walked out of the elevator, Uncle Ding and the political commissar, along with two well-equipped Special Forces officers, were waiting at the elevator entrance.

Seeing Chen Xin and Qin Lan in Power Armor, the four waiting at the elevator and passersby nearby were shocked, as the appearance of Power Armor was truly astonishing, something one would expect only in science fiction movies, far beyond ordinary imagination.

Fortunately, Uncle Ding and the commissar were not ordinary people and soon regained their composure, welcoming Qin Lan and Chen Xin: "Chen Xin, Qin Lan, you coming back at this time is very timely!"

"Let’s skip the pleasantries; under current circumstances, let’s get straight to business. Is there anything we can do?" Chen Xin looked at Uncle Ding, eschewing excessive courtesies.

"Well... we’re not short on manpower; the key issue now is heating. Although the city’s coal supply is still there, even though difficult to transport outside, railway line colleagues are striving, so there’s still coal. Just..." The commissar appeared grim, as having two people come to help was good, but too little amid the challenges.

"Is it about temperature and thermal efficiency? If that’s the case, I could help a bit, assist with modifying the boilers, which could improve heating efficiency but might increase coal consumption." Hearing the commissar, Chen Xin immediately understood and offered to help upgrade the heating equipment.

Upon hearing this, Uncle Ding and the commissar’s eyes lit up, realizing that though increased coal consumption might quickly deplete reserves, leaving a crisis if the blizzard persisted and coal ran out, raising temperatures was a priority, as continued cold might kill people before the coal runs out.

"Then let’s raise the temperature first! If coal runs out, we’ll find other solutions!" Uncle Ding decisively instructed, choosing for Chen Xin to assist in modifying the heating system: "Commissar, guide them, and Chen Xin, tell the commissar what’s needed for modification. I’ll find the director!"

Finishing his words, Uncle Ding entered the elevator, pressed the button, and went to find the police bureau’s director to discuss boiler modification.

While Chen Xin and Qin Lan followed the commissar into the refuge’s interior.

"Besides heating, healthcare is a major issue; many frostbite and hypothermia patients can’t receive timely treatment, and we can only let them take hot showers for relief." As the commissar walked, he discussed the current situation: "If it wasn’t for Comrade Chen Xin installing the geothermal heat pump earlier, life would be even tougher..."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 290 - 288: Boiler Transformation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the refuge within the city police station, Chen Xin had already taken off his Power Armor in the room where the SWAT team installed the geothermal heat pump, and was checking the equipment beside the geothermal heat pump.

For the SWAT team, the geothermal heat pump is a crucial piece of equipment. It is precisely because of its existence that the SWAT team can maintain a certain level of heating and hot water supply in this extreme cold weather, allowing some people suffering from hypothermia and frostbite to take a hot bath to relieve the damage caused by long-term exposure to a low-temperature environment.

However, the heat supply from the geothermal heat pump equipment is limited, and if heating is maintained, the bathroom cannot be kept open for a long time. Therefore, the SWAT team’s bathroom can only be opened at intervals, allowing only those with severe frostbite to come in and take a hot bath to alleviate the frostbite.

"Comrade Chen Xin, is there a way to enhance the heat supply of the geothermal heat pump?" Standing next to Chen Xin, the political commissar looked at him checking the equipment and asked him with a hopeful tone: "Thanks to the equipment you sold us back then, it really made a big difference! Can you find a way for the equipment to produce more thermal energy?

We really need more hot water right now! If you can do it, just say what you need! Even if it means throwing my 200ish pounds into the boiler to burn, I won’t need you to do it; I’ll jump in myself!"

When Chen Xin heard the political commissar say this, he shook his head with a smile and said, "It’s not that exaggerated! If we have to burn something, it should be coal. Burning a body is a pure waste of fuel since the human body has too low a caloric value! Besides, modifying the equipment requires materials, not burning people."

"So what materials do you need, Comrade Chen Xin? I’ll have someone prepare them right away!" The political commissar, seeing Chen Xin’s confident attitude, hurriedly asked what materials were needed for the modification, seemingly eager to have Chen Xin modify the geothermal heat pump.

"I’ll need to add two more compressors, enlarge the evaporator tank, and add new pipelines..." Chen Xin blurted out and then told the political commissar: "Get two compressors from air conditioners, and then some steel pipes and iron sheets, and that should be enough."

Just as Chen Xin finished speaking, the political commissar hurried to turn around to prepare the materials, but Chen Xin suddenly called him back.

He seemed to remember something, slapped his forehead, and said to the political commissar, "Also, send someone to notify the people at the Polytechnic College’s research institute, let them know I’m here, and have them send someone over."

"Alright, I’ll arrange that right away!" The political commissar, after confirming what Chen Xin said, jogged off to arrange Chen Xin’s requests, while Chen Xin continued to ponder whether there was a better way to enhance the heating from the geothermal heat pump.

While Chen Xin was contemplating the modification of the geothermal heat pump, Uncle Ding also reported the boiler conversion to the director. After receiving approval, he returned to the SWAT team and found Chen Xin.

"The director has agreed on modifying the boiler, Chen Xin. How do you plan to modify it? How long will the modification take, and do we need to shut down the boiler?" Uncle Ding approached Chen Xin without any formalities and directly asked the questions he was most concerned about.

Chen Xin thought for a moment and said, "I need to see the boiler first."

"Alright, I’ll have someone take you to inspect the boiler." Uncle Ding nodded and agreed.

————————————————————

Led to the boiler room of the city police station’s refuge, Chen Xin went to the working boiler. Listening to the roaring flames inside the boiler and feeling the heat waves emanating from it, he knowingly nodded.

"It can be modified without shutting down." Chen Xin turned to look at Uncle Ding, who had come along, and answered his previous question: "However, the modification will require quite a few things. I’ll write up a list later, and once the materials are prepared, we can start the modification."

Upon hearing this, Uncle Ding felt a sudden relief in his heart.

Addressing the heating issue in this extreme cold weather is a challenge facing all refuges in the city. If Chen Xin can solve this issue, even temporarily, it would save many lives and be a great relief for everyone.

With peace of mind, Uncle Ding also hurriedly urged Chen Xin to list the materials needed for the modification.

Chen Xin agreed and didn’t closely examine the boiler, as for the system, merely seeing the boiler was enough.

The coal-fired boilers used in the medium-sized refuges in the city are layer-fired boilers. The advantage of such boilers is that they have less auxiliary equipment, are easy to manufacture and install, and easy to operate, making them suitable for medium and small capacity boilers.

However, since their combustion form is to lay coal chunks flat on the chain grate for layered combustion, they have the drawbacks of incomplete combustion with a high content of combustibles in the slag and fly ash, and the efficiency of the boiler generally only reaches 75-85%, leaving much room for improvement.

Of course, this room for improvement is for Chen Xin, who has the system, because he can change its combustion method without shutting down or dismantling the boiler, transforming it from an inefficient layer-fired to a more efficient chamber combustion or vortex combustion type.

The difference between these boilers and the layer-fired type mainly lies in the combustion method, as both adopt the method of thoroughly pulverizing the coal into coal powder and then fully burning it, thereby achieving a higher degree of combustion and boiler efficiency than the layer-fired type.

However, chamber combustion boilers require the raw coal to be screened, crushed, and milled into coal powder with most particle sizes smaller than 0.1 mm and then blown into the furnace chamber for suspension combustion, needing much more auxiliary equipment and a higher level of automation compared to layer-fired boilers.

Such boilers are evidently less suited for medium-small refuges but are more suitable for large refuges due to their scalable size.

Thus, Chen Xin did not choose chamber combustion boilers as the modification direction but chose the Vortex Furnace.

This type of combustion method for boilers somewhat bridges layer-fired and chamber combustion. It first burns crushed coal particles or coarse coal powder with particle sizes smaller than 10 mm in a vortex pattern using a prefaced cyclone burner, producing high-temperature flue gas that then enters the main furnace for radiant heat transfer, allowing the coal to burn fully and release heat.

Compared with chamber combustion boilers, Vortex Furnaces can also be made smaller, thereby making them very suitable as a modification direction.

The only issue is that Vortex Furnaces are limited by the ash melting point and slag viscosity concerning suitable coal types; the range of boiler load changes is small, making rapid start-stop impossible.

And since the temperature inside the furnace can reach about 2000°C, the NOx emissions from harmful gases are significant, causing serious atmospheric pollution.

But now, in this extreme cold apocalypse, boilers run non-stop, and with the sky in its current state, concerns of atmospheric pollution have to be postponed, allowing humanity to survive this calamity first before addressing other issues.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 291 - 289 Isotope Generator
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A few hours after Chen Xin arrived at the city police station shelter, people from the research institute finally received the news and rushed to him.

Before meeting Chen Xin, these researchers were wondering how Chen Xin managed to traverse the blizzard to reach the city. But upon seeing the Power Armor parked with the SWAT team, all their questions vanished into thin air, replaced by an intense enthusiasm as they surrounded the Power Armor, eager to disassemble it immediately for a closer look.

Fortunately, Chen Xin stopped them.

"Just take a look. There will be blueprints and prototypes for you later. This one is my personal item, so I won’t give it to you for research."

When Chen Xin was working on the precursor to the Power Armor, the Exoskeleton Protective Suit, he envisioned handing these over to the state for mass production, so naturally, he wouldn’t reject the researchers’ desire to study the Power Armor.

However, he still needed this set of Power Armor to return to his own shelter and intended to keep it for personal use, so he didn’t want to hand it over to the researchers for disassembly.

Despite being disappointed, the researchers understood Chen Xin’s decision, and moving past the Power Armor, they were also concerned about why Chen Xin would risk traversing the blizzard to reach the city under such harsh weather conditions.

"Given the dire situation in the city right now, I wanted to see if there was anything I could do to help." Chen Xin explained to the researchers, while also elaborating on what he was doing: "I plan to upgrade the ground-source heat pump and coal-fired boiler of the city police station shelter’s heating system. I have some ideas about the problems caused by this blizzard that I need to discuss with you."

Upon hearing this, the researchers put away their curiosity about the Power Armor, adopting a serious and attentive demeanor.

Due to the loss of weather forecasting capability, this sudden blizzard caught everyone off guard, and no one was prepared for it.

Though the city government had plans for sudden disasters or emergencies, given the current conditions, their contingency plans did not account for a blizzard lasting more than a week with temperatures plummeting to minus seventy degrees.

This has made heating the most severe test for all shelters and survivors. If they get through it, everyone rejoices, but if not, sealing the shelter’s doors will turn it into a tomb.

In fact, this blizzard affected not only this city. According to contacts the researchers made through internal channels with higher authorities, the blizzard swept down from Siberia, affecting most of the eastern and central regions of Flame Country.

If there were satellite images available, one would see that over half of Flame Country is now engulfed by the raging blizzard.

This means the blizzard threatens not just this city, but has pushed half of Flame Country to a dangerous brink of life and death.

Under such circumstances, any method that can improve the current situation is a good one. If effective, it could save more than half of Flame Country’s population.

"So, Mr. Chen Xin, what do you plan to do? Under the current circumstances, the heating issue is really hard to solve!" The researchers, lacking a good solution, were eager to know what Chen Xin had in mind.

Currently, most shelters rely on coal or oil-fired boilers, as well as electric heating for warmth.

Oil and gas boilers are mainly pre-existing boilers from before the disaster, but considering the current tight supply of oil and natural gas, using these boilers is very problematic.

While coal-fired boilers are not as efficient or environmentally friendly as oil and gas boilers, coal supply is simpler and cheaper, making it the most prevalent heating method now.

As for electric heating, it mainly exists in small or private shelters. For these small-scale shelters, even the smallest boilers are a huge waste and unnecessary under normal circumstances.

After all, small and private shelters can’t effectively utilize all the heat output by the boilers, leading to significant waste.

Even under current conditions, boilers remain wasteful for small shelters and cannot heat effectively.

As for large shelters, it’s no use mentioning them, as their boilers are almost overloaded and still can’t raise the temperature above zero degrees.

Some might cite polar research stations, but the issue is that while they also use these methods for heating, their cold and heat insulation measures are unlike anything these shelters have.

The much larger space compared to a few rooms in a polar research station, combined with unavoidable heat loss due to the need for air circulation, prevents the temperature from rising.

To solve this problem, apart from preventing heat loss, the simplest method is to provide more heat. But this circles back to the starting point: improving the boiler or finding a new heating method.

The researchers had been working toward this goal but couldn’t come up with a better solution.

Retrofitting boilers or electric heating is actually not hard to achieve, but conventional methods require turning off the boiler for retrofitting. Beyond retrofitting costs, the downtime alone is enough to freeze everyone in the shelter to death, irrevocably.

Therefore, when Chen Xin claimed he had an idea, the researchers were ecstatic. They had high expectations for Chen Xin, a miracle worker in their eyes.

"The main issue with heating is insufficient heat. I can enhance burning efficiency and thermal efficiency without shutting down the boiler, but the improvement is limited, so a new heating method is still needed." Chen Xin organized his thoughts and said: "Electric heating is a good supplement. The main issue is some shelters lack electric heating equipment, and producing electricity is another problem. Now, power plants also need coal, and converting electricity into heat is less efficient than directly burning coal for heating.

So I plan to modify the previously made Isotope Thermoelectric Battery, and try to see if I can develop an Isotope Generator. Once installed in the shelter and connected to the shelter’s circuit, with ample electricity, electric heating could supplement the insufficient heating."

Upon hearing this, the researchers were stunned, unsure of how to react.

An Isotope Generator, something straight out of science fiction, was beyond their imagination.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 292 - 290: Upgrading the Ground Source Heat Pump
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Mr. Chen Xin, when you mentioned the isotope generator, are you referring to a radioactive isotope generator?" A researcher naturally associated the isotope generator mentioned by Chen Xin with ordinary radioactive isotope generators like the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery.

The so-called radioactive isotope generator is a generator that uses the thermal energy released from isotope decay to generate electricity. From its principle to structure, it is not much different from the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery. As for the power output, it’s about a hundred watts, which compared to the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery made by Chen Xin that can reach over a hundred kilowatts, is a literal thousand-fold difference.

As soon as this researcher spoke, he realized that there was something wrong with what he said. If Chen Xin could manufacture an Isotope Thermoelectric Battery with a power output of over a hundred kilowatts, how could the isotope generator he mentioned be such a simple thing as a radioactive isotope generator?

Sure enough, Chen Xin shook his head and said, "No, the isotope generator I’m talking about is not such a rudimentary thing, but an isotope fusion generator. It’s somewhat similar to the principle of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery I handed over to you for research. It uses certain methods to accelerate isotope decay, releasing more radiation energy for power generation."

"It sounds like connecting Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries in parallel and using them as a generator?" Chen Xin’s explanation quickly made the researchers understand what he was talking about with the isotope generator.

"That’s about right, but in terms of power output, the power is greater," Chen Xin nodded and continued to explain. "Although the decay rate of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery has been accelerated, the reaction rate is still relatively gentle, so one battery can last for ten years.

But the isotope generator is different. It’s like undergoing a fission reaction, and the isotope charge inside may need to be replaced in about a year. However, this exchange brings a strong power output, which can be used as a generator."

"Is that so? If there is enough electric energy, then each shelter can have more power for heating, and a large amount of coal would not need to be used for power generation but could be used directly for heating!" The researchers understood Chen Xin’s idea and were very supportive of it.

Chen Xin also nodded. Actually, he had more ideas, but under the current circumstances, this was the fastest way to see results.

In fact, if conditions allowed, he would prefer to build a device similar to the Energy Tower in "Frostpunk," directly dispelling the cold of the entire city, instead of now installing generators one shelter at a time.

"So please help collect some materials, the same ones I used to make the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery before. First, make an Isotope Battery, then modify the Isotope Battery into a generator. Installing one at each shelter can ensure all their power needs, with maintenance only requiring battery replacement once a year." Chen Xin outlined his requirements to the researchers.

The researchers agreed. Apart from leaving one person to help Chen Xin, the others quickly left to prepare the materials as requested by Chen Xin.

Meanwhile, Chen Xin needed to first modify the geoheat pumps for the SWAT team. While he was waiting for the researchers, the political commissar had already gathered all the materials needed for the geoheat pump modifications.

Upon hearing that Chen Xin was going to modify the geoheat pump, the researcher left behind to assist was very excited. He had long heard of Chen Xin’s capabilities, and witnessing Chen Xin demonstrating his skills, even if only modifying a standard geoheat pump, still filled him with excitement.

Chen Xin himself didn’t feel much about it. After the political commissar delivered the materials, he plunged himself into the room where the geoheat pumps were installed and opened the system upgrade interface, ready to start upgrading.

"Mr. Chen Xin, is there anything I can help with?" Seeing that Chen Xin was ready to start working, the researcher hurriedly inquired if there was anything he could assist with.

Chen Xin was momentarily stunned by the question, then responded by shaking his head and said, "No need for now, this is a minor task I can handle myself."

After speaking, Chen Xin directly selected the overall upgrade for the geoheat pumps installed for the SWAT team in the system interface.

Following Chen Xin’s upgrade, the originally single-unit geoheat pumps installed there combined into one under the system’s function. After absorbing the upgrade materials, it became a large piece of equipment. Although still consisting of sub-units, it was a complete whole.

In Chen Xin’s eyes, the power of this upgraded geoheat pump increased by nearly 50%, the heat dissipation efficiency of the supporting heating pipeline also improved by 30%, and it upgraded from water heating to air heating. It can be said that this upgrade gave a quite significant improvement to the heating for the SWAT team.

After Chen Xin completed the upgrade, a system prompt followed, just like before presenting him with a single reward of five thousand points and a daily increase of fifty survival points.

This was indeed a pleasant surprise, taking Chen Xin by surprise as he had forgotten he could earn survival points before the modification.

Nevertheless, this was good; the single reward of five thousand survival points was enough for Chen Xin to recoup his investment, with some profit left over.

While Chen Xin was inventorying his gains, the researcher beside him was still in shock, amazed by the "scene" he had witnessed.

Influenced by the system, the researcher saw Chen Xin single-handedly combine several separate geoheat pumps into one and transform it into a complete whole, achieving this even while the equipment was running, truly opening his eyes to something extraordinary.

"Is this your ability, Mr. Chen Xin? It’s incredible!" The researcher gave a genuine compliment.

Upon hearing the researcher’s voice, Chen Xin turned to look at him, recalling the previous time when someone saw him upgrading items. He just smiled and said, "These geoheat pumps are something I made before, and modifying them is relatively simple, nothing special."

"Even so, it’s still amazing. Is this equipment connected to their heating pipelines? Can it improve the heating here?" The researcher, filled with curiosity and inquiry, asked Chen Xin.

"Compared to before the modification, the power increased by 50%, and the heating mode was changed from water heating to air heating. It should provide a pretty good improvement for the heating situation here," Chen Xin briefly explained the modification results to the researcher. Still, he didn’t say much, as explaining too much would contradict his previous claim of not being technologically knowledgeable.

"Even that improvement is remarkable. For the shelters now, even a 1% increase in heating efficiency is beneficial." The researcher sincerely praised.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 293 - 291 Eating
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When Chen Xin and the researchers left the room where the geothermal heat pump was installed and came to the SWAT team’s cafeteria, the enhanced heating effect of the geothermal heat pump was already noticeably felt. Although the temperature was still cold, there was a more noticeable rise in the room’s temperature.

Before Chen Xin upgraded the geothermal heat pump, people here looked as if they desperately wanted to stick to the radiator to warm themselves. But now, having switched from water heating to air heating, they were merely gathered around the radiator, and there was a clear sense of comfort in their demeanor.

This is quite understandable. Water heating uses a boiler or heat source to heat the water for heating, usually at a temperature of around 60~80°C, which is a temperature one can touch directly without feeling very hot.

In contrast, air heating uses a heat source to turn water into high-temperature steam for heating, exceeding 100°C, a temperature capable of causing burns.

Although both provide heating, the difference in their heating efficiency can be said to be worlds apart.

For the heating system, without a sufficiently abundant heat source, it is impossible to adopt air heating.

However, currently, only the SWAT team at the city’s police station shelter can enjoy air heating, because the shelter overall uses water heating. Air heating and water heating cannot operate simultaneously, so earlier when Chen Xin was upgrading, he simply separated the heating system for the SWAT team area connected to the geothermal heat pump and converted it to air heating.

If the entire city police station shelter’s heating system were to be converted to air heating, it would only be possible after Chen Xin modifies the shelter’s boiler.

Regarding this matter, Chen Xin is not anxious at all, because it is really not troublesome at all; he just needs to wait for the city police station to gather the materials necessary to modify the boiler.

Hence, Chen Xin’s demeanor is now much more relaxed, and when he found Qin Lan and Ding Ning in the cafeteria, he had a smile on his face.

"Xiao Ding, Ah Lan!" Chen Xin greeted the two and walked to them, while the researchers beside him sensibly stepped aside, not disturbing Chen Xin and his friends.

"Ah Xin! Sister Lan said you were upgrading the geothermal heat pump, how’s the situation now?" Ding Ning enthusiastically greeted Chen Xin, noticing his cheerful demeanor and inquired about it with concern.

"The upgrade has already been completed. Didn’t you feel the increase in the radiator’s temperature?" Chen Xin smiled at Ding Ning, explained briefly, and then asked her, "Xiao Ding, have you been staying here recently? Do you know the situation at Long Niang’s home?"

Regarding this question, Ding Ning shook her head with some difficulty and explained, "I was on duty here when the snowstorm came. I intended to go back once, but later didn’t have time. That place should now be buried by the snowstorm."

At this point, Ding Ning’s expression was a bit embarrassed. Earlier, Mo Qingyan entrusted the place to her care when handing over the house, and now although the house is buried by snow due to weather reasons, which are beyond human control, it could indeed be said not properly taken care of.

Hearing this, Chen Xin nodded with understanding, having seemingly anticipated this situation, so he said to Ding Ning, "Long Niang mentioned it to me while I was coming here, I’ll go take a look later. After all, it’s her home, even if she moved to mine now, her home shouldn’t be abandoned like this."

"Okay, let me know when you’re going, and if I’m free, I’ll go with you because my stuff is still there!" Ding Ning thought about her belongings she didn’t have time to bring out and mentioned it to Chen Xin.

Chen Xin nodded and took note of it, then glanced at the cafeteria and asked Ding Ning and Qin Lan, "Can we get some food here? I’m a bit hungry, Ah Lan, are you hungry?"

"A bit, but there are too many people here; let’s eat what we brought." Qin Lan glanced at the cafeteria, saw the long queue at the meal serving window, and shook her head directly. She and Chen Xin had emergency food in their Power Armor, so there was no need to squeeze in here with the other survivors.

Chen Xin also nodded in agreement, then asked Ding Ning, "Xiao Ding, is Ah Lan’s room still available? Or do you know where we can conveniently eat something?"

"Sister Lan’s room is still available, you can go there directly." Ding Ning looked at Chen Xin and Qin Lan with some envy.

She was actually very hungry, especially having to be on duty in the cafeteria. The smell of food made her even hungrier.

But duty required her to remain at her post until she could rest, no matter how hungry she was, instead of leaving to get food just because of hunger.

Chen Xin and Qin Lan noticed her situation but didn’t say much, bid farewell to Ding Ning, and then walked out of the cafeteria together.

Seeing Chen Xin and Qin Lan walking out, the researcher nearby immediately approached again and asked Chen Xin, "Mr. Chen Xin, what are you going to do?"

"I plan to rest a bit and eat something. I left in a hurry in the morning, and after arriving here, I’ve been busy the whole time and am a bit hungry now," Chen Xin replied and after thinking for a moment, said to the researcher, "You should go get something to eat too, you don’t have to keep following me."

The researcher immediately shook his head, saying, "No need, no need, we’ve already had lunch before coming here. I’d rather continue to follow you, Mr. Chen Xin, as watching you upgrade the equipment is very insightful and beneficial for me!"

Chen Xin understood that the system’s information state concept could instantly impart a wealth of knowledge and images to those witnessing the upgrade process, allowing knowledgeable individuals to absorb more. Thus, Chen Xin wasn’t surprised by the researcher’s words, and nodded, accepting the idea of the researcher following him.

However, Chen Xin restarted his previously parked Power Armor, wore the Power Armor, and along with Qin Lan, arrived at her old room, and then they took off the Power Armor together and walked into the room.

The room seemed to be in its usual state, though a thin layer of dust had settled on the table from Qin Lan not being there for over a month.

Qin Lan took out a cloth and wiped the table and chairs before inviting Chen Xin to sit down, and the researchers who followed them didn’t enter but instead was fascinated by the Power Armor parked at the door, seemingly wanting to study it.

Regarding this, Chen Xin didn’t bother him, but instead, he and Qin Lan took out emergency food from the silo behind the Power Armor and began eating.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 294 - 292 Boiler Transformation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The advancement of the boiler modification in the shelter, which concerns the survival of all survivors within the shelter, progressed rapidly. Although Chen Xin required many materials, fortunately, the underground highways between various shelters remained open. After spending some time gathering them, the materials he needed had already been transported to the police station’s shelter.

"Comrade Chen Xin, the materials you needed have arrived. When do you think you can start the boiler modification?" The commissar found Chen Xin who was eating in Qin Lan’s room. Although he felt a bit embarrassed to interrupt Chen Xin’s meal, he couldn’t help but impatiently ask Chen Xin.

During the time Chen Xin was eating, the temperature in the special police team area had increased thanks to Chen Xin upgrading the geothermic heat pump and heating. The temperature rose from the original minus ten degrees to about seven or eight degrees and was still rising, with hopes of breaking zero.

Moreover, because the modified geothermic heat pump generated more heat energy than before, the bathhouse, which relied on the geothermic heat pump to provide hot water, now had more hot water available. It could stay open longer, allowing more people to enjoy a hot bath.

This point was crucial for the special police team and even the entire police station shelter. After all, the majority of frostbite patients most needed not medicine, but hot water.

Therefore, from the perspective of the police station’s top officials, if the shelter’s boiler could be modified like the special police team’s geothermic heat pump to enhance heating efficiency and output more heat, it would undoubtedly be the most important task at hand.

For this reason, even though the commissar knew that Chen Xin and Qin Lan were eating, he still immediately sought out Chen Xin, hoping he could promptly modify the boiler.

Chen Xin was well aware of the importance of this matter. Seeing the commissar come to find him, he did not show any sign of hesitation and quickly finished his meal, then followed the commissar towards the boiler room he had visited once before.

Seeing this, Qin Lan hurriedly followed.

But the researcher who had been standing at Qin Lan’s room door, studying the two sets of Power Armor, was feeling conflicted. On one hand, he wanted to see how Chen Xin would upgrade the working boiler, but on the other hand, he was reluctant to leave the two Power Armors in front of him.

The few minutes of exploration earlier had made him deeply fascinated by the two Power Armors, eager to try them out himself, or even to take them apart and study them.

Fortunately, he remembered that these two Power Armors were made by Chen Xin, and he had promised to provide samples for his department to research. So, the researcher quickly adjusted his attitude, chasing after Chen Xin, prepared with a camera in his pocket this time.

When Chen Xin upgraded the geothermic heat pump earlier, he hadn’t had time to take out his camera before Chen Xin was finished, which he regretted deeply. This time, he planned ahead and was determined to record the entire modification process of Chen Xin.

The group arrived at the boiler room, and the materials Chen Xin required were already piled up there. Since there was also coal stacked in the boiler room, and Chen Xin’s materials were numerous, it made everything quite cramped for the moment.

Chen Xin didn’t mind this, walked over to the boiler, and prepared to start the upgrade.

The researcher immediately got close with his camera, standing behind Chen Xin and positioning himself to start filming.

Chen Xin didn’t mind being filmed. Seeing everyone’s expectant expressions, he directly used his system’s upgrade function on the boiler.

A green light flashed, and under its glow, the entire structure and appearance of the boiler transformed significantly from a layered combustion furnace to a Vortex Furnace. Its size, structure, and auxiliary equipment changed drastically, virtually making the boiler room appear entirely different after the green light swept over it.

However, the boiler itself was still burning vigorously, and due to the different combustion method of the Vortex Furnace, it emitted an even more intense roaring sound, like a dragon roaring in the furnace.

No one minded the roaring sound from the furnace. The louder it roared and the fiercer it burned, the more heat the boiler could produce, which was the most crucial thing for the shelter now.

However, after capturing the upgrade footage, the researcher approached Chen Xin and asked, "Mr. Chen Xin, isn’t the structure of this Vortex Furnace a bit different from common horizontal vortex furnaces?"

To this question, Chen Xin, the person who single-handedly upgraded and remodeled the boiler, appeared quite baffled. He shook his head and explained, "I’m not sure. I just felt that this structure worked better and suited the current needs more."

Hearing Chen Xin’s answer, the researcher felt helpless. He had long known about Chen Xin’s habit of only focusing on modifications and upgrades without explaining the principles behind them. Yet, for a researcher, seeing something without being able to understand its design philosophy and principles was indeed frustrating.

Especially when you can only explore everything by yourself, while the creator knows nothing about it.

In the researcher’s view, the Vortex Furnace in front of him was not a common horizontal Vortex Furnace because the combustion method of a Vortex Furnace enlarges the furnace chamber’s volume. Theoretically, under the same conditions, a Vortex Furnace should be smaller than a layered combustion furnace. Yet, the boiler in front of them not only had increased in size but also differed structurally from common Vortex Furnaces, which inevitably spurred the researcher’s curiosity.

Thankfully, after a moment’s hesitation, Chen Xin explained, "I made some adjustments to the boiler’s structure. Now the heating system’s pipes are connected directly to it, heating water into steam through the boiler and inputting it into the heating pipes to provide warmth for the entire shelter.

The original water heating wasn’t very effective at this temperature. Air heating is slightly more efficient, allowing the temperature to rise higher."

"Air heating does work better than water heating, but why is this particular structure used for the pipeline connection?" the researcher pointed to the heating pipes connected to the boiler, full of questions, but evidently, Chen Xin wouldn’t be explaining any of that.

Luckily, he knew Chen Xin wouldn’t explain, so he squatted by the steaming boiler alone, repeatedly reviewing the footage he recorded, frowning as he pondered the reasons behind the boiler’s design.

Chen Xin couldn’t help with this. He could only leave with Qin Lan.

The commissar and several leaders who had arrived from the city bureau were continuously expressing their gratitude to Chen Xin. This move by Chen Xin truly saved everyone in the shelter.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 295 - 293 Being Underestimated
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After finishing the modification of the police station shelter’s boiler, Chen Xin and Qin Lan returned to Qin Lan’s room once again.

It must be said, the heating effect of the boiler after modification is completely different. At least, as Chen Xin and Qin Lan sat in the room, they could clearly feel the temperature rising, and the frost that had formed on the walls was slowly dissipating.

"It seems the temperature has risen above zero," Qin Lan said, looking at the disappearing frost on the wall, and then at Chen Xin. "Now, the people in the shelter should be much better off, right?"

"It’s definitely more comfortable than the previous temperatures below minus ten degrees. At least above zero, it’s not so easy to get frostbite." Chen Xin deliberately took a thermometer, glanced at it, and saw the room temperature had indeed risen to 1°C, which made him smile.

This time, although the modifications to the boiler and the ground source heat pump earned Chen Xin two survival points, totaling ten thousand survival points and an additional one hundred survival points per day, Chen Xin invested eleven thousand survival points in these two modifications.

And this was even with the police gathering the necessary materials for him.

The investment required for large equipment modifications is indeed not low. Even if Chen Xin can currently earn over four hundred survival points a day, it would only amount to more than twelve thousand points a month. Adding the previous accumulation he had, Chen Xin currently had enough survival points to modify four more boilers.

Of course, this means that without additional income, Chen Xin can only modify four more boilers. Counting the five thousand survival points earned for each completed shelter modification, Chen Xin could actually modify all the boilers in the city’s shelters.

However, this would require full cooperation from the municipal government, or else without materials, the tenfold increase in survival point consumption would make it impossible for Chen Xin to modify even one boiler.

Before doing that, the ones who approached Chen Xin first were still the scientific institutions.

They were already gathering materials according to Chen Xin’s requirements, but it takes time to collect the materials. However, these researchers were already eager to understand what Chen Xin meant by the isotopic generator.

Faced with the eagerness of these researchers, Chen Xin hesitated for a moment, but after a little consideration, he and Qin Lan took two Power Armor suits and returned to the scientific institution set up in the city with the researchers.

It was called a scientific institution, but in reality, it only occupied a laboratory at the Polytechnic College, essentially serving as a relay station.

After all, the tasks they took on were not mainly research. They were responsible for receiving projects that needed Chen Xin’s upgrades, collected from various national scientific institutions by a special team from the upper echelons, and then sending them to Chen Xin along with the materials according to priority. Then they’d return the upgraded project products to the original institutions for reverse engineering.

So for this scientific institution, they were essentially equivalent to a transit warehouse, but logistics and warehousing personnel couldn’t be in charge of these matters.

Because all the project samples sent here were considered high-tech products, many things were even more fragile than the most exquisite porcelain. Additionally, they needed to categorize and assign priority levels, tasks that could not be handled by non-professionals.

This resulted in a group of serious researchers essentially doing the work of porters here.

However, these researchers themselves didn’t really mind. Having witnessed Chen Xin’s entirely unscientific upgrading capability, they knew how important their work was and how carefully the project samples sent here needed to be handled. They didn’t mind being reduced to mere warehouse porters.

But just because the researchers themselves were not bothered doesn’t mean there weren’t others who had complaints.

At least, when Chen Xin and Qin Lan followed the researchers to the lab located at the Polytechnic College shelter, a young researcher passing by showed displeasure towards Chen Xin, humming in displeasure and walking around him as if Chen Xin was something dirty.

This left Chen Xin puzzled, while the researchers around appeared embarrassed.

After Chen Xin asked, the researchers explained somewhat sheepishly.

Although Chen Xin’s capabilities were truly heaven-defying, especially in these end times, Chen Xin’s abilities saved a lot of research funding and time, allowing many important ongoing research projects to be swiftly converted into products. This was of strategic importance to the entire nation.

But Chen Xin’s capabilities were also unscientific. Even though all processes were "handmade" by him, he couldn’t explain the rationale or principles behind them, let alone any design thinking. This made him seem like a typical pseudo-scientist, a fraudulent amateur in the eyes of many academic or traditional researchers.

In these people’s view, the nation should not spend a penny on someone like Chen Xin, someone who deceived the nation unknowingly with his notoriety, and should not waste resources on him.

These resources should be used in more beneficial areas, like their research projects.

That young researcher just now was precisely one of those academic-centric individuals, whose mentor was a traditional researcher with some achievements, always looking down on such folks as Chen Xin.

After hearing the researchers’ explanations, Chen Xin could only shake his head helplessly. In a sense, he was indeed a fraud because he didn’t possess any knowledge and abilities himself; everything relied on the system.

The upgrades depended on the system’s functions, not his own abilities. In a way, Chen Xin was indeed deceiving people.

But the things he could provide were real, and all the upgrades were visible and effective, prompting the nation to specially create a task force and allocate national resources for this matter.

However, matters at the national strategic level were indeed of a very high tier, and Chen Xin’s abilities were also too crucial from the national perspective. The truth of the matter was sealed off. While the researchers at the Polytechnic College knew that this scientific institution was established because of Chen Xin, the civilian, it was difficult for them to know Chen Xin’s actual capabilities.

This undoubtedly created the illusion for those unaware that Chen Xin seemed like a fraud squandering national resources.

"Alright, just let it go. We should just focus on doing our own thing." Faced with this situation, Chen Xin could only feel helpless. After all, he couldn’t explain it to everyone individually. He could only say to do his job well, and eventually, the truth would become clear on its own.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 296 - 294: Industrial Foundation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the laboratory, researchers sat around Chen Xin, all looking at him with anticipation.

Although these researchers were aware of Chen Xin’s existence and his capabilities, very few had actually met him. Having Chen Xin present here was an exciting event for those who had long heard of his fame.

Especially since Chen Xin mentioned he was going to create an isotope generator, which made these researchers even more thrilled.

The isotope generator is a type of nuclear power, but according to Chen Xin, it clearly falls within the realm of small or even micro nuclear power. Unlike the low-power experimental technology of radioactive isotope generators, with the backing of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery, the isotope generator Chen Xin could produce was undoubtedly a technology that could be put to practical use.

This is crucial and eagerly anticipated for the current Flame Country, because large-scale energy technologies presently available are mainly thermal and nuclear power.

Solar energy is no longer usable due to lack of sunlight, hydropower has stopped due to frozen rivers, and wind power is unusable because of excessively strong winds. Under such circumstances, stable, clean, and small-footprint small and micro nuclear power becomes highly worthy of promotion and application.

If the construction and application requirements for small nuclear reactors and power plants weren’t so high, the Flame Country’s leaders might indeed have had plans for a large-scale nuclear power promotion.

Now, Chen Xin’s proposal of an isotope generator is like giving a pillow to a dozing Flame Country leadership. As long as Chen Xin can build the isotope generator he described and reverse-engineer the technology, the leaders would undoubtedly implement widespread application.

"Regarding the isotope generator, its structure should be a triangular column design, with a part for batteries on top for rapid replacement of the batteries inside. As for the batteries, they are the Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries I provided earlier. How is the reverse engineering of this technology progressing?" Chen Xin introduced to these researchers the isotope generator he wanted to make and drew a diagram on a whiteboard.

Regarding the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery, after Chen Xin submitted it to the state, it received high attention from the top echelons, and the nation’s most elite experts and scholars were mobilized for reverse research. The researchers present, as related personnel, were aware of these matters.

So when Chen Xin asked, they quickly replied, "The reverse engineering of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery is progressing smoothly. However, due to many technical challenges needing resolution, the project team has only successfully trial-produced a 10KW power experimental prototype so far, and there is still some distance from creating an engineering prototype and practical application."

Hearing this from the researchers, Chen Xin couldn’t help but frown as he didn’t expect that despite providing a finished product, the state still faced significant challenges in reverse engineering.

Chen Xin then asked the researchers, "What exactly is the issue holding back progress? Is there anything I can help with?"

"Well..." The researchers exchanged glances before one of them answered Chen Xin, "It’s mainly an issue of overall industrial capacity. While many things can be made in the lab, mass production is a different story..."

"Currently, the nation’s industrial facilities have suffered severe damage. Many important large-scale industrial equipment pieces were severely damaged during the previous disaster due to transportation difficulties because of their massive size. The state now relies on the portion of industrial facilities preserved before the disaster, leading to problems with processing certain materials and manufacturing components." Another researcher continued, "Although the state is steadily restoring industrial strength according to pre-disaster plans, this takes time. Moreover, components and industrial materials that used to be imported are now in short supply, so we can only seek alternative substitutes, which presents significant difficulties for industrial production."

Upon hearing this, Chen Xin could only nod solemnly. This is something he couldn’t improve alone.

Enhancing a nation’s industrial strength requires comprehensive improvements and developments, backed by a strong country and robust comprehensive national power, reflecting a country’s true strength.

And this cannot be achieved through the upgrade of one or two devices or projects; it requires boosting everything involved, from a single screw to a nuclear power plant, all of which can be encompassed.

"If this is the case, does it mean that the isotope generator could only be manufactured on my side?" Chen Xin stroked his chin, furrowing his brows.

"That’s very likely, and if the supply of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery can’t keep up, the application of the isotope generator will also face problems," a researcher hesitantly replied.

If the state cannot manufacture the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery, promoting the isotope generator would be meaningless, as without the battery, the isotope generator would be an empty shell, which is clearly unacceptable.

After careful consideration, Chen Xin asked the researchers, "Is it feasible to establish a production line for manufacturing the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery?"

"Well..." The researchers exchanged glances again before responding, "That’s certainly feasible, but regarding the matching components and materials..."

"Then let’s include raw material processing and component manufacturing in the production line!" Chen Xin knew he was making a bold claim. If this were before the disaster, no one would build a production line this way, since no one would establish the entire set of facilities from raw materials to components for a single product.

Typically, supporting facilities are established following the construction of relevant projects, with many components relying on external inputs rather than building a complete system at once.

However, for Chen Xin, who holds the ultimate weapon of the system, this was not something unachievable.

"Is this really possible?" The researchers were still somewhat incredulous.

Chen Xin, however, responded without hesitation, "Of course it is. I’ll carefully study what support facilities are needed when I go back. Once identified, we can directly establish such a production line to address some employment issues and pave the way for the future promotion of the isotope generator."

Seeing Chen Xin’s determination, the researchers could say no more and could only prepare to report this matter immediately to the upper-level task force.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 297 - 295: Transformation Plan
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Are you still able to contact other places now?" Hearing the researchers mention the need to report issues about the isotope generators and isotope thermoelectric battery production lines to the special task group, Chen Xin couldn’t help but wonder how exactly they were planning to establish contact.

The researchers provided a detailed answer to Chen Xin’s question: "Currently, our main means of communicating with the outside world is still reliant on telephone lines and defense cables. These are parts of the national communication network that existed before the disaster, intended to cater to communication needs following satellite failures.

As for the communication base stations we intended to set up, the projects to build communication networks between cities have been delayed due to the snowstorm. The work can only continue once the storm subsides."

Chen Xin nodded in understanding and asked another question: "What about radio communication?"

"Radio communication is practically useless. The interference from the snowstorm is too severe; even if a connection is made, the signal is intermittent and cannot facilitate effective communication." The researchers also felt helpless about this. Now that satellite communication is unavailable, besides wired telephones and defense cables, radio is still a comparatively good means of communication.

However, none of these communication methods can be used freely. Defense cables are part of national defense and military infrastructure and aren’t normally available for civilian use. Wired telephones can be used by the public, but the bandwidth is limited, and the ability to communicate is confined to places where telephone lines are installed.

Radio communication is a fairly decent method, but in today’s stormy weather, it’s still challenging to contact others.

This is why the research department and the state previously attempted to establish a communication network. Although this network still uses wireless communication, the signal between base stations could remain stable, allowing the construction of an effective and stable communication network.

However, with the entire Flame Country shrouded by the snowstorm, it’s impossible to continue construction, and the communication base stations that started building have had to halt temporarily.

This research institution is connected by telephone lines and defense cables due to its involvement in significant national research projects. In necessary situations, they can directly reach higher departments or even higher authorities, and they can also mobilize the military.

In the present situation, this research institution can be said to be the most powerful department in the whole city.

After understanding the situation, Chen Xin said nothing more and, after discussing with Qin Lan, decided to temporarily stay at this research institution.

As for accommodation conditions, this research institution is quite decent. Originally, the Polytechnic College was the only prestigious university in the city, and its conditions were already good.

After it was converted into a shelter to provide logistical support for researchers and accommodate the needs of the research institution’s secrecy, the Polytechnic College, despite being one of several mid-sized shelters in the city, actually has a relatively small population. This results in it having some of the best living conditions among the many shelters in the urban area.

Nevertheless, it’s still cold here, unlike the police station shelter, which, thanks to Chen Xin’s modifications, has temperatures reaching above freezing levels for comfort.

Fortunately, for Chen Xin, this is not a big issue. Putting on the power armor can keep him from feeling cold, and the Polytechnic College itself has a boiler, which is better than those in other shelters. If needed, Chen Xin can completely remodel the boiler here.

In fact, after learning that Chen Xin had modified the police station shelter’s boiler and came to the Polytechnic College, the leaders of the College have been looking forward to him upgrading their boiler as well.

Indeed, these college leaders have come to request Chen Xin’s help in modifying the boiler.

Chen Xin did not refuse this request, but the materials required for the modification need time to gather, so he didn’t handle it immediately.

However, besides the Polytechnic College, other shelters also need boiler modifications, and after receiving a report from the city police station, the city government attaches great importance to this matter as well.

Moreover, the city government’s idea is a bit different. Besides wanting to modify the heating boilers in each shelter, they hope Chen Xin can help upgrade the power generator boilers at the power plant too.

In the city’s view, once the heating boilers at each shelter are modified, their heating capabilities will improve.
