Although this will consume more coal, it will reduce the consumption of electric heating, thus conserving coal. Calculating both ways, the total coal consumption might not increase significantly.

And modifying the power plant’s boilers can further enhance combustion efficiency and heat effect, allowing the coal’s contained thermal energy to be utilized better while conserving coal consumption.

Thus, the city government specially dispatched an official to meet Chen Xin, outlining the city government’s thoughts in detail and hoping he could come up with a feasible renovation plan to help complete the conversions of the heating boilers and the power plant’s generator boilers.

The city government’s requests were exactly what Chen Xin was planning to do. Since both parties had the same ideas, the matter was quickly agreed upon. As for how to modify and implement, it required careful discussion and consultation.

Chen Xin didn’t have anything else to attend to at the moment, and the city government was keen on this matter, allowing him enough time and resources to discuss and plan it.

Following detailed discussions, the entire renovation plan was finalized, and according to calculations, if Chen Xin can complete modifications on all the large and medium-sized shelters’ heating boilers and the power plant’s generator boilers, it can reduce the city’s total coal consumption by 10%.

This is already a very satisfactory result, as in saving coal, it also solves the heating problems in various shelters. Although the external temperature remains extremely low due to the relentless snowstorm, the internal temperatures in shelters can surpass freezing, preventing many survivors from suffering from frostbite or hypothermia.

And such modifications also offer significant advantages to Chen Xin. Although he might expend a batch of survival points, the subsequent daily survival point increase would be a continuous income, which is worthwhile for him.

So, after formulating the plan, Chen Xin agreed to the city government’s requests, and they immediately started actively coordinating the materials needed for Chen Xin’s boiler upgrades.

Once the city government official who came to discuss the renovation plan had left, Chen Xin turned to Qin Lan and smiled: "The problems in the urban area can be resolved now, and people in other shelters will fare much better too!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 298 - 296: Improvement of Hospital Conditions
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the hospital, Hong Rui rubbed her aching lower back and sighed with relief that today she could finally finish work and rest.

In recent days, the number of patients visiting the hospital remained high, but it was noticeably less than the previous days, mostly decreasing among those from the city police department, the Polytechnic College, and the power plant.

From what she had heard, Hong Rui knew this was because the heating boilers in these three shelters had been upgraded, the interior temperature had risen, and naturally, fewer people suffered from hypothermia and frostbite.

This made Hong Rui quite envious, envying those in the shelters who had heating to warm themselves, not feeling so cold.

Although the hospital is a prioritized facility, its heating specifications are the same as other shelters, just that the measures are relatively better, with electric heating in key wards.

Aside from feeling envious, Hong Rui was also happy for the patients’ reduction, as the hospital shifts had shortened from sixteen hours to twelve hours after the heating at the three shelters improved, and the hospital corridors were no longer crowded with waiting patients.

This also made Hong Rui hope that if the hospital’s heating could be improved, too, then many patients with hypothermia and frostbite could receive more effective treatment and recover faster.

Thinking like this, Hong Rui walked towards her office. With long hours of overtime lately, her dark circles were pronounced, and her face was severely puffy, almost unrecognizable.

Before the disaster, for this kind of situation, Hong Rui would have tried all sorts of methods to reduce swelling and dark circles, applying thick makeup on her face to cover it up. But now, she was just heading to her office, eager to get a good sleep.

However, as Hong Rui walked toward her office, she ran into Chen Xin leading a group of people in that direction.

"Are you... Dr. Hong Rui?" Chen Xin froze for a moment before recognizing her, hurriedly greeting her, "Are you just off work? You look really tired!"

"It’s nothing, the hospital has just been busier lately. Mr. Chen, where are you heading?" Hong Rui responded to Chen Xin but shifted her gaze to notice among Chen Xin’s companions were the hospital’s deputy director and a city government official she had met once.

This made Hong Rui wonder why Chen Xin was walking with these important figures and even appeared to be leading them.

"Oh, the city government plans to upgrade the hospital’s heating boilers, and I’m in charge of the renovations," Chen Xin explained briefly to Hong Rui and said, "I’ll go ahead first and talk to you later, Dr. Hong Rui."

Seeing this, Hong Rui didn’t delay Chen Xin’s matters, realizing only afterward as they walked away that Chen Xin was referring to the boiler upgrade.

This left Hong Rui surprised; she never expected Chen Xin, whom she’d met a few times, had such capability. From observing those around him, Hong Rui could discern that Chen Xin was not just an ordinary person; he must hold a more significant position, otherwise the highest-ranked person in that group should have been the city official.

Yet Hong Rui only felt curious and didn’t dwell on the matter; more than being concerned about such things, she wanted a good sleep.

—————————————————————

Hong Rui woke up from sleep sweating in her office.

Feeling the rising temperature in the office and the warmth enveloping her, Hong Rui rubbed her forehead, clearing her mind before glancing at the thermometer hung in front of her desk, displaying 3°C.

"The boiler temperature has risen?" Hong Rui felt a bit surprised; previously, even the hospital’s heating wasn’t as good, with temperatures only reaching -7°C.

It was quite remarkable for the temperature to jump ten degrees, no wonder she was awakened by the heat.

Though she wasn’t sweating or anything intense, the warmth was clearly felt by Hong Rui and made a noticeable difference.

At least she didn’t have to sleep in her office, wearing sweaters, with quilts, blankets, and coats piled on top of her, compressing her until she couldn’t breathe just to keep a little warmth.

Taking off the heavy coat and blanket, Hong Rui got up, rubbed her face, and checking the time realized she had only slept for two hours, but it was almost meal break.

This left Hong Rui hesitating, unsure if she should go to eat or return to the finally warm bed and continue sleeping.

Ultimately, appetite won over sleepiness, and after rinsing her face at the office sink to wake herself up, Hong Rui walked heavily toward the cafeteria.

These days of back-to-back overtime, even as a psychologist, being redirected to help in the emergency department had kept her on her feet, leading to swollen feet unable to fit in her beloved high heels. Due to the cold, she switched to oversized flat snow boots.

In the snow boots, Hong Rui trotted to the cafeteria, looked at the queue at the service window and headed to the doctors’ window with some expectation.

The hospital’s meals have always been guaranteed, even when food quality at other shelters declined, the hospital here still had solid meals, considering the staff needed good nutrition.

And after the city government utilized emergency reserves, the hospital’s meals became even more enticing and mouth-watering.

Considering the past few days, if she couldn’t enjoy the hot and delicious nutritious meals post-work, Hong Rui doubted she could handle those consecutive long-hour shifts.

Thankfully, the hospital’s boiler renovation was complete; Hong Rui believed her workload ahead should lighten a bit.

As Hong Rui contemplated this, her turn to get meals finally arrived. Ordering her favorite dishes from the master chef at the window, she carried the meals intending to find a spot to eat.

"Dr. Hong Rui, come sit with us!" As Hong Rui looked around for a seat, Chen Xin’s voice suddenly rang out, directing her gaze.

She saw Chen Xin, along with the deputy director she’d met earlier and the city official, seated together for a meal, with an empty seat beside them.

Seeing this, Hong Rui hesitated slightly before walking over and sitting in the empty spot.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 299 - 297 Captured Researcher
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After lunch, the city government leader and the vice dean of the hospital both had their own matters to attend to. They parted ways with Chen Xin and left, one returning to the city government to report on the hospital boiler modification, the other to handle the hospital’s daily work. They were only able to have lunch with Chen Xin because of his ability to modify the boiler.

Chen Xin did not feel neglected or belittled by the leaders’ departure; on the contrary, he was not accustomed to receiving such fawning attention.

Now that all those leaders had left, Chen Xin felt a bit more at ease.

"Mr. Chen, do you have anything else this afternoon?" Hong Rui asked politely as she prepared to return to the office after finishing her meal.

"Nothing much in the afternoon; I’ll be heading back shortly." When Chen Xin mentioned going back, he didn’t mean returning home but rather to his temporary residence at Polytechnic College. After speaking, he seemed to remember something and said to Hong Rui, "By the way, Dr. Hong, the herbal seeds you gave me last time have sprouted, and they’re growing well. Should I prepare them for you?"

Hearing this, Hong Rui laughed and explained to Chen Xin, "Those herbs need to grow for at least three months before they can be used medicinally. Sprouting doesn’t mean they’re ready. Some need to be planted for over a year to have adequate medicinal efficacy, so there’s no rush with this.

However, it’s rare for someone like you, Mr. Chen, to remember this. At times like this, everyone’s focusing on growing more food, so it’s rare and commendable for you to help me grow medicinal herbs."

"Food has to be grown, but we can’t neglect the herbs either! These things can save lives, can’t they?" Chen Xin smiled, not explaining further.

If he really only had the 100-square-meter greenhouse he initially built when creating a shelter, he would only plant enough to feed himself. Never mind helping grow herbs, even trading vegetables would require careful calculation to ensure his survival.

But after acquiring the system, he had the extra capacity to help others, so he wouldn’t hesitate to offer a bit of kindness and assistance.

Human nature may be selfish, but there’s a baseline of goodness in everyone’s heart. Reaching out to help when others are in need isn’t a monumental or odd thing.

"Whatever the case, thank you for your help, Mr. Chen. Not only for helping with the herbs but also for modifying the boiler," Hong Rui felt compelled to thank Chen Xin, for both public and personal reasons: "By modifying the boiler, you improved the hospital’s heating, which was a huge help. Patients with frostbite and hypothermia can now be treated, and us doctors no longer have to work non-stop for ten-plus hours a day.

Even just for that, both we doctors and the patients owe you our thanks."

"That doesn’t bother me. Rather than thanking me, their surviving healthily is the best thanks I could get." Chen Xin didn’t pay much mind to Hong Rui’s gratitude. For him, saving even a few lives meant his recent hard work wasn’t in vain.

Hearing Chen Xin say this, Hong Rui’s eyes showed some astonishment, but she nodded and said, "If everyone could think like you, Mr. Chen, that would be wonderful."

"I think most people would think this way, wouldn’t they? I have things to do, so I’ll get going first. I won’t disturb your work any longer, Dr. Hong." After speaking, Chen Xin turned and left.

Watching his departing figure, Hong Rui’s eyes flickered, looking at Chen Xin with newfound admiration.

————————————————

Leaving the hospital, Chen Xin returned to Polytechnic College. Though he hadn’t done any physically exhausting work, the morning’s busyness and running between two shelters left him feeling a bit tired, wanting to rest for a while.

Just as Chen Xin was about to rest in the room prepared for him, a young Researcher, whom Chen Xin had previously encountered and who had shown disdain for him, approached him, speaking harshly, "The professor wants you; come with me!"

Without waiting for Chen Xin to respond, the young Researcher intended to pull Chen Xin along to see this professor he mentioned.

Before Chen Xin understood what was happening, he was being dragged by this young Researcher toward the boiler room.

Although he stumbled a bit being pulled by the young Researcher, Chen Xin quickly regained his footing, reversed the hold, and pinned down the young Researcher.

"What’s with you? Acting like a bandit!" Chen Xin frowned. Although he could understand why traditional researchers might look down on someone like him from the civilian world, it didn’t mean he was temperless, allowing others to order him around.

"Let go! How can you be so barbaric? Let go! My arm’s breaking!" The young Researcher, pinned down by Chen Xin, cried out, never expecting Chen Xin to resist or to be overpowered by him.

The young Researcher’s loud cries quickly drew the attention of others.

However, even surrounded by a crowd pointing and whispering, Chen Xin didn’t release the young Researcher, only pressing him on what was going on.

The young Researcher, perhaps feeling embarrassed or wanting to shoulder the responsibility himself without involving the professor, merely twisted his arm and remained stubbornly silent, refusing to explain, only telling Chen Xin to let go.

Faced with the young Researcher’s pigheadedness, Chen Xin felt increasingly irked, tightening his grip, causing the young Researcher to cry even louder while remaining unwilling to let go.

Though Qin Lan criticized Chen Xin for lacking exercise, being able to date a female police officer from the SWAT team as a girlfriend, his so-called lack of exercise only meant compared to Qin Lan. It didn’t mean Chen Xin was truly weak.

Compared to those spending all day in labs, keeping their bodies in sub-optimal condition, their strengths were not at the same level.

This left the young Researcher, no matter how he tried, unable to escape Chen Xin’s grasp.

Just when the two were locked in a stalemate, an elderly professor, having heard the commotion, arrived and looked at the pinned young Researcher, asking, "What’s going on here? I asked you to invite someone, and you got yourself caught?"

After speaking, the professor looked up at Chen Xin. Although he couldn’t help frowning, he still said to Chen Xin, "Comrade, whatever happened, could you please let go of my student first?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 300 - 298: The Apologizing Old Professor
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

With the old professor stepping forward as a guarantor, Chen Xin finally released the young researcher he had captured.

The researcher, after being freed by Chen Xin, quickly darted to the old professor’s side, glaring at Chen Xin with resentment, and complained to the professor, "Professor, this is the fraud! This person is too barbaric. I told him that you wanted to see him, and he twisted my arm and wouldn’t let me go!"

On hearing his student’s words, the old professor didn’t jump to conclusions but instead confirmed with Chen Xin, "Is this what happened? My student came to get you, and you twisted his arm?"

"That’s roughly accurate, but he left out some details." Chen Xin, seeing that the old professor didn’t seem unreasonable and wasn’t losing his temper, calmly explained, "I had just returned from outside when your student came charging at me with a brusque ’The professor wants to see you, come with me,’ and even grabbed my arm. If I wasn’t someone who exercises regularly, I would have been pulled down immediately.

If you were in my position, wouldn’t your first instinct be to resist and subdue the person?"

Hearing Chen Xin’s explanation, the old professor’s face twitched, and he turned angrily to his student, scolding him, "I asked you to invite him, and this is how you do it? How many times have I told you!"

Watching the old professor scold the young researcher, Chen Xin didn’t react or say much, just observed everything with indifference.

However, while Chen Xin remained indifferent, the young researcher was visibly unwilling, arguing to the old professor, "Professor, this is a fraud. Why should we be polite to someone like him? He’s surely thinking of some new tricks to fool us! You can’t be deceived by him!"

Hearing this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but find it amusing. He had never interacted with this young researcher, yet the guy was quick to brand him a barbarian and a fraud. This amused and felt ironic to Chen Xin, considering this person was a researcher at the Polytechnic College, responsible for crucial research in these end times. It was utterly absurd and ridiculous.

With these thoughts, Chen Xin turned his gaze to the old professor, curious about how he would handle the situation.

To Chen Xin, if this professor had produced such a student, he probably wasn’t someone to be respected as a venerable elder.

Yet, the old professor’s handling of the situation surprised Chen Xin and everyone else.

"I’m sorry, my failure in teaching is to blame for not imparting proper manners to him as his professor." The old professor bowed to Chen Xin, sincerely apologizing, and then explained, "Previously, I admittedly had some prejudices, thinking you were one of those con artists peddling so-called high-tech concepts for money, so I had a negative opinion of you. My student overheard those words, which created a bad impression of you.

But today, I’ve checked the boiler you improved, and it indeed shows significant enhancement compared to before. This proves you’re not a fraud, and I apologize for my previous prejudice against you.

Originally, I wanted my student to invite you over to discuss some technical issues and formally apologize, but I didn’t expect things to turn out like this, causing you trouble. I’m truly sorry."

"There’s no need for such formalities, sir! I can understand why you might have thought of me as a fraud. Were I in your shoes, I wouldn’t have believed it either." Chen Xin was taken aback by the old professor’s humble attitude and quickly assured him that he wasn’t bothered.

If the old professor had confronted him head-on or ignored him, Chen Xin would have accepted it and not cared much. But for an elder, regardless of whether he was truly at fault, to apologize so sincerely in front of a crowd, Chen Xin felt he had to be magnanimous.

The old professor’s apology was certainly genuine. After apologizing, he looked at the gathered crowd around, understanding his actions might have troubled Chen Xin, so he didn’t linger longer. He simply said to Chen Xin, "It seems today is not the suitable opportunity, so let’s leave it at that for now. I will pay you a visit when the time is right."

With those words, the old professor turned to leave, seeing his student still looking rebellious, he didn’t scold him further, just said, "Come back with me first."

Then he left with his students.

As for the others who saw the outcome, they whispered amongst themselves but didn’t say much and soon dispersed.

But Chen Xin stayed behind, unconsciously rubbing his chin, pondering over what exactly was going on.

If all of this was just as it appeared on the surface, there wasn’t much of an issue. Perhaps the old professor did have a moment of prejudice, and now that he could sincerely apologize, Chen Xin could forgive him and his student.

But if this was all premeditated... Chen Xin could only say that the darkness of human nature exceeds our imagination.

However, he didn’t think the old professor was doing it intentionally. After all, from a certain perspective, the old professor could have apologized even more sincerely, even staging an act with his student to play the victim, forcing Chen Xin to a corner, turning his justified stance into unjustified.

But the old professor didn’t do that. After apologizing, he turned and left cleanly and straightforwardly, without going through the motions, which made it hard for Chen Xin to judge whether this professor was good or bad.

Shaking his head, he decided not to dwell on it and went back to his room.

Although this matter had troubled Chen Xin somewhat, it was merely an episode. After thinking it over, he decided to ignore it. In his current situation, he truly couldn’t spare the time or energy to worry about such things.

Instead of worrying about such scheming maneuvers, Chen Xin felt it was more practical to spend some time considering the isotopic generator.

It’s already the end times, yet some people are still focused on personal gain and schemes. It’s truly shallow.

To bicker with such people would just drag oneself down to their level and then be beaten by their extensive experience.

Moreover, even if one managed to win such disputes, it wouldn’t bring any benefits, nor would it improve or help the survival situation of Flame Country or humanity at large.

Even if Chen Xin could alter the boilers and heating systems of all the city’s shelters, he could only save this one city. For the Flame Country, which is nearly engulfed in blizzards, it would be like a drop in the ocean, given that 99.9% of Flame Country’s people are suffering from the blizzards.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 301 - 299 The Tragic State of the Large Shelter
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Under the organization and planning of the city government, Chen Xin spent about a week completing the renovation of three large city shelters and six medium-sized ones, providing a faint warmth in the city amidst the ravages of the snowstorm.

Previously, Chen Xin did not have a direct sense of what this faint warmth meant. However, when he completed the boiler renovation for the hospital and went to the pedestrian street shelter to renovate the boiler for this large shelter, the survivors’ living conditions were shocking to him.

The pedestrian street shelter is a fairly large underground space, and because its structure is a double-layered underground pedestrian street with a sunken plaza, it was originally designed as a civil defense facility, so its ventilation is excellent.

Good ventilation is, of course, necessary for underground buildings, but it also means that heat loss is easier, especially since larger underground spaces require more vents, leading to more heat dissipation.

This resulted in the temperature of the pedestrian street shelter being lower than that of other medium-sized shelters.

Particularly, the open nature of the central sunken plaza of the pedestrian street, even after reinforcement and insulation measures during shelter renovation, remains a significant problem for reducing the shelter’s temperature.

Additionally, the linear structure of the pedestrian street leads to rapid air circulation within the entire shelter, which might not be noticeable during normal walks, but when the temperature drops below zero, the circulating air causes any bit of heat anywhere in the shelter to escape and dissipate quickly.

This has led to a particularly high number of people suffering from frostbite and hypothermia in the pedestrian street shelter. Many are unable to leave their rooms to seek treatment and can only lie in cold and cramped rooms, wrapped in blankets and quilts that have long since become icy, huddled near the radiator, shivering as their conditions worsen.

Although the shelter managers have arranged for patrol staff to help survivors in need, the sheer number of people and the vastness of the area make it difficult for patrol staff to provide timely assistance and treatment to everyone.

Many only reach a hospital for treatment when their conditions become severe, which is still fortunate. Some, due to weakness or other illnesses, simply perish in the cold.

When Chen Xin arrived at the pedestrian street shelter, at the entrance of the sunken plaza, he saw no fewer than twenty body bags, already containing bodies, lined up at the shelter entrance, waiting to be sent for cremation.

This was a heart-wrenching sight. Seeing the survivors cramped in small rooms, converted from the storefronts of the original underground pedestrian street, Chen Xin could deeply feel their helplessness and despair.

Apocalypse, disaster, cold... all of this leaves no hope in sight.

It must be said, the pedestrian street shelter indeed has the worst insulation of all the shelters in the city. Compared to it, the other two large shelters do not lose heat as easily.

In fact, even after Chen Xin completed the boiler renovation and replaced the heating pipeline with gas heating, the temperature inside the pedestrian street shelter did not rise above zero.

Fortunately, the temperature inside did receive a significant boost after the boiler renovation, rising from minus twenty-something degrees to minus a few degrees, just barely enough to prevent the residents from feeling no warmth even when wrapped in blankets near the radiator.

After Chen Xin renovated the boiler and heating pipeline, as long as residents kept their room doors closed, they could, with the aid of the heating, allow the temperature in a small local space to rise above zero.

However, compared to other shelters where the overall temperature rose above zero, conditions here were undoubtedly much harsher.

Luckily, the hot water coming out of the boiler room was still scalding, allowing people here to enjoy a warm shower, alleviating the pain they had endured for days due to continuous low temperatures.

After completing the renovations of three large and six medium-sized city shelters, Chen Xin could finally focus his energy on the isotope generator project.

After all, though Chen Xin had completed the boiler renovations for all large and medium-sized city shelters, saving tens of thousands from freezing to death in the snowstorm, over 99.99% of Flame Country’s population was still facing the same fate.

Moreover, they were not as fortunate to have someone like Chen Xin helping them; they could only rely on themselves to withstand the snowstorm.

Regarding this, Chen Xin truly had no confidence that they could manage to get through it.

For this reason, Chen Xin consulted researchers, asking if they had other methods to help those still suffering in the snowstorm, but the answers were disheartening.

Even the nation had no better strategies to cope with this dreadful snow disaster, other than increasing heating efforts to endure through it.

This only made Chen Xin pay more attention to the isotope generator project.

Theoretically, an isotope generator could supply all electricity needs for a shelter, and the equipment itself doesn’t take up much space. Once assembled, it could fit into a normal-sized bedroom.

If mass-produced and deployed to shelters nationwide, they could provide ample electricity for electric heating, complementing coal heating, which would enable people to endure this snow disaster, the end of which is unknown.

This is why, despite knowing the type approval and manufacturing of the isotope generator would take time, Chen Xin still devoted his energy to the project.

In the past, with weather systems’ regulation, even the fiercest snowstorm would last only a few days, and widespread cold weather wouldn’t persist for long.

But with weather systems now collapsed and the world frozen over, this snowstorm has already lasted over two weeks, and no one knows when it will end.

It could end tomorrow, next week, next month, or possibly last an entire year like storms on Mars.

Therefore, to prevent the situation from worsening further, Chen Xin felt that if he could, he should do whatever possible, at least to save more lives, rather than watch them freeze to death.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 302 - 300: The Indescribable Upgrade Process
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The research on isotope generators still has to start from the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery.

As the energy source for the isotope generator, the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery is something Chen Xin has already mastered completely. He just needs raw materials, and he can manufacture them endlessly.

Of course, as a single person, the efficiency of manufacturing the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery has its limits. Even though he has the system’s upgrade functionality, allowing him to upgrade anything he sees, in terms of efficiency and cost-effectiveness, it’s still better to establish a production line and let others handle the manufacturing.

However, before that, to facilitate the construction and research of isotope generators, Chen Xin manually crafted a batch of Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries as research materials.

"Mr. Chen Xin, how is this isotope generator supposed to be made?" The researchers delivered the materials and components Chen Xin needed to his laboratory, eagerly watching him, wanting to know how exactly he planned to construct this seemingly sci-fi isotope generator.

Chen Xin only smiled and then explained, "It’s hard to explain, just watch. Generally, you connect three batteries together and increase their output power."

After saying this, Chen Xin started assembling the parts in front of him.

As before, the system can indeed achieve infinite upgrades, turning an ordinary car battery into an Isotope Thermoelectric Battery. But unless Chen Xin assembles the three Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries together and creates something that can be called a frame, forming a unit that can be viewed as a generator, he cannot upgrade it into an isotope generator.

After all, three batteries are just three batteries. Without assembling them together, they cannot be regarded as a generator.

Seeing Chen Xin assembling the parts, the researchers quickly gathered around, some helping, some recording. Although busy, they were organized and didn’t hinder Chen Xin, demonstrating a high level of professionalism and coordination.

While Chen Xin and the others were assembling the parts, an old professor who had previously had a conflict with Chen Xin also rushed over.

Even though this project was supposed to be highly confidential, preventing unauthorized access, the old professor managed to convince the gatekeepers to let him in after identifying himself to plead with Chen Xin.

"That old professor? Does he want to join this project too?" Chen Xin was a bit surprised, and at the same time, he questioned the old professor’s motives.

But the person pleading for the old professor explained to Chen Xin, "This old professor’s name is Zhou. He is the academic leader of the Polytechnic College and a leading nuclear physics expert in the country. If there’s anyone in the Polytechnic College who can assist you, Mr. Chen, in developing the isotope generator, it would be this old professor."

Chen Xin did not comment on this assessment, as he indeed did not understand these matters. Plus, too many so-called experts and scholars had emerged online in recent years, with everyone daring to claim to be a renowned scholar or authority in a certain field, turning the term "expert" into a joke.

However, considering that the people around him were all state-appointed researchers, Chen Xin hesitated for a moment before nodding and agreeing to let them bring in the old professor.

The old professor was quickly invited in. He saw the things Chen Xin and the others were assembling but refrained from making any rash evaluations or intervening; he just stood by and seriously watched Chen Xin and the others put the components together.

The assembled equipment was a triangular frame approximately two meters high, with a disc at both the base and the top, which had three slots just right for fixing the Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries.

Besides that, the entire base of the frame housed some simple circuits, seemingly intended to channel the battery’s electric power. The whole setup looked undeniably crude and rudimentary.

However, none of the people in the lab considered this odd.

Chen Xin knew that after his upgrade, the setup would look much better, so there was no need to waste unnecessary energy on the prototype.

Meanwhile, both the old professor and other researchers were already accustomed to prototype devices appearing crude, as scientific research has always been about cobbling something together to solve whether it exists or not, and then gradually improving it.

No prototype has ever emerged fully functional, nor has any researcher prioritized aesthetics in making a prototype.

Even so, the old professor frowned slightly and remarked, "Isn’t the circuit design a bit too crude? It’s just connecting the three batteries in parallel; why not optimize the connection method of the circuits?"

"We’ll assemble the equipment first; I’ll address the optimization shortly." Chen Xin turned back to the old professor and explained, "This is my ability. I accidentally acquired Savant syndrome, allowing me to optimize and modify any equipment. However, I know how to do it, but I can’t explain the principles behind it."

"Is that so?" The old professor clearly had his understanding challenged but didn’t express much beyond that. After all, Chen Xin’s previous feat of modifying the boiler was undeniable. He had no intention to comment further without firsthand witnessing Chen Xin’s ability.

As long as one can validate the results and replicate the process, it is scientific, according to the old professor. Science should not be rigid doctrines and formulas but a method of cognition that can be falsified. As long as it can be falsified, it aligns with scientific principles.

As for incomprehensibility, that merely indicates a lack of accumulated knowledge and an inability to comprehend all things.

Chen Xin didn’t plan to explain much to the old professor either. Sometimes, demonstrating once is far more effective than explaining a thousand times, so after attaching the three Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries to the device before him, he directly activated the system’s upgrade functionality.

A green light flashed, and in an instant, what was once a rough device underwent tremendous changes. The previously exposed components were now encased in a layer of pretty white shell, looking simple and clean.

The previously criticized circuit connections had gained a new appearance, although hidden beneath the shell, it was evident that the circuits had been newly optimized.

This was the first time the old professor had seen Chen Xin’s ability in action, leaving him in utter astonishment. Astounded by what he "saw," he couldn’t help but murmur, "What manner of... simply..."

The life-long rigorous academic old professor, at this point, didn’t know what to say. He couldn’t even find an accurate adjective to describe the scene he was "seeing."

Watching the old professor’s reaction, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel a bit helpless. Luckily, his ability wasn’t derived from Cthulhu Mythos; otherwise, with the system’s performance, the old professor would most likely be mentally impacted and turn into a follower of the Ancient Dominators.

After all, system upgrades, from the perspective of ordinary people, indeed appear indescribably mysterious.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 303 - 301 Electroweak Interaction Power Generation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Although the process of Chen Xin’s upgrade is an indescribable embodiment for ordinary people, the more knowledge one possesses, the more information one can obtain. Those who are ignorant, however, will not gain any information, and no matter what, ordinary people cannot comprehend the truth behind the system upgrade.

However, for knowledgeable individuals like the old professor and other researchers, witnessing how Chen Xin upgrades his equipment provides them with substantial insights.

"Is it really possible to do this? Circuits can actually be connected this way, and isotopes can be catalyzed to accelerate the decay process and release more radiation energy?" The old professor observed a process during Chen Xin’s upgrade that completely overturned the existing physical theories, almost leading him to a breakdown, as it nearly toppled his decades-old understanding of physics.

From a physics standpoint, the decay period, or half-life, of isotopes is an inherent property of radioactive elements, unaffected by external factors.

This is why scientists can calculate the age of Blue Star and even the entire universe based on the decay of radioactive elements, because this period is stable and immune to any interference.

If there were means to accelerate the decay of radioactive elements, then nuclear waste would no longer need to be buried or left in uninhabited areas, waiting for natural decay.

If this were true... it would indeed be a world-changing event because it would overturn the foundation of modern physics, requiring a complete re-establishment of the entire knowledge system of physics.

"How exactly do you accelerate the decay of isotopes?" The old professor excitedly grabbed Chen Xin’s clothing and pressed him for answers, his excitement akin to a man encountering a peerless beauty after decades of restraint.

Although this description might not be entirely appropriate, to a scientist who has dedicated his life to nuclear physics research, the technology demonstrated by Chen Xin to accelerate the decay process undoubtedly embodies the allure of a peerless beauty to any scientist who understands its significance.

"Sir, could you let go of me first? With you grabbing me like this, I can’t explain!" Chen Xin tried to pry open the old professor’s grip, but was surprised how the frail old professor’s hands were like iron clamps, unyielding and tough to move.

Upon Chen Xin’s urging, the old professor finally released him and continued asking, "How did you do it? Why can you accelerate the decay process? Don’t you realize this completely overturns the foundation of modern physics?"

"Please calm down, sir. As I said before, I know how to do it, but I don’t know how to explain it!" Chen Xin responded with a wry smile to the old professor.

This is the perfect example of how telling one lie necessitates ten more to cover it up. Right now, he truly has no way to explain to the old professor what is happening or how the system accomplishes something that completely subverts modern physics knowledge.

However, a nearby researcher seemed to have some understanding, thought for a moment, and proposed, "Could it be that Weak Nuclear Force influences the decay? This is feasible within the current theoretical framework of physics, it’s just that humans have no way to affect Weak Nuclear Force yet."

"Humankind is only in a stage of awareness regarding Weak Nuclear Force now; a complete theoretical framework hasn’t even been established, let alone discussing influence and utilization. If it’s really Weak Nuclear Force, isn’t it too terrifying?" Another researcher couldn’t help but feel an eerie chill; seeing a device capable of influencing a secondary force being handcrafted so nonchalantly in front of him was no less shocking than an atomic bomb exploding before his eyes.

The researchers debated endlessly, while the old professor, after releasing Chen Xin, immediately pounced on the equipment to scrutinize it. After carefully observing for a while, he directly asked Chen Xin, "Can I disassemble it? I want to take it apart for thorough research!"

"Of course, you can. The prototypes I make are meant for scientists like you to take apart for reverse engineering." Chen Xin readily agreed, as long as the old professor didn’t force him to explain the principles, he had no problem making two more for them to dismantle and study.

While the old professor called over two researchers to help remove the Isotope Battery from the machine and began disassembling the Isotope Generator, other researchers continued to argue over how this catalysis of radioactive element decay was achieved.

"Nuclear decay is a spontaneous change in the atomic nucleus, it’s a quantum leap process, and it should obey quantum statistical laws. The exact moment of decay cannot be predicted, but the decay period is consistent. I think the machine’s principle is concentrating the decay process into a short time frame, while the decay period itself remains unchanged." Based on his knowledge, one researcher shared his understanding.

"But how can you make decay concentrate within a short time frame? Doesn’t this still boil down to the application of Weak Nuclear Force?" Another researcher thought this idea was nonsense and very unscientific; the decay process should be uniform and constant in the absence of influence; otherwise, the half-life of substances wouldn’t be a significant metric for determining time.

As the researchers debated, Chen Xin could only stand by and watch their discussion. He wasn’t a top student; he only touched upon basic nuclear physics concepts in high school, not even scratching the surface. Watching these researchers almost come to blows over this bewildering topic left him speechless, while also marveling at the wonders of the world.

Who would have thought that something casually made by someone who isn’t exactly a top student but not a slacker either could nearly cause a fight among a group of top students?

"Is it the Weak Nuclear Force affecting the decay, and did you use the unification of Electromagnetic Force and Weak Nuclear Force to affect the decay through Electroweak Interaction?" the old professor, upon dismantling the equipment and studying its internal structure, asked, lost in thought but suddenly coming out with a question.

Chen Xin couldn’t answer; he had only searched for information on Weak Nuclear Force while writing novels before, knowing that it, along with Strong Nuclear Force, Electromagnetic Force, and Universal Gravitational Force, is referred to as one of the four fundamental forces.

And in pre-disaster online science articles, the four fundamental forces were prophesied by Einstein as the "Divine Power" that, once mastered by humanity, could allow entry into higher dimensional universes and transform the world, with some even claiming that once these four fundamental forces, especially Weak Nuclear Force, were mastered, time would merely become a coordinate, enabling human time travel.

And now a device based on the application of Electromagnetic Force and Weak Nuclear Force was laid out right in front of these researchers.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 304 - 302: Plan Finalized
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The impact of the isotope generator on the scientific researchers clearly exceeded Chen Xin’s expectations. He didn’t expect something like this to be so exaggerated.

For Chen Xin, it was just a new type of generator, but sometimes the importance of something is not determined by its complexity or cost. It may be a simple thing, yet it contains a high level of technological content.

It’s like the smartphones we see everywhere in our daily lives; if placed in the World War II era, these would be world-changing technologies.

Because people of that time could never imagine that a computer could be the size of a palm and fit into a person’s pocket.

So Chen Xin could understand the shock and impact brought by the isotope generator, but it did not affect his original plans and arrangements.

While many researchers focused their efforts on dissecting the isotope generator, hoping to crack its technology and find the physical laws of using the electromagnetic force to influence the weak nuclear force to change the decay cycle, Chen Xin and a few not-so-crazy researchers were preparing to establish a production line for the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery.

"From the current statistics, large shelters do need isotope generators, while medium shelters can also install them if they have high-energy-consuming projects. For small shelters, using isotope thermoelectric batteries is sufficient to meet their electricity needs," a researcher explained to Chen Xin while holding a chart.

Such statistics are necessary, although with Chen Xin’s help in setting up the production line, there is no worry about the output, but not every shelter can be equipped with an isotope generator, as that would cause severe waste.

In this post-apocalyptic world, obtaining every resource is more challenging and precious than before, so there should be and must not be any waste.

Moreover, with actual data surveys, it also helps control the production of isotope thermoelectric batteries and isotope generators, avoiding excessive generator production without enough battery supply.

After all, a generator requires three batteries, and if battery production can’t keep up, the generator is useless even if available.

"Let’s first establish the battery production line. We can initially produce a batch of isotope thermoelectric batteries to ease the situation at some shelters." Chen Xin nodded in understanding after hearing the statistical results.

The researcher holding the survey report also nodded, affirming, "This is the most appropriate plan for now. The country had already reverse-engineered isotope thermoelectric batteries before. If we set up a production line here, based on our technical experience, other places can quickly establish new production lines, thus effectively increasing output.

I don’t recommend hastily setting up an isotope generator production line. Its technology has not yet been analyzed, and even if we establish a production line, it would be challenging to control product quality effectively. If the yield rate is too low, it would only waste resources."

"There’s no need to worry about the yield rate. Workers just need to know how to operate the production line and don’t need to understand the product’s technical principles." Chen Xin retorted but didn’t oppose this decision: "However, you’re right. Let’s first set up the production line now."

"Okay, then we’ll contact the city government and first determine the site." The researcher nodded, acknowledging that although the decision was made, the ensuing work would not be easy.

Now, it is still snowing heavily outside, with temperatures below minus seventy degrees. In such an environment, let alone construction; even transporting materials is as difficult as reaching the sky.

In the case of gale-force winds, even a train filled with coal is at risk of being derailed by the wind. Although the country is now sparing no effort to ensure the transport of railway materials, and comrades on the railway front are risking their lives to maintain smooth railway transportation, the supply of materials remains tight.

With limited transport capacity, coal, the most important material, is prioritized for transport. If building the production line here requires importing materials from outside, it will inevitably squeeze the precious transport capacity and may have more severe consequences.

Therefore, how to allocate materials requires careful study by the staff.

As for construction and the site, this is relatively easier and less troublesome.

After researchers coordinated with the city government, a previously sealed small shelter was allocated. Although the place isn’t large and the interior requires renovation, at least they have a location.

And for construction workers... to put it bluntly, people are the least lacking right now.

Despite such a production line requiring professional construction workers, in current times, having someone willing to work is good enough, and finding highly skilled people is challenging.

In summary, with the cooperation of the city government, despite the blizzards and strong winds, the isotope thermoelectric battery production line is steadily being constructed, albeit with difficulties.

While the city government and research institutions were advancing the production line’s construction, Chen Xin and Qin Lan returned to their shelter together.

——————————————

"It’s still more comfortable here!" Qin Lan said while taking off her Power Armor, stretching her body, and asking Chen Xin, who was also taking off his Power Armor: "Why did you suddenly decide to come back? Is there something that absolutely needed you to return?"

"Yes, there are some things to do, and I find the workbench at home more convenient to use." Chen Xin also stretched his body joints. Although the Power Armor has an assist mechanism, wearing it for a long time still tires a person.

Actually, Chen Xin wanted to make a multitool to conveniently set up the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery production line.

Of course, this multitool is not the integrated handheld device with a micro-computer, analysis probe kit, and small-scale manufacturing unit from "Mass Effect." Chen Xin wanted to make something similar to a mechanical arm.

Simply put, it means integrating a micro-computer, some analysis and detection instruments, and a set of processing tools into a single mechanical arm that can be worn.

This idea came to Chen Xin after encountering some inconveniences while working in the city. If someone always needs to spend time searching for tools or frequently switching screwdrivers and wrenches while working, anyone would want a tool that could handle all work scenarios.

More importantly, in Chen Xin’s vision, this multitool should also have the processing capabilities of a workbench and be able to manufacture items based on blueprints.

This is very important to Chen Xin because he plans to use the mechanical arm to construct the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery production line.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 305 - 303: Master of Casting
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

At the workbench, Chen Xin was designing his own multi-tool.

Although this idea was inspired by the omni-tool from the game "Mass Effect," unlike in the game where a single chip implantation could achieve all functions, Chen Xin couldn’t create such a tool, or rather, didn’t have enough survival points and resources to upgrade it.

So, Chen Xin planned to make a mechanical arm integrating all commonly used tools together, which was equivalent to carrying a toolbox on him.

And after equipping it with a micro-computer and scanning analysis instruments, this mechanical arm could analyze the situation by itself and replace suitable tools, eliminating the user’s hassle of changing different types of wrenches and screwdrivers.

People who don’t work in factories may never understand how annoying it is to not have the right wrench or screwdriver when doing on-site construction.

Sometimes it might be just because a wrench is missing, the work has to be delayed for hours or even days to borrow or buy one, which often results in significant time delays, and a day’s downtime for factory equipment can cost hundreds of thousands or even millions.

So having such a mechanical arm would indeed be a fantastic choice, even if it turns out to be heavy, it’s worth it.

Chen Xin was imagining this mechanical arm in his mind while also gathering the items he needed.

There’s no ready-made micro-computer, but Chen Xin found an old phone as a substitute, it’s not useful for anything else now, perfect to use as a replacement.

As for analysis and detection instruments, Chen Xin put together a camera, a temperature gun, a rangefinder, a speedometer, a tachometer... a whole bunch of things, although it seemed a bit much, once integrated, it would be fine.

The tools part was the simplest, just taking out a toolbox can meet various needs.

But besides these, Chen Xin also wanted to add a 3D printer on it.

This idea came from Chen Xin’s father’s suggestion, long-time on-site construction personnel know that sometimes parts need to be processed on-site, like welding a bracket, connecting an elbow, or even temporarily processing a part...

Because on-site construction often encounters all sorts of situations, equipment that doesn’t fit, mismatched parts, it’s all too common. In Chen Xin’s father’s decades-long career, he can’t even remember how many parts he had to process on-site.

So seeing that Chen Xin wanted to integrate a multi-tool, as a father, he gave his son a bit of elder’s experience.

Chen Xin naturally accepted his father’s suggestion, and considered cutting, welding, sheet metal work, milling, these functions as necessary. But rather than materials processing parts on-site, Chen Xin was more inclined to directly 3D print a suitable part.

However, he currently didn’t have a 3D printer at hand, so this feature had to be delayed, waiting until he finishes the mechanical arm and then see if there are any alternative solutions for a secondary upgrade.

And not just the 3D printing wasn’t added, argon arc welding, electric welding, sheet metal, milling and these functions weren’t integrated due to lack of equipment, could only wait for a secondary upgrade.

But this wasn’t much of an issue for Chen Xin, it was just a matter of spending more survival points during the secondary upgrade.

Besides, even with these shortcomings, he was determined to finish this mechanical arm.

After gathering everything, Chen Xin found an angle iron, did some cutting and processing on the workbench, and started piling various tools and instruments on it.

This looked quite like a green-skinned tech brute contemplating a new big boom, as the sparks flew from metal cutting, it was a complete jumble of haphazardly piled tools, some instruments were even simply taped on...

The assembled finished product carried a strong wasteland punk style, very green-skinned in nature, rough, heavy, and couldn’t even be described as practical, completely just because "I feel like it’s good this way" and so it was done without any sense of reason or design guidelines.

Of course, what Chen Xin ultimately used wasn’t this green-skinned style object. In fact, the final assembled piece he couldn’t use either because the tools attached were simply too many, it was exhausting to even lift, let alone use.

So an upgrade was necessary.

After a green light flashed, the originally green-skinned style device turned into a mechanical arm with a Mechanical God Sect style. Although still robust and complex, it became much tidier, with no unnecessary wire ends or tape, the originally exposed angle iron turned into a silver-white support shaft, encased in a metal shell, becoming a mechanical arm in the true sense.

The various complicated tools and instruments that Chen Xin had jammed onto the mechanical arm were now well integrated, incorporated into the mechanical arm. Some replaceable parts and components were neatly stowed and could be easily switched at any time.

The entire mechanical arm looked like a large backpack, with a three-axis rotating mechanical arm folded at the back and a display screen next to it that could extend to beside the head for operation and data display.

Of course, the 3D printing, welding, sheet metal, cutting, and milling processes that Chen Xin needed earlier were now integrated into the mechanical arm, which could now handle all on-site construction needs. Chen Xin even added a detachable drill on it to mix cement if switched to a mixer.

Although the functionality was comprehensive, the integrated and upgraded mechanical arm also became lighter and more convenient to use, but after trying it, Chen Xin found it still couldn’t be easily used by one person.

At least, Chen Xin himself couldn’t easily lift it without wearing the Power Armor or Exoskeleton.

This left Chen Xin scratching his head, the tool was made, but he couldn’t lift it himself, which was truly ironic. Did it mean he had to get fit, train to be a weightlifter first, and then use it?

Or should he integrate it directly into the Power Armor and play the role of a Master of Casting from the Mechanical God Sect?

Chen Xin scratched his brow, thinking this was actually quite a good idea.

Moreover, he had to admit, he did feel quite like a Master of Casting now.

So he acted on his thoughts, brought over his Power Armor, and had Qin Lan, dressed in her Power Armor, help attach the entire mechanical arm to the back of his Power Armor.

Then... with another upgrade, a set of Power Armor with a mechanical arm appeared.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 306 - 304: Sniping Power Armor
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Once the manufactured multi-purpose mechanical arm was installed on the Power Armor, it was as if the Power Armor had a third hand on its back.

Although this "hand" is a three-axis linked mechanical arm, equipped at the end with a drill, pliers, and a screwdriver, the hidden saw blade makes it look more like a torture device rather than a tool, it can serve both purposes in terms of usage.

After all, as a tool, it is used for processing steel; if you use it to process flesh, it would only result in a bloody mess.

Especially now that this mechanical arm comes with a 3D printing function, as long as your imagination is wild enough, it could play out scenarios pleasing even to Evil God, allowing for demonic ascension gameplay.

Of course, Chen Xin created this mechanical arm not for 18G; for him, it is a legitimate mechanical tool, at most sometimes doubling as a close combat weapon for the Power Armor.

After donning the Power Armor again, Chen Xin tested the performance of this mechanical arm in front of the workbench, using the arm to create and attach accessories for Qin Lan’s Power Armor.

"Is this a targeting scope?" Qin Lan asked curiously as she watched Chen Xin install several lenses and mirrors onto the helmet of her Power Armor.

"A combination multi-vision mode targeting scope, with infrared, low-light, sonar, and other modes, can lock onto life signals within five hundred meters using various modes like sound waves and heat sources. Even if the target is hiding inside buildings or buried beneath ruins, it can easily find and lock onto them." Chen Xin explained to Qin Lan, while also testing the functionality of the scope through the mechanical arm: "Additionally, it comes with hacking capabilities; within a hundred meters, you can use the built-in signal transmitter to hack into any devices within your sight range that can connect to wireless networks. I’ve installed a small program for you; the hacking function is automatic, but you can also use wired connections to manually hack in, testing your hacking skills."

As Chen Xin spoke, he stretched out and pulled a connection cable from the hidden part of the scope, demonstrating the wired connection mode of this equipment to Qin Lan.

"What if the connector doesn’t match?" Qin Lan was somewhat stunned looking at Chen Xin, unable to believe he could make a scope with so many features, but what came to her mind wasn’t the tactical possibilities this scope could achieve, rather what would happen if the connection line didn’t fit.

"The cable connector has a deformation adaptive structure; theoretically, any interface can be connected. If there’s no interface, then hack wirelessly." Chen Xin activated the deformation feature of the connector, demonstrating an interface resembling liquid metal to Qin Lan.

At this moment, Qin Lan regained her usual calm and thoughtfulness, asking Chen Xin: "What is the magnification range of this scope?"

"1.5~16 times magnification, millisecond automatic zoom, with wind resistance and wind deviation correction calculations. I believe in the hands of a sniper like you, it can unleash tremendous power." Chen Xin put the cable back while adjusting the lenses of the scope for zooming, testing their sensitivity.

Upon hearing this, Qin Lan couldn’t help but feel a sense of awe; indeed, for a sniper, it’s almost like a divine tool.

"What about the gun?" As a sniper, with a scope akin to a divine tool, having a high-precision sniper rifle would allow one to transform into a battlefield grim reaper.

"I can build one, but currently..." Chen Xin glanced at the Power Armor, shook his head, and said: "First, there’s no weapon manufacturing permit; second, no actual need, because it’s meant to be a sniper rifle for the Mecha. Caliber below 12.7mm doesn’t make much sense, but for such large-caliber sniper rifles... Do you think I have enemies that require such powerful weapons?"

Qin Lan thought seriously upon hearing Chen Xin, and indeed, for Power Armor, the size of the gun needs to be custom-made, and due to the Power Armor’s bearing capacity, it can withstand much larger recoil than a human.

If that’s the case, why use small-power firearms? Why not build a big boom! Ideally one shot would solve all problems; just one shot could kill all enemies!

However, this greenish weapon manufacturing approach is indeed somewhat extreme, and it’s not suitable for Qin Lan’s Power Armor, which aims to develop along the sniper route.

Even if Chen Xin really made a single-soldier mushroom bomb launcher capable of planting mushrooms relying on the Power Armor’s radiation protection ability, it wouldn’t require a sniper Power Armor to do this.

The weapon truly suitable for Qin Lan should be a high-precision, high-power, high-penetration top sniper rifle, preferably effective within its range without wind deviation, ignoring wind resistance, bullets capable of piercing tank armor, and such weapon would fit a sniper-type Power Armor.

As for Chen Xin’s own Power Armor, with the versatile mechanical arm, he is a Master of Casting, not developing towards the battlefield. Thus, having sufficient self-defense capability is enough, and even if there’s something that requires combat, he can create a Fire God Cannon on-site using the 3D printing function on his mechanical arm to sweep all directions without needing dedicated weapons.

As they discussed these, Chen Xin completed the modification of Qin Lan’s Power Armor.

This time Chen Xin didn’t modify much; apart from adjusting the weight, protection, and power mode of the Power Armor itself to make it lighter and more agile, he only enhanced its agility and stealth capabilities, making it more suitable for sniper use, along with the targeting scope mounted on the helmet.

The newly modified Power Armor became much more streamlined, with five stacked scopes over the right eye on the helmet, besides, there weren’t many changes.

But for Qin Lan, she somehow immediately took a liking to this set of Power Armor, and as Chen Xin declared the modification completed, she couldn’t wait to put it on to test its performance.

Chen Xin didn’t stop her regarding this; indeed, the Power Armor needs performance testing after modification.

Moreover, his own set not only fitted with the versatile mechanical arm, he also made some other detailed modifications, just right to test performance together, familiarizing himself with the capabilities of this Power Armor for better usage.

Apart from this, another important task is to calculate the design blueprints for a universal Power Armor based on these two sets.

In the current stormy weather, a Power Armor can accomplish many tasks; Chen Xin needs to submit the blueprints of Power Armor and Exoskeleton Protective Suit to the country so they can produce more Exoskeleton Protective Suits and Power Armors, enabling better self-rescue in such blizzard conditions.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 307 - 305: Arrangement of the Production Line
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After upgrading the power armor, Chen Xin and Qin Lan hurried back to the city.

Initially, the brothers Han Ping and Han Ze wanted to follow Chen Xin. Han Ping was Chen Xin’s bodyguard and had no reason not to follow him, while Han Ze was called by Han Ping to lend a hand to Chen Xin.

However, Chen Xin refused the brothers’ proposal and left them at his family’s shelter for a very good reason.

"My parents and two friends live here, and they need protection. Han Ping, since you’re my bodyguard, you should protect my family. As for myself, with this set of power armor, no one can harm me," Chen Xin said to Han Ping, who wanted to go with him to the city.

With such a compelling reason from Chen Xin, Han Ping had no choice but to stay at the shelter, responsible for protecting Chen Xin’s parents and the Mo Qingyan sisters.

Chen Xin and Qin Lan, once again, braved the blizzard to return to the city.

This time, however, they did not go to the city police station or the Polytechnic College, but instead, went to a small shelter designated by the city government for the production line of Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries.

The city government had apparently already sent people to clean up the place. Even though the small shelter had started the heating boiler, the temperature of seventy degrees below zero was so cold that it made people shiver, to the point that water would freeze upon contact.

Yet, with the city’s mobilization, combined with slight improvements in the situation of various shelters, there were enough hands to clean up the place and begin tasks according to Chen Xin’s previous requirements, such as dismantling some partition walls.

These partition walls were installed before the disaster to divide inner spaces within the shelter. Some were ordinary light partitions that could be easily dismantled by removing the fixed clamps, but others were brick walls that served a supporting role, which were much harder to take down.

Even under normal circumstances, demolishing a brick wall would require considerable effort. But now, with the temperature dozens of degrees below zero, even soil was frozen harder than granite, leaving only a white mark when struck with a hammer. In such cold, demolishing a wall made of cement and bricks was not something that could be done with just brute force.

After arriving in power armor, Chen Xin attempted to use the power armor to demolish a brick wall. Despite the power armor being able to exert a force of over 1.25 tons with a punch, it only created a hole in the wall.

Moreover, this caused slight deformation to the mechanical structure of the power armor’s hand, indicating that striking hard objects with maximum force was damaging to the mechanical structure.

However, this was obviously not the correct use of the power armor. While it was possible, it’s similar to how anyone with sense would choose to use a hammer instead of their hands to break a wall. Power armor can punch through walls, but there’s no need to do so.

Activating the mechanical arm on his back, Chen Xin operated the steel hand to switch to an appropriate tool. Following up on the hole he had just punched, he easily expanded it, soon creating a person-sized opening.

This efficiency was undoubtedly amazing, but it was the expected performance of power armor.

Meanwhile, Qin Lan borrowed an iron hammer from the workers and wielded it single-handedly against the wall. After several swings, she successfully made a dent in another wall, and with a kick, created a large hole.

This rapid wall demolition was, of course, faster than the workers swinging hammers by themselves. In fact, even with the wall half-demolished by Chen Xin and Qin Lan, the workers still struggled with the demolition, usually taking a long time to remove just a few bricks, making the progress quite slow.

Despite this, Chen Xin and Qin Lan no longer used the power armor to demolish walls, instead seeking out the construction site manager to confirm the structure of the shelter.

Although it had been confirmed as the production line for Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries, the specific construction drawings for the production line had yet to be made. This was because the drawings needed to be modified based on the site’s structure, even though, under normal circumstances, the building should be constructed according to equipment requirements. There are also cases where equipment needs to be installed according to the site.

So, this was not a strange occurrence. Moreover, although the site preparations were almost complete, with no precedents to refer to, the production line was still just a concept in Chen Xin’s mind, not yet turned into a product.

Of course, this posed no issue for Chen Xin, as having a concept was enough for him to use the system’s upgrade function to upgrade the production line.

According to Chen Xin’s concept, the entire production line should include several aspects such as raw material processing, metal parts casting, radioactive material processing, and high-precision parts production, eventually converging on the assembly line.

The more challenging parts to arrange were metal parts casting and radioactive material processing.

Metal parts casting was mainly for radiation shielding within the battery, ensuring that the radiation emitted by the internal radioactive isotopes did not leak. Therefore, lead alloy would be used for high-precision integral casting.

The key issue with casting parts was needing a high-temperature furnace to melt the materials to a castable state.

Of course, manufacturing using stamping was possible, but under the current environment, a hydraulic press was clearly unsuitable; instead, a furnace was more appropriate.

Fortunately, since they had already made isotope generators, it was enough to meet the electrical demands of an electric furnace, eliminating concerns about the energy supply.

The energy consumption of the electric furnace was easy to solve, but the heat it released during operation was not as easy to manage, especially considering the enclosed underground location. Various problems brought on by the furnace needed to be addressed.

The furnace would consume oxygen during operation, release high temperatures, and need precise temperature control during cooling—all these were not decisions to be made on a whim. Without the system, Chen Xin would need to conduct detailed research and calculations to ultimately prepare for the furnace installation.

As for radioactive material processing, this was easier to arrange than the furnace. As long as radiation protection measures were taken, it could even be arranged for the state to process the radioactive isotopes elsewhere and send them here as raw materials, with only loading being required here.

Though radiation protection was not easy, considering that every part of the production line, except for the initial raw material processing, would be exposed to radiation, the entire small shelter needed to achieve full radiation protection. This was essentially a hard requirement, eliminating the need for additional calculations and arrangements.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 308 - 306 Process Parameters
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Though setting up a production line is quite troublesome and requires a lot of expertise, fortunately the professional matters are handled by professionals. Chen Xin only needs to submit the blueprints for the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery and specify the required materials. The relevant production line will naturally be planned by specialists.

Chen Xin originally intended to design the production line himself, but while he was confirming the site conditions with the construction staff at the small shelter, the special team dedicated to collaborating with him had already sent over the drafted design through military fiber optic lines, and it was even printed.

This is not to say the country is so impressive that a design can be made in such a short time. Even if the designers were driven bald, they wouldn’t be able to deliver. It’s not a matter of determining a plan, but simply that finishing a drawing can’t be done in such a short time.

In fact, the drawing wasn’t drawn recently; the country began designing the production line as soon as they received the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery sample provided by Chen Xin.

At that time, conditions weren’t as bad as now, and the country maintained some means of communication, so technical exchanges and communications were relatively smooth, and the drawing quickly had a draft after determining the plan.

However, the reverse engineering progress of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery wasn’t so fast; the pivotal technical specs were never settled. Although a 10KW sample was trial-produced in the lab, it was still far from being ready for full-scale production.

Thus, the drawing remained a draft without the final details.

This time, Chen Xin proactively proposed setting up the production line. After receiving this message, the special team thought of the unfinished drawing.

So they spent some time and, with several designers pulling out their hair, perfected the design and sent it over through military fiber optic lines.

Of course, despite several designers going bald, without specific technical parameters, the improved drawing still couldn’t easily be the foundation for starting up the production line.

Without specific technical parameters, it’s unclear what equipment should be used. Even if equipment is reluctantly chosen just to make do, it’s unknown what exactly should be produced.

After all, they are supposed to produce the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery, a dangerous item with high radioactivity, not some unlicensed product hurriedly made in a shady workshop.

Such highly radioactive products have extremely high precision requirements in production processes. Any tiny inaccuracy could cause a radiation leak or worse consequences, so researchers took the drawing to Chen Xin seeking confirmation on the production process standards.

This put Chen Xin in a difficult position. Although he could create the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery and use system upgrade capabilities to set up the production line or directly adjust it to optimal conditions once finished, specifying the relative production process parameters was clearly beyond his ability to explain.

Nonetheless, it’s not impossible. Since the country was able to trial-produce a 10KW Isotope Thermoelectric Battery, it’s evident they have a sufficiently deep understanding of the product and technology to attempt trial production. Chen Xin can completely use their copied technical parameters for reference.

Using these data as references, the production line can first be established, then Chen Xin can use system upgrades to "tune" the production line, making it sufficient for production needs, even enhancing its efficiency, ensuring higher yield and avoiding material waste.

Chen Xin’s suggestion to directly use existing data for reference evidently made the research institute’s personnel quite uncomfortable.

After all, establishing a production line requires first determining the product, quantity, and specific process parameters before using this data to establish and design production equipment, because no equipment exists that can produce all products, let alone a production line.

The Von Neumann machine in science fiction is so fantastical because it can produce all equipment and even replicate itself.

Certainly, as technology like 3D printing is invented, universal equipment is gradually emerging, but currently, there’s no production line that can adjust process parameters anytime to produce any product.

Different process parameters lead to different products, and changing process parameters requires modifications to the entire production line.

This demands substantial resources and time. If it weren’t for Chen Xin’s upgrade capabilities, he wouldn’t dare claim he could achieve this.

But these researchers understand Chen Xin’s abilities; they comprehend why he can’t provide specific process parameters, so they didn’t object, following his advice to use copied process parameters to design the production line and equipment.

In fact, these researchers have their own ideas. They believe they can adjust the production data through Chen Xin’s help and understand why the lab’s trial product’s power doesn’t match the level of Chen Xin’s sample.

After all, the gap between 10KW output power and 160KW is too significant, even with technical reasons.

Besides the production process parameters, there’s another vital issue that concerns Chen Xin greatly.

"Under the current circumstances, how will the equipment for the production line be brought in?" Chen Xin asked the researchers. This is the biggest issue now.

The site has been gradually cleared with sufficient manpower mobilized by the city government, the process parameters are aided by the lab, and later adjustments can be made by Chen Xin. Overall, it’s not a big problem.

But how to transport the equipment to the city and install it in the plant is a difficult problem.

Due to the blizzard’s onslaught, all train services are severely delayed. Although trains can still deliver goods to their destinations, to evade strong winds, they travel slowly and often have to stop to dodge them, obviously not a fluid transport state.

Under such circumstances, ensuring coal supply is problematic, not to mention freeing transport capacity to ship industrial equipment.

However, the researchers only have one response for Chen Xin: "Rest assured about this, the special team specifically arranged a special train set to deliver all needed equipment."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 309 - 307 Nuclear-Powered Train
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Although the researchers only gave Chen Xin a simple sentence in response, this sentence implies the hard work of many people.

Not far from the city where Chen Xin was located, in another city in Xiang Province called Jinning, workers were intensely testing a brand new train inside the factory buildings.

The meteorite disaster not only brought a long night with dust blocking the sun, but also destroyed the nationwide railway power supply lines, forcing the country to reuse old coal-powered steam locomotives, as most electric trains were incapacitated.

While there were still some diesel locomotives available, due to fuel shortages, these remaining operational diesel locomotives were allocated to the most critical areas to transport essential supplies.

Nevertheless, comrades on the railway front have always sought ways to restore railway transport capacity.

The train they are currently testing is a new model researched and developed by the Jinning Railway Factory to restore railway transport capacity.
