The train cars have been specially designed for thermal insulation, cold resistance, frost resistance, and wind resistance. While thickening the car walls, they also increased the stability of the train body.

According to the original design specifications, this new train was meant to withstand level 10 winds and temperatures as low as minus fifty degrees, but now it clearly faces more severe challenges.

The factory workers made adjustments and reinforcements, adding counterweights to the train body and replacing the insulation materials inside the car walls to ensure sufficient thermal insulation even in minus seventy-degree environments.

This train is not only intended for transporting valuable goods but also represents an attempt by the state to restore passenger transportation.

Before the disaster, Jinning was already an important train production base for Flame Country, and the locomotives produced here were renowned throughout Flame Country, even running on major high-speed railway lines.

Therefore, for the workers at the Jinning Railway Factory, both technically and experientially, they are quite equipped.

In fact, before the disaster, they had already developed high-speed trains capable of normal operation in temperatures below minus forty degrees, and now they have merely further optimized the technology.

However, after the disaster, the nationwide railway power supply network was destroyed, and electric locomotives were left without power, unable to operate. Even if they weren’t damaged by electromagnetic pulses, they couldn’t run due to lack of electricity.

After all, the preparation weight of an ordinary trunk freight locomotive reaches 240 tons, with a traction power of 9600KW, making it impossible to move without a robust power supply.

Thus, the focus of the testing of this new train is actually on the locomotive’s power system, not the cars.

Since it’s no longer possible to provide power through power supply lines, the locomotive itself must now generate power.

This is still achievable, as both coal and oil can drive steam turbines to generate electricity, thus providing ample power for the entire train.

However, this time, the new train produced by the workers at the Jinning Railway Factory does not use an internal combustion engine as its power source but instead uses a small nuclear reactor.

This is a nuclear-powered locomotive.

The reactor used is technology indigenous to Flame Country, originally intended for nuclear submarines, but the size couldn’t be reduced further, making it impossible to use on trains or civilian ships.

Moreover, the tonnage range of nuclear submarines varies from thousands to tens of thousands of tons, and a reactor capable of powering a submarine would provide too much power for a train, so apart from reducing its size, its power output had to be reduced.

However, this is not something easily accomplished. Excluding the nuclear fuel section of the reactor itself, the associated equipment occupies considerable space. On submarines, a dedicated compartment is designed to house the nuclear reactor, something possible on large warships, but clearly not feasible for trains, which lack sufficient space.

But Chen Xin’s Isotope Thermoelectric Battery provided researchers with numerous inspirations and technical support, allowing them to successfully miniaturize the submarine reactor and improve related technologies, successfully creating a small nuclear reactor suitable for installation on a train.

This reactor is not a conventional light-water reactor but is similar to an Isotope Thermoelectric Battery in thermal-to-electric conversion style, directly converting heat energy into electrical energy.

This approach has many benefits. The radioactive core can be completely sealed to prevent radiation leakage, as only heat conduction is needed to convert into electricity, not cooling water injected into the reactor for steam turbine generation, making the energy conversion efficiency of the former naturally higher.

With such a small nuclear reactor, powering a train becomes an easy task, even when a passenger train’s traction power is over 5600KW.

Generally, a small nuclear reactor refers to one with an output power below 300MW, where 1MW is 1000KW, and driving a train requires only 10~15MW of power.

The small reactor installed on this new train currently has an output power of 35MW, which can not only power the train itself but also provide ample electricity for heating, lighting, and various other electrical needs, including de-icing and obstacle removal.

The project was initiated and started resonating manufacturing three months before the meteorite impact.

Progress on other aspects was swift, with the only limitation being the power system, which was resolved when Chen Xin provided the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery to the state, lifting the constraints and allowing this new train to roll off the production line, ready for operation.

The first mission of this new nuclear-powered train is to transport production equipment to the city where Chen Xin is located.

This is the special train mentioned in the researchers’ message to Chen Xin.

Its successful operation not only marks the improvement and enhancement of railway transport capacity but also signifies a more convenient and safer application of nuclear power.

The electric output of the small nuclear reactors used on these trains is already sufficient to power all the electricity needs of a small county before the disaster. In the current situation, if a city had a small nuclear reactor, it would not have to worry about its electricity supply, and even its heating methods could be switched to electrical heating.

The reason Flame Country hadn’t done this before was due to many unresolved technical issues and the inability to build new nuclear plants quickly, which is why this method wasn’t adopted before the disaster to supply electricity to shelters.

But now, with safe and reliable small nuclear reactor technology, the leadership of Flame Country clearly won’t miss such a clean, efficient, and convenient energy source.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 310 - 308 Arrive at the Station to Unload (First Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When the new-style train, loaded with production equipment, arrived in the city where Chen Xin was located, the blizzard miraculously began to weaken.

This blizzard, which had ravaged for more than half a month, finally showed signs of calming down. Although the temperature was still below seventy degrees, the wind force had decreased from level twelve to level ten.

It seemed that after violently sweeping through most of the Flame Country, this terrible blizzard had exhausted its momentum.

This gave the survivors who had endured this blizzard for half a month a ray of hope, even though the days weren’t as difficult for this city.

The cause of the blizzard is currently inconclusive, but according to some information obtained by Chen Xin from research institutions, this blizzard should just be the past cold wave.

However, without the regulation of atmospheric circulation and the heat brought by sunlight, this cold wave did not dissipate in a short time but continued to affect for more than half a month and appears to persist for a while longer.

For Chen Xin, it’s hard to say this is good news, even though he has always hoped the blizzard could end sooner.

Standing on the platform of the train station wearing Power Armor, Chen Xin raised his hand to wipe off the snow accumulated on his visor and looked up at the end of the tracks within his view.

There, a train was pushing against the snow, driving towards the station like a beast.

Before the blizzard arrived, the tracks were already buried by snow. The train had to push against the thick snow to move forward, which made each train, just like cars before disasters that would splash water without slowing down, kick up two spectacular waves of snow.

Illuminated by the train’s headlights, the two snow waves were lit brightly, resembling the frill of a frilled lizard.

Though they appeared grayish and gradually became smaller as the train slowed down, in this raging snowy cold night, they were symbols of hope.

The train slowly docked at the platform, where the snow had long been cleared out, despite the howling wind covering the just-cleaned platform with a layer of snow within minutes.

As the train doors opened, several staff members came out, though clearly they were not yet accustomed to the nearly seventy-degree temperature difference between the inside and outside of the train. Instantly coated with a layer of white frost, their bodies froze for a moment.

Fortunately, their clothing was thick enough, and their faces were well covered. They quickly recovered and greeted Chen Xin and others on the platform: "Hello, comrade. We are here to deliver equipment. Who is responsible for receiving it?"

"I am. Please show me the delivery list," indicated a staff member beside Chen Xin, quickly stepping forward to handle the handover.

While the staff on both sides were handling the handover, Chen Xin was carefully observing the train before him.

Under the platform lights, the entire train appeared silver-white, looking somewhat similar to the high-speed trains of the past, with each car closely connected to the next. However, the cars lacked the windows of high-speed trains, making them look more like iron boxes.

But from the white vapor continuously effusing from the opened door, it could be seen that the temperature inside the cars was not low, and the lights were bright inside, clearly not lacking in electricity.

This piqued Chen Xin’s interest, prompting him to use his system upgrade perspective to observe this train closely.

Naturally, Chen Xin discovered that this was a nuclear-powered train.

"Is this a nuclear-powered train? Has our country already managed to apply nuclear reactors to trains?" Chen Xin couldn’t help but ask aloud.

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s inquiry, a staff member who had deboarded from the train responded with pride, "Yes, this is humanity’s first nuclear-powered train in a true sense. Without the nation’s breakthroughs in nuclear reactor technology, we wouldn’t be able to realize such an astonishing concept."

"A nuclear-powered train, indeed astonishing!" Chen Xin couldn’t help but nod in agreement with this sentiment.

"Comrade, the gear you’re wearing should be nuclear-powered too, right? It seems the nation has indeed achieved quite remarkable breakthroughs in nuclear energy applications!" remarked the staff member, looking curiously at Chen Xin in his ironclad suit.

"This? It counts as nuclear-powered, but it’s different from the reactor used on the train. I just use radioactive isotopes," Chen Xin explained, feeling somewhat eager to board the train for a tour, as anyone would want to broaden their horizons with such a marvel.

However, wearing Power Armor made it quite troublesome for Chen Xin to board the train.

Although there were doors on the train body, due to weather reasons, the doors were narrower than normal to facilitate cold protection.

Ordinary people could enter normally, but those wearing thicker layers might find the doors a bit narrow. In Chen Xin’s case, wearing Power Armor, no matter what, he couldn’t squeeze through.

But just then, the side wall of one train car opened entirely, rising upwards like a box lid opening to the roof, revealing the interior.

This greatly surprised Chen Xin, who had never seen a train open its "door" in such a manner.

"Since the train has ample electricity, we adopted this method to open the car, which also facilitates loading and unloading of goods," a staff member proudly explained.

Opening the door this way is indeed much more convenient than ordinary doors, allowing both bulk and loose goods to be loaded or unloaded more easily.

Like now, because the entire side of the car was open, forklifts that had long been waiting on the platform directly drove over, picking up goods within the car and transporting them out, loading them onto trucks beside.

Previously, forklifts were also used, but definitely not as convenient and swift as now.

In no time, the goods in this car were unloaded, and simultaneously, some prepared goods on the platform were loaded onto the train.

These were products from projects improved through Chen Xin by research institutions, and due to the blizzard, they had been stranded here. Now with the arrival of this special train, naturally, they had to be transported away.

Aside from this car, other cars were also fully loaded with goods; however, the loading and unloading capacity on the platform was limited, allowing only one car to be unloaded at a time.

Therefore, once one car was unloaded and started being loaded, the second car opened its wall and began unloading.

Watching the orderly loading and unloading on the platform, Chen Xin finally couldn’t resist asking the staff nearby, "Can I board to take a look? This train is not just a freight train, right?"

"Of course, we also have passenger cars. But if you want to board, you might need to take off this iron suit," the staff did not prevent Chen Xin from boarding but indicated he needs to remove his Power Armor.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 311 - 309 Snow Country Train · In Design (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin took off his Power Armor. Although it was very cold outside, he didn’t feel much chill because he had already changed into his specially-made driving suit.

This driving suit was specifically made by Chen Xin for the Power Armor. The innermost layer is a snug lining, somewhat similar to the integrated design of a drysuit, breathable, waterproof, and with excellent insulation.

On the outside of the lining is the main body of the driving suit. It draws from the design of the Exoskeleton Protective Suit and is equipped with electric heating structures. It looks somewhat similar to a pilot’s flight suit and also features an integrated design. A high-performance rechargeable battery powers the driving suit, which charges through the Power Armor. It can be used for 48 hours when fully charged.

The driving suit also includes various miscellaneous equipment such as radio, locator, and signal transmitter, all powered by the battery, ensuring that the driver can survive in harsh environments after leaving the Power Armor.

Additionally, Chen Xin specially improved the head structure of the Power Armor. He designed a lightweight helmet for the driving suit. When driving, the driver can wear this helmet and insert their head into the head of the Power Armor, connecting externally to the Power Armor, rather than inserting their bare head directly.

The advantage of this is that it provides more protection for the driver’s head, and prevents their head from freezing when they leave the Power Armor.

However, the driving suit itself does not supply oxygen. The helmet only has air filtration, so if necessary, the life support system inside the Power Armor needs to be removed and installed on the driving suit to provide oxygen.

Of course, Chen Xin doesn’t need these functions right now, so he directly boarded the train, wanting to take a good look at this nuclear-powered train.

Entering from the door, there is a small compartment that connects two carriages and separates the internal car space from the door area with a door. Its main function is to prevent cold air from entering the carriage when the train stops and the door is opened.

The interior of the carriage is not much different from an ordinary high-speed train, but the space is relatively compact, and the decor is not as beautiful and comfortable as a high-speed train. Overall, it appears more utilitarian and somewhat reminiscent of an old green train car.

However, this passenger carriage is quite good given the current environment. At least when Chen Xin walked in, he could see through the helmet display that the temperature inside the carriage was at least 10℃, which was a very comfortable temperature now.

"Will this train be used for passenger service?" Chen Xin asked the nearby staff member.

Right now, there were only a few staff members in the carriage, seemingly testing the train, and no other passengers were on board.

"Not at the moment. Although the country has plans to resume railway passenger services, currently there is only this one nuclear-powered engine. The carriages are specifically designed for electric locomotives, and steam locomotive engines cannot use these carriages," the staff member explained, then asked Chen Xin, "Would you like to see the engine room?"

Hearing the staff’s offer, Chen Xin would certainly not miss such a good opportunity. He nodded and followed the staff member through several carriages towards the engine.

The train seemed to be testing various carriage functions together, so as Chen Xin followed the staff towards the engine, he saw many carriages with different functions.

"This is the dining car, mainly used to provide food for the whole train. According to our design, as long as there are enough ingredients, it can provide at least five hundred main and side hot meals within an hour. If simply heating, it can heat two thousand portions of emergency shelter food within an hour," the staff member proudly introduced the dining car, evidently proud that in such conditions, heating two thousand meals an hour was a pretty practical and impressive capability.

At least, Chen Xin could envision that if necessary, attaching a dining car to the engine and dragging several food carriages could quickly provide hot meals to areas lacking food via rail, potentially saving many lives in critical moments.

In addition to the dining car, there’s a storage carriage on this train. To test its functionality, it holds some food, which appears to be the ration for the staff on board.

However, Chen Xin was a bit speechless that this storage carriage was temperature-controlled. It cools when external temperatures are high, but now, it’s heating, ensuring the inside doesn’t drop excessively.

This point was explained to Chen Xin by the staff. Though external temperatures were very low, freezing food for a long time in low temperatures doesn’t mean you can do nothing and leave it frozen for storage.

"This storage carriage isn’t just for food. Sometimes it transports medicines or other important materials needing consistent temperatures, so it must be controllable, not entirely ultra-low-temperature," the staff explained, smiling as they added, "Actually, because of the abundant power from the nuclear reactor, we made a temperature-controlled carriage. Otherwise, it would have been wasteful, a small storage box would suffice."

Chen Xin nodded to show understanding; this is the benefit of nuclear power. With ample energy, many things can be done freely.

For example, this train has a water storage carriage, a shower carriage, an ecological experiment carriage, a sleeper carriage, permanent residential compartment carriages, etc. It seems that with sufficient energy, these staff members have fully unleashed their creativity, materializing various ideas and bringing them out for testing.

"You all... have quite a variety," Chen Xin observed the diverse array of carriages. Although he could understand the necessity of some, he still felt some designs were unnecessary.

"Most are experimental models. Testing them doesn’t mean mass-production, but it’s good to be prepared. If there’s ever a need, we wouldn’t have to design on the spot since we’d already have the blueprints," the staff member shrugged, facing Chen Xin’s assessment.

Chen Xin nodded but suddenly thought of something, so he asked the staff, "You wouldn’t be trying to build the Snow Country Train, would you?"

Unexpectedly, the staff member actually nodded: "We do have such plans; the train is already in the design phase."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 312 - 310 Mobile Base · In Conception (3rd update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The Snow Country Train is both a train and a science fiction work, with the train itself being the most important stage in this sci-fi narrative.

In the original novel of "Snow Country Train," Blue Planet turns into a cold Hell for unexplained reasons. The last surviving humans cram into a perpetually moving train driven by a perpetual motion machine. If the train stops, humans will freeze to death, so it keeps moving forward endlessly.

On the train, the wealthy live lives indistinguishable from before the disaster. Coffee, tobacco, botanical gardens, aquariums, libraries... all these are enjoyed as a matter of course by the rich, while the poor live in the lowest-class carriages, surviving on protein blocks made from cockroaches. They must endure the torture of overcrowding, filth, and hunger, leading a life devoid of dignity.

This is quite a dystopian science fiction work that depicts, in an extremely satirical manner, the huge life disparities and social stratification between the rich and the poor. It’s a rather classic work.

Of course, for Chen Xin and the train’s staff, the actual significance is not substantial. Although the environment of Blue Planet is also an extremely cold Hell now, even harsher than depicted in the original "Snow Country Train," humanity hasn’t reached the point where survival is only possible on a train, especially one driven by an impossible perpetual motion machine.

However, the concept of the Snow Country Train is commendable. In such harsh environments, humans need railway transport, and trains that can operate normally under these conditions are crucial, making the Snow Country Train a pretty good reference.

"The design tasks required by the state include planning for mobile refuges, essentially developing trains for permanent or long-term habitation based on railways, moving survivors from the north to more favorable southern environments, or straightforwardly moving along the railway." The staff did not conceal this matter, as it’s not something that requires secrecy.

In response to this topic, Chen Xin thought for a moment and then shook his head, saying, "Moving along the railway is too costly. Not to mention how you would provide enough food for survivors on the train, a train can carry only one to two thousand people at most.

Given the need to also transport supplies and ensure survival, sustaining a few hundred people on a train would already be remarkable, and the cost of keeping the train running would be enough to build a shelter accommodating more people."

"That’s true, but this is considered a backup plan, after all. In case something necessitates it, it’s always good to be prepared," the staff naturally understood how much resources constructing a train for a few hundred people would require. But as he said, it’s better to have it and not need it than need it and not have it.

Chen Xin agreed with this. Technology is something that is never enough but always lacking. If someone thinks that technological development has reached its peak and there will be no new inventions, it only indicates that they are not thinking straight.

So Chen Xin also gave some of his suggestions for the Snow Country Train.

As a novelist, Chen Xin perhaps doesn’t have as much knowledge as scientists in some areas of conception, but in terms of creativity, he’s not inferior to those scientists. His inspirations are even more abundant because of his profession.

The staff were very interested in Chen Xin’s suggestions, and after recording some feasible ideas, they eagerly invited Chen Xin to visit the train manufacturing plant in Jianning and participate in the research and design work of the Snow Country Train when he had time.

Chen Xin was quite interested in this, but he didn’t directly agree, saying he would consider visiting when he had time, as he had his own matters to attend to for now.

As for why he accepted the invitation, it was because Chen Xin was inspired by the concept of the Snow Country Train to think of a more feasible yet equally difficult-to-achieve concept, which is the Mobile Base.

This is a concept more common in various science fiction or fantasy works, appearing in many games, novels, comics, and films.

A small one could just be a vehicle, like Chen Xin’s own RV, but a large one could be something as massive as Rhode Island, a Mobile City-State, or a Floating City.

Small Mobile Bases are easy to achieve, and with modern technology, creating one is not too troublesome.

Regarding larger Mobile Bases, it’s not that they don’t exist; for example, an aircraft carrier can be considered a Mobile Base on water.

But beyond these, building something like Rhode Island as a land-based Mobile Base is challenging.

It’s not that it can’t be made, but something that large lacks suitable roads to travel, not to mention the structural, material, shock absorption, energy, and sustenance issues all restricting the emergence of land Mobile Bases.

However, these problems may limit and trouble others, but for Chen Xin, they are no issue at all. At worst, he could just upgrade the system and it would eventually be made.

Only, after some thought, Chen Xin realized he might not need to build something as big, or there’s no need to make it that large.

For him, a Mobile Base is not a necessity but a means to avoid disasters with his family in the event of an unavoidable disaster, so it doesn’t need to hold many people and doesn’t need to be large.

Therefore, he doesn’t necessarily need to build something like Rhode Island; just a Mobile Base vehicle would suffice.

Having turned these thoughts in his mind, Chen Xin did not intend to immediately realize this idea, for a Mobile Base differs from a fixed shelter, involving too many aspects. Even if he could acquire help and resources from the state, crafting a usable Mobile Base wouldn’t be easily achieved.

However, he did feel that when upgrading his shelter in the future, he could add some appropriate features, integrate the existing shelter’s structure, and gradually adjust towards a Mobile Base. Maybe after several upgrades, it could become a Mobile Base that can move around.

While Chen Xin was contemplating where he would go with his future Mobile Base, he and the staff finally reached the front of the train, witnessing the nuclear reactor in operation.

"This is the nuclear reactor of this train. Currently, it can provide a maximum output power of 35 MW, and we’re running it at only 70% capacity," the staff was evidently very pleased and proud of the nuclear reactor, which was not even the size of a six-seat minivan.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 313 - 311: The Promised 4 Updates
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After the tour of the train, by the time Chen Xin got off, most of the cargo had already been loaded and unloaded.

The cargo on the platform was loaded onto transport vehicles and then driven directly to the small underground shelter that had now been cleared out. There, the staff would cooperate with researchers to install and debug these devices, preparing to produce Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries once everything was ready.

Chen Xin watched as the train, after finishing loading the cargo, closed its carriage wall and then slowly left the station. He asked the staff beside him, "What will this train be used for after this?"

A nuclear-powered train can do so many things, no wonder Chen Xin would ask this question.

"It’s still just a test run for now. After this run, it will go back to have its carriages adjusted. It should be used for emergency and important material transportation." The staff also wasn’t exactly sure where the nuclear-powered train would be used, but he felt that a train capable of operating normally in this weather would most likely be used to transport important materials.

Chen Xin nodded and didn’t ask any more questions. He just got into the car with the staff to head to the small cleared-out shelter.

The location of the small shelter is near the underground factories in the urban area. This used to be an industrial area in the city, mainly consisting of an ordnance factory producing logistics materials such as garments. In the past, there was a design for wartime production recovery underground.

So, three months before the meteorite hit, this place was urgently transformed into an underground factory, mainly producing living materials for the city and processing food for the shelter.

The small shelter is the underground garage of a residential area near the factory district, now cleared out. Once the equipment arrives, construction of the production line will commence, and the interior will be rearranged to meet production needs.

Chen Xin was happy to see this happening. After all, the more perfect the state’s construction of the production line in this small shelter, the easier it would be for him to upgrade it later.

After all, he only planned to adjust some process parameters of this production line, not make any major changes.

When Chen Xin and the staff arrived at the small shelter, the equipment that had arrived ahead of them had been placed in the space cleared out for warehouses within the shelter, and some staff were conducting inspection checks.

"How are the conditions of these equipments?" Chen Xin inquired with one of the staff responsible for checking the equipment.

"The condition of the equipment is quite good. Thanks to the nuclear-powered train this time, there’s no sign of frost damage." The staff reported to Chen Xin with a delighted expression.

It’s not uncommon for equipment to suffer from frost damage. Even metal materials can easily be frost-damaged at temperatures of minus tens of degrees, losing their original material properties.

One of the most common issues is thermal expansion and contraction causing equipment to crack due to freezing. In the past, one of the most important winter tasks for Chen Xin’s parents at work was winter antifreeze for the equipment.

Previously, when some scientific prototypes needed to be upgraded by Chen Xin were transported from other places, there were instances where equipment was frost-damaged or parts were damaged due to low temperatures, which is why this batch of equipment needed to be inspected upon arrival.

These boxes were not the ordinary wooden boxes used before, but specially insulated transport boxes.

However, even specialized transport boxes had experienced issues before, but this time, thanks to the nuclear-powered train, the temperature inside the carriages was maintained above zero, so the equipment didn’t encounter any problems during transit.

"That’s good. Quickly prepare all the equipment and accelerate the site construction." Seeing that the equipment had no issues, Chen Xin didn’t say much more, only urging the staff to prepare everything as quickly as possible, to install the equipment early, and put the production line into use.

"Don’t worry, we’ll keep an eye on this, it won’t be delayed." The staff knew the importance of the matter, and in fact, they had been working in shifts, around the clock, keeping a close watch here.

————————————————

Leaving the small shelter, Chen Xin and several researchers took a car back to the Polytechnic College since the small shelter wasn’t yet connected to the underground highway, they could only drive on the surface.

The snow and strong winds on the ground made it almost impossible for the car to move, but they only needed to drive to the nearby underground factory, from where they could return to the Polytechnic College via the underground highway, which wasn’t far.

"Have you sent the designs for the Exoskeleton Protective Suit to the research department?" In the car, Chen Xin asked a researcher about the designs he had previously instructed them to pass on.

"The designs have been sent through the military network. They said they’ll start the research and production immediately, and we should see results soon." The researcher hastily replied to Chen Xin. They dared not neglect anything concerning Chen Xin, especially such crucial matters.

Previously, they had been very envious of Chen Xin’s Power Armor and wanted to study it, but Chen Xin hadn’t given them any samples, and they were too embarrassed to ask him for one. They didn’t expect Chen Xin to turn around and give them a set of Exoskeleton Protective Suit designs.

Regarding that design, they had already evaluated it. As long as the weather wasn’t as harsh as before with the blizzards, the protective suit could provide good protection, and the exoskeleton could also assist with engineering tasks. In fact, it could play a significant role in the nationwide disaster recovery and reconstruction effort.

In fact, these researchers were not yet aware that after they submitted the designs, they had alarmed the highest leadership of Flame Country.

From the perspective of the highest leadership, such a set of Exoskeleton Protective Suit is worth promoting vigorously. It could completely change the current situation in Flame Country, and even shift the balance on the whole Blue Planet.

As disaster descended, all countries and powers on the Blue Planet suffered a tremendous impact. Small countries basically fell completely, without enough resources and national strength, they faced extinction in the face of such cataclysmic natural disasters.

Even large countries faced difficulties. Flame Country, as one of the five major powers, was in relatively good condition, having preserved the majority of its citizens, with the actual controlled territory not significantly reduced, the military intact, and production in the process of recovery.

Although the blizzard dealt a heavy blow to Flame Country, causing many surviving citizens to perish due to the cold, and halting many important engineering projects, the reconstruction work continues unstopped.

This Exoskeleton Protective Suit, although it seems inconspicuous, can actually significantly boost the progress of reconstruction work and also enable more people to operate outdoors. It holds immeasurable importance for the entire national reconstruction plan.

And this could make Flame Country the first country to recover among all the nations on Blue Planet.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 314 - 312: The Situations of Various Countries
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The series of disasters triggered by the meteor impact were an irresistible apocalypse for small countries.

The countries in the Europa region fared somewhat better. Although the small governments were powerless in the face of disaster, and order had collapsed due to social chaos even before the meteor impact, at least these countries were not wiped out by tsunamis like the South Seas Region. A substantial number of people managed to survive the first wave of the disaster through self-help.

However, survival in these places proved extremely difficult. Lacking governmental organization and appropriate responses, the survivors in these countries had to rely entirely on themselves, scavenging for usable materials from the ruins. Not only did they face various dangerous environments, but they also had to contend with other survivors, making every day a grueling struggle.

Those who had prepared for doomsday survival beforehand had some capacity to cope with such disasters, but the vast majority of ordinary people without such preparations could only wait for death.

On one hand, their governments had already collapsed due to the social chaos brought on by doomsday panic even before the disaster struck. On the other hand, these ordinary people lacked survival skills for the apocalypse; the vast majority may not even have been able to escape the first wave of disasters caused by the meteor impact.

In these regions, perhaps only true doomsday survivalists could likely make it through alive?

The situation in major countries was relatively better. At least these nations had the capacity to build shelters and military bunkers during peacetime, retaining some military power and organizational structures even after the disaster hit, thus enabling them to organize some social elites to take refuge and survive the initial disaster.

After the disaster, these nations also had the resources to resume production.

Moreover, for these Europa countries, without those that had to be supported with high welfare while always being a burden with multiple opinions, they could move more freely, focusing on rebuilding their nation with a core of social elites.

Of course, this elite model also has its problems. Without a large number of grassroots people, issues like labor shortages and slow development are inevitable. However, fewer people mean less burden, and slower progress is better than total collapse.

After all, there is a fundamental difference between the Europa countries and Flame Country. Their people’s compliance and the government’s control over the state are not as strong as in Flame Country. In situations where ordinary people cannot be restrained, elite groups are more suitable for these nations.

If there are still a few countries in the world that could retain their vitality in the face of disaster and rapidly start rebuilding afterward, on this blue planet, it would be only Flame Country, Federation, and Lucia.

The other two of the five major powers have already degenerated to being indistinguishable from average Europa countries. During peacetime, they might still have some strength, but in the face of an apocalyptic disaster that critically tests overall national power, they would find it hard to accomplish much.

Among the nations, Flame Country’s situation can be considered the best post-apocalypse. With a vast population and territory, not only is there a large number of survivors to serve as post-disaster labor, but there is also ample space to build shelters. They have also preserved a large amount of pre-disaster materials and production resources, allowing for a rapid recovery and commencement of reconstruction after the disaster.

As for Lucia, although this country has long fallen into decline, barely maintaining the facade of a major country, the heritage left by the red polar bear of the past is so vast that even after decades of its demise, Lucia has been living off this legacy for decades, still possessing the confidence and strength to handle disasters.

If you understand the history of Lucia, you would know that this country needs an iron-handed ruler; every famous monarch or leader in Lucia’s history is a symbol of strong-arm rule and power.

Thus, in this disaster, what Lucia lacks is not the strength to respond to the disaster, but a sufficiently powerful ruler, someone strong enough to steer the country, using all resources and means to survive the disaster and then rebuild the homeland.

Considering Lucia’s ability to rebuild a superpower from the ruins and embers after World War II, they actually do not lack the potential and opportunity for resurgence. It all depends on whether they can have a leader capable enough to govern the country.

As for the Federation, historically the world’s number one power on the blue planet, the situation is somewhat complicated.

When the meteor was falling, one headed for the Federation’s mainland. The Federation launched a nuclear missile to intercept it, but the result was not ideal.

The meteor still fell on the Federation’s West Coast, devastating the entire West Coast. Although it didn’t reach the worst-case scenario of hitting the Yellowstone Volcano and triggering an eruption, the outcome was not much better.

After all, the Federation’s previous nuclear missile carried radiation, which combined with meteor fragments and dust, scattered across most of the Federation’s territory, turning it into a wasteland apocalypse.

Moreover, due to the Federation’s unique political system, the federal government lost control over individual states post-disaster. Only a few states near the capital barely followed the federal government’s orders, and even that was merely following, not obeying.

As for the other states, the West Coast states have completely turned into wastelands. Without the barrier of the Rocky Mountains, even the central regions would be at risk of falling.

Now, fortunately, the Rocky Mountains are there, blocking the impact and subsequent secondary disasters of the meteor strike, as well as the radioactive dust from the previous nuclear explosion. This has preserved the central and eastern regions of the Federation east of the Rocky Mountains, yet they have fallen into a state of self-governance.

States like Florida in the south formed the Southern Alliance, while northern states like New York still nominally belong to the Federation. The Great Lakes region states like Wisconsin and Minnesota have independently yet loosely formed an alliance to resist the original Federation.

States in the central region like New Mexico and Texas have also declared independence, though their situations are slightly different.

These states, influenced by ethnicity, culture, and traditional customs, differ in style from other states in the Federation. The Texas people, known for their fierce independence, had long harbored independent intentions and were largely discontent with the local state government.

Thus, after the apocalyptic disaster, survivors in these places quickly formed small factions, governing themselves while starting to battle each other, turning almost the entire central plains of the Federation into a war zone.

If not for the cooling weather, these regions would have been engulfed by snow and ice, the desertified landscape indeed giving it a wasteland feel.

All these conditions significantly hampered the Federation’s ability and progress in post-disaster reconstruction. Although the Federation retained a considerable portion of its power, these forces were dispersed and could not be concentrated.

Now, nations have found themselves back at a new starting line, with some leading, others lagging, and some regrouping for a fresh start. Who can truly take the lead in this post-disaster new world ultimately depends on who can first break free from the clutches of disaster.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 315 - 313 Installing the Generator
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the competition following this disaster, taking the lead in post-disaster reconstruction is undoubtedly the most direct way to influence the outcome.

Speaking of post-disaster reconstruction, human resources are undoubtedly one of the most important resources. After all, regardless of any reconstruction project, the fundamental factor is the involvement of people.

In such harsh weather conditions now, it is extremely difficult for ordinary people to be active in the outside world, let alone work. Temperatures of several tens below zero are enough to freeze anyone not adequately protected, and even with enough protection, it is easy to form an ice shell on the body, hindering movement.

Therefore, it is quite normal for protective suits that can handle harsh environments to be in demand at this time, not to mention that the Exoskeleton Protective Suit presented by Chen Xin includes mechanical assistance, which can completely maintain sufficient labor capacity for workers in harsh environments, ensuring the efficiency of post-disaster reconstruction work.

This is also why a single Exoskeleton Protective Suit could alarm the highest leadership of the Flame Country, because it indeed holds considerable strategic significance.

Not everything that influences national strategy is as lofty as many might think. Often, these can be little gadgets we see everywhere in our daily lives, yet they tend to affect the nation’s economy and livelihood.

However, even though he provided something so important to the country, Chen Xin himself remains focused on the construction of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery production line.

Compared to the Exoskeleton Protective Suit drawings already submitted to the country, Chen Xin has also submitted the drawings and samples of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery to the country, yet it is clear that the technical content of the two is entirely different.

The Exoskeleton Protective Suit, once the drawings are available, even if an identical one cannot be recreated, similar products can be produced. But the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery is such that even with the drawings, samples, and technology, it may not necessarily be replicated.

Otherwise, India would not have their airplanes crashing dozens a year, even after obtaining the production line and being taught step by step.

Therefore, Chen Xin can confidently hand over the Exoskeleton Protective Suit drawings to the country for replication, but if there is an urgent need for the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery, its production line must be established no matter what.

Fortunately, with the country’s investment and the production line not having a large scale, constructing it is not an issue.

After the equipment arrived, with the supervision of researchers, the construction progress was not slow, and the factories were largely completed. Chen Xin also delivered the Isotope Generator, which will be the power source for this shelter, and it has already entered the installation and commissioning stage.

"Are you really planning to install this generator here?" The old professor who came with Chen Xin to install and commission the generator looked at Chen Xin wearing Power Armor and inspecting the generator with a mechanical arm, somewhat disagreeing and questioning Chen Xin.

In the old professor’s view, the technology involved in this generator is too important and should be thoroughly analyzed in the laboratory, and the technology thoroughly researched before putting it into practical use, rather than directly bringing it here to generate power.

To this, Chen Xin shook his head and said, "If it’s left in the lab, it’s just a pretty piece. Although technology analysis is important, I think it’s better to let it first play its original function and actual value, then consider what technology it contains could bring."

This is the difference between Chen Xin and the old professor. In the old professor’s view, the technology on the Isotope Generator should first be analyzed and then, through these technologies, promote scientific progress for the benefit of the whole country and even humanity. However, Chen Xin would first use the Isotope Generator to generate electricity and let it fulfill its fundamental role.

Of course, there is no right or wrong in these two attitudes, just different choices and focuses.

The old professor did not insist on having Chen Xin hand over the Isotope Generator for him to analyze, nor did Chen Xin prevent the old professor from researching the Isotope Generator.

"But speaking of which, you did some research and analysis on this generator before, any ideas?" After adjusting the generator and connecting it to the electrical circuit of this small shelter, Chen Xin asked the old professor.

Before, the Isotope Generator was kept at the Polytechnic College, where the old professor and other researchers conducted research and analysis, trying to parse its related technological principles.

However, the time was too short to make any discoveries practically impossible, so Chen Xin just asked the old professor if there were any ideas or thoughts, rather than asking if there were any discoveries.

"Currently, there are a few ideas, but for now, Weak Nuclear Force is still too early for humanity. To study it, too many theoretical foundations need to be completed." This is also why the old professor did not oppose Chen Xin using the Isotope Generator directly for power generation.

Because of the lack of theoretical foundation, even if some phenomena are discovered, interpreting the knowledge contained within is highly complex and tedious, and incredibly time-consuming.

Chen Xin was not surprised by this, merely suggesting after some thought, "If analyzing the generator seems too complicated, I think we could start with the battery. The Isotope Generator uses the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery as the filling material, so researching this should be a bit easier, and the country is also conducting research in this area, having made some progress with the production of replicas."

"I will consider it, but in scientific research, there is no option to avoid the difficult and choose the easy. We cannot avoid studying due to a lack of theoretical foundation." The old professor’s attitude was firm, unchanged by Chen Xin’s persuasion.

Chen Xin did not press the matter, merely telling the old professor, "After the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery production line is completed, I will establish a production line for the Isotope Generator. Although I may not articulate the technology, I can produce it, and by then, you can take one from the produced products for research."

"You’re able to make a production line for the Isotope Generator?" The old professor found this somewhat unbelievable, but recalling having witnessed Chen Xin upgrade the Isotope Generator before, he slightly nodded and said, "Given your ability, you should indeed be able to do it, and that’s truly enviable. Scholar Syndrome, it completely equals a lifetime of diligent study for an ordinary person! If it weren’t impossible to analyze the principles of this syndrome, probably everyone would want to have it, right?"

"Who can say about things like this? Also, the abilities gained from this syndrome can vary widely. Some might master eight foreign languages, others might understand advanced mathematics effortlessly, and still others might be able to play all musical instruments instantly. The human brain is indeed very mysterious," Chen Xin replied, half earnestly and half dismissively.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 316 - 314: The Frozen Dragon Lady’s Home (Three Updates)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

While the construction of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery production line was steadily advancing, the city government was also organizing manpower to open an underground passage from the subterranean factory to this small sanctuary.

After all, with the outside temperature being tens of degrees below zero and more than one meter of snow accumulation, if someone steps poorly, they could sink directly up to their chest.

In such conditions, the outdoors is completely unsuitable for survival and activities, so rather than bothering with ground-level movement, it is more sensible to create an underground tunnel.

There were workers responsible for these tasks, and Chen Xin finally had the time to visit Mo Qingyan’s house with Qin Lan.

Though, honestly, there wasn’t much to see; under the onslaught of minus seventy degrees and a level 12 blizzard, Mo Qingyan’s original small building had been completely engulfed by snow. The antenna that should have stood on the rooftop had long been blown away to who knows where.

Chen Xin and Qin Lan trudged across the snow to the courtyard wall. If the Power Armor wasn’t so heavy, causing both their lower bodies to sink completely into the snow, they would need only lift their legs to step over the snow and enter the courtyard with the snow’s thickness.

But it was not too troublesome now. With hands clinging to the top of the wall, a little exertion could easily flip them over.

Chen Xin and Qin Lan managed to get inside without much effort, yet the small courtyard of Mo Qingyan’s house was not much different from the outside, also buried entirely under more than a meter of snow. The original elevator was completely swallowed by the snow, and only a little bulge on the ground was visible, offering no hint that there was an elevator below the snow.

"Looks like we’ll have to use the stairs," Qin Lan said resignedly to Chen Xin as he looked at the situation in front of them.

Chen Xin nodded in agreement. In this situation, even if the elevator wasn’t frozen and still usable, there was no point in digging it out from the snow; taking the stairs would be more convenient.

Although Mo Qingyan’s house mainly relied on an elevator, it also had stairs available since no one would build a subterranean refuge with just one elevator shaft.

The two made some effort, found a way down the stairs from the unoccupied small building, but this time, it wasn’t Qin Lan breaking the door; it was Chen Xin using the Universal Mechanical Arm to pick the lock.

With the help of the Universal Mechanical Arm, Chen Xin quickly unlocked the door and entered Mo Qingyan’s sanctuary with Qin Lan.

Before the disaster, this place was originally a bar, like the gym in the small building upstairs, only open to members. Chen Xin and Qin Lan used to frequent there.

When they were dating, they were regulars here, but after they broke up, they never came together again.

However, each of them came here to find Mo Qingyan quite often, so both Chen Xin and Qin Lan were familiar with it.

The former bar had been converted by Mo Qingyan into a storage area, containing a large quantity of food supplies, most of which were now frozen into solid chunks.

"I remember our first date was here?" Chen Xin suddenly said to Qin Lan, bringing up the past.

Qin Lan, recalling the scene of their first date while looking at the completely changed bar, felt a sense of revisiting an old place, and nodded, "You were more likable back then than you are now."

"People change, just like this place has turned completely different," Chen Xin sighed, heading downwards with Qin Lan, navigating through the boxes frozen solid together on the ground.

When they got downstairs, they saw Mo Qingyan’s original wine cellar, and both couldn’t help feeling it was a pity; due to the low temperature, the wine cellar was also frozen over.

"If Qingyan saw her wine cellar like this, she might cry," Qin Lan knew how much Mo Qingyan treasured her collection, having arranged the bar upstairs for those treasures.

This wine cellar had cost her significant effort; now, seeing it all frozen, one could easily imagine how Mo Qingyan would feel.

"It’s just frozen; it should be salvageable. But for now, let’s leave it alone and let Qingyan handle it herself later," Chen Xin decided not to move the frozen wine cellar rashly.

All the wine inside were Mo Qingyan’s treasures. As it stands, they might be recoverable, but if Chen Xin were to handle them and cause damage, they would truly be irrecoverable.

Qin Lan nodded at Chen Xin’s words and continued downwards with him.

The condition of the lowest level of the sanctuary was relatively better than the two levels above. Thanks to the geothermal heat pump Chen Xin had installed for Mo Qingyan earlier, the temperature wasn’t too low. With the upper layers and the thick snow on the ground offering additional insulation, the temperature here was not unacceptably low.

At least when Chen Xin and Qin Lan descended, the tanks in the aquaponics system connected to the geothermal heat pump weren’t frozen. Although the fish inside weren’t very active, they were still breathing and circling the heating tubes, not belly up or dead.

The plants above the tank, however, were covered with frost, having a layer of white frost on the leaves, seemingly needing to be consumed quickly.

Seeing this situation, Chen Xin and Qin Lan felt slightly relieved compared to when they arrived. At least Mo Qingyan’s place hadn’t reached the worst-case scenario; although things looked grim, the losses were manageable, and the place was still inhabitable.

"The temperature here is low; it seems that if Qingyan wants to return, she’ll need an upgrade to the heating system," Chen Xin commented, shedding the Power Armor and maintaining his body temperature with the electric heating from his driving suit, as he wandered through Mo Qingyan’s house, examining the things around.

Appliances like the air conditioner and television in Mo Qingyan’s house were covered with a layer of white frost. Wiping it away showed that the frost was quite thick.

This low temperature apparently inflicted significant damage to these electronic devices, but it was obvious they were beyond recovery. Chen Xin merely glanced at them and gave up any thoughts of repairing them.

After wiping away the frost on the air conditioner’s panel, Chen Xin tried pressing the power button, but there was no response: "The air conditioner seems to be frozen. If Qingyan wants to return, she’d have to replace many things here."

"It’s better than having this place completely scrapped, and compared to the air conditioner, I think the geothermal heat pump might be more practical," Qin Lan remarked, glancing at the defunct air conditioner and the still-operating geothermal heat pump, noting which was better without needing further evaluation: "Ashen, will you help Qingyan repair this place?"

"We’ll see if she needs it. For now, let’s leave it as it is," Chen Xin shook his head, having no intention of fixing Mo Qingyan’s house right now: "Qingyan probably won’t be coming back soon, and Ding Ning can’t return either. Let’s just leave it for now, since fixing it would be a major project; let’s wait until this blizzard passes and then consider it."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 317 - 315 Safe Production (Part 1)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The blizzard that raged for nearly a month finally calmed down, but even so, the temperature didn’t rise significantly.

The cooling brought by the blizzard was an instantaneous process, and now, without the sun, the temperature’s recovery is a very slow process.

However, after the blizzard stopped, the temperature rose from the original minus seventy degrees to over minus sixty degrees. An increase of more than ten degrees was already quite difficult.

"According to the meteorological department’s forecast, the temperature should be able to rise to around minus fifty degrees, but that is already the limit. Unless the dust in the sky disperses, there won’t be much further increase," the researcher explained to Chen Xin about the weather prediction from the meteorological department and handed him a detailed analysis report.

Chen Xin took the report, and although he didn’t recognize most of the technical terms, he read it seriously and then said, "It seems the weather will remain like this for now. Will it affect our subsequent construction?"

"There will definitely be an impact, which is unavoidable in the current environment." The researcher sighed helplessly before continuing, "The meteorological department, aided by supercomputers, has calculated the current environment. In light of the global extreme cold weather, even if the dust disperses and we get sunlight back, it will take hundreds or even thousands of years for the cold weather to alleviate.

So dealing with cold weather during construction is a challenge that we must face now and in the future. Even if there are impacts, we can only find ways to overcome them."

To this, Chen Xin could only nod in agreement, "Unless we build dome cities like in science fiction, using a dome to cover the entire city and improve the environment inside, there’s no way to avoid or change this cold weather."

"Dome cities..." Hearing Chen Xin’s words, the researcher couldn’t help but shake his head, "That would be wonderful, but we simply can’t build that right now!"

Dome cities are certainly wonderful, but building a dome that can encompass an entire city requires extraordinary materials, which current human technology is far from achieving.

Even though Chen Xin has the ability to upgrade systems, constructing a dome city isn’t something easily achieved. The number of survival points required just to upgrade a city is an unimaginable figure for Chen Xin.

However, while dome cities are out of reach, other things might be possible.

"Has the country arranged for production of the Exoskeleton Protective Suit designs I submitted?" Chen Xin inquired of the researcher, as he didn’t want his hard work to be shelved.

On hearing Chen Xin inquire about the Exoskeleton Protective Suit, the researcher quickly responded, "The country has already begun organizing production, but I heard they’re producing the exoskeleton and the protective suit parts separately."

"Producing them separately? Why?" Chen Xin was a bit puzzled, not understanding why the complete Exoskeleton Protective Suit was split up.

"It seems there are still some technical challenges with the exoskeleton production that need to be overcome, and the country feels the protective suits should be rolled out first." The researcher wasn’t fully clear on the matter but had some insight into the reasoning.

Compared to a protective suit, which merely incorporates heating features with metal wires, the exoskeleton part is evidently more complex, requiring higher technical precision.

Moreover, a crucial component is the high-performance battery used for the functionality of the exoskeleton. Together with the exoskeleton control system, this is the core of the exoskeleton. Without these parts, the exoskeleton is just a hollow frame with no practical value.

Among these, the control system is merely a matter of chips and programming. With the system template from Chen Xin’s sample, replicating a system isn’t difficult. But deciphering how to manufacture high-performance batteries requires time.

Nevertheless, the country obviously can’t wait until all technical challenges are solved before producing the Exoskeleton Protective Suits, so they’ve decided to separate them, first producing the more straightforward protective suit part to meet the needs of shelter residents for outdoor use, which is an understandable choice.

Chen Xin quickly understood why the country made such a decision and didn’t say more, instead asking the researcher about the installation progress of the production line, "What about the installation progress of the production line? How long until it’s completed?"

"The site construction is all finished, and all the factory buildings meet national standards. Equipment is currently being installed and tested, with complete installation expected this week; equipment commissioning will start next week," the researcher checked his notebook to confirm the progress before reporting to Chen Xin.

After that, the researcher also expressed concern, "Currently, we’re using process parameters sent from the laboratory. They previously made a 10KW Isotope Thermoelectric Battery using these parameters, so production should be fine. However, the power of the batteries might not meet expectations.

Although there’s an idea to adjust the process parameters through trial production, I feel we shouldn’t waste too many resources on this. Quickly refining and adjusting the parameters to produce products that meet requirements is key."

"Don’t worry, I already have a plan for this. Once all equipment is installed and tested, I’ll adjust the existing process parameters on the equipment before the first production begins." Chen Xin assured the researcher, as this was part of his plan.

The researcher nodded, noting this in his notebook before continuing, "Regarding workers, the municipal government helped recruit a batch. They’re currently being trained and should be ready to start once the equipment testing is completed."

"Safety must be a priority; our standards should be high, not low, to eliminate any potential safety incidents." Although Chen Xin himself could manually handle Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries, he didn’t think others could do the same.

Especially since producing Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries involves a lot of radioactive materials, any radiation leakage or contamination incident could have severe consequences, so emphasizing safety is never excessive.

"Rest assured, we’re also highly focused on safety and will eliminate all potential hazards." The researcher, addressing this issue, looked serious and earnestly assured Chen Xin.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 318 - 316: Returning Home (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When the blizzard finally ceased, Chen Xin stepped out from the refuge to the surface, only to be unexpectedly greeted by the clear sky.

Though the thick dust still blocked the sunlight, as Chen Xin looked up, he felt that the air beneath the dust seemed remarkably clean.

It was a paradoxical sensation, yet easily understood.

Due to the blizzard, the dust and other particles in the air were swept away by winds over twelve levels in strength, so when the fierce winds suddenly stopped, the air appeared clean and clear, which was nothing unusual.

This is also why many say that the weather improves after rain or strong winds, simply because the dirty things in the air are carried away by the rain or wind.

However, the atmosphere is layered, and disturbances in the lower atmosphere rarely affect the upper atmosphere. The dust and particles suspended high up are the main factors that block sunlight from reaching the ground, and they weren’t affected by this blizzard.

This explains why when Chen Xin looked up, he still saw a dark sky, yet could also feel a hint of the sky’s clarity.

"The snow has finally stopped!" Chen Xin and Qin Lan went outside again together, and as they looked up at the sky no longer filled with snowflakes blown by the storm, they couldn’t help but release a sigh of relief.

"Yes, it finally stopped. If it hadn’t, who knows how many more people could have frozen to death because of this snow." Qin Lan expressed some lamentation as well.

Previously alongside Chen Xin, Qin Lan had hurried between various refuges, witnessing quite a few people who had frozen to death due to the cold, which made her feel very uncomfortable.

Now that the snow had stopped, she could finally lay down the worry weighing on her heart for the other refuge survivors.

"The wind and snow have passed, everything will get better." Chen Xin knew what Qin Lan was feeling for the people who had frozen to death. He too felt a heaviness in his heart, but he focused more on those who were still alive.

The deceased are already gone, an irreversible fact. Apart from mourning and remembrance, there isn’t much more we can do for them.

But the living still have to go on; we can do something for them to help them live better.

"Yes, everything will get better." Qin Lan looked intently at Chen Xin and nodded seriously.

Her confidence wasn’t towards the world, but towards Chen Xin. She believed he would make everything alright.

"Let’s head back. We’ve been out for days, I wonder what home is like now." Chen Xin said to Qin Lan, a slight bitter smile appearing on his face: "It’s been days since we left home, I wonder how things are now. Mom and Dad must be worried again."

However, this time Qin Lan shook her head and declined: "You head back alone, I have some matters to attend to, so I won’t be going back with you."

"Matters? What kind of matters?" Chen Xin wasn’t surprised that Qin Lan wasn’t returning with him, but he asked out of habit.

"It’s about the squad. Previously, when my hand got injured, I had already withdrawn from the front line, but I’ve never formally dealt with the paperwork. I intend to officially resign from the police force." Qin Lan explained her reasons to Chen Xin for staying behind.

Chen Xin looked at Qin Lan, though she appeared calm on the surface, he knew her heart must be quite agitated at this moment.

As Qin Lan’s former boyfriend, Chen Xin understood how much effort Qin Lan had invested to become a good police officer. Being a policeman was her life’s aspiration and biggest goal and pursuit.

But now she was saying she wanted to end this Chapter, she must have made a significant decision to do so.

However, Chen Xin didn’t try to persuade Qin Lan or interfere with her decision, he simply nodded and asked: "Do you want me to accompany you?"

"No need, I want to handle this alone. You should head home; Uncle and Aunt must be worried about you." Qin Lan shook her head, not intending for Chen Xin to stay.

"Alright, just let me know if you need anything." Seeing this, Chen Xin could only offer a statement of support.

"Yes, if there’s anything, I’ll ask for your help." Qin Lan wasn’t planning on asking for Chen Xin’s assistance, but she didn’t refuse his goodwill, instead agreeing though not intending to involve Chen Xin.

Of course, Chen Xin realized Qin Lan wouldn’t reach out for his help, but such matters can’t be forced, so he didn’t press the issue. After a few more exchanges, he turned towards the direction of his home.

Due to the ravages of the previous blizzard, the snow outside piled thick and deep, burying most refuge entrances which were now being cleared, making vehicle travel almost impossible. Thus, Chen Xin could only walk back as he had come.

Watching Chen Xin’s departing silhouette, Qin Lan kept her gaze until his figure vanished, before turning around to return to the refuge and attend to her own matters.

————————————————

With no longer any interference from wind and snow, Chen Xin found his path home unobstructed, but when he arrived at his home’s location, he couldn’t find the door.

The thick snow had entirely buried the refuge without leaving any trace, and even the once prominent observation room had disappeared beneath the snow.

"Seems I’ll have to dig out my own home?" Chen Xin felt slightly helpless, meanwhile using the radio to contact the brothers Han Ping and Han Ze inside to open the door, controlling the Universal Mechanical Arm on his Power Armor to clear the snow in front, using a metal detector to locate the exact position of the refuge entrance.

Han Ping and Han Ze had apparently remained at the radio station, receiving Chen Xin’s communication, and Han Ze’s excited voice quickly came through: "Mr. Chen, you’re back?"

"Yes, the blizzard has stopped, so I returned. Everything alright at home? The doorway is completely buried by snow; you should open the door from inside and dig a path upwards." Chen Xin told Han Ze his instructions while continuing his attempt to find the refuge entrance.

If he didn’t have the Power Armor, Chen Xin would indeed find it quite difficult to locate his own door, as the post-blizzard changed both terrain and landscape, making finding his door evidently a challenging task. Unless all the snow was dug away, finding his door wouldn’t be simple.

Yet fortunately, he had the Universal Mechanical Arm, which, through metal detection and echo location, allowed Chen Xin to swiftly locate the door buried beneath the snow.

Additionally, following his instructions, Han Ping opened the door from inside and, along with Han Ze, began digging outwards. So, Chen Xin also quickly cleared the snow, converged with Han Ze and Han Ping who were digging from inside, cleared the snow before the refuge entrance, and finally returned to his home.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 319 - 317 Trivial Matters at Home (3 Updates)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Together with the Han brothers, Han Ping and Han Ze, they cleared the snow at the entrance and closed the refuge door again. Only then did Chen Xin return to the shelter and take off his power armor.

"Mr. Chen, you’re finally back!" Han Ping was a bit emotional seeing Chen Xin return. The shelter had been sealed off by a snowstorm these past few days, and he was constantly worried about Chen Xin’s safety, regretting not going with him to the city that day.

Seeing Chen Xin back now, he finally let go of his worry. After all, as a bodyguard, he should be with the person he is protecting.

"Is everything okay at home? Nothing happened, right? Where are my parents?" Chen Xin shrugged indifferently, took off his driving helmet, and asked Han Ping.

"Everything here is fine. Although we were trapped by the snowstorm before, the shelter is self-sufficient, so there’s no problem. Your parents are downstairs," Han Ping quickly calmed down and answered Chen Xin.

Chen Xin nodded, patted Han Ping on the shoulder, and said, "You’ve worked hard. It’s good that nothing happened. I’ll go check on my parents first. You two go ahead and do your things."

After saying this, Chen Xin headed towards the elevator.

Upon reaching the lower level by elevator, because he had already heard from Han Ping that Chen Xin was back, his parents and the Mo sisters were waiting together for his return.

"Dad, Mom, I’m back." Chen Xin greeted his parents and also gave a smile to the Mo sisters nearby.

"It’s good that you’re back. What about Qin? Didn’t she come back with you?" Chen Xin’s mother quickly showed concern for Qin Lan upon seeing that Chen Xin came back alone.

Although Chen Xin and Qin Lan had broken up, when they were together, Chen Xin’s mother had already regarded Qin Lan as her daughter-in-law.

When they broke up, Chen Xin’s mother was quite upset about losing such a good daughter-in-law. Now that they showed signs of getting back together, of course, Chen Xin’s mother was very concerned, hoping they can reconcile so she can soon have a grandchild.

Therefore, seeing that Qin Lan did not come back together, she was naturally very concerned about the matter.

"A-Lan had some business in the city, so I came back first." Chen Xin explained briefly and then concernedly asked his parents, "Dad, Mom, did anything happen to you these past few days? Is everything okay at home?"

"Everything at home is fine, just a bit cold, nothing else. You’re tired from coming back, go wash up and have a good rest," Chen Xin’s father nodded slightly, relieved to see his son was fine, and gave him a couple instructions before turning to leave with Chen Xin’s mother.

After his parents left, Chen Xin turned to Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin, greeting the two and asking, "Hi, you two beauties, are you comfortable living at my place?"

"It’s okay, but the vegetables are almost ready for harvest again this month, and the few chickens are doing well. The hens even started laying eggs a few days ago." Mo Qingyan maintained her slightly cold demeanor but reminded Chen Xin after answering his question: "Uncle and Auntie were very worried about you before, if there’s nothing else, don’t run around. They’re too old to bear if something happened to you."

"I understand, thank you for your concern." Chen Xin smiled at Mo Qingyan to show he appreciated her advice.

Faced with Chen Xin’s attitude, Mo Qingyan could only nod and gave no further response.

On the other hand, Mo Qingxin was very concerned as she asked Chen Xin, "What’s the situation with the food supplies in the city now? The blizzard was so big, the situation in the city must be very serious, right?"

"The situation in the city is indeed very serious, nearly everywhere is buried under snow. In several official shelters I visited, people had frozen to death." Mentioning this topic, Chen Xin’s face turned somewhat heavy as well: "Fortunately, things have finally stabilized. By the time I left, the city government was already organizing post-disaster recovery and production resumption."

"People froze to death? It was actually that severe!" Mo Qingyan’s expression changed slightly upon hearing that people had frozen to death in the city from Chen Xin.

Even though she knew the blizzard was severe, because Chen Xin had made adequate thermal precautions here, keeping the temperature at a human tolerable level with the geothermal pump, Mo Qingyan thought the city’s situation shouldn’t be as bad. Yet she was surprised to hear Chen Xin mention fatalities, which was far beyond her expectations.

Chen Xin nodded heavily, then continued, "The low temperature issue is quite severe. Here we have a geothermal pump, so it’s not much of a problem, but in the city, the boilers couldn’t produce enough power to raise the temperature. Before I went into the city, many shelters had temperatures below minus ten degrees, causing many people to suffer from cold exposure and some even froze to death."

Speaking, Chen Xin detailed the situation in the city to the Mo sisters and explained why he stayed in the city for so long.

"I didn’t expect you did so much in the city, thank you for your hard work." Knowing what Chen Xin had been occupied with, Mo Qingyan’s attitude softened considerably, touched that he remembered to check on her family’s place: "I’m going to leave my place as is for now. Defrosting the wine is quite troublesome, and even if defrosted, the taste would be affected. Might as well leave it like that. I’ll deal with it when I return later."

Although she treasured her collection dearly, right now Mo Qingyan really couldn’t handle the wine, and with her home frozen under snow, she and her sister had no means or methods to clean it up.

So she wasn’t tied up on the issue and planned to leave it for a while, figuring she’ll find a way to handle it when she returned later.

"Alright, since you say so, we’ll talk about it then. When A-Lan and I left, we locked the door securely, no one will get in, so you can rest easy about that." Chen Xin had no objections to Mo Qingyan’s decision.

After finishing these matters, Mo Qingyan noticed the fatigue on Chen Xin’s face, thus pulling her sister who wanted to say more, she said to Chen Xin, "A-Xin, you should go rest first. Coming back from the city must be tiring, rest well and we’ll talk later."

"Yeah, I’m indeed a bit tired. I’ll go take a bath first. Being in the city for several days, I didn’t have a proper bath, it’s been bothering me." Saying so, Chen Xin smiled at Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin before heading upstairs to his room, planning to take a nice long bath to relax.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 320 - 318 The Concept of the Energy Tower (Part 1)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Returning home, Chen Xin threw himself into the bathroom, soaking comfortably in the warm, large bathtub.

This room, designed as a bathroom, had nothing more than a bathtub occupying 80% of the space and two showerheads, allowing one to fully enjoy the pleasure and comfort of bathing.

The water in the bathtub was sourced from underground hot springs. Although the extreme cold weather affected the water temperature to some extent, with auxiliary heating, soaking in it was still incredibly comfortable.

Although each room in the shelter had its own shower facilities, bathing and soaking are two completely different things: one is merely cleaning the body, the other is a relaxation of body and mind.

Before the disaster, this might have been an ordinary thing, something most ordinary people could enjoy if they wanted.

But now, after the disaster, this is an undeniable luxury. Without sufficient resources, enjoying a bath is exceedingly difficult.
