Previously in the city, while each shelter had better heating and enough hot water supply thanks to Chen Xin’s boiler improvements, survivors were only provided with hot showers, not baths.

Although from a therapeutic perspective, bathing has excellent effects on treating hypothermia and frostbite, the consumption for baths and showers is completely different.

After all, to save water and coal, the official shelter allowed hot showers, enabling survivors to have a warm wash, but with a time limit. Fifteen minutes was enough for a comfortable shower, but certainly not an indulgence.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin, soaking in the pool, couldn’t help but wonder if he should let Li Yulong set up a bathroom in his city shelter; perhaps many people would want to take a bath there?

However, this idea lingered briefly in his mind before he dismissed it. It’s not that it couldn’t be done; on the contrary, it’s too simple. It didn’t need his special attention, just a word to Li Yulong, who could handle it himself.

Nowadays, Chen Xin was focused more on helping the country’s post-disaster reconstruction and how to improve the survivors’ living conditions. He no longer had the energy for such trivial matters.

This was not because Chen Xin placed himself too high, nor was it some superhero fantasy of "great power comes with great responsibility." It was simply the plain principle of tending to oneself when poor and aiding the world when successful.

When one lacks capacity, taking care of oneself and one’s family is the goal; when one has capacity, one helps others to the best of their ability, which is neither hard to understand nor impossible to achieve.

Though in the apocalypse, survival has become more difficult, and everyone struggles to live, leaving little energy to help others. Some even become selfish, seizing others’ chances to survive for their own survival.

But for Chen Xin, he already lived well and could live better. Helping others live a bit better while living better himself wasn’t an issue.

Because he had that ability, and doing so cost him nothing.

Now, he had given the nation the exoskeleton protective suit blueprints, which could restore outdoor mobility for most people as the country organized production. The isotope thermoelectric battery production line was also completed and about to be operational; improved communication base stations had started construction before the blizzard, and the network’s spread was only a matter of time; and...

Counting things up, Chen Xin realized he had already done quite a lot and helped numerous people.

For instance, the two hundred thousand survivors in the city; without his help, a considerable number might have perished in the blizzard, and saving so many lives gave Chen Xin tremendous fulfillment.

Besides, for Chen Xin, enabling the country’s post-disaster recovery and restoring everything to pre-disaster conditions had a significant allure.

So now, he was pondering how to further improve the city’s living conditions and speed up post-disaster recovery.

The dome city idea discussed with researchers was undoubtedly a suitable choice, but the construction difficulty is too high and unrealistic.

Moreover, there are other methods to improve urban survival conditions.

Currently, the greatest challenge and difficulty facing survivors is the cold. If the temperature could be raised, many problems could be alleviated.

As for increasing temperature, the Energy Tower from the construction game "Frostpunk," which kept cities warm even in sub-hundred-degree temperatures, was clearly a good choice.

Though a science fiction concept, the Energy Tower is based on steam-era technology, making it feasible to replicate in reality.

However, even if replicated, an Energy Tower couldn’t heat an entire city; after all, it’s basically a giant boiler. Instead of just using a boiler, transporting steam or hot water through pipes is more efficient for heating.

This not only enhances heating efficiency but also the effectiveness; apart from people like Chen Xin who consider raising a whole area’s temperature, no one would think of the Energy Tower’s inefficient radial heating method.

Unless it doesn’t just generate heat by burning coal, but rather drives machinery to extract more heat from other sources.

This is indeed feasible, like the ground source heat pump Chen Xin used in his shelter, which, driven by electricity, uses a compressor and water pump to extract more heat from underground.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin recalled the game’s requirement of digging a deep foundation to build an Energy Tower, visualizing this as a viable plan.

First, dig a deep enough pit to reach deep layers, then by blowing air into the pit to create airflow, carry the deep layer’s geothermal heat to the surface, allowing significant thermal energy production with minimal energy consumption.

With enough thermal energy, whether for heating or power generation, the city’s condition can be significantly changed.

With this thought, Chen Xin gradually formed a construction plan centered on the Energy Tower.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 321 - 319 Planning and Plans (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin plans to build an Energy Tower, using it as the core to provide heating and electricity to a city.

Without the system’s presence, this would certainly be a beautiful idea. Although it’s theoretically technically feasible, actually building such an Energy Tower would be an impossible task.

First, drilling a deep pit that reaches deep strata is a massive construction endeavor. Although modern drilling technology can drill wells several kilometers deep, such wells lack the value of being used as the foundation for an Energy Tower.

Taking Lucia’s highly-touted Kola Deep Well in Sovia as an example, many people online boast that this well was drilled directly into Hell, and from it many monsters have flown out, earning the title "Gate of Hell." Years ago, Sovia even planned to drill through Blue Star...

However, upon truly looking up the information, one would find that the so-called "Gate of Hell" is 12,262 meters deep, with an upper diameter of 92 cm and a lower diameter of 21.5 cm. With such a diameter, even lowering a person in would be quite difficult.

The truth is that the so-called deep wells on Earth are not the gigantic tunnels people imagine, but tiny holes that only a drill bit and rod can go down—petroleum drilling is the same.

As for wells that can accommodate several people, appear thick, and are simultaneously very deep, there certainly are such wells. The world’s deepest mine shaft is over 4,000 meters deep, but it’s the result of more than a century of continual downward digging. It’s not one deep well reaching the bottom directly, but multiple excavation planes, with the so-called over 4,000 meters being the deepest section at the bottom of the mine.

Here, the environment is extremely hostile, and temperatures can reach 57℃, surpassing the limits of human tolerance.

Of course, this also indirectly proves the feasibility of drilling deep wells to the deep strata to utilize geothermal energy.

In Chen Xin’s conception, the structure of the Energy Tower should be to drill two deep wells over 3,000 meters underground, with their bottoms connected. One well is responsible for blowing air downwards, and the other for extracting air upwards, using air to tap into deep geothermal energy.

The ground part would actually be equivalent to a gigantic blower and thermal converter, to blow air into the deep well depths, extract geothermal energy, and convert the extracted thermal energy into electricity or directly send it to needed areas.

Moreover, Chen Xin does not intend to take the bold approach of directly discharging into the air. Instead, he plans to use the current underground transport network to lay heating pipes and distribute thermal energy throughout the city.

This way, the overall temperature of the city could be raised, and given that many shelters are not located very deep underground, with their temperature rising, the excess heat could diffuse through the base to the surface, indirectly raising the surface temperature.

Certainly, if it’s truly necessary to create a heating field on the surface, connecting a radiator with pipes to the surface could create a warming zone.

This is somewhat akin to radiators or stoves in games, allowing a manually created small heating area. Even though the heating efficiency might be negatively impacted by environmental factors, raising the temperature from -50℃ to -30℃ still holds practical significance!

Whether it’s ground construction, opening surface greenhouses, or other uses, it all presents high practical values.

Not to mention, with Chen Xin’s improvements, this kind of radiator-made small heating field would definitely not only raise the temperature by a mere 20 degrees.

If this idea could indeed be realized, as long as the Energy Tower and radiators are efficient enough, humanity could truly return to surface living.

In the future, even if surface temperatures remain low, once sunlight returns, with the Energy Tower and radiators allowing humanity back to the surface—not confined to underground shelters—it presents a fundamentally different meaning for human civilization.

However, it’s not difficult to construct radiators for small heating fields, but it’s not easy to build an Energy Tower capable of providing thermal energy to a city.

This involves knowledge from engineering, thermodynamics, material science, and architectural mechanics—not something one person can handle alone.

Even if Chen Xin can skip many steps by system upgrades, if it can’t be replicated elsewhere relying solely on Chen Xin’s system upgrade, its practical significance remains limited.

After all, Chen Xin is but one person, constructing such an Energy Tower requires a substantial amount of survival points. To build such towers in all cities or most cities in Flame Country, the survival points and time required would be too much for Chen Xin. It’s even questionable whether he could achieve this in his lifetime.

Therefore, the best solution remains the same: Chen Xin constructs a finished Energy Tower and then hands it over to the nation to reverse engineer, allowing replication elsewhere without relying on Chen Xin. Only with national support can Energy Towers be erected in most cities nationwide, achieving the goal of returning humanity from underground to the surface.

However, building an Energy Tower is evidently a very troublesome and complicated matter. After all, Chen Xin’s ability is merely upgrading, not creating out of nothing. He must have a foundation that can be regarded as an Energy Tower to upgrade it into a genuine Energy Tower.

This is the system’s usage rule—he’d still need to weld a frame even if he merely intended to burn coal inside it, claiming it’s an Energy Tower.

Thus, this matter can’t be quickly resolved or produce results, so Chen Xin decides not to think about it for now. This sort of long-term commitment that’s only evident with time doesn’t need to be hurriedly started—better to first focus on what can be done now.

"The battery production line is almost installed. After an upgrade, it should be usable." Chen Xin, lying in the bathtub, counts on his fingers the tasks he’s completed or is completing, planning upcoming tasks: "Once the battery production line begins operation, a generator production line could be considered. This would provide sufficient energy. The remaining matters include building a large surface greenhouse and the Energy Tower—these two might proceed together..."

Chen Xin thinks about his previous plan to construct a large planting greenhouse on the surface, pondering that it could be related to the Energy Tower plan, as the Energy Tower’s primary function is heating, which is exactly what a planting greenhouse requires on a large scale.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 322 - 320: Clearing the Snow (Third Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After resting in his room for the night, Chen Xin got up the next day, had breakfast, and took the elevator to the upper level once again. However, this time he wasn’t alone; he was accompanied by his parents and the Mo Family sisters.

Not only that, but everyone, including Chen Xin, was wearing an Exoskeleton Protective Suit and holding tools like shovels that Chen Xin had just made.

They were going to clear the snow.

When Chen Xin returned yesterday, Han Ping had only dug a path out from the shelter, allowing Chen Xin to enter. The main entrance of the shelter was still buried under snow, and clearing the snow in front of the door was necessary to get out.

Clearing snow deep enough to bury a person is obviously not something one or two people can manage, so Chen Xin naturally mobilized some labor from the shelter.

Initially, Chen Xin only planned to call Mo Qingyan and Mo Qingxin, but his parents felt that they could handle this little task, so they came along.

The group reached the surface, where Han Ping was already dressed in a protective suit, waiting at the main entrance.

Han Ze didn’t come along, as Chen Xin had assigned him to continue keeping watch to prevent anyone from sneaking up on them while they were working.

After all, during the snow clearing, the shelter’s door was open. If someone attempted a surprise attack on the shelter without a guard, it would be very dangerous.

Although this was just a precaution, given the current situation outside, whether on foot or by car, movement was nearly impossible. Previously, Chen Xin relied on his Power Armor to return from the city, so he didn’t think others would have similar capabilities, but necessary safety measures are still needed.

"Han Ping, please assist my parents a bit. They’re getting on in years, so look after them." Chen Xin distributed the tools to everyone and whispered to Han Ping when handing him a shovel.

Han Ping nodded knowingly and approached Chen Xin’s parents, offering to assist them with their tools.

However, Chen Xin’s parents declined Han Ping’s help. Seeing this, Han Ping stayed close by, planning to do more work himself so that Chen Xin’s parents wouldn’t have to overexert themselves.

After all, among all these people, only Chen Xin’s parents were elders, unlike the young who are physically strong. It’s only natural to do a bit more themselves.

Chen Xin was the first to climb out along the path Han Ping dug yesterday. He didn’t start shoveling immediately but estimated his position relative to the door and climbed up to clear the snow on top of the observation room, aiming to at least expose the observation window.

Fortunately, Chen Xin was quite familiar with his shelter. Using the door as a reference, he quickly found the approximate location of the observation room, dug open the snow with his shovel, and created a foothold for himself, then started digging with the help of the exoskeleton.

The reason for not wearing the Power Armor today was that it was too heavy. Even if Chen Xin compacted the snow at his feet, standing was still challenging while wearing it. Unless he forcefully squeezed through the snow with the Power Armor’s strength, like when he returned, movement would be difficult.

Thus, the lighter and more flexible Exoskeleton Protective Suit was a more suitable choice compared to the Power Armor.

Compared to the Power Armor, which is more suited for combat, Chen Xin found the Exoskeleton Protective Suit more comfortable for regular work and excursions after using it for some time.

With the exoskeleton’s assistance, Chen Xin quickly shoveled away a large area of snow, pushing it aside as much as possible towards his target. He soon hit something solid with his shovel, jolting his hands.

Clearing away the snow, Chen Xin had reached the wall of the observation room. The previous strike of the shovel had hit the wall.

Since he found the wall, the rest was simple. Chen Xin quickly cleared along the wall, digging out the observation room buried deep in snow, exposing the observation window beneath the snow.

However, some ice had formed on the observation window, so Chen Xin needed to break it off, being careful not to break the glass in the process.

After removing the ice on the observation window, Chen Xin greeted Han Ze inside and turned around to continue clearing along the observation room, not forgetting about the ventilation vent installed next to it.

Even though the existence of a greenhouse and oxygen generator minimizes the impact of airflow through the vent, burying the vent would still cause some inconvenience, so the opportunity needed to be seized to clear it.

Clearing the ventilation vent was effortless; although ice formed near it as well, it didn’t take much effort from Chen Xin.

When Chen Xin finished removing the snow around the ventilation vent and descended along his previous ascent, Han Ping and the Mo Family sisters had already cleared a large area at the shelter’s entrance.

"Quick move, Long Drone!" Chen Xin greeted Mo Qingyan.

"You’re quick too, cleared all the snow up there?" Mo Qingyan seemed in a good mood today and wasn’t as cold towards Chen Xin as before.

"Yes, it’s all cleared. The observation window and ventilation vent had some ice, but it wasn’t hard to deal with," Chen Xin explained the situation above to Mo Qingyan, then resumed shoveling alongside her to clear the snow at the entrance.

The snow from the previous blizzard had piled up over four meters deep at the entrance of Chen Xin’s shelter. Although the snow was this deep only near the door, extending outward meant it got shallower, removing all the snow was still no easy task.

Even with the exoskeletons providing assistance, allowing rapid clearing of large areas without much effort, digging through an average of over two meters deep and at least one or two hundred square meters of snow in front of the door was quite a task.

Fortunately, including Chen Xin, all six wore exoskeletons, and despite Chen Xin’s parents being older and less capable of labor, the overall clearing efficiency remained high.

By lunchtime, they had cleared a large section of snow at the entrance, revealing the snow-buried main door and a small area around it.

"Alright, let’s stop here for the morning. Let’s have lunch and rest, we’ll finish the rest in the afternoon," Chen Xin suggested, inviting the Mo Family sisters and his parents to take a break.

Just as Chen Xin said this, Han Ze’s voice suddenly came through the communicator: "Mr. Chen, there’s a light in the distance!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 323 - 321: Food Shortage (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Hearing Han Ze mention lights in the distance, Chen Xin was a bit surprised and puzzled. The blizzard had just passed, how could someone be coming here?

Even though he was perplexed, it didn’t affect Chen Xin’s response. He let his parents and the Mo sisters return inside the shelter, then closed the shelter door with Han Ping. Only then did Chen Xin go to the observation room, taking the binoculars handed over by Han Ze to look into the distance.

Indeed, just as Han Ze said, there were a few points of light speeding towards Chen Xin’s home across the snowy landscape.

"So fast! What is that?" Chen Xin estimated the speed at which the lights were approaching and found that they were very fast, clearly not like a car.

Because of the snow, cars couldn’t move quickly on the snow, and the lights appeared to belong to a vehicle with just one headlight, unlike cars that should have two.

As Chen Xin felt puzzled, the vehicle represented by those lights also approached Chen Xin’s shelter and stopped at the clearing that Chen Xin and others had just cleared out, sending over communications.

"Comrade Chen, it’s me, Lao Li from the SWAT team! Please open the door!" Lao Li’s voice came through the radio, finally putting Chen Xin at ease and prompting him to have Han Ping open the door.

Meanwhile, Chen Xin looked closely through the binoculars again from the observation room before going down to the door himself.

Once the door was opened, Lao Li and the others quickly got off the vehicle, leaving two people to watch the vehicle while the others swiftly entered Chen Xin’s shelter.

With the door open, Chen Xin was finally able to see clearly under the light inside the doorway that Lao Li and the others were riding a snowmobile.

This type of vehicle was originally meant for snow use, and under current circumstances, it proved to be very practical. Especially since the snowmobiles Lao Li and the others rode also had tracked sleds, looking like they were meant for hauling cargo, which made Chen Xin take notice.

"Snowmobiles? Riding these in such cold weather, you all must be freezing!" Chen Xin couldn’t help but quip at the sight of Lao Li and the others with a layer of ice on them, and snowflakes hanging from their eyebrows as they walked into the shelter.

"Who can say otherwise? But there’s no choice; cars can’t get through, so we can only ride these." Lao Li shook off the snow and ice from his body before smiling bitterly at Chen Xin: "Comrade Chen, what are you wearing? It looks quite good!"

"Exoskeleton Protective Suit. The country already has a production plan, and the first batch should be coming out soon. See if you’ll be part of the distribution." Chen Xin shrugged nonchalantly, steering the topic back to business: "In this cold weather, instead of engaging in post-disaster reconstruction in the city, why did you come to me? To collect the vegetables you left here last month? Rest assured, they’re frozen in the warehouse. You can haul them away, but this month’s harvest isn’t collected yet. If you want them, you’ll probably have to wait until tomorrow."

"That’s truly a relief!" Lao Li was overjoyed upon hearing this from Chen Xin.

They had indeed come for the vegetables, but when they arrived, they didn’t have much hope. After all, after a blizzard, no one expects vegetables to last for a month.

However, at Chen Xin’s place, the vegetables were stored in the warehouse, and the low-temperature freezing had successfully preserved these vegetables for a month. Though some might be frostbitten, at least they were still edible.

Realizing that last month’s vegetables were still intact, Lao Li relaxed and began explaining to Chen Xin why they had come over: "The city’s supply of materials is a bit tight right now. Although reserve grains are still sufficient, almost a year has passed since the meteorite fell, and the grains stored in the shelters have been consumed by nearly half. Vegetables and fruits are long gone, and many of the breadworms and rats raised before have frozen to death in this blizzard. The city government is quite worried about this."

Even though Lao Li didn’t describe it as severe, the information revealed still made Chen Xin realize the gravity of the situation.

"Isn’t the city government working on underground farming? I went over to help them renovate the boiler before and took a look at it. Don’t they have vegetables, fruits, fish, and poultry there? And what about the mushrooms grown in the various shelters?" Chen Xin asked Lao Li, frowning. Although he had anticipated the impact of the cold brought by the blizzard, he hadn’t thought it would be this severe.

Lao Li sighed deeply and shook his head, explaining to Chen Xin: "Although the underground farming area still produces quite a bit, it’s only a drop in the bucket compared to the consumption of over 200,000 survivors in the city. Moreover, this blizzard severely damaged the underground farming area, with many crops suffering severe reductions even if they didn’t die from freezing.

The mushrooms grown in various shelters did yield some food, but only partially supplement consumption. The previous blizzard destroyed most mushroom rooms, so everything needs to start over, making it hard to resume production in the short term.

Plus, the city government had previously used emergency reserves. Now the city’s material supply is indeed tight, and they’re considering rationing."

Upon hearing this from Lao Li, Chen Xin realized the seriousness of the issue.

Faced with this problem, however, he had no good solution.

He could upgrade the planting greenhouse, increasing the planting area to allow for more crops, but crop growth takes time. Even with high-yield seeds and fertilizers boosting production, they couldn’t override the natural rules to make seeds grow from today to tomorrow.

As for his own small output here, it’s more than enough to feed himself while also allowing some support for others. But solving the food crisis would require Chen Xin to upgrade his planting greenhouse several levels to a planting factory for large-scale cultivation; otherwise, relying on him for a solution is unlikely.

The real solution to this problem must rely on the country.

Though the nation has abundant reserves, having stored vast quantities of materials targeted for disaster beforehand, after over a year of consumption, how much remains is a concerning question.

After all, feeding today isn’t the end of the issue; even the largest reserves face depletion, necessitating plans for national food supply when reserves run out.

Despite Flame Country having the highest post-disaster population, providing vast resources for disaster reconstruction labor and technical personnel, it’s also a heavy burden, posing a significant challenge to Flame Country’s logistic support and material production capabilities.

Still, such matters are beyond Chen Xin’s capacity to intervene, and not something he needs to concern himself with. Rather than worrying about national food supplies, he should focus on his planned large-scale planting greenhouse, which if succeeded, could create food production areas and provide technical accumulation for the subsequent Energy Tower project, serving as a multi-purpose beneficial development.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 324 - 322
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After handing over the previously frozen vegetables from the warehouse to Li Tou, watching them load box after box onto the snowmobiles, Chen Xin increasingly felt that he could get a few of these snowmobiles for himself.

So he asked Li Tou, "Where did you get these snowmobiles from? If I wanted to barter for them, would you exchange?"

"These are allocated by the city bureau, so I don’t think we can exchange them?" Li Tou didn’t really know how to answer. These were police supplies, and according to management regulations, they obviously couldn’t be used for trade. But in this day and age, some things are uncertain, and no one can say for sure.

Moreover, given Chen Xin’s current cooperation with the government, if he really wanted to get two snowmobiles, it wouldn’t be difficult for him.

Thinking of this, Li Tou said to Chen Xin, "Comrade Xiao Chen, don’t you have a good relationship with the research institutes at the University of Science and Technology? It should be pretty easy for you to get two snowmobiles. The ones our SWAT team has are also issued from above; it might be easier for you to directly request them from the higher-ups."

Hearing this from Li Tou, Chen Xin gave up the idea of exchanging snowmobiles through the SWAT team.

If he hadn’t established a cooperative relationship with the national research institutions, Chen Xin might indeed have had to find a way through the SWAT team. But now that he had secured cooperation with the government, getting two snowmobiles would not be difficult.

Chen Xin gave up the idea of exchanging snowmobiles through the SWAT team, but this month’s vegetable output still had to be used for trade.

However, as Chen Xin’s output became increasingly bountiful, the resources he needed to exchange gradually shifted from food to everyday necessities.

Although Chen Xin was not worried about food and drink at the moment, as various vegetables and staple foods were beginning to be produced — even to the point of being able to vary his diet — and with the chicks growing, a steady supply of meat was also ensured, when it came to daily necessities, most of them still couldn’t be self-produced.

The most obvious of these is toilet paper, which is something you’d need to use every day but is basically impossible to make yourself.

Soap, shampoo, and so on are also common items that aren’t usually considered for homemade solutions and are hardly self-made.

Currently, Chen Xin can only acquire these items through bartering with others. While it’s not limited to the SWAT team, and Chen Xin can directly obtain necessities through research institutions, he currently doesn’t plan to do so but prefers to maintain the trade channel with the SWAT team.

Maintaining a relationship with the SWAT team is necessary, even if it’s not for trading resources.

————————————————

After seeing off Li Tou and his men, Chen Xin returned to the lower level of the shelter.

His mother was making lunch, while Mo Qingyan was helping out nearby.

Chen Xin walked to the kitchen door and told Mo Qingyan about what had just happened, and reminded her, "Dragon Lady, you shouldn’t clear snow this afternoon. Work with Sister Xin to harvest the vegetables in the greenhouse."

"I can handle the vegetable picking on my own. Xiao Xin should go help you guys," Mo Qingyan didn’t intend to have her sister help.

Chen Xin’s greenhouse was fully automated. While it might be a bit tiring for one person to harvest, it wasn’t strictly necessary for two people to do it.

Besides, having shoveled snow the entire morning, Mo Qingyan knew how much work there would be. Missing one person today might still be manageable, but missing two would definitely push things to tomorrow.

Hearing Mo Qingyan say that, Chen Xin didn’t object, only nodding in agreement.

"By the way, I saw you have a dehydrator here and meant to mention it, but you went to the city for supplies and I forgot about it," Mo Qingyan paused her work and approached Chen Xin, saying, "I think you can make use of the dehydrator to make these vegetables into dehydrated vegetables. This way, it can reduce storage space and also extend the shelf life of the vegetables.

You always have fresh vegetables here, so you don’t need this. But you could give it to Xiao Long to sell. I think selling dehydrated vegetables is more practical than selling strawberry jam."

"Dehydrated vegetables? That’s doable, but is my dehydrator efficient enough?" Chen Xin’s first thought upon hearing Mo Qingyan mention it was whether the dehydrator would suffice.

After all, drying vegetables from over a thousand pounds down might be a huge task if using a dehydrator that could only handle a few dozen pounds at a time.

While Chen Xin was discussing this with Mo Qingyan, his mother suddenly spoke up, "Why use a dehydrator? Just wash the vegetables, let them dry, then put them over that geothermal heat pump pipe, and they’ll be dried in a day.

When you were little, I used to dry pickles like this in the pumping station, but back then it was the heat from the motor, not laying things directly on the pipes."

Hearing his mother say this, Chen Xin also slapped his forehead, realizing his lack of life experience.

Their generation was used to buying everything online that wasn’t readily available, relying on machines or various tools to achieve goals, while people from Chen Xin’s parents’ generation were more accustomed to solving problems with what was at hand.

Like the dehydrated vegetables, Chen Xin and Mo Qingyan’s first thought was the dehydrator, but Chen Xin’s mother thought first of sun drying and then of dehydration. And any heat source could work for the drying — it wasn’t limited to using a sufficiently large dehydrator.

When Chen Xin was a child, if they wanted to prepare pickled vegetables in winter and there was no sun to dry them, they would place the washed and dried vegetables on a radiator to dry them with the heating system.

This method, compared to using a dehydrator, was not only easier and less labor-intensive but also cost-effective.

Chen Xin realized this, so he said to Mo Qingyan, "If that’s the case, Dragon Lady, first harvest all the vegetables. Set aside the portion for the SWAT team and put it in the warehouse above, leaving some for us to consume. Dry the excess to make dehydrated vegetables."

"Okay, but if that’s the plan, I won’t be able to handle so many vegetables on my own. Later..." Mo Qingyan hesitated before continuing, "You’ll have to help me out in this case, won’t you? After all, aren’t you a mid-level chef?"

Hearing Mo Qingyan say this, Chen Xin chuckled but still nodded: "Alright, go ahead and harvest the vegetables today, and I’ll help you process them tomorrow."

After that, Chen Xin entered the kitchen, approached his mother, and asked if there was anything he could help with.

Mo Qingyan watched as Chen Xin rolled up his sleeves to help with cooking, paused for a moment, and then walked over to continue her earlier work, helping with lunch preparation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 325 - 323 Dehydrated Vegetables (First Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The total amount of vegetables that Chen Xin’s greenhouse can currently harvest in a month has exceeded two thousand pounds.

Apart from cabbage and lettuce, there are also carrots, cucumbers, and tomatoes, and for the people of Xiang Province, the indispensable chili peppers.

The cucumbers, tomatoes, and chili peppers were planted by Mo Qingyan last month and haven’t borne fruit yet. If these crops do bear fruit, the total vegetable output here would rise to a new level.

Up to now, Chen Xin’s six-layer hydroponic planting trays in the greenhouse have two layers of upland rice, one layer each of cabbage, lettuce, and chili peppers, half a layer of carrots, and a quarter layer each of cucumber and tomatoes.

Among them, cabbage and lettuce can be harvested once a month, which is the main vegetable output here and the bulk of what Chen Xin exchanges for supplies from the special police team each month.

As for the carrots, chili peppers, cucumbers, and tomatoes, they are harvested every few months. Although most of these are also exchanged for supplies by Chen Xin, more are stored for personal consumption.

Moreover, other vegetables and fruits are sporadically planted in the corners of the greenhouse, as well as the herbs that Chen Xin promised to plant for Hong Rui. However, these only occupy a small area in the greenhouse, with yields negligible, just enough for the people in the shelter to have a taste and change their palate.

Nevertheless, even with more than two thousand pounds of total vegetable output a month, and Chen Xin having expanded the total trade volume with the special police team, exchanging most of the monthly output for living supplies, the leftover amount is still quite overwhelming.

While several hundred pounds of vegetables can be thrown into the warehouse, stored directly as frozen due to current low temperatures without issue, Chen Xin’s warehouse needs to store other items and can’t hold many vegetables. When rice and carrots are ready to be harvested simultaneously, the warehouse will inevitably be overloaded.

Therefore, turning fresh vegetables into dehydrated vegetables has become necessary.

Dehydrated vegetables are not the same as traditional dried vegetables; both the processing methods and nutritional value differ significantly.

Traditional dried vegetables are dried under the sun for an extended period to remove water. Due to the long drying time, the vegetables not only lose coloration and moisture, but also a lot of nutrients are lost, and their texture is damaged. Even if soaked in water, they won’t recover, and the taste is quite poor.

Typically, sun-dried vegetables are not eaten directly but used to make pickles, salted vegetables, or fermented products, which are convenient for storage and enhance flavor.

In usual circumstances, these pickled dried vegetables are, of course, appetizing side dishes that enrich the flavors, enhance taste, and cut through greasiness.

But in a food shortage situation, consuming pickled vegetables long-term would undoubtedly lead to malnutrition and many other health problems, which is quite detrimental in this frigid post-apocalyptic environment.

In contrast, dehydrated vegetables are different, as they use artificial means to remove moisture in a short time, greatly preserving both nutrition and texture, and can restore their original state when placed in water. Thus, they are convenient for storage and consumption, making them an excellent way to store vegetables in a post-apocalyptic setting.

The method to make dehydrated vegetables isn’t difficult. After helping Mo Qingyan store the vegetables to be delivered to the special police team in the warehouse, Chen Xin began preparing to make dehydrated vegetables.

"Are you planning to chop these vegetables?" Mo Qingyan asked curiously as she watched Chen Xin carry the fresh vegetables into the kitchen.

Chen Xin nodded, taking the vegetables out of the plastic boxes, placing them in the sink, turning on the tap to wash them, while replying to Mo Qingyan, "Cutting them into strips is easier to store; whole stalks are not easy to drain or dry."

"Then let me help. How should I cut them?" Mo Qingyan was eager to assist upon hearing this from Chen Xin.

"Just cut them into long strips, right?" Chen Xin raised his head in surprise to look at Mo Qingyan and asked with some confusion, "I remember you should have done this before, haven’t you? Why do you seem like you don’t know how?"

As a wilderness survival enthusiast, Mo Qingyan certainly knows how to make this kind of emergency reserve like dehydrated vegetables, but she seemed like she didn’t know how.

Facing Chen Xin’s question, Mo Qingyan stiffly replied, "I’m just worried that your method might be different from what I know! You’re a mid-tier chef, who knows if you’ll want to add some seasoning or something."

"Uh..." Faced with Mo Qingyan’s obvious tsundere remark, Chen Xin could only rub his brow and say, "This is just making dehydrated vegetables, not dried fruit or veggies. Why should I add seasoning? But you did remind me, next month I can make some adjustments, plant fewer cabbages and lettuces, plant sweet potatoes and other crops, and make some dried fruit and veggies to eat then."

"...Alright, suit yourself." Mo Qingyan glanced at Chen Xin, heard him say this, and couldn’t argue further.

Seeing Mo Qingyan had nothing more to say, Chen Xin focused back on processing the vegetables in front of him, placing some in the sink to wash clean and breaking them into pieces before saying to Mo Qingyan, "These veggies I’ve washed, cut them into strips. The cutting board and knife are over there. Be careful not to cut your hand."

"Got it. Do I look like someone who’d cut themselves while chopping veggies?" Mo Qingyan retorted to Chen Xin and then walked to the stove, took up the cutting board and knife, placed Chen Xin’s washed vegetables on the board, and started chopping.

While chopping the veggies, Mo Qingyan suddenly asked Chen Xin, "By the way, are you and Alan planning to reconcile?"

"What’s with the question out of the blue?" Chen Xin was somewhat surprised. Mo Qingyan’s question was certainly unexpected.

"Hmm? Isn’t that so? You two are sharing a bed. If you say you don’t want to be with Alan, then you’re a scumbag." Mo Qingyan’s attitude was very direct, and her words were blunt.

"Well, that... it depends on Alan’s feelings. You know the situation back then. Whether she wants to be with me, I really can’t say." Chen Xin replied evasively.

This answer didn’t satisfy Mo Qingyan, who, rather displeased, shouted at Chen Xin, "What do you mean you can’t say? Alan’s already sharing a bed with you, and you say it depends on her feelings? What more do you need from a girl to know she wants to be with you? Does Alan need to directly say ’let’s be together’ for you to get it?"

"Uh... alright, alright, I know, I’ll have a good talk about this with Alan." Chen Xin awkwardly nodded, but as he continued washing vegetables, he muttered under his breath, "Then what does it mean when you’re staying at my place?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 326 - 224 Planting Plan (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Mo Qingyan’s questioning of Chen Xin could only be considered a minor episode because Qin Lan was not related to the shelter. Although it made Chen Xin somewhat embarrassed and troubled, it didn’t cause any impact or bring about a change.

Chen Xin washed hundreds of pounds of fresh vegetables clean and handed them to Mo Qingyan to chop up. Afterward, he took these vegetables to the engine room and spread them out on the racks he made yesterday.

These racks were very simple, just welded together with a few steel tubes and covered with layers of netting. Chen Xin made them last night specifically to facilitate today’s vegetable drying.

Placed the shredded vegetables on the netting, spread them out to allow moisture to evaporate, Chen Xin then stationed these racks on the heating water pipes of the ground-source heat pump.

"Is this alright? Will they dry out properly?" Mo Qingyan saw Chen Xin only placing the racks over the pipes, not directly putting the chopped vegetables on the pipes as Chen Xin’s mom said, and couldn’t help but worry about whether they’d dry properly.

Chen Xin shook his head and explained to Mo Qingyan, "Putting them directly on the pipes is fine, but the utilization would be low, and it wouldn’t allow the steam to evaporate well. Considering the quantity, drying them like this is better."

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s explanation, Mo Qingyan didn’t say much more but asked, "Is the temperature enough? I remember dehydrated vegetables used to be dried at 80°C in the oven. Even if you place them on the warm water pipes, the temperature might not be sufficient."

"The temperature of the heating pipes exceeds 80°C, and the air temperature above is close to 60°C, which is fine for drying vegetables." Chen Xin had considered this issue, so he wasn’t worried, "Besides, drying fruits and vegetables at around 60°C is usually enough. Plus, being open allows quick evaporation, so there’s no concern for them not drying out."

Despite Chen Xin’s assurance, Mo Qingyan still felt uneasy and reached out to touch the heating pipe out of caution. Indeed, she felt it was hot to the touch, and the air above the pipes was warm. Only then did she nod assuredly.

Although Chen Xin’s racks were multi-layered, several hundred pounds of vegetables couldn’t fit on just a few racks. Especially considering adequate ventilation was necessary, Chen Xin couldn’t place too many vegetables on the netting, so after filling all the racks, more than half of the vegetables were left unplaced.

Seeing this, Chen Xin had no choice but to tell Mo Qingyan, "Looks like we’ll have to dry them in batches. This batch dries here first, and when they’re almost done, we can place the rest."

"Do we need to gather the remaining ones?" Mo Qingyan looked at the leftover vegetables, worried they might go bad if left sitting like that.

Chen Xin thought for a moment and said, "Just leave them here, spread them out a bit, and turn them over every few hours to avoid water accumulation. A day won’t spoil them."

"Alright, let’s do as you said," Mo Qingyan nodded and, with Chen Xin, piled the remaining vegetables in the corner of the engine room.

Leaving the engine room, Mo Qingyan was heading towards the greenhouse when she suddenly stopped and asked Chen Xin, "By the way, those sweet potatoes you brought back, are you planning to plant them after this season’s rice harvest? You mentioned reducing the planting area for cabbage and lettuce, so what do you plan to plant next?"

"Hmm..." Chen Xin pondered when Mo Qingyan asked him this question. After considering it, he finally spoke up, asking Mo Qingyan, "Are the sweet potatoes I brought back ready to plant?"

"They are ready. I did the seedling preparation after you brought them back, and after a month, they are ready for planting," Mo Qingyan nodded, indicating that the sweet potato seedlings were already prepared for planting.

Upon hearing this, Chen Xin made up his mind and then said, "Since the lettuce and cabbage are harvested, this time, let’s plant only a quarter of them, freeing up a layer for the sweet potatoes. As for the remaining half-layer, look at what vegetables or fruits you’d like to eat and plant several types. Just having enough variety for us to consume is fine."

"Is that so? That’s fine, then I’ll plant randomly," Mo Qingyan didn’t object to Chen Xin’s decision, just confirmed with him, "Do you want more leafy greens or more fruits and vegetables in the new planting?"

"Please plant more leafy greens, I remember there are seeds for green onions, garlic, and cilantro, plant some of those too. They can be used as seasoning when cooking," Chen Xin recalled that he hadn’t eaten green onions, garlic, and cilantro in his meals for almost a year, and couldn’t help but crave them.

Mo Qingyan gladly accepted Chen Xin’s suggestion and nodded earnestly, "We should plant these, or meals won’t taste good! Plus, the fish in the pool below the greenhouse have been kept for so long, shouldn’t they be ready to eat now?"

"I almost forgot about those fish; have they laid eggs?" Chen Xin asked with care. He was very concerned about the fish in the greenhouse and had specifically asked Li Wensheng for half male, half female fish for breeding new fry.

However, Mo Qingyan shook her head in response, saying, "Haven’t seen any spawn yet. They might not be in the breeding season, but I’m not knowledgeable about fish keeping. This needs to be asked of an expert to find out."

"Alright then, let’s leave it for now. Dragon Lady, if there’s anything you want to eat, feel free to plant it," Chen Xin didn’t dwell on the issue. He believed that the fish would eventually produce fry, delaying the time to eat them wasn’t a big deal.

After chatting with Mo Qingyan, Chen Xin planned to take a look at the greenhouse, so they walked towards the greenhouse together.

Inside the greenhouse, the six-layer hydroponic planting tiers looked impressive, while the fish in the pool below appeared very healthy. If it weren’t for the recent harvest, causing two tiers of the hydroponic planting to be vacant and looking somewhat absent, this place would resemble a futuristic planting factory.

After inspecting each area in the greenhouse and the growth condition of the various crops, what Chen Xin cared most about were the young chicks kept in a nearby breeding box.

These chicks had now grown, feathers starting to show, as proper chicks rather than hatchlings.

Originally, these chicks were cared for by Qin Lan, but due to Qin Lan following Chen Xin to the city, they were subsequently handed over to Mo Qingyan for care, and now they seemed to be growing well. A hen lay in the breeding box, in the chicken nest Qin Lan specially prepared with straw, and appeared to have eggs underneath.

Seeing this, Chen Xin enthusiastically opened the breeding box lid, intending to reach in to touch the eggs.

However, just as Chen Xin’s hand was about to reach inside, Mo Qingyan smacked his hand, saying, "Don’t touch! These eggs are specifically set aside for hatching chicks!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 327 - 225 Production Line Adjustment (Third Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin’s leisurely days at home did not last long. He stayed in his own shelter for just two or three days, merely managing to dry the vegetables needed for dehydration and hadn’t even had the chance to pack them away before the people from the research institution showed up at his door. They informed him that the production line for the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery had been installed and tested.

Upon hearing this news, Chen Xin didn’t dare to be negligent, so he quickly handled some affairs of his family’s shelter and took Han Ping back to the city.

Riding snowmobiles with the research institution staff as they sped back to the city, Chen Xin and Han Ping were adequately protected by their Exoskeleton Protective Suits. However, the staff who came to pick them up were visibly struggling with the cold.

This obviously made the staff very envious of the equipment Chen Xin was wearing, unlike them who could only wear as many layers as possible.

Of course, the clothes that these staff members wore were also extremely warm, including down jackets, windbreakers, sweaters, and thermal underwear.

For fieldwork staff like them, their clothing was already equivalent to that of the Antarctica expedition team, ensuring they would not suffer frostbite or get frozen due to inadequate clothing.

Even though the current temperature was more than fifty degrees below zero, just wearing one more thick sweater could still resist the cold.

But how could such clothing warmth compare to the Exoskeleton Protective Suits worn by Chen Xin and Han Ping? These suits were electrically heated and, compared to merely relying on body heat, were obviously more comfortable being windproof, warm, and electrically heated.

While envy remained just envy, the nation had begun to replicate Chen Xin’s Exoskeleton Protective Suit; however, imitation still required time. In the short term, it was almost impossible for these fieldwork staff to be issued such suits, and they had to wait a while longer to wear similar protective suits.

Chen Xin, along with Han Ping, entered a small shelter, which by now had been completely transformed. Upon entry, not only was there a rigorous safety check, but entering the production area also required wearing special radiation protective suits, with dust removal and sterilization processes to ensure cleanliness and safety within the production area.

"Mr. Chen Xin, you finally arrived. Everything is ready; we were just waiting for you to conduct the final adjustments," said a few of the research staff who had been waiting for a long time as they approached Chen Xin upon seeing him arrive.

"Has all the equipment been installed?" Chen Xin greeted these research staff and then inquired about the main business.

"Everything has been installed and tested, and it’s currently ready to run. However, since we are using experimental parameters provided by the lab, we have not yet conducted a trial run," the research staff, speaking with a serious expression, explained this matter to Chen Xin, with their faces unable to hide their complete worry.

This was not because they lacked confidence in Chen Xin, but because manufacturing matters could not be joked about or handled sloppily.

The nation put great effort into this project, even allocating massive resources to build the production line amidst blizzards, knowing from the start that the process parameters had issues and were ready to waste some materials to experiment and explore the correct process parameters. However, a tiny error in these process parameters could trigger a severe safety accident, and the research staff were clearly not confident enough to proceed directly.

Moreover, under the current circumstances, every resource is incredibly precious, and nobody wants to waste anything if they can avoid it.

"Don’t worry, now that I’m here, there will be no problem; adjusting the process parameters is relatively straightforward," Chen Xin assured them confidently, without the concern the research staff had.

Both sides didn’t waste any more words, and after a few more questions from Chen Xin to the research staff, he directly prepared to enter the production area.

Since Chen Xin needed to enter the production area, naturally he also had to change his protective suit.

According to regulations, Chen Xin and Han Ping removed the Exoskeleton and helmets and, with the help of staff, put on the special radiation protective suits, went through strict dust removal and sterilization procedures, and then entered the production area.

Inside the production area, all equipment had been installed, illuminated by a lighting system powered by an isotope generator, making the entire area as bright as daylight.

In this post-apocalyptic world, where everything is pitch-black and only artificial lights provide any illumination, the brightly lit scene was truly long-awaited.

Chen Xin couldn’t help but look up at the fluorescent tubes installed on the ceiling, feeling a sense of a world apart.

However, he quickly put these thoughts out of his mind and, accompanied by Han Ping and the research staff, began inspecting the production equipment.

Chen Xin’s inspection was different from the others; he merely opened the system upgrade interface to check the survival points required for the equipment upgrades.

Unlike previous upgrades, this time Chen Xin didn’t aim for a major transformation or qualitative leap in the equipment; he was only adjusting the process parameters, which strictly speaking, was still an upgrade, but it was a minor one, not involving significant modifications.

Accordingly, the survival points consumed were not much, and even without Chen Xin investing materials to reduce the consumption, the required survival points were not many.

Nevertheless, regardless of how few, Chen Xin wouldn’t waste survival points and, after taking a lap around the production area and examining all the production equipment, chose to upgrade them collectively as a whole, only optimizing the production process and technical parameters, as well as making minor adjustments and improvements to the equipment’s production efficiency, thereby achieving the purpose of adjusting process parameters and incidentally boosting production efficiency.

Chen Xin had to restrain himself from the notion of transforming the entire production line into a fully automated one, as such a transformation would consume resources and survival points far beyond the budget, and it would be contrary to the original plan.

After all, according to the plan, this production line was intended to adjust the process parameters to ensure the produced Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries could meet the expected performance, and then replicate the new production line elsewhere.

Therefore, Chen Xin couldn’t undertake large-scale upgrades and renovations to the production line but could only make small adjustments.

Fortunately, this wasn’t a difficult task, and simply controlling his thoughts could achieve the goal of adjusting and upgrading the scheme.

As for the materials needed for this upgrade, as long as Chen Xin asked, someone would naturally deliver them to him.

Once he had gathered the upgrade materials, Chen Xin didn’t delay and directly used the upgrade function on the entire production line.

Though it was a minor upgrade and materials were prepared, upgrading the whole production line still consumed two thousand of Chen Xin’s survival points.

Fortunately, the upgrade effect was absolutely satisfactory, and after seeing the green light sweep over, Chen Xin said to the research staff, "I have completed the adjustments; prepare for a trial run."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 328 - 226 Successful Production (First Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The so-called trial run refers to a period of test operation before the official run, aimed at identifying and addressing potential issues.

This is a common occurrence in production, as there are always discrepancies between theoretical design and actual operation; no one knows what problems might arise.

After Chen Xin announced the completion of adjustments to the production line, the isotope thermoelectric battery production line naturally needed to undergo a trial run.

There’s not much to say about the trial run itself; workers start up the equipment and operate according to actual procedures. As long as there are no issues and the equipment runs smoothly, the trial run naturally transitions into official operation.

The carefully selected and trained workers followed regulations to put on protective suits for cleaning and disinfection, and then entered the factory to their respective positions.

For these workers, working in such a radiation environment might be dangerous, but in this apocalypse, having a job that doesn’t require facing the storm outside and offers good benefits is extremely valuable. No one would be foolish enough to make mistakes and get fired or give up such a job.

A job where one person works to feed the entire family is extremely rare in this apocalyptic world.

It was mentioned earlier that the food in the shelter is graded, and one must work to eat their fill. Even the best-treated field staff don’t have many privileges; they just get two extra energy bars in their food, which they can eat themselves or share with family members.

However, the job on this production line comes with double the food ration, and even without the energy bars, this is enough to feed a family of three or four.

So, all workers who can work on this production line are motivated and determined to do their job well.

Standing in the control room, Chen Xin watched through the monitors as the workers entered their posts and then said to the expectant researchers beside him, "If you’re ready, start."

"Okay." The researcher responded, picking up the public address system on the console and shouting loudly to everyone on the production line, "Equipment trial run prep, all departments, attention! Prepare to start!"

"Power system is normal, equipment is being powered up!" A staff member at the control room console reported loudly.

"Circuit breaker opened, equipment powered up! Prepare to feed materials!" The commanding researcher quickly issued a new order.

Through the monitor, Chen Xin could see raw materials being fed into the production line’s intake as the command was issued.

Watching these scenes and feeling the serious and focused atmosphere from the staff around him, although the production line shouldn’t have any issues after the system upgrade, the atmosphere made Chen Xin feel both expectant and tense.

"Feeding materials ready!" A staff member reported.

"Equipment powered up normally, ready to start!" Another staff member checked the console, and after seeing all the indicator lights on, shouted loudly.

"Start the production line! All departments monitor closely!" The commanding researcher issued the start command while pressing the big red button on the control console to start the production line.

With the button pressed, all equipment on the production line began operating.

———————

At the end of the production line, Chen Xin and the researchers were tensely gathered here.

The first isotope thermoelectric battery produced by this line was about to leave the production line, and everyone was nervously and expectantly waiting for the completion of this battery.

The researchers were filled with anxiety, worrying whether this battery would meet the expected quality standards.

Although the trial run might produce a first batch that isn’t perfect, the researchers still held great expectations for this first isotope thermoelectric battery, hoping it could achieve the anticipated performance.

Soon, a silver-white battery was delivered to Chen Xin and the others via the conveyor belt of the production line, with a prominently green light bar on the side display screen indicating the battery’s current state was good.

Two researchers who had been waiting for a long time quickly reached out together to lift the battery and began testing it nearby.

"Radiation levels are normal." A researcher used a Geiger counter to carefully scan the battery, and the counter did not raise any alarms.

Due to the use of radioactive isotope material, the battery itself emits radiation. If the production process isn’t up to standard and sealing is inadequate, radiation leakage could occur, which would be extremely dangerous.

Now that the battery has passed the Geiger counter test, everyone felt more at ease, as at least the sealing quality of the battery was up to standard.

Next is to test the battery’s power output, which is what everyone present is most concerned about.

After all, the purpose of this creation is to obtain energy. If the product is similar to the 10KW output power battery from previous laboratory trials, it’s not unusable, but a 10KW battery compared to a 100KW battery will obviously serve entirely different purposes.

According to expectations, the production line’s batteries are required to achieve at least 120KW of output power, although still short of Chen Xin’s 160KW isotope thermoelectric battery, 120KW is sufficient for most use cases.

The researchers connected the battery to instruments for testing, and everyone’s eyes focused on the instruments, waiting for the measurement results.

"Output power 120! 120KW! Stable output!" The staff in charge of measurement looked at the increasing readings, getting excited and loudly reporting the data displayed on the instrument.

"We did it!" Many researchers jumped up excitedly and cheered loudly.

Although it’s not a major exciting project, after working tensely and busily for so long, building this production line amidst raging blizzards, now successfully producing batteries that meet expected quality standards is something worth celebrating for everyone.

"Quickly record the process data and send it to the laboratory!" While everyone cheered, the leading researcher hadn’t forgotten important tasks and instructed several other researchers to record the process parameters.

Watching them continue working joyfully after their success, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel an indescribable sense of achievement.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 329 - 227 Celebration Wine (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The successful production of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery is not just good news for Chen Xin and the researchers, it is also a significant event for this city, the entire Xiang Province, and even the whole of Flame Country.

This means that in this era, Flame Country is the first to obtain the key to breaking free from old energy constraints.

To put it more bluntly, Flame Country does not need to burn coal but can rely on nuclear power to meet the energy needs in this extreme cold apocalypse.

In the field of nuclear power, the most widely used radioactive element is uranium, and the energy produced by the complete fission of one kilogram of naturally occurring uranium-235 isotopes is equivalent to the heat released by burning 2,700 tons of standard coal.

Before the disaster, Flame Country was one of the top ten uranium-producing countries in the world, with an annual output exceeding 1,600 tons.

Uranium ore is also an element widely found in nature, with a higher content than gold and silver. Mastering the use of uranium means there is no need to worry about energy problems at all.

For Flame Country, having the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery means that they can fully issue two batteries to one shelter, allowing the shelter not to worry about energy issues for the next ten years.

Before the isotopes in the battery decay completely, the battery will provide sufficient power for the shelter.

Freeing all shelters nationwide from coal dependency means the country can release a large number of human and material resources used to maintain coal transport.

Rail transport will no longer have to devote most of its capacity to maintaining coal supplies and can transport more production materials.

The mining department does not have to directly burn the extracted coal but can use it in coal-to-oil, coal gasification, and other coal chemical industries to produce more industrial raw materials, accelerating the recovery of industrial production.

It can be said that if there is no longer a need to provide coal for heating and power generation to shelters nationwide, Flame Country’s post-disaster reconstruction speed could even double!

It is precisely because the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery is so significant that the successful operation of the production line brings such joy to these researchers.

To celebrate this event and to thank Chen Xin for his contribution to the construction and operation of this production line, the head of the researchers specially invited Chen Xin for a meal to properly thank him.

Chen Xin could not refuse, and considering it was indeed something worth celebrating, he agreed.

Following the head into a lounge, the head took out a bottle of baijiu and two cans, and also prepared two self-heating meals. This was considered a lavish feast.

Looking at the items on the table, Chen Xin raised his eyebrows. Before the disaster, these items could only make a makeshift meal, but in this post-apocalyptic world, these are undoubtedly good things.

The two cans, one of luncheon meat and the other of canned fish, had no fancy advertising, just a simple black label with the name and production information, with the production date being two months ago.

These are not stocks from before the disaster but newly produced. Chen Xin realized this when he saw the production date on the can.

As for the self-heating food and baijiu, those are indeed pre-disaster stocks because neither can be produced now due to a lack of sufficient raw materials.

Now every grain is extremely precious. The main staple of the food that survivors eat in the shelters is from the nation’s reserve grain from before the disaster, with supplemental ingredients being mice and mushrooms raised in the shelter, along with flour grub made into protein powder.

Therefore, the country does not have extra grain for brewing, and neither does it have extra grain to raise livestock, so the baijiu and the self-heating food labeled as cumin lamb are obviously not post-disaster productions.

Though according to data known to Chen Xin, the country still retains biological samples in some areas, and they rear some pigs, cows, and sheep as meat livestock. But those who can enjoy natural meat are only the high-level authorities and some people with special privileges.

If Chen Xin wished, he could probably enjoy some special treatment, but it held little attraction for him.

While Chen Xin was examining these things, the head also found two cups, poured Chen Xin a cup of liquor, opened the cans on the table, and heated the self-heating foods with water.

"Here’s a toast to you. Without your help, Mr. Chen, we would never have been able to establish such a production line! Thank you for what you’ve done for the country!" The head raised his cup to clink glasses with Chen Xin and finished the liquor in his cup in one go.

Seeing the head’s excited expression, Chen Xin couldn’t help feeling a bit confused and surprised. In his impression, the head had always been a very serious figure, seldom smiling and rarely showing any expression.

However, after the alcohol settled, the head opened up to Chen Xin: "Honestly, I really envy those predecessors back in the day. Although the conditions were harsh, people at that time had spirit. While material conditions were scarce, they were rich in spirit!

They battled against heaven and earth, building the nation we have today with an indomitable spirit and enthusiasm. We juniors are envious! The reason I chose this job back then was to contribute to the country like those predecessors did!"

"You shouldn’t say things like that. Aren’t we contributing to the country just the same?" Chen Xin could understand the head’s thoughts. Although as material life became richer, many people began to seek pleasure, there are still people like us dedicating their youth and passion to the country.

"I can’t compare to those predecessors, having idled away most of my life, only doing a little insignificant work for the country." The head’s eyes were slightly wet, and he was noticeably agitated: "Speaking of which, I really must thank you, Mr. Chen, thank you for giving me this opportunity to contribute to the country! This has truly been the opportunity I have hoped for half my life! Thank you!"

With that, the head shook Chen Xin’s hand firmly with both hands.

Through the power of the handshake, Chen Xin could clearly feel the head’s sincerity.

"You’re too kind. I’ve only done what I could, it’s not worth such praise." Chen Xin said, a bit embarrassed by the head’s praise, humbly.

"Haha, Mr. Chen, you are too modest! Perhaps it’s precisely people like you who can better contribute to the country? I’ve been too vain, always wanting to make something big, never being satisfied with the small things I do..." The head shook his head, sighed, raised his glass again: "Let’s not speak of it, anyway, thank you, thank you for all you’ve done!"

With that said, the two glasses clinked together.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 330 - 228 Food Contingency Plan (Three Updates)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A bottle of not-so-good baijiu, yet Chen Xin and the person in charge drank happily.

On one hand, today’s events were indeed worth celebrating, and on the other hand, times have changed. Even if it’s not premium liquor, it’s still liquor.

Having something to drink is already good enough; neither Chen Xin nor the person in charge had any notions of being picky.

There was both liquor and food, and with two men gathered together, once they drank to the point of warmth and excitement, they naturally started to chat.

However, Chen Xin and this person in charge didn’t talk about aimless topics; what they discussed was still related to the current situation and national livelihood issues.

"Speaking of which, the whole world is frozen now, where will the food come from? We can’t just rely on growing mushrooms and raising mice in each shelter, right? That’s not enough to eat?" This question was something Chen Xin was always very concerned and worried about.

The person in charge was, of course, aware of this matter. He took a sip of his drink and then explained to Chen Xin, "The country had considered this issue before the disaster, so in the three months before the disaster, the country had already been constructing large-scale food cultivation greenhouses and breeding zones in the mountainous areas of Lingnan and the Qiong Province to ensure food supply after the disaster."

"So the country had early plans, it seems I was worrying over nothing." Hearing this, Chen Xin felt as if a weight had been lifted from his heart.

Although he himself didn’t worry about food or drink, if a nationwide food crisis occurred, it would still greatly impact him.

"Plans were made, but these measures can’t guarantee feeding everyone in the country." The person in charge shook his head at Chen Xin’s words, denying, "How many survivors are there nationwide? It’s impossible to fill so many mouths just with Lingnan and Qiong Province.

According to previous statistical data, the output from Lingnan and Qiong Province only meets a third of the nation’s food consumption. Each shelter growing mushrooms and raising mice could fill about one-fifth of the gap, as for the rest... you calculate how much is still lacking."

The person in charge didn’t finish his words, just shook his head helplessly.

Hearing this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but be shocked. He hadn’t imagined there would be such a big food gap nationwide.

Even though he had guessed there might be problems with food before, Chen Xin thought it would just be a bit tight, not directly missing two-thirds.

This is not a small number. If the issue isn’t resolved, it means that at least half the nation has to starve to death!

"Is there any country response measure?" Chen Xin asked concernedly.

If the issue is this serious, then the country must have countermeasures; it’s impossible for them to really watch more than half of the nation starve to death.

"Measures do exist, the country certainly can’t stand idly by as so many people starve." The person in charge nodded and, addressing this issue, seriously toasted to Chen Xin again before continuing, "We owe you a thanks, Mr. Chen. The emergence of the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery has solved many problems! With ample energy, it’s much easier to construct new cultivation greenhouses or maintain existing ones!"

When saying this, the person in charge seemed delighted and refilled both his and Chen Xin’s drink before continuing, "Actually, the country initially had plans, but in the three months before the disaster, the nation’s main focus was on meteorite defense, so it was hard to carry out these plans on a large scale. Back then, we were all prepared just in case, after all, if the meteorite defense succeeded, there wouldn’t have been so many issues.

Therefore, the nation’s original plan was to use reserve food stocks to tide over a period right after the meteorite hit, and then in that period, large-scale cultivation greenhouses were supposed to be constructed in the Sichuan Basin and Qinghai-Tibet Plateau to make up for the food production deficit.

However, due to energy issues, the implementation of this plan was not going smoothly, as most of the railway’s capacity had to be dedicated to maintaining coal supplies nationwide. Coupled with the difficulty of material transport to the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, only the Sichuan Basin parts were reasonably proceeding as planned."

Listening to this, Chen Xin understood much more clearly. According to the nation’s original plan, it was evidently divided into two parts: Before the disaster, by constructing large-scale cultivation and breeding areas in the tropical areas like Lingnan and Qiong Province, taking advantage of the tropical temperatures to maintain agricultural production post-disaster.

After the meteorite impact, they would establish a second large-scale cultivation and breeding area in the Sichuan Basin and Qinghai-Tibet Plateau regions, which were shielded by mountains and less severely affected, to make up for the shortfall from Lingnan and Qiong Province.

As for each shelter growing mushrooms, raising mice, and mealworms, that’s the third-phase plan intended to compensate for the deficiencies of the first two phases.

If everything proceeded according to the country’s expectations, food issues might arise, but clearly wouldn’t be too severe, but now from what the person in charge said, the plan is evidently not progressing smoothly.

"Does this mean food will still be tight?" Chen Xin thought, more firmly developing his idea of initiating large-scale cultivation greenhouses, "If that’s the case, I have an idea to create cultivation greenhouses, which should also help address some of the national food shortages, right?"

The person in charge initially wanted to say that with the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery provided by Chen Xin, the nation’s plans could progress smoothly without significant food issues, but hearing Chen Xin also has greenhouse plans, he paused and instead asked him, "Mr. Chen, how do you plan to start?"

"Currently, it’s just a concept. I plan to drill wells to bring deep geothermal energy to the surface to create a warm zone, and then build a greenhouse to retain heat, creating an environment for growing food crops." Chen Xin briefly elaborated on his idea without going into details on how to execute it.

Even with a brief outline, the person in charge understood clearly and, after a slight contemplation, nodded, finding the concept feasible.

But although it’s feasible, it doesn’t mean it can necessarily be realized.

"But how can you bring deep geothermal energy to the surface? The country indeed has technology related to geothermal energy, and because of the apocalyptic environment, it’s been heavily researching related technology, but effectively utilizing deep geothermal energy is not easy!" The person in charge frowned, despite Chen Xin’s consistent exceptional performance, he didn’t find this problem easy to solve.

"Drill deep wells underground and use air for heat exchange to transfer the heat to the surface." Chen Xin provided his plan but also candidly told the person in charge, "But these are just my ideas, I haven’t figured out exactly how to achieve it yet."

However, while Chen Xin was humble, the person in charge’s eyes lit up, "Are you talking about Hot Dry Rock technology?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 331 - 229 Deep Geothermal (Part 1)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Deep geothermal energy is an emerging energy technology that began being researched in the Federation around the 1970s.

Its main principle is to guide the heat contained in dense and impermeable high-temperature rock located 2000~3000 meters underground to the surface using methods such as drilling and injecting water, and then apply it in areas such as heating and power generation.

This technology is favored for its low cost, cleanliness, and recyclability, and has quickly been promoted and applied in many countries rich in geothermal resources. However, due to the slow development at the technical level, a relatively mature utilization system for this technology wasn’t formed until this century.

"The utilization of deep geothermal energy started late in our country and only recently formed a considerable scale after the technology matured." The person in charge seemed to know a lot about this area and talked non-stop to Chen Xin, "A few years ago, a national new district in Chang’an adopted this kind of deep geothermal well for heating, which is considered a successful case of utilizing deep geothermal resources in our country. Later, a high-temperature dry hot rock was discovered in Qinghai, and we are at the forefront of the world in this area."

"Our country only started using deep geothermal energy in recent years? But I remember when I was in elementary school, the geothermal power station in Yangbajing, Tibet Province, should have already been established, right? Doesn’t that count as deep geothermal resource development?" Chen Xin was a bit puzzled; in his memory, Yangbajing was already quite famous when he was in elementary school.

Faced with this question, the person in charge shook his head with a smile and then explained, "Yangbajing in Tibet Province belongs to medium-temperature shallow geothermal energy. The geothermal resources there are basically ready-made and do not require deep drilling to utilize. However, it is true that geothermal power generation in Yangbajing started quite early and achieved megawatt-level geothermal power generation in the 1990s, making it a famous case of geothermal development."
