"I see, shallow geothermal and deep geothermal are indeed different things." Chen Xin nodded knowingly, with a sense of sudden realization, but he continued to ask, "The high-temperature dry hot rock discovered in Qinghai, is it really valuable? Is the country mature with technology in this area?"

Chen Xin was very concerned about this issue; if the country has mature technology, his idea of building an Energy Tower to construct greenhouses or even regional heating facilities would be easier to achieve.

"Haha, you’ve hit the nail on the head with this question!" The person in charge seemed tickled by the question, became excited, adjusted his sitting posture, took a swig of his drink, and was evidently planning to discuss it with Chen Xin at length.

Seeing this, Chen Xin also adopted a serious listening posture, wanting to understand the knowledge in this area better.

"For many years, our country has been the first in the world in terms of total geothermal energy utilization, but it has mainly focused on the exploitation of shallow medium and low-temperature geothermal energy. As for deep geothermal resources, on one hand, we lack the necessary technology, and on the other hand, there’s insufficient resource exploration. It wasn’t until recently that the country made significant breakthroughs in this area." Talking about this, the person in charge seemed a bit wistful and sighed, "Geothermal resources are wonderful! Their application cost is much lower than solar and wind energy, and they are clean, environmentally friendly, with enormous reserves!

Especially in our country, the continental dry hot rock resource at a depth of 3~10 kilometers is approximately equivalent to 856 trillion tons of standard coal, accounting for about one-sixth of the world’s resources. Unfortunately, our country did not research and develop this kind of energy in the past, and it was not until the discovery of dry hot rock resources on the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau that we filled the gap in not finding any previously."

"Given that, why didn’t the state consider large-scale mining of dry hot rocks to use deep geothermal for building shelters to avoid this crisis?" Chen Xin found it strange; according to the person in charge, the exploitation of dry hot rock resources is easy, with low application costs. Logically, the state should exploit it on a large scale, so why is coal still being burned now?

Regarding this issue, the person in charge wore a wry smile, "Do you think the state doesn’t want to? The southern Qinghai-Tibet Plateau accounts for about one-fifth of the total dry hot rock resources on our mainland. The state’s plan for establishing planting and breeding areas on the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau is to use geothermal energy for heating and power generation, but the harsh construction environment on the plateau severely slows this plan.

Moreover, our dry hot rock resources are primarily distributed in regions such as the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, Songliao Basin, Bohai Bay Basin, and the southeast coast. Apart from the Qinghai-Tibet Plateau, the Songliao Basin, Bohai Bay Basin, and the southeast coast are all frozen by the rising sea level. Under such circumstances, large-scale development and application of deep geothermal resources are extremely challenging."

Upon hearing this, Chen Xin nodded knowingly. The seawater had already reached Dongting Lake, and the Shandong Peninsula had become Shandong Island. In such a situation, developing geothermal resources in coastal areas was obviously impractical.

However, despite many difficulties, the prospects for utilizing deep geothermal energy remain broad.

After thinking about it seriously, Chen Xin started to speak, "Since the country has this technology, it should be no problem for me to use geothermal resources to construct a planting greenhouse, right?"

"That’s really hard to say." The person in charge shook his head, refilled Chen Xin’s glass, and then continued, "The current deep geothermal technology applied by the country is mainly for developing dry hot rocks, which are non-permeable rock layers without fluids or with minimal fluids.

The specific method is to inject water into deep wells to create fissures, forming a planar artificial dry hot rock heat storage structure, and then recover high-temperature water or steam to extract heat. This technology has requirements for geological structures, and in places like Xiang Province, where underground water resources are too abundant, extracting deep geothermal energy requires technical adjustments, which poses many problems!"

"I’ll handle the technical issues. As long as we can set up the framework, I have ways to make it practical." For this issue, Chen Xin did not see it as a problem.

With the system as a backup, extracting deep geothermal energy wasn’t a difficult task.

"I almost forgot Mr. Chen, your expertise is in technological improvement!" The person in charge laughed, tapped his head, and then made a promise, "Since Mr. Chen has such an idea, I am naturally willing to give full support. I will report this to higher authorities and strive to create a pilot project here. When the time comes, Mr. Chen, we will need you to improve the technology.

If this succeeds, the development technology for deep geothermal resources can be widely promoted. This is a great benefit for the country and its people!"

"Then I’ll be looking forward to that!" Chen Xin raised his glass and clinked it with the person in charge again.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 332 - 230: "You’ll take care of me from now on" (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"You’re awake? Have some water."

Chen Xin opened his eyes and saw a cup of hot water being handed to him, held by a red mechanical prosthetic limb.

"Qin Lan? What brings you here?" Chen Xin sat up from the sofa, rubbed his forehead to clear his mind, took the water cup, and took a sip to wash away the sour taste in his mouth, and asked Qin Lan, who had appeared without him noticing.

He also remembered drinking too much with the supervisor earlier and falling asleep directly on the sofa in the lounge.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin glanced at the table next to him, only to see that the previous mess of cups and plates had been cleaned up.

"I originally came to find you, but they said you were drunk, so I stayed here to watch over you," Qin Lan explained her presence there, took the empty cup from Chen Xin’s hand, and asked, "Want some more?"

"Yeah, a bit thirsty," Chen Xin nodded, indicating Qin Lan to pour him another cup of water.

Qin Lan nodded, got up, and went to the kettle to pour water. As she poured, she curiously asked, "Haven’t seen you get drunk in a while. What’s up, in a good mood?"

Receiving the cup Qin Lan handed over and drinking it in one gulp again, Chen Xin finally nodded, "It was okay, mainly just got caught up in the conversation and ended up drinking a bit more than usual."

Hearing Chen Xin say this, Qin Lan sat back beside him, shook her head slightly disapprovingly, "You should drink less, it’s not good for your health. Remember that next time."

"Alright, I get it," Chen Xin agreed, shook off the sleepiness, and stood up from the sofa.

Smoothing out the creases in his clothes, Chen Xin then asked Qin Lan, "By the way, where’s Han Ping? Didn’t you see him when you came?"

"He was keeping watch outside while you slept. I sent him to rest when I got here," Qin Lan stood up too, helped Chen Xin straighten his collar, but smelling the alcohol on him, she said with some dislike, "You have a strong smell on you, go take a shower and change into some clean clothes."

Hearing Qin Lan’s words, Chen Xin’s expression turned a bit strange.

He glanced at Qin Lan, didn’t say much, just nodded and walked towards the door.

Watching Chen Xin leave the room, Qin Lan then realized her words just now were a bit too intimate with Chen Xin.

Realizing this, Qin Lan blushed slightly, but quickly calmed her emotions.

For her, if she really wanted to get back together with Chen Xin, she certainly wouldn’t hesitate.

————————————————

Chen Xin, after leaving the room, found a staff member to help him get a set of clean clothes, and then headed to the bathroom.

This place was originally a shelter, naturally equipped with complete bathroom facilities, which were preserved during previous renovations, even upgraded.

According to management regulations, since this place produces radioactive products, necessary radiation decontamination measures are required, which demands certain standards for bathroom facilities, as not all can accomplish radiation decontamination.

Arriving at the bathroom, Chen Xin took off his alcohol-scented dirty clothes, took the toiletries he had just received from the staff, and prepared to shower in a stall.

These toiletries were all new, even the soap was unopened, which satisfied Chen Xin, who had a slight obsessive-compulsive cleanliness.

Even though in a post-apocalyptic world, conditions are limited, Chen Xin could technically use things others had used, but if possible, he still preferred not to.

Turning on the faucet, scalding hot water poured out, and he stood under the stream, washing away not only the alcohol smell but also a heavy burden on him.

Chen Xin showered quickly, as the current environment didn’t allow for long showers.

Of course, if he was at home, he’d likely soak in the bath after showering, usually not getting out for at least half an hour.

Once cleaned and in fresh clothes, Chen Xin left the bathroom feeling refreshed.

As for the dirty clothes, he naturally packed them into a bag, planning to wash them later.

It was clear that Chen Xin’s clothes were a set of work uniforms provided here, which were one of the workers’ benefits, including a full set of change clothes.

It’s not that the nation had excess resources to waste, even though chemical industries had resumed production, providing everyone with clothing made from synthetic fibers. The production capacity couldn’t be wasted lightly.

The main reason for providing a full set of clothes was the need for standardized attire when working in production areas.

Don’t think this is just bureaucracy; standardized attire significantly improves production safety. Personal clothing in a factory could be caught in machinery, causing accidents from minor interruptions to fatal injuries.

Especially since what they were producing here were Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries with high radiation, any safety hazards had to be eliminated.

Wearing a set of work clothes, Chen Xin returned to the lounge, where Qin Lan was sitting on the sofa with her legs crossed.

That’s when Chen Xin noticed Qin Lan wasn’t wearing the usual police uniform but had changed into private clothing.

Dark jeans and a light knit top highlighted Qin Lan’s beautiful curves, and black boots wrapped her calves, making them look even slimmer and longer.

For Chen Xin, he hadn’t seen Qin Lan in civilian clothes for a long time, as he remembered her always in uniform, even wearing combat gear on dates with the patches and rank removed.

"Never seen you in this outfit before?" Chen Xin sat down beside Qin Lan, directly took her right hand, looking at the Power Glove-equipped mechanical prosthetic, and started checking his creation, "By the way, are you used to this prosthetic? Anything you need me to adjust?"

"I wore uniforms daily for work! Even on dates with you, I was always cautious about being called back for missions, so I had to wear combat gear. Now that I’ve resigned from the team, I can wear whatever," Qin Lan said nonchalantly, letting Chen Xin inspect her right hand.

Hearing her say this, Chen Xin paused and then casually asked, "Really resigned?"

"I did, from now on, you take care of me." Qin Lan leaned on Chen Xin’s shoulder.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 333 - 231: Important Materials (First Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

On the platform of the train station in a certain city, a group of people was anxiously waiting.

"The train isn’t delayed, right?" A leader among the group, who seemed accustomed to a high position, asked his secretary, though he knew there shouldn’t be an issue, he still felt a bit uneasy.

Seeing the leader’s inquiry, the secretary hurriedly replied, "Don’t worry, Mayor, the train coming this time is a newly operational nuclear-powered train by the state, it won’t be late. It’s almost time, it should be arriving soon."

Upon hearing this, the Mayor did not feel much more at ease. He knew these circumstances as well, asking the secretary was merely for psychological comfort.

Waiting long for the train’s arrival left both the Mayor and the others around increasingly agitated.

Since the disaster struck, city governments everywhere have been under immense pressure, facing survival issues for populations ranging from hundreds of thousands to over a million, keeping everyone on edge, allowing no room for error or negligence.

To use an idiom, it feels like walking on thin ice, fearing any small mistake.

But the previous blizzard made everyone realize the old saying about misfortunes never coming singly. One blizzard severely damaged overstressed shelters.

Not every city was fortunate enough to have someone capable of improving boilers and increasing heating efficiency.

During the previous blizzard, over ten percent of this city’s population froze to death directly, nearly thirty percent of the survivors faced varying degrees of amputation threats, and more than half required medical treatment.

The influx of so many patients caused the medical system to collapse, with many medical staff themselves suffering from cold-induced ailments, needing treatment.

The food and coal shortages exacerbated the city’s predicament, and if it weren’t for comrades on the railway front risking their lives to deliver supplies through the storm, the city might have already fallen.

With the snowstorm now subsiding slightly, reconstruction work is multifaceted. Though in such a situation, those still on duty are commendable, knowing what to do, coordination among various departments still requires oversight.

As Mayor, he should be in his office handling these matters, but the arrival of this particular shipment of supplies is exceptional, as the state mandated the Mayor himself must personally receive it, forcing the Mayor to put aside his work and come to the train station to receive these crucial yet unknown supplies.

Thus, the Mayor’s impatience was understandable.

Fortunately, the train was not late; just as the Mayor became increasingly impatient, frequently checking his watch, a train finally broke through the darkness, slowly approaching from a distance.

Seeing the train appear, everyone on the platform breathed a sigh of relief—it had finally arrived.

The train entered the station and came to a steady stop on the platform.

Seeing the train stop, the Mayor quickly adjusted his emotions and expression, walking towards the train door to see exactly what supplies were so significant that the Mayor himself had to sign for them.

However, the heavily armored train in front of him, with its sharp and heavy armor plating and the bright heavy machine guns and turrets on top of the carriages, made the Mayor realized the supplies were indeed very crucial.

Otherwise, they wouldn’t have deployed such a fully armed armored train, rumored to be nuclear-powered.

The train car door opened, and a staff member, holding documents, accompanied by several armed soldiers, stepped down, meeting the approaching Mayor.

Upon seeing these individuals disembark, the Mayor couldn’t help but raise an eyebrow. It wasn’t that he lacked composure, but the attire of the people before him was somewhat startling.

The staff member holding the documents was fine, simply clad in a silver-gray one-piece Protective Suit with a helmet on.

But the soldiers behind him were dressed entirely differently, donning gray-white snow camouflage jumpsuits with tactical-style helmets, adorned with an array of gear familiar and unfamiliar to the Mayor.

Yet this was not what surprised the Mayor the most; if it were just this, he would merely assume the state had made new breakthroughs in protective gear.

However, these soldiers were also equipped with snow-camouflaged Exoskeletons with defensive armor protecting vital body areas, and two of them carried tri-barrel heavy machine guns on their shoulders.

"What kind of important supplies require such full armament?" the Mayor murmured, quickly dismissing the thought as he directly addressed the staff member walking over, "Comrade, thank you for your hard work. May I ask what supplies require my personal receipt?"

The staff member didn’t engage in pleasantries with the Mayor. After carefully verifying his identity and ensuring no discrepancies, he explained, "It’s a Nuclear Battery. According to state directives, from the moment these supplies are received, all shelters must convert from existing coal heating and power supply to using Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries for power and electric heating."

"What? Battery? This is nonsense!" Upon hearing this, the Mayor’s eyebrows shot up instantly, the first thought flashing through his mind was that it was absurd.

In the Mayor’s understanding, batteries were for emergencies or small appliances, toys, and remote controls, even car batteries were just larger, nothing fundamentally different.

But now someone was saying batteries were to be used to power and heat shelters, no wonder he thought it was nonsense.

"These aren’t ordinary batteries, they are Nuclear Batteries," the staff member responded without any reaction, diligently performing the handover: "This batch of batteries each has an output power of 120KW, with a sustainable lifespan of ten years. According to our calculations, a small shelter for five hundred people only needs one battery to ensure power and electric heating.

Based on the number of shelters in your city, there are 230 batteries that you need to receive, in addition to two thousand Protective Suits and five hundred sets of Exoskeleton equipment. Here’s the list, please confirm and sign, and affix your city government’s seal upon verification."

The Mayor, upon hearing the staff’s explanation, was immediately taken aback, feeling slightly embarrassed, but years of experience quickly steadied his emotions. He took the list offered by the other party, checked over the items listed, and pointing to a line of text, asked, "What are these Protective Suits and Exoskeletons?"

The staff member, seeing the Mayor’s inquiry, pointed to the soldiers wearing Exoskeleton Protective Suits beside him and said, "These are the Exoskeleton Protective Suits."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 334 - 232 Safe Production (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the factory, a new batch of Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries are being packaged and boxed.

Each battery is neatly packed into specially designed fixed boxes, labeled and marked as qualified by the staff, and then immediately taken away, without the procedure of entering inventory storage.

Currently, all national resources are planned and coordinated by the state, and the Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries produced here are in a state of short supply.

A batch of raw materials is transported by the state, and before production starts, the finished products are already reserved for delivery.

The entire factory is now operating in three shifts, with people resting but not the machines, producing at full capacity 24 hours a day, and can produce about a hundred Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries a day.

The train responsible for transporting materials and taking away finished products runs every three days, which is basically just enough to meet the needs of a small-to-medium-sized city.

As for large cities, it is a bit more troublesome, requiring the train to run two or three times.

However, even so, as batches of batteries are taken away, more and more cities are freed from their dependence on coal.

Due to geographical factors, the first batch of cities to use battery-powered electricity is mainly located in Gan Province.

Although this province is surrounded by economically developed provinces, Gan Province itself has always lagged economically, and with its mountainous terrain, the situation wasn’t favorable after disaster struck.

Thus, during the previous snowstorm, Gan Province’s disaster situation was especially severe compared to surrounding provinces and cities.

The first batch of Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries transported to Gan Province was truly a case of sending charcoal in snowy weather for them.

During the days the factory has been operational, Chen Xin has been basically living here, because everyone is "a maiden riding in a flower sedan — the first time", even though Chen Xin himself has absolute confidence in the production line provided by the system, he couldn’t help but keep a close watch to prevent issues from workers being unfamiliar with operations or other reasons.

It should be known that after the factory began full-load operations, a large amount of uranium isotopes are transported daily as raw materials, and this stuff has a name everyone is familiar with: uranium-235, the material for nuclear weapons.

Although the uranium isotopes were processed into fuel before being transported here, and the concentration didn’t reach weapon-grade levels, only general nuclear power plant fuel concentration, even so, if a safety accident occurred resulting in nuclear radiation leakage or other problems, it still wouldn’t be a small matter.

After all, it’s something related to nuclear, and even knowing it’s safe, the heart can’t help but feel uneasy when handling it, prompting extra caution.

In fact, during this period, there have been issues.

Although workers were trained strictly before starting their jobs, during actual production, some problems, whether large or small, still occurred.

Some issues weren’t severe, just workers not properly executing safety production regulations, and emphasizing this basically resolved them.

But some problems involved negligence, not only operating improperly but directly handling isotope fuel, nearly causing a safety incident, which angered both Chen Xin and the responsible scientific institutions, leading to the immediate dismissal of the worker responsible for the problem.

It’s not that they’re being unkind, rather strict management is essential.

Moreover, due to these issues occurring, both Chen Xin and other scientific personnel have been on high alert, fearing any recurrence of problems.

This has led to Chen Xin not sleeping well during this period, basically only sleeping a few hours each day before being woken up for various reasons, leaving him to squeeze in naps whenever he finished urgent tasks.

Due to irregular sleep patterns, visible dark circles appeared under Chen Xin’s eyes, and the overall impression was one of much weariness.

This undoubtedly brought some heartache to Qin Lan, who had been accompanying Chen Xin recently.

Having resigned, she is now unburdened, and since expressing certain sentiments that day, she and Chen Xin’s relationship has become closer, staying by his side these days, completely taking over Han Ping’s bodyguard duties.

Furthermore, due to her more intimate relationship with Chen Xin, and being a woman, Qin Lan not only fulfills the role of a bodyguard but also significantly cares for his daily life.

Though Qin Lan isn’t very skilled at cooking, nor is she inherently gentle and caring, it still greatly improved Chen Xin’s quality of life.

Regarding their relationship change, Chen Xin himself actually quite enjoys it; having someone who knows your needs and can serve tea and water is a novel, yet enjoyable experience for Chen Xin.

Chen Xin could see that Qin Lan was learning many things for the first time, having previously held a gun more than anything, things she previously wouldn’t have done.

"Alright, I can do it myself, I haven’t gotten to the point where I need help washing my face." Taking the towel Qin Lan handed over, Chen Xin washed his face and wrung out the towel, wiping the water off his face.

Standing beside Chen Xin, Qin Lan watched him wash his face, feeling somewhat dazed.

Previously, while dating Chen Xin, she never served him like this, often getting called away during their dates and leaving Chen Xin’s romantic or mood-setting plans dashed.

During such times, Chen Xin always said it was fine and helped Qin Lan gather her things for an immediate departure, rather than relying on Qin Lan to do things for him.

In many ways, this was one major reason for their breakup, these repeated instances slowly accumulating tension. Even with Chen Xin’s good nature, he couldn’t help feeling frustrated.

So, during a discussion about marriage, Qin Lan again left in the middle, causing Chen Xin to finally explode.

After a big argument, their breakup became inevitable.

"If I hadn’t been so focused on work back then and cared more about you, wouldn’t we have been together long ago?" Qin Lan poured out the water from the basin, hung up the towel, and asked Chen Xin, who was shaving.

Hearing Qin Lan’s words, Chen Xin glanced at her in surprise but shook his head: "Hypothesizing about those things is meaningless, with your personality, even if you cared more for me, a phone call would still have made you go on a mission; it wouldn’t have been any different."

Chen Xin’s words made Qin Lan feel a bit embarrassed, but she understood it was indeed as Chen Xin described.

Thinking about it, she wanted to compensate Chen Xin somewhat, so she walked over to him, took the razor from his hand, pressed him onto the couch, and straddled him, saying, "Sit down, I’ll help you shave."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 335 - 233: Lin City’s Plea for Help (End-of-month request for monthly votes!)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Mr. Chen, these are the process parameters we’ve organized. Do you see any areas that need adjustment?" In the office of the factory, the person in charge placed a meticulously reorganized set of process parameters in front of Chen Xin.

Although the factory is already operating at full capacity, producing one hundred isotope thermoelectric batteries daily, which is enough to meet the electricity needs of a city in three days, the nation can’t rely solely on this production line and this factory.

Before the disaster, excluding the special two coasts and three regions, Flame Country had a total of 663 cities, including 4 municipalities, 293 prefecture-level cities, and 366 county-level cities.

Even if each city only needs three hundred batteries to achieve full power supply, which is the factory’s production for three days, producing batteries to meet the needs of 663 cities would still take a full five and a half years.

And there are still counties, towns, and villages without city status. Wanting to replace the original coal-powered system nationwide with isotope thermoelectric batteries is undoubtedly a grand project.

Though the nation has not planned to replace all power supply methods with isotope thermoelectric batteries, it’s obviously impossible to rely on just this production line to meet national needs.

Building new production lines is imperative.

Chen Xin took the specially organized set of process parameters and seriously went through it.

Although not entirely knowledgeable about it, having spent so many days at the factory, he had read and memorized the related parameters many times.

After verifying them and finding no issues, Chen Xin nodded and handed the process parameters back to the person in charge: "These match the parameters we’re currently using, so I don’t think any changes are needed. Please send them to the higher-ups. The sooner a new production line is built, the more people we can help."

"Okay." The person in charge stored the process parameters and then asked Chen Xin: "Mr. Chen, now that the factory’s production is stable, do you have any plans next?"

"I should be busy with the geothermal greenhouse project I mentioned to you before. The food issue is still quite important." Chen Xin thought for a moment and decided that this should be his next priority: "The previous cooperative projects should also resume, right?"

Previously affected by the blizzard, the cooperation between Chen Xin and the research institute was also interrupted. Now that the storm has subsided, the cooperation naturally needs to continue.

"Of course, we will handle the cooperation matters. The state will send someone to take over the factory operations." The person in charge nodded.

It’s only logical. Currently, the factory’s production supervision and management tasks are in the hands of the previous research personnel, but they can’t handle production and management perpetually. Eventually, it will be handed over to professional administrative management personnel.

Chen Xin understood this well and didn’t say much. He just intended to pack up his things and leave to go home.

However, just as Chen Xin was preparing to say goodbye to the person in charge and pack up, Qin Lan suddenly walked in with Mo Qingyan from outside.

"Dragon Lady? What brings you here?" Chen Xin was surprised to see Mo Qingyan, but he couldn’t suppress a surge of concern: "Did something happen at home?"

Mo Qingyan had always stayed in her shelter, so why did she suddenly come here? Could it be that something happened at home?

Just as Chen Xin was worried, Mo Qingyan stood in front of him and said, "Everything’s fine at home. Han Ze received a distress message from Lin City. Uncle and Auntie asked me to find you."

"From Lin City?" Hearing this, Chen Xin raised an eyebrow: "What’s going on?"

Seeing Chen Xin’s inquiry, Mo Qingyan quickly explained.

Lin City is a county-level city under the jurisdiction of this city, and in terms of area, it’s significantly larger than this city’s downtown, with a population just slightly less than the city center.

Although it’s a county-level city, it’s not much different from some smaller prefecture-level cities.

Geographically, Lin City is located in the northeast of this city, precisely acting as the transportation hub from Hubei Province into Xiang Province.

However, despite Lin City’s advantageous geographical position, since it’s only a subordinate administrative unit of this city and the cities are close together, Lin City only has a small train station where most trains do not stop, failing to leverage its geographical advantage fully.

Before the disaster, this might only have marginally impacted Lin City’s economic development. After all, whether it’s a high-speed rail station or an airport, both are built in this city, not Lin City, so Lin City’s residents might have to detour to this city for traveling.

But once the disaster struck, it severely impacted Lin City.

The small train station struggled to handle the city’s needed material loading and unloading, often requiring unloading materials directly outside the station, on the trains, without using the platform.

Before the blizzard, this might only have posed a minor inconvenience for material unloading in Lin City, and they were actively expanding the station platform to facilitate unloading.

But with the arrival of the blizzard, Lin City’s small train station, along with its platform, was buried under snow.

This undoubtedly brought disastrous consequences to Lin City.

During the blizzard, unloading materials in the minus seventy-degree cold and under twelve-level gales was a literal fight for survival.

Every material unloading operation risked lives.

It could be said that the impact of the blizzard on Lin City was severe.

Although Lin City actively organized production and self-help after the disaster, the ravages of the blizzard were too overwhelming. Despite the efforts of Lin City’s government, there was an overwhelming sense of helplessness.

Without external assistance, Lin City could hardly hold on much longer.

If even the official forces were in such a state, one can only imagine the condition of private shelters.

So it’s not surprising Han Ze received a distress signal from Lin City, even though it came from a friend from the survival group Chen Xin belonged to, who lived in Lin City.

"You mean the ’Well-digging Expert’ from the group sent a distress signal, saying Lin City’s situation is dire and hoping we can help them?" Chen Xin summarized Mo Qingyan’s words and asked her in return.

Mo Qingyan nodded: "The expert said Lin City’s situation is really terrible. A shelter was even buried by the previous blizzard, and there are so many sick people, but they lack medical supplies and doctors. They can barely hold on."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 336 - 234: Responding to a Call for Help (Seeking Monthly Tickets at Month End!)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Did Well-digging Expert ask me for help? But the question is, what can I do to help him?" After hearing Mo Qingyan relay the rescue request from Lin City, Chen Xin didn’t immediately go to rescue anyone.

Hearing this, neither Qin Lan nor Mo Qingyan expressed any dissatisfaction, because it was the truth.

Perhaps now that Chen Xin was cooperating with the state, plus having the system as a booster, he was capable of helping the friends in Lin City a bit.

But speaking solely of his personal abilities, without counting the system booster, he was probably more likely to feel powerless in this kind of situation.

Chen Xin understood why the "Well-digging Expert" from Lin City asked for his help, because after the snowstorm, facing difficulties themselves, they could only reach out to the friends they were still able to contact for help, as this might be their only channel for rescue.

But the problem remains the same: what can Chen Xin do?

"Or rather, what does he hope I help him with? If he’s unable to survive and hopes I can get him out of difficulty, that’s easy to say, just drive over, pick him up, and take him to an official shelter." Chen Xin was still willing to lend a hand to his friend on this point, as making a trip wasn’t particularly troublesome for him: "But if it’s for other help, I’m not sure I can help him."

Mo Qingyan shook her head and explained to Chen Xin, "Well-digging Expert is holding on, but the situation in Lin City now... he hopes to convey Lin City’s situation to the municipal government through you, and organize forces for rescue. He knows that individual power is limited, so he hopes we can seek help through official channels."

"Seeking help through official channels? Strange, if Lin City really can’t hold on, why wouldn’t Lin City’s leaders directly ask our city for help? Neither the municipal government nor the provincial government would sit by and do nothing." Chen Xin frowned, it was very strange indeed.

Even if Lin City needed help, there are official channels to go through, Flame Country’s traditional capability of red phone service isn’t just for show. In such circumstances, it’s quite normal, and also very convenient and direct for Lin City’s government to seek help from superior levels of government, whether it be the municipal government or the provincial government, without needing to go through private channels and take such a detour.

Even if Chen Xin could convey Lin City’s request for help to the municipal government, it would be difficult to clarify all the details. Even if the government wanted to send forces for rescue, they would need to first send someone to understand the situation clearly.

In this back and forth, leaving aside the trouble, the key delay might lead some people to face life and death differences.

"I think he might be acting out of compassion. Although he can still manage himself, if everyone around him is falling into difficulty, he might want to find some ways to help others." Qin Lan, with her professional habit as a police officer, tried to analyze the motive of the person asking for help: "There might be two possibilities in this matter. One is that Lin City’s government, due to some reason, can’t ask for help externally and can only send out a call for help through private channels, or maybe because they think they can still hold on with the railroad delivering supplies, so they haven’t reached the point of needing to ask for help.

But for ordinary people, it’s understandable to seek help from the outside under living difficulties. Maybe it’s just a spontaneous action made by the person you talked about, seeing the survivors of Lin City struggling."

"That’s possible, ’Well-digging Expert’ is a very enthusiastic person when it comes to helping others. If, as he says, the situation in Lin City is really difficult, with a shelter buried by snow, he might indeed take such actions." Chen Xin nodded in agreement with Qin Lan’s analysis.

However, no matter the motivation behind the "Well-digging Expert"’s call for help, the current focus remains on how to assist Lin City.

"I think we can first report this to the municipal government and see what they decide to do." Qin Lan’s thoughts leaned more toward official actions, since she was still a police officer, she somewhat retained procedural habits.

Moreover, facing this kind of city-level disaster, the official rescue force is much stronger and more professional than the meager help that could be offered by private efforts.

However, Mo Qingyan disagreed with Qin Lan’s proposal.

"I think our urgent priority is to first figure out what’s really going on in Lin City, so we can proceed accurately. No matter if it’s about asking the government for help or deciding to use other methods for rescue, it’s important to have a basis and reference, to know what they need, and what they’re lacking." Mo Qingyan spoke, looking intently at Chen Xin, seemingly trying to convince him.

Seeing Mo Qingyan looking at Chen Xin, Qin Lan also turned her gaze to him, awaiting his decision.

Chen Xin shook his head, clearly not too satisfied with either plan proposed by the two.

He thought for a moment and then said to the person in charge who had been listening to them, "Can you help me get in touch with the municipal government to ask about the situation in Lin City?"

"No problem, I’ll make a call and find out." The person in charge readily agreed and even asked with concern, "Is there anything we can help with?"

As a research institution with very high authority, if the person in charge was willing to help, they could mobilize resources through their channels and provide significant assistance.

However, Chen Xin still shook his head, not requesting the person’s assistance: "We’re not clear on the situation yet; if help is needed, we’ll wait until we figure things out."

"Alright, I’ll make the call then." The person had no objections to this and turned to leave to make the call.

After the person in charge left, Chen Xin explained to Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan, "We don’t know the situation of Lin City yet; even if we’ve received the ’Well-digging Expert’s’ distress signal and understand some situation, we can’t easily make decisions. So, we should first ask whether the municipal government is aware of Lin City’s situation, and whether there is a rescue plan.

If the government has a rescue plan, we don’t have to worry, just gather some resources we can collect ourselves, make a trip, and help ’Well-digging Expert’ and others with our own efforts."

After thinking about Chen Xin’s approach, both Mo Qingyan and Qin Lan expressed agreement.

This is a relatively prudent handling method, allowing us to understand the situation timely and also avoiding some unnecessary waste of rescue resources.

However, while nodding in agreement with Chen Xin, Mo Qingyan still couldn’t help but ask, "But what if there’s really a problem in Lin City?"

"Then we’ll try to go to Lin City, find out what really happened, and then contact the municipal government to prepare rescue forces." Chen Xin also had a corresponding contingency plan and response for this.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 337 - 235 Rescue Problem (Third Update Seeking Monthly Votes)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The situation in Lin City was beyond Chen Xin’s expectations.

The situation was indeed as dire as the "Drilling Expert" described because, due to the snow disaster, the entire Lin City was severely affected.

The important train station was buried by the previous blizzard, and although the wind has stopped now, the unloading of supplies can still only be done manually outside the station. Currently, Lin City has no way to organize enough manpower to dig out the original train station.

Moreover, the fact that the shelter in Lin City was buried by the blizzard is true; not only was one shelter buried, but about one-fifth of the survivors in the shelter also died in this disaster. Now that shelter has become a tomb of ice and snow.

Furthermore, Lin City’s medical system is now severely overwhelmed, completely in a state of collapse. Although it is still operational, it cannot effectively treat a large number of frostbite and hypothermia patients and other patients in the urban area. Currently, it can only resort to palliative care to buy time, hoping patients can self-recover.

Fortunately, the snow disaster has passed, and some mild patients are indeed in the process of self-recovery. However, severe patients remain in serious condition, and many frostbite patients have already experienced limb necrosis and need amputation.

But what caught Chen Xin by surprise also lies here: Lin City’s government is currently striving for self-rescue. Although they have reported the situation to this city, they did not request a rescue team but hoped for more support in terms of supplies.

This is actually understandable because if this city didn’t have Chen Xin’s help to improve the boilers, facing this blizzard, even if the situation in this city is a bit better than Lin City, it wouldn’t be much better.

Faced with the same dilemma of medical collapse and difficult livelihood, even if this city could send rescue forces, it would be very limited. Lin City, even if requesting help, probably wouldn’t get much assistance, and it might as well strive for self-rescue.

In fact, apart from this city benefiting from Chen Xin’s improvements to the boilers, facing the blizzard situation is more ideal; the situation in most cities in Flame Country hit by the blizzard is very bad.

The problems are mostly concentrated on the medical system because the sheer number of patients caused by the low temperature has burdened all medical institutions far beyond expectations. Large numbers of patients poured into hospitals or health clinics, making it difficult for most people to receive effective treatment.

Moreover, a large number of patients also consume a tremendous amount of medicine, which has led to an emergency of medicines in most cities in Flame Country.

If Lin City seeks help from this city now, what it needs most is also a medical team to provide help to Lin City’s already overwhelmed medical system.

As for supplies, since railway transportation has not been interrupted, Lin City is actually not very pressed for supplies other than medicines.

That’s why Lin City hasn’t requested aid from this city because they don’t expect to get much assistance from this city. As for supplies, it’s best to have them, but it’s not impossible if you don’t.

Learning about this situation from the person in charge, Chen Xin naturally asked concernedly, "So does the city government have any rescue plans for Lin City now? Shouldn’t this city still have the ability to aid Lin City?"

"I asked about this just now, and the city government said they would quickly organize a medical team to go to Lin City, but how to go is a big problem!" Saying this, the person in charge shook his head, his face not looking good: "Currently, the two highways leading to Lin City are completely buried in snow and can’t be passed. Although the railway is unobstructed, the positioning of this city and Lin City is too close. Trains stopping at this city don’t stop at Lin City. To travel by rail, coordination of schedules must be done, but it’s a very troublesome task!"

Chen Xin could completely understand what was going on because the highway distance between this city and Lin City is 45 kilometers, slightly shorter by rail. In the past, people usually traveled between the two cities by driving on the road, and the trip took only about half an hour.

Although there are also two K-trains running between this city and Lin City, the running time of trains averaging speeds of 70-90KM is only half an hour.

Such a short distance is a waste of time for regular trains, not to mention high-speed trains.

So after the apocalypse, Mainland Railway set the schedules to stagger this city and Lin City; trains stopping in this city don’t stop in Lin City, and those stopping in Lin City don’t stop in this city.

The schedules were staggered to improve train operation efficiency.

This has led to a situation where, although the railway between this city and Lin City is unobstructed, there is actually no train traveling between them.

Under such circumstances today, adjusting railway schedules or temporarily adding trains or having a train stop at one more station is a very troublesome task.

Because there isn’t just one train running on the railway, all trains run according to a pre-planned schedule at a specified speed and time on the railway. If a train experiences an error in its running time, it will impact the entire rail network.

And now, unlike before the disaster, when there was a complete communication network, railway coordination could be handled at the main control center, controlling the movement of all trains nationwide. Temporary stops or adding trains could be adjusted and no problems would arise.

However, even then, nationwide train delays often occurred due to issues with one train.

But now, with communications mainly relying on landlines, there is no way to coordinate train schedules nationwide. When a train stops on the railway for too long, it risks colliding with the train behind it, causing a serious accident.

This is true even now when there aren’t as many trains on the tracks.

So under these circumstances, even if this city organized a rescue team or medical team, getting to Lin City is a very difficult task.

However, this is not a problem for Chen Xin. After thinking for a moment, he said, "Going by road should be possible. When I went from Xiang Province to Anhui Province, I took the road. My car had no problems, and if it’s tracked chassis, driving there might not be a problem."

"The city government has considered this too, but with unclear road conditions, driving there is very risky." The person in charge’s face was full of worry. Because he knew the situation well, he understood the dangers involved.

However, Chen Xin thought for a moment and said, "If the city government organized a medical team to go to Lin City, my car could come in handy. My car is equipped with radar and drones, which can be used to detect road conditions and avoid many dangers."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 338 - 236 Rescue Team (Requesting Monthly Tickets)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Although the city government has decided to organize a medical team to support Lin City, it still requires time to organize, and it cannot immediately send a medical team to Lin City.

Moreover, due to transportation issues, going to Lin City is not an easy task.

As Chen Xin envisioned, driving on the original highway to Lin City is currently the most feasible plan.

Although the city government is actively coordinating the railway aspect, there are too many complications, and it cannot be resolved quickly.

If one insists on taking the railway to Lin City, the only feasible plan is to take a train from the city, transfer at Santown, and then board a train that stops at Lin City to reach Lin City.

This is indeed a highly feasible plan, but for now, it still lacks practicality.

After all, this round trip would waste quite a bit of time. Compared to driving directly to Lin City, it is still too troublesome.

The city government obviously considered this issue, so it made two preparations: on one hand, coordinating railway transport for large-scale rescue; on the other hand, deploying all-terrain vehicles from the city government to organize a rescue team to enter Lin City first.

Upon hearing the news of the city government organizing a rescue team, Chen Xin immediately signed up, hoping to participate in the rescue team.

Although he is not a professional medical staff, his reasons are quite sufficient, which is his previous experience traveling from Xiang Province to Anhui Province, and his RV equipped with radar and drones as reconnaissance tools.

With the help of the head of the research institution, Chen Xin smoothly joined the rescue team.

————————————————

The rescue team was successfully formed but did not set out immediately.

The deployment of personnel was not difficult. Thanks to Chen Xin, the temperature inside the shelters was suitable, the number of patients remained at a normal level, and the medical system was functioning properly. Deploying some doctors to form a rescue team was not a problem.

As for other personnel, it was even less of an issue. With the city government’s coordination, all personnel were quickly in place.

However, for the rescue team, equipment and vehicles were more important than personnel.

Professional personnel naturally prepared medical equipment, and relatively speaking, Lin City’s main problem was insufficient medical strength, with many patients suffering from frostbite and hypothermia, as well as diseases caused by cold weather. Warmth is the best treatment method, so the demand for equipment was not particularly high.

But considering the possibility of surgical treatment for severe frostbite, the rescue team still brought a set of mobile surgical equipment.

This is a new piece of equipment that has only been equipped to the city’s hospitals in recent years, used primarily for emergency medical rescue and temporary surgery, regarded as a treasure among the city’s medical facilities.

While medical equipment was readily solved, sourcing vehicles was not so easy.

Ordinary cars were clearly not qualified to make this trip. Even with the track components Chen Xin previously made for Li Yulong, fitting tires with tracks might suffice for relatively smooth snow-covered roads within the city but were inadequate for challenging the mountain roads between the city and Lin City.

Truly qualified and capable of making this trip must be track-type all-terrain vehicles.

Fortunately, the city government found a few of these vehicles, but these were not specifically medical vehicles. Most were just passenger vehicles, with no way to tow the mobile surgical trailer.

If not for Chen Xin’s help, the city might have considered sending the rescue team to Lin City first and transporting the mobile surgical unit via rail or other means to Lin City later.

Fortunately, with Chen Xin’s proactive offer to assist in vehicle chassis modifications, the vehicle issue was resolved, and the entire mobile surgical unit was brought along.

"Mr. Chen, we are truly grateful to you this time. Not only are you personally joining us, but you also helped modify the vehicles, saving us so much trouble. We really don’t know how to thank you enough," the rescue team leader hastily expressed his gratitude to Chen Xin after he assisted with the vehicle modifications.

"It’s nothing, just what I can do. Besides, helping you solve these problems means you can reach Lin City earlier and help more people." Chen Xin brushed it off politely but didn’t waste time, directly asking the team leader, "How many people are you taking to Lin City this time? Besides this surgical vehicle, do you have any other equipment?"

"Due to the tight timeframe and limited vehicles, we only deployed fifteen medical personnel and five drivers, a total of five vehicles: one surgical vehicle, one for transporting supplies, and three for passengers." The team leader had no need to hide the composition of the rescue team from Chen Xin and directly informed him, "Additionally, there’s your vehicle and a police car. A SWAT team will be responsible for protecting the safety of the rescue team in Lin City."

"When are you planning to set off?" Chen Xin was satisfied with their rescue strength since they were just the advance team, entering Lin City to assess the situation and provide limited assistance.

More rescue forces were still being organized and coordinated by the city government and would arrive in Lin City after them.

"We have some supplies not yet in place, but once everything is ready, we can set out." The team leader checked his watch, estimated the time, and said to Chen Xin, "If everything goes smoothly, we should be able to set out early tomorrow morning. You can go back and rest well, and once refreshed, we start early tomorrow to reach Lin City sooner."

Listening to the team leader, Chen Xin nodded in agreement. "Alright, I’ll come over tomorrow morning. The earlier we leave, the better. The road to Lin City probably won’t be easy. When I went from the city to Anhui Province before, it was this section of the road. Back then, the snow wasn’t as thick as it is now, and it took me most of the day just for these dozens of kilometers. Now it’s likely harder; you need to be prepared."

"Does it really take most of the day for a few dozen kilometers? Is the road that hard to travel?" The team leader was somewhat surprised. He thought that even if the roads were tough due to snow, it should only take a few hours to cover the tens of kilometers from the city to Lin City, but Chen Xin mentioned it taking most of the day.

"All the roads were buried under one or two meters of snow, and without a path, advancing on snow that thick, moving five to eight kilometers an hour is already fast." Recalling his previous experience, Chen Xin also felt helpless. The dozens of kilometers of mountain road were indeed hard to traverse.

Hearing Chen Xin’s account, the team leader thoughtfully nodded, having a better sense of the situation in mind. He also planned to hold a briefing with the rescue team members that evening to prepare them mentally.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 339 - 237 Heading to Lin City (Second Update, Requesting Monthly Pass)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A convoy formed by seven vehicles drove out of the city center in the early morning, heading towards Lin City under Chen Xin’s lead.

Chen Xin’s speed wasn’t fast. In fact, after leaving the city, he had already slowed down, keeping his car at a pace of 10 km/s.

It’s not that he couldn’t go faster, but Chen Xin needed to ensure that the six vehicles behind could keep up, while also maintaining driving safety.

The road conditions leading to Lin City were much worse compared to when Chen Xin last drove home. The snow had become thicker and deeper, burying the ground more severely.

Don’t assume that deep snow makes for an easy path. In this pitch-dark night, without reference points, it’s easy for people to veer off course and lose direction.

Even though Chen Xin had radar positioning and drone reconnaissance, he could ensure he didn’t veer off, but also had to guarantee the six vehicles behind did not stray either.

For Chen Xin, this was a completely new experience, as he hadn’t been the lead car of a convoy before.

Maintaining contact through radio with the other six vehicles, Chen Xin drove while constantly keeping an eye on the convoy’s situation.

Thankfully, the distance wasn’t far, and the drivers in the convoy were all seasoned, with the special police team’s patrol car at the rear as a backup. Generally speaking, there weren’t any issues.

Initially, Chen Xin planned to only bring Han Ping with him, but both Mo Qingyan and Qin Lan insisted on coming along in the end.

Mo Qingyan had a compelling reason; she was the first to receive the distress signal, and Lin City’s "drilling expert" was her client and friend. Both emotionally and rationally, she should come.

Meanwhile, Qin Lan stated she wanted to provide close protection for Chen Xin. Given Han Ping also had to drive, he couldn’t adequately ensure Chen Xin’s safety.

Faced with their insistence, although Chen Xin felt inexplicably helpless, he eventually agreed to let both accompany him.

After all, Qin Lan had Power Armor made by Chen Xin, and Mo Qingyan had an Exoskeleton Protective Suit. Once in Lin City, even if only to help with moving things as labor, one more person meant more aid.

As for the discomfort and issues brought on by having two women in the car...

First, neither Qin Lan nor Mo Qingyan were the type to cause unreasonable trouble; even if there were problems, they wouldn’t disrupt important matters.

Moreover, even if issues arose, Chen Xin felt he could handle them.

With concerns towards Lin City, this convoy of seven vehicles moved towards Lin City in the dark, hoping to bring assistance.

——————————————————

"Radar ahead shows soft snow; rear vehicles avoid and keep close to the car in front; move according to the tracks of the front car," Chen Xin said into the walkie-talkie, notifying the vehicles behind of the road conditions.

After the last upgrades and adjustments, the radar system could now detect snow conditions and the situation below the snow. The drones were equipped with sonar probing capabilities, allowing Chen Xin to have a very clear grasp of the road conditions.

Controlling the steering wheel, avoiding the soft snow ahead that might collapse, Chen Xin drove along firmer parts, deliberately slowing down to give the rear vehicles a chance to react and follow his tracks.

This stretch of road looks flat, but through drones and radar detection, Chen Xin knows the bypassed area beneath is actually a slope.

They should now be on the site of the former highway, which is buried under more than two meters of snow.

It’s not just that; this highway was actually an elevated line, with a bridge underneath supporting the path, maintaining relative firmness and solidity.

Alongside the path Chen Xin took, the snow was much softer because beneath it was empty, with no support—just pure snow.

Traversing this section, even though Chen Xin had full confidence in his car, he proceeded cautiously.

Last time he passed through here, he could still see the highway bridge. Though the surface was severely damaged and many places had collapsed, forcing him to turn back a bit, at least it was still passable, and traces of the old highway were visible.

Now, however, not a hint remains visible; everything is a flat, slightly undulating snowy plain.

Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan sat in the backseat of the cab, gazing at the snowy fields illuminated by the headlights, seemingly lost in thought.

"A Xin, did you take this kind of road when you went back home last time?" Qin Lan recalled Chen Xin’s trip back home last time and suddenly asked about it.

Chen Xin held the steering wheel, controlling the direction, nodding as he answered: "Last time I also came through here. Back then, the surface still showed meteor craters; the highway bridges were smashed, forcing me to take the national road."

"Was this place a highway before? Doesn’t that mean it’s the road we used to take frequently?" Mo Qingyan recalled often organizing outdoor activities with friends along this route to Lin City, exclaiming in surprise: "But I remember that highway should’ve been elevated high, at least seven or eight meters above the ground. Is it all buried now?"

Chen Xin felt similarly astonished at this, nodding slightly to say: "It seems so. At least from the radar’s display, the original highway bridge is below us."

"This is really..." Mo Qingyan was momentarily at a loss for words.

Glancing at Mo Qingyan in the rearview mirror, Chen Xin shook his head, somewhat thoughtful about her reaction, then spoke: "Actually, such monstrous snowstorms like before, causing this is not impossible. You must know, it was a -70℃, 12-class wind speed snowstorm, enough to freeze and destroy everything."

"But it shouldn’t lead to at least ten meters of snow accumulation, right?" Mo Qingyan calculated the distance between the snow surface and the original ground, unable to help but remark.

Chen Xin merely smiled wryly, saying: "In the city, due to building debris blocking, most places have only around two meters of snow; better conditions might be just over one meter, while severe areas see five or six meters. But in suburbs now barren of vegetation, a snowstorm sweeps without obstruction, carrying massive amounts of snow, forming snow piles of over ten meters isn’t exaggerated."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 340 - 238: Launching the Rescue (Third Update, Beginning of the Month Seeking Support)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The journey to Lin City did not encounter any unforeseen circumstances. After all, it’s merely a distance of several dozen kilometers, and Chen Xin’s vehicle was equipped with comprehensive detection methods, having a well-informed grasp of road conditions, which made the likelihood of accidents quite low.

However, when the group arrived in Lin City, they were astounded by the current state of the city.

The city’s topography is typical of a valley between two mountains, with the other end of the valley facing the Yangtze River, and a lake located between the city and the river.

The lake is a remnant of the ancient Yunmeng Marsh, long since disconnected from the Yangtze River and Dongting Lake. Before the disaster, the crabs produced there were a famous local product, with a large number of crabs consumed during the crab-eating season every year coming from here.

But now, not to mention the entire lake has frozen into ice, making it impossible to enjoy delicious crabs, the backflow of seawater caused by the previous tsunami also led to a surge in the Yangtze River’s water level, flooding the lake, with the "coastline" once approaching the city district of Lin City.

In fact, it was not just Lin City — even though the city is located in the central region of Flame Country, the backflow of seawater caused by the tsunami had reached its limit by the time it got to Dongting Lake but still raised its water level significantly, causing most of the areas along the lake in the city to suffer from flooding.

If not for the hilly terrain and relatively high elevation of the city, most parts of the city district would have been submerged in water.

Lin City, on the other hand, was in an even worse situation, as the rise and freezing of the water level left the side of Lin City facing the Yangtze River exposed and unguarded.

When the blizzard struck, the valley topography between two mountains, along with the flat ice surface, made Lin City entirely like a wind pocket. The orientation of the mountains caused the wind speed of the blizzard blowing towards Lin City to be faster and the snowstorm more concentrated.

The abundant moisture initially brought by the proximity to the Yangtze River and lake all turned into ice and snow in the low temperature, blown towards Lin City by the blizzard.

It was precisely because of these factors that the entire Lin City was almost completely buried under snow.

The vast majority of the city’s buildings were buried under the snow, with only a very few of the taller buildings having their roofs exposed.

After all, Lin City is just a county-level city, with not many high-rise buildings in the city district. Under the ravages of the blizzard, most buildings didn’t have sufficient height and were naturally engulfed by the snow.

But facing this situation, everyone in the rescue team felt as if they might have arrived too late.

"Lin City... could everyone be buried under the snow?" A female voice queried this over the walkie-talkie.

She was a doctor in the rescue team, who volunteered to come for the sake of saving lives. As a local, she also had some relatives and friends in Lin City.

However, upon seeing a city completely buried under snow, she found it hard to believe there could be any survivors left in the city.

Chen Xin naturally had similar worries, although he was relatively more optimistic.

"The situation shouldn’t be that bad. In previous communications with the city district, Lin City hadn’t reported any major issues. It’s impossible for the entire city to have disappeared in such a short time before we arrived." Chen Xin stated calmly, and proactively proposed a response plan: "The drone and radar in my car can detect life signals. Let’s find a place to park the car first, and carefully search for any signs of survivors."

"I agree with Chen Xin’s opinion. Everyone find a place to park first and understand the situation." As the leader of the rescue team, the captain chose to support Chen Xin at this moment.

——————————————————

Seven vehicles drove into Lin City, heading towards the area that used to be the city center. During this journey, Chen Xin released his drone, switching the radar to life signal search mode, hoping to find traces of survivors.

Meanwhile, on the other vehicles, the captain was trying to contact Lin City Hall to relay the message that the rescue team had arrived. The people in the other cars couldn’t help but feel anxious. They were worried about Lin City’s situation, concerned about whether they really had arrived too late, while also actively making various preparations.

After all, if there were still survivors in Lin City, once they were found, the rescue team would have to immediately commence rescue operations.

In this process, Chen Xin’s radar and drone were the first to detect signs of survivors.

"We detected heat signal nearby, preliminarily determined to be the vent of a shelter." The display of heat sources on the radar was quite evident, as Chen Xin merely elevated the drone to a high altitude, switching to infrared mode to locate several obvious heat sources.

Chen Xin was unsure of the exact situation beneath the snow, but judging from the number and distribution of the heat sources, he believed these to be the main shelters currently existing in Lin City.

Learning that the shelter was still operational, the people in the cars could not help but breathe a sigh of relief. This was indeed good news; at least they hadn’t come for nothing, and Lin City hadn’t become an empty city.

Apart from Chen Xin’s news, the captain also contacted Lin City Hall and informed them that the rescue team had arrived.

Lin City Hall was extremely excited and delighted by the arrival of the rescue team. Despite the small number of rescue personnel and the limited assistance they could provide, it at least indicated that the city district still had the resources to aid them. The arrival of the rescue team injected hope into their cold and dark circumstances.

Following the information obtained from the captain’s contact with Lin City Hall, the rescue team stopped at a location in what should have been the city center’s square.

This place used to be a commercial plaza, nearby is the largest shelter in Lin City.

Compared to the city district, Lin City had only one large shelter and three medium-sized shelters, accommodating only a total of tens of thousands of survivors post-disaster.

During the previous blizzard, one of the medium shelters was buried by snow, and now abandoned. The remaining population survival number in Lin City might already be less than a hundred thousand.

Understanding the situation, after parking the cars, the captain approached Chen Xin. Along with him were the commanding SWAT officer and a doctor.

"Sorry, Chen Xin, only your car has more space, so we’ll use it for a briefing." The captain apologized to Chen Xin, then sat down with others at Chen Xin’s invitation, to discuss business: "Now that we have arrived in Lin City, the city government just briefed us on the relevant situation. The situation in Lin City is dire, and we must discuss how the rescue team should carry out the rescue work."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 341 - 239: Emergency Rescue Station (Requesting Monthly Tickets at the Beginning of the Month)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After a discussion, the rescue team did not directly enter the shelter in Lin City to carry out the rescue operation.

For one, the internal situation of Lin City’s shelter is unclear, and rushing in may pose some safety risks. Second, there are too many patients in need of help in the shelter, and without proper classification, directly investing the rescue force might not exert its maximum effectiveness.

Take a simple example: if the rescue team enters a shelter to treat patients, who will be treated first?

Some might think that severely ill patients should receive treatment first. However, the fact is quite the opposite—in this situation, those who get treated first will be light patients who still have the strength to come before the rescue team.

Because severely ill patients are too weak to compete with light patients for treatment opportunities.

As for having the rescue team search for severely ill patients inside the shelter and give them treatment...this notion is beautiful, but only feasible if the number of patients in the shelter does not exceed a certain limit. After all, everyone understands priorities; letting those seriously ill be treated first could allow more people to survive.

But when the number of patients breaks a certain limit, especially as people start to die in the shelters, most are already ill, everyone thinks first about keeping themselves alive rather than considering holding on a bit longer to give precedence to those more in need.

Without survival pressure, people might be willing to help others. With some survival pressure, as long as it’s not threatening their own life, some people are still willing to help those more needy.

But in the face of life and death, most people first think about how they should survive, and might even seize others’ survival chances rather than help them.

Therefore, for the rescue team, if they wish to conduct effective rescues, going deep into the shelters to treat patients may not yield good results.

Of course, this doesn’t mean the rescue team does nothing.

Though the two supply vehicles brought most drugs and medical supplies, they also had a set of medical tents, which could be deployed in suitable places as a temporary hospital.

In fact, after discussion, the rescue team did just that.

They arranged seven vehicles in a circle, then set up the medical tents brought within the circle to form a temporary medical station.

The entire medical station looked a little like the shape of the letter "m." Two tents joined together formed a horizontal row, while the other six connected to form three vertical columns.

The two horizontal tents served as consultation rooms, and the six vertical ones were planned as wards, with closed passageways connecting all tents. An entrance was specially made with a windproof compartment to protect the heat inside the tents from dispersing.

The tents themselves are specially designed for cold resistance, and with heaters installed internally, the temperature was reasonably acceptable.

As for the tent’s power supply, initially, the rescue team planned to use a generator. However, Chen Xin connected the tent’s power lines to his vehicle, using the onboard Isotope Thermoelectric Battery for power, which was much more convenient than using a generator.

After installing the brought-in medical equipment within the tents, the rescue team’s temporary medical station officially started operating.

According to the pre-discussed plan, the five drivers from the rescue team and two SWAT officers, with assistance from staff sent by Lin City Hall, would enter the nearby shelters to transport out severe patients for treatment.

Meanwhile, the medical station would also be open to ordinary patients for necessary treatment to help them recover their health.

However, unlike severe patients who would be admitted into wards, light patients wouldn’t stay in the medical station after receiving treatment.

Prior to departure, the city hall had already equipped the entire rescue team with Protective Suits, while the SWAT team in charge of safety also were equipped with newly issued Exoskeletons, ensuring safety.

Once the medical station began working, survivors emerged from shelters beneath the snow and came for consultations.

Considering the medical station’s reception capacity and to avoid chaos, approximately only fifty light patients would receive treatment at each time. After the doctors handled these fifty patients and they returned to the shelters, new patients would then come for treatment.

Under the command and coordination of staff from Lin City Hall, this operation went fairly orderly.

However, most patients had various degrees of frostbite, and a few severe cases needed amputation.

This undeniably made many reluctant, as nobody wishes to lose arms or legs, even if his limbs are already blackened and necrotic, and not amputating could seriously endanger life.

Faced with such circumstances, besides explaining and persuading patients as much as possible to accept amputation surgery, the rescue team had no better options.

Though amputation wasn’t entirely out of question, the rescue team’s doctors hoped to persuade patients to voluntarily undergo surgery when feasible, even though they didn’t have much time to persuade.

Fortunately, despite reluctance, most chose to survive over the option to die without amputation.

It’s just that a few still believed that the doctor was hurting them, intending to cut their limbs, turning them from healthy to disabled.

Holding such a belief, refusing to cooperate with doctors’ treatment would only lead to their demise, but a very few even tried to attack doctors.

Though those severely frostbitten requiring amputation were typically too incapacitated for actions, and any aggression towards doctors was quickly suppressed by SWAT officers maintaining order, the situation still created a negative impact.

For Lin City’s survivors, being in an already stressed state, having endured the blizzard in cold only to drag their sick bodies towards death, finally seeing doctors there for healing only to have disruptions—it could easily frighten these survivors.

In a stress-induced condition, people, once startled and scared, could easily overreact, even incite disturbances.

Luckily, the SWAT officers maintaining order were fully armed, wearing police-colored Exoskeleton Protective Suits with warning lights, exerting enough deterrence to maintain order effectively.

As for those controlled by officers while trying to cause trouble, they will face enforced measures.

Though they aren’t abandoned in treatment, it’s clear they need to wake up and bear responsibility for their wrongdoing.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 342 - 240 Accidental Discovery (Request for guaranteed monthly votes at the beginning of the month)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The situation in Lin City is indeed dire. Just a few hours after setting up the medical station, the rescue team had already filled the six tents initially prepared with various critically ill patients.

The emergency tents brought by the rescue team weren’t small; each tent could accommodate eight beds, and squeezing six tents together could hold 50 to 60 patients.

Although this number isn’t a lot, providing so many beds for a temporary medical station is quite a feat.

You have to know that even a health clinic in a shelter can only offer about the same number of beds.

Yet within a few hours, the beds in the six tents were quickly filled with various critically ill patients and those who had just undergone amputation surgery.

Meanwhile, more and more patients continued to be sent here.

Faced with this situation, a nurse approached the captain who was discussing matters with Chen Xin, reporting that the ward was already full: "Captain, all the beds are now full, but patients are still being sent in, and we can’t accommodate anymore!"

Upon hearing this, both Chen Xin and the captain were taken aback, but neither showed signs of panic.

After assuring the nurse that he understood and asking her to return to work, the captain said to Chen Xin, "This won’t do, it’s only been a few hours! I didn’t expect the situation in Lin City to be this severe. Our tiny rescue effort is like a drop in the bucket!

We must find a way to expand the medical station’s capacity for patients; otherwise, our rescue won’t be effective and will only drag us down too!"

"Let two supply trucks follow my car to the city to request more aid." In this scenario, Chen Xin hadn’t anticipated it, and the only solution he could think of was sending someone back for more aid, to have the city send more supplies over.

However, besides asking for help, Chen Xin thought they couldn’t just wait idly. After pondering for a moment, he said to the captain, "Besides asking for help, we also need to think of other solutions."

"Other solutions aren’t that easy to think of! The tents are only so big, no matter how you cram them, at most, you can squeeze twelve people into one tent." The captain seemed experienced with such matters, shaking his head, clearly unable to fit more people into the ward: "A ward isn’t like a shelter. In a shelter, you can stack bunk beds, and it doesn’t matter if more people are crammed in, but you can’t stack patients in the wards!"

"Can we ask Lin City’s staff to find a way to clear some space in the shelter to accommodate patients?" Chen Xin thought for a moment and found this to be feasible.

Most patients accommodated in the medical station suffer from conditions exacerbated by prolonged exposure to low temperatures, which can gradually improve with proper rest and treatment by a doctor.

However, some of these patients require support from medical equipment and must remain in the medical station’s wards.

Another group whose conditions are slightly better and do not require medical equipment support can be relocated elsewhere.

As for those who suffered frostbite leading to amputations, they can also be relocated elsewhere once past their surgery’s danger period.

Most amputees didn’t lose an entire leg or arm, but rather just fingers or toes. There’s no need for them to occupy a hospital bed in the medical station.

Although this may seem harsh, moving these patients out of the medical station will allow it to more effectively treat other patients, making it worthwhile.
