The captain had clearly considered this. After thinking it over carefully, he nodded, agreeing with Chen Xin’s suggestion: "I’ll go coordinate with Lin City’s staff; we can indeed move some of the moderately ill patients."

This is indeed like establishing specialized hospitals and makeshift hospitals during a major epidemic, where specialized hospitals treat moderate to severe cases, while mild cases continue treatment in makeshift hospitals.

In this way, medical resources can be effectively utilized, allowing more patients to receive treatment.

However, the rescue team’s current medical station can only treat the severely and critically ill, with mild cases returning to the shelter after treatment, and moderate cases being relocated to designated zones for follow-up treatment.

Most patients here do not have contagious diseases, and most mild and moderate cases can naturally recover after treatment without the need for hospitalization.

Seeing the captain busy coordinating with Lin City’s staff, Chen Xin returned to his own vehicle.

In the car, only Han Ping was monitoring the in-car radar in case of any emergencies, while Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan went to see the "Drilling Master."

Although Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan accompanied Chen Xin and the rescue team to Lin City, the rescue team primarily needed professional medical personnel, and the two couldn’t help much, so they sought out the "Drilling Master" and several other group friends in Lin City to check on their situation.

If they needed help, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan could assist as needed.

As for their safety, Chen Xin wasn’t worried. Qin Lan wore her Power Armor, and Mo Qingyan borrowed Chen Xin’s set.

When making the Power Armor, Chen Xin designed the interior to be adjustable for comfort, so as long as the wearers’ physical sizes weren’t too different, it could be worn by multiple users.

Therefore, Chen Xin didn’t mind lending his Power Armor to Mo Qingyan for her safety.

Entering the cockpit, Chen Xin greeted Han Ping, then pulled up the radar monitor from the console. Seeing the red dots representing Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan, he launched a reconnaissance drone from the vehicle, sending it to their location.

Even though he knew the two were safe, Chen Xin couldn’t help but worry about them.

The drone flew quickly, yet before it reached Mo Qingyan and Qin Lan, it unexpectedly detected a heat source signal.

Chen Xin was surprised to find that this heat source signal did not come from Lin City’s official shelters but from a private shelter.

This surprised Chen Xin as, besides the "Drilling Master," several other group friends in Lin City also built private shelters. Finding one now piqued his curiosity about whose shelter this might be, prompting him to steer the drone towards the heat source.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 343 - 241 Rescue (Beginning of the Month Request for Monthly Tickets)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Beneath the snow, Bao Zhen was trying to dig an upward tunnel with the hoe in his hand.

Beside him, a German Shepherd was also vigorously helping to dig the snow. However, a dog’s ability is limited. While Bao Zhen was strenuously digging the tunnel upwards, the dog only managed to scrape out a pit.

"Alright, Jackal, I know you want to help. Go rest to the side, I can handle it myself." Seeing his beloved dog dig for half a day without much progress and shivering with cold, Bao Zhen tenderly told the dog to go back and rest.

The German Shepherd named Jackal was quite obedient. Although it indeed went to lie down and rest a little way off, it kept watching its owner and did not wander far.

This was the 37th day that Bao Zhen was trapped underground. Because his shelter was at a low elevation and due to structural issues, the entrance of his shelter was completely buried in snow during the ravaging blizzard, and it was deeply buried.

If it weren’t for the fact that he had initially connected the shelter’s ventilation duct to the roof through the building he was in, and the duct hadn’t been buried, Bao Zhen might have perished by now.

Though the ventilation duct was still usable and the indoor planting boxes provided enough oxygen to prevent suffocation, the four-story building he was in was already buried by snow up to three and a half stories. If Bao Zhen didn’t dig his way out, he would remain buried underground.

Although Bao Zhen had prepared adequately enough before the disaster, with a complete set of self-constructed living facilities that were sufficient for him and his dog to eat and drink, his fuel reserves were nearly exhausted. He had to find a way to replenish them. Otherwise, stuck underground without fuel, he wouldn’t last a few days.

Moreover, no one would want to be buried in snow underground; even if you wouldn’t die, such a life was akin to being in prison – a life nobody wants to live, particularly when you’re alone.

While Bao Zhen was arduously digging a path for survival, the drone controlled by Chen Xin also reached his location, pinpointing Bao Zhen through the heat emanating from the rooftop ventilation outlet.

Yet, through the drone, Chen Xin saw the building almost entirely covered by snow and, after searching for a while, could not find an entrance to the building.

Even though some parts of the building’s top were not completely buried in snow, entering through broken windows in the top floor revealed that the interior had already been filled with snow during the previous blizzard. While there were some gaps, it was impossible to enter the building’s interior from the top floor.

However, although he couldn’t enter the building, Chen Xin discovered Bao Zhen, who was diligently digging upwards underground, through the drone’s life detection equipment.

Yet, perhaps because Bao Zhen’s communication equipment was damaged, Chen Xin tried contacting him via radio but got no response.

Faced with this situation, Chen Xin, after some thought, decided to go for the rescue.

Although his Power Armor had been taken by Mo Qingyan, Chen Xin himself still had an Exoskeleton Protective Suit, which was sufficient for his needs outdoors.

Chen Xin instructed Han Ping to continue monitoring the radar from the vehicle, while he called two workers from Lin City to accompany him towards the location where he discovered Bao Zhen.

After the blizzard subsided, Lin City also received a batch of Protective Suits and Exoskeletons. Although they were few, the two workers accompanying Chen Xin, possibly due to work requirements, were both equipped with Protective Suits. One worker, carrying a shovel, even had an Exoskeleton.

The exoskeletons produced by the state differed from the versions provided by Chen Xin, clearly incorporating some of Flame Country’s own technology instead of merely copying Chen Xin’s blueprints.

Moreover, the state-produced exoskeletons were categorized into civilian, police, and a military version that Chen Xin knew existed but had never seen.

The worker’s set was evidently civilian, possessing only some basic assistant functions, having less output than Chen Xin’s original version, and appearing simpler, lacking the protective armor plates found on police exoskeletons used by rescue teams.

Nonetheless, even so, a civilian exoskeleton like this was already more than sufficient for ordinary workers.

At least, accompanied by Chen Xin heading to the location of Bao Zhen, the worker without an exoskeleton had difficulty walking through the snow, whereas the worker with an exoskeleton moved with ease and could even assist his companion.

The three didn’t travel long before reaching Bao Zhen’s location. After all, Lin City was just a prefecture-level city, with a narrow urban spread, and Bao Zhen’s location wasn’t far from the rescue station.

"Mr. Chen, is the survivor you mentioned really under this building?" The worker, observing the building, mostly buried in snow with only a little of the top left exposed, seemed uncertain about what to do.

"That’s right. The life signal detected by the drone earlier is right beneath here. He should also be actively self-rescuing, digging a tunnel upwards. We simply need to dig downwards." Chen Xin explained the situation to the two workers and used the communicator on his Exoskeleton to contact Han Ping, asking him to relay the footage captured by the drone to him.

Although the two workers thought that the three of them alone might not manage to dig out the person buried below, they didn’t decline and each picked up a shovel, ready to help.

Relying on the footage projected inside his helmet visor, Chen Xin identified the spot, then took his first shovel to dig downwards.

Though there were only three people, and the only tools were three shovels, with the help of two sets of Exoskeletons, the trio quickly dug a large pit in the ground.

"Don’t dig straight down like this; let’s dig downwards in a spiral, otherwise we’ll have to figure out how to get back up later." Seeing the pit getting a bit deep, Chen Xin said to the nearby workers, adjusting the direction of excavation.

Although digging a spiral tunnel meant a significantly larger workload, both workers understood its necessity and resignedly cooperated with Chen Xin, continuing to dig downwards.

While those above were digging downwards, the man below wasn’t idle either. Bao Zhen was equally diligently digging upwards, and he had been digging in a spiral from the start. After all, he couldn’t possibly dig himself out of the ground with a vertical tunnel.

The only thing Bao Zhen, still diligently digging his path to survival upwards, didn’t know was that someone was already on their way to rescue him from above.

Now, the problem is, with snow approximately over ten meters deep and dual excavation efforts, the ground excavation speed is five times that of the underground. If the ground crew can dig one meter down in half an hour, how long will it take to excavate through this stretch of snow?

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 344 - 242: Bao Zhen (One more)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When the snow above collapsed down, splattering all over Bao Zhen’s face, and even knocking two people down, he was completely bewildered.

However, when he shook off the snow from his body and stood up, he called the jackal that was barking fiercely at the two figures who were also sitting up, and cheerfully asked them: "Are you here to rescue me?"

Chen Xin patted the snow off himself, looking at the figure in front of him. Although he hadn’t yet recognized the person as Bao Zhen, he nodded and said: "Yes, we’re here to rescue you."

Honestly, Chen Xin hadn’t expected the collapse to happen, leaving both sides in a bit of a mess, but fortunately, there were no serious consequences, and they smoothly dug out the trapped people. This outcome was quite ideal.

Having successfully opened a passage upward, Bao Zhen was not in a hurry to go out, instead enthusiastically inviting Chen Xin and the two staff members into his shelter for a break.

Chen Xin and the two staff members didn’t decline the invitation, after all, several hours of digging, even with the assistance of the Exoskeleton, was indeed quite tiring and they needed rest.

The four returned to Bao Zhen’s shelter. Once he took off the helmet wrapped around his head, Chen Xin recognized him, and Bao Zhen also recognized Chen Xin without his helmet.

"Author? How come you’re in Lin City? Is that an Exoskeleton you’re wearing? Where did you get it? It’s really cool!" Bao Zhen was actually a few years younger than Chen Xin, and after recognizing Chen Xin, he greeted him simply, focusing his attention completely on the Exoskeleton Chen Xin was wearing.

"’Daren’ sent me a distress signal, saying that things in Lin City were getting really tough, almost impossible to hold on, and asked me to find a way to rescue people. Luckily, the city was sending a rescue team over, so I followed along." Chen Xin was surprised to meet Bao Zhen, and felt quite happy inside, explaining to him: "As for this, the Exoskeleton is part of national supplies recently distributed, and I got a set because I came along with the rescue team. But what about you, how are things?"

After hearing Chen Xin’s explanation, Bao Zhen’s face was full of envy, and after inviting the three to sit down and serving them a cup of hot water, he said: "My situation isn’t very good. Though food and water are quite adequate, fuel and living supplies are running low.

During the blizzard, the antenna I had outside got blown off, cutting off communication with the outside, and then it was simply buried in snow. Only after hearing from the ventilation that the wind outside had calmed did I start to dig out, trying to make a way outside, and then you guys ended up knocking me down."

"It sounds alright, at least you haven’t frozen or starved." Chen Xin listened as Bao Zhen explained his situation over the past period, offering a genuinely fair assessment.

Upon hearing this, Bao Zhen was immediately dissatisfied and said: "What are you talking about! How could I possibly freeze or starve to death? Are you cursing me?"

Although his tone was of dissatisfaction, Bao Zhen was evidently joking, not taking it seriously.

"No, you’re actually doing okay. At least you have food and drink, plus a dog to keep you company. Even being buried in snow for about a month didn’t bring any serious trouble; no sickness, no disasters. This is much better than most people." But Chen Xin earnestly explained again, clearly not treating it as a joke.

The two staff members beside them also nodded along, similarly feeling that Bao Zhen’s survival situation was quite good.

This surprised Bao Zhen, prompting him to ask Chen Xin: "If being buried in snow for over a month is considered good, just how badly off are other places?"

"Due to the blizzard earlier, Lin City had a shelter completely buried in snow, causing many deaths." Chen Xin’s expression turned somewhat somber, his tone inevitably carrying a hint of sigh: "Many people suffered frostbite and got ill, unable to receive timely treatment, resulting in necrosis and needing amputation. The rescue team came here for support in Lin City too."

Saying this, Chen Xin patted Bao Zhen’s shoulder, sighing deeply: "Brother, you’re really lucky!"

What Chen Xin described about Lin City’s situation shocked Bao Zhen, and after confirming with the two staff members, he had no choice but to believe it, realizing he truly was lucky and his survival conditions were indeed quite good.

Though now facing some difficulties due to the fuel shortage issue, Bao Zhen wasn’t lacking food and drink, and even used a planting machine to grow some vegetables, raised some chickens and ducks, and kept a dog and a fox as pets. His situation could be said to be much better compared to most people.

Having realized the situation, Bao Zhen felt increasingly worried about Lin City and asked Chen Xin: "Is there anything I can do to help?"

"Currently, what we need are doctors to treat patients, hands otherwise are not scarce. You should first resolve your own issues." Chen Xin shook his head, declining Bao Zhen’s offer to help.

From Chen Xin’s perspective, since Bao Zhen hadn’t even solved his own problems, it wouldn’t be much help to have him come and aid them.

Bao Zhen thought about it and agreed, thus didn’t insist on trying to help, only asking Chen Xin: "Author, can you get me some diesel? My fuel reserves are about to run out. Without replenishment, the generator won’t run, and if I could get some toilet paper and similar household items that would be great too."

For Bao Zhen’s request, Chen Xin couldn’t provide at the moment, but he turned his gaze to the two staff members who came with him. They were workers from Lin City, and if he needed to acquire the supplies Bao Zhen wanted, asking them would be more appropriate than asking Chen Xin.

Under Chen Xin’s gaze, the two staff members didn’t decline, as getting these supplies was within their reach, though they clearly couldn’t provide them to Bao Zhen for free. Therefore, they asked him: "The things you want, we can get for you, but it’s not without charge."

Bao Zhen understood this, even before the disaster, during national relief efforts, at most some emergency supplies would be provided, and exchanging with personal belongings was entirely expected.

"Then I’ll trade you with my chickens or ducks! Do you want chicken or duck? How about 150 liters of gasoline per one?" Bao Zhen thought for a moment, taking out what he could trade.

"150 liters is too much! At most, we can get you 100 liters; 150 liters, we can’t manage that!" The staff member swallowed upon hearing Bao Zhen say he could trade chickens or ducks, and began bargaining with him.

Watching them negotiate, Chen Xin didn’t intervene, merely stepping aside to play with Bao Zhen’s pet fox and dog.

It was, after all, the first time since the disaster that Chen Xin had seen rather large living animals.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 345 - 243 Busy (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin did not stay long at Bao Zhen’s place. After negotiating with two staff members to exchange a chicken for 120 liters of diesel, Chen Xin and the staff members left.

Bao Zhen escorted Chen Xin and the others out of the shelter and then picked up a shovel to continue digging the tunnel. Although the tunnel was already opened, Bao Zhen needed to reinforce and modify it if he wanted to use this passage to come and go.

Meanwhile, Chen Xin returned to the rescue station with the staff.

"Chen Xin, you’re back? I was just looking for you!" As soon as Chen Xin returned, the team leader immediately approached him and said, "The convoy heading back to the city for help is ready. We need your car to lead the way, but your driver said he couldn’t take the responsibility without you here. Can you quickly let him know so the convoy can set off?"

Upon hearing the leader’s words, Chen Xin wanted to smack his own forehead as he realized he forgot to inform Han Ping about this matter before leaving.

So Chen Xin hurried back to his car, found Han Ping, and quickly instructed him to lead the convoy back to the city for help.

Han Ping did not object. In fact, he had already prepared to set off when the leader approached him earlier; he was just hesitant to agree to the task without Chen Xin’s permission.

Now that Chen Xin was back, confirming they indeed needed to head back to the city for help, Han Ping naturally wasted no time and drove the convoy off.

While it was convenient to agree to head back to the city for help, with the car gone, Chen Xin was left wondering where he would sleep that night.

Since both Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan were sleeping in the car, with the car gone, their beds essentially left too, meaning they’d have to find another place to sleep that night.

However, that wasn’t too much of a problem; they could still sleep in other rescue vehicles, although the conditions weren’t as good as Chen Xin’s car. It was just a temporary inconvenience for a night, and everything would be fine once Han Ping returned with the car.

The main issue was that Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan were two women, who needed extra care.

Therefore, Chen Xin wasn’t overly worried about it. He mentioned it to the leader, asking him to arrange accommodations for the night, and then walked into the rescue tent to see if there was anything he could help with.

Inside the emergency station, there were still many patients undergoing treatment in front of the doctors. Several SWAT officers, wearing exoskeletons, were maintaining order in the tent, their bodies flashing the distinct red and blue lights typical of police.

Compared to when Chen Xin previously observed people causing trouble, things seemed to have improved quite a bit. The patients who came for treatment were mostly stable emotionally, and their eyes were full of expectation when looking at the doctors.

Even those who needed surgery or amputations could generally accept it calmly under the doctor’s persuasion.

This change surprised Chen Xin a little, but upon reflection, he could understand. After all, only a few individuals would cause trouble; most people still desire to survive and wouldn’t kick up a fuss needlessly.

Chen Xin was completely unfamiliar with medicine, so naturally, he couldn’t assist the doctors. He walked through the triage area and went to the back wards.

The wards were a bit emptier compared to before; at least Chen Xin saw a few empty beds upon entering, which was a marked improvement over the previously overcrowded situation.

"Head nurse, is there anything I can help with?" Chen Xin approached the head nurse in the wards, seeing her conducting an examination on a patient, and waited until she finished to inquire if there was anything he could assist with.

The head nurse, somewhat dazed from being busy, paused at Chen Xin’s appearance but quickly thought and said to him, "Could you help with moving the oxygen cylinders from the vehicles? We’ve got all girls here; they can’t manage those, and the drivers have driven off. I was just worrying about not having anyone to help!"

Catching the head nurse’s words, Chen Xin asked her, "Where are the oxygen cylinders?"

The head nurse hurriedly called over a junior nurse to lead Chen Xin to move the oxygen cylinders.

Leaving the wards with the junior nurse, and arriving near a vehicle parked around the area, she opened the car door, pointed at the blue oxygen cylinders inside, and said to Chen Xin, "The cylinders are in the vehicle; please move two down and deliver them to the ward over there."

Chen Xin nodded, signaling the junior nurse to stand aside, then climbed into the vehicle and carefully moved a person-high oxygen cylinder from inside the compartment to the vehicle door, finally embedding it carefully in the snow with the nurse’s help.

The oxygen cylinder was quite heavy, and filled with liquid oxygen, it can’t be horizontally placed. All it could do was stand upright in the nearby snow.

After moving another oxygen cylinder down, Chen Xin asked the junior nurse, "Weren’t you issued exoskeletons when you arrived? Why aren’t they in use?"

"We don’t know how to use those; only a few drivers were trained before coming here. We were afraid of accidents, so we didn’t dare use them," the junior nurse explained to Chen Xin and thanked him, saying, "Thanks for your help; if it weren’t for you, handling these oxygen cylinders would’ve been tough for us."

An oxygen cylinder is indeed heavy. Typically, moving it involves tilting it on the ground and rolling it, making it easier to push along. But lifting it, a filled oxygen cylinder would be challenging for fewer than two adults to manage.

Luckily for Chen Xin, with the aid of an exoskeleton, these seventy-kilogram cylinders were still manageable to carry.

Holding an oxygen cylinder in his arms, Chen Xin suggested to the junior nurse, "Let’s do it in two trips; moving both at once isn’t ideal. Is it to the tent up front?"

"Yes, just deliver it there; thank you so much," said the junior nurse, watching Chen Xin easily lift the cylinder, and feeling a wave of envy. After thinking a moment, she asked Chen Xin, "Can you teach me how to use the exoskeleton?"

"Of course! It’s not complicated; you’ll learn quickly. If you all could use them yourselves, it would make many tasks much easier," Chen Xin readily agreed to the nurse’s request.

After moving the oxygen cylinders into the wards and placing them properly, Chen Xin did not leave but rather stayed to assist the junior nurse with chores.

There was plenty to do in the wards: assigning beds to new patients, moving beds for post-operative patients, transporting and integrating medical equipment...

With just a few junior nurses on the ward, it was indeed insufficient to handle everything. Even with Chen Xin’s help, everyone was busy with no moments to spare.

For these junior nurses, who had arrived from the city after a several-hour car journey in the morning, and immediately jumped into the thick of work in the afternoon, it was indeed a grueling day.

By the time dinner came around and most of the patients were settled, they finally found a moment for a brief rest.

At this time, the earlier junior nurse sought out Chen Xin, hoping to use this downtime to learn how to operate the exoskeleton.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 346 - 244 Teaching (Seeking Monthly Tickets!)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"The Exoskeleton is a supportive mechanism. It’s not really troublesome to use because it’s designed to assist you, not for you to operate. When using it, you just need to adapt to the assistance it provides." Helping the young nurse to attach a set of Exoskeleton to her Protective Suit, Chen Xin instructed her on how to get used to the Exoskeleton.

Wearing the Exoskeleton for the first time, the young nurse couldn’t help but feel a bit nervous. The fatigue from working all day made her eyelids droopy, making it hard for her to concentrate. Yet, she listened to Chen Xin’s explanation earnestly, earnestly trying to learn how to use the Exoskeleton.

Chen Xin noticed the young nurse’s nervousness and merely smiled, soothing her emotions. He then brought out a pre-prepared toolbox and said to her, "The Exoskeleton mainly plays a supporting and assisting role. Simply put, it allows you to lift heavy objects more easily.

For example, this box in my hand. Normally, lifting it might require some effort, and it might feel a bit heavy. But with the Exoskeleton, you’ll feel it’s quite easy to lift."

As he spoke, Chen Xin gestured for the young nurse to raise her arm and then handed the toolbox to her.

The young nurse initially felt the toolbox might have some weight, but when she received it, she realized she could easily lift it. She clearly felt that the Exoskeleton she wore provided a leverage point, making her hand feel like it had extra support.

"It’s amazing, it feels like someone’s holding my hand at the wrist!" The young nurse excitedly described her feeling to Chen Xin. This sensation was novel to her, making her feel very intrigued.

With a smile, Chen Xin nodded and explained to the young nurse, "That’s the support and assistance function of the Exoskeleton. It gives you a new leverage point and also provides some support itself. The toolbox isn’t too heavy, so you might not feel it clearly. If it were an oxygen tank like before, you’d sense the leverage much more evidently."

Hearing Chen Xin’s explanation, the young nurse couldn’t help but turn her gaze toward the oxygen tank placed aside.

It was brought over by Chen Xin in the afternoon. Since it was not used immediately, it was placed aside. Chen Xin’s words made the young nurse eager to experience what the leverage he talked about felt like.

Seeing this, Chen Xin didn’t stop her; he just signaled her to be careful, while he himself held the oxygen tank at the side to prevent any accidents due to mishandling.

"When carrying heavy objects with the Exoskeleton, be sure not to use too much force. As the Exoskeleton operates on force feedback, the more force you use, the more force the Exoskeleton provides. Although the civilian version of the Exoskeleton you’re using won’t exert much large force, it wouldn’t be enough to fling the oxygen tank somewhere, but it might strain your back," said Chen Xin as he gently patted the nurse’s back, indicating that she should pay attention to the exertion from her back. "This is the most common issue when using the Exoskeleton. Initially, due to unfamiliarity with the leverage, excessive force leads to muscle strain."

"I understand, I’ll be careful," replied the young nurse, heeding Chen Xin’s advice. She dialed back her exertion as she bent down to grab the oxygen tank.

However, it’s difficult to change one’s habits in a short time. As she lifted the oxygen tank upward, she applied slightly too much force, pulling the oxygen tank up like pulling a carrot, losing her balance and stumbling backward two steps, almost falling with the oxygen tank in her arms.

Chen Xin quickly supported her shoulder from behind, helping her regain her balance.

The young nurse was also startled by this, regaining her composure before placing the oxygen tank down again, patting her chest and saying, "I’m sorry, I used too much force! That was a bit scary!"

"It’s alright. It’s natural for the first attempt to be a bit off-balance if you haven’t adjusted. Don’t worry too much; you’ll adapt," comforted Chen Xin, finding nothing unusual about it.

The young nurse quickly adjusted herself, calming her emotions before starting the attempt again under Chen Xin’s guidance.

This time, she encountered no problems, effortlessly lifting the oxygen tank as Chen Xin had previously demonstrated.

"Well done, it seems you’ve got the hang of it," Chen Xin said, casting a look of approval at the young nurse as she successfully lifted the oxygen tank.

"It’s all thanks to your excellent teaching, Mr. Chen. Thank you so much!" The young nurse said sincerely, setting down the oxygen tank and expressing her gratitude towards Chen Xin earnestly.

"It’s just a small thing, no need to be so formal," Chen Xin replied modestly, reminding the young nurse, "The Exoskeleton isn’t difficult to use once you grasp the key. But I still suggest you practice more. Carrying heavy things like oxygen tanks is fine, but when it comes to moving patients, poor control over force could cause injury. It’s better to practice more."

"Alright, I’ll practice more. Thank you, Mr. Chen." The young nurse expressed her gratitude sincerely once more and even boldly gave Chen Xin a hug.

Being caught off guard by the nurse’s sudden embrace, Chen Xin stepped back, trying to pull away. However, the Exoskeleton she wore provided her with some momentum, preventing him from pushing her away immediately.

Just then, a shout came from behind Chen Xin, "What are you doing?!"

Chen Xin quickly turned around and saw Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan just coming back together.

Startled by the shout, the young nurse quickly released Chen Xin, awkwardly saying, "Mr. Chen, I won’t disturb you anymore, I’ll take my leave now."

With that said, the young nurse hurriedly ran away.

Seeing the young nurse run off, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan approached, both of them looked Chen Xin up and down, with Qin Lan somewhat hostile in her tone, "What were you just doing? Why were you hugging? Who was that woman?"

"A-Lan, Dragon Lady, you both are back?" Seeing the unfriendly expressions on Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan’s faces, Chen Xin quickly explained, "She’s a nurse from the rescue team. She asked me to teach her how to use the Exoskeleton, and she only hugged me just now because she fell, and I went to support her."

"Really?" Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin doubtfully, her eyes full of disbelief.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 347 - 245 Support (Third update asking for monthly votes)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When Chen Xin woke up the next day, Han Ping had already rushed back overnight with supplies from the city center.

Although driving at night is indeed dangerous, considering there’s no sunlight at all now and visibility is the same, this point is completely negligible.

However, Han Ping drove back and forth without rest all night, which was really quite exhausting. When Chen Xin saw him, the sleepiness on his face was undeniable.

"You’ve worked hard, Han Ping. Go wash your face and catch some sleep." Chen Xin, having just woken up, saw Han Ping looking like this and urged him to take a rest.

Han Ping indeed couldn’t hold on any longer. Although the round trip was less than a hundred kilometers, it took him the entire night to go back and forth. He only rested a bit while waiting in the city for the hall to gather supplies. Hearing Chen Xin ask him to rest, he said nothing more and just nodded before going to the car to sleep.

Seeing Han Ping going to rest, Chen Xin then approached the captain who was receiving and inventorying the supplies, and asked, "What supplies did the city hall send over? Looks like quite a lot of stuff!"

The rescue team brought two supply transport vehicles with them, and before going back for aid the previous day, they had already unloaded all the supplies from the vehicle. Now the returning vehicles were packed not only with essential supplies for the rescue team but also with two additional regular freight trucks filled with supplies parked here.

At this moment, all four vehicles were unloading, leaving heaps of supplies in front of Chen Xin.

The captain was also delighted by this, excitedly introducing to Chen Xin, "The city hall allocated two medical tents, with accompanying medical equipment all brought along, and there’s an extra spare tent. Although it doesn’t come with medical equipment, there are enough folding beds and heaters. With these, we can set up two more intensive care units and one mild symptomatic ward, which means the rescue station can accommodate more people!"

"That’s really great! It’s just that..." Chen Xin naturally felt quite happy for this, but recalling the busy nurses he saw while helping in the ward yesterday, he hesitated a bit before asking the captain, "If the number of patients increases, can the rescue team handle it? We didn’t bring many medical staff; won’t they struggle to manage?"

The captain nodded, affirming Chen Xin’s concerns but explained without worry, "No need to worry about that. Lin City Hall has already helped to gather volunteers. A group of volunteers will be coming to help today. They can share quite a bit of the medical staff’s work without a problem."

Upon hearing the captain say this, Chen Xin, although relieved by the increase in manpower, still hesitated a bit. After all, not just anyone could replace the work medical staff do. Volunteers could only take on physical tasks and some basic medical work, and might not be able to alleviate much.

However, having help is naturally better than none, so Chen Xin naturally wouldn’t say much more about this. He just remembered the matter of the young nurse wanting to learn how to use the Exoskeleton from him yesterday and decided to tell the captain about it: "...That’s how it is. I think we should take some time to train everyone in the rescue team on using the Exoskeleton. At least when it’s needed, someone will know how to use it. We can’t have a situation where it’s needed, yet no one knows how to operate it."

"That’s indeed an oversight on my part. I’ll make arrangements today. Chen Xin, do you think you could teach this, or should we have someone else do it?" The captain realized he’d overlooked this matter and was considering who should teach it.

To the captain, Chen Xin seemed like a suitable candidate. Although he was also part of the rescue team, he wasn’t a medical staff member, so relatively speaking, he wasn’t that busy. Assigning him to teach the rescue team’s medical staff how to use the Exoskeleton didn’t seem like a troublesome task in the captain’s eyes.

However, Chen Xin rejected this responsibility.

"I’m afraid I don’t have the time. Yesterday I checked, and the medical tents are still a bit too rudimentary. Even with heating equipment inside, the temperature is very low, which isn’t conducive for patients’ recovery. So I plan to modify the tents a bit." Chen Xin shared his thoughts with the captain, hoping he could coordinate with Lin City’s staff to get the materials needed for the modification.

The captain knew beforehand that Chen Xin had the ability to modify things. After all, Chen Xin was considered a precious national talent. His safety and whereabouts had extensive implications, something the rescue team captain was definitely aware of.

Therefore, hearing that Chen Xin wanted to modify the medical tents to improve their insulation effect was something the captain was naturally happy to see.

However, he still had some concerns, so he asked Chen Xin, "Mr. Chen, what parts of the tent will you be modifying? Will it affect the patients inside? What materials will you need for the modification? I spoke with Lin City’s staff yesterday, and their resources are pretty tight now, so they might not be able to spare any materials."

Currently, Lin City’s resources are in a somewhat polarized state. Supplies like food and daily necessities were brought based on Lin City’s previous population count, but due to the blizzard, the population in Lin City suddenly decreased quite a bit. So, in this regard, there is actually a surplus of supplies.

Yet, apart from these, the coal used for heating is just sufficient since although one refuge center is gone, there are still many people needing warmth, and other refuges have to crank up their boiler power somewhat.

As for medicine and other supplies, they are evidently more strained because previously, railway capacities were prioritized for transporting crucial supplies like coal, leaving insufficient logistics capacity for other materials.

After the captain’s explanation, Chen Xin understood this point as well. While nodding, he thought about it. Although the system’s functions were powerful and those who saw it would automatically understand, Chen Xin didn’t want to draw too much attention. So he said to the captain, "In that case, give me the set of spare medical tents. I’ll use that spare tent for modification, and once it’s done, you can transfer patients to that tent while I modify the other tents."

"That works. Let’s do that! When the volunteers arrive, I’ll assign a few people to help you. As for the materials needed, I’ll ask Lin City’s staff. If they don’t have it, then I’ll have to trouble you, Mr. Chen, to find another way." The captain, after a bit of consideration, agreed to Chen Xin’s request.

Seeing the captain agree, Chen Xin didn’t see the potential shortage of materials in Lin City as a big deal, simply smiling and saying, "Don’t worry, even if they can’t gather the materials, I have other ways. It’ll just be a bit more troublesome, that’s all."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 348 - 246 Setting Up a Tent (Request for Monthly Tickets)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The number of volunteers assigned to the rescue team by Lin City was quite large. After Chen Xin finished breakfast, a volunteer team of around fifty people was led by Lin City staff to the rescue station to help.

These volunteers were immediately assigned work upon arrival, assisting in maintaining order in the admission area, helping patients with consultations, and aiding in the care of critically ill patients in the wards... I have to say, with this batch of volunteers, the rescue team’s work has indeed become much easier, allowing medical staff to better focus their energy on treating patients.

The captain, as promised, sent five volunteers to assist Chen Xin in modifying the spare tent.

"Hello, we are the volunteers here to help. Could you tell us what we need to do?" The five volunteers approached Chen Xin, looking at him wearing his Power Armor today, with eyes full of admiration and a slight hint of fear.

Power Armor, something formerly seen only in science fiction works, naturally evokes envious feelings. These five volunteers were all young, and their eyes were even more covetous of the Power Armor, as it’s not something one usually gets to see.

However, the mechanical arm on Chen Xin’s back was somewhat intimidating, coupled with an unknown fear of the Power Armor, feeling some apprehension was not surprising.

Chen Xin looked at them and smiled, saying, "Why don’t you start by helping set up this tent? I need to make some cold weather modifications to it."

"Alright, what do we need to do?" The volunteers did not refuse, approaching Chen Xin to inquire about their tasks.

"These white bags are tents. I’ll choose the location; you just need to spread them out and use the air pump over there to inflate them, which will set up the tents." Chen Xin pointed to several white bags at his feet, explaining to them, "These tents are easy to set up, but later we’ll need to install lighting and arrange beds inside, and I appreciate your hard work."

"It’s just what we should be doing; you’re helping save lives, and we’re just doing some physical labor, it’s nothing." The volunteers all expressed humility and politeness.

Seeing this, Chen Xin didn’t say much more, instructing the five individuals to unfold the tents and spread them out in the chosen locations, then use the prepared air pump for inflation.

These tents are supported by air columns inside, just needing full inflation to erect the whole tent, making them a convenient temporary setup. With the help of the five volunteers, the first tent was set up in just over ten minutes.

However, this can’t be considered a fully set-up tent; besides needing to continue inflating to maintain the strength of the air columns, and ensuring the tent’s support, ground stakes are required for fixation. Also, lighting tubes for illumination, heating stoves, and matching beds need to be arranged.

Chen Xin sent two volunteers to fix the ground stakes, while he and another volunteer installed the lighting tubes that would be fixed to the tent’s top. The remaining two volunteers were dispatched by Chen Xin to transport beds and heaters.

The interior space of the tent isn’t low; a typical person might need a stool to install the lighting tubes, but Chen Xin could use the mechanical arm for assistance.

Watching Chen Xin fixing the lighting tubes into pre-designated positions on the tent’s top, a nearby volunteer suddenly got curious and asked, "Bro, is your Power Armor issued by the government? Does our country have such a great thing? How can someone get a set of this? Are you from a special unit?"

"You mean this? I’m not really sure. If you want to know, you should ask the relevant departments." Chen Xin replied vaguely, without mentioning that the Power Armor was made by himself.

Although he fully understood the volunteer’s curiosity and envy regarding the Power Armor, some matters didn’t need to be casually discussed everywhere.

The volunteer heard Chen Xin’s words and assumed he needed to keep it confidential, so he didn’t ask further, but looked at the Power Armor with even more admiration.

A group of them worked diligently for over half an hour. With the help of the five volunteers, the set of spare medical tents was finally all set up.

This set of spare tents was of the same specifications as the ones currently serving as the rescue station, except it lacked the matching medical equipment and personnel; everything else was the same. Once medical staff and equipment were secured, it would become a second rescue station.

However, due to limited medical staff within the rescue team, even with the volunteers who came to support, it was only enough to use the second set of already set-up medical tents as new wards, lacking enough doctors to open a second rescue station.

As for the medical tents themselves, their cold-weather effectiveness wasn’t bad. According to the technical parameters on the manual Chen Xin found, the tents could withstand temperatures ranging from -40℃ to 65℃, and still function optimally even under harsh cold weather conditions.

However, being able to withstand a -40℃ environment isn’t sufficient for the current situation, thus requiring a cold weather upgrade and modification.

The tents mainly rely on space between their inner and outer layers for insulation to maintain warmth in this way.

It’s similar to how double-glazed glass is more cold-resistant than single-pane—different mediums have different heat conduction efficiencies, and creating space between two insulating materials prevents direct heat transfer to the outer layer, thereby achieving good thermal insulation.

But the effect of this approach is quite limited, so the medical tents are equipped with stoves for heating, maintaining the tent’s internal temperature at a relatively not-so-cold level.

In addition, a very important element for the tent is the ground mat. Without an effective insulating mat to prevent heat from dissipating downward, it’s still difficult to maintain the tent’s internal temperature.

Likewise, when Chen Xin planned to upgrade and modify the tent’s insulation efficiency, he focused mainly on these two aspects.

Chen Xin had previously inspected that the material of this spare medical tent and the ones already set up were both PVC airtight fabric, achieving insulation through the extra cold-insulating cotton layer and the interlayer space between the inner and outer fabric.

To improve insulation efficiency, the first choice is naturally to update the materials. Replacing the tent fabric and ground mat with materials that offer better insulation will naturally enhance the tent’s insulation efficiency.

The other choice is to improve the tent’s structure, blocking heat conduction paths and minimizing heat loss, thereby achieving insulation effects.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 349 - 247: Transformation Choices (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

There isn’t much room for modification structurally, as the tent itself is meant to contact the air. Additionally, this is a tent made of soft materials, making it impossible to create a vacuum layer or fill it with rigid high-performance insulation materials between the outer and inner fabrics to make an insulation layer.

The traditional double-layer design is already a reasonable structure, even for expeditions on Mount Everest and in the North and South Poles, this double-layer tent is adequate.

All Chen Xin could do was add an inflatable insulation layer to the tent’s base to prevent heat from dissipating to the ground, and add a small porch with a two-layer curtain at the entrance, preventing cold air from entering the tent directly when someone opens the curtain, thus reducing heat loss indoors.

For large tents like medical tents, the tent door and floor are the main channels of heat loss. As long as these two areas are insulated, the tent can achieve a decent thermal retention effect.

Beyond this, the improvement comes in terms of materials.

Due to cost issues, large tents use PVC airtight fabric. As previously mentioned, PVC materials tolerate temperatures between -40°C and 65°C. Beyond this range, the material properties change, and it loses its original performance.

For small tents, manufacturers are less concerned about cost, so they can use PU and PE materials with better performance as coatings for the tent fabric, providing better protection for the tent.

Among these, PU material, polyurethane, is currently the best insulating material internationally. Chen Xin and his survival group friends used polyurethane coatings as insulation material when they built their shelter.

Depending on the material formula, it can withstand temperatures from -50°C to 150°C, and TPEE thermoplastic polyester elastomer, also known as polyester rubber, performs even better, tolerating temperature ranges from -70°C to 200°C, making it an excellent insulation material today.

If possible, Chen Xin would naturally want to use this material to upgrade the current medical tent. However, it is unfortunate these high-performance insulation materials are scarce resources highly demanded by the nation. Despite having a decent production, Lin City doesn’t have much stock.

Faced with this situation, all Chen Xin could do was consider using some existing materials to make do.

The captain has put in effort to help Chen Xin with the tent modification, arranging Lin City’s staff to coordinate the material requirements he needs.

PU materials, being high-performance insulation materials, are essential resources for Lin City, unlikely to be spared for Chen Xin’s tent improvement.

However, the staff could provide Chen Xin with some insulating felt.

"Insulating felt? This can make do, right? So how much can you get me?" Chen Xin asked upon hearing the staff mention only insulating felt. Although it was far from the materials he desired, given the resource scarcity, he had no choice but to use what was available.

Seeing Chen Xin not despising the insulating felt, the staff quickly said, "This was initially ordered for building greenhouse tents before the city experienced the meteor impact, which prevented the construction. Quite a lot of the ordered insulating felt was left over. If you need it, I can arrange a batch for you."

"Alright, get a few rolls first. Covering the tent with it can at least enhance the warmth," Chen Xin said, as this was all he could suggest.

While the staff arranged for the insulating felt to be delivered, Chen Xin wasn’t idle. He returned to the already pitched tent to see what could be improved.

Inside the tent, five volunteers allocated to him were gathered around a heater.

Unlike Chen Xin and the rescue team members who had Protective Suits, they only wore ordinary winter clothing, sitting around the heater, roasting their snow-drenched shoes, causing an unpleasant smell inside the tent.

Though Chen Xin wore Power Armor, with its air filtration system preventing him from smelling it, he could still imagine what the smell was like.

Obviously reminding him, besides enhancing the tent’s insulation, the heater could be improved to provide more heat.

This is clearly Chen Xin’s old expertise, and now he even has a versatile mechanical arm, not requiring a system upgrade for the heater’s modification.

Taking an unused heater beside him, Chen Xin clumsily maneuvered the Power Armor to sit on the ground.

Though this suit of Power Armor wasn’t exaggerated like those weighing one to two tons in sci-fi works, it still weighed several hundred kilograms, making it unsuitable to sit on a temporary sickbed beside him for fear of breaking it. Thus he could only sit on the ground.

Fortunately, the initial design of the joints considered flexibility, so sitting on the ground wasn’t a problem.

Using the mechanical arm to disassemble the heater, while using the scanning equipment on the mechanical arm for an overall scan of the heater, Chen Xin looked at the scan results projected on the helmet screen, contemplating how to proceed with modifications.

In the tent, there were four heaters in total. Considering safety issues, all were electric heaters powered by an external source, the source being the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery from Chen Xin’s car yesterday.

Given the output power of one Isotope Thermoelectric Battery, supplying power to several tents is entirely sufficient.

Therefore, without worrying about electricity consumption, the simplest and most direct way to modify the heater is to increase power output directly. Though brute force solutions like this are wasteful, many times problems can be solved this way.

Considering the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery provides stable power output for ten years, amplifying the power is indeed the simplest choice now.

Chen Xin briefly considered and chose this approach.

However, this requires optimizing the heater’s circuit to prevent burning out or short-circuiting due to excessive power.

Controlling the mechanical arm for further disassembling the heater, Chen Xin decided to try modifying the heater without the system upgrade this time. Having a versatile mechanical arm, a top tool, he didn’t think he couldn’t handle a heater.

However, after disassembling the heater into a pile of parts, Chen Xin discovered an embarrassingly inconvenient truth.

That is, without raw materials, even with a mechanical arm capable of 3D printing parts and performing various mechanical maneuvers, Chen Xin couldn’t complete the heater’s modification out of thin air.

```

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 350 - 248 Electromagnetic Metallurgy Furnace (Third Update Requesting Monthly Ticket)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The embarrassment of lacking raw materials indeed troubled Chen Xin for a little while, but he quickly freed himself from this trouble, temporarily gathering the dismantled heater parts together and setting them aside. Chen Xin told the volunteers who were warming themselves by the fire that he was going out for a bit, then walked out of the tent.

The shortage of raw materials is a difficulty Chen Xin faces, and it’s also one of the problems this city is currently dealing with.

Therefore, Chen Xin decided not to seek help from the staff of Lin City to get some raw materials for the transformation, but rather planned to search the city to collect usable items.

Although Chen Xin believed that getting enough materials from the staff to transform the heater wouldn’t be a problem, considering the current shortage of supplies in Lin City, he felt it wasn’t necessary to do so.

After all, with the Power Armor and the mechanical arm, finding some materials was an easy task for him.

The rescue station is located next to a shelter in Lin City, which used to be a bustling commercial area. Although most of the surrounding buildings were buried in snow, quite a few things could still be found inside the buildings.

Chen Xin found a building not yet completely buried in snow and climbed in through a window exposed above the ground.

A considerable layer of snow had accumulated inside the building, preventing Chen Xin from standing up straight after entering; he could only half-crouch on the ground.

He cleared away some snow with the mechanical arm, barely lifted his head, and made room for himself.

"Such a hassle... But luckily, just as I expected, there are still things here." Complaining slightly, Chen Xin raised his hand to catch the metal material cut from the ceiling using the mechanical arm, feeling quite satisfied with his find.

As Chen Xin anticipated, although people had three months before the disaster to prepare and gather supplies, most of the items found in the buildings had been taken, but the materials contained within the buildings themselves had not.

The simplest example is the ceiling panels Chen Xin was cutting.

This place seemed to have been a shopping mall, with panels on the top floor ceiling used to hide the ventilation ducts and unsightly electrical lines.

This ceiling panel was built with an aluminum alloy frame. It appeared old, with a thick layer of dust inside, the metal parts between the panels looking rusted, and some had even become deformed due to long-term disrepair.

However, this did not hinder Chen Xin from recycling and repurposing them. He disassembled them piece by piece using the mechanical arm, performed some simple cutting, cut them into suitable lengths, and then twisted them together for easy carrying.

These disassembled aluminum alloy parts would have been sold as scrap waste in the past, without any significant use.

But for Chen Xin now, he can completely recycle and repurpose these scraps.

As long as these materials get recycled, extracting the useful parts, and then using the 3D printing function of the mechanical arm to transform several heaters is really no trouble at all.

Of course, these materials are merely additional; they’re not the main things Chen Xin came to find.

Cutting open chunks of ceiling panels, he cut the ventilation ducts behind the panels into sections, and after dismantling most of the ceiling within his sight, Chen Xin finally found what he was looking for.

The fan coil unit of the central air conditioning.

This piece of end equipment of the central air conditioning contains a small fan, motor, and air heat exchanger, and it’s exactly what Chen Xin was primarily trying to find this time.

Such units are typically installed discreetly in ceilings or other positions, so even if most of the materials in the building had been recycled and dismantled by people, few would think to dismantle these.

Chen Xin bundled the previously twisted metal strips and cut-down ventilation ducts together into a roll, then raised the mechanical arm to dismantle the fan coil unit.

Based on Chen Xin’s estimation, dismantling one unit should be sufficient to transform those four heaters and still have leftovers.

Yet while dismantling the fan coil unit, Chen Xin couldn’t help but chuckle to himself; he seemed particularly inclined to dismantle air conditioners. In the past, he dismantled outdoor units for ground source heat pumps; now, he dismantles central air conditioning fan coils to retrofit heaters, appearing to always be at odds with air conditioners.

This actually makes good sense, as air conditioners themselves are equipment manufactured using significant quantities of metal materials, and due to their working principles, they contain many components with considerable technological content.

Plus, in this dystopian world, no one would have thought during the initial gathering of supplies to dismantle two air conditioners for recycling purposes. Naturally, such things, containing large amounts of metal material and capable of being disassembled into various components, became the most suitable recycling sources for materials.

Chen Xin didn’t think much of it, and after dismantling the fan coil unit, he embraced it, along with the previously bundled metal materials, and hauled them out of the abandoned building.

Using the mechanical arm to lift the metal materials, Chen Xin turned around and returned to the rescue station.

When Chen Xin returned to the rescue station, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan happened to come looking for him. Seeing Chen Xin carrying a heap of scrap metal, both couldn’t help but be quite curious.

"Xin, did you just... come back from scavenging?" Qin Lan surveyed Chen Xin, finding only this description plausible.

However, Chen Xin nodded, affirming, "I plan to retrofit the heaters. Not having the right materials at hand, I went collecting some."

Hearing this, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan felt somewhat speechless but didn’t say much more, merely asking him, "Is there anything we can help with?"

"No..." Chen Xin initially wanted to say he didn’t need any help but reconsidered and swallowed his words, then said, "Actually, yes, I just happen to need your help with something, come along."

Saying this, Chen Xin held the materials he brought back and headed toward his car.

Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan quickly followed behind Chen Xin.

Once beside the car, Chen Xin tossed the materials he brought back onto the ground, then asked Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan, "Lan, Dragon girl, have you ever heard of electromagnetic metallurgy?"

"Electromagnetic metallurgy? What’s that?" Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan exchanged glances, seeing confusion in each other’s eyes.

Seeing they didn’t understand, Chen Xin explained to them, "Electromagnetic metallurgy, simply put, is a technique that utilizes the electromagnetic force formed by current and magnetic fields to control and influence the metal smelting process."

"Why mention this? We don’t get it! Just say what you want to do and how you want us to help!" Mo Qingyan didn’t like Chen Xin’s cryptic manner, directly asking about his intentions.

Chen Xin raised an indifferent eyebrow, then said, "I plan to build an electromagnetic metallurgy furnace to use electromagnetic metallurgy for recycling and repurposing this scrap metal into usable metal. I need you to help me build this furnace."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 351 - 249: Arc Melting Iron (Happy Little New Year, requesting a monthly ticket)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan couldn’t quite grasp what Chen Xin meant by electromagnetic metallurgy, but since Chen Xin asked for their help, they naturally wouldn’t refuse and followed his instructions on the tasks he assigned them.

"Qingyan, these metal rods are already wrapped with copper wire, take them to Axin." Qin Lan handed several metal rods wrapped with copper wire to Mo Qingyan, instructing her to take them to Chen Xin.

Mo Qingyan took the iron rods passed by Qin Lan, nodded, and walked towards Chen Xin.

At this moment, Chen Xin was working on a rather peculiar-looking machine, with several metal rods wrapped with copper wire connected to a circular turntable, resembling an upside-down tripod stool that could rotate.

Mo Qingyan handed the metal rods she brought to Chen Xin, glanced at what he was making, and asked, "Axin, is there anything else you need help with? Is it really sufficient to just wrap copper wire around these few iron rods?"

"That’s enough, I can handle the other parts myself, and this machine itself isn’t complicated." Chen Xin smiled at Mo Qingyan, indicating that it was sufficient.

Using a mechanical arm, Chen Xin welded the metal rods brought by Mo Qingyan onto another turntable, then assembled the two turntables and another mechanical arm together.

Mo Qingyan watched as Chen Xin finished assembling the item, thinking it looked much like an egg beater, which made her somewhat curious about how it operated.

Even though Mo Qingyan voiced her curiosity to Chen Xin, she wasn’t expecting an answer, as she assumed she wouldn’t understand his explanation even if he provided one.

"This device uses these metal rods as electrodes to discharge onto the metal materials placed in the center, using the arc produced by the discharge to melt the metal materials." As Chen Xin assembled the machine, he spun the metal rods on the turntable, which looked like chicken claws, introducing the setup to Mo Qingyan: "As long as the current is strong enough and the arc’s temperature is high enough, anything can be melted."

"(⊙o⊙)..." Mo Qingyan indicated that she didn’t understand what Chen Xin was talking about.

Chen Xin wasn’t annoyed, simply smiled and said, "It’s okay if you don’t understand, just know that I can use this machine to remelt recycled scrap metal into new materials."

"Oh, so that’s how it works!" Mo Qingyan showed a face displaying "I totally understand now."

Chen Xin could tell that Mo Qingyan actually didn’t understand at all.

However, he didn’t mind, once the machine was assembled, he connected it to another Isotope Thermoelectric Battery he had removed from his vehicle, planning to use it for power supply.

Currently, one of the car’s three batteries was removed to power the rescue station, one was used here, and the car only had one battery left for power. Unless Chen Xin reinstalled the battery, the car wasn’t capable of driving for now.

This didn’t significantly affect Chen Xin, as he only had residential needs for his car now, not driving needs, so there was no immediate necessity to drive. Even if there was a need, temporarily making a battery or reinstalling the battery wouldn’t take much effort or delay anything.

Of course, the setup before them was fairly rudimentary; to make it functional, Chen Xin had to invest some survival points into it.

The investment of survival points transformed the machine, making it appear newly upgraded and more sci-fi, like a real deal. Simply put, it shifted from a green style to a NEC aesthetic.

The overall design of the machine didn’t change much, as it still featured a pillar connecting two circular turntables with metal rods protruding from them.

The bottom turntable resembled a large tray, surrounded by three vertical metal rods that looked like chicken claws, whereas the top slightly differed with the metal rods turning black, seeming more like a plug.

After upgrading, Chen Xin was quite satisfied with the machine, deciding to test its effectiveness.

Placing some scrap metal onto the bottom turntable, after signaling Mo Qingyan to step back, Chen Xin pressed the machine’s start button.

Upon power supply engagement, the three metal rods at the base began to rotate, and as the speed increased and the current intensified, the scrap metal on the tray surrounded by the metal rods gradually started vibrating and then levitated.

"Those three metal rods are actually electromagnets. Their rapid rotation forms a magnetic field, thus lifting those scrap metals." Chen Xin explained to Mo Qingyan and Qin Lan, who had arrived at some point, while observing that the metal rods’ speed was stable enough to keep the scrap metal levitating. Following this, he pressed the second button.

The plug at the machine’s top lowered slowly, its metal rods touching the floating scrap metal in the center, then began discharging.

Bright arcs were released from the electrodes at the metal rods’ ends, striking the suspended scrap metal, creating snapping sounds and intense sparks which made both Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan unable to resist shielding their eyes with their hands, while Chen Xin had already lowered the shading mask on his face shield.

As the arcs crackled, the scrap metal bound by the magnetic field started to melt gradually, and the sparks released from the electrodes grew increasingly intense, resembling welding operations.

Qin Lan’s Power Armor also had a shading mask, so she lowered it to clearly see the scrap metal in the machine transforming into a mass of orange-red liquid under the arc’s effect.

The melted liquid metal emitted massive heat, causing the surrounding snow to melt, generating copious steam which immediately sublimed into water vapor due to the high temperature.

Although they had melted into a mass of liquid, the liquid was still held by the magnetic field, suspended in the machine without falling.

"A-Lan, what’s happening exactly?" Mo Qingyan, whose Protective Suit helmet lacked a shading mask, could only shield her eyes with one hand while turning her head to inquire Qin Lan.

"I don’t know either; it seems like after Axin started the machine, many sparks appeared, causing the scrap metal to melt. Now it seems impurities are dripping off." Qin Lan adjusted the aiming scope on her helmet, switching visual modes to clearly see black impurities continuously surfacing on the molten metal surface and then falling to the tray below.

At this point, Chen Xin wasn’t keen on answering the doubts of Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan. With the machine’s operation, Chen Xin was fully engrossed in observing the machine, focusing on the orange-red liquid metal, continuously monitoring its condition with a scanning device on the mechanical arm.

"The machine is somewhat simplistic; it can’t control temperature, and impurity discharge is ineffective. It seems improvement is necessary." Watching the liquid metal gradually exhibit layering, Chen Xin felt somewhat dissatisfied.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 352 - 250: Get the Train Station Running (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Through arc smelting, the recovered scrap metal was transformed into new metal materials. These were then processed into the necessary parts using a mechanical arm, and Chen Xin finally completed the modification of several heaters inside the tent.

While Chen Xin was conducting arc smelting, workers from Lin City delivered the promised cold-resistant felt, which allowed Chen Xin to upgrade the material of the medical tent.

Although it was a bit of a hassle, completing the medical tent’s modification given the lack of materials made Chen Xin quite satisfied.

After finishing everything, Chen Xin found the captain and informed him that the new medical tent was ready for use.

"Is it ready to use? That’s great news, now we can accommodate more patients." The captain was very pleased and promptly arranged for the rescue team doctors to transfer new patients into the newly modified medical tent.

The workers from Lin City felt very excited about this development too. For Lin City now, other aspects were still manageable, and the city government was striving to improve the situation, actively engaging in post-disaster reconstruction.

Yet on the medical front, the stress from the overwhelming number of patients directly collapsed the medical system in Lin City. Although the arrival of the rescue team brought a slight improvement to the situation, the impact they could make across Lin City was still minuscule.

Having additional beds to accommodate more patients is undoubtedly good news.

While it remains limited in effect, only a small portion of severely ill patients can be admitted to rooms for treatment, but this still represents a precious glimmer of hope for Lin City.

As he watched the captain organize the transfer of new patients to the wards, Chen Xin did not disturb his work but instead pulled aside a worker to ask, "What is the biggest difficulty facing Lin City’s post-disaster reconstruction now?"

Although modifying medical tents and assisting the rescue team in treating patients is crucial, Chen Xin is not a doctor and such work is not his expertise, so he feels he ought to exert himself in areas more suitable for him.

The area where Chen Xin could undoubtedly exert more power is assisting with upgrading and transforming urgently needed resources to address Lin City’s current challenges.

"The lack of medical capacity is our primary issue; besides that, there’s the train station." The worker was unaware of Chen Xin’s purpose but responded with the main issues Lin City faced presently: "You’ve seen the medical problems—too many patients and severely insufficient medical staff, leading to many local patients not receiving effective treatment. Your arrival solved part of the issue, but many people still can’t receive timely and effective treatment.

The train station, on the other hand, is completely buried by snow; even if trains could bring resources in, effectively loading and unloading them is very challenging. This has created significant difficulties for obtaining resources, and even if the warehouse at the station, buried alongside it, has goods unloaded, there’s nowhere appropriate to store them. It’s quite troublesome."

"Haven’t you tried to dig out the train station?" Chen Xin wondered. Knowing what the problem is, why not organize manpower to clear the snow from the station and uncover the train station?

"We’d like to! But the issue is most people are unwell now; we can barely gather people to unload goods, let alone clear snow from the train station!" The worker said with a bitter smile.

Of course, they’d want to clear the platform to let trains enter for unloading, but the problem is they genuinely lack manpower for clearing the train station.

The previous blizzard severely impacted the city, leaving many survivors sick due to the cold, while those who remained healthy were redeployed by the government to maintain supplies, venturing into the railways under storm conditions to load and unload train freight.

This struggle endured for a full month, consuming significant live resources in Lin City—after all, amidst minus seventy degrees conditions, braving wind and snow to transport goods risked frostbite, colds, or even death occurring with high likelihood.

Now although the blizzard has passed, there are very few healthy survivors left in Lin City who haven’t fallen ill.

This scenario resembles that of "Frostpunk," dealing with blizzards; unless cold resistance and heating technologies are maxed out, a blizzard certainly results in an overwhelming number of patients, some of whom will even be doctors ill themselves, mostly tending toward critical condition.

In such times, the limited able workers are naturally dispatched to maintain the city’s resource acquisition, meanwhile letting the medical system operate as fully as possible to restore the sick.

In these conditions, wasting resources to upgrade houses is meaningless, since the blizzard has passed, and the houses’ insulation remains at an acceptable level.

Reality is not a game; in games engineers can both research and act as doctors, immediately constructing hospitals with resources, but in reality, that’s impossible.

A professional medical practitioner requires five years of medical study in university, and those skilled and seasoned need substantial time accumulating clinical experience, making each caregiver incredibly precious.

This is why during disasters and epidemics in Flame Country, medical staff from across the nation were mobilized to support affected areas; indeed, facing suddenly soaring patient numbers can genuinely collapse the medical system.

And the result of a medical system collapse would be an absolute tragedy and disaster.

Currently, Lin City’s medical system has indeed crumbled; though the rescue team aided recovery from the collapse, it has not drastically altered the situation realistically.

Ultimately, this is the rescue team’s utmost capability; unless Chen Xin could upgrade the medical station’s equipment into the medical pods present in sci-fi works, the situation won’t improve drastically.

After all, even receiving treatment, patient recovery requires time, and without sci-fi-like pods where one sleeps inside and emerges healed, Lin City’s predicament won’t easily change significantly.

Even if Chen Xin used survival points to modify medical pods, under present conditions of resource scarcity, he couldn’t produce anything—not relying solely on his partial electromagnetic furnace to recycle scrap iron for resource gathering?

Therefore, to improve Lin City’s condition, Chen Xin believes excavating the train station from the snow and ensuring resources and external support can smoothly enter Lin City is the priority task at hand.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 353 - 251 Evaporation of Snow (Third Update, Seeking Monthly Tickets)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When Chen Xin, Qin Lan, and Mo Qingyan arrived at what used to be the train station of Lin City, they were met with a ravine resembling a canyon and a snowfield completely buried in snow, with almost no trace of the buildings that once stood there.

The ravine was once a railway. Although it was also covered with some snow, trains frequently passed through, leaving not much snow on the tracks, resulting in a gash-like mark in the snowfield.

Before the disaster in Flame Country, Lin City’s train station was classified as a fourth-class station, only one level above the lowest grade stations, which served as passing and waiting stations for trains.

The station itself was just a one-story flat building, even shorter than the surrounding residential houses. Naturally, it was completely buried by snowstorms, with no trace remaining.

After all, it was a small station with only five trains passing by each day before the disaster, so there was no need for a larger space.

Although there were plans to expand the train station and build loading and unloading facilities before the disaster, it was clear that this work was not completed.

Beside the ravine caused by train passages, traces of the previously built loading and unloading facilities were still visible, as Lin City still needed to use this area to load and unload supplies brought by trains.

However, the surrounding snow was over ten meters deep, and standing beside the ravine looking down was akin to looking down from the top of a four-story building, making one reflect on how insignificant human civilization’s creations are in the face of nature’s power.

The snow here was deep, but a large section had been dug out, forming a fairly gentle slope.

Clearly, this was used by Lin City for loading and unloading supplies; otherwise, jumping down from the top of the four-story-high snow to the tracks below every time would not have been an easy task.

Even if falling onto the snow wouldn’t kill, transporting supplies from below to the top without digging out a slope would be impossible.

"Ah Xin, how are you planning to dig out the train station? Although the station isn’t big, with this much snow, even if you have enough manpower, it would still take several days to dig out, wouldn’t it?" Qin Lan stood beside Chen Xin, cautiously glancing at the tracks below and asked him.

Chen Xin shook his head, feeling somewhat helpless about Qin Lan’s question, he said, "Several days? Ah Lan, you’re underestimating the workload. Even with 200 people, it would take at least ten days to half a month to dig out the train station, and that’s only after equipping them with Exoskeleton Protective Suits; otherwise, it might take a month or two to reach the original roof of the train station."

"Huh? In that case, how are you going to dig out the train station?" Qin Lan was somewhat puzzled by what Chen Xin was thinking.

They were supposed to come for an on-site inspection to figure out how to dig out the train station for more efficient freight handling, but now he was saying that even with enough manpower, it wouldn’t be possible to clear out the train station in a short time. Isn’t this contradictory?

"Of course, not by hand; that would be too inefficient, and there isn’t enough manpower for that." Chen Xin walked towards the slope ahead and slid down, adding as he went, "I’m here to see if there’s a more effective method to clear the snow without needing much manpower; it would be fine even if it takes a bit longer."

"Have you thought of a method then?" Qin Lan followed Chen Xin in sliding down, both of them wearing Power Armor, which made sliding down more convenient than walking.

Standing at the bottom of the ravine, they looked up, gaining a more direct sense of the snow’s thickness. Chen Xin activated the measuring function of the mechanical arm and measured. Finding the snow to be 10.12 meters thick, he realized that digging it out by hand to clear out this section of the train station was a massive project.

Even with the help of engineering machinery, it would not be an easy task.

As for simply clearing a platform to facilitate unloading, that could be done, but the current issue was that Lin City could not spare the manpower for such work.

So now, they either had to wait for Lin City’s situation to improve, with patients recovering from minor symptoms to provide enough labor, or they had to hope the city could find a way to send new reinforcements, or Chen Xin had to come up with a way to directly evaporate the more than ten meters of snow before him.

Evaporation was actually a feasible idea: as long as a big enough heat source could be created, raising the temperature within the train station’s range above zero degrees, melting the snow, it would only take a few days to clear the snow.

But the problem was that in the current environment, creating a heat source large enough—never mind where the energy would come from—and ensuring the heat could be distributed across the entire train station was also a challenge.

The fourth-class train station might only be a few hundred meters long and look small, but using a single heat source to maintain enough heat to evaporate the snow over such an area reminded Chen Xin of the heat supply fields created by the Energy Tower and heating hubs in the game "Frostpunk."

Fortunately, for Chen Xin, this was not entirely impossible, as he had been considering this very problem before. However, instead of using the Energy Tower to build a greenhouse, this time it would be used to dig out the train station first.
```
