However, building an Energy Tower required not just a large amount of materials but also a sufficiently deep geothermal well.

The issue of materials was easy to solve. With his electromagnetic metallurgy furnace, Chen Xin could completely recycle waste metal from within Lin City to acquire the materials needed to construct an Energy Tower.

But drilling a deep enough geothermal well not only required specialized equipment but also professional drilling personnel.

At this point, Chen Xin realized there was a drilling expert in Lin City, wasn’t there?

So he quickly asked Qin Lan, "Ah Lan, how did it go when you guys visited the ’expert’ yesterday? How’s he doing?"

"The ’expert’s’ situation? He’s doing quite well. When he drilled a well for himself, he hit a hot spring, and with that, he’s been living quite comfortably. Why are you asking, Ah Xin? Do you need his help with something?" Qin Lan asked, sensing that Chen Xin seemed to need something from the "drilling expert."

Chen Xin nodded and said to Qin Lan, "Then let’s go find the ’expert.’ I need him to help me drill a well."

"Drill a well? But Ah Xin, weren’t you just talking about clearing the snow from the train station? What does drilling a well have to do with that?" Qin Lan was confused by Chen Xin and didn’t understand how drilling a well was related to clearing snow.

With a confident smirk, Chen Xin explained to Qin Lan, "Didn’t you yourself mention the answer just now? The ’expert’ found a hot spring, so he could rely on that to fend off the cold. So I need him to help me drill a deep well to utilize geothermal heat to evaporate the snow here."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 354 - 252 Drilling Expert (Thanks to Innocent Little Bird Boss for Rewarding Silver Alliance)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin is not one to procrastinate; he and Qin Lan went straight from the train station to the "Drilling Expert’s" home, intending to ask him for help in drilling a deep geothermal well to construct an Energy Tower.

The "Drilling Expert’s" home is located at the foot of a mountain in the suburbs, and due to its location, the snow here is not that deep and has been cleared, allowing Chen Xin to see that this place was originally a courtyard akin to a small factory.

The courtyard and factory seem abandoned, but the snow has been cleared to allow Chen Xin and Qin Lan to enter from outside and see the clearly added protective gate in the courtyard.

Behind the protective gate might be the "Drilling Expert’s" shelter; it appears that, like Chen Xin, he opted to dig a living space into the mountain, effectively utilizing the mountain as a protective layer for the shelter.

Chen Xin and Qin Lan contacted the "Drilling Expert" via radio in their Power Armor, and soon he opened the protective gate, appearing before Chen Xin and Qin Lan.

"Been a long time, ’Expert,’" Chen Xin greeted the "Drilling Expert" while scrutinizing this friend he hadn’t seen for about a year.

The "Drilling Expert" is a middle-aged man nearing forty. In Chen Xin’s memory, he was initially a very prosperous person who maintained himself well, although he worked on sites like a foreman, he was always cheerful and warm, like an elder brother in the survival group.

However, the "Drilling Expert" now looked much thinner and noticeably aged, his once-black hair now graying at the temples. Clearly, the past year and more had placed many pressures on him to cause such a change.

Nonetheless, the "Drilling Expert’s" cheerfulness and warmth hadn’t changed; he eagerly greeted Chen Xin, saying, "It’s the writer! It’s been ages, welcome, welcome! What brings you here? Come, hurry inside to sit!"

With that, he welcomed Chen Xin and Qin Lan into his shelter.

Although similar to Chen Xin’s shelter, both being spaces dug into the mountain, the "Drilling Expert’s" place is a bit different—he didn’t dig a tunnel like Chen Xin, but directly dug the shelter along the mountain wall.

While this slightly reduced the protective capability, the construction difficulty was much less and the cost relatively lower, making it a more economical and practical method.

After Chen Xin and Qin Lan entered the shelter and sat down, Chen Xin finally stated his purpose: "’Expert,’ do you still have your drilling equipment? I need you to drill a deep well."

"A deep well? How deep do you want it drilled?" When talking about his area of expertise, the "Drilling Expert" showed the confidence fitting his name: "My drilling equipment is still here. With some maintenance, it can start work. I’m not boasting; my drill can easily handle hundreds of meters. How deep do you want? Three hundred or five hundred?"

Hearing the "Drilling Expert" say so, Chen Xin shook his head, smiled, and said, "Not three hundred or five hundred, but three thousand. I want to drill a well three thousand meters deep."

"Three thousand meters?! You aren’t kidding, are you, writer?" Hearing Chen Xin’s request for a well three thousand meters deep, even self-proclaimed "Expert" couldn’t help but shake his head: "A well of three hundred to five hundred meters; I can drill easily, but three thousand meters... that’s really not doable."

Seeing the "Drilling Expert" say it’s not doable, Chen Xin wasn’t surprised, instead earnestly asking him, "Why do you think it’s not doable? Is it due to your drilling machine or your expertise?"

"Both. I’ve never drilled a well that deep, and my drill can’t handle it either," the "Drilling Expert" shook his head helplessly and gave Chen Xin a deep look, finding his request quite unreasonable.

However, despite this, the "Drilling Expert" still explained to Chen Xin: "Although I call myself ’Drilling Expert,’ I’m really just someone who helps drill wells; you know the Xiang Province here—if you drill down fifty meters and find no water, it’s considered a dry area. The few deep wells drilled before were on the mountain, owing to the thick soil layers, we drilled a few wells of hundreds of meters."

But now you ask me to drill a well three thousand meters deep, not to mention my machine’s maximum drilling depth is only one thousand meters, and the issues that could arise at such depth—I have no experience with them!

Drilling isn’t like other things; if drilling straight down hits rock layers, that’s manageable. But if we hit a water layer or magma, this well could be wasted."

The "Drilling Expert’s" real name is Cao Daren, which is why he gave himself such a nickname, and due to the one-letter similarity with ’Tiger of the Major Crimes Unit,’ he prefers others to call him by his nickname.

Even though he dubbed himself "Drilling Expert," Cao Daren is very self-aware; drilling isn’t like drilling other things. As long as the drill bit is hard enough and the rod is long enough, any depth can be reached, but various problems can arise that aren’t solvable just by using hard drills.

Blowouts, overflow, stuck drills, well leakage, well collapse... Since he started in this industry at the age of twenty and within those ten-plus years, Cao Daren has encountered various issues. He knows deeply that every meter the drill advances underground increases the chances of problems.

A well three thousand meters deep isn’t something that can be drilled easily.

After listening to Cao Daren’s points, Chen Xin considered them for a while before saying to Cao Daren: "If I can help you with the drilling machine issue, can you attempt to drill a well three thousand meters deep?"

"It’s not just the machine issue; I’ve never drilled a well so deep before!" Cao Daren responded with a bitter smile, not unwilling to help but truly aware of the dangers a well three thousand meters deep could pose.

However, faced with Chen Xin’s gaze, he still pondered before replying: "If you, the writer, can get me a professional deep well drilling machine, I might give it a try. But I can’t guarantee reaching that depth, and I can’t do it alone, you’ll need to find me some helpers."

Upon hearing Cao Daren say this, Chen Xin knowingly nodded, realizing—as he had anticipated—that directly drilling a deep well to reach the deep layers to extract geothermal heat isn’t feasible.

For now, the most appropriate approach seems to be drilling a shallower geothermal well first, establishing the Energy Tower, and then enhancing the well depth by upgrading the Energy Tower, thus extracting deeper geothermal resources.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 355 - 253: Deep Wells and Shallow Wells (Second Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Drilling seems to be a highly technical task, and indeed it is.

Flame Country has a long-standing tradition of drilling, known in ancient times as well-digging, which included not only common water wells but also salt wells, mines, and even natural gas wells.

In ancient times, digging a well was no easy feat, relying entirely on human effort to dig down to the well bottom, making the task of digging a deep well extremely time-consuming and labor-intensive.

Therefore, before digging a well, one must first choose the right location, and only then can the well-digging commence.

Nowadays, although drilling is aided by machinery, making it easier than ancient methods, the technical demands have not diminished in the slightest.

Ordinary water wells, a few dozen meters deep, are manageable. With modern drilling rigs capable of drilling anywhere from tens to hundreds of meters deep, drilling a well a few dozen meters deep can be completed in a few hours, provided the geological conditions aren’t too complex.

However, locating water veins, determining drill sites, and analyzing geological structures is not as simple as randomly picking a place to dig downwards.

Drilling a deep well is even more complicated; it requires professional engineering personnel and geological experts to analyze the local geological environment to ensure drilling won’t cause geological damage, and only then can a suitable location for drilling be selected.

During drilling, one must also handle the three major challenges from the geological formation itself: high temperature, high pressure, and high tectonic stress.

These are the three main challenges that constrain the depth of drilling and are a major test of drilling technology.

"Here is my drill rig, just an ordinary one. Though it can drill to a depth of seven hundred meters, with imported drill bits specifically designed for high temperature and pressure, it’s still impossible for this equipment to drill deep wells of several thousand meters you mentioned. You would need a large-scale oil drilling rig for that," Cao Daren accompanied Chen Xin and Qin Lan while introducing the drill rig he had in his warehouse.

Chen Xin had already learned the situation from Cao Daren earlier, so he had no doubts about what was said. After carefully examining the drill rig in front of him, he said to him, "I can modify your drill rig to reach the required depth."

"Modify the drill rig? Do you have the skills for that?" Cao Daren raised an eyebrow in surprise, but seeing Chen Xin in his power armor, he nodded somewhat in disbelief but still cautioned, "The drill rig is just a tool; the main issue is solving any problems that might arise during drilling. I have never done this kind of work, and if I have to figure everything out from scratch, it will take at least half a year to complete, and even then, there’s no guarantee of success."

In matters like this, Cao Daren had sufficient authority to speak. Although his statement about not guaranteeing success carried some modesty, he indeed was not confident.

"Would having high-performance drilling equipment increase the chances of success?" Chen Xin, somewhat uncertain, asked Cao Daren. With the system’s capabilities, even using laser drills to bore into the moon was not out of the question. If experience cannot ensure success, then equipment and technology must fill the gap.

Cao Daren didn’t question this, considering for a moment before saying, "If we have a good drill, the chances of success are certainly higher. Professional drilling machines find it as easy as pie to drill one or two thousand meters deep. But this also depends on the geological layer; drilling into granite or such would be much more troublesome. Soil layers are much quicker."

"Alright, let’s just do that for now. I’ll first find a way to enhance your drill rig so it can become a large rig capable of drilling that deep. Then, we’ll prepare for the drilling operation," Chen Xin felt relieved hearing Cao Daren’s words.

However, Cao Daren suddenly asked out of curiosity, "Why are you drilling such a deep well for? A water well is done at fifty meters, and even a deep one is just three to five hundred meters. You’ve already hit a hot spring at such depths!"

Lin City’s geothermal resources are fairly rich, though there aren’t any surface geothermal springs, geothermal zones do exist underground. Cao Daren himself discovered a hot spring after drilling over a hundred meters down.

"I’m developing deep geothermal energy, so I need to drill that deep," Chen Xin explained the reason for the three thousand-meter deep well, though he clarified to Cao Daren that it’s not necessary to do it all at once: "Deep hot dry rock geothermal resources are located at depths of three to six thousand meters. Only by drilling a sufficiently deep well can they be mined. But if you, ’Daren,’ find deep-well drilling difficult, we can first drill a few hundred meters and utilize the underground hot spring."

Xiang Province is rich in geothermal resources, with much of the province having abundant shallow geothermal energy and hydrothermal geothermal resources. Deep hot dry rock resources are also widely distributed. Lin City is within the district rich in hydrothermal geothermal resources, i.e., hot springs, though the water temperature is not high.

So whether Chen Xin plans to drill a deep or shallow well, geothermal resources can be exploited. However, deep well-extracted geothermal resources are richer, possess higher temperatures, and better meet Chen Xin’s needs.

Upon hearing Chen Xin say to initially drill a few hundred meters to test the waters, the previously worried expression on Cao Daren’s face disappeared, he even seemed relieved as he smiled and said to Chen Xin, "If that’s the case, then it’s incredibly simple. I can’t guarantee elsewhere, but within Lin City, if I drill a well, there’s an 80% chance it’ll hit warm water even if not 100% hot water!"

"Really? In that case, I need to drill a well at the Lin City Railway Station to introduce the geothermal energy and evaporate the station’s snow accumulation," Chen Xin was a bit taken aback by Cao Daren’s confident demeanor but since he seemed so confident, Chen Xin decided to humor him and have him drill a test well.

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s words, Cao Daren was almost shocked to his core, his eyes nearly popping out. He skeptically asked Chen Xin, "Using geothermal to evaporate the railway station’s snow? This... can it actually be done?"

"According to physics, it’s possible, but first, you need to help me drill the well and ensure we hit a hot spring," Chen Xin tasked Cao Daren.

This time, Cao Daren felt less assured, but after hesitating briefly, he nodded, "If you can improve the drill rig so it can reach a thousand meters, I can guarantee to bring out a hot spring for you!"

"Really? Then I’ll be looking forward to it," Chen Xin finally smiled when seeing Cao Daren agree.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 356 - 254: Begin Construction (Third Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In what was once the forecourt of Lin City Railway Station, a drilling machine had been erected and was vigorously drilling downwards.

"Make sure to clean up the gravel and soil brought up by the drill, don’t let them pile up around the borehole!" Cao Daren, full of energy, was standing next to the drill bit, wrapped up like a bear, directing several workers assisting by the drilling machine.

For Cao Daren, having the chance to return to his old trade was quite a rare opportunity, filling him with enthusiasm for the work.

These workers were found by Chen Xin. After the rescue team set up a medical station and started treating patients, some of the mild cases recovered their health after two days of rest and were able to work.

Most of them had been assigned by Lin City Hall to places urgently in need of manpower. Chen Xin, leveraging the influence of the rescue team and Lin City Hall’s own desire to clear the train station, managed to pull ten workers from the staff to help him and Cao Daren with the task.

Since Cao Daren had promised to help, he wouldn’t mess up. After Chen Xin found the workers, he brought his drilling machine to the train station plaza covered with more than ten meters of snow and started drilling without conducting any geological surveys.

This wasn’t due to reckless behavior by Cao Daren; instead, it was because the entire city of Lin City was situated over geothermal fissures, where drilling anywhere would hit the geothermal belt.

This geothermal fissure is the largest in Xiang Province; it stretches across the entire province and reaches up into Hubei Province, where Chibi City, a place Chen Xin had visited, is also part of this geothermal belt.

The geothermal resources contained within this belt are quite abundant. The two hot springs within Xiang Province with water temperatures exceeding 100°C are manifestations of this geothermal belt.

However, while drilling with enthusiasm, Cao Daren was not without concerns.

"Writer, the snow here is so deep. If we drill into the geothermal layer and the heat causes the snow to melt, it could affect the stability of the drilling machine. Moreover, your aim is to evaporate the snow here, so you can’t place the wellhead ten meters above the ground, right?" Cao Daren approached Chen Xin, who had just returned from gathering materials, expressing his worries.

Chen Xin had already considered this issue, and he reassured Cao Daren, saying, "Don’t worry about this. I’ll place support columns underneath the drilling machine to secure it. Even if the snow starts to melt, the support columns will keep the machine stable."

"Okay, as long as you’ve got it covered, I’m at ease." Cao Daren did not pursue the matter of where Chen Xin intended to place the wellhead; as long as the drilling machine remained stable, everything else was not a problem.

Seeing that Cao Daren had no more questions, Chen Xin carried his recently collected scrap metal to the electromagnetic metallurgy furnace set up nearby.

For ease of construction, Chen Xin had moved this electromagnetic metallurgy furnace here, and upgraded it a second time, fixing issues encountered previously to make it more efficient and convenient.

Placing the scrap metal onto the central tray of the machine, he switched it on. After a display of sparks and steam, a still-red-hot spherical metal ingot was smelted.

Turning off the machine, he allowed the metal ingot to cool quickly and fall back onto the tray. While using a mechanical arm to pick up the ingot, Chen Xin also made sure to clean the residue off the tray and toss it into a metal bin beside the machine.

This residue was waste material but couldn’t be discarded haphazardly; it would be collected for centralized processing later.

Of course, the spherical metal ingot itself was not immediately usable; Chen Xin still needed to conduct secondary processing on it.

With the help of a mechanical arm, the once solid lump of metal was processed into various metal components, which were then set aside.

These parts appeared mostly as long metal strips and assorted rings, making it impossible to discern what they were until Chen Xin fully assembled them.

But Chen Xin paid no mind to this. These parts were being made according to blueprints stored in a microcomputer on the mechanical arm, each piece tagged with a corresponding number; he just needed to assemble them according to the blueprint later.

As for what the final assembled product would be, even if someone saw Chen Xin’s design plans, they’d likely have to guess quite a bit to realize what it was.

After all, before Chen Xin completed this creation, nobody had ever constructed a large radiator over ten meters tall.

This device was designed by Chen Xin, modeled after the heating hub in "Frostpunk." The structure resembled the heating hubs in the game, but its principle of operation was entirely different, as it didn’t use coal.

Its base would connect with the geothermal well drilled by Cao Daren, continuously extracting underground thermal energy through pipes and releasing it via radiator fins on the heating hub aboveground, thereby raising the temperature around the hub.

This was quite a crude and wasteful way of using energy, since it released heat directly into the air with no insulation around to contain it, requiring a lot of thermal energy to raise the temperature. Employing such energy elsewhere in electricity generation or home heating would be much more efficient.

However, for Chen Xin, who aimed to melt the snow surrounding the train station, such a method was indeed the most suitable.

Also, for someone with a system like his, Chen Xin could completely improve the design to make the heating hub operate more efficiently, truly establishing a stable heating field around it like in the game.

For Chen Xin, this was not difficult to achieve.

According to his plan, once the heating hub was built, he would continue upgrading it into a true Energy Tower, and extend the geothermal well at the tower’s base into the dry rock layer to efficiently exploit deep geothermal energy.

Regarding concerns about waste due to rough usage, this was unnecessary. Compared to the geothermal resources Chen Xin was using, the geothermal resources beneath the layers could be considered inexhaustible.

Unless the core of the Blue Planet extinguished, geothermal resources would remain endless, as long as exploitation methods were appropriately managed, without causing geological damage or localized over-extraction, no issues would arise.

Using the electromagnetic metallurgy furnace, Chen Xin continuously smelted the collected scrap metal, already gathering most of the components. Once Cao Daren finished the well, Chen Xin could start constructing this heating hub.

Once the heating hub began operating, the continuously flowing thermal energy would melt the surrounding snow, fulfilling Chen Xin’s goal of clearing away the snow.

However, thinking that all the snow would eventually melt, Chen Xin had to consider a problem: how to manage the drainage after the snow melts.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 357 - 255 The Train Brings (Fourth Update)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin and Cao Daren were working vigorously, but the operations at Lin City’s train station did not stop.

Or perhaps it should be put another way; the transportation of railway supplies at Lin City did not come to a halt.

During the time when the rescue team arrived in Lin City, trains still delivered urgently needed supplies and coal to the city every week.

And Lin City also needed to organize a large workforce to unload as much of the supplies and coal as possible during the train’s stopover.

Perhaps such activities were organized before, or maybe it was because the snowstorm had subsided making it easier to load and unload supplies without interference from the storm, or perhaps both. Chen Xin saw that Lin City Hall had organized a large labor force, and as soon as the train stopped, they began to unload the supplies from the train like ants moving house.

It had been mentioned before that, due to the time restrictions imposed by the train schedule, the stopping and traveling time of each train was fixed. Under such circumstances, if the supplies couldn’t be unloaded as quickly as possible, the train would leave as soon as it reached the designated stopping time.

For a city that already lacks supplies, this would certainly be unacceptable.

Although the staff on the train were not without compassion and would help as much as they could, the extent of their assistance was limited, only extending a thirty-minute stop by three to five minutes as they could cover the lost time by traveling a bit faster on the tracks.

Thus, Lin City Hall organized a large workforce to carry the supplies delivered by the train in an ant-like manner.

Even though Lin City allocated a large workforce and everyone worked hard to carry the supplies from the train, time was ultimately limited, and the efficiency of loading and unloading was limited as well. Although most of the supplies were unloaded within the time limit, a portion of the coal still wasn’t fully unloaded.

The train sounded its whistle, as staff helped Lin City’s people load the last bag of coal, closing the doors only after the wheels began to roll.

Watching the train depart, these people then started to organize the supplies they had just unloaded, preparing to transport them away.

At this time, Chen Xin also shifted his focus back, preparing to return and continue manufacturing parts for the heating hub. He had already cast enough metal ingots, the parts were mostly made, and the heating hub could soon be constructed.

But just as Chen Xin turned to leave, someone called out to him from behind, "Mr. Chen, wait a moment!"

Chen Xin stopped and turned around, seeing two people dressed in protective suits.

This surprised Chen Xin somewhat, as although Lin City had similarly received exoskeletons and protective suits, the quantity wasn’t large. Among the people unloading supplies earlier, quite a few were just in their own clothes without protective suits.

When the two reached him, Chen Xin recognized one of them as someone he knew, a researcher from a scientific institution in the city, while the other person seemed familiar, as if he had seen him in Three Towns before, but he couldn’t quite recall.

"Hello, Mr. Chen, I’m from a scientific institution in Three Towns, we met once before." This person introduced himself to Chen Xin and explained his purpose: "I came from Three Towns to find you."

"Coming all the way from Three Towns to find me? Is there something going on?" Chen Xin raised an eyebrow, curious about what could have warranted this person’s special trip from Three Towns to find him.

"Here’s the thing. Because of your presence in Lin City, many projects we collaborated on have been affected. So, I specifically came to ask when you might return, or if we should transport project samples to Lin City instead." This person clarified his purpose in finding Chen Xin, and then looked at him expectantly, awaiting his response.

Chen Xin froze for a moment after hearing his words, then he realized that coming to Lin City with the rescue team meant he essentially left all the work in the city behind.

He was no longer just by himself. After agreeing to cooperate with the country, he was involved in many projects. A special team was organized just for him, and numerous research institutions were impacted because of him...

It could be said that now, even if Chen Xin sneezed, it would make a bunch of people anxious, yet here he was, insisting on coming to Lin City to join a rescue team!

Knowing this, the person in charge of the special team almost got high blood pressure from stress.

However, they didn’t want Chen Xin to feel his freedom was being restricted, as his abilities were extremely crucial for Flame Country at the moment. Given that Chen Xin hadn’t made any demands, he could have otherwise enjoyed top-tier treatment.

So, after balancing the situation, they decided to send someone to inquire. If Chen Xin returned to work soon, it would be a win-win situation. If he didn’t want to return for a while, they would have to appease him, sending various project samples to Lin City for Chen Xin to modify and upgrade, as to not delay the research progress as much as possible.

Chen Xin naturally understood the meaning behind the visitor’s words, so he nodded and said, "I’ll be in Lin City for a while longer. If you can send the project samples to Lin City, that would be best. However, considering the current situation in Lin City, I think you should consider having someone drive over from the city, as it’s quite troublesome to unload on this side of the railway."

"Understood, I will convey your message to the higher-ups, for them to coordinate. As for how to transport the project samples to Lin City, I believe the higher-ups will make appropriate arrangements." The person who came to see Chen Xin seemed to be a clerical officer, speaking with a somewhat bureaucratic tone.

Chen Xin didn’t mind this, simply nodding in agreement, then he turned to the researcher beside him and said, "Your visit is actually timely. I’m working on a project here in Lin City. I mentioned it to the person in charge before; if we make progress, we could achieve an efficient way to utilize deep geothermal resources."

"Utilizing deep geothermal resources?" The two exchanged a glance, very curious as to what Chen Xin was talking about.

"It’s hard to explain just like this, it’s easier to see it with your own eyes." Having difficulty explaining, Chen Xin decided to take the two to the drilling site, pointing to the rig and saying, "We’re drilling a well here with plans to reach the hot dry rock layer three thousand meters underground. Equipment will be installed at the wellhead to extract deep geothermal energy for heating and power generation, among other uses."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 358 - 256 Geothermal Heating Network
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"What’s so advanced about this thing?" The researcher looked at the mechanical parts piled up by Chen Xin, feeling not the slightest sense of advancement, even thinking the unassembled heat network seems somewhat primitive, like something from the steam era.

Although steampunk was a popular element before the disaster, many people associated with science fiction enjoy this style full of steel, steam, coal smoke, and a strong industrialization vibe, relishing the charm as if living in the eighteenth-century Victorian era during the peak of the Industrial Revolution.

But from a scientific standpoint, this is still a very backward and wasteful way to utilize energy.

Despite many science fiction works injecting ineffable black technology into steampunk—making steam engines capable of flying a fortress, magnifying the energy conversion efficiency of steam engines a hundredfold, and steam sources providing unlimited steam, even using punched tape in difference engines to study artificial intelligence...

But only inefficiently burned fuel causes coal smoke; only inefficient use and recovery of waste heat generate vast amounts of steam, which from the perspective of modern industrial aesthetics is undoubtedly a huge waste.

In this researcher’s eyes, these heat network parts, into which Chen Xin poured much effort, are no match for the enticing electromagnetic metallurgical furnace beside them.

You should know that the furnace uses the magnetic field constraint—a technology hard to replicate even in laboratories—not to mention keeping liquid metal magnetic even at high temperatures and levitating.

If one could master magnetic field constraint technology, merely controlling nuclear fusion research would make great progress, turning nuclear fusion into a new energy source. Isn’t this more efficient and cleaner than extracting geothermal energy?

But Chen Xin doesn’t think so. Facing the researcher’s doubts, he merely smiled and shook his head, saying: "It’s not something advanced. The geothermal resource utilization technology is nothing worth mentioning—just injecting water into the ground, using geothermal to heat the water flow, then pumping it up to dissipate heat.

However, this thing itself has high heat dissipation efficiency. According to my design, it can create a heating field with a temperature rise of up to 50°C within a 200-meter radius. If everything goes well, I can use this heat network to evaporate the snow at this train station instead of needing manpower to clear it.

You’ve seen how the people in this city load and unload supplies; improving the efficiency of loading and unloading supplies without clearing the train station is challenging."

"That’s true, but it still feels wasteful. Directly dissipating heat into the atmosphere is truly..." The researcher hesitated with his wording, uncertain how to phrase it politely.

Faced with the researcher’s dilemma, Chen Xin merely glanced at him without much comment, but still explained: "Dissipating heat directly into the atmosphere is certainly wasteful. If this energy could be utilized more effectively, it would have a greater impact, such as converting heat energy into electricity to drive electrical equipment, which would clear the snow more effectively.

But Lin City clearly doesn’t have these conditions now, so only this relatively crude method can be used to clear the snow at the train station. Temporarily, there will be some waste, but after clearing the snow, I’ll modify this heat network to provide heating and power to the train station. Then the heat dissipation won’t be so crude."

"I see, in this case, it’s indeed feasible." After listening to Chen Xin’s explanation, the researcher thought it over and found the approach feasible, his mind quickly thinking of other applicable areas for this technology: "For example, construction sites, first establish such a heat network, raising the temperature of the area to facilitate construction, and after completion, modify the heat network to become the source of heating and power for the building. It is indeed a very feasible technology!"

Hearing the researcher’s thoughts, Chen Xin nodded with delight: "Exactly right! Your idea is broader and more feasible than my initial application. Originally, I thought of using the heat network to build greenhouse shelters; now it seems the application scope of this thing should be broader!"

"Greenhouse shelters? Using this to build greenhouse shelters is a great idea! This could repurpose some farmland covered by snow! The country is short of grain now; if some farmland can be restored, it would be a great blessing!" This researcher clearly cares about broader issues beyond merely scientific research, intuitively connecting greenhouses with agriculture and food production.

Now, Chen Xin was also interested, eager to discuss the heat network in depth with professional researchers. He not only projected the heat network’s design onto the snow with his robotic arm, but also attached modified drawings and plans of the Energy Tower, pointing to these three drawings: "This is the completed heat network. According to the design, it has two modes: one is this dissipation mode, which can extend a heating field with a 200-meter radius; the other is this energy mode, not dissipating heat, but converting thermal energy into electrical energy for nearby needs."

"Is this large one the amplification mode of the heat network?" The researcher glanced at the two modes of heat network and concentrated on the Energy Tower plans.

"This is the Energy Tower. According to my design, it can tap deep geothermal resources below three thousand meters and can be used in conjunction with the heat network," Chen Xin introduced enthusiastically, seeing the researcher’s interest in the Energy Tower. "Build an Energy Tower in one area, then construct heat networks where needed, connecting them through pipes to provide thermal energy from the Energy Tower. This way, one can construct a heating network."

"Is this for urban heating? If a geothermal heating network can be constructed in the city, it would be wonderful!" The researcher greatly anticipated this, shifting his attitude and having high hopes for Chen Xin’s project.

Conversely, the nearby civil officer couldn’t find an opportunity to interject; he barely understood the conversation between Chen Xin and the researcher, and they quickly changed topics before he could grasp what they were discussing. This uncomfortable situation led him to interject during a pause: "Since Mr. Chen, you’ve decided to stay in Lin City temporarily, I will report back to the higher department. Do you need someone sent here to assist with this project?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 359 - 257: Conversation and Needs
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Following the previous train, not only did the two individuals looking for Chen Xin arrive at Lin City, but another rescue team also came from downtown.

This was the second rescue team to arrive in Lin City. Compared to the first rescue team, this group was much more poorly equipped and carried few supplies. Most of them only had their own clothes, without protective suits, and did not bring medical tents, but they did carry a lot of urgently needed medicines.

The arrival of this rescue team greatly pleased Lin City Hall, and they quickly sent them along with their supplies to the rescue station.

Since they came by train, the number of people in this second rescue team significantly increased compared to the first rescue team. The arrival of sixty members greatly supplemented the staff shortage at the rescue station, allowing more patients to receive treatment.

Chen Xin learned from the researchers that this group first boarded the train at the downtown station. After arriving in San Town, they transferred and reached Lin City through the coordination and arrangement there.

This was exactly the route that Chen Xin and the others had discussed when they departed as the first rescue team.

After the first rescue team opened the path, the downtown area sent a second rescue team, using this more time-consuming route to transport people and materials.

"Mr. Chen, if there’s anything you need to be transported from the downtown, it can be transferred via this route. You only need to load it with those project prototypes." After explaining the situation to Chen Xin, the researchers also indicated that they could transport some things for Chen Xin.

Chen Xin did not have any specific needs but after some thought said, "I personally don’t have anything to transport, but if possible, I’d like you to bring some materials over. Resources are scarce here in Lin City, and I have to rely on collecting scrap metal to remelt and manufacture parts, which is not very efficient, and the material quality isn’t very reliable."

"That’s easy! Anything Mr. Chen needs, we can get it for you!" The researcher assured him confidently, with no hint of difficulty.

But that’s the truth; when Chen Xin was in the downtown, he just needed to tell the research institution what materials he needed, and they would get them for him.

"Mainly it’s steel. We need steel for building the heating hub. I’ll write a list for you later. Just send the raw materials over, and I can process them here." Chen Xin, hearing the researcher’s assurance, didn’t hold back and planned to write out a list of what he needed.

Chen Xin and the researcher chatted as they walked to the place where Chen Xin, Cao Daren, and the workers were temporarily staying, a row of tents buried under the snow.

These were specially modified tents by Chen Xin, while his RV was left at the rescue station for Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan to reside in. He hadn’t driven it over.

As for the power supply here, Chen Xin found a few car batteries and newly developed two Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries: one for his electromagnetic metallurgy furnace and the other for the drill. The heating and lighting in the tent were connected to the battery supplying the electromagnetic metallurgy furnace.

Due to the constraints of raw materials, Chen Xin didn’t use the electromagnetic metallurgy furnace much.

Consequently, the power supply in the tent was ample and stable, with guaranteed lighting and heating. Apart from the slightly cramped space, it was quite comfortable.

Chen Xin took off his Power Armor and left it outside the tent before leading the researcher inside.

After pouring a cup of water for the researcher, Chen Xin invited him to sit down.

However, due to limited space in the tent, there were neither tables nor chairs, just a small low table for placing items, so the two sat on the ground.

"Mr. Chen, you live here? It looks nice!" The researcher glanced around the tent and couldn’t help but praise it.

Although the space inside the tent wasn’t large, it wasn’t cramped with two people sitting. The floor was at least two meters wide, allowing three or four people to lie side by side, making it quite decent for a tent used by Chen Xin alone.

"Yes, since we’re working here, I decided to set up a living space here. Besides me, the workers also live here." Chen Xin poured himself a cup of hot water, adding a bit of salt and sugar.

In such a cold environment, sugar and salt water effectively replenish the body’s needed water, electrolytes, and energy, making it quite a good drink.

As for the heating hub, it was mentioned earlier that qualified steel is mainly needed. The strength of materials from collected scrap metals is concerning." Chen Xin brought the conversation back to the main subject, telling the researcher about his current needs: "Besides materials, I also need a geologist, especially one who specializes in geothermal resources, because neither I nor the friend responsible for drilling has much knowledge in this area, and we need professionals to assist in dealing with possible problems during the geothermal resource extraction process."

"I understand. I will inform the downtown about this and ask them to try to send over a geothermal expert." This was a reasonable request, and the researcher knew such matters must be handled by professionals, so he readily agreed.

In addition to agreeing to Chen Xin’s request, this researcher also inquired about the heating hub and Energy Tower.

Chen Xin had previously mentioned using the heating hub to build greenhouses and constructing a city heating network with the Energy Tower, which was quite appealing to this researcher.

Although this researcher did not specialize in this field, he had plenty of enthusiasm for something that could improve people’s livelihoods.

If Chen Xin’s current project were completed, the technology could be applied to most areas rich in geothermal resources within Flame Country. Cities could then build Energy Towers and heating hubs, melting the snow on the ground, raising the temperature, and allowing people to return to life on the surface. It would be wonderful!

"Mr. Chen, did you mean to use this heating hub under preparation to build greenhouses?" The researcher recalled Chen Xin’s mention of greenhouses and curiously asked him.

In the researcher’s opinion, the area around the train station didn’t seem suitable, considering the numerous buildings around. If Chen Xin wanted to build greenhouses, it would be better to do so on the original farmland.

After all, the original farmland is cultivated land, and even if it’s frozen with snow, it’s better than dismantling abandoned buildings and clearing a plot of land.

It’s not just the workload; it’s also about soil fertility.

"I don’t plan to put the greenhouses in the city. We’ll drill another geothermal well and construct a second heating hub." Chen Xin naturally understood this reasoning, so he didn’t have any plans to build the greenhouses in the city.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 360 - 258: A Hot Spring Appeared (Part 3)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The researchers didn’t seek out Lin City staff to arrange accommodations like the civilian officials who came with them but instead stayed directly at Chen Xin’s place.

Fortunately, Chen Xin had prepared plenty of tents, so he just needed to make a few adjustments to free up a tent for the researchers.

However, staying in a tent can’t quite compare to staying in a shelter. After all, it’s not like before the catastrophe; of the five essential human needs—eating, drinking, sleeping, defecating, and urinating—eating, drinking, and sleeping are manageable inside a tent, but personal excretion isn’t something that can be handled inside a tent.

Even if one doesn’t mind the smell, it’s a troublesome matter to deal with.

So usually, everyone has to handle their personal hygiene issues outside the snow caves where the tents are pitched.

In this kind of weather, going out for the bathroom is quite a daunting challenge; squatting for just a bit longer could result in frostbite on one’s behind.

Moreover, considering the smell issues after heating, even though Chen Xin dug another hole near the snow caves to serve as a toilet, it’s still impossible to install a heater to warm one’s behind.

This leads to each bathroom visit being a major challenge.

Besides, bathing is another issue that’s bothering everyone; while the tents aren’t too cold, there’s no place to take a bath. One can only fetch a basin of hot water and wipe down inside the tent.

The movements have to be quick; otherwise, even though there’s no wind inside the tent, after a while, one’s body will become very cold, and catching a cold would be a troublesome matter.

However, if someone really wants to take a bath, neither Chen Xin nor Cao Daren would stop them; instead, they’d give them half a day off to go back to the shelter to bathe.

After all, the shelter is just within Lin City’s urban area, so going back to take a bath doesn’t take much time.

Of course, because the drilling work had only started a few days ago, none of these workers wanted to go back for a bath yet; they could still endure it.

After all, even though there are bathing facilities within the shelter, taking a bath in this current environment is a luxury; usually, the survivors in the shelter could just bathe once a week, and that would already be quite good.

The available water resources are extremely valuable right now. Though the country is still maintaining tap water supply, bathing consumes water not just for the actual bathing but also for washing clothes, which everyone considered when deciding to bathe weekly.

Chen Xin previously didn’t have to think about these issues; all kinds of living conditions in his shelter were very well-equipped, allowing him to bathe daily without issue, and the same for washing clothes.

Apart from worrying about clothes wearing out from frequent washing, Chen Xin didn’t really have much to worry about.

But now, living in tents inside snow caves, many issues Chen Xin wouldn’t care about before emerged one by one, forcing him to allocate some energy to tackle them.

This makes Chen Xin reflect on how well he’d lived before, while also examining his current environment to see if there are areas to improve, for both his and others’ living conditions.

Chen Xin hasn’t forgotten that his system gains survival points extensively through improving his living conditions.

During this period, products generated by the system, like the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery and Exoskeleton Protective Suit, have been distributed nationwide, and Chen Xin already started harvesting survival points on a grand scale.

Although there is an upper limit, one must say, Flame Country is vast, and the survival points brought to Chen Xin by numerous shelters have been remarkably impressive, with his total survival points reaching six figures.

This is a significant amount of survival points, and if Chen Xin wished, he has enough resources to implement many of his ideas.

For instance, Chen Xin always liked large mechas in the past; although building a Gundam is impossible, a Titan is not entirely out of the question, and if Chen Xin genuinely wished, he could construct a Titan from the game "Titanfall."

Thinking about this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel a bit excited.

However, he restrained his desire well; the most important task at hand is to build the heat supply core and then erect the Energy Tower.

As for the mechas and Titans, they’re not impossible to manage, but they need to be pushed back slightly.

After all, while others are struggling at the edge of life and death, Chen Xin can’t be indulging himself, building large robots for fun. Can he?

Although such selfish behavior isn’t entirely unacceptable, to Chen Xin, he isn’t very fond of this approach, and the system’s way of acquiring massive survival points also dictates that Chen Xin must help others.

Even though he can convince himself to restrain some impulsive thoughts and can also convince himself that humanoid machinery like Gundam and mechas are pretty useless, with no need to create such high-powered toys...

But thousands of theories and numerous reasons always pale in comparison to a single question: "Isn’t a big robot cool?"

Cool, indeed very cool.

For boys, robots, Transformers, Gundam...such large humanoid iron creations always possess irresistible allure.

Otherwise, why are there always boys crying and fussing for their parents to buy the handsome toys in the store?

Though Chen Xin passed the age where he’d cry and fuss for something he wanted, now that he can build a real one, why should he hold back?

Despite numerous reasons convincing himself otherwise, Chen Xin still wishes to create a robot for fun.

Yet while he considers building a robot, he’s also taking its practicality into account, deciding to first create an engineering mecha.

This kind of thing holds practical value; in this current environment, an engineering mecha can provide significant utility, potentially being more effective than dozens or even hundreds of workers when used well.

Just like the train loading and unloading work in Lin City; with a few engineering mechas, the efficiency could be greatly increased!

Thinking like this, Chen Xin couldn’t hold back his inner impulse and began drafting his engineering mecha design using the microcomputer on the Power Armor and mechanical arms.

But just when he’d finished designing the skeleton of his engineering mecha, Cao Daren suddenly rushed in from outside, found Chen Xin, and excitedly shouted: "We hit it! We hit it! Writer, we struck a hot spring!"

Stunned by Cao Daren’s shout, Chen Xin paused before reacting and hurriedly asked: "A hot spring? Did we really hit a hot spring?"

"We hit it! We hit it! Really hit it!" Cao Daren was extremely excited, pulling Chen Xin and wanting to go outside, yet unable to pull the Power Armor, but that didn’t dampen his excitement: "The drill reached over four hundred meters deep, pierced a rock layer, and encountered a water-bearing layer; now it’s already producing water!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 361 - 259 Subsequent Arrangements
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The drill rig had stopped, and several workers were gathered around, pulling out the drill rods segment by segment.

Meanwhile, steam was billowing from the borehole beneath the drill rig, and a hot spring was continuously spraying water upwards, creating a misty atmosphere around the scene.

Chen Xin and Cao Daren arrived at the side of the drill rig, watching the wellhead below continuously gushing out scalding hot water. Chen Xin excitedly asked Cao Daren, "Have you measured the water temperature? What’s the temperature?"

"The water temperature is over seventy degrees, quite hot to the touch." Cao Daren was equally excited, answering Chen Xin while explaining the situation, "When the drill broke through four hundred meters, I had a feeling we’d hit water today, so I paid special attention. Sure enough, we hit the rock layer, and once we broke through, the water started gushing."

Hearing Cao Daren say the water temperature was over seventy degrees made Chen Xin very happy. A high water temperature means abundant thermal energy, which means more heat can be supplied. This is excellent news for the heat supply hub.

However, looking at the continuously gushing hot spring water, Chen Xin was also a bit worried and asked Cao Daren, "Will this water keep spraying continuously? If it keeps spraying, will it impact future construction?"

"Don’t worry about that, the water pressure isn’t high," Cao Daren reassured him quickly, "It’s spraying because we just broke the aquifer, and there’s a process for the pressure to release. Plus, the drill is still inside the casing, with limited space for the water to gush out. Once we pull the drill out, the water won’t spray like this, and we can block it with a cover."

By this time, researchers also learned about the hot spring discovery and hurried over. Watching the continuously gushing hot spring water, they excitedly asked Chen Xin, "Mr. Chen, I’ve seen your design drawings. The heat supply hub internal hot water should circulate, right? Is it necessary to drill an injection well?"

Of course, extracting geothermal resources can’t be done by just drilling a single water well. Although Xiang Province has abundant groundwater resources, it can’t be endlessly used.

Under normal circumstances, geothermal resource utilization involves circulation through two wells. One well extracts groundwater to obtain heat, and another well similarly drills into the underground aquifer, connecting with the extraction well underground. The extracted hot water releases its heat and is reinjected underground through the injection well for renewable use.

"An injection well must indeed be drilled, but its location and method require professional guidance, so this matter is not urgent for now." Chen Xin knew an injection well was necessary, but it wasn’t something to rush.

The injection well is not like the extraction well; it’s not just a matter of drilling into the aquifer and accessing water.

Since it’s needed to reinject extracted groundwater underground to form a cycle with the extraction well, the positions of both wells must be in the same aquifer. Determining this requires analyzing the aquifer’s trend, geology, and size, area, permeability, and other factors, not simply drilling a hole several hundred meters away.

This explains why Chen Xin said they need the help of geologists; such specialized analyses indeed require true professionals.

While discussing the drilling of the injection well with the researchers, Cao Daren was more concerned about construction issues.

Seeing the drill rods pulled up from underground, while the scalding hot water kept gushing without any sign of stopping, Cao Daren instructed the workers to continue construction and asked Chen Xin, "Mr. Chen, now that the well is done, what do you suggest we do next?"

"Next..." Chen Xin habitually raised his hand to touch his chin but ended up touching his face mask, so he reluctantly lowered it before saying, "Once the water stops spraying, have the workers clear the snow around the well and start erecting the framework. There’s no need to clear too much space, just dig around the rig to expose the ground, facilitating foundation work later."

"Are you preparing to build the heat supply hub?" The nearby researchers immediately understood what Chen Xin wanted next.

Chen Xin nodded affirmatively and answered, "Exactly, clear the snow and prepare the foundation for the heat supply hub, then erect the construction scaffolding. After that, it’s just a matter of assembling the prefabricated parts I’ve prepared."

Understanding Chen Xin’s plan, the researchers and Cao Daren didn’t raise objections, though Cao Daren had one concern: "Mr. Chen, once the heat supply hub is built, it’s for melting snow, right? Surely this place will accumulate a lot of water then, especially since there’s no injection well yet. The extraction well will need to keep drawing water, which brings up water discharge as a potential issue."

"Regarding water discharge, ’Daren’, you don’t have to worry. I’ll wait until the injection well is drilled and the entire system can operate cyclically before activating it. For now, we might only test the equipment, so direct discharge isn’t a concern." Chen Xin had considered this issue and didn’t intend to discharge water haphazardly.

However, the discharge of melted snowwater mentioned by Cao Daren is indeed worth noting. The site for drilling and constructing the heat supply hub is right at Lin City Railway Station’s forecourt. Due to the small railway station size, and the primary goal to clear railway station snow, when the heat supply hub receives power and melts the snow, water is likely to flow along the track trenches, potentially freezing the tracks.

This isn’t a minor issue — frozen tracks could cause serious derailments.

So water discharge must be considered.

"What about using pumps to extract and discharge to other places?" Cao Daren suggested a simple yet practical method: "Besides, in this snowy weather, directly discharging outside would cause it to freeze instantly without much impact."

Hearing Cao Daren’s suggestion, both Chen Xin and the researchers found it feasible, though something felt off.

Considering this, a researcher remarked, "Isn’t direct discharge inappropriate? As Mr. Chen said, the heat field generated by the hub covers a radius of two hundred meters, and calculating a snow thickness of more than ten meters, it’s akin to a small lake. Randomly discharging such a large volume of water might freeze creating an iceberg. Maybe we can find a way to utilize it."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 362 - 260: Each Busy
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

How can the accumulated water from melting snow be utilized? Before the disaster, probably no one would have considered this issue.

Although freshwater resources on the Blue Planet have always been relatively scarce, generally in areas where water resources are not particularly lacking, there aren’t many restrictions on water usage. Moreover, the majority of the time, people let snow naturally melt and evaporate, with few considering recycling it.

This is similar to how few people consider collecting rainwater unless they are in drought-stricken areas.

But now, to prevent the formation of ice layers on the railway after the snow melts, which would affect railway traffic, Chen Xin must consider the problem of water drainage after melting the snow around the train station using the heating hub.

Letting it naturally flow away is obviously not an option because it would follow the slopes along the railway, which is precisely what Chen Xin wants to avoid.

"By the way, can we pump away this accumulated water and make ice bricks to build houses? Aren’t we living in snow caves? Building an ice house with ice blocks shouldn’t be a problem, right? Didn’t the Inuit in the Arctic use to live in snow houses?" Cao Daren suggested, recalling videos he had seen of Inuit snow houses.

After exchanging a glance with the researchers, Chen Xin shook his head, ultimately explaining to Cao Daren: "The snow houses you saw built with regular snow bricks are just attractions for tourists. Real Inuit snow houses involve digging a pit and then covering the top with snow bricks, leaving only a small entrance.

Those neat and pretty snow houses are either artistically crafted or commercial attractions for tourists. Real Inuit snow houses aren’t much better than the snow caves and tents we’re living in now."

"Uh, in that case, can we only drain it?" Cao Daren asked, feeling a bit embarrassed by Chen Xin’s explanation, but still inquiring about how to handle the snow.

Chen Xin shook his head, thought carefully, and said: "Building a snow house is actually feasible. Since the snow here will eventually melt, and we’ll need to relocate our tents, instead of continuing to dig snow caves, we might as well utilize the snow and build a semi-submerged snow house, which would be more comfortable to live in."

"That makes sense, but are we still going to live here afterward?" Cao Daren was puzzled. With the hot spring tapped and the heating hub built, would they still live here? Chen Xin surely has a refuge, and even if he had no place to stay in Lin City, he wouldn’t live in the snow for long, right?

"We won’t stay here permanently, but I will remain here until the heating hub operates normally and I confirm there are no issues. ’Daren,’ after you finish drilling the well, you can go home." Chen Xin understood Cao Daren’s implication and did not prevent him from going home.

After all, Cao Daren was just helping him and once the work was done, he would naturally want to return to his life.

Cao Daren was relieved to hear Chen Xin’s words. Although living in a tent in a snow cave wasn’t uncomfortable, it wasn’t convenient for bathing or using the bathroom compared to the comfort of his own refuge.

If it hadn’t been for Cao Daren’s previous engineering experience of often eating and sleeping outdoors, he wouldn’t have found it easy to leave a good refuge and come live in a tent with Chen Xin under current circumstances.

Seeing that Cao Daren had no issues, Chen Xin turned his gaze to the researchers and said: "Since construction has started on this side, please report to the higher-ups as soon as possible about inviting geologists to help. Also, please have the materials I need sent over promptly."

"Don’t worry, I’ll contact the higher-ups right away. I believe the next train from the three towns will deliver both the supplies and the geology experts." The researcher nodded and immediately turned to find a place where he could contact the upper departments.

Watching the researcher leave, Chen Xin said nothing, just nodded toward Cao Daren and said: "’Daren’, arrange for the workers to start construction as we discussed."

"Okay! I’ll arrange for the workers to start as soon as the water stops spraying." Cao Daren nodded, as this was his job.

————————————————

"Sister Lan, could you please help take this patient to the back ward? Thank you!" A special police officer wearing an exoskeleton handed a patient who had just undergone surgery over to Qin Lan, asking her to take the patient to the ward.

Qin Lan nodded, took the patient, and headed towards the ward.

Chen Xin was busy with the heating hub, while Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan stayed behind at the rescue station to help.

Although the rescue station had been bolstered by sixty more medical staff from the three towns, alleviating the shortage of personnel significantly, the patient load from Lin City was overwhelming, and nearly a hundred medical staff and fifty volunteers were still extremely busy every day.

However, with the arrival of the second batch of medical personnel, the operation of the rescue station had become smoother, and more patients could be treated each day. The situation was gradually improving.

The reason Qin Lan and the special police were helping to transport patients was mainly due to the rescue station being short-handed, and they volunteered to assist. Given that Qin Lan had Power Armor and the special police had Exoskeletons, they were better equipped to handle many tasks compared to ordinary medical staff.

After all, Power Armor and Exoskeletons are far stronger than ordinary people, allowing one person to transport a patient single-handedly rather than needing two or three people.

Qin Lan transported the patient to the ward and helped the medical staff place the patient onto the bed before leaving the ward.

Although she was merely assisting, there were numerous tasks to handle.

Fortunately, the staffing situation at the rescue station had improved, allowing medical staff to rotate shifts, rest, and eat without overloading.

Qin Lan was kept busy until a medical staff member called her to eat, only then did she realize it was mealtime.

Joining the medical staff at the temporary canteen, Qin Lan saw Mo Qingyan managing a team of volunteers to serve meals to the medical staff and greeted her.

Mo Qingyan originally wanted to help in the wards as well but later found the logistics side to be more suitable for her, so she assisted in the canteen.

It’s undeniable that Mo Qingyan’s ability to manage such a large outdoor goods store demonstrated her strong organizational skills, and the simple operation of a canteen was extremely orderly under her management.

"Ah Lan, you’re here? We received some fresh vegetables today; make sure to eat some to supplement your vitamins!" Mo Qingyan smiled at Qin Lan, but remembering something, she asked: "Is Ah Xin still busy over there and hasn’t come back?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 363 - 261: Not Coming Back Tonight
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In the evening, when Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan came to find Chen Xin after finishing their own things, they saw he was still busy.

The drilled well had stopped spewing water, and the workers had already moved the drilling rig and were now clearing snow to prepare the site for constructing the thermal hub.

The workers were clearing the snow quickly, and there was already a snow pit over three meters deep on the ground. Relying on his Power Armor, Chen Xin was helping the workers lift the cleared snow from the bottom of the pit for easier transport.

"Ah Xin!" Qin Lan called out to Chen Xin, who was helping to lift snow from the pit.

"Ah Lan, Dragon Girl! Why are you guys here?" Chen Xin greeted the two, dumped a basket of snow onto a small cart beside him, threw the basket back into the pit, and then paused his work to ask the two: "Isn’t it busy at the aid station? How come you have time to come find me?"

"The aid station is okay. With the arrival of the second batch of rescue teams, things are running smoothly. After finishing up, we still have time to rest," Qin Lan replied, smiling at Chen Xin and answering his question.

Mo Qingyan, watching the busy workers on the site, asked Chen Xin curiously: "Foodie, you guys aren’t resting yet this late? It’s tough!"

Although it was always dark and hard to distinguish day from night, most people still maintained their pre-disaster schedules to avoid confusion over time.

So considering the time, it was already eight or nine at night, theoretically time for Chen Xin and his crew to have finished work for the day and be preparing to rest, yet they were still busy.

"It’s alright. Today we mainly waited for the hot spring well to stop spraying, so we started late. The night work is almost done. We’ll work on it during the day tomorrow, and then we’ll have the snow cleared and start construction the day after," Chen Xin explained why they were still working at night.

But when Mo Qingyan asked, Chen Xin looked at the time and the depth of the snow pit, considered for a moment, and shouted to the workers: "Let’s call it a day, folks. Go back and rest well. We’ll start early tomorrow and try to clear everything in one day, so we can start construction the day after."

"Oh!" The workers cheered upon hearing they could rest, put their tools down, climbed out of the pit, and headed towards the tents.

Seeing the workers heading towards the tents, Chen Xin also walked towards his temporary place with Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan.

Upon reaching the snow cave, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan were visibly startled. Though they knew Chen Xin’s conditions weren’t great and he wasn’t staying in a house, they hadn’t expected his accommodations to be such an outdoor makeshift setup.

Even though neither Qin Lan nor Mo Qingyan expected Chen Xin to actually be camping in a snow cave, their reactions were quite different.

Looking at Chen Xin’s tent in the snow cave, Qin Lan’s first instinct was to feel sorry for him, and she instinctively said: "Ah Xin, are you sleeping in just this tent at night? The conditions are too harsh. Why don’t you sleep in the car instead? I can ask Han Ping to drive the car over!"

"It’s not as bad as you think. There’s a heater in the tent, so it’s not cold," Chen Xin smiled at Qin Lan. Although he appreciated her concern, he didn’t feel his situation was so harsh.

With that, Chen Xin took off his Power Armor and led the two of them into the tent.

The tent wasn’t very soundproof, and the workers’ voices outside could be heard clearly inside Chen Xin’s tent.

Listening to the noise, Qin Lan couldn’t help but frown, feeling even more sympathy for Chen Xin. As Chen Xin’s girlfriend, she knew how much he hated noise, especially since even the slightest sound could wake him up when sleeping.

"The tent isn’t soundproof. If someone snores at night, won’t it keep you from sleeping?" Qin Lan looked at Chen Xin, still hoping to persuade him to sleep in the car.

She didn’t have any ulterior motives, just purely hoped Chen Xin could sleep a bit better at night.

"It’s fine. With earplugs, you can’t hear much, and if it’s really bad, I can wear the driving suit and helmet, and hear nothing at all," Chen Xin patted the driving suit on him. The suit was theoretically fully isolated from the outside, so blocking some noise was just a minor issue.

Chen Xin hadn’t done this because wearing the driving suit to sleep was a bit uncomfortable for his head.

Seeing Chen Xin say this, Qin Lan realized there was no use in persuading him and gave up on convincing him to sleep in the car, though her gaze towards Chen Xin carried a hint of reproach.

Of course, Chen Xin sensed the grievance in Qin Lan’s gaze but couldn’t agree to go sleep elsewhere.

After all, he also wanted to sleep comfortably, but everyone was sleeping in tents, and Chen Xin couldn’t bring himself to single himself out by sleeping in a luxurious RV.

In the past, Chen Xin thought that sharing hardships was just an act to win people over, but when it came down to doing things, who really cares to think that much?

For someone genuinely doing the work, eating and living with the workers on-site is perfectly normal.

"Since you don’t want to go back, let’s leave it at that. Do you have anything to eat here?" Mo Qingyan tugged Qin Lan and then casually looked around Chen Xin’s tent for food: "I remember you said before you wanted to see how the Inuit live in snow houses in the Arctic. This should count as experiencing it firsthand, right?"

"That’s true, but traveling and actually living in such an environment are two completely different experiences. Dragon Girl, didn’t you also say before you wanted to experience an ice hotel that only operates in winter?" Chen Xin joked back with Mo Qingyan, using what she had said before.

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s words, Mo Qingyan nodded generously: "Yes, I really like the ice hotels in Northern Europe. They say the rooms have furniture made of ice and snow, even the beds are laid with fur-covered ice blocks, but you can sleep in a sleeping bag only wearing underwear. Just thinking about it is exciting!"

Listening to Mo Qingyan, Chen Xin couldn’t help but laugh: "If you really want to experience sleeping in just your underwear in below-zero temperatures, it’s simple. Stay the night. The tent has a heater, and it’s around five or six degrees. Even if you kick off your blanket, you won’t freeze into an ice block."

Chen Xin was originally joking, but Mo Qingyan, somehow excited, nodded happily and said: "Alright, I won’t go back tonight!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 364 - 262 Shared Lodging
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Qin Lan’s eyes sharpened instantly when she heard Mo Qingyan say she wasn’t going back that night. However, seeing Mo Qingyan making a placating face with her back to Chen Xin, she hesitated for a moment before nodding in agreement, saying nothing.

On certain matters, they had actually reached a tacit understanding, though what this understanding was wasn’t something to be shared with outsiders.

The only thing known was that this consensus between the two was something good for Chen Xin.

Even though Qin Lan did not object, Chen Xin couldn’t help but raise an eyebrow. He was just joking, yet Mo Qingyan seriously wanted to stay. He couldn’t help but say, "Are you really going to stay? It’s all guys here. Where will you sleep? All the tents are occupied."

"I’ll sleep in yours, can’t I? You’re the only one sleeping in it, right? Your tent should have no problem accommodating two people," Mo Qingyan said nonchalantly as if testing whether Chen Xin’s tent could fit two people.

But as soon as she lay down, Mo Qingyan had to sit back up. She hadn’t taken off her exoskeleton, and lying down obviously poked her in the back.

Seeing Mo Qingyan’s display, Chen Xin looked pleadingly at Qin Lan for help, but Qin Lan acted as if she hadn’t seen it and simply discussed with Mo Qingyan whether it would be cold sleeping here at night.

"Oh, it won’t be cold! There are sleeping bags and a heater, the temperature should be sufficient," Mo Qingyan said, unconcerned, patting her impressive chest and pointing to the sleeping bag Chen Xin had set aside. "I’ve stocked this brand of sleeping bags before, the quality is very good. Even if the outside temperature is -10℃, you can strip inside the sleeping bag and still not feel cold. Also, the inside space is large, even a single-person sleeping bag can fit two people in a pinch, or you can put clothes in to warm, so you won’t freeze when you get up."

Seeing Mo Qingyan’s demeanor and Qin Lan’s lack of action, Chen Xin glanced at them helplessly. Although he didn’t understand what these two women were up to, he had no way of truly driving Mo Qingyan away.

"How could two people squeeze in? You girls can squeeze into one sleeping bag since you’re small, but men can’t." Though Chen Xin couldn’t directly drive Mo Qingyan away, he still tried to refuse her stay. "And I only have one sleeping bag, Dragon Lady, you couldn’t possibly squeeze into the same sleeping bag with me, right?"

"Why not? Didn’t we all squeeze into one tent before when we went camping?" Mo Qingyan said with an indifferent attitude.

Though Mo Qingyan’s indifference was a façade, what she said was true. In the past, when a group of them went camping and tents were limited, they indeed squeezed into one tent without distinction between male and female.

At that time, everyone wore plenty of clothes and each had a sleeping bag, so squeezing together wasn’t a problem.

With Mo Qingyan saying this, Chen Xin really couldn’t refute and reluctantly shook his head, left the tent, and fetched two spare sleeping bags from the storage area. After throwing one to Mo Qingyan, he grudgingly accepted her staying.

"A Lan, why don’t you stay too? It’s been a while since the three of us had a chat," Chen Xin said, simply letting go. To avoid Qin Lan overthinking or misunderstandings between her and Mo Qingyan, he invited Qin Lan to stay the night too.

When Qin Lan heard Chen Xin say this, she glanced at Mo Qingyan, whose expression had turned sour, and couldn’t help but smile mischievously, nodding. "Sure, although we usually live together, it has indeed been a while since we had a heart-to-heart chat."

Upon hearing Qin Lan say this, Mo Qingyan’s face fell, clearly showing things had strayed from her original expectations.

------------------------------------------

At night, the workers’ rhythmic snoring came from outside the tent, inside three sleeping bags were laid out, and the three of them were already nestled in them.

Both sets of Power Armor had been placed outside the tent, and Mo Qingyan had also taken off her Exoskeleton Protective Suit, all three dressed in close-fitting garments.

The temperature inside the tent wasn’t low; the heater worked well, providing enough warmth to keep it at an acceptable level.

Chen Xin slept on the edge, Qin Lan in the middle, and Mo Qingyan on the other side.

Originally, according to Mo Qingyan’s plan, she should have been sleeping next to Chen Xin, using the excuse of the cold at night to sneak into Chen Xin’s sleeping bag, making certain things happen naturally.

But clearly, Chen Xin was still traditional or had certain boundaries, placing Qin Lan between them, conveniently separating him and Mo Qingyan.

Mo Qingyan had to give up a plan she prepared for a long time, finally mustering the courage, just shy of the crucial moment, and truly engaged in the long-lost chatting with Chen Xin and Qin Lan.

"Speaking of it, why did you come to Lin City, A Xin? Even with ’Daren’s’ cry for help, you actually didn’t need to get involved, right?" Mo Qingyan inquired curiously. To her, Chen Xin passing on Cao Daren’s call for help to the city authority was fulfilling his duty; there was no need to come to Lin City.

Doing so much, not to mention sleeping in a tent and living so frugally, made it confusing for Mo Qingyan.

It wasn’t that Mo Qingyan thought Chen Xin shouldn’t have come, she just felt there was no need for him to go through such hardships.

Regarding this question, Chen Xin smiled and replied, "Perhaps it’s just a sense of self-satisfaction. I feel like I have the capability, so I want to help those in need."

Chen Xin’s answer was heartfelt.

Even before obtaining the system, Chen Xin was someone who enjoyed helping others, but back then, he only helped within his means and wouldn’t do things as daringly as he does now.

However, after obtaining the system and gaining abilities others couldn’t, he naturally felt he should do more.

This wasn’t the "with great power comes great responsibility" superhero trope, but rather the ancestral teaching of "help oneself in poverty, help the world in wealth."

Chen Xin now met the standard of "wealth," so naturally, his consideration was "helping the world," not the previous goal of "help oneself" of ensuring good living for himself.

Regarding Chen Xin’s answer, neither Mo Qingyan nor Qin Lan felt anything was wrong. Since Chen Xin wanted to do it, they naturally had no reason to oppose.

However, Qin Lan still asked a follow-up question, "So, to what extent do you plan to do this, A Xin? You can’t stay here forever, right?"

"Of course not. My plan is..." Chen Xin smiled and began to explain his intentions to the two of them.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 365 - 263 Switch
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin’s plan wasn’t fully developed before he arrived in Lin City.

His ideas and plans gradually took shape and outlined after arriving in Lin City, and then slowly matured into a coherent concept.

In Chen Xin’s plan, clearing the train station is the first step, the second part is to build the heating hub and Energy Tower, which is what Chen Xin is currently working on.

Among them, Chen Xin will also construct a loading and unloading station at the train station to improve freight handling efficiency, and use the heating hub to build a greenhouse to attempt crop cultivation.

Among these, the loading and unloading station at the train station is crucial for Lin City. After all, it’s not feasible to always mobilize the city’s labor force to unload trains, not only delaying other tasks but working together in large numbers isn’t efficient and doesn’t maximize individual strengths.
