Therefore, a station that can effectively improve material handling efficiency is very necessary for Lin City.

It’s like in construction games where without building resource buildings, it’s impossible to obtain enough resources to construct other buildings and maintain city operations.

But these are just the beginning for Chen Xin.

According to his plans conceived during this period, what he truly wants to achieve is to build a heating network in Lin City, composed of Energy Towers and heating hubs.

Initially, Chen Xin only envisioned upgrading the heating hub to an Energy Tower and increasing regional temperature to cover the greenhouse. However, after discussions with researchers, Chen Xin found his ideas weren’t complete. Thus, after continuously improving his concepts, the idea of the heating network emerged.

If the heating network can be successfully established, humans can break free from the constraints of shelters and return to living on the surface, which holds tremendous significance for all humanity.

Not to mention, once the dust in the sky settles and sunlight gradually returns, using sunlight to illuminate crops can save a lot of energy.

In Chen Xin’s plan, Lin City, as a pilot city for the heating network, will establish a large Energy Tower at the city center to spread heating, paired with several heating hubs to provide heating and power for buildings, allowing most citizens to return to surface living and resume production.

Meanwhile, numerous greenhouses will be established around the city outskirts, relying on heating hubs, for agricultural production.

Thus, Lin City’s overall living standard can be restored as much as possible to pre-disaster levels while also trying to bring the city’s productivity and food output back to its pre-disaster state.

It’s essential to know that the production capacity of Flame Country now is significantly different compared to the pre-disaster times. The remaining industrial production ability doesn’t even reach one-tenth of what it was.

Though the country is now striving to restore production, and after a year’s construction efforts, a considerable amount of production capacity has been recovered, the overall gap still exists.

Take Chen Xin’s district as a simple example. Previously, yearly production of chemical products from several large chemical and refining plants around the district was calculated in tens of thousands of tons. Now, all that production capacity is gone, leaving only a small portion in underground factories within the district, with overall recovery still far from realization.

Although some industrial production has been placed in shelters, various problems inherent in industrial production make placing it underground possible but problematic.

In addition, the previously raging blizzard lasting over half a month has slowed the overall recovery progress of the industrial system, making it understandable.

If Chen Xin’s concept of Energy Towers and heating hubs is successful and the heating network can be smoothly built, the country can utilize this to resume industrial production on the surface, bringing national post-disaster reconstruction into a new phase.

Of course, impacting the whole nation is Chen Xin’s ultimate goal for the plan. His current objective is to construct the heating network in Lin City, using Lin City as a pilot city to evaluate the impact of the "Energy Tower + heating hub" heating network on city production and living recovery.

This plan has been reported to the special group by researchers and has gained attention and support from the top levels of Flame Country.

The needed geologists and associated materials for Chen Xin have been prepared, along with a batch of engineering equipment and newly produced Exoskeleton Protective Suits, ready to be transported to Chen Xin.

————————————————————

Meanwhile, after discussing his aspirations with Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan, Chen Xin engaged in some other conversations with the two ladies, noticing they were beginning to doze off. Chen Xin reached out to turn off the tent lamp hanging from the tent top and told them, "Sleep well, we have to get up early tomorrow!"

Seeing this, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan said goodnight to Chen Xin, then tucked into their sleeping bags and closed their eyes.

Soon, only the sound of gentle breathing remained in the tent.

However, after a while, Qin Lan quietly opened her eyes and sneaked a glance at Chen Xin, seeing him with earplugs on and already asleep, she cautiously turned her gaze to the side towards Mo Qingyan.

As expected, Mo Qingyan hadn’t slept either, she also opened her eyes upon hearing the movement.

"What do we do now?" Mo Qingyan silently mouthed to Qin Lan, her eyes carrying a hint of grievance.

Qin Lan looked at Mo Qingyan, then turned back to glance at Chen Xin, and after thinking for a moment, she mouthed back to Mo Qingyan, "Later, I’ll sneak into Chen’s sleeping bag, when he lets his guard down, I’ll pretend to go to the bathroom, and then we switch."

"Will this work?" Although she was the one who insisted on staying overnight, Mo Qingyan still felt flustered and shy about this situation.

"It’ll work, just follow my lead." Qin Lan said confidently to Mo Qingyan, showing no signs of pushing her boyfriend away.

In fact, it’s nothing major, just two people squeezing into one sleeping bag for the night, Qin Lan didn’t think anything significant would happen.

Seeing Qin Lan’s confident demeanor, Mo Qingyan realized there’s no turning back now; missing this opportunity might mean waiting a long time for a second chance. So she bit her lip, overcoming her tension and embarrassment, and nodded firmly at Qin Lan.

Seeing Mo Qingyan’s agreement, Qin Lan’s face immediately showed a mischievous smile, as she crawled out of her sleeping bag, ready to sneak into Chen Xin’s sleeping bag. Meanwhile, she imagined the amusing scene when they exchange places, and how Chen Xin would react upon waking up to find someone different.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 366 - 264 Morning Rise
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"What are you doing!" Qin Lan’s scream early in the morning woke Chen Xin up directly.

Chen Xin, woken up by the scream, felt a bit confused and sensed that he was holding someone in his arms. He vaguely remembered that Qin Lan had crawled into his sleeping bag last night, but now Qin Lan was speaking, so who was he holding?

Chen Xin squinted his eyes hard, a bit unsure of the situation. He opened his eyes and looked to the side, only to see Qin Lan sleeping in the next sleeping bag, looking at him with a face full of anger, and yelling at him, "What are you doing!"

This left Chen Xin a bit puzzled. Qin Lan was beside him, so who was in his arms...?!

Chen Xin realized, looked down into his arms, and sure enough, Mo Qingyan was shrinking in his embrace with a blushing face, glanced up at Chen Xin, and seemingly out of shyness, buried her face in again.

"Qingyan? How did you end up in my sleeping bag?" Chen Xin wanted to push the person away, but because of the limited space inside the sleeping bag, he couldn’t, so he hurriedly and awkwardly pulled his hands away from Mo Qingyan.

After unzipping the sleeping bag and crawling out, Chen Xin looked at Qin Lan beside him, though his mind was a mess, he was thinking about what exactly happened.

Chen Xin remembered that when he went to sleep last night, Qin Lan was sleeping next to him, Mo Qingyan on the other side of Qin Lan, and the tent lights were always on, making it impossible for someone to crawl into the wrong sleeping bag at night.

Moreover, Chen Xin clearly recalled that even though someone had crawled into his sleeping bag last night, he distinctly felt an icy cold metal hand, making him sober up because Qin Lan’s prosthetic was too cold. It’s something he couldn’t have mistaken.

But why is it now that the person in his sleeping bag has become Mo Qingyan? What exactly happened in between?

Although Chen Xin couldn’t figure out the situation, he could be sure that something deliberately orchestrated happened last night.

Qin Lan, who had been in his arms, couldn’t have been replaced by Mo Qingyan unless Qin Lan had crawled out on her own. If Mo Qingyan hadn’t climbed into his sleeping bag actively, how could she have entered it from a sleeping bag separated by another person?

How exactly did these two change positions last night?

It was because he couldn’t figure this out that Chen Xin suddenly became suspicious.

With his suspicions, Chen Xin’s originally frantic emotions calmed down. He glanced at the still shy Mo Qingyan in the sleeping bag and the angry-faced Qin Lan next to him, suddenly noticing a detail.

Whether it was Qin Lan or Mo Qingyan, Chen Xin had known them for many years, and he was very familiar with their personalities.

Although Mo Qingyan often appeared very outgoing and was quite carefree, similar to a brother towards Chen Xin, Chen Xin knew she was always conservative and reserved about relationships and male-female interactions, so her current shy behavior was a normal response.

But Qin Lan was different. As someone he had been in a relationship with for a long time, Chen Xin understood very well that if he had any infidelity, Qin Lan would have immediately hit him hard, impossible to just calmly ask "what are you doing?"

What particularly caught Chen Xin’s attention was Qin Lan’s hand, which was relaxed at this time. But he clearly remembered, Qin Lan, whenever angry, would unconsciously clench her fists, a subconscious habit she herself wasn’t aware of.

So she wasn’t really angry? Was it just an act?

Thinking of this, Chen Xin seemed to have an understanding, as if he had figured it out. However, he was still unsure if his guess was correct, so he thought of testing them. Collecting himself, he put on a look of sudden realization, composed himself, and then seriously asked Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan, "Whose idea was it?"

As soon as Chen Xin spoke, Mo Qingyan’s face changed immediately, from shy to a bit panicked, while Qin Lan next to her tried to maintain her angry look, pretending to be furious as she said to Chen Xin, "What do you mean, whose idea? Xin, you haven’t explained yet, what exactly is going on! Hurry up and make it clear, what is going on?"

Hearing Qin Lan say this, Chen Xin immediately had a sense of confidence. If it was just a guess before, now he could be certain that what happened was something they planned.

Although Chen Xin didn’t know why Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan did this, he was sure that Qin Lan’s anger was feigned.

So, Chen Xin felt more at ease, but still maintained a serious expression on his face, looking over at Qin Lan: "Don’t you guys know what’s going on? Weren’t you the one who crawled into my sleeping bag last night, Lan? If you didn’t crawl out on your own, how could Qingyan have gotten in? And if you were truly angry, would you ask me to explain what happened? With your personality, I’d be eating a fist right now."

"Are you saying I have a bad temper!" Qin Lan, hearing Chen Xin say this, suddenly wanted to reach out and grab his collar.

"Look at that, that’s precisely what you really look like when you’re angry!" Chen Xin pointed at Qin Lan’s reaching hand, loudly proclaiming her behavior while confidently asking her, "So this was your idea, wasn’t it, Lan?"

Being pointed out by Chen Xin, Qin Lan couldn’t keep up her angry expression, let out a humph, and then asked Chen Xin, "How did you figure it out?"

Mo Qingyan was also embarrassed as she crawled out of Chen Xin’s sleeping bag and sat up straight next to Qin Lan.

"I understand Qingyan; she wouldn’t actively do something like throwing herself at me. On the other hand, you, Lan..." Chen Xin paused here, casting a meaningful glance at Qin Lan before continuing, "If you could see me embarrassed and in a tricky situation, you’d definitely want to prank me."

Hearing Chen Xin’s words, both Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan’s expressions became a bit awkward, unsure of what to say.

Although the situation had proceeded as they planned, the outcome was evidently beyond their expectations, especially being exposed by Chen Xin, putting both of them in quite an awkward position.

However, Chen Xin obviously had no intention of delving deeper into the matter. He fully understood what it meant for the traditionally-minded Mo Qingyan to have spent the night in his arms—it was a clear expression of her feelings.

Qin Lan’s active role in plotting the event clearly indicated she did not oppose Mo Qingyan’s feelings for him, and the two might have already reached some agreement.

Though enjoying the happiness of two lovers should be a delightful thing, and Chen Xin didn’t resist the idea, he just felt that the current scenario wasn’t the right time to address this matter.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 367 - 265 Dumplings
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After the morning’s events, Chen Xin didn’t stay in the tent, as that would only deepen the awkwardness between the three.

He dressed, washed up a bit, grabbed something to eat, and left the tent; other workers outside were also gradually getting up and preparing to work.

Mo Qingyan and Qin Lan, inside the tent, watched Chen Xin leave, both with expressions that weren’t particularly pleasant.

Qin Lan felt embarrassed because Chen Xin saw through things, and felt guilty towards Mo Qingyan, while Mo Qingyan felt disappointed and shy; after all, she had been so proactive, yet Chen Xin had shown no response.

However, neither of them chose to stop Chen Xin to ask for an explanation, since it could still be said that last night was a joke and there’s room for maneuver. If they forced things out into the open for Chen Xin to make a statement, and he refused, there would be no room left at all.

So, after exchanging a silent gaze, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan also tidied up, left the tent, and prepared to go back.

-----------------------------------

This interlude didn’t affect Chen Xin’s work. Although deep down he might have fantasized about having Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan in bed with him, he still had to focus on work seriously.

The snow near the hot spring well had been cleared out into a pit yesterday; according to Chen Xin’s estimated schedule, today’s clearing of snow would be completed, preparing the site needed for construction.

After breakfast, Chen Xin and the workers headed to the site together, continuing the work unfinished yesterday.

The weather now was much better compared to before. Although still very cold with occasional strong winds, compared to the snowstorm days where snow could pile meters deep in a day, it was pretty good that there was just a thin layer of snow on the tools left by the workers at the site for clearing snow last night.

The workers descended into the snow pit, shook off the dusting of snow on the tools, and began working again.

The workers actually didn’t know what exactly they were supposed to do there. Chen Xin merely issued tasks, and they just worked accordingly.

Regarding what they were specifically doing and its significance, although it might effectively improve work efficiency by telling the workers, Chen Xin still did not explain these to them.

In fact, it made little difference. Given the circumstances of the world now, as long as there’s work, even work like shoveling snow without reason, the workers would diligently do it. After all, working a day means getting a day’s ration, which in these times is something no one can resist.

The workers’ speed in clearing snow wasn’t slow. Although paid daily, these workers did not attempt to slack off to extend the deadline to cheat more days’ rations. On the contrary, they were working very hard, neither needing supervision nor motivational tactics.

Chen Xin understood the workers’ mindset very well. In fact, the vast majority of people did not have thoughts or intentions of slacking off; as long as the daily wages were paid promptly, people would naturally be earnest and diligent in completing the work.

Knowing that in today’s environment, having a job, being able to eat one’s fill, and support a family is already a very fortunate thing.

Thus, these workers worked diligently, progressing even faster than Chen Xin had anticipated.

By noon, more than two-thirds of the total workload had been cleared in the snow pit.

At this pace, Chen Xin estimated that the entire clearing would be finished by afternoon.

Naturally, Chen Xin was very satisfied with this. Though today’s planned work only included clearing the snow pit, if the clearing was completed earlier, the saved time could be used to either let the workers off early or prepare the parts and materials for the heating hub at the site for tomorrow’s construction—both very good options.

As Chen Xin was contemplating whether to let the workers leave early to rest and recharge for tomorrow’s work, or first transport the prepared parts and materials from the snow cave, staff from Lin City drove a somewhat unusual vehicle to find him.

"Hello, are you Mr. Chen? We’re from Lin City Hall. The city hall knows you guys are working here, and specifically sent us to greet you." The staff introduced themselves to Chen Xin and pointed to the vehicle they arrived in, saying, "Today is New Year’s Eve. The city hall is very grateful to your rescue team for coming from the city to help us. They feel sorry about you not being able to go back and reunite with family for the New Year, so they specially prepared dumplings for you."

"Is today already New Year’s Eve?" Chen Xin looked at the staff, surprised. Since the meteor struck and plunged the sky into a long night of darkness, he had long stopped paying attention to the days, mostly checking his watch just to confirm whether it’s AM or PM.

But now it was already New Year’s Eve, something Chen Xin truly hadn’t expected.

Thinking of this, he decided on the matter he had hesitated over. He said to the staff, "Thank you so much, I actually forgot today was New Year’s Eve! Are the dumplings in the car? Please send them to our quarters where I can let the workers eat after they’re cooked."

"This vehicle is a field canteen, and we can cook right here." The staff, hearing Chen Xin’s words, quickly responded that the vehicle could cook the dumplings right there.

However, Chen Xin shook his head: "Out here without cover, even if cooked, the cold wind would freeze them; our quarters have heaters, a slightly warmer temperature where workers can have hot dumplings."

"Oh, I see! I’ll have the driver move the car over there now." The staff understood, nodded quickly, and directed the driver to head towards the snow cave Chen Xin indicated.

Meanwhile, Chen Xin hurried to find Cao Daren and told him about this matter.

"Oh my, I actually forgot it’s New Year’s Eve!" Cao Daren slapped his forehead, clearly forgetting about it too.

But being able to eat some dumplings was undoubtedly a hard-to-resist temptation for Cao Daren.

He checked the time and said to Chen Xin, "Writer, how about letting the workers go back to eat dumplings first? Anyway, there’s just a bit of work left, it will surely be finished this afternoon. Let them eat some hot dumplings, then work hard this afternoon to finish early, and give them a holiday to properly celebrate New Year’s Eve. What do you think?"

"That’s what I was thinking as well." Chen Xin laughed.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 368 - 266: New Year Celebration
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A steaming plate of dumplings was something these workers hadn’t had in a long time.

Throughout the year after the disaster, various difficulties kept coming one after another. Though food was never scarce to the point of starvation, those who didn’t work still only managed to not go hungry, and to be full, they had to work.

Even for those who came to work outdoors every day, the meals were just standard shelter food, which everyone was already sick of.

If you wanted to eat something different, unless you were like Chen Xin, who had a private shelter and abundant supplies, otherwise, no matter how sick of it you were, all you had was the monotonous shelter food.

So, a plate of dumplings for these workers, they probably last had it before the disaster during the New Year.

Thus, these dumplings acted as an extraordinary catalyst for these workers, making them work energetically in the afternoon after eating.

Thanks to the greatly increased efficiency of the workers, they even completed the work earlier than Chen Xin initially anticipated, and transported the heating hub components to the construction site, ready for construction.

Seeing the workers working so diligently, Chen Xin didn’t keep them, but after letting them finish work, he told them to go home and reunite with their families today.

Moreover, Chen Xin also had them take some of the uneaten dumplings sent by the staff at noon as a New Year’s benefit to share with their families at home.

Even though these dumplings were vegetable-filled without meat, they were still considered a rare delicacy.

Because it’s New Year, when Cao Daren saw Chen Xin releasing all the workers, he also went home after apologizing to Chen Xin, as he is also from Lin City, with his family in his own shelter, needing to celebrate the New Year with them.

Thus, only Chen Xin was left at the construction site. After hesitating and wavering for a while, he too simply left, not staying alone.

——————————————————

Dumplings were also delivered by the Lin City staff to the aid station, allowing the hard-working medical staff to enjoy a good dumpling meal.

Originally, the medical staff planned to share the dumplings with the patients in the ward, but neither the Lin City staff nor the patients accepted this goodwill.

In the eyes of the Lin City staff and these patients, this batch of dumplings was for the medical staff, representing the gratitude of everyone in Lin City for the aid team that came to help them; they couldn’t and shouldn’t share these dumplings.

The aid team’s medical staff couldn’t refuse further and, to avoid waste, quietly accepted this goodwill. However, when dividing dumplings in the canteen, they still added a few to each patient’s lunch, so everyone celebrated the New Year together.

Because it’s New Year, the Lin City Hall staff made an effort to organize and decorate, hoping to bring some holiday atmosphere in this first New Year after the disaster, to dispel the heavy gloom in front of them.

But the resources were really limited, the staff did their best and only managed to get some red paper, which a few skilled staff and medical personnel from the aid team wrote Spring Festival couplets and cut into paper decorations, pasting them at the entrance of the medical tents as a decoration, bringing a bit of festive atmosphere.

Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan were also helping the aid team in their respective roles; they only realized it was New Year when they saw the staff delivering dumplings around noon.

This made the two somewhat regretful; after the commotion last night, they feared Chen Xin wouldn’t see them today, and Chen Xin might end up spending New Year alone.

Thinking of this, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan both wanted to find Chen Xin, hoping that no matter what, he wouldn’t spend the New Year all alone.

However, after a day of busyness, when the two got dumplings from the canteen at night, intending to take them to Chen Xin and searching in the RV for a thermal bucket, they opened the door to the long-missed aroma of meat.

The two focused their eyes on the table, where several steaming dishes were laid out, including not only fresh vegetables but also plenty of rare meat, and in the middle of these dishes was a white clay pot, covered with a lid, with unknown contents inside.

Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan looked in astonishment toward the RV’s kitchen, and as expected, Chen Xin was at the counter, stir-frying dishes in the pan, releasing a rich fragrance.

"You’re back? Wash your hands and get ready for dinner, it’s New Year today, so I specially made old duck soup and red-cooked chicken, unfortunately couldn’t get fish, so no ’fish every year’." Chen Xin smiled at the two while pouring a prepared starch mixture into the pot, thickening the red-cooked chicken.

Seeing Chen Xin appear, and his familiar actions, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan felt a bit at a loss; they hadn’t expected Chen Xin to return after this morning’s event, and prepare a table full of dishes.

But standing there dazed wasn’t a solution, so it was Qin Lan who reacted quickly, placing the dumplings they brought back on the counter, saying to Chen Xin, "We brought back some dumplings, let’s heat them for tonight! You should eat some dumplings for New Year."

"Just leave them there, I’ll cook them later." Chen Xin nodded, glanced at the dumplings, and told Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan, "Hurry up and wash your hands, I’m almost done, ready to eat soon."

Hearing Chen Xin say this, Qin Lan didn’t say much, pulling Mo Qingyan past Chen Xin, back to their cabins to take off their Protective Suits, then came out to wash their hands at the sink and sat down at the dining table.

After the two sat down, Chen Xin also brought out the red-cooked chicken from the pot, placing it on the dining table.

Habitually, Chen Xin cleaned up the counter, then sat down at the dining table amid Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan’s apprehension.

No one moved their chopsticks, as if waiting for something.

"Ah Xin, why did you come back?" Finally, Mo Qingyan asked the question.

"The workers finished early, knowing it’s New Year, I gave them an early leave to go home for New Year," Chen Xin smiled at Mo Qingyan, explaining to her, "Daren is a local, he also needed to go home for New Year, leaving only me at the site, thinking it over, it wasn’t necessary to stay, so I came back. Knowing it’s New Year, still thought to make something good, exchanged for the chicken and duck with Bao Zhen."

"Shall we start eating?" After listening to Chen Xin’s explanation, Qin Lan, seeing Chen Xin hadn’t mentioned the morning event again, was very relieved, thus urging Chen Xin to start the meal.

"Let’s wait for Han Ping, I sent him to fetch some drinks, he should be back soon." As Chen Xin spoke, the door opened, Han Ping came back with the drinks.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 369 - 267: Caught in a Dilemma
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A rare New Year’s Eve dinner, whether for Chen Xin, Qin Lan, Mo Qingyan, or even the freeloading Han Ping, was precious enough and endlessly memorable.

Now that it’s the apocalypse, the lavish feasts of previous New Year’s Eves with a dozen or so dishes of chicken, duck, fish, and meat are hard to come by. But having meat, vegetables, chicken, and duck is already considered very bountiful.

Plus, it’s New Year’s, and the four of them are away from home, not celebrating with their families. In such a unique setting, everyone inevitably drank a few more glasses.

Han Ping, who at least still had enough alertness and owing to his good tolerance for alcohol from his army days, wasn’t drunk after a few drinks. So, after finishing the meal, he packed some leftovers and took the remaining unfinished alcohol off the vehicle, planning to drink a couple more glasses with the special forces officers he met recently, also to avoid the three on the vehicle.

Though he wasn’t talkative, he had the ability to observe, and he naturally noticed the strange atmosphere between Chen Xin, Qin Lan, and Mo Qingyan. Now that all three had been drinking, if something happened and he happened to witness it, wouldn’t it be like getting an eye infection?

After Han Ping got off, Mo Qingyan and Qin Lan managed to carry the drunken Chen Xin to his cabin.

"Xin smells of alcohol and hasn’t bathed for days. Why don’t we help him take a shower?" Qin Lan suggested to Mo Qingyan.

"Huh? Again? It was your idea last night, and the morning was already so awkward. Now you want to do it again? Aren’t you afraid he’ll really get mad tomorrow?" Mo Qingyan was a bit unhappy hearing Qin Lan still planning more antics and was also a bit hesitant.

After all, such an event just happened in the morning; even if she wanted to regain momentum, it wasn’t something she could immediately overcome.

But for Qin Lan, perhaps fueled by the alcohol, she was evidently a bit too excited now. Mo Qingyan knew this state very well, simply put, she was drunk and tipsy.

When she was still running a bar, although it was membership-based and relatively calm, there were still people who drank too much and made a scene. Mo Qingyan was very familiar with such situations.

Now, Qin Lan was undoubtedly taking advantage of the alcohol to try and do something reckless.

Yet somehow, whether it was Mo Qingyan herself having drunk too much, after a moment of fear, a sense of unwillingness began to rise in her heart.

She had already crawled into Chen Xin’s bed, and in the end, it turned into a farce. How could Mo Qingyan bear that?

"Hmph, so what? Anyway, he can’t beat me. At worst, it’s forceful persuasion! The first time I slept with him, that’s exactly how I did it, and didn’t he still yield to me?" Qin Lan, with the demeanor of a rogue, was hard to reconcile with her previous image as a calm and serious special forces sniper.

Provoked by Qin Lan’s words, Mo Qingyan didn’t bother to ponder whether Qin Lan’s ways were a result of drunkenness or suppression of her true nature. She was provoked by Qin Lan’s phrase "forceful persuasion."

So Mo Qingyan nodded in agreement with Qin Lan’s suggestion and reached for Chen Xin’s collar.

——————————————

Silence throughout the night.

When Chen Xin woke up, the first thing he saw was the familiar ceiling of his RV cabin.

Surrounding the ceiling are soft light strips, providing illumination without being glaring. Though the light wasn’t very bright, it was enough for Chen Xin to see his situation clearly.

Both his arms were pinned down, feeling heavy on both left and right. Especially on his chest, it felt like two weights were pressing down.

Looking down, on his right was someone with neat short hair and a black prosthetic limb. From the familiar posture and the touch under his right hand, Chen Xin recognized it as Qin Lan.

Sighing helplessly, Chen Xin turned his gaze to the left.

A mass of dark hair rested on his chest, the strands scattered over the face, and from the weight his left hand felt, Chen Xin, despite how dense he might be, knew who this was and what the situation now was.

Though a man’s dream might be to embrace on both sides, realizing it unknowingly wasn’t the result Chen Xin desired.

Especially after such a hard-earned embrace on both sides with no memory of it, only to be responsible for something he couldn’t recall—what a lousy feeling!

Although Chen Xin had no intention of shirking responsibility, he had actually contemplated taking responsibility for Mo Qingyan since the morning. Yet, speaking of responsibility under such circumstances was difficult, considering that no matter how he put it, Qin Lan was his woman; opening up to another woman would tantamount to cheating.

Or to put it more aptly, two-timing in the presence of his girlfriend.

Yet with both girls throwing themselves at him, freshly cleaned and offered to him, aside from wanting to relive the evening and recover his memory, Chen Xin decided to take responsibility for Mo Qingyan.

As for Qin Lan, Chen Xin likewise had no intention of letting her down.

Still, caught between the two, Chen Xin found it hard to overcome that barrier.

Despite the talk, achieving the ideal embrace was a dream for a man, yet when the moment arrived, dealing with two women was challenging—given the monogamic doctrine instilled in him from childhood.

However, reality allowed little time for thought. As Chen Xin pondered what to say, Mo Qingyan moved distinctly on his left chest.

This made Chen Xin instinctively move his left hand pinned under Mo Qingyan, inadvertently grasping what he touched previously due to positioning.

"...Let go!" Mo Qingyan’s voice was barely audible, but filled with embarrassment.

Seeing this, Chen Xin quickly released his left hand, shifting his arm away from Mo Qingyan.

This movement also affected Qin Lan on the right, who turned over and awoke.

To worsen matters, Qin Lan instinctively hit the light switch on her side.

The room illuminated instantly, allowing Chen Xin to see the two uncovered bodies clearly.

Evidently, neither he nor Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan had worn anything to bed.

"Wow, I didn’t know you had such a great figure, Qingyan! Xin got a great deal!" Qin Lan teased Mo Qingyan, deepening her blush, while tapping Chen Xin’s chest: "Hey, are you still going to play dumb now, Xin?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 370 - 268 Laying the Foundation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Of course, Chen Xin is not an irresponsible person, but given the current situation, he really can’t make any promises to Mo Qingyan.

In fact, no promises are needed between him and Mo Qingyan. What he needs to offer Mo Qingyan is merely emotional recognition and a change of relationship, from their original good friends to a romantic couple.

As for what’s truly important, it’s still how he plans to manage his relationship with the two women in the future.

Chen Xin has never experienced dating two girls at the same time, nor had he ever imagined he’d be sharing a bed with both Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan.

This leaves him somewhat at a loss with the new dynamic among the three of them.

So after saying some comforting words to Mo Qingyan and agreeing to some unequal treaties from Qin Lan, Chen Xin fled his RV in a slightly embarrassed manner and returned to the construction site.

Though Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan couldn’t help but feel a bit annoyed by Chen Xin’s escape, as no woman would want the man to run off on the first day of confirming a relationship.

However, both expressed understanding generously, willing to give Chen Xin, and also themselves, some time to adapt to the change in their three-way relationship.

To be honest, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan indeed also need time to adapt to the changes in their relationship.

After all, even if Qin Lan had already come to an agreement with Mo Qingyan, their relationship had always been good, but neither Chen Xin, Qin Lan, nor Mo Qingyan had ever imagined their relationship turning out this way.

So not only does Chen Xin need time to adapt and adjust, but they do as well.

————————————————

Back at the construction site, Chen Xin quickly refocused his energy on his work. Although he had just experienced changes that greatly impacted his life, his work still required his serious attention.

The workers who had gone home for the New Year holiday had returned to the site early in the morning, brimming with energy for work.

Chen Xin donned his Power Armor and went down to the bottom of the snow pit, where he saw the workers demolishing the exposed cement on the ground in preparation for laying the foundation of the heating hub.

The entire heating hub is over ten meters high, roughly equivalent to four or five floors, and inside, a complete set of evaporative cooling systems had to be installed, making the completed heating hub look like a standing iron tower.

Of course, the heating hub is now just a pile of unassembled parts, requiring workers to assemble piece by piece after the foundation has been laid.

Down in the snow pit, Chen Xin observed the workers using a pneumatic pick to break the cement on the ground, but it was ineffective, and the work progress was unsatisfactory.

Chen Xin had anticipated this situation. After all, at temperatures several degrees below zero, the cold had made the ground rock-hard, and even the snow that had turned into ice next to it was as hard as granite. Trying to punch through the already hard cement to create a hole large enough for the heating hub’s foundation was not an easy task.

However, Chen Xin obviously wouldn’t just watch the workers wasting time and effort slowly chiseling the floor. With the Power Armor, Chen Xin could easily break through the cement and make holes that met the design specifications.

"Step aside, let me handle this." Chen Xin said to the workers, stopping their work and moving aside. Only then did he squatted down, directing the mechanical arm at the spot where the workers had tried to demolish with the pneumatic pick.

The area just struck by the workers with the pneumatic pick only left a few white points on the ground, showing no signs of cracking.

Clearly, the freezing cold made the already sturdy cement even more robust and challenging to break through.

But this was no obstacle for Chen Xin’s mechanical arm. With the various tools on the mechanical arm working together, Chen Xin soon cut out a chunk of cement from the ground.

Though the cement was extremely hard and couldn’t be shattered by the pneumatic pick, it couldn’t withstand the cutting machine.

Cutting through the cement didn’t mean the work became any easier, only relatively less difficult.

Beneath the cement lay rubble and soil, materials used for the plaza’s past construction foundation, compacted by a roller ensuring the ground was level while pressing the soil and stones more tightly.

If it were just like this, letting the worker dig with hoes would finish the job, but the cold temperatures had turned the soil into frozen ground, not something that could be casually dug out by manpower.

In fact, Chen Xin let the workers give it a try, and even with the pneumatic pick, all they could do was chip a small dent in the frozen rubble, which wasn’t entirely useless, but creating the required foundation using just the pneumatic pick would take a very long time.

Faced with this situation, Chen Xin could only continue swinging his mechanical arm.

Thus, under the astonished gazes of the workers, Chen Xin’s mechanical arm created the entire foundation required for the heating hub construction on the ground with nearly ten times their efficiency.

"Alright, the foundation is done. The subsequent construction is in your hands now." Chen Xin stood up, feeling a bit sore in his waist, then told the workers to continue their work as he climbed out of the snow pit.

The workers were amazed at the efficiency of Chen Xin’s Power Armor, but they could only envy it, as they still had to continue their tasks using their diligent hands.

The main support columns of the heating hub were buried in the bases that had just been dug according to the markings on Chen Xin’s blueprint, assembling the other supporting components to secure the main columns and maintain horizontal and vertical positions, only then did they start pouring the cement into the foundations.

The cement the workers used wasn’t just ordinary past cement; while it could be used, it required temperatures above zero, which Chen Xin and the team currently lacked.

Therefore, they used anti-freezing cement mixed with Antifreeze and a Water Reducing Agent, ensuring the cement wouldn’t freeze rapidly due to water content, preventing cavities from forming within.

These workers were skilled, so there were no issues at this stage. All the support columns were successfully finished cementing according to construction requirements.

Furthermore, due to the cold weather, the cement soon became as hard as stone, firmly securing the heating hub’s support columns to the ground.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 371 - 369: Geological Expert
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

With several support columns as the meridians and dozens of circular rings as the parallels, a cylindrical structure made of a grid quickly rose in the snow pit.

This is the main structure of the heat hub; all the rings are nested on the support columns and connected to support points via welding and bolts, making the whole setup extremely stable. Even when Chen Xin pushed against it twice using Power Armor, it did not wobble.

The stability of the main support frame also means a solid foundation for the subsequent construction.

Workers began Phase Two of the construction by installing evaporative cooling devices and related piping on the main support frame and the snow pit’s walls.

The entire installation consists of four phases, with the remaining two involving the installation of the outer cooling panels and the overall assembly.

Compared to drilling and clearing snow, the installation is relatively simple. According to the original construction schedule, it should take the workers two days to complete the full installation.

However, thanks to the workers’ high morale, these energetic workers have significantly accelerated the schedule. Based on the current progress, the entire schedule has been advanced by approximately half a day, shortening the timeline by 30%, which is quite an impressive achievement.

Nonetheless, compared to the schedule progress, Chen Xin is more concerned that another issue might delay the timeline.

The concern is about the location of the water reinjection well.

This water reinjection well can’t be drilled just anywhere; the reason has been explained earlier. Until geological experts arrive, the construction of the reinjection well cannot commence.

Thus, Chen Xin is worried that the extraction well might be completed, and the heat hub could be operational, but the reinjection well might still lack a suitable location.

In such a case, unless Chen Xin decides to discharge the extractable geothermal water directly—which could be reused—the construction schedule will inevitably be delayed.

Fortunately, Chen Xin’s worry didn’t materialize. As workers were installing core components of the steam cooling devices on the main support frame, another train arrived at Lin City’s train station.

This train brought, besides government-dispatched disaster relief supplies in response to Lin City’s situation, the materials Chen Xin needed, along with the crucial geological experts.

Not just one geological expert arrived but a geological survey group composed of several geological experts.

"You must be Comrade Chen Xin, right? Hello, hello! I’m from Xiang Province’s Geological Bureau, my last name is Zhang, you can call me Old Zhang." An elderly man, with robust and powerful hands, warmly greeted Chen Xin: "These are my colleagues, as instructed by our superiors, we will cooperate with your work. Just let me know if you need anything."

This self-proclaimed Old Zhang is clearly nearing his eighties but is very vigorous. Even in a Protective Suit, Chen Xin could sense the vitality and spirit emitted from this elder, along with his energetic enthusiasm.

Chen Xin was somewhat surprised by Old Zhang’s age, but considering the time needed for scholarly work and the field data collection required in geological work, he wasn’t too shocked by the elder’s age.

As for the other geological survey team members who came with Old Zhang, they were relatively young, but only compared to Old Zhang. Besides two who seemed to be college students, the remaining three members were also over forty.

Through Old Zhang’s introductions, Chen Xin learned that these three middle-aged men were Old Zhang’s students from back in the day. After graduating, they have worked in geology for over twenty years, from young teens like the two college students, to now middle-aged uncles.

Looking at these geological experts, Chen Xin deeply felt what it means to dedicate a lifetime to one’s career.

Chen Xin did not engage in too much small talk with the geological experts. After greeting and introducing himself, Chen Xin used the mechanical arm of the Power Armor to project the heat hub design and explained to them: "This setup uses hydrothermal geothermal resources buried at a depth of about 400-800 meters to obtain thermal energy. The main working principle is to extract geothermal spring water, which is far warmer than the surrounding environment, for the evaporative cooling device to dissipate heat externally.

While the device can operate independently, directly extracting underground spring water would be a huge waste of underground water and geothermal resources. It would also affect the stability of underground geological structures, hence the need to drill a reinjection well in the same aquifer to re-inject the cooled spring water back underground.

Since we lack relevant geological knowledge and related data, we cannot determine where the reinjection well should be drilled. Therefore, we need Professor Zhang and your team to assist us in locating the distribution and paths of the aquifer we’ve reached and determining the location of the reinjection well so that we can promptly commence the reinjection well’s construction."

"This equipment is very convenient! Directly projecting the blueprints; if we had this when doing geological work in the past, we wouldn’t need to carry around a bunch of heavy and bulky blueprints. Technology is really advanced now!" Old Zhang remarked, nodding at Chen Xin: "I understand the situation already. Before coming, my superiors also briefed me about it, so I brought the geothermal exploration data collected in Xiang Province over the years. With these, we only need to conduct a field survey to determine the aquifer distribution and suitable drilling site for the reinjection well that Comrade Chen Xin needs."

Old Zhang seems to have developed the habit of swift action over years of work. While speaking with Chen Xin, he had the two young college students open their luggage and unfold the relevant maps before Chen Xin.

Old Zhang pointed to the symbols marked on the map and said to Chen Xin: "Comrade Chen Xin, look here. This is the mountainous area where Lin City is located. This mountain range is a fracture zone, and its geothermal resources are quite rich and dense.

The well you are drilling now should be in a geothermal layer covering the entire Lin City area. The underground energy storage is very abundant. It would be great if it could be effectively utilized!"

Old Zhang’s expression was filled with determined enthusiasm. Even though his eyebrows had turned white, he was much more spirited than the young college students responsible for moving items.

After explaining the relevant situation to Chen Xin, he left the two young college students to settle the luggage and set up their accommodation, while he and his three students, along with a Lin City staff member found by Chen Xin, headed out with the necessary surveying instruments.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 372 - 370: Location Confirmed
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The team of geological experts that came to support Chen Xin did not disappoint him, nor did they break their promise.

Professor Zhang, along with his three students, and the staff of Lin City, determined the location of the return water well in about half a day’s work.

"The distribution area of this aquifer is quite broad, generally spread from the train station as the central point, northwest towards the Seventh Middle School, and southeast to this hospital. You could say the area is indeed quite extensive." Professor Zhang spread out a map in front of Chen Xin, on which he had carefully marked the distribution range of the aquifer where Chen Xin and his team had drilled an extraction well at the train station square.

Chen Xin looked closely at the map. Although he didn’t understand much about geological science, he could clearly see the range marked with colored pencils by Old Zhang on this map.

Looking at the area covered by the shaded colors on the map, which included the former shopping plaza where the rescue station was located, he pointed at this spot on the map with his finger and asked Professor Zhang, "Professor Zhang, this shopping plaza is currently a refuge location in Lin City. The rescue teams from the city have also set up a rescue station here. Is this spot suitable for a return water well? Can we drill a return well here?"

"Here?" Professor Zhang looked at the position Chen Xin pointed to on the map, thought carefully, and responded, "This is indeed within the aquifer range, and the rock layer distribution is relatively even, so drilling a well wouldn’t be a problem."

Professor Zhang’s words made Chen Xin quite elated. If a well could be drilled at the rescue station’s location, he could place the return water well next to the rescue station and build the corresponding heating hub here to provide heating and power for the rescue station and the underground refuge.

This would be very useful and effective in aiding the survivors suffering from disaster in Lin City.

Although Professor Zhang didn’t deeply understand Chen Xin’s plans, he knew the impact and role a completed heating hub would have, and he also hoped that each heating hub could perform its best, being built in its most efficient position.

Given that multiple well sites had been determined during previous explorations, he added to Chen Xin, "In fact, aside from this point, Lin City’s Seventh Middle School and Xiangbei Hospital are also places where you can drill wells and establish the heating hubs you designed."

"Seventh Middle School? Xiangbei Hospital?" Chen Xin looked at the two points Professor Zhang indicated on the map, examined them closely, and asked Professor Zhang, "Professor Zhang, are these two points also suitable for well drilling? Are they for return wells or extraction wells?"

Faced with Chen Xin’s inquiry, Professor Zhang pointed to Lin City’s Seventh Middle School position on the map and introduced it to Chen Xin, "Lin City’s Seventh Middle School is located at the mountain’s base, at a higher altitude than the train station square, surrounded by what used to be farmland. Drilling a return well here is suitable, and it’s not far from your current extraction well at the train station square.

As for Xiangbei Hospital, the elevation here doesn’t differ much from the refuge and rescue station at the shopping plaza, so it doesn’t matter much which is the return well and which is the extraction well. If a distinction must be made, I suggest drilling the return well at the shopping plaza since, as you mentioned, there is a higher demand for heating there."

"Since you say so, Professor Zhang, let’s go with your suggestion. You’re the expert in geology, I’ll listen to you." Chen Xin readily agreed, finalizing the upcoming plan based on Professor Zhang’s advice.

Seeing that Chen Xin had determined the subsequent construction plan, Professor Zhang felt he had completed a task.

Yet, as he celebrated the task’s completion, Professor Zhang also inquired with some concern to Chen Xin, "Comrade Chen Xin, do you only plan for these four heating hubs? What about other sites in Lin City? How have you arranged them?"

"Other sites?" Chen Xin hesitated for a moment but quickly responded, "I’m not very clear about the situations elsewhere. The extraction well at the train station square was drilled purely to clear the train station, and it wasn’t selected by geological survey, they just dug a well here arbitrarily.

It’s only because you’ve come that we can properly select sites, but now we’ve only surveyed this aquifer, and the four places within its range are where heating hubs can be built. Other areas in Lin City are not on this aquifer, so even if I want to build heating hubs elsewhere, we need to first survey the underground geothermal resource distribution to be accurate!"

Hearing Chen Xin’s explanation, Professor Zhang, appearing well-prepared, pulled out another map and spread it out in front of Chen Xin, grinning as he said, "Young comrade, you can’t be sloppy in your work! How could you forget other places? This is a map drawn during the provincial geological bureau’s survey of geothermal resources across the province. Although it’s old, the general geography hasn’t changed.

Comrade Chen Xin, turn on your projector device and project Lin City’s current map onto this. Then, you can see whether Lin City’s current important positions have geothermal resource distribution, so you can know if heating hubs can be built."

This method clearly amazed Chen Xin. He raised his eyebrows in surprise and complied as Professor Zhang requested, activating the projection equipment on his Power Armor, and projected the city map of Lin City onto the existing map, aligning them.

"Now this is clear! Comrade Chen Xin, look at this; this is Lin City’s municipal government and the First People’s Hospital. This spot is right above an underground aquifer! I remember this aquifer is above a sulfur hot spring, which doesn’t meet the standards for medical-grade hot springs and has no tourism development value, but the water temperature is over sixty degrees, so it should be enough for your heating hub, right?" Professor Zhang cheerfully asked Chen Xin, looking at the map.

Chen Xin also nodded, pointing on the map, "It’s not just the city government and the First People’s Hospital, but also here, Lin City’s Sixth Middle School, Second People’s Hospital, and this First Middle School, all of these places can be used to build heating hubs, and these sites overlap with some of the planned refuges for Lin City. Constructing heating hubs here would cover these refuges!"

Saying this, Chen Xin adjusted the projection, also projecting the locations of several refuges in Lin City onto the map.

Seeing the projected refugee locations on the map, Professor Zhang nodded excitedly and happily said to Chen Xin, "This way, more than half of Lin City’s refuges would fall within the coverage of heating hubs!"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 373 - 371: Difficulties at Every Turn
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Since the well-drilling location has been selected, Cao Daren’s drilling rig naturally came into play once more.

He took the workers Chen Xin assigned to him to Lin City’s Seventh Middle School to prepare for the return water well drilling. Meanwhile, Chen Xin needed to continue working with the remaining workers to complete the installation and debugging of the heating hub.

As the two groups had to operate separately, Cao Daren’s team needed to establish a new base at the Seventh Middle School to set up their tents.

This led them to dig a pit in the snow as the first thing they did upon arriving at the Seventh Middle School.

After Cao Daren and his team of workers left the space in the snow cave near the railway station, it was not wasted. Professor Zhang and the members of the geological exploration team moved in and helped make some improvements to the snow cave, making it more comfortable to live in.

For those who have spent years conducting geological exploration in the wild, the time they spend in tents far exceeds the time they spend in houses.

Their constant travels through mountains and rivers have long eroded the bookish air from these geological scholars. Instead, they have accumulated a wealth of wilderness survival experience, and each one of them can be regarded as a master of wilderness survival.

In Professor Zhang’s view, the living conditions in snow cave tents were not much worse than when they were in the wild back then, and in many aspects, they were even better.

"This is much better than when I first started working! Back then I was just in my twenties, a young kid fresh out of school, joining the geological team to Wulingyuan for surveying. We didn’t have tents at that time; we just used branches and leaves to construct a shack and endured the wind and frost outdoors for a whole month!" During lunch, Professor Zhang, holding a well-worn aluminum lunch box, recounted his youthful experiences to Chen Xin while eating: "Unlike now, with tents, sleeping bags, and rain ponchos, we’re fully equipped! Back then, having one rubber raincoat was already quite remarkable."

The stories told by Professor Zhang touched Chen Xin quite a bit.

Considering Professor Zhang’s age, when he was in his twenties, it was the most turbulent era of the Flame Country. To endure harsh conditions to conduct geological research instead of engaging in movements was truly commendable in those days.

"Professor, why have you always used this old lunch box? In such severe weather, this old-fashioned metal lunch box cools down quickly and is very inconvenient to use. Let me get you a new lunch box!" Chen Xin noticed the lunch box in Professor Zhang’s hand. Though he didn’t know why the professor used such an old lunch box, Chen Xin wanted to get him a better one that at least could retain heat.

However, Professor Zhang shook his head, smiling as he declined Chen Xin’s kindness: "No need. This lunch box has been with me my whole life; there’s no need to replace this old companion when I’m old, and this lunch box holds great significance for me."

Professor Zhang’s words made Chen Xin raise an eyebrow, but he said nothing more about it.

"Professor, what is the distribution of dry hot rock resources around Lin City?" Chen Xin shifted the topic to work, asking about another issue he was currently most concerned about.

Compared to utilizing hydrothermal resources at a heating hub, what Chen Xin truly wanted was the Energy Tower capable of extracting deep-layer geothermal resources. After all, whether in terms of power or efficiency, an Energy Tower is far superior to a heating hub.

But what the Energy Tower requires is drilling deep into the ground for several kilometers, not just a few hundred meters.

Both in terms of construction volume and technical difficulty, they are entirely different.

"The terrain of Lin City is typically flanked by two mountains with a valley in between, with higher terrain in the south and lower in the north. Although the city is aligned east-west, the overall terrain still forms a north-south geological fault. The major geothermal resources of Xiang Province are distributed along this fault line." While eating, Professor Zhang explained to Chen Xin the topography and geothermal resource distribution of Lin City: "On this fault line, the main reserves of dry hot rocks of Xiang Province are distributed.

Due to the cause of the geological fault, the exploitation of shallow and intermediate geothermal resources around here is relatively easy, with many areas forming natural hot springs erupting to the surface. However, because of the fault formation, the geological structure here is relatively complex. If you want to exploit deep geothermal resources, drilling will be a huge challenge."

Hearing this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but feel somewhat disheartened.

Professor Zhang’s implication was clear: Chen Xin should focus first on developing shallow and intermediate geothermal resources, and think twice before exploiting deep underground resources.

Chen Xin naturally took Professor Zhang’s advice to heart, but he did not abandon his ideas about developing deep geothermal resources; instead, he considered them more carefully.

"Even if it’s a challenge, we have to face it now." Chen Xin’s tone was somewhat heavy, but equally determined, as he asked Professor Zhang, "Professor, if we want to extract deep geothermal resources in Lin City, what difficulties would we need to overcome?"

"There are indeed many. The first problem you must solve is the drilling rig issue." Putting down his spoon, Professor Zhang pointed out the biggest difficulty upfront: "To extract deep geothermal resources, you need a professional drilling rig. Your friend’s drilling rig not only falls short in depth but also has limited bore diameter. It’s fine for developing shallow and intermediate geothermal resources, but to go deeper is not so simple.

The geological structure of Xiang Province is very complex. When you drill, you never know what you’ll encounter. Rock layers are okay; you can drill slowly, but hitting other geological layers could be very troublesome. Without enough experience, it’s difficult to deal with various problems that may arise during drilling."

"Don’t worry about the drilling rig issue; I will resolve it." Of the problems Professor Zhang pointed out, the drilling rig was the least of Chen Xin’s worries, as it only required an upgrade to solve.

But the geological complexity mentioned by Professor Zhang was not something Chen Xin could address, because no matter how much he upgraded, he could only improve the drill head, without affecting the strata itself.

Unless Chen Xin could develop a gigantic laser drilling rig or a planetary mining device to cut through strata directly, any drilling machine would have to contend with different layers.

As for laser drilling rigs and planetary mining devices, these were clearly not issues Chen Xin should consider now. With that free time, he might as well come up with a world engine to directly transform the Earth’s environment.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 374 - 372 Warm Air
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Since Chen Xin had prefabricated all the components in advance, the workers only needed to assemble them, so the construction of the heat supply hub did not take much time.

Including the time spent laying the foundation, it took only two days to complete all the work.

After the installation of the heat supply hub was completed, Chen Xin originally planned to wait until the return water well at the Seventh Middle School was also completed, connect the pipes on both sides, and then start the heat supply hub.

After all, besides considering the drainage issue, there was also the risk of large-scale extraction of underground water causing rock strata cavities and collapses. Under the current circumstances, where natural replenishment of underground water is difficult, it was still necessary to avoid excessive extraction of underground water as much as possible.

However, after consulting Professor Zhang for related knowledge, Chen Xin put these concerns aside.

Large-scale extraction of underground water does indeed cause rock strata cavities, but this requires long-term and continuous large-scale extraction. If the extraction is only for a short time and the volume is not large, the impact is not significant.

Because geological collapses caused by underground water extraction mostly occur in regions where groundwater has been extracted on a large scale for many years. Those regions have been using groundwater recklessly, and coupled with being relatively water-scarce areas with insufficient rainfall, groundwater, being extracted long-term without replenishment, causes the formation of underground cavities, leading to geological collapses.

As long as it is not large-scale and long-term extraction, it will not cause such severe consequences.

Moreover, although Lin City’s geological structure is complex, it is relatively stable and has not experienced geological disasters like earthquakes or collapses for many years.

Under such circumstances, Chen Xin did not hesitate much and chose to start the heat supply hub.

As the heat supply hub began operations, underground hot spring water carrying abundant thermal energy was drawn up. After the heat was dissipated through cooling equipment, it was discharged as cold water.

According to the design, this cold water should be transported via pipes to the return water well and then reinjected underground.

But since the return water well was not yet completed, this cold water could only be naturally discharged.

The situation that Chen Xin was worried about, where water accumulation would flow along the railway, did not occur. Thanks to Lin City’s topography being high in the north and low in the south, the workers discharged this accumulated water south of the train station through pipes.

The area south of the train station was originally the residential area for train station employees, separated by only a wall, and could be reached by crossing the railway.

Further south is wasteland and a pond, considered a relatively low-lying area of Lin City. Although covered by a large amount of snow, raising the terrain slightly, when measuring elevation, it is still a low-lying area.

Therefore, with a water pipe set over the railway in the gully, water can be discharged in this direction with confidence.

Not only cold water after heat dissipation needed to be discharged but also the accumulated water formed by melting snow.

However, the situation of accumulated water was not particularly severe because while the heat emitted by the heat supply hub melted the snow, the environmental temperature also affected the snow melting, slowing down the snow melting speed.

Just standing beside the snow pit, watching the heat supply hub in operation emitting waves of heat melting the surrounding snow bit by bit while wisps of white "steam" rise around, really gives one the feeling of being in the steam era and appreciating the beauty of heavy industry.

The "steam" is not produced by high temperatures causing melted ice and snow to turn into white steam directly but rather by some water vapor evaporating into the air when the snow melts. These tiny water droplets rapidly condense into small ice crystals once leaving the heat supply hub’s heat field.

The tiny ice crystals, being too small and light, do not fall to the ground but are carried up by air currents, naturally becoming white "steam."

Chen Xin wiped away the water vapor accumulated on his helmet, watching the gradually melting snow, and finally showed a satisfied smile on his face.

Although the snow melting speed of the heat supply hub could not compare to the instant snow evaporation of heating devices in the game "Frostpunk," seeing the snow melt into water at a speed visible to the naked eye, flowing and falling to the bottom of the snow pit, turning into accumulated water and pumped away, still gave Chen Xin a profound sense of satisfaction.

These are things he created, bringing warmth back to the earth in this era, even though it’s just in this small area, it’s still something to be proud of.

With this in mind, Chen Xin walked to the bottom of the snow pit, where the water had already reached the foot of his Power Armor, but he didn’t mind.

It’s because the water needed to be stored at such a level for convenience in pumping, otherwise, if it was too shallow, it would be difficult for the pump to draw water.

Through the environmental monitoring of the Power Armor, Chen Xin knew clearly that the temperature around the heat supply hub had reached 27℃. However, because the surrounding snow was over ten meters high, almost flush with the heat supply hub, the current heat field coverage was still limited to the snow pit, not yet reaching the 200 meters radius designed by Chen Xin.

However, as the snow continues to melt, the snow pit is starting to widen at the top and narrow at the bottom, and with this status continuing, it will become a large funnel shape, like a sea bowl.

Watching the heat supply hub operating in front of him, Chen Xin thought for a moment before unlocking the Power Armor, stepping out of it, and taking off his helmet.

The warm and moisture-rich air greeted him, prompting Chen Xin to take a deep breath, inhaling the humid air through his nostrils, filling his chest.

This feeling is truly long-missed!

This sensation of standing outdoors and breathing freely in the warm air!

Chen Xin let out a long breath and then looked up at the heat supply hub standing like a tall tower before him.

Thanks to it, the air around is warm and humid, reminiscent of past summer days.

Compared to the slightly cold and dry air inside the shelter, the air quality here might not be significantly better, but the feeling is entirely different.

Just being able to stand outdoors and breathe the warm and humid air is enough to excite someone.

After all, the entire Earth is now a massive frozen Hell, and being able to stand outside and breathe warm air freely is a rare and long-missed event.

Feeling this, which was once taken for granted but is now so rare, Chen Xin shook his head slightly and grew a more determined belief in spreading it further.

Bringing humanity back to the ground and reducing dependence on underground shelters, Chen Xin believed that with these heat supply hubs and the Energy Tower planned in the future, he would certainly achieve it within his lifetime.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 375 - 373: Camp Relocation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The operation of the heat hub undoubtedly injected new hope and vitality into Lin City.

Although currently only one heat hub is operational, another is still in the process of drilling wells, and the currently operational heat hub has not yet demonstrated particularly impressive performance. But standing beside the snow pit, watching the gradually melting snow, everyone feels a sense of hope slowly igniting.

However, Chen Xin did not allow many people to approach the heat hub, and even standing beside the snow pit was prohibited, because as temperatures rise and snow gradually melts, small-scale collapses are occurring on the pit walls. Although the impact is not significant, there is still a certain level of danger.

Fortunately, handling this situation is quite easy; simply prohibit random access to the snow pit.

The heat hub is currently in smooth operation, requiring no additional maintenance aside from constant drainage.

The entire heat hub is powered by electricity. Although there are plans to use heat energy for power generation in the future, considering that no generator is currently connected to the heat hub and there is no extra heat energy for power generation, Chen Xin chose to use an Isotope Thermoelectric Battery to power the heat hub in his design.

It must be said that the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery is quite useful. Chen Xin only needs to install the battery into the heat hub, and a single battery is sufficient to keep the entire heat hub running smoothly for ten years.

During this time, workers only need to conduct regular maintenance and inspections on the heat hub to complete its entire upkeep.

Chen Xin’s design of the heat hub focused on stability and reliability, resulting in a very dependable heat hub.

Of course, due to material limitations, the entire heat hub still requires regular inspections and maintenance to prevent damage from accidents.

With the snow around the heat hub continually melting, Chen Xin, along with the workers and the geological survey team, has moved from their original camp and set up a new camp.

However, this camp is still a temporary one. Once the range of the heating field is completed, and the snow within a two-hundred-meter radius is melted, Chen Xin will move the workers and geological survey team into the heating field range of the heat hub.

This is partly to enjoy the warmth benefit provided by the heat hub and also to perform maintenance on the heat hub more conveniently.

After all, this is the first time using this equipment, and it’s uncertain whether malfunctions or problems will occur.

Building the new camp was not too troublesome; Chen Xin, with his Power Armor coupled with a mechanical arm, could manage such tasks alone, outperforming all others.

The group found a building not completely buried in snow, still relatively intact, as a support, dug a row of snow pits along the building walls, and set up tents beneath the snow surface to construct a new camp.

The new camp is farther from the train station and heat hub than the old camp, but with the support of the building’s exterior wall, it seems more stable and provides a windbreak effect for the building fully buried by snow.

Since this is just a temporary camp, as long as it’s livable, it hasn’t been meticulously managed.

But Chen Xin put considerable effort into choosing a spot for the Electromagnetic Metallurgy Furnace, each operation releasing a lot of heat, requiring a sturdy placement to prevent potential accidents like the snow-covered ground melting, causing the machine to tilt.

The importance placed on the Electromagnetic Metallurgy Furnace is because other planned heat hubs still require Chen Xin to supply components, including the Seventh Middle School where Cao Daren is drilling wells for the second heat hub.

Thus, Chen Xin must hurry to produce the components needed for the second heat hub to avoid delaying the construction schedule.

This was one reason Chen Xin chose a building as the new camp location, clearing space on the rooftop for the Electromagnetic Metallurgy Furnace.

After all, the furnace Chen Xin built isn’t heavy; as long as it’s placed on a concrete surface, it can stand firm, unlike a snow-covered surface prone to melting, ensuring stable operation.

Thanks to the research department sending materials, Chen Xin no longer needs to collect scrap metal, as the materials transported with the geological survey team fully meet his needs.

Compared to Chen Xin’s previously collected scrap metal, these materials are undoubtedly superior in performance, and can be used in various components according to performance differences, enhancing the heat hub’s performance.

For instance, the radiator of the first heat hub was made from recycled scrap copper. Despite copper’s better cooling performance than iron, and Chen Xin’s Electromagnetic Metallurgy Furnace being able to refine and process materials, these recycled copper radiators are inferior in quality compared to those made with new materials.

After all, one is a recycled product, and the other is a copper alloy made explicitly to improve cooling performance. Although the former meets the heat hub’s design requirements, the latter’s cooling efficiency and material performance are significantly higher.

The research department provided quality materials, and using them to produce components improves the heat hub’s work efficiency and energy output by ten percent.

If system upgrades and design optimizations are applied, Chen Xin feels he can further improve work efficiency and energy output by twenty percent, possibly extending the heating range radius to two hundred fifty or even three hundred meters.

Based on Chen Xin’s previous design, when the heat hub retracts the radiator without opening the heating field, its energy output for power generation reaches 1MWh.

With several heat hubs forming a heating network, they can meet a city’s electricity needs.

Even given the current environment where energy consumption is needed for heating, the remaining power output is sufficient for usage.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 376 - 374 Special Forces
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin’s upgrade ability indeed holds extraordinary significance for a nation, but it is not indispensable.

The country can develop without Chen Xin, and having Chen Xin merely accelerates the development process.

To make an analogy, it’s like playing Stellaris; at the start, you find a research hub that can provide you with a substantial amount of research points each turn, allowing you to shorten the time needed for technology breakthroughs from dozens of turns to just a few or even one turn.

This indeed allows you to gain advantages and lead quickly, but it does not bring significant short-term improvements, because converting technology into power requires time.

Regardless of colonizing other planets or building fleets, everything requires a large amount of resources, energy, and most importantly, time.

And these are not things that can undergo a qualitative leap merely by simply enhancing technology.

Moreover, the contribution Chen Xin can make doesn’t compare to a research hub affecting the entire situation; his abilities are limited by the things he can interact with.

Simply put, if you don’t bring the research to him, he can’t contribute to it.

Nonetheless, Chen Xin’s importance to Flame Country right now is undoubtedly significant, which is evident from the full special forces unit just re-equipped for the train heading to Lin City for his protection.

This is a fully organized special forces unit, roughly equivalent to an augmented company, with a total of over 150 members all fitted with the latest military exoskeleton, equipped with new individual heavy firepower, and various newly issued snow terrain vehicles.

In terms of firepower, mobility, and individual combat capabilities, this is an elite force sufficient to instill fear in the enemy.

In the past, such an elite special forces unit could, if used correctly, exert effects far beyond a regiment, even up to a brigade or a division.

But now they’ve been dispatched to Lin City just after being re-equipped, before having time to adapt to the new equipment, with the main task of protecting Chen Xin.

Given Chen Xin’s importance to the nation, enjoying the protection of a special forces unit is entirely appropriate, as his role fully justifies such treatment.

It’s just that Chen Xin had always resisted this kind of thing; on one hand, he felt uneasy about having people following and protecting him, much like Buddha who didn’t want 500 arhats constantly trailing behind. Chen Xin didn’t want a bunch of people following him whenever he went out, which felt very uncomfortable.

On the other hand, he felt he shouldn’t waste the nation’s resources like this. He considered himself just an ordinary person with no special talents, only having acquired abilities that regular people don’t have through the system, and he didn’t think he was significant enough to need personal protection.

So initially, after the nation reached a cooperation intention with him, they proposed sending people to protect Chen Xin, but he rejected the idea.

At the time, the official responsible for negotiating with Chen Xin, respecting his personal wishes, also considered that Chen Xin had been residing in his shelter, which wasn’t too much of a security threat, so they didn’t proceed with it.

However, this time, after Chen Xin completed the production line for the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery and the design and construction of the Exoskeleton Protective Suit, both of which hold significant meaning for the nation, his presence had reached the highest levels of Flame Country, and his importance had greatly increased.

The nation, considering the protection of valuable talents, decided to send a specially re-equipped special forces unit for Chen Xin’s protection.

Of course, the military played a substantial role in this; after all, usually a combat team of a dozen people is more than enough to protect one person. Before the disaster, even the Federation’s Grand Leader only had a unit on standby for him.

Of course, the military had no ill intent in doing this. What bad intentions could soldiers possibly have? They just wanted more "big boom-booms."

In fact, anyone would have some thoughts after understanding Chen Xin’s capabilities, and the emergence of new products exacerbated such speculation.

Given the exoskeleton, wouldn’t it be great to upgrade to Power Armor?

Wouldn’t it be nice to switch tanks to full electric propulsion?
