Doing this not only allows the three to be upgraded as a whole, saving the trouble of upgrading separately, but also integrates them into one entirety convenient for Chen Xin’s use.

After the series of events in Lin City, Chen Xin also had some new ideas for his home that he wanted to implement, and now that the research base and military camp were to be built at his doorstep, it was just convenient for him to realize these ideas one by one.

According to Chen Xin’s plan, the military camp and research base would be placed on the surface, while underground would be his own shelter, the three should form a whole rather than simply placing the military camp and research base at his doorstep.

After all, Chen Xin is not skilled in true scientific research, all he mastered is using the system to create various kinds of black technology equipment.

So in such a situation, truly pushing Chen Xin to do research is certainly not feasible, the research base is not really meant for him to use for research.

In fact, the upper departments are also clearly aware that the role of this research base in Chen Xin’s hands is inferior to that of a machine workshop, but they still provided it.

Because a research base does not involve just Chen Xin alone.

Previously, Chen Xin collaborated with the country, continually helping the country upgrade research projects, this collaboration cannot be said to be inefficient or bad, but for the country, it is somewhat wasteful.

Because various research projects can still be researched out by other scientists without Chen Xin, just much slower than Chen Xin’s quick results, requiring more resources and time.

Though the country realized Chen Xin’s value earlier and accelerated various technological processes through collaboration with him, which indeed brought huge profits to the country, yet after Chen Xin manufactured a mini nuclear reactor, the higher-ups clearly realized that this was not the way to use Chen Xin.

Using existing research projects to have Chen Xin upgrade them is certainly a method to accelerate technological progress and develop rapidly, but it also limits Chen Xin’s potential.

This time with the mini reactor and the heating network, the higher-ups realized allowing Chen Xin to freely play is better than letting him follow the existing path.

After all, the distance between large Tokamak devices still under research and the mini fusion reactor in Chen Xin’s hands is immeasurable by logic, if following normal technological development, not to say within a hundred years, but at least within decades, achieving what Chen Xin can do would be impossible.

Therefore, this time, what the higher-ups propose is to equip Chen Xin with sufficient manpower, build a research base for him to freely develop, Chen Xin just needs to turn his ideas into reality and leave the rest to these scientists.

This change in collaboration mode is something Chen Xin is happy to see.

Through this journey to Lin City, Chen Xin also realized that although he has the system and can play a part in many things, often his contributions are actually limited, and he might even affect others because of himself.

Just like with this assassination attempt, his single disruption almost caused chaos in Lin City, delaying almost all affairs.

This is obviously not a good thing, even can be said as quite terrible.

A child of fortune does not sit under the eaves, this has been ancient ancestors’ teaching, though this saying may not be entirely correct, it indeed has its reasoning.

Just like with Chen Xin today, even if he gets a slight injury, it would alarm many people, including many big figures Chen Xin couldn’t reach before would come to check up on him, not to mention if something really happened to him, some people would genuinely be implicated.

These are just secondary, with the impact of Chen Xin’s demonstrated capabilities now, if something happens to him, it would be a great loss to the entire Flame Country.

In such circumstances, the higher-ups of Flame Country naturally hope to protect Chen Xin like the most precious porcelain by placing him in a safe spot, avoiding any bumps.

Though when the country sent people to discuss this matter with Chen Xin, he hadn’t experienced the assassination yet, but during his over-a-month stay in Lin City previously, multiple research projects were delayed, not just one or two.

Compared to the losses caused by delaying these projects, providing Chen Xin with a dedicated research base is utterly insignificant, and Chen Xin himself is certainly worth such national investment.

Now with such assassination incidents, naturally, Chen Xin’s safety becomes even more crucial.

Though having a whole special forces unit at the doorstep might seem like being reared, for Chen Xin, this is indeed protection isn’t it?

Moreover to be fair, these special forces are just here to protect Chen Xin’s safety, they won’t restrict his personal freedom, if Chen Xin really wants to go out, they can only follow him, and cannot impose any restrictions.

Therefore, upgrading the research base, military camp, and his shelter together is what Chen Xin plans to do next.

As for whether Chen Xin has enough upgrade points for such a large-scale upgrade?

Since he started his journey to Lin City earlier, it has been a while since Chen Xin last checked his total survival points. After all, ever since the Isotope Thermoelectric Battery production line started operating, with a hundred Isotope Batteries produced daily, averaging three to five batteries bring 5000 survival points straight to Chen Xin, his total survival points have long exceeded six digits.

Unless the first digit changes, otherwise, there is really no need to watch the constantly growing number every day.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 400 - 398 Ecological Dome
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin wasn’t surprised at all that his survival points had surpassed six figures, he even found it somewhat modest.

The benefits of cooperating with the country lie here; by indirectly helping others, Chen Xin can also earn survival points. Through the country’s broad efforts to improve the survival conditions of others, he naturally acquires a vast amount of survival points.

In fact, if it weren’t for the limited production capacity of the Isotope Batteries, Chen Xin would have gained even more survival points.

This is why Chen Xin is becoming increasingly indifferent to the growth of his survival points; there’s no real need to focus too much on it. As long as he makes sure not to spend them all at once, after a few days, a large sum of survival points will be credited again.

With a substantial amount of survival points, Chen Xin has enough confidence and strength to do what he wants, without worrying about the issue of survival points.

If Chen Xin had not chosen to cooperate with the country but had instead accumulated survival points on his own, he might not have gathered as much in a year as he does in a day now.

After all, the base amount of survival points a country can provide is enormous. Even with a cap on individual transactions, the total naturally increases as the numbers go up.

As for a private shelter, no matter how well it is developed, its speed of development cannot compare to that of a country.

Chen Xin had once thought carefully that perhaps this is the true usage of the Survival System he obtained for the apocalypse.

After all, before disaster and apocalypse, personal heroism is meaningless. Only by uniting and bringing everyone’s strength together can more things be accomplished, allowing more people to survive, rather than just a few who can’t even continue civilization and species.

Chen Xin didn’t want to be the standout hero, nor did he think he could lead others. Being an auxiliary of black technology output, as he is now, actually suits him quite well.

Back when playing RPG games, Chen Xin preferred to play an auxiliary or healer role in the team, not the damage dealer or tank, and certainly didn’t want to be the team commander, even though his command ability wasn’t bad.

Now, even though Chen Xin doesn’t want to be a leader, when a large group of people revolves around him, he’s naturally put into that position, which forces him to think about the path ahead.

According to his plan, Chen Xin intends to transform the area where his home is located into an Ecological Dome.

This is not a pipe dream, nor arbitrary because of the system, but something feasible from the standpoint of current technology.

When people hear ’Ecological Dome,’ they often first think of those protective domes enveloping entire cities in science fiction novels, considering it a very advanced technological feat.

However, the concept of the Ecological Dome has been mentioned before, and humans have built similar structures long ago.

The famous Biosphere 2 experiment is essentially an Ecological Dome. It wasn’t constructed as a spherical dome but had a pyramidal structure with step-like levels.

The entire project was built in the 1980s, when technology wasn’t as advanced as it is today, so its structure couldn’t truly become a large spherical dome.

Moreover, due to various technical limitations at the time, the Biosphere 2 experiment ultimately failed, unable to achieve internal circulation without relying on external materials.

But with technological advancements, large-span spatial structures have matured, and the internal space of related buildings has become more expansive, making it feasible to construct a small Ecological Dome.

As for the ecological circulation within the Ecological Dome, if the goal is to create a completely self-contained ecosystem, without upgrades from the system, it would indeed be difficult for Chen Xin.

But if it’s just to create an environment similar to a plant greenhouse, it’s incredibly simple.

After all, the vital air and water can be supplied from the outside, maintaining the operation of the entire Ecological Dome under such conditions is not challenging.

According to Chen Xin’s design, the plan is to build a large dome approximately five hundred meters in span to cover the area before the mountain where his shelter is located.

Then, the temperature in this area will be increased and research and military facilities will be constructed within this area.

Although building a dome with a span of around five hundred meters is highly challenging, given the Flame Country’s infrastructure capabilities, it’s certainly achievable; after all, the internal spans of large airports, stations, and stadiums exceed this size.

Moreover, Chen Xin doesn’t intend to make a hemisphere placed on the ground but only plans to construct a flat-arc dome, making the construction technically less difficult than imagined, even without internal support columns.

Once there’s a relatively enclosed environment, as long as the building materials are adequately insulated and there are enough heating facilities inside, it is easy to create a sufficiently warm greenhouse environment.

As for the research base and military camp, their construction can be completed simultaneously with the dome.

With a suitable temperature and a stable supply of freshwater under the greenhouse environment, planting vegetation and building a small ecological system is not difficult.

This way, a small Ecological Dome essentially has all the necessary elements, but how to make the ecosystem self-sustaining involves too many aspects to be managed by Chen Xin alone.

Fortunately, he has the system; when the time comes, a single upgrade would solve all problems.

Even without using the system for upgrades, having the state send over a couple of biologists to assist in constructing a stable ecological system is feasible.

For Chen Xin, he certainly won’t let the powerful tool of system upgrades go unused, nor will he bury himself in hard work alone.

By then, having the country dispatch biologists to build the ecological system first, followed by using his system for upgrades and optimization himself, using both approaches, to create a comfortable Ecological Dome under ice and darkness, recreating past landscapes, would be such a delightful thing!

This Ecological Dome is not meant for enjoyment, though living in such a warm and comfortable, green ecological environment is inherently enjoyable in the current conditions; the establishment of the Ecological Dome holds significant scientific research importance as well.

Large-span spatial structures can not only be used to construct Ecological Domes, but they can also be applied to large greenhouses for planting. Small ecological cycles can create comfortable green ecological environments and help study cyclic ecological agriculture.

Using the technology to construct Ecological Domes for building greenhouses for planting can be considered utilitarian.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 401 - 399: Shelter Renovation
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Lan, Dragon Lady, do you think there are any areas in the shelter that need modifications?" In his shelter, Chen Xin spread out a blueprint of the current shelter on the table and asked Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan, who were sitting in the room.

Since deciding to upgrade his shelter along with the planned research base and military camp, Chen Xin decided to seek everyone’s opinion on how to adjust his shelter.

As Chen Xin questioned, the blueprint spread on the table projected a 3D model, with pale blue lines forming the structure of the current shelter, thoughtfully accompanied by textual explanations.

Looking at the shelter’s projection before her, Qin Lan curiously asked Chen Xin, "Are you planning to renovate the shelter, Xin?"

"Yes, I previously accepted the title of the highest academician awarded by the state, along with the corresponding benefits, including the construction of a research base for me. I intend to place this research base on top, while simultaneously renovating the shelter." Chen Xin explained to Qin Lan, also giving them a glance: "Previously, only I lived here, and the renovations merely added a few rooms and adjusted the structure. But now, with you, Mom and Dad, and Han Ping and his brother living here, it’s better to renovate it again."

"That’s true, but renovating the shelter involves a large amount of construction, with walls to break and earth and stone to excavate, isn’t it a bit too much trouble? The shelter is quite comfortable now, I feel there’s no need to fuss." Mo Qingyan shook her head after listening to Chen Xin, seemingly not supporting the renovation of the shelter.

This was understandable, since if not using system upgrades, from a normal person’s perspective, wanting to undertake a large-scale renovation of an already built underground shelter indeed involves significant construction, which Mo Qingyan saw as unnecessary.

Chen Xin did not refute, just nodded slightly, but still asked them, "About the construction issues, I’ll handle them. Without considering construction problems, do you think there are any areas in the shelter that could be improved?"

Upon hearing Chen Xin’s question, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan exchanged a look, not understanding why Chen Xin wanted to modify the seemingly comfortable shelter, but they didn’t strongly oppose Chen Xin either, as just drafting a design and posing ideas was harmless.

Therefore, after considering for a moment, Qin Lan was the first to speak, "I think it would be nice to have a fighting training room. This time, Xin, you were assassinated. If you had undergone some fighting training, reacted faster, that person wouldn’t have easily caught you by the throat."

"A fighting training room? That can be added, and it can also be used for exercising," Chen Xin thought for a moment and agreed, feeling it was a good suggestion. After all, he had considered setting up a place for exercise before, but got too busy and forgot.

As he agreed, Chen Xin touched the blueprint, adjusted it a few times, and extended a room next to the medical room on the first floor, making the area quite large.

Seeing Chen Xin enlarging the space on the blueprint, Mo Qingyan, assuming he was just planning, half-jokingly said, "In that case, I think we could specifically design a poultry farm. Although the growing greenhouse area is still sufficient, the chicks are growing bigger. To raise chickens or other poultry, a larger area is needed."

Mo Qingyan’s suggestion was serious. The hens and chicks that Chen Xin raised from the eggs were kept in the growing greenhouse, but only a small area was fenced for these chickens to roam, which wasn’t very big.

As the chicks were gradually growing, the space was clearly becoming cramped.

If Chen Xin didn’t want to cage them, like poultry farms where chickens are confined from chick to slaughter, then creating a larger area as a feeding ground was necessary.

Therefore, after Mo Qingyan’s suggestion, Chen Xin also extended a space next to the original growing greenhouse to serve as a poultry farm.

Chen Xin also adjusted the structure and position of the growing greenhouse and the shelter’s overall floor height, exposing the top of the growing greenhouse above ground.

"Eh? Why expose the greenhouse roof above ground?" Qin Lan was puzzled by Chen Xin’s modification.

The entire shelter was located underground to avoid various natural disasters caused by previous meteor impacts, the growing greenhouse naturally wrapped in soil. But now Chen Xin adjusted to expose the greenhouse top above ground, making people confused.

"In Lin City, I observed that during the time of day originally strongest in sunlight, the sky appeared faintly bright," Chen Xin said something seemingly unrelated to the greenhouse roof exposure but soon explained, "The dust in the sky is expected to gradually fall within a year and restore the original sunlight level in about three years."

Although a large amount of dust remains in the stratosphere affecting sunlight, overall, the sky will brighten, and the light intensity will reach the level of strong haze weather in three years.

Under such light intensity, plants grow slowly, but with supplemental lighting, crops can still be normally planted. Therefore, I exposed the greenhouse top above ground for this purpose, to use transparent glass and light panels on the greenhouse top to gather sunlight and provide light to the crops."

"I see, you plan to use light panels to refract sunlight into the greenhouse?" Mo Qingyan suddenly understood Chen Xin’s vision.

This method of refracting sunlight for lighting has been used since ancient times. Many skyscrapers in modern architecture have adopted similar designs, using light panels on the building exterior to refract sunlight into the interior for lighting, thus reducing artificial lighting usage.

"This is indeed a good design, but wouldn’t the shelter undergo major renovations?" Qin Lan, observing Chen Xin’s blueprint modifications, asked somewhat uncertainly.

Qin Lan’s question was valid because if Chen Xin really did this, the modifications to the shelter would be substantial.

Chen Xin simply nodded and added some more elements to the blueprint before answering, "The state will cover this renovation, so I might as well add back some designs I abandoned before due to various reasons."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 402 - 400 - s: Circular Structure
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When Chen Xin and the survival group’s friends initially designed the bunker, the ideal plan they proposed was to convert missile silos or abandoned military air raid shelters into bunkers, turning them into small communities.

However, this plan was abandoned due to policy restrictions and economic issues in Flame Country, leading everyone to build private bunkers instead.

In the initial conception, there were indeed many ideas regarding the design of the bunkers.

Some of these ideas were very practical, such as greenhouses for planting, water circulation systems, air filtration, and communication facilities, but there were also some ideas that were somewhat unrealistic yet still functional.

For instance, building a library and bringing a large number of paper books into the bunker was actually a great idea. After all, compared to electronic products, paper books could be preserved for much longer. If you take care of the books while reading, one book could last for decades.

This was certainly a very practical idea, but unfortunately, before the disaster, with the internet being so prevalent, people rarely read physical books anymore. Even bookstores, once found everywhere on the streets, had disappeared for many years. It had become challenging to purchase physical books.

Plus, since everyone brought electronic products into the bunker, a library was no longer necessary. Therefore, when Chen Xin initially built the bunker, the library was omitted.

When they referred to some foreign bunker designs back then, they noticed that the bunkers even had cinemas, supermarkets, and swimming pools, which sparked some ideas within everyone.

However, due to the actual situation, as it wasn’t the initially envisioned community model, the supermarket became unnecessary. For cinemas, a home theater could be set up in the living room, which was sufficient.

As for swimming pools, they required large areas and consumed precious water resources. In cold weather, they would also need heating equipment, which was too extravagant and troublesome. It was more practical to have a large bathtub, so no one really built a swimming pool.

Apart from this, there were functional facilities like the bar at Mo Qingyan’s home, billiard rooms at the homes of some friends who liked playing billiards, and VR experience rooms for friends who liked playing VR. These facilities belonged only to one or two individuals, and others could only admire but not attain them.

Although later on, Chen Xin managed to acquire somes functional facilities like a fish pond for breeding fish and a medical room through material exchange, there were still some things he didn’t have.

However, now, Chen Xin felt that he could afford to build some facilities to improve his quality of life, making his life not only better but also more comfortable.

For instance, the frozen collection in Mo Qingyan’s wine cellar; Chen Xin had discussed with Mo Qingyan about when to retrieve those wines.

If he could get hold of that batch of wine, Chen Xin thought he could build a wine cellar and bar in the bunker.

Moreover, although he had built a hot spring bath, when he was using it alone it didn’t seem like much. Now with more people in the bunker, Chen Xin felt it was necessary to expand it so there would be separate areas for men and women, avoiding the need to schedule usage times based on gender.

Additionally, since he wanted to conduct research and manufacture things, the original workspace seemed a bit small, so it also needed some adjustments.

With many ideas emerging in Chen Xin’s mind, the plans for the bunker had already changed drastically.

Several new spaces appeared, significantly expanding the overall footprint. The main part of the bunker, originally deeply buried with a difference of several dozen meters from the entrance, was adjusted on the plans so that the difference was now only one floor’s height.

This height difference posed no issues in terms of architectural structure and protective performance. The bunker was initially buried so deeply to withstand meteor impacts and subsequent disasters.

Now that the disasters had passed, there was no need to live so deeply underground.

As for water, electricity, heating, and air filtration facilities, Chen Xin separated them from the main structure of the building, specially setting them aside in a different space. This meant that these facilities no longer needed to be placed in the main area of the bunker, and any problems wouldn’t affect the residents inside the bunker.

After a series of adjustments, the overall planning of the bunker could be said to have clearly defined functional areas and even added several production and life facilities, making the functions more complete.

However, looking at the slightly chaotic layout of the bunker, Chen Xin felt that it should be reorganized.

Even though there was no need to worry about construction difficulty and space limitations, the entire planning of the bunker should still be more reasonable.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin recalled a video he had seen about Federation wealthy elites converting abandoned missile silos into bunkers. The missile silo-converted bunker was quite successful, satisfying both in terms of comfort and functionality, and its design was very reasonable.

So, after some consideration, Chen Xin decided to readjust the layout of his bunker.

Firstly, there was no need for the newly added functional areas to be laid out separately; they could be stacked. The entire bunker could be designed into a multi-story structure, accessed via elevators and stairs. This would make the architectural layout more compact and convenient.

After all, moving up and down stairs in a bunker and walking through corridors are completely different experiences.

Moreover, Chen Xin intended to adjust the current square structure of the bunker into a circular shape, emulating the missile silo-converted bunkers.

This was not about copying or imitating but rather because a circular shape generally had a higher space utilization rate and facilitated easier layout.

Another aspect was that a circular structure had inherently stronger structural integrity and was more material-efficient, although Chen Xin didn’t particularly need to prioritize this.

Hence, after a series of adjustments, the entire bunker transformed into a multi-layered cylindrical structure, still connected to the bunker’s entrance via corridors. However, it was no longer necessary to go down several dozen meters via elevators to access the inside of the bunker.

It was possible to directly reach the first floor of the main part of the bunker and then descend to lower levels using the central elevator or stairs in the circular structure.

As for the main part of the bunker, functionalities like the living room, kitchen, and hot spring bath, which were originally connected with the bedrooms, were separated and placed on different floors, concentrating the bedrooms on one floor to increase living space.

After this adjustment, everyone could have a single room and a more comfortable bed, no longer having to sleep on bunk beds.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 403 - 401: 7-Layer Shelter
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"After the renovation, the shelter will be divided into seven floors. From top to bottom, the first floor is the living room, the second floor is the living quarters, dining room, and kitchen, the third floor is the workspace and medical room, the fourth floor is the bedroom, the fifth floor is the bar and entertainment room, the sixth floor is the gym and martial arts training ground, and the seventh floor is the pool and hot spring bath." Chen Xin presented the revised design of the shelter to the people inside, explaining each floor.

Looking at the design, Chen Xin’s father was the first to ask, pointing to the two annex buildings on either side of the main body of the shelter: "These should be the greenhouse and power distribution room, right? You’ve combined the water treatment and power distribution rooms together? Are there any safety concerns?"

"This part serves three functions: power distribution, air filtration, and water treatment, spanning three levels." Chen Xin, seeing his father’s inquiry, explained: "Previously, due to space limitations, the power distribution room and water tank were placed in the main part of the shelter, which indeed posed some safety risks and mechanical noise issues.

So, in this renovation, I’ve relocated these facilities to a separate area on the side.

The top level houses the air filter, connected to the outside via ventilation ducts; the air is filtered before being delivered inside the shelter. Additionally, oxygen transported from the greenhouse will mix with the filtered air to meet the shelter’s internal needs.

The middle level is the power distribution room, with the main power supply still being the three previously installed Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries, along with a fuel generator for emergency use only.

The bottom level contains the water reservoir and treatment equipment, drawing groundwater via pumps. Due to the previous presence of hot springs, the water is split into two parts: one portion, after being treated, is sent directly to the hot spring bath, while the other is stored in the reservoir for domestic use.

The overall setup is like this; I’ve readjusted all the equipment, so there shouldn’t be any safety issues.

Plus, as these facilities are placed separately, even if a safety issue arises, it won’t affect the main part of the shelter. The connecting passage can be severed, and maintenance is accessible through the top repair route."

"Safety must be prioritized, especially concerning water and electricity use. I’ve witnessed many safety incidents at the factory due to slight oversights, so caution is imperative." Chen Xin’s father seemed somewhat nagging, but perhaps because he’d dealt with these things his entire life, he was particularly sensitive and cautious about safety issues.

Chen Xin nodded firmly and confirmed, "Safety is definitely a priority. Once the renovations are complete, I hope you can check everything daily, Dad, since no one here understands these things better than you."

"Okay, later you’ll have to tell me the areas needing attention." Chen Xin’s father nodded in agreement.

Seeing his father agree to take on safety inspections, Chen Xin felt a lot more at ease. An experienced technician like his father, having worked in a state enterprise, would cost at least four hundred thousand a year to hire in pre-disaster times. With his help in handling safety issues, Chen Xin couldn’t be more relieved.

"Does anyone else have other questions or think there are further modifications needed?" Chen Xin asked everyone in front of him again.

"Ah Xin, what about the heating equipment? You initially installed a ground-source heat pump for heating. Where are those pumps now?" While Chen Xin’s father was concerned about the safety of water and electricity, Mo Qingyan focused on other aspects.

Chen Xin wouldn’t overlook the heating issue. Smiling, he enlarged the design diagram in front, pointing to a section in the basement where the pool and hot spring bath are located, and explained to everyone: "The original heating equipment has been moved here. Hot water is extracted, dissipated through the ground-source heat pump, converted to thermal energy to supply the circulation system and provide heating for the entire shelter. Afterwards, the cooled water is directed to maintain the pool’s temperature, before being returned underground."

"The pool is actually temperature-controlled?! That indeed sounds like an extravagant luxury!" Qin Lan couldn’t help but find it a bit lavish, hearing that the pool would maintain its temperature.

Chen Xin openly nodded and affirmed, "Outside is freezing cold, but inside is as warm as spring, with a hot spring to soak in and a temperature-controlled pool. It truly is a luxury. However, since we have the means and have moved past the hard times, I think there’s nothing wrong with enhancing our living quality a little."

"Hmm, there’s indeed nothing wrong with that." Although Qin Lan didn’t quite agree with such luxury, she nodded, not opposing Chen Xin.

Chen Xin noticed Qin Lan’s stance but said nothing. As long as she didn’t strongly oppose, he knew she was still supportive, albeit a bit displeased. There was no need to discuss such matters publicly; he could comfort and persuade her when they were alone at night.

"If a comfortable life is what we seek, why not make the kitchen more functional? That way, everyone could enjoy better meals." Chen Xin’s mother suggested or maybe requested, especially since, after her arrival, she was primarily using the shelter’s kitchen, rather than Chen Xin, the best cook amongst them.

Chen Xin nodded in agreement to his mother’s request; it was a simple demand and didn’t require much discussion.

Usually quiet, Han Ping and Han Ze, the brothers, finally raised their hands after everyone else had spoken: "Mr. Chen, could you improve the accommodation conditions in the observation room above? It’s quite cold at night."

"Yes, that’s one of the key focuses of this upgrade. After the upgrade of the observation room, I’ll make new adjustments to improve your living conditions, and eventually, you won’t have to keep night watch." Chen Xin half-playfully explained while revising the blueprint, clarifying with Han Ping and Han Ze: "A communications base station will also be established outside, allowing national network access, so your future job will involve maintaining communications, with special forces taking over security responsibilities."

"Will that special forces unit be stationed here permanently?" Han Ping was aware of the special forces that escorted Chen Xin back, though he didn’t know they would be permanently stationed, overseeing Chen Xin’s security henceforth.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 404 - 402 Overall Planning
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

While Chen Xin was planning how to upgrade his shelter, the engineers and technicians sent by the state had already arrived in the city and came to Chen Xin’s shelter to discuss with him how to construct the research base and barracks.

Theoretically, these issues didn’t require consulting Chen Xin; they just needed to ask for his opinion to ensure any necessary requests, and then they could proceed with construction step by step.

However, the situation on Chen Xin’s side was a bit different, so the engineers and technicians in charge of this matter, along with the researchers assigned to the research base, needed to connect and negotiate with Chen Xin to determine his exact needs and the main areas he intended to explore.

Only after confirming these could the specialized research base being built for him be properly constructed.

Besides, another crucial point was that Chen Xin proposed a new design scheme, which required further coordination with the engineers and technicians.

"So, Academician Chen, you want to construct this research institution into an enclosed structure and then build a miniature ecosystem inside?" The researcher, who came to liaise with Chen Xin, quickly grasped his idea and said approvingly, "This is a very meaningful research topic! Constructing a self-sustaining miniature ecosystem could be of great significance for rebuilding the Earth’s ecological cycle after the natural environment recovers in the future!"

"I haven’t considered it so far ahead; the research on a miniature ecosystem is mainly for studying ecological agriculture, considering how to increase the country’s grain production, and incidentally make my life a bit more comfortable, that’s about it. I haven’t thought much beyond that." Chen Xin hadn’t really considered the topic brought up by the researcher; the restoration of the Earth’s ecosystem seemed too far from his concern.

Even if such issues were to be considered, Chen Xin had better options available, not relying solely on a miniature ecosystem for studying how to restore the entire ecological cycle.

World engines, planetary terraforming, megastructural domes, ringworlds... innumerable technologies could be employed. Although a miniature ecosystem could be considered fundamental to these planet-affecting technologies, it certainly didn’t deserve undue focus.

"Restoring the global ecosystem indeed seems distant, but studying a self-sustaining ecosystem is still meaningful, not to mention the restoration of pre-disaster biological samples, which is very worthwhile," the researcher remained passionate, specifically telling Chen Xin, "Before the disaster, the state preserved large quantities of plant seeds and animal embryos, all of which are frozen in the Seed Banks in Kunlun Mountain and the Himalayas.

If a self-sustaining ecosystem can be built, we could use these preserved plant seeds and animal embryos to restore pre-disaster flora and fauna! This way, we can restore precious biological resources to leave our descendants with something other than a barren world."

Chen Xin appeared unmoved by the researcher’s excitement, and the engineer shared a similar expression.

It’s not that either of them didn’t care about the significance of this matter, but the topics brought up by the researcher seemed too far-fetched.

After all, people across the country are still grappling with basic survival issues, figuring out how to provide enough food for everyone. Talking about restoring flora and fauna resources and Earth’s ecosystem seems excessively remote.

No matter how meaningful this research might be, it’s meaningless unless there’s enough food and more people can survive before any major restoration happen.

However, Chen Xin didn’t want to dampen the researcher’s enthusiasm, as the subsequent research was still dependent on them. Discouraging their enthusiasm could adversely affect future research.

So, after the researcher calmed down, Chen Xin continued to speak to the engineer: "Once the dome construction is complete, its interior will primarily be used for research on miniature ecosystems and ecological agriculture. Additionally, the scientific department also needs to conduct reverse engineering on the things I’ve made and assist me in completing upgrades of projects transferred from other research departments.

Thus, the scientific facilities need to be all-encompassing since I don’t know exactly what will be researched or what resources will be needed."

"If that’s the case, it might be troublesome! Without a clear direction, constructing this research base could be very difficult," the engineer frowned at Chen Xin’s request.

Even though they knew this task was unlike previous ones, meeting such undefined and all-encompassing demands was indeed challenging for the engineers.

Different scientific directions have completely different requirements for research facilities. Some require dust-free environments, some require sterile environments, and others demand perfectly clean or even vacuum settings... Even with existing blueprints and integrated modular cabins, constructing them is extremely challenging.

Not to mention some large research equipment requires specially designed facilities to accommodate them.

At this point, however, the researcher seemed to grasp Chen Xin’s intent, thinking for a moment before saying: "This means we need at least a biological laboratory for miniature ecosystem research; a physics laboratory and a mechanical laboratory are needed as well. Other facilities can be deferred, leaving space to be added as necessary later."

"Hmm, I have no objections to this plan, but for miniature ecosystem research, besides a biological laboratory, I also need a greenhouse for agricultural research," Chen Xin added.

The engineer visibly relaxed upon hearing this, as it meant they finally had some concrete direction to guide their efforts.

"By the way, since this research facility will use a completely enclosed dome structure, how will the internal heating be solved? Should we use electric heating or boiler heating?" the engineer asked Chen Xin, as these were decisions that needed to be confirmed before construction could begin.

"For heating, I plan to use geothermal energy. The associated technology is the Deep Geothermal Energy Tower technique, which I proposed in Lin City." Clearly, Chen Xin didn’t intend to use either electric or boiler heating but instead the new technology he proposed in Lin City: "Building an Energy Tower nearby can provide heating for the entire research facility and the excess heat can also be used for power generation."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 405 - 403 Design Problem
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After confirming the relevant requirements with Chen Xin, the preparation work for the research base quickly began in full swing.

Compared to the research base, which required designing blueprints first and then considering how to construct on site, the special forces’ camp was quickly established outside Chen Xin’s shelter.

It was still the previous mobile barracks, but after being upgraded and modified by Chen Xin, they became easier to set up and move, with better insulation performance, so construction was completed quickly, allowing these special forces to settle in.

However, this is only a temporary camp. After the research base is completed, these special forces will move to the permanent camp built alongside the research base.

Regarding the issue of the permanent camp, Chen Xin also discussed it with the political commissar of the special forces because Chen Xin was unsure whether they would be stationed permanently, so some issues needed to be discussed.

Fortunately, this unit had already received clear instructions before arriving here, and they will be responsible for protecting Chen Xin until they receive new orders.

They also received new instructions from their superiors that once the research base is completed, they will be stationed here to be responsible for the security work of the research base.

This reassured Chen Xin and also allowed him to plan for the construction of the barracks.

After all, if this special forces unit is not stationed here, and another unit or the armed police is transferred instead, some facilities in the barracks need to be changed.

If it’s the special forces, Chen Xin can confidently hand over some of his improved weapons and equipment to them, but if it’s the regular army and armed police, some equipment obviously cannot be handed over so directly.

Although the special forces, regular army, and armed police forces are all part of Flame Country’s military, there are still many differences between these forces in terms of equipment and confidentiality levels.

Now that it is confirmed this unit will continue to protect him, Chen Xin can naturally confidently provide some obviously high-tech weapons to this special forces unit.

For example, the heavy mecha that had already been provided to them, Chen Xin again supplied an amount sufficient to arm two combat teams, allowing this special forces unit to rise to a higher level in terms of heavy equipment.

Especially since Chen Xin kindly replaced the original 12.7 mm rotary cannon on the right shoulder of the mecha with a 50 mm handheld short-barreled recoilless rifle and added a short chain saw to the right arm of the mecha, making this heavy mecha increasingly reminiscent of the terminator armor from Warhammer 40K.

The unit was, of course, ecstatic about the equipment provided by Chen Xin. After all, what unit doesn’t like big, tough cannons like this?

Furthermore, since they were already tasked with obtaining new equipment from Chen Xin, Chen Xin’s proactive provision allowed them to effortlessly complete their superiors’ tasks, so why not do it?

As for their military superiors, upon seeing the heavy mecha provided by Chen Xin, they were overjoyed and only wanted more, with no complaints whatsoever.

Although many say that mechas are inferior to tanks in terms of firepower and defense, mechas are more flexible and mobile, making them better suited for flexible deployment compared to tanks.

In environments where tank deployment is inconvenient, mechas can just fill the gap in defense and firepower support during infantry combat, providing strong support to the troops and filling this gap in the army.

After these heavy mechas were equipped to the special forces, the military even planned to send combat experts to study how to use these heavy mechas and formulate new combat tactics for them.

This also led to an additional military combat research room in addition to the various scientific laboratories at Chen Xin’s place.

Chen Xin was pleased with this outcome, given he plans to develop more exciting things in the future, and having the military support would make many issues much simpler.

————————————————

Regarding the blueprints, the special apocalyptic environment meant that many original private design companies could naturally no longer exist, leaving only state agencies’ design institutes to assume design work under these circumstances.

Chen Xin’s research base was to be handled by a design institute in Star City, the capital of Xiang Province.

The reason for choosing the Star City design institute was largely due to Chen Xin being located in Xiang Province. He had previously dealt extensively with the three towns of Hubei Province, which had caused dissatisfaction among Xiang Province’s leaders. Therefore, the design and construction of the research base was seized by Xiang Province’s leaders to build a good relationship with the highest academician produced by their province.

In terms of design, the design institute planned to use steel crossbeams as support and then use lightweight materials and tempered glass to construct the dome.

This design posed no particular difficulty. Although a 500-meter diameter might sound exaggerated, similar applications exist in airports, high-speed rail stations, and stadiums, which are nothing challenging for Flame Country, a giant in infrastructure construction.

The design institute did not falter in this regard and soon produced the first draft of the design blueprint.

The ecological dome was designed in a shape similar to a Mongolian yurt, with a slightly curved circular dome supported by twelve root-like support pillars. The circular dome itself was constructed with lightweight materials styled similarly to dougong structure, mounted on the support pillars and then paved with a hexagonal honeycomb structure of tempered glass as the dome.

Overall, the design institute did not introduce any avant-garde designs; apart from the hexagonal honeycomb structure of the glass, the rest could even be described as plain.

This is understandable, as under the current broader environment, building something with design flair is impractical, and everything should prioritize functionality over artistic sense.

Although there are designs that balance art and function, at present, practicality should take precedence.

Artistic elements should only be considered when there is additional capacity.

After finalizing the design plan, the next steps naturally involved drafting detailed construction blueprints and preparing the relevant materials and parts.

However, a tricky problem arose here, almost bringing the project to a halt.

The twelve support pillars needed for dome construction were large metal components, requiring monolithic production for the necessary support strength, but Flame Country’s recovering industrial production capacity, post-disaster, couldn’t complete such large metal component processing.

According to the design institute’s plan, each support pillar was over thirty meters high, with cross branches over fifty meters long. The requirement for monolithic processing and guaranteed support strength indicated extremely high demands on processing technology.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 406 - 404: 3D Printing
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A steel component over thirty meters high and fifty meters long cannot be made; this is quite inconceivable for Flame Country.

Before the disaster, even larger and more demanding metal components were produced, and the people of Flame Country were never limited by precedent in their imagination.

However, now that the national industrial production capacity has been greatly affected, manufacturing such a steel component truly becomes difficult, and without a sufficient industrial foundation, it is indeed unattainable.

There are of course solutions; Flame Country, as a major infrastructure nation, naturally has alternative plans.

The simplest method is to alter the design, replacing the current support column plan with more processable materials or simply cast concrete columns and support beams, which can equally complete the construction of the dome, but correspondingly requires more support columns within the dome.

This approach is more practical, but additional support columns within the dome will impact the interior space. Although reasonably planned, the impact is minor, yet ultimately not as good as having a transparent integral structure.

Method two is employing welding technology, fabricating the components of the support columns in segments and then assembling them through welding.

This method certainly meets the requirements, as Flame Country’s welding techniques are sufficient for such demands. However, on the whole, segmented welding still affects the support strength, less sturdy compared to a monolithic construction.

Both solutions can serve as substitutes, but solution one affects space utilization, and solution two affects structural strength. The design team couldn’t decide for a moment and had to send both solutions to Chen Xin for the client’s choice.

It’s not that the design team is shirking responsibility; they are hoping Chen Xin might suggest other solutions.

Does Chen Xin have a solution here?

The answer is affirmative.

"Regarding this issue, there are two solutions: one is to create a machine to manufacture such size steel components, and the other is using 3D printing technology to print the required components." Chen Xin didn’t consider the problem too difficult to solve and thus proposed two solutions.

"What are the characteristics of these two solutions? Can 3D printing provide adequate structural strength?" The engineering technician who submitted the plan to Chen Xin inquired. Although 3D printing technology has rapidly developed in recent years, no company dares to claim their technology can print such large metal structures.

Furthermore, even if such large structures can be printed, how can 3D printing assure the components meet strength requirements?

As for Chen Xin’s creation of a machine capable of processing such size components, the engineering technician doesn’t doubt Chen Xin at all.

"Creating a machine to manufacture components may not be remarkable beyond updating processing precision and technology. It’s mainly similar to a standard fabrication of such components, except my machine might consume less energy and produce better quality components." Chen Xin briefly explained and did not elaborate on his first proposed solution.

However, regarding 3D printing, he spoke enthusiastically: "As for 3D printing, this is essentially no different from the existing technology on the market, using a nozzle to spray material, then laser sintering to melt and solidify the material, forming a stable structure."

Chen Xin paused here, waiting for the engineering technicians to digest his words, then continued: "However, the laser sintering technology I use is superior to the current market, with sintered materials exhibiting stronger and more durable structural strength.

Additionally, my technology doesn’t require a sealed environment to strictly control air flow and humidity, suitable for the print manufacturing of large structures or buildings, and in terms of raw materials, ordinary metal powders can be proportionally fed, enabling the printing of alloy."

"Printing alloys?" The engineering technician showed a surprised expression.

Printing large metal components alone isn’t enough to astonish the engineering technician, but the ability to mix single metal powders and print alloys directly is incredibly remarkable.

Alloy manufacturing not only involves mixing metals but requires various treatments to achieve different performances.

For example, your stainless steel utensils and shipbuilding steel for creating battleships are both nickel-chromium alloys, but their performance differs greatly.

However, this isn’t challenging for Chen Xin. If willing, he could even create a molecular metallurgical furnace, directly decomposing materials at the molecular level and rearranging molecules as needed to obtain desired materials.

Of course, such metallurgical technology on a molecular level is too extravagant, and Chen Xin isn’t planning to implement it for now.

For the time being, this 3D printer capable of printing alloys is already sufficient.

"Regarding the specific principles and technology, it’s hard for me to explain clearly, but you only need to know I can indeed accomplish this." Chen Xin maintained his attitude of "I just make things, you research the principles yourself," yet he clarified: "I can first make a small one for you to test and observe the effects before further discussions on subsequent construction plans."

"That’s the best!" The engineering technician was already too excited to say much more, eagerly anticipating Chen Xin’s promised 3D printer capable of printing alloys.

If Chen Xin could truly achieve this, even printing some basic alloys would have extraordinary significance in practical applications.

Chen Xin naturally noticed the engineering technician’s excitement. He was accustomed to such reactions from individuals because of his statements, and since he didn’t think much of it, he merely nodded and said, "Then I’ll first make a small prototype for you to experiment with; once the results are confirmed, we can discuss the follow-up construction plan."

"Okay, Academician Chen!" The engineering technician agreed, finally suppressing his excitement.

As for the change in construction plans, it’s naturally necessary. Traditional construction methods require scaffolding with workers gradually installing and welding.

But if utilizing 3D printing, a skyscraper can be erected from the ground up, requiring fewer construction workers and allowing the 3D printer to print the main structure on its own, with workers handling subsequent tasks.

The difference is stark in terms of scale and difficulty between the two approaches.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 407 - 405: Printing Robot
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As we all know, metals exist in nature in compound forms, known as compounds.

Only those extremely inactive metals exist as elements, such as gold, silver, platinum, palladium... These precious metals are stable in nature and rarely react with other substances, therefore they are also known as inert metals.

Hence, most of the metals that humans can obtain in nature exist as mineral compounds, which require smelting to obtain new metal elements.

However, pure metal elements have singular physical properties and often do not meet usage requirements.

For instance, it is widely known that pure iron is actually very soft, and only when it forms an iron-carbon alloy with carbon does it have a hard texture.

Moreover, depending on the temperature, the lattice arrangement within iron crystals varies. Based on this physical property, iron-carbon alloys can be processed at different temperatures to obtain iron-carbon alloys with differing properties, commonly known as steel.

Humanity has a long history of using alloys, dating back over six thousand years to ancient Babylonians, who are known to be the earliest civilization to smelt bronze, which is a copper-tin alloy.

The Flame Country also began using alloys early on; over three thousand years ago during the Shang Dynasty, the ancestors in the Flame Country were already smelting and using bronze on a large scale, and could even produce sharp swords through forging and other processing methods.

The famous King Goujian’s Sword remains incredibly sharp even after thousands of years, capable of slicing through dozens of layers of paper with just a gentle swipe.

Therefore, in human life, alloys are ubiquitous.

However, using 3D printing to print alloys remains exceptionally challenging.

Theoretically, this is feasible, because the definition of an alloy is a solid product with metallic properties formed by mixing, melting, cooling, and solidifying one metal with another or several metals or non-metals.

So theoretically, to 3D print alloys, one just needs to mix the elemental metal raw materials according to the required proportions, then process with lasers to get the required alloy.

However, achieving this is not easy.

Firstly, the mixing of metal powders must ensure the material for each sintering is uniformly proportioned overall, or errors in metal proportions will cause issues in the material’s performance, disrupting the overall structure of the printed product.

Before the disaster, Blue Planet already had laser 3D printing technology; some even printed a set of Iron Man armor from titanium metal, capable of resisting rifle shots.

But this is done within enclosed equipment where metal powders are spread in a layer roughly 0.1 mm or even a few micrometers thick, and lasers are used to sinter the areas to be printed.

Only after repeating such sintering layer by layer can parts be printed.

If airflow disturbance occurs during this process, it will directly blow away the unsinted metal powders.

Slow efficiency and interference from airflow disturbance are minor issues compared to the stringent requirements; such printing is hard to print large parts and cannot print alloys.

If merely processing already alloyed powder particles, then naturally it is simple, but to process elemental metals into alloys is not that easy.

A crucial factor here is temperature.

Sintering is processed at temperatures slightly below the material’s melting point to bond the powder particles, but turning different elemental materials into alloys clearly requires actual melting of materials beyond just sintering.

Moreover, crucially, for some special alloys, processing temperatures need to be much higher than the material’s melting points and must be maintained for some time to allow significant changes in material properties.

These are precisely the aspects that current 3D printing technology struggles to achieve.

However, these are not issues for Chen Xin, who can use lasers to melt elemental metal powder particles, mix various metal elements together to form alloys, then cool and sinter the alloy to become the required parts, which his 3D printer can accomplish.

Chen Xin’s 3D printer is unlike traditional equipment that prints using a fixed framework and print head, instead resembling a small robot.

A small robot carries a portion of raw materials, then melts them into alloys following pre-designed programs, then prints and forms them.

With enough robots, a large part can be printed quickly.

This technology draws inspiration from some sci-fi games where so-called nano robots are often used to manufacture items through 3D printing, which Chen Xin has uniquely adapted.

He just didn’t make the robots so tiny but rather akin to small drone-like robots.

After agreeing to create a small prototype for the engineering technicians, Chen Xin quickly completed the prototype using the workbench and mechanical arms in the shelter and even upgraded it.

"This... this is just astonishing!" The engineering technicians, watching the small 3D printing robot like a drone print a miniature model of the Eiffel Tower right before them, could not help but exclaim, yet appeared somewhat speechless.

"This is the small prototype of the 3D printing equipment I mentioned. It can be made larger or smaller according to different practical usage needs, although this size is quite versatile for various contexts." While demonstrating the prototype of this 3D printing equipment, Chen Xin explained, "It can carry about two kilograms of raw materials each time, which can be pre-processed alloy powders or metal element powder mixtures. Moreover, multiple devices can be networked to print large metal components required."

"With this, architectural construction can become much simpler!" The imagination of engineering technicians began envisioning the sight of hundreds of such 3D printing robots simultaneously starting work, making buildings sprout like bamboo shoots from the ground.

"It’s not just architectural construction; it can also be used in mechanical manufacturing and metal processing. The advantage of using 3D printing lies in sufficient printing precision; printed metal parts may not require further processing, and regardless of how peculiar your needed shape is, 3D printing can realize it." Looking at the excited expression of the engineering technician, Chen Xin smiled and said, "For fields requiring high precision components, this is extremely useful."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 408 - 406: Preparing to Start Work
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"After discussing and synthesizing multiple opinions, here’s the final design plan." In the living room of Chen Xin’s shelter, the designer responsible for submitting the design proposals was introducing the whole plan to him: "The main part of the Ecological Dome has a maximum diameter of 512 meters, the radian of the dome itself is 1.1 rad, making it a perfect circle structure. The diameter of the ground part is 496 meters, the dome’s highest height is 41.2 meters, and the building is divided into three above-ground levels and one underground level."

"The interior adopts a circular layout, with the center consisting of a small ecological circle and a heating hub providing heat for the entire building, forming an atrium. The main body of the building is distributed around this central atrium."

"The above-ground part is mainly used for various laboratories, while the underground part serves as a weapons testing ground and storage warehouse, with space reserved for experimental institutions that may need to be located underground for contingency purposes."

After listening to the structural introduction of the Ecological Dome by the designer, Chen Xin was quite satisfied with this design. However, he pondered and asked, "Where are the military camp and the planting greenhouse I previously mentioned located, and are you planning to use the heating hub to provide warmth to the Ecological Dome?"

"Yes, the heating hub and Energy Tower technology are very efficient ways to utilize geothermal energy and can be directly converted into electricity. Compared to building another power plant for power supply and a boiler for heating, constructing a geothermal power plant can not only obtain efficient and clean energy but also provide heating for the Ecological Dome, making the investment much smaller than other options." The designer clearly considered many plans before finally choosing the current design scheme.

Chen Xin didn’t say much about this. Although the technology originated from him, he didn’t feel proud or have other emotions; he simply nodded affirmatively and said, "Using the heating hub to warm the Ecological Dome is indeed sufficient, but it seems like you’re thinking of calculating the electricity separately?"

"Yes, according to our design, an Energy Tower will be built near the Ecological Dome to extract underground heat for power generation, and the remaining energy will be transmitted to the heating hub inside the Ecological Dome for warmth, while the circulating medium will be re-injected underground through the return well beneath the heating hub." The designer, having adopted this plan, is undoubtedly well-versed in the relevant technology.

"Hmm, this is quite a good application; it can generate electricity and provide heating. Very good." Chen Xin nodded, affirming the design of this plan. But he looked over the blueprints again and continued to ask, "So, you placed the planting greenhouse and military camp outside the Ecological Dome? Although connected, they’re not placed inside the main body of the Ecological Dome?"

"That’s right, because according to our calculations, while it’s not impossible to fit the planting greenhouse and military camp inside the Ecological Dome, it would mean a lot of space inside will be occupied, making it feel very cramped. So, it’s better to adopt this layout that spreads out more." The designer explained his design concept and even made notes on the blueprints: "We’ve designed the military camp as the main entrance. To enter the Ecological Dome area, you’d have to go through the area where the military camp is located, which also serves security and screening purposes.

On both sides, there are the geothermal power plant and planting greenhouse areas, respectively located on the sides of the Ecological Dome, presenting an overall triangular layout. This way, each part gets enough space while being relatively compact."

"You guys know better in these aspects, so let’s go with your design as long as it meets my usage needs. There’s no problem otherwise." Chen Xin didn’t express differing opinions or views on this matter; he nodded before continuing to ask, "Regarding construction, what are your plans? Using the heating hub to melt the snow first and then leveling the ground before construction?"

This was the method Chen Xin previously used at Lin City Railway Station and was also the original intention of his heating hub design. Considering that there was similarly more than ten meters of snow in front of his house, Chen Xin first thought of this construction method.

"Yes, so we will first complete the construction of the geothermal power plant and the heating hub, and then proceed with the construction of the Ecological Dome and other buildings." Facing Chen Xin, the client, the designer answered his questions in great detail, leaving nothing unsaid: "Once the geothermal power plant and heating hub parts are completed, we’ll begin to lay the foundation of the Ecological Dome and simultaneously start the military camp construction.

After laying the foundation, we’ll proceed with the construction of the main steel structure support of the Ecological Dome using the 3D printing robots you, Academician Chen, provide. Once the entire structure is completed, we’ll install the glass facades of the dome and construct the internal structures."

"Are the glass facades reliable? Although the frequency of meteor falls has greatly reduced, occasional meteor fragments still fall to the ground, right? How did you consider this aspect?" It’s no wonder Chen Xin had this question, as Blue Star’s current disasters stem from meteor impacts.

In the past year or more, even though Flame Country wasn’t the main disaster area for meteor collisions, fiery meteors were often seen falling from the sky.

In such circumstances, not to mention whether it can withstand sporadic meteor impacts, at least it should ensure that post-impact, the entire building facade doesn’t shatter completely, necessitating a complete rebuild.

"We have adopted a multi-layer composite structure for the glass facades, with tempered glass as the main material, added with lamination to prevent shattering. Moreover, the glass itself has a heat-absorption coating, capable of adjusting transparency according to light intensity, ensuring the maximum acquisition of natural light and radiation, while providing insulation." The designer clearly gave it thorough consideration, particularly flipping through the blueprints to show the glass facade design, introducing it to Chen Xin proudly: "Considering potential meteor impacts, we adopted a hexagonal honeycomb-like splicing design for the glass facades. If an impact occurs, some glass might be shattered but won’t shatter completely.

Additionally, we’ve adjusted the building’s internal structure. Even if explosions or meteor impacts occur due to various factors, the impact on the glass facades won’t be strong, nor will it lead to the breakage of all glass facades on the entire building."

"If that’s the case, I have no other opinions. Proceed with the construction according to your design plan." Chen Xin felt he had learned enough and decisively said, "As for the 3D printing robots needed for construction, I’ll provide them to you before the commencement of work."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 409 - 407: Nanotechnology Armor
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Because the high-level officials of Xiang Province wanted to win over the newly appointed academician, Chen Xin, the construction of the Ecological Dome proceeded rapidly.

Once the design plans were finalized, and Chen Xin provided 3D printing robots for the construction, the area in front of his shelter quickly became a bustling construction site.

Two drilling rigs, brought in through connections with Xiang Province’s high officials, began working simultaneously; one was responsible for the geothermal power plant’s Energy Tower pump well, and the other for the Ecological Dome’s heating hub return well. At the same time, another team was installing pipelines.

With multiple teams working simultaneously, the area in front of Chen Xin’s house was quite lively and noisy, and the stationed special forces became more vigilant about the situation.

Even though Chen Xin’s shelter had defenses comparable to the main armor of a battleship, with heavy machine guns installed on both sides of the main entrance, allowing for self-sufficiency as long as Chen Xin stayed indoors, the special forces commander, who had finished handling matters in Lin City, still felt it was not safe enough.

Therefore, besides conducting checks upon entering the construction area and patrolling the site, they also allocated some personnel to set up a sentry post in front of Chen Xin’s shelter, preventing anyone unnecessary from approaching, all to ensure Chen Xin’s safety and avoid any repeat of the previous incident.

However, despite this, it was impossible for Chen Xin to stay in his shelter indefinitely.

"Academician Chen, I still recommend you don’t go out," the new officer, replacing Wang Hai to serve as Chen Xin’s security guard, tried to dissuade Chen Xin from going out.

In response to the officer’s persuasion, Chen Xin displayed a rather helpless attitude and said, "Second Lieutenant Deng, you can’t just not let me go out, can you? How is that different from putting me in jail?"

"But your safety, Academician Chen..." Second Lieutenant Deng clearly had been ordered firmly by his superiors to prioritize Chen Xin’s safety at all costs: "Even though the assassination incident in Lin City has come to an end, there’s still a risk if you go out, I must ensure your safety!"

"If you want to ensure my safety, you can simply accompany me outside and deal with any danger promptly, rather than saying that for the sake of my safety, I’m not allowed to step out," Chen Xin understood that Second Lieutenant Deng had good intentions, but he agreed to let the state send people to protect him, not to be restricted in his freedom.

Regarding safety issues, after the previous incident, Chen Xin had reflected deeply and not only began learning some basic self-defense and combat techniques from Qin Lan but also improved his gear.

Power Armor was naturally Chen Xin’s first choice, but the earlier model he had was too heavy and cumbersome, making it safe to wear but lacking in convenience and flexibility.

Exoskeletons were certainly a good option as well, but unless fitted with military-grade protective plates, an exoskeleton didn’t offer significant protection.

For various reasons, gear from StarCraft’s Ghost Agent, the famous Iron Man suits, and Crysis’s Nanotech Combat Suit seemed more suitable for Chen Xin.

The protection was sufficient, and they were light and agile, not overly bulky.

However, Chen Xin wasn’t fond of the early Iron Man suits, favoring only the last one equipped with nanotechnology; yet to recreate it would require spending thousands of survival points.

Ghost Agent’s Protective Suit and Nanotech Combat Suit were relatively simple to create, and by not trying to replicate those unscientific or magical in-game features, it was still feasible to make similar equipment.

But in doing so, the outcome would be rather ordinary, not much different from wearing military Exoskeleton Armor.

After much deliberation, Chen Xin gritted his teeth and, leveraging his substantial survival points income, managed to produce a set of nanotechnology armor similar to the Iron Man suit in the movies.

However, Chen Xin’s nanotech armor differed from the Mark 50 and Mark 85 models seen in the movies. At the very least, he didn’t opt for the gaudy gold-red color scheme, nor was he keen on placing an Energy Core on his chest like a light bulb.

The final nanotech armor was designed as a watch on Chen Xin’s wrist, which would release a mass of nanites to cover his body when needed, transforming into a protective armor.

According to Chen Xin’s own tests, the full coverage of the nanotech armor took about three seconds, not very quick, but sufficient for most scenarios.

Chen Xin also considered the potential for unexpected situations, so his watch included an Energy Shield function, forming a close-fitting energy layer around his body to repel external threats without hindrance or leaving any blind spots.

As for the shield’s strength, it could withstand shots from a large-caliber anti-materiel sniper rifle without being penetrated.

With such protection, Chen Xin felt confident enough about his safety to step outside.

Otherwise, after experiencing an assassination attempt, he was very concerned about his safety.

Initially, Chen Xin was reluctant to show off his nanotech armor and Energy Shield to others, but seeing Second Lieutenant Deng’s staunch "I won’t budge no matter what reason you give" attitude, Chen Xin reconsidered and decided to display his nanotech armor.

With the need to use his nanotech armor, Chen Xin typically wore a Power Armor pilot suit on his body, though he didn’t wear the helmet.

This way, he could deploy the nanotech armor in the shortest time without being hindered by his clothing.

Second Lieutenant Deng was clearly stunned by Chen Xin’s display of nanotech armor, watching as Chen Xin swiftly enveloped himself in a shiny, liquid-like armor, and then, in a blink, the whole suit became dull and subdued; it’s impressive Deng’s jaw didn’t drop, which says something about his composure.

Of course, he might have simply been mesmerized.

In any case, after showcasing his new equipment, Chen Xin asked Second Lieutenant Deng: "This is my newly created nanotech armor; it can withstand direct hits from rocket launchers. Plus, with your team fully geared to accompany me, we should be safe enough, right? If someone could harm me with such protection, staying indoors wouldn’t matter anyway."

Second Lieutenant Deng hesitated for a moment before replying to Chen Xin’s argument: "I need to consult with the captain and political commissar."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 410 - 408: Leaving the House
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The emergence of nano armor clearly surpassed the imaginations of the Commander and the Commissar. They initially thought that even if Chen Xin came up with something, it wouldn’t be too extravagant, perhaps something like liquid bulletproof vests.

After all, Chen Xin left an impression as a highly skilled technical talent, but they never expected that what Chen Xin created was a genuinely science-fiction product beyond the reach of current technology.

The so-called nano armor has different portrayals in various science fiction works. In the movies, Iron Man’s suit uses a control core to arrange nano molecules into armor, thereby realizing various functions and shape changes.

In contrast, the nanosuit in "Crysis" is different. It has a fixed appearance and achieves different armor functions by switching between modules using nanomaterials.

The nanosuit is just a standard combat armor. Although its performance is cool, it pales compared to Iron Man’s suit in the movies.

Through different arrangements of nano molecules, various functional component combinations are achieved, exhibiting a variety of capabilities, which from a visual and cinematic effects perspective, are indeed very cool.

In practical terms, nano armor is also very useful, because the armor is composed of so-called nano molecules. They can be arranged and combined at will, obtaining new molecular structures at the microscopic level, granting it various physical properties.

At the same time, it can also form various components with different functions at the macroscopic level, able to function in any kind of environment.

Just like in the movies, when you need a hammer, it becomes a hammer, when you need a gun, it can turn into a gun, and when defending, it can become a shield...

Equipping a set of armor equates to possessing the capabilities of countless sets of armor. It’s hard to imagine equipment more favored by logistics and frontline soldiers, as just one piece of equipment can meet all needs.

Logistics also no longer need to be troubled by various sizes and specifications of ammunition or the increasingly bulky and complex equipment varieties of modern armies; they only need to send nano molecules to the front line.

"As you can see, this set of nano armor can transform into power armor within three seconds, can become a shield to block weapon attacks, and with my combat squad following closely for protection, I don’t think my safety will be an issue."

Chen Xin demonstrated his nano armor to the approaching Commander and Commissar and conducted a close-range shooting test, feeling very assured about his safety.

The truth is, after Chen Xin demonstrated the nano armor, the Commander and the Commissar were no longer worried about his safety.

At this moment, they were concerned about another issue.

"Academician Chen, can this nano armor be mass-produced?" The Commissar looked at Chen Xin with burning eyes and asked.

To this question, Chen Xin touched his nose, shook his head, and said, "I can do it, but with the current industrial production capabilities in the country... it’s not possible, but if it’s the Crysis type of nanosuit, with disregard to cost, small batches can still be made to equip elite forces."

Upon hearing this answer, the Commander and the Commissar couldn’t help but be a little disappointed, but they didn’t mind too much as Chen Xin had already provided the military with a lot of good things, and they weren’t at the stage of coveting every good thing Chen Xin produced.

Moreover, the Commander and the Commissar understood the gap between conceptual equipment created in the laboratory and finalized equipment that can be equipped by the troops.

"So can I go out now?" After retracting the nano armor back into the watch on his wrist, Chen Xin looked at the Commander and the Commissar, expressing again his desire to go out.
