Having confirmed that Chen Xin has the ability to protect himself, the Commander and the Commissar didn’t have the justification to continue preventing Chen Xin from going out. After a discussion between them, they agreed to Chen Xin’s request.

However, when Chen Xin finally went out, Lieutenant Deng’s combat team still accompanied him, and he sat in an armored vehicle.

"Speaking of which, did the person who tried to assassinate me in Lin City have any accomplices? You have mobilized such a large operation, making me believe he might have accomplices still keeping an eye on taking my life!" Sitting in the armored vehicle, Chen Xin couldn’t help but joke with Lieutenant Deng.

Although Chen Xin was indeed greatly affected during the assassination attempt, after the incident passed, especially after his safety was ensured anew, Chen Xin could still view this situation with a relatively light-hearted mindset.

"The person who attempted to assassinate you in Lin City has been interrogated, and we’ve confirmed he acted alone. No related accomplices were found." Compared to Wang Hai, Lieutenant Deng was much more formal. Faced with Chen Xin’s inquiry, his response was thorough: "This individual was originally a mercenary from the South Seas Region. Due to the disaster, he came to this city, and the terrorist attacks that occurred earlier in the urban area were launched by him and his accomplices."

"So it was him!" Chen Xin suddenly realized.

Chen Xin had heard Qin Lan mention this matter before. Although most of the terrorists were captured at the time, with two escaping, one was killed, and only one had gone missing.

The police originally did not conduct a large-scale manhunt due to the harsh environment, thinking the person might freeze to death in the cold. They hadn’t expected him to wander into Lin City.

"This guy is really something; managing to walk from the city to Lin City in such weather." Chen Xin couldn’t help but marvel, as the endurance displayed by this individual exceeded his expectations: "So what was done with him in the end?"

"Bang!" Lieutenant Deng pointed his fingers at his own head to make a shooting gesture.

"Alright then, there’s indeed no need to keep such a person around." Chen Xin shook his head and switched the topic: "If it were you and your team, could you manage to walk from the city to Lin City in this kind of weather?"

Faced with this question, Lieutenant Deng thought for a moment before replying: "If it were under prepared circumstances, every member of our unit could do it. In fact, we have previously gone to the Northeast, training shirtless in minus twenty to thirty-degree environments to develop cold resistance.

This kind of outdoor trek over dozens of kilometers, surviving and marching in extremely cold environments, is also part of special forces training, so walking from the city to Lin City in the current environment is doable.

But preparatory measures must be taken ahead to have enough assurance. If attempting to traverse these dozens of kilometers of mountain roads unprepared, there’s a high chance of freezing to death.

Especially in the current dark environment without sunlight, losing direction inevitably results in freezing to death."

Hearing Lieutenant Deng say this, Chen Xin couldn’t help rubbing his chin and fell into contemplation.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 411 - 409 Vegetables
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Chen Xin’s trip this time was not deliberately to antagonize the special forces protecting him or to express a desire for unrestrained freedom.

Although there might be a hint of that sentiment, the primary reason was indeed that Chen Xin had some things he wanted to handle outside.

With Chen Xin having attained the status of an academician, and with a research base being constructed outside his refuge, the original deal he had with the city’s SWAT team naturally couldn’t proceed further.

It’s not that Chen Xin was unwilling to continue providing vegetables and fruits for the SWAT team, but under the current circumstances, their transactions had become awkward.

Therefore, Chen Xin’s outing this time was to discuss with the SWAT team how to handle this situation.

Escorted by armored vehicles and special forces, Chen Xin and his entourage went not to the city bureau, but to the city government.

The change in his identity meant that the people he interacted with also changed accordingly.

When Chen Xin was just an ordinary online writer, the people he dealt with were friends of similar ordinary status, with no contact at all with government officials.

It was only after the disaster, with the transformation of the old order due to natural calamities and his own system-aided advancement, that he began interacting with people he previously couldn’t have reached.

Now that he is an academician at Flame Country’s highest academy of sciences, the people he deals with have naturally risen in status as well.

In the past, when Chen Xin visited the city government, he would at most meet a few ordinary staff members, and the responsible leaders were rarely seen. But now he was greeted by a Deputy Mayor.

"I didn’t expect our city to produce a talent like Academician Chen. Not discovering your talent earlier was a failure on our part. I hope you won’t mind!" After some pleasantries, the Deputy Mayor asked about the purpose of Chen Xin’s visit: "I wonder what brings Academician Chen here today? If there’s anything you need us to handle, we’ll do our best."

Chen Xin didn’t take the Deputy Mayor’s flattery seriously and, after a modest smile, explained: "Previously, I engaged in a transaction with the city’s SWAT team exchanging supplies for fresh vegetables and fruits. This transaction greatly assisted me, and the SWAT team also helped me a lot.

However, my side is now constructing a research base, and there are plans to build a large planting greenhouse to research ecological agricultural technology. The output of agricultural products will significantly increase, and this change affects my dealings with the SWAT team.

So, I wanted to ask if the city government could sign a new agreement with the research base to procure its agricultural products. This would help consume the research base’s output and improve the living quality of city residents."

Chen Xin’s proposal left the Deputy Mayor pondering; it’s neither a trivial issue nor an insignificant one.

The city’s shelters already have underground farming facilities, which can’t fully meet the entire city’s demand for vegetables and fruits, yet they ensure a certain level of production.

However, this limited production cannot satisfy the entire city’s needs, and it’s even difficult to solely meet the demand of a single shelter.

Thus, the government adopted a distribution method in which fresh produce and other agricultural products are used as rewards for individuals or collectives that made outstanding contributions each month, maximizing resource usage and preventing inequitable distribution issues.

But now, with Chen Xin saying that his research base is to set up a large planting greenhouse and research ecological agriculture technology, leading to a significant agricultural output, it’s clear that the original material distribution plan of the government would be affected.

Therefore, the Deputy Mayor must carefully consider how to respond to this.

"I wonder, Academician Chen, how much agricultural output do you expect from your side?" After some thought, the Deputy Mayor cautiously inquired.

If the output is limited, it can easily be absorbed by the city and continue as rewards alongside the original agricultural products. However, if the research base yields a substantial amount, it brings about complications.

After all, it’s just a research base; although the agricultural output may not be small, it can’t meet an entire city’s needs. But if the output is quite high, continuing to use it as rewards would cheapen it, awkwardly affecting the original reward system.

"Currently, since the planting greenhouse is not yet under construction, it’s hard for me to give a precise answer, but the planned area for the greenhouse is 12 hectares. It will be used to research three-dimensional ecological agricultural planting techniques. In terms of output, we should have several tens of tons of agricultural products each month." Chen Xin also found it hard to estimate, as different fruits and vegetables have different yields and the greenhouse will use three-dimensional planting, so the specific output is uncertain.

This output is obviously considerable, yet not much; several tens of tons may sound a lot, but for a city, it’s still insufficient. Averaged among all residents, it might just be enough for one meal, and using it as a reward, like before, would be too much.

"That’s quite an output! If there really are so many fruits and vegetables, it would be a huge boon to the citizens of the city!" Whatever the Deputy Mayor thought internally, this was undoubtedly a good thing, and he naturally agreed with enthusiasm.

As for how to handle it afterward, he could only say to Chen Xin: "But with so many agricultural products, we need to hold meetings with the relevant departments to determine a conclusion. Academician Chen, how about this: we draft a preliminary plan, and once your produce is harvested, we can finalize the specific handling scheme?"

"That works too, handle it as you see fit; I’m not very knowledgeable about these things." After a few formal exchanges, Chen Xin took his leave.

Upon leaving the city government, Lieutenant Deng, in the armored vehicle, suddenly curiously asked Chen Xin: "Academician Chen, isn’t several tons of fruits and vegetables not much? Can’t the research base consume it internally once it’s completed?"

"Hehe, do you think there’s only one greenhouse?" In response to the question, Chen Xin smiled meaningfully.

Although only one greenhouse is currently planned, Chen Xin had already planned large-scale greenhousing since before going to Lin City, and now that conditions are ripe, how could he stop at just one?

Ignoring the puzzled Lieutenant Deng, Chen Xin said to the driver, "Don’t be in a hurry to return—I still want to visit a friend."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 412 - 410 Opportunity
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

In Li Yulong’s shelter, he looked at Chen Xin and the special forces soldier standing beside him, his eyes filled with envy and undisguised shock.

Li Yulong was certainly very impressed by Chen Xin, especially since the apocalyptic disaster struck. Chen Xin’s various responses and his transformation after the disaster made Li Yulong see him in a new light and admire him even more.

But he never imagined that Chen Xin would become an academician at the highest science academy in Flame Country, and have special forces assigned for personal protection.

This kind of treatment could only be seen in online novels before. In reality, which of those academicians isn’t of high age, virtue, and with a plethora of publications to earn such treatment?

"Brother Chen, you are truly amazing with the academician title and special forces protection, such prestige! If there’s ever anything I can help with, you must let me know!" Li Yulong praised Chen Xin.

Coming from a merchant family, he had almost an innate instinct and ancestral skill about whom to flatter, network with, and the attitude to adopt.

However, to Chen Xin, these were things he had heard so often lately that his ears were practically calloused.

And for Chen Xin, who considered Li Yulong as a brother and friend, he didn’t like him being like this now.

"If you keep talking like that, do you believe I’ll punch you?" Chen Xin shook his head, saying to Li Yulong, "If there’s a benefit, you won’t miss out, and if there’s trouble, you won’t miss that either. I consider you a brother, so don’t play games with me."

Upon hearing Chen Xin say this, Li Yulong immediately laughed, but his expression became much more natural: "Hehe, it’s just that Brother Chen, you’ve become so incredible all of a sudden, I’m having a hard time adjusting!"

"What’s so hard to adjust to, aren’t we still interacting as before?" Chen Xin said indifferently, but he was well aware that it was impossible to truly interact as before.

Back when writing novels, it was often depicted that some people in high positions wanted to interact and have fun with friends as before but couldn’t. Even though they maintained their past mindset, as their identity and status changed, friends around them could no longer return to the past.

Calculations of various gains and losses, there was no longer the simple and straightforward manner of the past.

Originally, Chen Xin thought this only happened in novels or was only felt by those really in high positions, lamenting the coldness at the heights, but now he had to experience the same feelings himself.

Li Yulong, as a second-generation rich kid, was originally the best-off among Chen Xin’s group of friends. Combined with his decent personality, everyone was willing to interact with him, considering him a friend and brother. Although they didn’t flatter him because of his status, it inevitably had some impact.

But now, even second-generation rich like Li Yulong had to flatter and please Chen Xin. With the previous Deputy Mayor being careful about him, it forced Chen Xin to reflect. Indeed, once the identity changes, not only do the people you interact with change, but others’ attitudes towards you undergo a significant change as well.

Taking a moment to calm himself, clearing away these unnecessary thoughts, Chen Xin finally spoke to Li Yulong: "I’m here to discuss serious business with you."

"Brother Chen, speak, I won’t shirk from anything I can accomplish!" Li Yulong saw Chen Xin’s serious expression and also became more serious, looking at Chen Xin while waiting for him to speak.

"I am currently building a research base, and later there will be approximately 15 hectares of a planting greenhouse for ecological agriculture research. My plan is to use it to find ways to increase the yield of grain and vegetables." Chen Xin explained the situation to Li Yulong, and after letting him understand the general idea, he continued: "Later, my place will produce quite a bit every month. I’ve already talked with the municipal government; they will purchase these outputs, but they haven’t decided how to allocate them yet."

"So do we have an opportunity to get involved?" Li Yulong’s eyes lit up immediately, his business intuition sensing an opportunity.

"No, no, if it were just getting involved, it wouldn’t need to be this troublesome. I’d just give you a share of the output and be done with it." Chen Xin raised a finger and shook it, his face also breaking into a smile, as he said, "I want you to handle the distribution of these fruits and vegetables’ output. On one hand, this counts as looking out for you, and on the other hand, it’s for the allocation of more outputs in the future."

"More output? But..." Li Yulong hesitated for a moment but quickly understood.

Since Chen Xin could build one greenhouse, he could surely build more in the future. Once there are more greenhouses, there will be a continuous output of food.

Now it may only be a little, insufficient to satisfy everyone’s needs, but what if there are more greenhouses?

Eventually, it would be possible to satisfy everyone’s needs.

And during this process, Li Yulong would undertake the distribution of outputs. As the total output increases, he would rise with the tide, eventually participating in the supply of the city’s resources.

This is undoubtedly a great opportunity for Li Yulong, even before the disaster, such opportunities were rare and incredibly hard to come by, almost nonexistent.

For ordinary people, food, clothing, shelter, and transportation are the four most important things, with eating being the most crucial, otherwise, there wouldn’t be the saying that food is the paramount necessity of life.

And being able to control a city’s food supply, even just a part of it, would be enough for Li Yulong to grow into an influential figure in the city, perhaps surpassing his father.

Originally, such an opportunity would never have fallen to Li Yulong, but now Chen Xin has placed it in front of him.

Next, it depends on whether he can seize this opportunity and whether he can grow.

"Brother Chen, from now on, you are like my real brother! You tell me, what should I do?" Li Yulong burst with excitement, pounding his chest, ready to face any challenge.

However, Chen Xin still shook his head and told him, "How to do it is something you need to think about yourself. I’m just informing you about this matter. Also, consider it as a test for you; handle the material distribution work with your ability, or else even if I give you the opportunity, you will struggle to maintain it in the future."

Upon hearing Chen Xin say this, although Li Yulong felt a bit disappointed, he nodded in understanding.

Having been educated by his father since childhood, he understood that doing business based on relationships could only last for a while. People could help you once, but not for a lifetime. Only if your capabilities are strong enough can you sustain the business long-term.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 413 - 411 Super Warrior
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Returning to his own refuge, Chen Xin settled into a peaceful life here. The life inside the refuge was orderly, and the construction site outside was also proceeding smoothly, as if everything had temporarily calmed down.

However, Chen Xin did not idle, and he even felt a certain impulse to find things to do.

As a newly appointed academician, although Chen Xin had not formally started work, he still needed to handle the research projects previously assigned to him. Despite these projects becoming fewer after the country adjusted its cooperation model with him, they still took up some of his time.

In addition, Chen Xin needed to establish some research topics for himself.

This is one of the things Chen Xin needs to do after becoming an academician, since, as an academician, he couldn’t just upgrade the research projects assigned to him by the country.

Although the research base has not been completed, and research work cannot begin immediately, first setting the topics is still possible and necessary.

The main topics Chen Xin currently established include ecological agriculture, geothermal heating, controlled nuclear fusion, and military equipment research, which are also the primary research projects once the research base is built.

Among these four projects, however, the military equipment research does not have specific weapon or equipment types. Instead, they research whatever Chen Xin creates, so it can only be considered a big topic rather than a specific study.

Just like the heavy mecha and mobile camps provided by Chen Xin to protect his special forces, these could already be divided into two completely different research topics, establishing two research groups.

As for geothermal heating and controlled nuclear fusion, these two are crucial energy topics for the country. But after Chen Xin provided the related equipment and blueprints to the country, he actually no longer needed to do much in this area.

The country has already sent more specialized researchers to take over the relevant research, and also does not require Chen Xin to do anything further.

After all, be it openly or on paper, all he could do was directly upgrade the equipment without considering the theoretical basis, and any related theoretical research could only be left to others.

As for ecological agriculture, it is even more complex. Trying to create a self-sustaining ecosystem is not something someone like Chen Xin, who couldn’t even set up a water cycle in the refuge, could handle. The related knowledge involved is such that no one could learn it all in a lifetime.

Researching ecosystems inevitably requires a large group of people working together.

Although Chen Xin just wanted to create a small self-sustaining ecological circle, not study the entire ecosystem, this is still not an easy task.

Moreover, with the Ecological Dome not yet completed and related work unable to proceed, this topic is merely a topic at the moment, and even relevant researchers are in the process of being dispatched and recruited.

So, for the short term, Chen Xin genuinely does not know what he can do or wants to do.

Continue upgrading his equipment, upgrading the Nanomech Armor and Power Armor?

Chen Xin shook his head to dismiss this thought. In terms of defense, power, or mobility, he already had enough. With the existence of the Nanomech Armor, he could completely act like Iron Man in the movies, flying and tackling evil.

Even though there is currently no occasion for Chen Xin to play the superhero, he doesn’t want to do something that’s akin to wearing underwear outside.

Moreover, the key is that individual might, no matter how strong, is useless without an enemy.

If a bullet can kill the enemy, what’s the big difference in using a missile to blow them up?

After giving it serious consideration, Chen Xin firmly gave up the idea of upgrading his equipment in the short term, despite planning to build something like a Gundam or Titan for fun, but that is not now.

However, while his own equipment doesn’t need upgrading, the army’s equipment is another matter. For Chen Xin, not only can the army’s equipment be upgraded, but also the soldiers themselves.

"Second Lieutenant Deng, I’d like to know, as a special force soldier, what do you think about body modification?" Chen Xin asked while walking along the construction site, questioning Second Lieutenant Deng beside him.

"Body modification? Isn’t that kind of thing evil?" Second Lieutenant Deng did not know much about it but was influenced by movies and TV set on evil scientists conducting body modifications, naturally feeling that this is not good.

However, Chen Xin doesn’t see it that way and wasn’t seeking this answer.

"You know the Master Chief from the Halo series, right? Do you want to be strong like him?" Chen Xin changed the way he asked.

"Master Chief? Spartan Warrior? Academician Chen, can you achieve this level?" Second Lieutenant Deng was quite young, even younger than Chen Xin, and naturally aware of this hot sci-fi game: "But Spartan Warriors require modification from childhood? Academician Chen, do you..."

"That’s not your concern, just answer my question, do you want that kind of power?" Chen Xin disregarded Second Lieutenant Deng’s wild imagination, requiring only his response.

Pressed by Chen Xin’s questioning, Second Lieutenant Deng thought carefully and replied, "If I could, of course, I’d want such power, but if it’s based on modifying children, it would seem too cruel."

"Spartan Warrior technology is indeed somewhat brutal, and its mortality and cost are too high. If it were up to me, I’d choose adults willing to undergo modification and reduce the risks and costs of surgery." Chen Xin shook his head, obviously not very optimistic about the Spartan modification technology in the game.

In actual fact, although the Spartan Warriors in the Halo series are indeed very strong—especially the Master Chief who can face heaven and earth, seemingly invincible—when compared to macho men in other sci-fi works, the Master Chief seems somewhat lacking.

Speaking solely of physical prowess, Space Marines from the Warhammer 40K series, who can live for hundreds or even thousands of years, are undoubtedly much stronger than the Master Chief.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin suddenly recalled a thought that had popped into his head earlier and asked Second Lieutenant Deng, "If you could gain stronger power and physique, but the cost might be significant changes in your body, even developing some animal characteristics, do you think this is acceptable?"

"Animal characteristics? Changes in the body? What specific characteristics and changes are you talking about? Academician Chen, can you elaborate?" Second Lieutenant Deng did not reject immediately but wanted to evaluate. If the impact is minimal, he doesn’t find it unacceptable.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 414 - 412 - Space Wild Wolf
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

If you’re familiar with science fiction and enjoy playing games, you should not find the name "Warhammer" unfamiliar.

This war game tabletop series created by a British tabletop game company is known for its grand setting and intricate design and has a considerable number of enthusiasts around the world.

The entire Warhammer series is divided into the Medieval era, the 30K era, and the 40K era. The Medieval era has a fantasy setting, which greatly differs from the 30K and 40K eras’ space opera-style science fiction themes.

Chen Xin, as an online writer, is also an enthusiast of Warhammer, but he mainly enjoys the science fiction series of 30K and 40K.

In these two series, human civilization has developed to an incredible level, with the empire’s territory spread across the entire galaxy, a population numbering in the trillions, fleets outnumbering the stars, and enormous legions recorded only by designation in casualty lists, fighting relentlessly with countless enemies capable of destroying the entire galaxy.

Among the entire lineup of the human empire, the most famous and distinctive are undoubtedly the Space Marines.

The eighteen unique Space Marine Chapters and their combat prowess akin to semi-divine beings undoubtedly make Space Marines the absolute protagonists of the Warhammer series.

Each of the eighteen Space Marine Chapters has unique appearances and Chapter features, and the one Chen Xin likes the most is undoubtedly the Space Wolves, whose prototype comes from Vikings.

Their slightly primitive and rugged aura, Viking characteristic design, and wolf-like combat style make this Chapter extremely distinctive even among the eighteen Space Marine Chapters.

In the background setting of the Space Wolves, their most distinctive and legendary feature is their Wolf Gene.

This unique gene, obtained through oral pharmaceutical means, causes drastic changes in the Space Wolves’ warriors both mentally and physically, increasing body size by 80%, reshaping most of their skeletons, growing lush hair and massive wolf fangs, and possibly transforming them into "Werewolves."

Not only does it change their form, but warriors possessing the Wolf Gene also harbor savagery and bloodlust deep in their souls. Though they can usually suppress it through training and rationality, it resurfaces as instinct under extreme pressure and roaring battles on the battlefield.

What Chen Xin wanted to do was to create a real-life version of the Space Wolves.

They are true semi-divine beings, whose sheer physical strength is enough to oppose an entire army, and it is said that a single squad of Space Marines is enough to suppress a planetary rebellion.

Of course, due to the background setting, the modification technology of Space Marines carries a considerable degree of mysterious essence, including their birth, manufacturing, growth, and combat processes, all influenced by mysterious elements, which are almost impossible to replicate in the real world.

Moreover, it’s crucial to note that the Warhammer 30K and 40K series settings are set in a distant future, thousands of years from now, where human technology has developed to an unimaginable stage, making it very difficult to realistically replicate such distinct entities as Space Marines, especially Space Wolves.

Even with the assistance of a system, it’s difficult for Chen Xin to achieve, especially in terms of equipping each modified warrior with a full set of gear, nor does he have hundreds of years to wait for them to grow.

But if it’s just a certain degree of replication, such as through the implantation of the Wolf Gene to enable ordinary soldiers to quickly grow their physiques and have powerful combat abilities, it is still quite feasible.

This is exactly what Chen Xin wanted to do.

Genetic modification technology and the manufacture of the Wolf Gene is not something Chen Xin cannot achieve.

Although the system’s upgrade feature can’t be used on humans or other animals and doesn’t even affect plants, it is still capable of manufacturing an instrument that can conduct genetic modification or genetic compilation.

Chen Xin only needs to manufacture the relevant instrument and then use it to create the Wolf Gene as well as the Genetic Seed required for the nineteen surgical modifications of Space Marines, thus being able to replicate the existence of Space Marines, comparable to semi-divine beings, in reality.

Even the most ordinary Space Marine stands taller than the giant Yao Ming, with a physique even more robust and muscular than the great shark O’Neal.

Imagine a group of warriors over two meters tall, with robust physiques akin to Wolverine, appearing on the battlefield, equipped with powerful weapons and armor—how terrifying would that be?

Someone might think, why would we need such formidable warriors in the current environment? Every nation must take care of its own; who would initiate war at a time like this? Is there a need for such powerful soldiers?

To this question, Chen Xin only wants to answer with one sentence: having no sword and having one that is unused are two different matters.

And indeed, perhaps every country now really is preoccupied, but the more that’s the case, the more careful one should be, because there will always be those who, unable to survive, will act recklessly and desperately.

Ordinary small countries are not frightening, but the northern bear of the Flame Country is certainly not a pushover.

Among the five rogue powers, the history of wars between it and the Flame Country is more than once. That country sandwiched between the two was originally Flame Country’s territory, forcibly split apart by the northern bear to become a so-called nation!

Perhaps due to the changes of time, the two countries had times as close as brothers, and relations are friendly now, but when faced with a nation’s survival, any hope placed in an enemy’s goodwill is akin to massacring their own country’s people.

Moreover, it is vital that Space Marines are designed for combat in space environments; their physical strengths are extremely formidable enough to withstand any harsh environment.

Especially Space Wolves, who live on a planet where half the year the seas are frozen, the immense power endowed by the Wolf Gene even allows them to stride across thousand-mile ice plains.

In such a situation, having a force capable of combating extreme environments is great not just for warfare but also for disaster relief, as our country’s army has a long-standing tradition of leading rescue efforts.

After carefully considering, ensuring his thinking was correct, Chen Xin then organized his thoughts, deciding to make the creation of Space Wolves his next undertaking, and pondering how to commence it.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 415 - 413: Declaration
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Does the country have stored genetic samples or live embryos of animals from before the disaster?" Chen Xin asked a research staff member next to him while tinkering with a prototype of a project.

This research staff was here to deliver a prototype of a research project that needed upgrading. Although the total number of projects assigned to Chen Xin had significantly decreased, prototypes still frequently arrived at Chen Xin’s place for upgrades.

Hearing Chen Xin’s question, the research staff member recalled for a moment before replying, "The country established seed banks in the Kunlun Mountains and the Himalayas before the disaster, preserving a large number of plant seeds, animal embryos, and genetic samples. Is there something you need, Academician Chen?"

The research staff’s answer reminded Chen Xin. He had previously heard that the country froze a large quantity of plant seeds and animal embryos in the seed banks of the Kunlun Mountains and the Himalayas, as a foundation for rebuilding Earth’s ecological environment post-apocalypse.

If Flame Country had anything that Chen Xin needed now, these two places were undoubtedly the most promising.

After confirming this, Chen Xin nodded and said to the research staff, "Can the genetic samples and live embryos from these two seed banks be applied for research? I intend to conduct some genetic modification research and need samples and live embryos of wolf genes."

"Genetic modification research? What kind of research are you planning to do, Academician Chen?" The research staff was immediately curious upon hearing Chen Xin’s words.

After all, Chen Xin had previously mostly dealt with machines and had no contact with biological research. Now, although Chen Xin was preparing to enter the field of ecological agriculture research, which indeed requires genetic modification technology, why did he need wolf genetic samples?

"I plan to breed a type of wolf that can adapt to the current climate environment to help humans better survive in the current environment." Chen Xin did not reveal his ultimate goal to the research staff in front of him.

Regarding the breeding of Space Marines, Chen Xin decided it was better for him to convey this himself. If the information were transmitted through ordinary research staff and leaked, it would have undesirable consequences.

"Indeed, Academician Chen! Even if others had this idea, they wouldn’t have the capability to implement it. You are truly remarkable! If you can breed a batch of animals that adapt to the current environment, it could play a significant role!" The research staff understood the prospects of Chen Xin’s proposal with just a little thought.

Leaving aside other uses, even if these animals served no other purpose, slaughtering them for meat and acquiring their fur would be beneficial.

Not to mention, Chen Xin aimed to breed wolves, close kin to dogs, which, if properly nurtured, could certainly become good helpers for humans.

"So can these embryos and samples be applied for?" Chen Xin disregarded the research staff’s compliments, only concerned about obtaining the genetic samples and live embryos.

After all, no matter how skilled he was, and no matter how powerful his system upgrade function was, he couldn’t create something from nothing. He needed genetic samples and live embryos for modification to make the entire transformation work.

Moreover, the key point was that this was just the beginning. Once he bred a new species of wolf, he would need to extract the so-called "wolf gene" from these wolves for his real objective—the modification of space wolf warriors.

Of course, the modified creatures in the future certainly couldn’t be called Space Marines or space wolf warriors, as they were only ground forces, far from reaching the level of galloping through the starry skies and hunting the firmament as originally envisaged.

"If it’s you, Academician Chen, it should be possible, right? I’m not sure about this matter. The seed banks are for preserving species samples to restore the ecosystem post-apocalypse, which might take hundreds to thousands of years to activate once the natural environment recovers." The research staff pondered for a moment and said to Chen Xin, "If you apply in the name of research, you should be able to get approval, considering you are going to conduct ecological environment research, applying for a batch of genetic samples should be justified."

"Thank you for your suggestion, I will consider it carefully." Chen Xin nodded, already devising a plan in his mind.

After finishing the upgrade of the project prototype in hand, Chen Xin sent it off with the research staff and then proceeded to the temporary military camp of the special forces. He found the Commander, explained his purpose, borrowed their communication equipment, and sent his request for wolf genetic samples and live embryos, along with his conceived genetic modification proposal.

After transmitting the information, Chen Xin also spoke with the Commander about the idea of genetic modification to create super soldiers.

"Can such genetic modification really be done? Would there be any bad consequences?" The first thing the Commander thought of was whether such genetic modification would have side effects.

Chen Xin shook his head. He was confident in his technology, or rather, the upgrade capability of his system. "There would be no bad consequences, but according to my concept, the modified soldiers’ height could reach 2.3~2.5 meters, and their weight would exceed 200 kilograms, with single-arm impact power surpassing half a ton.

There might be instances of dense hair, abnormally growing canines, and some degree of aggression and bloodthirsty tendencies. If these are the adverse effects you’re referring to, some can be controlled and adjusted, while others cannot."

"Which aspects can be controlled?" the Commander inquired with concern. If one simply grows taller and stronger, those are acceptable, but aggression and bloodthirsty tendencies are not traits desired in soldiers, at least not in Flame Country.

Chen Xin anticipated this question from the Commander, as it was a reasonable concern. He smiled and answered, "The increase in height and weight is inevitable, which is the intended effect of genetic modification. As for the dense hair and abnormally growing canines, those are not major issues, while aggression and bloodthirsty tendencies can be controlled and adjusted.

However, I personally believe that retaining a certain degree of wildness is more conducive to combat effectiveness. Although it may indeed bring some negative effects, overall, it remains controllable."

"Sounds good, a bit of hairiness on a man is nothing!" the Commander evidently didn’t consider dense hair on a man to be much of a side effect, merely a matter of using razors more frequently. "But the aggression and bloodthirstiness are very dangerous, we can’t have our soldiers like that."

Chen Xin neither confirmed nor denied, merely nodding and saying, "It’s not difficult to remove that, and it can be controlled through postnatal training."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 416 - 414: Weak Flesh
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Genetic Modification is a highly controversial topic, with various moral and ethical issues that are sufficient to hinder anyone who wishes to research it.

Even with unwavering determination to study it, there will still be people questioning you: "If a person modifies their genes, can they still be called human?"

Similar ideological conflicts and disputes are common in various science fiction works. No matter when, there’s always a group of so-called human supremacists who shout "for the purity of the blue world" stepping forward to stop it.

They might even concoct more horrifying and anti-human atrocities to stop the so-called crime of "staining human genes," justifying them as necessary sacrifices.

"...But, what truly qualifies as being human? What makes a human, a human?" Faced with the same question from Qin Lan, Chen Xin countered her with another question.

"This, humans are human, aren’t they? Humans are called humans because we are intelligent, have wisdom and thoughts?" Qin Lan, having also read some science fiction works under the influence of Chen Xin, could answer his question without being at a loss.

However, this answer is clearly not comprehensive enough, at least not entirely correct.

"Humans are considered human because we have wisdom and thought, but are wisdom and thought innate to humans? The answer is not necessarily." At this moment, Chen Xin truly appeared like a scholar, sitting in front of Qin Lan and speaking eloquently: "If a baby grows up in human society, then they naturally possess the wisdom and thought that can be called human. But if a baby grows up away from human society, even raised by animals, then they will never possess the wisdom and thought that a human should have."

Qin Lan did not refute Chen Xin’s statement, instead, she nodded in agreement.

In 1920, in a small town called Midnapore northeast of Kolkata in India, two wolf-raised girls, known as wolf children, were discovered and became a sensation.

The older girl was about seven or eight years old, the younger about two. These two little girls were sent to an orphanage in Midnapore to be raised, given names, with the older named Kamala and the younger Amara. The following year Amara died, while Kamala lived until 1929.

From this incident, people concluded that, if wolf children do not have inherent defects at birth, this case illustrates that human knowledge and abilities are not innate and that bipedal walking and speech are not natural instincts. All these are products of social practice and labor acquired later.

"So, the reason humans can be called humans, possessing wisdom and thought, is because of the existence of entire human civilization and society. Without these, we aren’t much different from beasts." In Chen Xin’s view, humans are defined by their thoughts and souls, not their bodies: "From the perspective of species, humans are Homo sapiens, just one species under the order Primates of the subclass Eutheria in the class Mammalia."

"Even under the subtribe Hominina of the family Hominidae, there were 16 other species listed alongside Homo sapiens. Although they are extinct, from a biological point of view, they could also be called human."

"So looking purely from the angle of biological taxonomy, there is no essential difference between humans and other animals. The so-called human supremacy is just human arrogance."

"But even if there’s no fundamental difference between humans and animals, humans are a type of animal. Isn’t genetic modification still polluting the purity of human genes?" Suddenly, Mo Qingyan, who appeared nearby, interjected: "And putting aside other issues, A Xin, if you use wolf genes to modify humans, is the result a wolf or a human? Such things that go against moral ethics shouldn’t be done, right? Didn’t the state just introduce a law against illegal gene editing before the disaster? A Xin, aren’t your actions already illegal?"

"That’s the new article added in Article 336 of the Criminal Law, which criminalizes implanting gene-edited and cloned human embryos into human or animal bodies, or implanting gene-edited and cloned animal embryos into human bodies." Because he needed to conduct related genetic modification research, Chen Xin certainly researched relevant legal texts: "Embryo implantation is a crime. Purely researching genes is not illegal."

"But even if it’s not illegal, it violates ethics! Human genes are a perfect product of evolution. How can they be modified at will?" Due to her former professional habits, Qin Lan didn’t like the attitude of Chen Xin, who believed he could do anything as long as it wasn’t stipulated in the legal texts.

"Ethics should indeed be followed, but if ethics themselves are ignorant, do we still need to adhere to social ethics? In the feudal era, it was considered a virtue for women to have no talent, this was the social ethics of that time. Do we still need to follow such things now? Society is constantly developing and progressing, and so is ethics." Chen Xin patiently explained to Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan, trying to persuade the two of them.

It’s also because it’s the two of them; if it were someone else, Chen Xin would probably have lost patience long ago. There wouldn’t be such good patience to explain slowly.

"Even if social ethics need to constantly progress and develop, it’s not a reason to modify human genes at will, right?" Mo Qingyan comforted Qin Lan, who was somewhat emotional, and turned to inquire Chen Xin: "The human body is a perfect masterpiece of natural evolution. If there is anything inappropriate, can’t we wait for humans to evolve naturally? Why insist on such a radical means as genetic modification?"

"The human body is perfect? Hahaha, you must have read too many novels, really thinking that humans are some Innate Dao Body, where monsters have to transform into humans to cultivate?" Hearing this, Chen Xin laughed as if his stomach hurt until Mo Qingyan, dissatisfied, pinched him. Only then did he restrain his smile and continue to explain: "Humans are the way they are now merely as a result of continually compromising with the natural environment because the evolution of humans is not physical but rather cerebral. From the biological evolution perspective, the human body is actually cumbersome and bloated.

Moreover, not only our body but even our genes are filled with a plethora of redundant chains, some are remnants from when we evolved from ocean to land, some evolved emergency mechanisms just to adapt to a specific period’s environment, but all these things remain in our genes, verbose and useless.

If it were merely excessive waste, there wouldn’t be so much concern, but these complex and intricate genes are constantly mutating and can even collapse, forming life-eroding tumors within the body.

Stripping away the sanctity of humans, purely assessing the strengths and weaknesses of biological structures, none of the human organs are the most excellent, and many are woefully subpar.

Overlapping organ functions lead to tremendous wastage in neural pathways, and the blood and nervous network spirals complexly like the most intricate maze instead of following a more direct route, along with various abandoned and useless information present from the genesis of genes...

If we consider humans as an engineering product, this product could only continue to function through constant hammering and compensation by various coincidences and external factors, and is indeed a substandard item.

Therefore, with the advancement of human technology, progress in medicine and biology, gene optimization and genetic modification is an inevitable development of technology."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 417 - 415 Ethical Review
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Although Chen Xin’s reasons were compelling, ultimately he failed to persuade Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan.

Of course, neither did they persuade Chen Xin to abandon his idea of genetic modification.

The discussion among the three nearly ended on a sour note, but eventually, Chen Xin chose to compromise, promising to proceed gradually, starting with simple genetic enhancement and adjustment, creating the Adjuvant from the Gundam series, instead of jumping straight into creating Space Marines.

While essentially there is no fundamental difference between the two, in terms of acceptability by ordinary people, it was much more palatable.

Moreover, for Chen Xin, creating Adjuvants was also considerably simpler.

The reason Space Marines are so powerful and possess extraordinarily strong physiques is that they undergo nineteen surgical modifications over ten years, implanting various enhancement organs, to grow into super soldiers standing two and a half meters tall and weighing over two hundred kilograms.

Furthermore, they need to undergo a hundred years of recruit training afterward, inheriting a thousand years of combat memories passed down through their genes, to truly become a Space Marine, don the Power Armor that has also been passed down for hundreds if not thousands of years, and become the Empire’s most formidable force, the demigods riding the Galaxy.

As for the Adjuvants from the Gundam series, although in the original work, genetic modification starts from the embryonic stage, for Chen Xin, creating the gene enhancement serum mentioned in those old sci-fi novels, a simple injection to achieve genetic optimization and adjustment, was much easier than truly replicating a Space Marine.

After thinking it over, Chen Xin realized there was indeed no need to take too big of a step.

With Chen Xin’s promise, Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan ceased to say much, although they still retained reservations about the matter.

However, while Chen Xin managed to placate Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan, pushing this matter forward with the state was not so easily accomplished.

The state, knowing Chen Xin wanted to conduct research in the genetic field, originally had a supportive attitude, but the genetic modification plan to create super soldiers proposed by Chen Xin encountered much opposition.

Following repeated meetings, debates, and even disputes, the state dispatched a moral and ethical review group composed of experts in related fields to Chen Xin to examine his research project.

Chen Xin did not refuse this; instead, he cooperatively explained his intentions, the relevant ideas, and the means to achieve them.

"So, Academician Chen, your genetic modification experiment is not achieved through gene editing of embryos?" After hearing Chen Xin’s explanation, one expert in the review group, whose stern expression slightly relaxed, asked Chen Xin.

Faced with this question, Chen Xin did not nod just to pass the review. Instead, he explained: "I envision that the modification of wolves still needs to be achieved through genetic adjustments to embryos, but for human modification, considering the growth time of a normal human, I think modifying an adult individual directly is a quicker method to see results."

"Gene editing itself is not illegal, and the state is actively conducting research in the relevant field; the restrictions in this field are not as stringent as one might imagine." The expert nodded slightly, refraining from explicitly stating anything but exuding a good-natured attitude.

However, while this expert held a benevolent attitude, another expert appeared extremely hostile, displaying an attitude of strong dislike toward Chen Xin.

"Have you considered the consequences of human genetic modification? Pollution of the human gene pool violates social moral ethics and endangers national security!" As soon as this expert spoke, he placed a significant accusation.

Regarding such accusations, Chen Xin merely looked at this expert and retorted nonchalantly, "Cyberpunk full-body prosthetic modifications are acceptable to you, so why oppose simple genetic enhancement? Human supremacy? Fundamentalism? If scientists maintain such notions, human civilization will not progress."

"You!" Chen Xin’s remarks were quite harsh, almost directly accusing the expert of being stuck in the past, naturally angering the other party.

However, this expert was held back by the review group’s leader beside him.

"Academician Chen Xin, this review is mainly to examine whether your research conforms to moral and ethical standards. Researchers must revere life, follow rules, and safeguard the purity of frontier scientific fields to let innovative achievements better benefit humanity." The review group leader emphasized to Chen Xin, neither harsh nor kind in attitude: "Although technological development has brought earth-shattering changes to society, and we are now in exceptional times and environments, research must still adhere strictly to ethical norms, and the ethical baseline cannot be touched or challenged.

Your research, although not performing gene editing on embryos, even so, will the individuals who complete genetic modification transmit the modified genes to the next generation? Will such inheritance result in side effects or unforeseeable outcomes? Are these genetically modified individuals still considered humans, or should they be called new humans?

Academician Chen Xin, you are young and have many ideas, which we old folks might lack in vigor, but I think you should be more cautious. Do you have comprehensive consideration for these issues?"

This speech from the review group’s leader softened Chen Xin’s previously tense attitude, allowing him to reassess his emotions before continuing, "Genetic modification inherently involves optimizing and enhancing existing human genes, directly affecting adult individuals, and indeed, this will be inherited by future generations.

Regarding whether there might be side effects or unforeseeable outcomes, I cannot give you a definitive answer, as only less than 2% of human genes function in encoding proteins, with the remaining 98% whose roles are uncertain.

Although the genetic modifications I intend to do will only adjust this 2%, I can guarantee that at least for three generations, there will be no issues, but whether the enhancement will conflict with inherent human genes or cause new mutations is something needing long-term experiments and observations to ascertain.

As for the question of new humans and old humans, it is not a problem at all, as everyone can accept genetic modifications to become new humans."

Pausing deliberately, Chen Xin then looked at the expert who had previously exhibited hostility toward him before continuing, "Of course, if anyone believes human genes are sacred and inviolable, viewing any alteration as desecration, they can also choose to retain their original genes to maintain the purity of the human gene pool."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 418 - 416: Reconstructive Surgery
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The moral ethics review committee did not immediately reach a conclusion. After all, what Chen Xin mentioned was merely conceptual, and once actual research begins, many things may differ from the initial assumptions.

Especially as this involves human modification and gene editing, which can have extremely significant impacts, the committee has to be cautious.

While the committee was still carefully discussing and reviewing Chen Xin’s research, the military had already approached him, seeking details about his previously proposed super soldier enhancement plan.

The military also understood the issues and impacts brought by human modification and gene editing, but the super soldiers described by Chen Xin still sparked their curiosity and interest.

After all, wasn’t the big guy in the movies, wearing the Federation flag as a cape and carrying a shield through WWII, a genetically modified human?

If all soldiers... no, even if just a division, a special operations unit could achieve such human limit combat capabilities, it would be highly tempting for the military.

Of course, the military’s support for Chen Xin’s research was based on the premise that it wouldn’t cause harm or adverse effects.

"With the current technology I have, achieving combat power like the captain in the movies wouldn’t cause any adverse effects." Chen Xin used the familiar power standards from his past fan fiction writing as an example to explain to the military’s envoy: "This level of genetic modification strictly speaking isn’t even modification, it’s enhancement, allowing the human genes to display their optimal state. This enhancement is actually akin to how genetically modified soybeans can enhance resistance to pests and diseases.

As for whether there would be any adverse effects, I can only say that within three generations, with an individual lifespan of up to 50 years, there would be no problems. Further long-term impacts would need to be observed through extended experimentation to draw more conclusions."

In fact, with the black tech displayed by the system upgrade, achieving completely side-effect-free genetic enhancement would be easy for Chen Xin, but saying so at this moment wouldn’t be believable.

So Chen Xin opted for a more conservative answer.

"No issues within three generations?" Even the military is primarily concerned about heredity and side effects.

"Yes, this conclusion is derived from calculations. As for the potential consequences after three generations, I’m currently short of a supercomputer to calculate possible outcomes." Indeed, a supercomputer can facilitate such calculations, though Chen Xin isn’t directly asking for one, he’s merely stating a fact.

Moreover, once the research base is established, equipping it with a supercomputer is a certainty.

"Three generations... that roughly means at least 50-70 years!" The military’s envoy quickly calculated the time, understanding that Chen Xin’s previous comment about individual survival for fifty years actually coincides with the generational timeframe.

"That isn’t a long timeframe, and the probability of significant issues is actually lower than the probability of a person’s own genetic mutation occurring. Simply put, the chances of genetic enhancement going wrong are far lower than someone developing cancer." Chen Xin gave a typical comparative example, but he also knew it wasn’t a great comparison.

True, the likelihood of cancer is high, and everyone has some chance of developing it. However, cancer is just a disease, non-contagious, and at most harms only one person, with limited impact.

But if genetic enhancement were to go wrong, it could potentially affect all individuals who underwent the enhancement, and potentially their descendants as well.

Being extremely cautious regarding this matter isn’t excessive.

The military’s envoy lacks the authority to make decisions and was merely gathering information from Chen Xin for potential inquiries and discussions by higher authorities.

After inquiring about genetic enhancement, the military envoy also asked about Chen Xin’s previous plan involving obtaining the genetic enhancements of wolf species for creating more powerful super soldiers.

"Academician Chen, how did you think of using wolf genes to modify humans? This isn’t just a simple ethical issue, is it? To put it bluntly, isn’t this human-animal hybridization?" The envoy’s words were heavy, as any person would instinctively reject the idea of turning humans into beasts.

However, in response to this, Chen Xin merely stroked his chin, then took out his notebook, and displayed a set of data in front of the military envoy.

This was a background setting and related data for the Space Wolves in Warhammer 40K, which was indeed the inspiration for Chen Xin’s entire modification plan.

"This is a game setting, right? Academician Chen, your inspiration comes from this? But this so-called Space Marine modification surgery listed involves inserting nineteen artificial organs to forcibly turn an ordinary person into a superhuman. Can this truly be accomplished?" The military envoy didn’t dismiss the idea because it came from a game setting; instead, they earnestly evaluated the content, seriously considering the potential impact if Space Marine modification could truly be realized.

"Regarding solely the modification surgery, I can assure you there’s no problem." Chen Xin dared to make this promise because realizing Space Marine modification technology isn’t particularly difficult.

The real challenge lies in cultivating artificial organs, which is the basis for the existence of Space Marines in Warhammer 40K—the Genetic Seed.

"Ah, no wonder you require wolf genetic samples and live embryos as well as human genetic modification!" The military envoy understood Chen Xin’s point and expressed a face of sudden realization.

Chen Xin affirmed by nodding and pointed to an image of a Space Marine on the computer to explain to the military envoy: "Through genetic modification of wolf species to develop Genetic Seed for cultivating artificial organs, then those artificial organs are surgically implanted into the bodies of candidates.

The entire process, technically speaking, does not alter the individual’s original genes, because the modification surgery doesn’t use gene editing to achieve Space Marine enhancement, but instead relies on the artificial organs to influence the functioning of the body’s natural organs, thus achieving Space Marine enhancement. Theoretically, this shouldn’t affect the original genes of the individuals.

This is also why, in the game setting, completing all nineteen surgical procedures required for Space Marine transformation takes a full ten years, as it’s not gene coding causing mutations but second-stage development under the influence of artificial organs."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 419 - 417: Father’s Persuasion
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Despite Chen Xin’s patient and detailed explanation, neither the ethics review committee nor the military delegates were convinced by his words.

After a strict investigation and review, the ethics review committee only approved Chen Xin’s application for genetic modification experiments on wolves, but rejected and prohibited research on human genes.

The military did not support Chen Xin in this matter either. Although they were tempted by the powerful combat capabilities of the Space Marines that Chen Xin described, they did not choose to support Chen Xin’s research on genetic modification. Instead, they advised him to focus his efforts on research projects related to Mechas—projects that were tangible and unlikely to cause adverse outcomes.

The military even directly indicated to Chen Xin that if he wants to create Titans, Gundams, Space-Air Mother Ships, or Combat Fairy Snow Wind as seen in games, as long as he can make them, the military will fully support him.

Clearly, ever since Chen Xin successfully created Exoskeletons and Power Armor, which originally only existed in science fiction, the military has been researching some sci-fi concepts, evaluating which things Chen Xin could feasibly create, and which would be beneficial to the military.

If it were before, Chen Xin might be very interested in this, as Gundams, Titans, and Space-Air Mother Ships were also very enticing to him.

But now, given his gloomy mood, even directly receiving the military’s indication, he was somewhat uninterested, only agreeing to attempt making Titans.

This matter is thus settled; although Chen Xin feels dissatisfied and gloomy, he couldn’t possibly carry out the research secretly on his own.

After all, although he was eager to conduct research and modifications in this area, he had his limits. Without legal permission, he wouldn’t secretly conduct such research on his own.

With Chen Xin’s capabilities, even if he really did conduct secret research, others couldn’t do anything about him. After all, by the time any issue arose, he might have already created the actual Space Marines.

Although he won’t proceed with the research, Chen Xin still felt very gloomy in his heart.

His gloominess didn’t stem from others stopping him from conducting genetic modification research, but from wondering why these people were so resistant to genetic modification.

Whether the review committee or the military delegates stopped Chen Xin from conducting the genetic modification research out of responsibility for the matter, rather than personal sentiment, so Chen Xin didn’t hold any objections to that.

But during the review process, their apparent mistrust and aversion to genetic modification made Chen Xin feel they were somewhat overly conservative and traditional.

Feeling gloomy, Chen Xin certainly wanted to find someone to talk to, but Qin Lan and Mo Qingyan had previously expressed aversion to the matter, making it inappropriate for him to talk to them.

Thinking it over, Chen Xin sought out his father to discuss the matter, hoping to hear advice from a senior.

After listening to his son’s complaints, Chen Xin’s father did not directly comment on genetic modification but asked Chen Xin, "Do you think your technology is ahead of current technology by how many years? Can you be stronger than anyone else?"

"I’ve never done any research in this area before, but if given the opportunity to start researching, I am confident that I can lead others by 50 to 100 years! I can indeed outperform them all!" Chen Xin was full of confidence on this matter, although this confidence primarily stemmed from an unspeakable system.

However, after hearing Chen Xin’s words, his father shook his head and advised him, "You’re a grown man now; don’t be so hasty in your actions. Since you’re stronger than everyone else, why not wait for others or simply lend them a hand? When everyone’s at the same level, then you can do what you want."

"The old saying goes, ’The protruding beam rots first.’ If you’re too strong individually, making others seem like waste, you’ll encounter unnecessary pressure. Being a step ahead makes you a genius, but being several steps ahead, leaving others far behind, makes people afraid of you."

Chen Xin’s father didn’t evaluate whether his son’s research ambitions were good or bad but instead advised him from another perspective, not to surpass ordinary human understanding.

If this advice came a few years ago, Chen Xin might not have listened at all, thinking that lying low is just for those who lack abilities, or wondering why he should hide his talents when he can be capable. But now, over thirty, having passed the impulsive age, he was able to listen to his father’s recommendations.

"Dad, you think I’m showing off too much, so they’re afraid... No, actually trying to prevent me from going awry?" Chen Xin thought about his father’s words, suddenly switching to another thought process.

"In a very dangerous task, if you have two people do it, and you know the capability of one—not very capable but you know what consequences he’s likely to face even if he fails," Chen Xin’s father looked at his son with an expression of apparent appreciation, giving an example, "while the other one, you know he’s competent and strong but don’t know the extent of his capability, nor the consequences if he messes it up."

"In such a case, are you more willing to trust the first person and have him handle the matter, or the second person, allowing him to freely take charge?"

This example from Chen Xin’s father made Chen Xin take a deep breath, realizing that in some matters he was acting too hastily.

While the saying, ’The tree that stands out gets blown down,’ might not always hold true, there’s a grain of truth; one can be excellent but excessively excellent poses an oppression and threat to ordinary people.

Although most people might not harbor bad thoughts due to another’s excellence, a certain degree of fear and caution is something everyone might have.

"I understand now, Dad." Chen Xin nodded to his father, and upon understanding what his father wanted to convey, his mood improved significantly.

With this newfound calmness, Chen Xin began to reflect on himself, realizing that some of his previous behaviors may have been too inflated, or one might say, too rash.

Previously, things like Exoskeletons, Isotope Thermoelectric Batteries, or geothermal power generation were technologies that ordinary people can understand and accept—fields the nation is researching. Even controlled nuclear fusion reactors, though not yet accomplished, are at least understood in principle, with success appearing achievable.

But genetic modification, this is something far too ahead and sensitive, though Chen Xin knows he has the capability to control it, others might not necessarily understand this point.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 420 - 418: Adjusting the Mindset
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After readjusting his emotions, Chen Xin also reorganized his thoughts and plans.

Since human genetic modification is still too premature right now, this plan can only be shelved temporarily until a more suitable time in the future.

However, while the human genetic modification plan has been put on hold, the modification and breeding of wolves can continue for now. Developing large-sized, high-cold-resistant wolves as a new type of livestock for the current environment is a promising area of research, and the country views it favorably.

If it weren’t for Chen Xin proposing the human genetic modification plan later, it’s believed that the modification and breeding of wolves alone would definitely receive strong support from the state.

Luckily, it’s not too bad now. The ethics review committee has already approved the experiment. Once the research facility is complete, relevant genetic samples and live wolf embryos will be delivered.

Completing this study isn’t difficult. After all, there are massive creatures like the North American Kenai Mountain wolves, which stand 1.2 meters tall and exceed two meters in length, as well as Arctic Wolves, which can withstand temperatures as low as minus fifty degrees. Moreover, since wolves and dogs are closely related, breeding large wolves with sufficient size, good endurance, and cold resistance should be fairly straightforward.

Of course, breeding using dogs as a base is also possible. However, generally speaking, dogs are smaller than wolves, with just a few large breeds being comparable in size. Thus, using wolves as the base makes it easier to breed the large creatures Chen Xin requires.

It’s just that breeding a truly suitable large creature isn’t simple. Besides genetic modification, it’s necessary to modify and adjust based on live samples bred and to explore suitable breeding methods.

After all, it’s a living animal, not a machine that can operate on pre-set programs.

Speaking of machines, Chen Xin couldn’t help but wonder if the Sos Mechanical Beast might be a good choice too?

Although it’s just a setting in some toys, Chen Xin particularly likes the Long-toothed Lion series and has even collected them.

However, after serious consideration, Chen Xin had to abandon this idea. While the Sos Mechanical Beasts appear to be mere steel machines similar to Gundams and Titans, they are, in fact, designed as bona fide metallic life forms.

Even if he only wanted to build a Mecha that resembles it, making it combat-ready would require very high standards in artificial intelligence and mechanical processing, which is beyond the current manufacturing capabilities of the country.

So, it’s better to first produce the relatively easier Titan, and then consider creating mechanical beasts and Gundams.

Moreover, in developing large cold-resistant wolves, there’s a very important issue that needs solving.

It’s well-known that mammalian reproduction requires a maternal body. Mammals undergo development within the maternal body to a certain stage before being born, unlike oviparous animals that develop within eggs.

So even if Chen Xin can acquire live embryos, to develop them into mature organisms, he will still need a maternal body for gestation, or use artificial breeding methods to mature the embryos.

Artificial breeding is evidently not easy. Even before the disaster, nations worldwide were researching it, but the artificial womb technology needed for breeding embryos hasn’t matured.

However, this is simple for Chen Xin. Manufacturing cultivation chambers isn’t difficult; the key issue remains the technology itself.

After all, Chen Xin only provides the equipment, not the technology. Therefore, specialist assistance is needed, which is precisely the main work and research topic of the biological laboratory after the research facility is completed.

In summary, after clarifying his thoughts, Chen Xin once again made a list of what he needs to do next.

The first priority is, of course, waiting for the completion of the Ecological Dome at the research facility and then conducting another overall upgrade connection between the research facility and his refuge.

Second is the cultivation of cold-resistant wolves, which is divided into sub-tasks like building cultivation chambers, adjusting wolf genetics, embryo development, individual training, and live reproduction. It’s a long-term endeavor.

The third task, manufacturing Titans, doesn’t differ in priority from the wolf breeding work. For this task, Chen Xin primarily focuses on making the prototype of a Titan, then assisting factories in technology cracking and reverse research to complete production line construction and begin producing this combat weapon.

As for manufacturing other beast-like machines and constructing Gundams, or even starting to cultivate genuine metal life forms like Sos Beasts, those are future projects that are not urgent now.

The fourth task, energy project research, is another important topic. Although controllable nuclear fusion, deep geothermal extraction, and thermoelectric isotope generation technologies exist, these still possess significant potential for enhancement.

Apart from these significant projects, Chen Xin is also considering the manufacture of individual protective armor.

While the Exoskeleton Protective Suit has already begun widespread use and the military has started deploying military Exoskeletons, which have considerable protective capabilities, Chen Xin still wants to create something different.

For example, the Power Armor that covers the entire body, though it was made, hasn’t been imitated or mass-produced. The military has been eagerly eyeing it for quite some time.

Besides the Power Armor itself, associated weapons and the vehicles equipped for Power Armor-wearing soldiers all require concurrent development.

Weapons can still make do with existing ones, but vehicles are challenging to adapt.

Since when soldiers wear Power Armor, both their total weight and body dimensions change considerably. For instance, a Z-20 helicopter can originally seat ten ordinarily equipped soldiers without weapon modules, but only eight with weapon racks.

If it’s carrying soldiers wearing Power Armor, even without weapons racks, it can only carry six soldiers and their equipment, and there isn’t enough space in the cabin for both them and their Power Armor.

As for armored personnel carriers, their internal space restricts even normally sized soldiers, let alone those in Power Armor.

Especially the Heavy Mecha provided by Chen Xin to the military, although not yet mass-produced, its size means it can’t be carried by any vehicle apart from trucks, and even helicopters can only transport it using sling methods.

Therefore, development of related auxiliary vehicles is necessary and is a key focus of the military’s attention.

Fortunately, in this regard, the military hasn’t relied solely on Chen Xin but has tasked other related research institutes with developing original vehicles. Current progress is reportedly satisfactory.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 421 - 419: New Equipment Design
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

After readjusting his state, Chen Xin no longer tangled with the matter of genetic modification and instead focused his energy on several more important tasks at hand.

However, most projects need to wait until the research base is completed before they can be launched. The only thing that can be done instead is military weapons research.

Research on Titans and large vehicles is restricted by the site, so Chen Xin did not proceed immediately, but planned to wait until the research base was completed and conditions were met to carry out research work. He now only intends to use the time before the research base is completed to produce a few weapons as part of the exoskeleton and power armor.

If it is just an exoskeleton, ordinary weapons can be used. Although the assistance brought by the exoskeleton is not very strong, it can at least allow soldiers to use heavier and higher-recoil firearms and weapons to exert stronger firepower on the battlefield.

Power armor is different. Without saying anything else, the trigger of ordinary firearms is designed according to the thickness of a normal person’s finger. It’s fine if you wear tactical gloves, but if you wear thick anti-freeze gloves, your fingers become about as thick as carrots, and they probably won’t fit into the trigger guard.

Therefore, the weapons equipped for power armor must at least enlarge the size of the trigger.

More critically, soldiers equipped with power armor have much greater strength and load capacity.

Under such circumstances, using ordinary firearms, they could even hold an automatic rifle and continuously shoot with one hand without needing to hold and press the gun to maintain ballistic stability, so naturally, they could be equipped with heavy weapons like the 12.7mm rotary machine gun originally meant to be used with vehicles.

However, the 12.7mm caliber is still a bit too powerful; aside from the main battle tank, normal vehicles, even armored cars, can hardly withstand the armor-piercing incendiary rounds of the 12.7mm caliber.

Chen Xin remembers that the Flame Country’s weapons research department tested the 12.7mm rotary machine gun using a small car. Not only was the car directly penetrated, but the entire vehicle was also set ablaze.

Thus, designing a weapon with moderate power, more suitable for soldiers equipped with power armor to use as an ordinary weapon, becomes an inevitability.

After all, they can’t always be carrying a heavy machine gun capable of tearing a normal person in half on a mission, right? That’s simply excessive firepower.

"If we were to design a weapon for power armor, Second Lieutenant Deng, what would you think it should look like?" Chen Xin asked Second Lieutenant Deng, holding a notepad, as Deng was currently testing the newly made power armor by Chen Xin.

This set of power armor was newly made by Chen Xin, using the prototype design diagram that Chen Xin had completed when creating the power armor for both him and Qin Lan.

Moreover, Chen Xin improved its power system by using the same small isotope thermoelectric battery as the exoskeleton power source, but the output power has been increased somewhat, and adjustments have been made to flexibility.

The whole set of power armor looks somewhat like medieval knight plate armor due to its lack of edges, making it appear rather sleek.

From the design structure, this set of power armor is actually exoskeleton packaged armor plate design. A set of exoskeleton was designed first, and then armor plates were installed on the exoskeleton frame.

Unlike the two sets belonging to Chen Xin and Qin Lan, their internal structure is the more complex power armor frame, not just exoskeleton packaging armor.

The reason Chen Xin designed this way is not because he is unwilling to provide the military with good items, but he needs to consider whether manufacturing costs and processes can support complex design structures.

Those who understand World War II know Hans Military Industry was famous for its precise design and high quality, especially their tanks. The Tiger, Panther, and King Tiger tanks were invincible on the battlefield, but accompanied by complex structures and numerous components, not only troublesome to repair but also very labor-intensive to manufacture.

Correspondingly, Suyia’s T34 tanks had simple, robust structures and were easy to repair. Even with the haphazard maintenance style of the Soviets, it could withstand the rigors. Manufacturing was also simpler, even having stories of T34s made by tractor factories during the Stalingrad defense that hadn’t been painted before being sent to the battlefield.
