────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 467 - 465: A Letter
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"Mr. Chen, Vice Mayor Zhao asked me to hand this over to you." In what had become Chen Xin’s office in the command center of the settlement site, a city government employee solemnly handed both a document and a letter to Chen Xin.

Chen Xin took the items from the staff member and, after a moment of surprise, asked, "What is this?"

"This contains the results of the public opinion survey on whether to initiate post-disaster reconstruction in the original city area, along with a letter from the settlement site’s citizens to the city government. Vice Mayor Zhao read it and asked me to pass it on to you. He said you should take a look." The staff member seemed to have already read the letter, with emotions mixed between sadness and excitement, but mostly a sense of pride.

"Alright, I will take a look," Chen Xin replied seriously, sensing something significant.

After the staff member excused himself and left, Chen Xin opened the document, setting the letter aside for the time being.

The results of the public opinion survey were, unsurprisingly to Chen Xin, beyond his expectations.

Among the surviving citizens, over ninety percent supported rebuilding the city, opting for reconstruction on its original site.

Citizens supporting the city’s reconstruction was, of course, anticipated by Chen Xin; after all, Flame Country has long held the notion that leaving one’s homeland is unfortunate. Although it may be relocated to safer places, it ultimately distances one from their home, turning them into rootless algae.

Whose heart would desire leaving their familiar surroundings unless absolutely no alternative existed?

The survey results were no surprise to Chen Xin; the only surprising aspect was that ninety percent supported rebuilding on the original site.

This indeed surpassed Chen Xin’s expectations.

Especially when below this entry, there was a special note handwritten, leaving Chen Xin deeply moved.

This handwritten sentence, while seemingly simple, felt profound and weighted as Chen Xin read it.

"All citizens of our city accept and understand the loss of victims’ bodies during the reconstruction."

At first glance, a short sentence, simple and clear without misinterpretation.

Yet the more straightforward, the heavier the weight behind this message.

This wasn’t about one or two bodies, nor the simple consent of one or two individuals, but the remains of ten thousand victims, combined with two hundred thousand survivors enduring the immense sorrow after losing loved ones, making an arduous and noble choice.

The choice they made meant forsaking their relatives’ remains, not only abandoning the interment of their loved ones’ bodies but also the preservation of their posthumous integrity.

In Flame Country, matters of life and death are taken very seriously, particularly whether the deceased can rest in peace, of great importance.

Flame Country’s traditional culture emphasizes posthumous affairs; treating the deceased as living, and ensuring the completeness and burial of their body are valued.

If the dead cannot be securely buried, or if the body is incomplete at interment, it signifies the deceased’s soul cannot rest, forever lingering in suffering.

This is heavily taboo in Flame Country’s traditional culture. Despite modern society discarding some old-fashioned superstitions, the mourning and respect for deceased loved ones remain crucial, ensuring their posthumous tranquility.

The inability to properly inter bodies, leaving them exposed in the wilderness, damaging the corpses—such acts are still greatly taboo in modern society, disrespecting both the deceased and the living.

Yet now, the twenty thousand survivors of this city have chosen a decision most painful to them.

Chen Xin dares not imagine the mindset of these survivors who lost their homes and family, weighing such a choice, along with the sacrifices represented by this decision.

Though living is more important than the deceased, under current circumstances, enabling more to survive is paramount.

But the deceased aren’t merely cold corpses.

Not long ago, they were vibrant lives—our most cherished family and friends, now passed, significantly impacting the survivors emotionally, a pain hard to fathom.

Not only can their remains not be interred, but we must rebuild our homes upon these ruins, leveling the burying heaps, leaving incomplete bodies unable to find peace in burial—emotionally and ethically, this is not easily acceptable.

Indeed, even if all opposed leveling the ruins, Chen Xin would find it understandable.

Yet now, these twenty thousand people’s decision lies before him, choosing their most painful option.

Chen Xin feels weighed down, biting his lip, setting down the poll results, pinching the bridge of his nose before picking up the accompanying letter.

Although unaware who authored it, nor its contents, Chen Xin believes it should hold answers he seeks.

With reverence, Chen Xin opens it to read.

The letter is written on a plain sheet, seemingly found at the moment, with smudges but not impairing readability.

The contents are brief, only spanning a few lines.

"The earthquake took our family and friends, their bodies buried beneath ruins unreachable, an indeed painful reality."

"Though difficult and agonizing, I willingly agree with the city government’s decision to clear the ruins for reconstruction."

"Though my family’s remains lie beneath, the living are more important than the deceased."

"Rebuilding homes allows more to survive."

"Flattening ruins, harming bodies, is undeniably hard to accept."

"My family, forever departed, fills me with sorrow and pain, but I cannot allow others’ families to depart because of me."

"If I were among the victims, if my body lay deep beneath ruins too, use my body as the cornerstone for a new home—my only contribution."

"A departed me has no hope; living people, carry my share into the future, in building new homes."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 468 - 466: The deceased are gone, the living choose hope
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

"A moment of silence for all!" In front of a ruin, led by the Deputy Mayor, Chen Xin, and the rescue team leader, everyone bowed their heads, mourning the victims beneath the rubble.

Following the previous public opinion survey results, the plan to clear the rubble and rebuild homes was ultimately approved.

However, despite this, when deciding on the construction plan, they still chose a method that would minimize damage to the bodies beneath the rubble.

Moreover, before the construction began, the city government specially arranged this moment of silence, hoping the deceased under the rubble could rest in peace.

The weather in the apocalypse could hardly be called good. Today’s wind was strong, snowflakes carried by the mad wind slapped against the body with crackling sounds, even causing some people to stand unsteadily.

But no one moved or sought shelter. Everyone had their heads bowed, sincerely praying for the victims under the ruins.

Not only here, but all the survivors at the settlement were doing the same thing.

Everyone paused their work, stood silently and solemnly, bowed their heads in mourning for the deceased.

Even the injured who could not stand were also praying for those unfortunate people.

In the sky, aboard the Chenglong, the captain personally sounded the whistle, sending out a long tone, resonating among the particularly quiet ruins, as if consoling the souls of the dead.

Strangely, after the moment of silence began, the originally fierce wind actually subsided.

As if even the heavens were unwilling for the boisterous wind to disturb the peace of the dead, ruining the solemnity of the silence.

The duration of silence was not long, yet the short three minutes felt extremely lengthy, causing many to shed tears thinking of their deceased loved ones.

Chen Xin also bowed his head, mourning the victims.

While praying for the dead, wishing them peace, Chen Xin pondered on what methods could prevent such tragedies from happening again.

The disaster itself is unavoidable. Aside from taking preventive measures and issuing early warnings, there’s actually not much people can do.

Perhaps, by relying on system upgrades, using certain elements from science fiction as a basis, one could eventually develop a device capable of controlling the earth’s crust movement, making disasters vanish without a trace, but that seems far too distant.

For Chen Xin now, a more practical approach is to improve existing construction techniques, enabling the rebuilt refuges and cities to withstand disaster assaults.

Thinking of this, Chen Xin couldn’t help but recall that the best in earthquake prevention is actually the island country to the east. After all, they experience more than a thousand earthquakes a year, averaging three times a day, even more frequent than three meals a day.

Although among these more than a thousand earthquakes, the vast majority are mild and imperceptible, there’s no denying that island country indeed accumulated substantial experience in this regard.

Perhaps I could learn from their architectural experiences and techniques?

While Chen Xin was pondering these thoughts, the moment of silence ended.

Though his mood remained heavy, the construction began according to plan.

Watching the engineering machinery start clearing the ruins still deeply entrapping victims’ bodies, finally, someone couldn’t help but break down and cry.

Along with this cry, it seemed that the stress and pain everyone endured during this period, as well as the sorrow of losing loved ones, were all released.

However, while releasing the anguish, this wail seemed also akin to a newborn’s first cry, heralding the rebirth of this city.

————————————————

Chen Xin did not stay at the site to watch the construction but returned to the settlement.

Though he hadn’t gone to the frontline of the disaster relief, nor participated in it due to others’ prevention, his identity gave him a more direct and genuine perception of this catastrophe.

For ordinary people, perhaps what they could feel was merely the passing of loved ones around them and the suffering they personally endured.

The death of 300,000 people in a city is a number that provides a visceral impression, and for the survivors, their most direct experience is the death of their own family members.

That alone is enough to occupy their entire mind, leaving them unable to contemplate aside from the demise of their loved ones and the destruction of their homes, the other damages this disaster brought.

However, for Chen Xin, the death of 300,000 people only represents the casualty number of this one city.

His status allowed him to learn from the nation what a dreadful disaster this earthquake, which affected the entire Southwest region, had caused.

Chen Xin clearly remembers that during his university years, a major earthquake in the Flame Country had caused nearly 20 million people to be displaced, nearly 70,000 people died, over 17,000 were missing, severely impacted ten counties, heavily impacted 41 counties, and generally impacted 186 counties. It was the most destructive, far-reaching, devastating, and hardest to rescue earthquake since the founding of the Flame Country.

Yet, this earthquake was even stronger than that one, and the death toll was several times more.

Upon receiving the statistics sent by various disaster relief teams, Chen Xin did not sleep all night. The shocking data made him feel as if he could see the faces of countless victims when he closed his eyes, and the cries of agony seemed to ring in his ears.

If this disaster had occurred before the meteorite fall, perhaps it wouldn’t have caused such massive casualties.

But since the meteorite fell, everyone was living in shelters, and few ventured outdoors; this resulted in most people staying inside shelters meant to protect them from disasters when disaster struck.

Those who survived were fortunate. They might have been lucky not to be inside a shelter when the disaster struck, or they were in shelters that didn’t collapse, or they managed to escape in time.

No matter the reason, they survived, and compared to the unfortunate victims, they were fortunate enough.

However, if this sliver of luck is to be compared with the misfortunes of the victims, no one can find joy in it.

This caused a heavy atmosphere in the settlement during this period, with the oppressive figure of 300,000 dead weighing on everyone, suffocating them.

Yet now, the atmosphere in the settlement is not as heavy as in previous days; it seems that the earlier moment of silence also allowed the sorrow in everyone’s hearts to be released, imbuing the settlement with a sense of hope.

Feeling this change, Chen Xin’s mood also lightened somewhat.

Indeed, as the letter he received stated, the deceased are gone, and the living must carry on.

Dwelling in past sorrow serves no purpose; only by embracing hope can one begin a new life.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 469 - 467 Su-style Brainstorm
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The new shelters can no longer be built underground, and since there’s no need for air defense anymore, the plan to dig deeper has naturally been abandoned.

Nowadays, the new shelters adopt the same dome design as the research base of Chen Xin. After all, it’s rare for meteors to fall anymore, and the glass dome poses no safety risks.

Moreover, the new shelters with this architectural style are more comfortable to live in compared to the traditional rib-like structures, and they also allow for a larger internal space.

Apart from the shelters themselves, related facilities such as greenhouse farms, factories, power plants, and water plants are also under construction.

In a short time, although the entire city is still in ruins, it has nevertheless regained its vitality.

Chen Xin originally wanted to stay to help with disaster reconstruction. After all, with the system at his disposal, Chen Xin could greatly accelerate the speed of reconstruction in many areas.

However, this idea of Chen Xin fell through.

As the railway line was urgently repaired and opened, a nuclear train entered the disaster area. While it was responsible for relocating the evacuees to other places, it also brought orders for Chen Xin to return.

"What’s going on? Why am I being called back so suddenly?" On the train, Chen Xin asked an officer who came to deliver the orders.

This also puzzled Chen Xin. Even if the higher-ups had some requests, they could send a government official or contact him through communication devices. Why specifically send an officer to deliver the message and particularly arrange for him to return by train?

"This involves national secrets." The officer glanced at Qin Lan, who was sitting beside Chen Xin, and did not explain the reason.

Qin Lan was returning with Chen Xin. Her Titan was now also loaded onto the train to be transported back together, so naturally, she accompanied Chen Xin.

However, after hearing the officer say this, Qin Lan immediately stood up proactively, said to Chen Xin, "You two have a talk," and then walked out voluntarily.

After Qin Lan left, the officer placed the briefcase that had been handcuffed to his wrist in front of Chen Xin, opened it, and took out a document to hand to Chen Xin.

"This was brought back by comrades who went to aid Lucia." The officer’s expression was very serious, and after handing the document to Chen Xin, he finally spoke: "The country has urgently organized experts to analyze this, but there are almost no clues, so the leadership thought of you, Academician Chen, hoping you could help with this matter."

Chen Xin listened to the officer’s seriousness, took the document, and began to read it carefully. But just after turning the first page, he was surprised, looked up at the officer: "Are you joking with me?"

"Certainly not." The officer still maintained a serious and earnest expression, nodding affirmatively: "When we first got the information, we also thought it was a colossal joke, but Lucia repeatedly assured the authenticity of the data.

Moreover, the comrades who went there also saw the original documents; they are indeed old materials from the Suyia era. Unless they started faking it during the Suyia era, the authenticity of the data shouldn’t be doubted."

"This is simply the most absurd thing I have ever heard!" Chen Xin threw the documents onto the table, with disbelief written all over his face.

But after rubbing his nose, he picked up the documents again and started leafing through them carefully, page by page.

Chen Xin read slowly, and the officer did not rush him, waiting patiently until he finished and even skimmed through again, only then did he ask: "Academician Chen, what do you think about this matter?"

"What can I think?" Chen Xin gave a wry smile, somewhat amused yet helpless: "Unless I can see it on site, with just this document alone, I’ll just think you’re joking, or it’s a setting for some science fiction game."

After saying that, Chen Xin flipped through the documents on the table and revealed the picture that made him both laugh and cry.

It was a scanned copy of a somewhat blurry black-and-white photo, but what was captured in the photo was relatively clear.

Yet it was precisely this clarity that made Chen Xin think the possibility of this thing actually existing was quite low.

But the officer’s serious demeanor and his mentioning of Lucia’s guarantees made Chen Xin somewhat skeptical yet curious about its existence.

Mentioning Suyia, everyone would remember the era when it competed with the Federation, and the most representative aspect was that series of mind-boggling Soviet-era tech marvels.

Whether it was the Caspian Monster Ground Effect Vehicle, the Mig-105 Space Fighter, the DON-2N Radar Array, the SVL High-speed Train, or the K7 Heavy Bomber...

or the seemingly inconspicuous but humiliating to the Federation Truman Bus Card, all were glories created by that former red giant bear.

After the disintegration of Suyia, many former secret documents leaked out, mentioning several brainstorming technology projects conceived by former Suyia’s high-level leadership during the Cold War with the Federation.

These projects were either whimsical or too avant-garde, though abandoned for various reasons, some were actually feasible, merely shelved due to high costs or lack of practicality.

For instance, the ball-shaped tank, LPL Flying Submarine, 1K17 Laser Tank all had considerable practical value and were actually feasible, but abandoned for various reasons. When the Flame Country sent people to Lucia this time, these precious materials were obtained.

But amidst these documents were also things like the one placed in front of Chen Xin, appearing overly fanciful and unrealistic at first glance.

For example, passenger-carrying rockets, gigantic hydrofoil cruise ships meant to cross the Atlantic Ocean...

And the thing exactly placed right in front of Chen Xin.

Chen Xin scratched his forehead and asked the officer: "Where is this thing now? Did they really build it?"

"The thing is in Siberia, back then Suyia built two prototypes, and conducted several experiments on them in Siberia, after the final designing they built one finished product, and all three are currently in the experimental base built by Suyia in Siberia." The officer explained to Chen Xin, though these details were already in the document, since Chen Xin asked, he had to answer.

Chen Xin nodded, then asked the officer: "So the real mission of this train, besides transferring evacuees, is to take me to Siberia to check on this thing?"

"Yes, we will first transfer the evacuees to nearby cities for settlement, then follow the railway northward to send you to Siberia." The officer nodded affirmatively.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 470 - 468 Nuclear-Powered Train (End of the Month, Request for Monthly Tickets!)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The wheels rolled forward, colliding with the steel tracks, making a clattering sound.

Sitting by the window, Chen Xin watched the scenery flying past outside, with the document in front of him still feeling like a cruel joke.

But now he had come to believe in the authenticity of the document and that the Suyians, during the Cold War era, indeed had the wild imagination to create such a thing.

However, even though he believed in its existence, Chen Xin still found it somewhat hard to accept, as it was simply too astonishing.

Although Chen Xin had considered creating something similar, he felt his confidence in being able to make it was because he had a system upgrade as a trump card, daring him to claim he could build it.

But back then, the Suyians had nothing like that; they relied entirely on their robust industrial strength and engineers’ wildly imaginative minds to create this, a truly vast difference between the two.

Taking a sip of the now somewhat cold water from the table, Chen Xin decided not to think about it anymore for now and planned to head to the dining car to get something to eat and change his mood.

This train was among the nuclear-powered trains that officially went into service after the nuclear-powered locomotive was deployed.

Different from the experimental nuclear-powered train Chen Xin had seen before, this train, although it also towed 27 carriages, did not have as many flashy functions.

Among the seven carriages, apart from one carriage designated for Chen Xin’s exclusive living use as a short carriage, 20 carriages are long sleeper carriages used to carry disaster victims, and the remaining six carriages are functional.

One purifying carriage is responsible for providing drinking water for the entire train, mainly sourced from the purified snow along the way.

One dining car provides catering services for the entire train, which is no different from the dining car Chen Xin had seen before; it’s the same model product.

Two storage carriages carry food supplies. Due to the mission’s nature, a large amount of food needs to be provided, so the train carries shelter food with no fresh food.

However, the substantial shelter food is already good enough for the disaster victims on the train.

As for the remaining two carriages, one is a freight carriage now hauling Qin Lan’s Titan and some other goods carried with the train, while the last carriage is a laboratory carriage specially prepared for the upcoming northbound mission.

The train’s configuration is quite interesting, with the foremost, naturally, being the most important nuclear-powered locomotive, followed by the carriage Chen Xin is in, then the scientific experiment carriage, freight carriage, purifying carriage, dining car, two storage carriages, and then the 20 long sleeper carriages.

According to the officer who came to pick up Chen Xin, this train temporarily undertakes the transfer of disaster victims. If an emergency occurs, they will abandon the 20 long sleeper carriages carrying the victims and advance lightly.

Upon learning that other locomotives will handle the disaster victims, Chen Xin saw nothing wrong with this arrangement.

Passing through each carriage to the dining car, since it was not mealtime, it seemed a bit deserted here.

This dining car is very different from those on past passenger trains because it’s not a passenger train and has to provide food for the entire train; thus, the carriage lacks tables and seats.

The entire dining car is a large kitchen, with integrated stove equipment made entirely of stainless steel, managed by five chefs who can simultaneously cook or heat large amounts of food to serve the whole train.

"Academician Chen, do you need anything?" Seeing Chen Xin walk into the dining car, the two duty chefs hurriedly greeted him, inquiring if he had any requests.

Being staff members, they didn’t know the train’s subsequent northbound mission, but the food supplies specifically prepared for Chen Xin, different from others, and his dedicated carriage were enough to make the staff aware of Chen Xin’s importance.

"Looking at documents has made me a bit hungry; is there anything to eat right now?" Chen Xin did not stand on ceremony, expressing his need to the two duty chefs.

These duty chefs were originally there in case staff missed mealtime and wanted something to eat, and even more so as Chen Xin enjoyed special treatment on the train.

So after Chen Xin spoke, the two chefs nodded quickly while busily preparing food for him.

Since it was pre-prepared for possible staff needs and Chen Xin also had special provisions, the food he wanted was ready quickly.

Taking the food prepared by the chefs, Chen Xin carried it back toward his carriage.

"Academician!" However, as Chen Xin passed through the laboratory carriage, he encountered an officer delivering orders, seemingly inspecting the equipment inside.

This piqued Chen Xin’s curiosity since the officer was always in uniform and did not seem to be a researcher.

However, Chen Xin did not stop to converse with him, merely nodded, and then continued through the laboratory carriage back to his own carriage.

Though called a short carriage, Chen Xin’s carriage is indeed relative to other standard length carriages and the 20 long carriages behind.

Historically, the Flame Country’s standard sleeper carriage had a hard sleeper capacity of 60 people, soft sleeper 40 people, with 11 compartments per carriage equipped with toilets and showers, running between 24.4 to 26.6 meters in length.

Now, the long carriages have been lengthened, with a hard sleeper carriage accommodating 90 people, the carriage length reaching 35 meters.

While termed a short carriage, Chen Xin’s carriage is actually 18 meters long, containing bathrooms, showers, a separate kitchen, and designed with one large and two small rooms plus a living room that can function as a conference room, with a communications room equipped accordingly.

The two small rooms in the carriage are designed to serve as guest rooms or for the use of security for important passengers, while the large room even has an independent bathroom, which was slightly luxurious in the past, but now, it’s considered quite extravagant.

In case of necessity, the train can discard all the carriages behind, carrying only this carriage forward at maximum speed, ensuring the safety of important passengers aboard.

Returning to his carriage, Chen Xin sat in the living room, ready to enjoy his meal, but as he ate, Qin Lan emerged from her room.

"Why not sleep a bit longer? It’s still early," Chen Xin glanced at his watch and asked Qin Lan.

Qin Lan poured herself a cup of hot water, drank half of it in one go, then said, "I couldn’t sleep, and there’s something I want to discuss with you."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 471 - 469: Setting Off North (End of Month Request for Monthly Votes!)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Qin Lan seemed to have something very important to tell Chen Xin, but she was interrupted just as she was about to speak, so she didn’t get a chance to say it.

Chen Xin intended to find another time to ask her, but the train soon arrived at a station where the 20 long cars behind were detached, and they, along with the disaster victims on board, were to be taken by other engines to resettlement places.

The nuclear-powered train where Chen Xin was located was also reorganized, with four more carriages added, topped with supplies, and headed directly north.

Originally, Qin Lan was supposed to go north with Chen Xin, but for some reason she didn’t, and instead, she and her Titan were unloaded with the freight carriage to return to the research base.

This puzzled Chen Xin, but at Qin Lan’s insistence, Chen Xin agreed to her request to go back.

After all, Qin Lan’s reason was quite normal: her Titan had some malfunctions during the previous disaster relief work and needed to be sent back for repairs.

Without her Titan, following Chen Xin north wouldn’t be of much use, so there was no need for her to go to Siberia.

So, after the train was reorganized, Chen Xin continued north, while Qin Lan and her Titan were sent back to the research base.

As for the newly organized four carriages, one was an armed carriage, one was a maintenance carriage, and two were troop transport carriages.

The reason for the troop transport carriages was that they were heading to Siberia.

That place is a famously cold and harsh land, where even before the disaster, most of it was still primal forest and wilderness.

And throughout Lucia’s history, there’s always been a tradition of exiling criminals to that land.

From the Decembrists to old nobility, purged criminals, prisoners of war from the Patriotic War, though most of that land is wilderness, it also houses many poachers, criminals, local indigenous people, and wild animals.

Additionally, during the Suyia era, many idealistic people came to this land wanting to build their homeland and develop this vast and fertile land.

But with the disintegration of Suyia, Lucia could no longer continue the development of Siberia, and these idealists either left or drowned in their former ideals, struggling on this land until they lost hope and chose death.

In Chen Xin’s memory, he remembered reading a report that there was a small city in Siberia originally built during the Suyia era development of Siberia, and back then it was prosperous and beautiful, though the environment was harsh, people were full of hope.

However, with the disintegration of Suyia, it gradually became desolate, and people living there gradually lost hope, spending their days accompanied by vodka, living decadent lives, numbing themselves with alcohol.

It was said that people there would drink until they were completely drunk every day, then not return home, but find a cold place to sleep on the snow.

If they didn’t wake up the next day, they were lucky to have frozen to death in their sleep, not having to continue living a hopeless life.

If unlucky and they woke up again, they’d continue getting drunk and find a colder place to repeat the process.

Besides, it was once an important weapon testing ground during the Suyia era, and no one knows how many things Suyia hid on that vast land back then, even current-day Lucia doesn’t know.

They only know based on their information that Suyia once tested a large number of weapons and verification technologies on that land, built so many secret bases that they themselves couldn’t count, and left a vast number of military equipment.

Heading to such a place without enough manpower and weapons wouldn’t work.

Even now, Siberia is colder and less suitable for human survival than ever.

As for bringing engineering maintenance carriages, it’s to prepare for the things mentioned in the received document.

After all, that stuff was built before the disintegration of Suyia, and even if Suyia’s technology was reliable, it’s been over thirty years since Suyia’s disintegration; no matter how well-preserved, it still needs inspection and repairs to possibly get running again.

Although for Chen Xin, with just a system upgrade, he could completely renew those things.

The train headed north all the way, the speed not particularly fast, after all, it was extreme cold, and there was snow and ice on the tracks; going too fast, even a train, risked derailing.

This also made the entire journey somewhat long.

According to the information he got from asking the train crew, it would take four to five days within the Flame Country, and another four to five days after leaving the country.

Considering potential issues along the way, and the fact that the former capital, Imperial Capital, had been flooded by seawater, and the original route was no longer viable, requiring a detour through Jiang Province to leave the country, entering Lucia through Kazakhstan, this trip might ultimately take two weeks to reach its destination.

Chen Xin wasn’t too concerned, after all, there was food and drink on the train, the carriage prepared especially for him was very comfortable, and there were others on the train too, so it wasn’t boring.

As for choosing other ways to get to Siberia, it was almost impossible, even before the disaster, the main way to get to Siberia was by rail.

Moreover, they were going to a Suyia-era secret military base, and besides a secret railway also built during the Suyia era, there was only a simple military airport, long abandoned and unusable.

Taking a train there was the only feasible option at present.

If it were before the disaster, this route’s scenery would be considered quite good.

From the Southwest of Flame Country to the Northwest, then entering Central Asia’s Kazakhstan, finally arriving in Lucia’s Siberia, the landscape along the route would change with the rising latitude, offering excellent natural scenery to admire.

But now, outside the train window, it was pitch black, with only a little illumination from the train window’s light on the snow, revealing nothing else.

This made the journey somewhat dull indeed.

However, compared to being stuck in a bunker underground, being able to travel by train crossing the Ancient Mountains and Rivers was still a very meaningful thing.

Even if there wasn’t much scenery to see along the way, guessing their location and imagining what their surroundings looked like as the train moved along was still an interesting pastime, enough to while away the spare time during the journey.

Moreover, everyone on board had a lot of work to do along the way, so it wasn’t really boring or dull.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 472 - 470: Land Cruiser (End of the month, ask for monthly ticket!)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

When it comes to cannons, many people are quite familiar with them. These heavy attack weapons, hailed as the gods of war, have a long history. From the moment they were created, they were destined to dominate the main theme of warfare.

The earliest cannons can even be traced back to over two thousand five hundred years ago during the Spring and Autumn period of Flame Country, when a military weapon called "礮" emerged. It is the ancestor of the various types of artillery that exist today.

However, these early so-called cannons were largely used to launch stone shots or create incendiary bombs made from materials like pine wood. Rather than being true cannons, they were more like giant stone throwers.

With the progression of history and the invention of gunpowder, true cannons began to take shape during the Song Dynasty. By the Yuan Dynasty, the true cannons — smoothbore cannons — were invented, laying the foundation for all the artillery used throughout human history thereafter.

The emergence of cannons, such powerful weapons of attack, naturally garnered the love of a plethora of military strategists throughout history.

Anyone who wishes to achieve victory cannot refuse this ultimate solution to winning.

With the Urban Giant Cannon, the Turks ended the thousand-year glory of the Roman Empire. Since then, there has been no fortress that wouldn’t fall.

By concentrating the use of artillery, Napoleon conquered Europe, and from then on, no famous battle in human history has lacked artillery.

In fact, conversely, since the mass utilization of cannons, is there any famous battle without the presence of artillery?

As cannon barrels were made thicker, increasing their power, their weight also became heavier, naturally limiting their mobility and use.

Because of this, the Urban Giant Cannon remained the largest caliber cannon ever made by humans for quite a long time.

Until modern times, with the development of industry and technology, super giant cannons with calibers reaching astounding levels appeared, and the method of transporting them no longer relied on manpower or animal power but on trains.

This led to the creation of the strongest artillery ever used in warfare: train cannons.

These super giant guns that rely on railway movement are undoubtedly the largest caliber weapons used in warfare by mankind. The Paris gun used by Germany to bombard France during World War I, the Gustav Giant Cannon, and the Dora Cannon during World War II, and the mass-produced K5(E) Type Train Cannon, etc...

Though super giant cannons are extremely troublesome whether in terms of moving or setting up, in wars, once these cannons fire, their power is unparalleled.

The Gustav Giant Cannon once penetrated 10 meters of reinforced concrete fortifications with a single shot, leaving a crater as large as four football fields. In war, nothing can withstand its bombardment.

However, such giant cannons still moved too slowly and were too cumbersome, while the mass-produced train cannons lacked sufficient caliber.

Is there a cannon that can meet the mobility requirements on the battlefield and guarantee the caliber of artillery?

The answer is yes, and someone has made it; it resulted in what Chen Xin received in the document, the ultimate land warfare weapon created during the Suyia era — the land cruiser.

Honestly, when Chen Xin first saw this thing, he seriously thought it was something fabricated by a science fiction novelist, as it was just too outrageous.

After World War II, the Suyia acquired a large amount of German technical data, which naturally included the train cannons that had shone brightly in World War II, and the technical data of the Gustav heavy cannon that hadn’t been completely destroyed.

At that time, the Suyia didn’t pay much attention to this and merely sent some weapon experts to research and attempt to replicate their own train cannons.

But with the post-war confrontation with the Federation and the escalation of the Cold War, the Suyia felt the need for a ground weapon that could resist the Federation’s invasion from the East Coast in Siberia. Train cannons naturally became a focal point of consideration by the Suyia leadership.

After all, between the easternmost point of Suyia and the westernmost point of the Federation lies only the Bering Strait, which is merely 35 kilometers wide at its narrowest point.

The Suyia could completely deploy super large cannons here to directly bombard the Federation mainland.

While such actions may not be very meaningful, if the Federation plans to cross the Bering Strait to attack the Suyia by land, it’s still necessary.

However, Siberia is desolate, and constructing railways for deploying train cannons is quite troubling.

So when the Suyia’s weapon experts designed a giant cannon with a 600mm caliber supported by two rails, the plan was rejected.

Firstly, the mobility was still insufficient, as missiles and rockets had begun entering service, making train cannons seem less attractive and useful.

Secondly, the Suyia leadership felt that its caliber and power were still not enough, especially with the Suyia having developed nuclear technology by then, train cannons no longer held charm.

Thus, the Suyia’s weapon experts underwent a transformation and developed a second improved version.

They replaced the train wheels under the cannon trailer with tracks and created a super large chassis with sixteen tracks, an 800mm caliber, and a range of over 70KM super cannon equipped with specially designed nuclear shells.

This time, the Suyia leadership was satisfied and ordered a prototype to be made and test-fired in the Siberian weapons testing range.

However, this thing never saw real practical use, as it was still too heavy.

Even with sixteen tracks, allowing it to walk on land with difficulty, in Siberia it could only move normally after the ground frozen in winter, and the snow would severely hinder its advancement.

When the temperature rose and ice melted, the muddy frozen soil could cause it to sink entirely into the mud.

The key issue is that the thing is powered by steam turbines, requiring huge fuel consumption to start moving.

So after test-firing, the Suyia leadership proposed new demands for the weapon experts, hoping that it could be made more practical, smaller, with less power, yet still practical and mass-producible.

This time the request was reasonable, so the Suyia’s weapon experts somewhat restrained their imagination and thus created a land cruiser that could be put into practical use, completing two prototype vehicles and one follow-up improved model.

And this is what Chen Xin got from the document, a 110 meters long, weighing 307 tons, equipped with twin-mounted 305mm caliber main cannons, using a small nuclear reactor as its power source "super tank".

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 473 - 471: The True Purpose (Seeking Monthly Tickets at Month-End)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The train continues to move forward without stopping.

As a nuclear-powered train, although not as exaggerated as the perpetual motion machine used by the Snow Country Train in novels, which can run indefinitely, it can still operate for a long time without needing to stop for fuel replenishment, as long as supplies are sufficient.

Nevertheless, when the train entered the capital city of Jiang Province, it stopped at the station for the last resupply before leaving Flame Country.

"After we cross the border, we’ll have to wait until we reach Lucia for supplies. Although Kazakhstan allows us passage, they can’t provide us with supplies," the officer explained the itinerary to Chen Xin in the lounge car.

"Can the railways in Kazakhstan and Lucia still be used?" Chen Xin wasn’t particularly concerned about the travel plan; he was more focused on whether the railways could still be used.

Unlike Flame Country, where efforts have been made to maintain the railway lines for domestic material transport, ensuring smooth operations, Kazakhstan and Lucia’s railway maintenance is uncertain, especially considering the current self-preservation difficulties and lack of international communication.

However, the officer was not worried about this, saying to Chen Xin, "You can rest assured, Director Chen. We brought engineering vehicles that can perform simple repairs on the railway, and if necessary, lay tracks ourselves, so the itinerary won’t be affected."

"Is that so? Then I’m relieved." Chen Xin nodded upon hearing the officer’s assurance, seeming genuinely reassured.

Even though such matters weren’t something Chen Xin needed to worry about, as he could convert the train to tracks or repair the original sections, or lay new railway tracks if necessary.

Still, there was one issue that made Chen Xin uneasy, and he turned to the officer, asking, "I recall Lucia’s railway gauge differs from ours, so we’ll have to switch tracks, right?"

A change is inevitable since, strategically, Lucia uses broad gauge, unlike Flame Country and other countries that use the international standard gauge.

"Yes, after entering Lucia," the officer nodded affirmatively but showed a somewhat helpless expression, saying, "Lucia has indicated that we can use their stations and train wheels, but these require us to operate them ourselves, as they have no personnel there anymore."

Hearing this, Chen Xin sighed and said helplessly, "Their stations are probably completely buried in snow, right? How are we supposed to use them? Do we have to spend months digging them out first?"

With the surging power of the nuclear engine, the snow on the tracks isn’t a concern, and can be cleared using snow removers.

However, a completely snow-buried station, like the one Chen Xin dealt with in Lin City, is not something that can be excavated by manpower.

If the track change after entering Lucia requires clearing their stations first, it becomes exceedingly difficult.

"Yes, we are also troubled by this issue. Although Lucia indicated they stored wheels in warehouses and provided a station map prior to the disaster, suggesting we only need to clear the warehouse and crane at the platform, it’s still very troublesome," the officer’s serious face also showed concern.

Changing tracks is necessary; the different gauge could easily cause derailment, especially in snowy weather, posing a risk of the whole train turning over.

But Chen Xin wasn’t as worried as the officer about this issue. If faced with such a situation, he could completely upgrade the train and change the gauge directly, which wouldn’t take him many survival points.

So Chen Xin replied, "Don’t worry about it. If changing tracks requires clearing the train station, I can use the engineering vehicles to directly modify the wheel gauge—it won’t be a big problem."

"Directly change the wheel gauge using engineering vehicles? Is this troublesome?" The officer didn’t doubt Chen Xin’s capability but was concerned about the complexity of the task.

Chen Xin simply shrugged nonchalantly and laughed, "It certainly wouldn’t be more troublesome than digging out the whole station."

"Indeed, then we’ll leave it to you, Director Chen," the officer didn’t dwell further on the matter, as the engineering vehicles were brought along for such contingencies.

After setting aside this issue, the officer glanced at the documents in front of Chen Xin and curiously asked, "Director Chen, do you have any thoughts on this matter?"

"Thoughts? My only thought is why the country wants this thing," Chen Xin glanced at the monstrous dual-mounted 280 depicted in the document and shook his head helplessly, "If this thing existed during World War II, it would certainly be a powerful weapon.

But it’s the 21st century now; aren’t missiles and rockets more appealing than 280 caliber gun barrels, which even the navy no longer uses? What’s the use of bringing this thing back? The chassis might be dismantled and repurposed, but the other parts are hardly useful."

"Well..." The officer’s expression was awkward as he subtly picked up the document and flipped to the last few pages before asking Chen Xin, "Didn’t you see this part, Director Chen?"

Seeing the pages the officer flipped to, Chen Xin raised an eyebrow indifferently and said, "Removing the gun barrels and converting the thing into an all-terrain vehicle? I guess that’s not a bad idea, but did the person proposing this idea consider the cost?"

"Cost issues?" The officer was momentarily taken aback, not having considered that aspect.

"It’s a Cold War-era relic, sitting idle for so many years; there’s still uncertainty about its usability, requiring thorough inspections and component replacements, especially since it uses nuclear power, necessitating comprehensive checks," Chen Xin pointed at the document, earnestly explaining to the officer, "Even setting aside these concerns and assuming Suyia’s technology is impressive enough for it to still be usable, it’s, after all, a half-century-old artifact.

Even if it can function, many components need upgrading and replacing. Rather than repairing and refurbishing the antique, I’d rather get data from Lucia, then build a new one from scratch.

Moreover, I don’t understand the purpose of converting it into an all-terrain vehicle. Even if the military needed something like that, they wouldn’t require such a large object. So what is the true intention behind this decision? Or is there something else I’m unaware of?"

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 474 - 472 Energy Crystal (End of the month, asking for monthly votes)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The train cannon might have indeed been a very powerful battlefield support weapon during World War II, but after nearly a century of technological advancements post-war, the technology mastered by humans has undergone a tremendous transformation.

A mere train cannon, in front of the missiles, rockets, bombers, and even more sci-fi space-based weapons equipped by modern armies, is practically like a large target.

In terms of military equipment owned by modern armies, dismantling a train cannon, which could still be considered a sharp tool during World War II, only requires two armed helicopters.

So although the Suyia land cruiser seems mighty and terrifying, in reality, this thing is already a historical relic.

In fact, after its actual construction, the Suyia leadership realized that it wasn’t practical, and soon abandoned the idea of mass production.

Otherwise, considering the craze of Suyia and the Federation during the Cold War, the Suyians had no reason not to equip such a giant weapon carrying two 305-caliber barrels extensively after its successful development.

As for whether the Suyians could produce so many, envisioning their tank production plans which were measured in thousands, it wouldn’t have been a problem for Suyia’s industrial capability to spare a bit of production to fabricate a hundred or so of such land cruisers.

But the Suyians did not choose to mass-produce the land cruisers; they only built two experimental units and one production model and then sealed them away in a secret military base in Siberia, never to be used again.

Investing tremendous resources and time, the end products were built, yet directly sealed away—it is indeed a wasteful approach.

However, during the Cold War era, this was not unimaginable: pouring vast resources into technical research only to find the results already obsolete was common in military research.

Especially after World War II, the rapid development of military technology led to many military pieces of equipment becoming outdated right after they were finalized.

Some could still be used reluctantly, while others could only have their plans discarded and archived.

Furthermore, the Suyians had not ruled out the use of these three land cruisers; during the most tense relations between Flame Country and Suyia, when border conflicts even erupted, the Suyians considered deploying these three beasts, which had just been sealed, into the border conflict.

Due to the long distance, the three land cruisers couldn’t be transported by train and would have had to rely on their own power to reach the conflict area, which would take too long.

Moreover, it was only a border conflict at the time; although both sides engaged in fighting, they restrained themselves from escalating into a full-scale conflict. While the Suyians indeed contemplated using the weapon, they eventually abandoned the idea.

Thus, this matter became a forgotten file, left undiscovered for half a century.

Until this time, when Lucia, seeking assistance from Flame Country, revealed all their hidden reserves, they found records of this event in dusty Suyia file cabinets.

This also informed Flame Country that during that border conflict, the Suyians had planned to use such a massive weapon.

Fortunately, it’s all in the past now; Suyia disintegrated over thirty years ago, so revisiting this matter doesn’t signify much, aside from sparking Flame Country’s interest in these three land cruisers.

As for why these three land cruisers should be brought back, it’s indeed not as simple as modifying them into all-terrain vehicles, as stated in the documents.

Although the leadership does plan to convert the three land cruisers into all-terrain vehicles, there are deeper intentions involved.

The power source for these three land cruisers is nuclear reactors, which, given Suyia’s nuclear technology at the time, was feasible since they even developed mobile nuclear power stations back then, only later abandoned due to the Che City incident.

However, through declassified top-secret documents, Chen Xin learned that the Suyians only used nuclear reactors on the second experimental machine and the production model; regarding the power source of the first prototype, the Suyians didn’t employ nuclear reactors.

Instead, they used an Energy Crystal accidentally acquired by the Suyians, which is the true objective of Chen Xin and his team’s journey to Siberia this time.

If they can retrieve all three land cruisers, they will, but if they can’t, they will map them and bring the blueprints back to make new ones themselves. But the Energy Crystal used in the prototype is what truly interests the leadership and what they want to acquire.

It’s precisely because of this that the leadership dispatched this team to Siberia, aiming to retrieve this Energy Crystal, comparably powerful to a nuclear reactor.

According to another piece of top-secret documentation gifted by Lucia, the Energy Crystal is only fist-sized yet can stably output energy equivalent to a small nuclear reactor of that era.

If not for the fact that Suyia’s scientists couldn’t replicate it by any means, unable to even understand its principle and material structure, they wouldn’t have applied it to the land cruiser, essentially wasting it.

This also piques Chen Xin’s interest, because in the Suyian research materials, it is recorded that although this crystal continuously releases energy, its mass remains unchanged. Even with the most precise instruments and long-term observation, the Suyians noticed no change in its mass.

This is highly anomalous because, during the material-to-energy conversion process, the greater the released energy, the more the material’s mass decreases. With continuous energy release, theoretically, its mass should be continuously reducing.

However, this crystal, after being studied for a considerable period by the Suyians, continuously released energy without any mass reduction, evidently defying the laws of physics.

This scenario suggests two possibilities: either the crystal absorbs new material during its energy release to replenish itself, forming a cycle; or it is an extremely high-density energy crystal that releases energy at a "low-power" mode. Although energy is released, the mass reduction is too trivial for Suyia’s observational capabilities at the time to detect.

As for the actual situation, Chen Xin cannot determine it now. Only after obtaining the object can he assess what it truly is.

It is also because of the emergence of this object that Chen Xin finally finds interest in this journey.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 475 - 473 Arriving at the Destination (Last Half Hour Asking for Votes)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Having traversed the desolate border between Kazakhstan and Lucia, even before the disaster, Chen Xin and his team arrived at the train station on the Lucia border as planned.

However, as they had anticipated, they encountered the issue of switching tracks. Moreover, the so-called station had long been buried under snow. To complete the track switch, they not only had to clear the snow from two different gauge tracks but also dig out the crane used for switching, the warehouse with broad-gauge wheels, and the platform from under the snow.

Fortunately, for Chen Xin, this was not a problem. He instructed the soldiers accompanying him to help clear the two tracks and, after finding the broad-gauge wheels, had them temporarily set up a gate between the two tracks with an engineering vehicle.

Then Chen Xin had their train pass through the gate onto the broad-gauge track and upgraded their train with the broad-gauge wheels found by the soldiers.

With Chen Xin’s command to upgrade, the distance between the wheels of the train was widened to the appropriate gauge, quickly solving the track-switching problem without needing the soldiers to dig out the crane and spend hours hoisting the carriage onto the broad-gauge track.

After completing the track switch, they moved forward again.

However, the roads in Lucia after crossing the border were not as smooth as in Flame Country or Kazakhstan.

The entire Siberia was covered with thick snow, and combined with the damage caused by meteor impacts and post-disaster neglect, many tracks were damaged. Chen Xin and his team often had to repair sections as they went, expending considerable effort to traverse the endless snowfields and reach their destination.

This place lies deep within the Siberian forest, a typical no-man’s-land. Before the disaster, apart from a secret military railway leading to a secret military base deep within the forest, no other roads led here.

This was meant to prevent accidental intrusions and the discovery of Suyia’s secrets hidden here.

Before sealing this place, the Suyians built a small town to serve the military base’s soldiers and scientists. The town included a station, school, hospital, and a full range of facilities to ensure the soldiers and scientists living here could meet all their living needs.

However, with the military base sealed, the soldiers, scientists, and staff who once lived here have all been evacuated, leaving the town and the sealed military base together in the depths of this Siberian forest.

If it was before the disaster, the scenery here would have been quite beautiful.

With various town buildings distinctively in the Suyian style, the surrounding lush primal forest covered the rolling hills, and there was a beautiful small lake nearby.

Living here during the Cold War era, without the threat of war or the noise of big cities, life was extremely pleasant.

Of course, there might have been fewer entertainment activities, but that era itself had fewer entertainment options; living a simpler life wasn’t really a problem.

But now, everything here has been covered by thick snow, with almost no traces of human habitation visible.

"According to the information provided by the Lucia side, we should be at the train station, located on the west side of the town. The town center has an office building with an underground passage leading to the military base. We can access it through the underground passage connecting the train station to the office building," the officer explained to Chen Xin, pointing to the map labels in Russian provided by the Lucians.

Looking at the map, Chen Xin, unable to understand the Russian text, paid no further attention to the map and simply told the officer, "Since you’re in charge of the operation, you make the decisions. But be cautious; it’s hard to guarantee that no defensive measures were left behind when Suyia withdrew from such a sealed military base back in the day."

"Rest assured, we’ve brought specialized explosive disposal engineers this time; there won’t be any issues," the officer replied solemnly. This was a consideration he’d already made, so not only did he bring in explosive disposal engineers, but he also equipped them with heavy explosive disposal mechas specifically developed by the military.

When Chen Xin saw the two engineers appear in the explosive disposal mechas, he raised an eyebrow and asked in surprise, "Are these modified from the heavy mechas I designed?"

"Yes, after analyzing the heavy mechas designed by you, the military’s weapon experts developed several different models based on it. This explosive disposal type is one of them, although it’s still in the testing phase. We borrowed these two units specifically for this mission, which also helps the design department with field testing," the officer explained to Chen Xin as the two heavy explosive disposal mechas entered the excavated train station interior, locating the passage to the underground.

It’s one of the hallmarks of Suyia’s architectural design from back in the day, with air raid shelters built underground.

In those days, this design was effective; in the event of an air raid, ground personnel could hide in bunkers and underground passages, effectively resisting the airstrike’s damage.

However, such designs seem to be a bit of a relic nowadays. After all, the depth of air raid shelters beneath most civilian buildings isn’t sufficient. Given modern bomb penetration, hiding in these old-style underground passages or bunkers could easily lead to being buried by collapsing earth layers.

Of course, the odds of civilian buildings getting hit by large bombs are low. If it’s just ordinary shelling or bombing, the above-ground buildings might be flattened, but people underground would still have a chance of surviving.

After the two heavy explosive disposal mechas entered the underground passage, they indeed found explosives left over from the Suyia era.

This is standard practice, after all, an important military base is sealed here, and it’s quite normal to leave some security measures in place at civilian facilities connected to the base to prevent unauthorized entry.

But these are relics from decades ago. Many of the setup methods and corresponding disarming techniques have long been known. With the help of the explosive disposal mechas, the two engineers quickly defused the explosives, allowing the other soldiers and staff to smoothly enter the underground passage.

After traversing the town through the underground passage, they walked a bit further and finally arrived at the mission’s destination, the secret military base sealed by Suyia for over half a century.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 476 - 474 Entering the Base (Support at the beginning of the month!)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

As the explosion-proof door of the military base, sealed for many years, opened, a gust of wind with a rotten odor blew out from inside.

However, Chen Xin and the others didn’t pay it any mind, directly walking in.

This wasn’t some reckless bravado, nor was it that they were unaware that such longtime-sealed underground facilities should be aired out before entry; it was simply that everyone was wearing fully enclosed protective suits.

Knowing that two of the three land cruisers were nuclear-powered, and according to the records provided by Lucia, the power sources of these land cruisers had not been dismantled when they were sealed.

In other words, except for the prototype that uses the Energy Crystal, the other two land cruisers still contain nuclear reactors.

Considering the potential radiation contamination and leakage issues after being stored for so many years, everyone cautiously donned fully enclosed protective suits capable of isolating radiation contamination to avoid inadvertently exposing themselves to excessive radiation and ending their lives here.

"There is indeed radiation leakage, but it doesn’t seem serious." Inside the base, the Geiger counter in a staff member’s hand started to beep, but the frequency was not fast, and the radiation dose hadn’t reached a dangerous level.

The officer heard the staff’s report and instructed the waiting soldiers next to him: "Search the entire base, and stay safe!"

"Yes!" One of the soldiers in charge responded, dividing the soldiers entering the base into several teams to begin the search.

Meanwhile, Chen Xin, along with the officer and several staff members, headed towards the deeper parts of the base under the protection of several soldiers.

Everyone was aware that the radiation originated from the sealed three land cruisers, and there was no need to deliberately seek it out. Lucia’s map had already clearly marked where these were stored; they just needed to follow the map.

As for why they were tasked to search this long-sealed base, it was because, according to the data provided by Lucia, this place wasn’t only storing the three land cruisers.

As a base for conducting secret military experiments and testing various equipment, it also held numerous items left over from the Suyia era.

Not only were there various experimental equipment and test records from those days but also many other items left behind when evacuated.

After all, according to the data from Lucia, this base was abandoned after the dissolution of Suyia, and a large amount of materials and records were left behind during the evacuation; these items were of great value, even after decades, and were considered quite an outstanding haul worthy of retrieval.

Additionally, their other responsibility was to eliminate potential dangers that might exist in this base.

No matter what, it was an old base sealed for decades; no one knew if any changes occurred over these years or if the items stored had expired or turned into dangerous substances.

Therefore, as the soldiers searched the entire base, they were also examining these potential safety hazards.

As the soldiers proceeded with the search and gradually uncovered some gains, Chen Xin and the others arrived at a huge warehouse within the base and saw the sealed three land cruisers.

Although Chen Xin had seen photographs and clearly understood how large these things were, the comparison between a photograph and personally standing in front of them to feel their enormity was a completely different experience.

The massive land cruisers, over ten meters tall, were covered with a huge dark green woolen blanket, dust-covered, looking almost like a dull hill under the floodlights of several soldiers around.

Even though they were covered with woolen blankets, they couldn’t hide the fierceness and majesty of the iron beasts underneath, with the two leveled 305mm cannon barrels propping the blankets up; if not carefully discerned, one might even mistake it for a small mountain.

Even with the cannon barrels level, the space between the barrels and under the blanket was still spacious enough to fit all of Chen Xin and the others without feeling crowded.

"Uncover them, uncover all three." Chen Xin glanced at the lined-up three land cruisers, indicating to remove the blankets.

Several soldiers heard Chen Xin’s words and began to uncover the blankets on the land cruisers bit by bit, even climbing up for convenience.

As the soldiers moved, the blanket sealed for over thirty years was gradually pulled away, dust rising with their actions, temporarily affecting everyone’s vision, but simultaneously seeming to amplify the imposing presence of the iron beasts seeing daylight after more than thirty years.

"Truly magnificent! I can’t imagine how shocking it would be if this thing fired a shot!" Chen Xin shone his flashlight on the cannon barrels, looking at the barrel thick enough for a person to crawl into and couldn’t help but marvel.

Not only Chen Xin, but everyone else also shared the same thought as they watched the cannon barrels, too thick for a person to embrace.

In modern warfare, although artillery such as this old yet remarkably effective heavy weapon is still used, as human technology has advanced, we have more efficient and direct means, and artillery calibers have not been made increasingly large but rather smaller.

Of course, this hasn’t decreased the potency of artillery; it just made it increasingly rare to witness the spectacular scene of artillery with calibers over 150mm firing.

Yet before them now were six super guns that in the past could only be mounted on battleships or large fortresses.

"Speaking of which, are there shells here? Can we try firing one?" Chen Xin eagerly turned to the officer next to him and asked, "Since you brought me here to attempt to take these things back, it’s reasonable to inspect whether they’re still functional, isn’t it?"

"Uh, this..." The officer felt a little awkward, unsure whether to agree to Chen Xin’s request.

The higher-ups instructed Chen Xin to bring back the components, referring to the Energy Crystals of the prototype and the chassis of these three land cruisers. As for the cannon barrels, they were not particularly favored; after all, wouldn’t the launch container of a DF missile be preferable to these bulky barrels?

Two shots from these massive cannons might be slower than a dozen DF missiles flattening the target.

Nonetheless, though phrased as such, the officer secretly wanted to witness what firing the 305mm giant cannon would look like himself.

He hesitated for a moment, gazing at the land cruiser before him, then somewhat awkwardly asked Chen Xin: "Is this test necessary, Chen Academician?"

"Well, since we’re bringing them back, naturally, it’s better to take them back intact. If the cannon barrels can’t fire or have issues, it’s better to disassemble them now to avoid taking up space later." Chen Xin shrugged and replied.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 477 - 475 Preparation for Sealing the Heap (Seeking Guaranteed Monthly Tickets at the Beginning of the Month)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

It’s said that they were going to conduct an artillery test, but in reality, firing a shot is not an easy task.

These three land cruisers, though perfectly preserved when sealed, after more than thirty years since the disintegration of Suyia, whether these behemoths can still operate remains an unknown.

Thirty years is enough time for a baby to grow into an adult, get married and have children, for a piece of metal to rust beyond recognition, and for two metal ingots placed together to bond completely.

After going through thirty years, these three land cruisers face problems like part aging, rust, nuclear reactor radiation pollution and leakage. A thorough inspection and overhaul are needed to determine if these things can still be used.

Fortunately, these cruisers were sealed in a secret military base with comprehensive preservation facilities. Although it’s technology from over thirty years ago, at least those years have not caused significant damage to the land cruisers.

However, except for the prototype using the Energy Crystal as a power source, the nuclear reactors of the other two land cruisers inevitably suffered damage during over thirty years of idleness, with radiation leaks and pollution turning them into massive sources of radiation pollution.

Though their metal shells partially prevent radiation leaks, this also makes the internal radiation level of the two land cruisers off the charts. Even with Protective Suits, entering is enough to instantly get contaminated by a large amount of radiation, posing a life-threatening risk.

Upon measuring the radiation levels of the two nuclear-powered land cruisers, neither Chen Xin nor the officers were interested in bringing these two back.

What they wanted were usable devices, not two large nuclear radiation pollution sources.

However, these things couldn’t just be left alone. Though they posed no real danger of a catastrophe like in Che City, it’s still better to deal with them.

Though humans can’t enter, robots and instruments can still get inside the land cruisers for inspections.

"Seems like Suyia’s nuclear technology back then was indeed quite solid. Not only did the nuclear beacon operate beyond its service life for so many years, but these two nuclear reactors have only minor leaks after thirty years. Their quality is truly remarkable." Chen Xin couldn’t help but praise the technology and quality of the Suyia era upon seeing the footage shot by the robot.

In fact, not only was the nuclear reactor technology solid, but the overall quality of Suyia-era goods was excellent. Even the military base that was sealed for over thirty years restored power after Chen Xin and the team found a power source and rebooted the generator. The thirty-year-old lighting facilities still functioned normally.

"That said, this reactor can’t be used anymore, right? Do we really have to deal with this thing?" The officer frowned as he looked at the radiation sign captured in the footage.

It’s not that he didn’t want to deal with these two nuclear reactors. Such things constantly leaking nuclear radiation are extremely dangerous. Even though there’s no concern now about someone accidentally digging them up or doing something dangerous, they remained a threat as long as they were here.

But now, the nuclear radiation inside the two land cruisers was already deadly, and they couldn’t let soldiers and workers risk their lives to deal with them.

After all, this wasn’t on their own territory, there was no need for sacrifices.

"It’s necessary to deal with it a bit, this is Siberia, if radiation leaks and spreads into the atmosphere, flowing southwards, it could reach Flame Country." Although Chen Xin didn’t want to solve the historical issues left from the Suyia era for Lucia, he still hoped to handle it properly as it might affect Flame Country.

However, Chen Xin wasn’t someone who would risk lives for such things. He thought for a moment and then said, "My nano armor can withstand nuclear radiation, I’ll go in to deal with it, dismantle the reactors and then seal them."

"Let me check first to see if the reactors can be removed. Do you have the blueprints for these things? Are there instructions for replacing the reactors? Otherwise, we’ll have to dismantle them forcibly."

Saying this, Chen Xin activated the watch on his wrist, and a layer of nano molecules covered his body, transforming into a thick Power Armor.

"Academician Chen!" The officer quickly grabbed Chen Xin’s wrist, stopping him: "That’s not possible! Your importance is above all else! Even if we all have to go in, we can’t let you take the risk!"

The officer shouted to the soldiers nearby, who came to carry Chen Xin away.

Although the power of the Nano Armor could easily allow escape, Chen Xin didn’t do so. He simply persuaded the officer: "There’s no danger, the Nano Armor can completely block nuclear radiation. It’s safer for me to go in than for any of you."

"No way! Even if a soldier wore your Nano Armor to go in, you still can’t go in!" The officer insisted, firmly opposed to any risky actions from Chen Xin.

Seeing the officer’s firm stance, Chen Xin had to give up on personally going inside. He thought for a moment and said, "In that case, don’t let anyone go in and risk it. I’ll switch the Nano Armor to remote control mode, let the armor go in, and I’ll stay outside to operate it."

Actually, the Nano Armor didn’t have such a function, Chen Xin was going to upgrade it temporarily to enable remote control mode.

With these words, a green light flashed over the entire armor, and the nano molecules flowed down from Chen Xin like mercury. In front of him, they shaped into a silver humanoid and then appeared as armor.

The remaining nano molecules on Chen Xin’s body gathered on his head, forming a pair of Brainwave Control Glasses.

Seeing this scene, the officer finally signaled the two soldiers holding Chen Xin to release him.

While controlling the Nano Armor to move into a land cruiser, Chen Xin asked the officer beside him: "Did you bring Neutron Absorbers?"

"We didn’t, but the Suyia people prepared them back then. We found a batch of usable boron and lead-containing cement in the warehouse." The officer reported to Chen Xin. According to the records found, this batch of materials was brought after the Che City incident.

"Looks like the Suyia people intended to seal the reactors back then?" Chen Xin was surprised but didn’t dwell on it much. He simply instructed the officer: "Get the materials ready for sealing the reactors, and arrange for radiation protection. If the reactors can’t be directly removed, we might need to dismantle the entire setup."

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Chapter 478 - 476: 2 Lithium Crystals
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

The handling of the nuclear reactor was far more troublesome than Chen Xin had imagined.

The nano armor smoothly entered the interiors of the two land cruisers and inspected the condition of the nuclear reactors. These two small nuclear reactors were models used in nuclear submarines from the Suyia era; their inherent quality wasn’t bad, and even after being left for so many years, they only had minor leaks.

But even minor leaks in nuclear reactors are very serious incidents, especially when such leaks could have persisted for quite a long time, causing the entire base to become flooded with radiation, making the need for handling increasingly urgent, yet increasingly difficult.

The reactor casing had already been corroded by radiation, causing the entire reactor to deform. Trying to remove it was no longer possible using the original normal installation method.

Furthermore, Suyia’s design for the land cruisers did not account for replacing reactors every so often, meaning that to remove the reactor now, a big hole must be cut in the entire land cruiser.

Just like replacing the reactor on a nuclear-powered aircraft carrier requires cutting open the entire deck, replacing the reactor on a land cruiser requires cutting open its casing.

After all, typically only changing the nuclear fuel is necessary; once a reactor is installed, it could potentially be used until the equipment is decommissioned, lasting decades without needing replacement. Who would think to dismantle a reactor for no reason?

But now, this issue becomes one that Chen Xin must face: how to dismantle this reactor with a corroded casing from within the thick armor of the land cruiser.

"We can cut from underneath the chassis. The chassis of this land cruiser is a )( type structure mounted on four track bases, so there is enough space underneath." A technician who came along pointed at the structural cross-section diagram of the land cruiser and said, "We can cut open a gap here and then build a concrete sarcophagus here. After dismantling the reactor core, we can place it directly into the sarcophagus through the gap, then seal the sarcophagus as quickly as possible!"

Chen Xin glanced at the diagram, used the nano molecules to simulate the structure of the land cruiser, demonstrated it once, then nodded and said, "This plan indeed works and could minimize harm and impact, I think it’s viable."

Having found all the design plans for the land cruiser, Chen Xin and the others gained a comprehensive understanding of the large beast before them.

According to Suyia’s design, there were three paths available for removing the reactor installed inside the land cruiser.

First, naturally, was the earlier mentioned method of cutting open a gap from underneath the land cruiser to dismantle the reactor core.

Besides this, a door at the rear of this behemoth could be opened, designed for the loading of ammunition and personnel escape. After opening, it was very close to the reactor and could serve as a path for transporting the reactor out.

However, compared to cutting from underneath, using this path requires more work; not only does it require a crane, but also cutting open some internal structures of the land cruiser to clear the path to the reactor, demanding substantial construction work.

As for the other path, it’s not as troublesome as it doesn’t require cutting; it was the original method Suyia used to install the reactor, which simply involves using a crane to lift the double-barreled 305mm turret on top of the land cruiser, allowing the complete dismantling of the reactor.

This method is indeed simple, but it requires a large gantry crane capable of lifting the twin 305mm turret.

Though this military base is well-equipped with various facilities, it lacks such a large gantry crane, only having a small crane used for lifting shells.

So after some discussion, the choice was made to cut from underneath to open a path accessible from outside to the reactor.

Since the plan was settled, everyone didn’t waste time. Several soldiers began preparing the sarcophagus needed for sealing the reactor, while Chen Xin controlled the nano armor once again to enter the land cruiser, starting preparation for cutting its interior and also preparing to dismantle the reactor.

However, the extreme cold made preparing the sarcophagus for sealing the reactor not a simple task, so this could not be rushed and preparations had to be done slowly.

While controlling the nano armor to cut within one of the land cruiser’s interiors to prepare the passage for taking out the reactor, Chen Xin finally entered the interior of the prototype machine, seeing the vital target of his journey— the Energy Crystal.

To be honest, Chen Xin had high expectations for this thing before arriving; he thought it should be similar to the Cosmic Rubik’s Cube, something high-energy, or look extremely cool or have a sci-fi feel.

But when he finally saw the Energy Crystal, the expression on Chen Xin’s face could be summarized in two words: "Just this?"

The Energy Crystal embedded by the Suyians within this prototype machine as an energy source looked dim and very badly damaged, entirely resembling a scorched crystal stone left from an explosion site.

If not for the few faint traces of orange light inside it, Chen Xin would have truly thought the original Energy Crystal was swapped.

With final curiosity, Chen Xin cautiously dismantled this thing from the frame fixing it, then packed it in the container he brought, and took it outside intending to analyze it with instruments.

There was actually a more efficient way, which was checking through the system’s upgrade interface. Up to this point, Chen Xin hadn’t found anything that the system upgrade interface couldn’t identify.

And indeed, after taking out the Energy Crystal from the prototype machine, he did just that.

"Damaged Lithium Crystal"

"Upgrade Plan One: Repair crystal structure, points needed for upgrade: 120000"

"Upgrade Plan Two: Fill internal energy, points needed for upgrade: 1100"

Chen Xin was somewhat surprised looking at the so-called Lithium Crystal under the size of a fist, not expecting such an inconspicuous piece of junk to require so many survival points to upgrade.

This was still the first time he saw something so small requiring six-digit survival points; this greatly exceeded Chen Xin’s expectations.

And even with the help of the system, Chen Xin still couldn’t understand what this was, or what function it had.

However, based on the upgrade plans the system displayed, this Lithium Crystal seemed broken, and unlike the so-called Energy Crystal, because the survival points required for filling internal energy were too few compared to repairing the whole crystal structure, it was indeed vastly different.
